title: Move remaining integrated status-bar rendering into yazelix_bar adapter
description: Continue the status-bar ownership split by moving generic integrated zjstatus command-definition and placeholder rendering into the yazelix_bar child crate/API where it is not Yazelix-session-specific. Keep runtime helper paths, status-cache ownership, cursor/session facts, and Home Manager apply semantics in Yazelix core. This is a child-repo transfer candidate only if the main repo deletes duplicated generic rendering instead of adding a broad adapter.
acceptance_criteria: The bead records a LOC scorecard for zellij_materialization.rs and status-bar helper code, moves only generic rendering into yazelix_bar or explicitly rejects the move, keeps the Yazelix adapter thin, preserves integrated zjstatus behavior tests, and estimates main-repo LOC removed versus child-repo LOC added.
notes:
Created from yazelix-wn52 master audit as a top-three follow-up. Expected main-repo impact: likely 300-900 Rust lines from zellij_materialization/status-bar rendering if the child API absorbs generic command placeholders without duplicating Yazelix session/cache ownership.
Completed 2026-05-08. Moved generic integrated zjstatus command-definition KDL rendering into yazelix-bar at commit 1846686fee43a16dbf8b9252025836965de9af66. Yazelix core now keeps only resolved runtime helper path construction and calls yazelix_bar::render_yazelix_runtime_command_definitions. Main LOC scorecard: zellij_materialization.rs 2,994 -> 2,857 raw lines; tracked main Rust 73,220 -> 73,083 raw lines; tokei Rust code 67,108 -> 66,977. Child LOC cost: +169 Rust lines and +2 README lines in yazelix-bar. Verification: child cargo fmt --check && cargo test; main cargo fmt --all -- --check; cargo check --workspace; cargo test -p yazelix_core zellij_materialization; nix build .#runtime --no-link; nix build .#yazelix_bar --no-link; validate-rust-ownership-budget; validate-contracts; validate-docs-experience; validate-flake-interface.
close_reason: Completed: moved generic integrated zjstatus command-definition rendering into yazelix-bar, kept Yazelix runtime helper path/status-cache/session ownership in main, ratcheted the Rust budget to 73,083, and verified Rust/Nix/docs/flake gates.

title: Reroll Ghostty random cursor effects for every Yazelix window
description:
When terminal.ghostty_trail_color, terminal.ghostty_trail_effect, or terminal.ghostty_mode_effect is set to "random", Yazelix currently resolves that random value when generated Ghostty terminal configs are regenerated. Desktop fast-path launches can then reuse the same generated Ghostty config across many windows, so random can look stuck on one palette such as cosmic/purple.

Make Ghostty random cursor settings reroll once per new Yazelix Ghostty window while keeping fixed settings stable and without mutating yazelix.toml.
design: Add a narrow launch-time reroll seam for Yazelix-managed Ghostty configs. Before launching a Ghostty terminal candidate, if terminal.config_mode = "yazelix" and any Ghostty cursor setting is random, regenerate the Ghostty terminal config and matching generated effect shaders from one resolved cursor state. Keep non-Ghostty terminals and fixed Ghostty cursor settings unaffected. Do not trigger broad Yazi/Zellij generated-state repair just to reroll cursor visuals.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Each new Yazelix Ghostty window launch rerolls random Ghostty cursor palette/effects.
2. Generated Ghostty config and generated effect shaders still use the same resolved palette for that launch.
3. Fixed Ghostty cursor settings do not force launch-time rewrites.
4. Desktop fast path and normal launch path share the same behavior.
5. Non-Ghostty terminal launches are unaffected.
close_reason: Completed: desktop fast-path Ghostty launches now reroll random cursor palette/effect settings before each Yazelix window while fixed palettes stay stable; generated config and effect shaders continue sharing one resolved cursor state.

title: Define cross-window Yazelix session/update semantics
description: Define the product contract for how multiple Yazelix windows interact with shared runtime state, shared materialized state, and live Zellij sessions.
design:
Yazelix now has a clear runtime model, but its cross-window/session behavior is still under-specified. This epic should define the session contract that later fixes can rely on instead of continuing to infer behavior from launch helpers, Zellij defaults, or stale shell state.

The contract should separate:
- shared durable state: user config, installed/runtime code, generated configs, launch-profile state, rebuild hashes
- live session state: the actual Zellij session and attached clients in each open Yazelix window

The default non-persistent mode should be defined as independent live sessions that happen to consume the same durable state. New launches may use newer shared state than older still-open windows, but open windows must not be silently hot-swapped under the user.

Persistent-session mode should be defined as a different product contract: multiple windows are clients of one named logical session. Path/bootstrap semantics, restart scope, and last-client behavior are different there and must be called out explicitly instead of being treated as edge cases.

This epic should also anchor later session-lifecycle fixes such as detached zero-client Zellij servers burning CPU after windows close. Those bugs should be evaluated against the explicit non-persistent versus persistent contract rather than treated as generic cleanup quirks.
acceptance_criteria:
1. The docs define shared durable runtime/materialized state separately from live window/session state.
2. The docs define the default non-persistent behavior as independent Yazelix sessions/windows rather than implicit reattachment or hot replacement.
3. The docs define persistent-session behavior as multiple clients of one named logical session, with different restart/path/last-client semantics from the default mode.
4. The docs define how `yzx refresh`, `yzx update runtime`, and `yzx restart` relate to already-open windows instead of leaving that behavior implicit.
5. The contract is concrete enough to guide follow-up fixes for detached-session lifecycle bugs, desktop launch, and cross-window update UX.
notes:
Child split:
- `yazelix-1zti`: default non-persistent multi-window semantics
- `yazelix-vxrb`: persistent-session multi-window semantics
- `yazelix-jehj`: refresh/update/restart semantics across open windows

Delivered specs:
- `docs/specs/nonpersistent_window_session_contract.md`
- `docs/specs/persistent_window_session_contract.md`
- `docs/specs/open_window_update_transition_contract.md`

Concrete bug anchor:
- `yazelix-0ol6` captures the observed failure mode where detached zero-client Zellij servers kept burning CPU after windows closed. The fix should be evaluated against this contract rather than patched in isolation.
close_reason: Completed: documented the cross-window session/update contract across non-persistent windows, persistent-session mode, and open-window refresh/update/restart transitions.

title: Collapse the remaining yzx_core and yzx_control bridge owners
description: The remaining Nushell bridge around `yzx_core` and `yzx_control` still owns request shaping, runtime checks, and error-surface glue in places like `config_parser.nu` and `runtime_contract_checker.nu`. Track the real delete-first bridge-collapse lane so those owners shrink or disappear instead of hardening into permanent architecture.
design: Do not turn this into a vague Rustification bucket. Define the exact remaining bridge owners first, then collapse them only where Rust can become the single typed owner for the same decision logic. Keep shell/process orchestration and host integration in Nushell when they are still the natural owners. Success is deleting or materially shrinking real Nu bridge owners, not adding one more helper layer.
acceptance_criteria: 1. A first child defines the exact deletion budget and names the remaining bridge owners in scope. 2. A follow-up implementation child deletes or materially shrinks real bridge owners such as `config_parser.nu` and `runtime_contract_checker.nu` without duplicating the same logic in Rust and Nu. 3. The lane records which bridge logic is irreducibly Nushell-owned so future beads do not reopen the same ambiguity.
close_reason: all steps complete

title: Define the deletion budget for the yzx_core and yzx_control bridge-collapse lane
description: Before touching the remaining `yzx_core` / `yzx_control` bridge owners, inventory the live bridge layer around `config_parser.nu`, `runtime_contract_checker.nu`, and adjacent request/error-surface helpers. Decide which decision logic can move behind one typed Rust owner, which glue should stay in Nushell, and which bridge files can simply be deleted.
design: Use the live code and the current migration matrix, not older broad rewrite assumptions. Classify each surviving bridge owner as one of: delete now, collapse into an existing Rust helper, keep in Nushell as irreducible shell/process glue, or defer because the current owner boundary is still mixed. The output must name the exact files and the exact success metric for the implementation bead.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The bead names the exact remaining bridge owners in scope, including `config_parser.nu`, `runtime_contract_checker.nu`, and any adjacent helpers that still own the same request/error logic. 2. It states clearly which owners should collapse into Rust and which should stay in Nushell. 3. It defines the deletion budget and verification target for the implementation child.
notes:
2026-04-21 deletion budget recorded from the live code:
- collapse now: generic yzx_core helper transport and error shaping should no longer live inside `config_parser.nu`; default `require_yazelix_runtime_dir` plus default-error-surface request boilerplate should not be duplicated across report and preflight shims
- keep in Nushell for now: config-specific normalize/report behavior in `config_parser.nu`; startup/launch and doctor-specific request assembly in `runtime_contract_checker.nu` where it still feeds shell/process orchestration or user-facing rendering
- defer to other lanes: doctor/install report family collapse belongs to `yazelix-osco`; Yazi/Zellij compatibility-wrapper deletion belongs to `yazelix-vf0u`; launch/startup orchestration and materialization_orchestrator cleanup are separate lanes
- implementation success metric for `057w.2`: move the shared helper bridge into one minimal owner, update existing callers to consume that owner directly, and materially shrink `config_parser.nu` without adding another wrapper layer
close_reason: Completed: the deletion budget now names the immediate bridge collapse, the intentionally Nushell-owned glue, the deferred follow-up lanes, and the exact success metric for the implementation slice

title: Implement the remaining yzx_core and yzx_control bridge collapse
description: Collapse the remaining `yzx_core` / `yzx_control` bridge owners according to the deletion budget from the first child. The main targets are the surviving `config_parser.nu` / `runtime_contract_checker.nu` style owners and any adjacent bridge glue that still owns the same typed decision logic.
design: Delete first. Do not add more wrapper layers. Move only the decision logic that can become singly owned by Rust, keep irreducible shell/process glue in Nushell, and remove the duplicate bridge code that remains after that move. Success is specific bridge owners shrinking or disappearing end-to-end, not a broader public CLI rewrite.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The implementation follows the explicit deletion budget from the parent lane. 2. At least one real bridge owner in the `config_parser.nu` / `runtime_contract_checker.nu` cluster disappears or materially shrinks. 3. The remaining Nushell glue is clearly limited to irreducible host/shell behavior, and focused regressions defend the new boundary.
notes:
2026-04-21 implementation result:
- added `nushell/scripts/utils/yzx_core_bridge.nu` as the single minimal Nu owner for generic `yzx_core` helper path resolution, argv/JSON transport, and shared error-surface rendering
- removed generic helper transport ownership from `config_parser.nu`; it now keeps config-normalize-specific request shaping, config diagnostic handling, and `parse_yazelix_config`
- updated the existing helper consumers (`runtime_env.nu`, `config_state.nu`, `materialization_orchestrator.nu`, `launch_yazelix.nu`, `yazi_config_merger.nu`, `zellij_config_merger.nu`, doctor/install report bridges, `nushell_externs.nu`, `core/yazelix.nu`, `yzx/menu.nu`) to import the shared bridge directly instead of treating `config_parser.nu` as the generic transport owner
- added `run_yzx_core_runtime_request_json_command` so `runtime_contract_checker.nu` no longer re-owns the default runtime-dir plus default-error-surface request boilerplate for the two preflight helper commands
- recorded the new boundary in `docs/specs/rust_migration_matrix.md` and `docs/subsystem_code_inventory.md`
- verification: `nix develop -c nu nushell/scripts/dev/validate_syntax.nu`; popup bridge regressions; Helix runtime-env regression; workspace launch/preflight regressions; source-checkout helper-selection regression; config-parser invalid-config regression; `nix develop -c nu nushell/scripts/dev/validate_specs.nu`; `git diff --check`
- resulting boundary: `yzx_core_bridge.nu` is the shared transport seam, `config_parser.nu` is config-specific, and the remaining `runtime_contract_checker.nu` logic is still intentionally Nu-owned startup/launch/doctor request glue rather than duplicated typed classification
close_reason: Completed: the generic yzx_core transport moved into one shared bridge owner, config_parser lost bridge ownership, and the main helper consumers now call the shared bridge directly while the remaining Nu preflight glue stays intentionally shell-bound

title: Fix custom sidebar command argument handling
description: Setting [editor].sidebar_command to tools such as lazygit or opencode currently passes the default launch_sidebar_yazi.nu path as an app argument, so lazygit treats it as an invalid git subcommand and opencode treats it as a directory. Custom sidebar commands should not inherit the default Yazi launcher arg unless the user explicitly configured sidebar_args.
design: Trace the config normalization and Zellij layout render path for sidebar_command/sidebar_args. Preserve the default managed Yazi launcher, but make non-default sidebar_command values run without the default launch_sidebar_yazi.nu argument unless sidebar_args is explicitly present in user or Home Manager config. Add behavior-backed tests for lazygit/opencode-style custom sidebar commands and keep the config/Home Manager surface in sync.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Default config still renders sidebar_command=nu with the managed launch_sidebar_yazi.nu argument. 2. A user config that sets sidebar_command="lazygit" without sidebar_args renders no inherited Yazi launcher argument. 3. A user config that sets sidebar_command="opencode" without sidebar_args renders no inherited Yazi launcher argument. 4. Explicit sidebar_args are still respected for custom commands. 5. Tests defend the default and custom-command behavior.
close_reason: Completed: custom sidebar commands such as lazygit and opencode no longer inherit the default Yazi launcher argument; default Nu+Yazi adapter behavior and explicit custom args are preserved; generated-state fingerprinting now uses the effective sidebar args so stale broken layouts regenerate.

title: Move native pane and tab movement defaults to Ctrl Alt HJKL
description: Change Yazelix's default native Zellij movement policy from Ctrl Shift H/L and Ctrl Shift J/K to Ctrl Alt H/L and Ctrl Alt J/K so Ghostty's Ctrl Shift J write_screen_file binding no longer intercepts pane movement. Update settings defaults, action registry defaults, docs, release notes, and focused tests.
close_reason: Completed: native tab and pane movement defaults now use Ctrl Alt H/L and Ctrl Alt J/K, existing writable settings repair exact old defaults, docs/release notes updated, and focused Rust plus config/upgrade validators pass.

title: kill enable sidebar
description:
Imported GitHub issue #569.

now that using the sidebar is good and easy, might not make sense disabling it anymore

what could be more useful is a setting for new tabs to always start with the sidebar closed
acceptance_criteria: The enable_sidebar decision preserves current sidebar users unless a replacement option covers the same need; a new-tab default closed/open policy is evaluated; Home Manager and direct config behavior stay in sync; no external user config is taken over automatically.
notes: 2026-04-26 subsystem review: evaluate removal of enable_sidebar only after the sidebar capability boundary is clear. The likely better user option is initial_sidebar_state or start_new_tabs_with_sidebar_closed, not silently deleting sidebar behavior.
close_reason: Completed: replaced the primary sidebar-disable surface with editor.initial_sidebar_state open/closed, kept legacy enable_sidebar as compatibility input, added collapsed managed-sidebar startup layouts, and synced the pane orchestrator so closed-base startup still opens/toggles through the managed sidebar contract.

title: Flatten user-editable Yazelix config surfaces
description:
Implement the accepted migration from nested Yazelix user-config paths to one flat user-editable config directory. Target contract: every Yazelix-owned user-editable config file lives directly under $YAZELIX_CONFIG_DIR, normally ~/.config/yazelix/. Generated/runtime state remains under $YAZELIX_STATE_DIR, normally ~/.local/share/yazelix/. Native app configs stay in their native locations unless explicitly imported or opted in.

Accepted flat paths:
- ~/.config/yazelix/yazelix.toml
- ~/.config/yazelix/cursors.toml
- ~/.config/yazelix/helix.toml
- ~/.config/yazelix/zellij.kdl
- ~/.config/yazelix/yazi.toml
- ~/.config/yazelix/yazi_keymap.toml
- ~/.config/yazelix/yazi_init.lua
- ~/.config/yazelix/terminal_ghostty.conf
- ~/.config/yazelix/terminal_kitty.conf
- ~/.config/yazelix/terminal_alacritty.toml
- ~/.config/yazelix/terminal_foot.ini
- ~/.config/yazelix/shell_bash.sh
- ~/.config/yazelix/shell_zsh.zsh
- ~/.config/yazelix/shell_fish.fish
- ~/.config/yazelix/shell_nu.nu
design:
Update Rust path ownership, bootstrap, yzx config/edit/reset/onboard/import/doctor surfaces, Home Manager manage_config output, docs, contracts, stubs, terminal/Yazi/Zellij/Helix materialization inputs, and tests together.

Naming decision: keep names flat and human-readable. Do not add a blanket yzx_ prefix because the parent directory already carries the Yazelix namespace. Use category prefixes only where the base filename would be ambiguous: terminal_* and shell_*.

Migration rules: auto-migrate only when unambiguous. Old-only regular files under user_configs/ should move/copy to the accepted flat path with visible output and backups or migration markers. Home Manager/Nix-store symlinks must not be mutated by startup code; report that the user must update/switch Home Manager. If old and new paths both exist with divergent content, fail with a clear duplicate-surface diagnostic. If both exist with identical content, prefer the new path and report the old path as removable. Preserve the rule against automatic takeover of native app configs outside Yazelix-owned paths.
acceptance_criteria: New installs and first launches create ~/.config/yazelix/yazelix.toml and any needed flat sidecar stubs directly under ~/.config/yazelix/. Home Manager manage_config generates the flat main config path. yzx config --path and yzx edit config report ~/.config/yazelix/yazelix.toml. yzx edit lists flat target paths for cursors, Helix, Zellij, Yazi, terminal overrides, and shell hooks. yzx reset config resets the flat main config and warns clearly about adjacent flat sidecars. Materializers read the accepted flat sidecar paths. Doctor reports old-only, new-only, identical duplicate, and divergent duplicate states with actionable remediation. Tests cover bootstrap, old-only migration, duplicate divergent paths, Home Manager target rendering, edit/reset/config path output, sidecar path resolution, and docs/contract references.
close_reason: Completed flat user-config surface migration, docs, validators, and focused regression coverage

title: Standardize canonical runtime env across Yazelix-managed pane launches
description: Yazelix-managed pane launches do not consistently normalize the canonical runtime/editor environment. The main session and managed editor panes use the shared runtime env contract, but popup/helper paths still rely on ambient inheritance in important places. That breaks tools like `jjui` that run inside a Yazelix popup and then spawn a nested editor for commit messages.
design:
Relevant code seams:
- `nushell/scripts/core/start_yazelix.nu` seeds the main Yazelix session with `with-env (get_runtime_env $config)`
- `nushell/scripts/utils/runtime_env.nu` defines the canonical runtime env, but currently exports `EDITOR` without `VISUAL`
- `nushell/scripts/integrations/zellij_runtime_wrappers.nu` and `nushell/scripts/integrations/zellij.nu` explicitly serialize/inject canonical env for managed editor panes
- `nushell/scripts/zellij_wrappers/yzx_popup_program.nu` now handles `popup_program = ["editor"]` through `run_runtime_argv`, but non-`editor` popup commands still use raw `run-external`
- `rust_plugins/zellij_pane_orchestrator/src/transient.rs` opens transient panes with an empty env map, so popup wrappers depend on whatever env the runtime wrapper/session happened to inherit

Implications:
- Ordinary Yazelix session panes should already inherit the main session env from startup, but helper/transient launches are less explicit and therefore less reliable
- The `jjui` symptom is likely a generic env-contract gap rather than a `jjui`-specific integration bug
- Official `jj` precedence is `JJ_EDITOR` > `ui.editor` > `VISUAL` > `EDITOR`, so adding generic `VISUAL` support is a better fix than exporting `JJ_EDITOR` globally

Likely direction:
- Standardize Yazelix-managed popup/helper launches on the same canonical runtime env contract used elsewhere
- Add `VISUAL` alongside `EDITOR` in the shared runtime env
- Avoid app-specific global exports like `JJ_EDITOR`
- Update env-contract docs if `VISUAL` becomes part of the stable surface
acceptance_criteria: Popup-launched tools receive the canonical Yazelix runtime env including EDITOR and VISUAL; tools launched from popup panes that spawn nested editors honor the configured editor without app-specific global env vars; managed editor panes and ordinary Yazelix session panes keep one consistent editor/runtime contract; regression tests cover popup env normalization and a jj-like nested-editor path
notes:
Implemented canonical popup runtime env propagation with VISUAL and restored explicit missing-popup PATH errors after routing popup commands through run_runtime_argv. Verified with popup and Helix managed-config contract tests. Remaining follow-up before closing: add a more literal jj-like nested-editor regression path if we want the acceptance criteria to be fully explicit.
Refactored yzx_popup_program.nu to resolve config, runtime env, popup argv, and command preflight through one popup launch-context helper. Behavior stayed the same; popup and Helix managed-config contract tests remain green.
2026-04-20 tracker hygiene: moved out of in_progress. The popup runtime-env and VISUAL propagation work was implemented earlier, but there is no active dirty tree for this lane right now. Remaining question before closure: either add a more literal jj-like nested-editor regression path or tighten the acceptance story around the existing popup/env contract tests.
2026-04-21 investigation: the bead design is now stale relative to the code. `nushell/scripts/zellij_wrappers/yzx_popup_program.nu` routes all popup commands through `run_runtime_argv`, so the old split between `popup_program = ["editor"]` and non-editor commands no longer exists. `nushell/scripts/utils/runtime_env.nu` now computes the canonical runtime env through `yzx_core`, and `nushell/scripts/integrations/zellij_runtime_wrappers.nu` carries `EDITOR` and `VISUAL` into managed floating/editor launches. The remaining acceptance question was whether we still needed a literal `jj` integration test. Current generic regressions are sufficient and more durable: `test_runtime_env_includes_visual_equal_to_editor`, `test_popup_program_receives_canonical_env_with_visual`, and `test_get_runtime_env_wraps_helix_with_managed_wrapper` prove the popup/helper env contract that a `jjui`-style nested-editor flow depends on without baking app-specific behavior into the suite.
close_reason: Completed: popup/helper launches now use the canonical runtime env with VISUAL plus EDITOR, and the existing popup/Helix contract regressions are sufficient generic proof for nested-editor flows without an app-specific jj integration test

title: Cut v16.1 release
description: Prepare and tag the v16.1 Yazelix release from the current main branch: write release notes, run the maintained version-bump flow, verify release metadata, and leave push/tag publication gated on explicit approval.
acceptance_criteria: CHANGELOG.md and docs/upgrade_notes.toml contain v16.1 notes; YAZELIX_VERSION is v16.1; README latest release surface is updated; the release commit and annotated v16.1 tag exist locally; release validation passes.
notes:
Prepared v16.1 unreleased release notes for Home Manager config ownership, local-input packaging speed, screen rendering stability, and Zellij status-widget fixes. Initial repo_upgrade_contract test passed before release-note prep commit.
Release flow completed locally. Version bump created v16.1 metadata, README latest-release surface, YAZELIX_VERSION update, release commit, and annotated local tag. Validation passed: cargo test -p yazelix_core, validate-upgrade-contract, validate-readme-version, git diff --check, and nix build .#runtime --no-link.

title: Regenerate shell initializers from stable runtime after Home Manager switch
description:
A temporary local runtime symlink such as result_yazelix_helix_runtime can be baked into ~/.local/share/yazelix/initializers/* when that runtime launches Yazelix. If the symlink is removed, normal user shells that source the generated initializers fail before prompt setup, even after Home Manager switches to a fresh Yazelix runtime. The local recovery is to run the active profile yzx_control generate_shell_initializers with a clean PATH headed by ~/.nix-profile/toolbin and ~/.nix-profile/bin, but the product should make this self-healing.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. Home Manager activation or the managed profile runtime refresh regenerates shell initializers from the active stable profile/runtime path, not from stale local result symlinks.
2. Running Yazelix from a local ./result* runtime cannot leave deleted result paths in ~/.local/share/yazelix/initializers after the stable runtime is active.
3. Doctor or startup diagnostics detect deleted paths in generated initializers and report a clear repair command.
4. Tests or a focused validator cover stale initializer paths and the repair/regeneration path.
close_reason: Implemented Home Manager activation regeneration from the active runtime with runtime-first PATH, added doctor detection for deleted transient result* initializer paths, and covered it with shell-initializer and Home Manager ownership tests plus config/runtime validation.

Core hook: rustfmt --edition 2024 --check for ^rust_core/.*\.rs$. Pane orchestrator hook: rustfmt --edition 2021 --check for ^rust_plugins/zellij_pane_orchestrator/.*\.rs$. Updated docs/yazelix_collection.md to recommend  for incremental checks instead of . title: Replace initial sidebar setting with hide-sidebar-on-file-open
notes: Replace editor.initial_sidebar_state with editor.hide_sidebar_on_file_open. The old startup-state setting is misleading because each tab owns a managed sidebar/Yazi pane; the useful behavior is whether the sidebar is hidden after opening a file from the Yazi sidebar. Use hide rather than close because the pane remains managed and available. No legacy alias unless explicitly requested.
close_reason: Completed: replaced editor.initial_sidebar_state with editor.hide_sidebar_on_file_open, wired file-open sidebar hiding, removed old alias support, updated Home Manager/default config/docs, and verified Rust/config validators.

title: Fix workspace widget stale bootstrap roots
description: The status-bar workspace widget can show the wrong directory when switching tabs because the pane orchestrator exposes bootstrap workspace state from the session initial cwd for tabs that do not have an explicit workspace root. The widget then looks live because status cache refreshes on focus/tab events, but the displayed root is often stale or unrelated to the active tab.
acceptance_criteria: Workspace status facts must not present a stale initial-cwd bootstrap root as the active tab directory; explicit workspace roots remain authoritative; non-explicit tabs should use a current tab-local observed cwd when one is available or avoid showing a misleading root; pane-orchestrator source, tests, synced wasm, and main repo sync stamp are updated.
notes: Implemented local fix across child repos: yazelix-zellij-bar commit 09f36a3 switches the workspace tray token to a zjstatus pipe widget; yazelix-zellij-pane-orchestrator commit b3e2057 targets the active tab's zjstatus plugin and pushes explicit workspace labels through the workspace pipe. Main repo has synced the new pane-orchestrator wasm and permission/cache-renderer updates locally. Remaining integration blocker: push child commits and update main flake.lock so Yazelix consumes the new child bar template from GitHub.
close_reason: Completed: workspace label now uses a child-owned zjstatus pipe widget fed by the pane orchestrator for the active tab only; stale bootstrap roots are hidden in the legacy cache renderer; pane-orchestrator wasm is rebuilt/synced; child repos are pushed; main flake.lock consumes yazelix-zellij-bar 09f36a3. Verified child cargo tests, focused main status/materialization tests, permission materialization, pane-orchestrator sync validator, contracts, Rust traceability, and flake interface.

title: Make the pane orchestrator own sidebar identity and remove the sidebar cache from correctness paths
description: Stop relying on the filesystem sidebar-state cache as part of normal correctness for current-tab sidebar targeting. Move sidebar identity and related targeting knowledge behind the pane orchestrator contract so workspace retarget, reveal, refresh, and focus flows stop depending on 'last seen' Yazi cache state.
design: The current workspace/session contract is strongest where the pane orchestrator owns per-tab truth, and weakest where Yazi identity still leaks through cache files. Extend the plugin protocol or state model so the orchestrator can answer the sidebar-targeting questions Nushell actually needs. Keep any remaining filesystem cache only as integration telemetry, bootstrap hinting, or debugging aid, not as a correctness dependency for active-tab operations.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Active-tab sidebar targeting no longer depends on scanning ~/.local/share/yazelix/state/yazi/sidebar for correctness. 2. Workspace-retarget and reveal flows use plugin-owned sidebar identity or an equivalent explicit contract. 3. Cross-tab contamination risk from stale cache files is materially reduced. 4. If a cache remains, its reduced role is documented explicitly.
notes: Pipeline stage 2A. This is the first hot-path workspace ownership move: sidebar identity must stop depending on cache files before broader workspace targeting truth can move behind the pane orchestrator.
close_reason: Completed: the pane orchestrator now stores sidebar Yazi identity keyed by the managed sidebar pane id, Nushell sidebar targeting queries that plugin-owned state instead of scanning sidebar cache files, the cache is documented as telemetry only, and the synced pane-orchestrator wasm carries the new contract.

title: Add repairable install-artifact staleness checks to yzx doctor
description: Teach yzx doctor to detect stale or broken installed Yazelix artifacts that are repairable from userland, such as desktop entries, shell hooks, CLI symlinks, runtime symlinks, and generated config references. Focus on actionable diagnosis with exact repair commands rather than maintainer-only invariants.
design: Keep yzx doctor focused on user-facing installed-state health, not repo parity. Start with artifact families that have already broken in practice during no-clone/flake installs. Checks should distinguish missing, broken, and stale-but-repairable states and print the minimal repair command for each case.
acceptance_criteria: 1. There is a bead-level plan for repairable install-state staleness checks in yzx doctor. 2. The plan explicitly includes desktop-entry staleness and distinguishes it from broader install-state drift. 3. The plan keeps maintainer-only validators out of yzx doctor.

title: Collapse config, state, env, and preflight bridge owners
description: Rust already owns config-state, runtime-env, and runtime-contract typed work, but Nushell still carries meaningful request-shaping and classification ownership in config_parser.nu, config_state.nu, runtime_env.nu, and runtime_contract_checker.nu. Track the next delete-first bridge-collapse lane for that cluster.
design: Delete first. Do not add another shared helper veneer on top of the current bridge files. First define the exact deletion budget across config normalize, config-state, runtime-env, and launch-preflight request assembly. Then move the machine-owned shaping into Rust or one thinner surviving seam and delete or materially shrink the Nu owners.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The child budget bead names which of config_parser.nu, config_state.nu, runtime_env.nu, and runtime_contract_checker.nu should disappear or materially shrink. 2. The implementation bead lands a real collapse instead of a fresh wrapper layer. 3. Focused config/env/preflight regressions still defend the surviving contract.
close_reason: all steps complete

title: Define the deletion budget for config, state, env, and preflight bridge collapse
description: Name the real remaining owners and decide exactly what counts as success for the config/state/env/preflight bridge lane before implementation starts.
design: Audit config_parser.nu, config_state.nu, runtime_env.nu, and runtime_contract_checker.nu by ownership, not by file count. Success means deleting or materially shrinking the Nu owners that still duplicate typed Rust work, while keeping any surviving user-facing rendering or shell execution seams explicit.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The bead records the exact surviving owners and their current role. 2. It names the deletion budget for the lane and the specific files expected to shrink or disappear. 3. It explicitly names anything that stays in Nu and why.
notes:
Deletion budget for this lane:
- delete `nushell/scripts/utils/runtime_contract_checker.nu` entirely; it no longer owns the doctor path and mainly survives as a generic startup/launch preflight veneer
- move startup preflight ownership into `core/start_yazelix.nu`, launch preflight and terminal-candidate resolution into `core/launch_yazelix.nu`, and the one-off launch-script runtime check into `yzx/launch.nu`
- keep `config_parser.nu` in this lane as the config-normalize and config-diagnostic owner; it is already much closer to its honest boundary after `yzx_core_bridge.nu`
- keep `config_state.nu` as the small active-surface/state-path wrapper around Rust-owned config-state compute/record unless a later cut can delete it cleanly
- keep `runtime_env.nu` as the shell-bound env application seam for now; it may shrink opportunistically, but deleting the preflight veneer is the real success metric for `0ksx`
- verification target: workspace preflight regressions, launch candidate selection regressions, and any yzx launch runtime-script regressions must still pass
close_reason: Deletion budget recorded

title: Implement the config, state, env, and preflight bridge collapse
description: Implement the delete-first collapse for the config/state/env/preflight bridge cluster once the exact ownership budget is decided.
design: Use the budget bead as the contract. Delete or materially shrink the surviving Nu bridge owners instead of layering on another helper file. Keep only the irreducible execution and user-facing rendering seams that still belong in Nushell after the cut.
acceptance_criteria: 1. At least one of config_parser.nu, config_state.nu, runtime_env.nu, or runtime_contract_checker.nu disappears or materially shrinks. 2. The surviving request shaping lives in a smaller and clearer owner set. 3. Focused config/env/preflight regressions still pass.
close_reason: Deleted runtime_contract_checker.nu and moved startup, launch, and runtime-script preflight ownership into direct owner files

title: Pay down native Zellij key policy Rust LOC
description: The zellij.native_keybindings implementation made the remaining native policy configurable, but the LOC scorecard for yazelix-jm1w shows +781 main-repo code LOC. Evaluate a smaller representation, such as a compact registry shape or metadata-backed policy table, while preserving the shipped config contract.
acceptance_criteria: Preserve zellij.native_keybindings behavior and diagnostics while reducing main-repo runtime Rust ownership, or document why the current static registry is the smallest maintainable shape.
close_reason: Closed by maintainer direction: do not keep payback beads as planning debt. Future simplification should be done directly when touching the relevant code path, not tracked as a standalone payback bead.

title: Clean stale docs that still describe deleted yzx edit/import Nu modules
notes:
Several docs/spec inventory files still mention nushell/scripts/yzx/edit.nu and import.nu as live Nu owners, but those modules are gone and edit/import are Rust-owned. This did not block CI after the contract fix, but the docs are stale and should be cleaned in a follow-up pass.

2026-04-26 note: keep this as a docs-only cleanup bead. The broader Rust canonical-form audit is tracked separately under yazelix-9opk so stale docs do not get mixed with code deletion decisions.
close_reason: Completed

title: Implement immutable per-window config snapshots
description: Make launch-time config immutability a first-class runtime contract. Replace the narrow session_facts cache with a versioned per-window config snapshot that carries the normalized Yazelix config and derived pane/runtime facts for the running window.
design: Build on yazelix-6hzg. Mutable config remains owned by startup, config, doctor, edit, and runtime-materialization commands. Running windows consume immutable snapshot data through a Rust API. Prefer Yazelix-owned state/sessions/<session_id>/config_snapshot.json plus an env pointer; treat Zellij as a carrier of the process environment/pane state, not as canonical config storage unless the decision bead proves otherwise.
acceptance_criteria: A fresh Yazelix window writes and exports a per-window config_snapshot.json; live panes and session-local yzx commands read that snapshot instead of mutable user_configs/yazelix.toml; editing yazelix.toml affects only future windows or yzx restart; multiple simultaneous windows can hold different snapshots; persistent-session attach semantics are explicitly decided; stale snapshot/config mismatch errors are clear and non-ugly.
close_reason: all steps complete

title: Define versioned config snapshot schema and loader
description: Introduce the first-class Rust data model for immutable Yazelix session config snapshots. The snapshot should supersede the narrow SessionFactsCache shape while preserving derived facts as a read-optimized projection.
design: Likely replace or split rust_core/yazelix_core/src/session_facts.rs into a snapshot owner while keeping a compatibility projection named around session facts only if useful. Default to std/serde already present; do not add a crate unless the implementation bead records a packaging reason.
acceptance_criteria: A versioned config_snapshot.json schema exists with schema_version, snapshot identity, source config path/hash metadata, runtime identity metadata, full normalized_config, and derived facts; Rust exposes typed loaders for full snapshot and facts projection; malformed or wrong-version snapshots fail with clear CoreError diagnostics; crate-vs-in-house decision is recorded; serde roundtrip and failure-mode tests defend the schema.
notes: Dependency decision: no new crates. Use existing std, serde/serde_json, and sha/runtime hashes already produced by the current config-state path. The snapshot schema and atomic file IO are small in-house domain logic; adding a config/session framework would increase package closure and obscure the failure model.
close_reason: Added the versioned session config snapshot schema and strict loader API with full normalized config, source/runtime metadata, derived facts projection, and failure-mode tests. Dependency decision recorded: no new crates.

title: Create per-window config snapshots during startup
description: Move snapshot creation out of the global state/session_facts.json copy path and into the launch handoff. Each fresh Yazelix window should get its own immutable config snapshot before Zellij starts.
design: The current start_yazelix_inner.nu prepare_session_facts_snapshot copies state/session_facts.json after runtime-materialization.materialize. Replace this with a Rust-owned helper or materialization response that writes the per-window snapshot directly once the launch/session id is known.
acceptance_criteria: Startup profile smoke writes state/sessions/<stable_launch_id>/config_snapshot.json and exports YAZELIX_SESSION_CONFIG_PATH; no shell cp is needed for the main path; materialization no longer treats a global session_facts.json as the canonical expected artifact; simultaneous launches create distinct snapshots; profile-exit-before-zellij output records the snapshot path.
close_reason: Completed: startup now writes launch-scoped config snapshots and exports YAZELIX_SESSION_CONFIG_PATH; runtime materialization no longer owns a global session facts artifact.

title: Route live session commands through config snapshots
description: Update session-local command surfaces to load immutable config snapshot data instead of rereading live user config or relying on a narrow facts-only cache.
design: Start from current compute_session_facts_from_env call sites. Replace YAZELIX_SESSION_FACTS_PATH with YAZELIX_SESSION_CONFIG_PATH while keeping a deliberate short compatibility path only if needed for old open windows.
acceptance_criteria: transient-pane-facts, workspace commands, Zellij runtime editor launch, front-door screen/welcome helpers, and yzx status config-problem fallback read through the snapshot API; tests prove stale or unsupported edits in user_configs/yazelix.toml do not break already-open popup/menu/sidebar/reveal/open flows; tests prove editing config changes a new window snapshot but not an existing one; remaining live config readers are documented as startup/config/doctor/materialization/edit/update surfaces.
close_reason: Completed: live session facts now prefer YAZELIX_SESSION_CONFIG_PATH snapshots, old facts env is explicit compatibility only, and popup/workspace/status tests prove stale live config does not break active windows.

title: Decide persistent-session semantics for immutable snapshots
description: Resolve how persistent Zellij sessions interact with launch-time immutable config snapshots, or decide to remove the persistent_sessions config surface before hardening snapshot behavior.
design: This should build on yazelix-w3so and the current start_yazelix_inner.nu branch that attaches to an existing session without layout/shell options. Do not let a newly generated snapshot silently claim ownership of an already-running Zellij session that inherited older pane env.
acceptance_criteria: Decision covers attach to existing session, create new persistent session, yzx restart, multiple windows with the same session_name, path override behavior, and stale runtime/config upgrade cases; if persistent_sessions stays, implementation contract says whether attach keeps the old snapshot, rejects mismatch, or refreshes via restart-only flow; if removed, migration and config-diagnostic behavior are specified.
close_reason: Resolved by yazelix-w3so: persistent sessions will be removed from the supported config surface; implementation tracked in yazelix-0mha.6.

title: Document config immutability and snapshot diagnostics
description: Make the new immutable-config behavior visible and understandable in user-facing docs and diagnostics once the runtime contract lands.
design: Do this after the code path is settled. Avoid adding docs that imply hot-reload semantics unless the implementation explicitly supports them.
acceptance_criteria: README or focused docs explain that config edits apply to the next Yazelix window or yzx restart, not live panes; yzx status or doctor exposes the active snapshot path/source in a concise way; stale snapshot/missing snapshot diagnostics are human-readable; README edits preserve the repo no-final-period style.
close_reason: Completed: status/inspect expose session_config_snapshot diagnostics, bad snapshots report readable errors, and docs explain launch-time config immutability.

title: Remove persistent-session config surface
description: Remove zellij.persistent_sessions and zellij.session_name from the supported Yazelix config surface so the immutable per-window snapshot model has one default live-session contract.
design: Decision source: yazelix-w3so. Remove the feature instead of preserving a shared-session branch. Default Yazelix windows are independent live sessions with per-window immutable snapshots. Zellij-native session persistence remains outside the supported Yazelix config contract.
acceptance_criteria: persistent_sessions and session_name are removed from yazelix_default.toml, config_metadata/main_config_contract.toml, Home Manager options, onboarding, startup facts, session snapshot/facts, status output, generated Zellij render inputs, and live docs/spec inventory; startup no longer conditionally lists or attaches to an existing named Zellij session; Zellij close behavior follows non-persistent Yazelix semantics; stale removed fields get a clear config diagnostic, with no silent migration of persistent_sessions=true; tests defend the behavior change without adding removal-only absence tests.
close_reason: Removed the supported persistent-session config surface, simplified startup to always create a fresh Zellij session, removed status/startup/session fact exposure, updated docs/spec inventory, and added clear removed-field diagnostics.

title: Fix welcome screen width contract so the card does not stretch edge-to-edge
notes:
Rust dependency gate: use in-house/std only for this fix. Production crates unchanged: crossterm, serde, serde_json already present in yazelix_core. Dev-only crates unchanged. Build the welcome width contract fix and regression tests in-house inside front_door_render.rs because the logic is small, repo-specific, and not worth a new dependency. Rejected alternatives: terminal layout/rendering crates or ANSI helpers, because they add packaging and maintenance cost without solving the core contract bug.
Implemented in rust_core/yazelix_core/src/front_door_render.rs. Verification: nix develop -c cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core front_door_render::tests -- --nocapture
Root cause clarified: the remaining intermittency was a wide->hero breakpoint jump in welcome assets. At width 119 the installed renderer used a 60-column outer card; at width 120 it jumped to 78 columns. Fix: flatten hero minimum_inner_width from 76 to 58 across logo, boids, and game_of_life welcome specs, and add regressions defending the 119->120 case. Verified with cargo test front_door_render::tests and PTY cargo-run probes at widths 119 and 120.
Final fix: stop the welcome card from relying on stacked vertical side borders in terminals that visually gap adjacent rows; keep the stable 60-column hero width; verified with nix develop -c cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core front_door_render::tests -- --nocapture and PTY cargo-run welcome probes.
close_reason: Completed

title: Define and defend a real first-party macOS support floor
description:
After `yazelix-z5vf`, Yazelix can make the first-party flake package honest and installable on darwin, but that still does not define what macOS support actually means. The current tree contains a mix of signals:

- the top-level docs say Yazelix works on Linux and macOS and that Ghostty is first-party on both
- `packaging/runtime_deps.nix` deliberately uses `pkgs.ghostty-bin` on darwin, so the runtime does own a macOS Ghostty path
- `nushell/scripts/utils/terminal_launcher.nu` already has a macOS-specific Ghostty launch branch, and `test_ghostty_macos_launch_command_omits_linux_specific_flags` defends that narrow command-shape difference
- but the main installation guide and `assets/macos/README.md` still describe a clone-era `Yazelix.app` flow that copies an app bundle from a source checkout into `/Applications/`
- that app bundle launcher still executes `$HOME/.local/bin/yzx` and describes the real code as living in `~/.config/yazelix`, which conflicts with the current package-first v15 runtime contract
- current validators defend the general package/install surface and the first-party flake package metadata split, but they do not define a macOS end-user support floor or prove Linux parity

This bead exists to turn those mixed signals into one explicit contract. The goal is not “make macOS perfect.” The goal is to define a real first-party macOS support floor for the top-level flake/package surface, say what is actually guaranteed, and stop the docs from implying more than the maintained contract proves.
design:
Keep this bead contract-first, narrow, and delete-first. The purpose is to classify the current first-party macOS surface, not to broaden it.

Use one explicit status per surface:
- `supported`: Yazelix is willing to document and defend it now
- `best_effort`: it may work, but Yazelix does not promise it as a stable contract
- `historical_or_out_of_scope`: stale or intentionally unsupported for the current v15 package-first product

The bead should force decisions on these surfaces:
- first-party flake/package installation on macOS
- `yzx --version-short`
- `yzx doctor`
- `yzx launch` on the built-in Ghostty path
- Ghostty shell-integration behavior on macOS
- Spotlight/Launchpad/Dock launcher integration

Constraints and delete-first guidance:
- Keep Ghostty as the first-party macOS terminal assumption. The open question is Yazelix integration quality, not terminal selection.
- Do not silently keep the clone-era `assets/macos` app-bundle story. If launcher integration is not part of the defended floor, the main docs must stop implying it.
- Do not overstate automation. From a Linux maintainer host, current validators can defend packaging/metadata shape and some launch-command shape, but they do not prove a real macOS runtime experience.
- Treat Ghostty shell integration as a separate sub-decision from `yzx launch`. The current runtime wrapper does not provision a macOS resource-discovery story, and Ghostty's official docs say automatic shell integration depends on either the app bundle or a valid resources layout.
- If Spotlight/Launchpad/Dock launcher support is still in scope, do not absorb the full repair here unless the remaining work is tiny. Create a follow-up bead for the launcher contract and implementation seam.

Recommended implementation shape:
1. Add a live spec such as `docs/specs/macos_support_floor.md`
2. In that spec, classify each surface as `supported`, `best_effort`, or `historical_or_out_of_scope`
3. Update `docs/specs/spec_inventory.md` and any branch-level live contract references that should point at the new spec
4. Align `README.md`, `docs/installation.md`, and `assets/macos/README.md` with the spec
5. Add the smallest credible validation story: automated checks where they tell the truth, plus an explicit named macOS manual smoke gate where only a real macOS run can prove the contract

Recommended manual smoke contract for the spec:
1. Install the first-party package on macOS
2. Run `yzx --version-short`
3. Run `yzx doctor --verbose`
4. Run `yzx launch --terminal ghostty`
5. Confirm a Ghostty window opens into Yazelix and note whether Ghostty shell-integration features are actually present
6. If launcher integration is still claimed, launch via Spotlight/Launchpad/Dock and confirm it resolves the installed `yzx`, not a clone-era or `~/.local/bin/yzx` path

Sequencing: do this before later macOS-facing fixes and before the broader Rust rewrite. This is product-surface truthfulness, not Rust work.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Yazelix has a live documented first-party macOS support-floor spec for the top-level flake/package surface, separate from Linux parity claims.
2. That spec classifies at least these surfaces as `supported`, `best_effort`, or `historical_or_out_of_scope`: install, `yzx --version-short`, `yzx doctor`, `yzx launch`, Ghostty shell-integration behavior, and Spotlight/Launchpad/Dock launcher integration.
3. The spec explicitly states that Ghostty is the intended first-party macOS terminal path and does not promise automatic Ghostty shell integration unless the underlying resource-discovery/app-bundle story is actually defended.
4. `README.md`, `docs/installation.md`, and `assets/macos/README.md` no longer imply stronger or broader macOS support than the new support floor actually defends, and they do not keep the stale clone-era app-bundle flow as an implied primary path.
5. The validation story for the support floor is explicit and honest: automated where credible, otherwise a named manual macOS smoke procedure or maintainer gate.
6. If macOS launcher integration remains a supported surface, the remaining implementation work is tracked separately instead of being left as stale clone-era docs.
notes:
opus 4.6 review:

The spec is well-written and the core doc cleanup is correct. Two issues worth fixing before shipping.

ISSUE 1 — assets/macos/README.md body still actively guides users through the out-of-scope app bundle.

The historical note was added at the top (good), but the remaining 100 lines still tell users how to install, configure, troubleshoot, and use the app bundle as if it is current. Key contradictions:
- line 9-11: "Copy the app bundle to your Applications folder" with active install commands
- line 17-21: "After installation, you can: Search for Yazelix in Spotlight..."
- line 104: "the actual yazelix code lives in ~/.config/yazelix" (the exact stale claim the spec rejects)
- line 105: "Updates to yazelix (via git pull) dont require reinstalling the .app" (clone-era assumption)
- the full Troubleshooting section actively helps users debug the out-of-scope launcher

Per acceptance criterion 4: docs should "no longer imply stronger or broader macOS support than the new support floor actually defends." A disclaimer at the top followed by 100 lines of active how-to instructions still reads as an implied active path. Either trim the body to match the historical status (keep just structure/context, remove active install/use/troubleshoot guidance) or add a clear "the instructions below are preserved for reference only and are not part of the current support floor" marker before the active content.

ISSUE 2 — the app bundle launcher was updated, but the spec declares it out of scope.

The launcher script was changed from hardcoded ~/.local/bin/yzx to PATH-based command -v yzx discovery with an install error message. This is a speculative improvement to a surface the spec just classified as historical_or_out_of_scope. Per delete-first protocol, stale surfaces should be marked, not improved. The change is harmless but contradicts the scope decision: if the launcher is truly out of scope, updating its code suggests it is maintained. Either leave the original launcher as-is with just the historical comment, or accept that you are partly reviving it.

MINOR:
- Missing newline at end of assets/macos/Yazelix.app/Contents/MacOS/yazelix (shows as "No newline at end of file" in git diff).

WHAT IS GOOD:

- The spec itself is excellent. The surface classification table is honest and clear. The three-tier status model (supported/best_effort/historical_or_out_of_scope) makes the contract unambiguous.
- The Ghostty section correctly identifies the resource-discovery gap for shell integration on macOS and does not overclaim. The explicit callout of login-shell behavior as a platform concern is good.
- The manual smoke gate is well-defined with concrete steps.
- The Platform Scope Discipline section correctly cross-references the nixpkgs contract and warns against silent scope widening.
- docs/installation.md macOS section replacement is clean — honest about the current state without being dismissive.
- README.md platform line now links to the spec instead of claiming broad support.
- spec_inventory.md correctly lists the new spec as Live.
- Acceptance cases in the spec match the bead criteria exactly.

NOTE: The spec lists validate_flake_interface.nu as a defender, but from the yazelix-z5vf review, that validator has a bug (passes list instead of pkg to availableOn, always returns true). That is a pre-existing issue from z5vf, not introduced here, but the spec is referencing a broken defender until z5vf is fixed. Since z5vf is a dependency of this bead, the z5vf fix should land first.

VERDICT:

The spec and core doc changes are correct — ship those. Fix the assets/macos/README.md body to match its own historical classification (trim active install/use guidance or add a clear archive marker). Either revert the launcher script update or accept you are partly maintaining the out-of-scope surface. Add trailing newline.
2026-04-19 implementation landed:
- removed the legacy `assets/macos` app-bundle surface instead of keeping it as a half-supported path
- kept the macOS support-floor spec and package-first docs
- created follow-up bead `yazelix-b63b` for any future package-first macOS launcher work
close_reason: Completed: verified the package-first macOS support-floor spec and docs are in place, stale clone-era app-bundle docs are no longer tracked, and future Spotlight/Launchpad/Dock launcher work is explicitly deferred to yazelix-b63b and its children.

title: Split launch command internals before workspace extraction
description: Split launch_commands.rs by terminal selection, config override handling, process launch, desktop/macOS handling, restart/enter, and shared state helpers. Keep the public command surface and behavior stable; this is an internal boundary to avoid dragging the whole launch surface into a later workspace extraction.
notes: 2026-05-07 complete: split terminal selection/config path/argv construction into private launch_commands/terminal.rs and split one-shot session config override materialization/parsing into launch_commands/config_override.rs. Top-level launch_commands.rs now keeps the public enter/launch/desktop/restart flow and shared process/desktop helpers. Created follow-ups yazelix-0nvl.1 for process execution + desktop/macOS adapters and yazelix-0nvl.2 for restart/enter flow isolation. Rust dependency gate held: no new crates. Budget impact: +2 Rust files and +30 tokei code LOC from module headers/visibility, but the global budget remains below the pre-gr41 ceiling; this is organization debt recorded in docs/rust_code_inventory.md. Verification: cargo fmt --all --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml --check; cargo check --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core --all-targets; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core launch_commands; yzx_repo_validator validate-rust-ownership-budget, validate-contracts, validate-docs-experience; git diff --check.
close_reason: Completed

title: Split launch process execution and desktop/macOS adapters
description: Continue the launch_commands.rs private boundary split by moving command execution/probe helpers and desktop/macOS install/launch helpers behind private modules. Preserve public yzx enter/launch/desktop/restart behavior and keep tests focused on command argv/env contracts.
notes: Implementation: split launch process/probe execution into launch_commands/process.rs and Linux desktop plus macOS preview ownership into launch_commands/desktop.rs. The parent launch_commands.rs keeps public yzx enter/launch/desktop/restart dispatch, launch flow, arg parsing, runtime setup, cursor facts, and restart-specific Zellij/session handling; restart/enter are intentionally left for yazelix-0nvl.2. LOC accounting: launch_commands.rs 2617 -> 1632, added desktop.rs 693 and process.rs 315, net +23 raw Rust lines but -47 tokei code LOC. This is accepted organization debt for a private boundary, not extraction. Verification: cargo fmt --all -- --check; cargo check --workspace; cargo test -p yazelix_core launch_commands; yzx_repo_validator validate-rust-ownership-budget; validate-docs-experience; git diff --check.
close_reason: Completed: split launch process/probe execution and desktop/macOS adapter ownership into private modules, kept restart/enter for the follow-up bead, and verified launch command behavior.

title: Split restart and enter flow after launch helper extraction
description: After process execution and desktop/macOS adapters are split, isolate restart and enter orchestration from launch_commands.rs so a future workspace extraction does not inherit the whole front-door launch surface.
notes: Implementation: split enter orchestration into launch_commands/enter.rs and restart orchestration into launch_commands/restart.rs. Public run_yzx_enter/run_yzx_restart remain in launch_commands.rs as thin wrappers, while the parent keeps launch fallback flow, yzx launch/desktop dispatch, parsing for launch/desktop, and shared cursor/terminal facts. LOC accounting: launch_commands.rs 1632 -> 1111, added enter.rs 260 and restart.rs 297, net +37 raw Rust lines/+36 tokei code LOC accepted as organization debt. Verification: cargo fmt --all -- --check; cargo check --workspace; cargo test -p yazelix_core launch_commands; yzx_repo_validator validate-rust-ownership-budget; validate-docs-experience; git diff --check.
close_reason: Completed: split enter and restart orchestration into private modules while preserving parent public command functions and verified launch command behavior.

title: Reduce config-surface duplication between Nushell and Nix
description: Reduce unnecessary duplication between the Nushell and Nix config surfaces after the canonical config and launch-profile contract is defined. The goal is not total collapse into one file, but fewer parallel sources of truth and fewer places where the same runtime choice must be expressed twice.
design: This work should follow the canonical config-surface contract rather than guess its own target. First identify which values genuinely need dual representation across Nushell and Nix and which ones are accidental duplication. Then delete redundant surfaces before optimizing the survivors. Prefer one clear source-of-truth plus explicit derivation or validation over two independently maintained copies of the same intent.
acceptance_criteria: There is a concrete duplication-reduction plan grounded in the canonical config and launch-profile contract. The bead records which duplicated fields should be unified, which must remain mirrored with validation, and which existing duplication is acceptable. Follow-up implementation work is narrower than this planning bead.
close_reason: Completed: documented the config metadata centralization plan and classified which duplication should be unified, validated, or kept.

title: Introduce canonical machine-readable contract for the main Yazelix config surface
description: Introduce one checked-in, language-neutral metadata artifact for the main Yazelix config surface so defaults, ownership, validation rules, and rebuild sensitivity stop being redefined independently across the TOML template, Home Manager, parser, schema logic, and validator work.
design: The first target is the main config surface only, not the pack catalog. The artifact should carry machine-meaningful semantics such as TOML path, owning surface, default value, type or shape, validation metadata, rebuild sensitivity, and mapping hints where parser or Home Manager names differ. Keep prose comments and integration-specific descriptions out of the first contract so the centralization stays about semantics, not documentation generation.
acceptance_criteria: There is a canonical checked-in metadata contract for the main Yazelix config surface. The contract is rich enough to drive downstream parser, schema, rebuild-sensitivity, and parity-validation work without forcing those consumers to rediscover defaults and validation rules from hand-written TOML or Nix code.
close_reason: Completed: added the canonical main-config metadata artifact and a loader module for downstream consumers.

title: Make config_parser.nu consume the canonical config contract
description: Refactor the Nushell config parser so defaults, enum handling, numeric-range validation, and field mapping read from the canonical main-config contract instead of re-encoding those rules by hand.
design: Treat this as a consumer-migration bead, not as the place where the contract is invented. Preserve the current parser output shape where it is intentionally consumed elsewhere, but move machine-meaningful defaults and validation data behind the contract artifact. Prefer deleting hand-maintained fallback duplication over wrapping it in another helper layer.
acceptance_criteria: config_parser.nu reads the maintained defaults and validation metadata for supported fields from the canonical contract instead of hardcoding a parallel rule set. Existing parser behavior stays coherent, and any intentional output-key renames are represented explicitly in the contract.
close_reason: Completed: config_parser.nu now consumes the canonical main-config contract for defaults, parser mapping, and existing validation behavior.

title: Make config_schema.nu and config diagnostics consume the canonical config contract
description: Refactor schema comparison, enum validation, and startup config diagnostics so they consume the canonical main-config contract instead of maintaining another partial copy of allowed values and field knowledge.
design: Keep this focused on validation and diagnostics. The goal is not to auto-generate every error message, but to stop maintaining separate enum lists, known-field sets, and type expectations by hand. Preserve the distinction between structural schema checks, migration-aware diagnostics, and human-facing remediation text.
acceptance_criteria: config_schema.nu and the config diagnostics layer derive their maintained field knowledge and enum constraints from the canonical contract where appropriate. Validation drift between parser, schema checks, and diagnostics is materially reduced rather than redistributed.
close_reason: Completed: config_schema.nu and config diagnostics now derive maintained field knowledge and enum constraints from the canonical main-config contract.

title: Move rebuild-sensitivity metadata into the canonical config contract
description: Stop maintaining rebuild-required keys as a separate manual list by moving rebuild sensitivity into the canonical main-config contract and making config-state logic consume it directly.
design: This bead is specifically about the rebuild boundary, not all parser behavior. The contract should be the place that states whether a field change forces a new devenv profile build. config_state.nu should then consume that metadata instead of reading a parallel handwritten list. Keep non-config inputs such as devenv.lock and devenv.nix outside the field table unless there is a clear reason to unify them.
acceptance_criteria: The rebuild-sensitive config subset is derived from the canonical contract rather than a separate handwritten key list. config_state.nu still computes the correct rebuild hash boundary, but the maintained source of truth for field-level rebuild sensitivity lives in one place.
close_reason: Completed: config_state.nu now derives rebuild-sensitive field paths from the canonical contract artifact instead of a separate handwritten list.

title: Centralize pack catalog metadata separately from main config metadata
description: Create a separate canonical artifact for pack declarations and pack-surface metadata so pack defaults and declarations stop being repeated independently across the pack template, Home Manager, and future validation work.
design: Do not fold the pack catalog into the same flat field metadata table used for scalar config options. Packs are structured content with different evolution pressure. Reuse the same architectural idea as the main config contract, but keep a separate artifact and contract boundary so pack declarations, enabled defaults, and user package handling can evolve without distorting the main option model.
acceptance_criteria: There is a concrete standalone centralization path for pack metadata that does not overload the main config contract. The bead identifies the canonical artifact boundary for pack declarations, enabled defaults, and related pack-surface semantics.
close_reason: Completed: added separate canonical pack catalog contract artifact and loader helpers

title: Fix config metadata drift revealed by the centralization audit
description: Fix concrete config drift bugs uncovered during the metadata audit, starting with mismatches where the default TOML, Home Manager defaults, and Nushell parser fallbacks do not currently agree.
design: Treat this as a targeted cleanup lane for already-observed mismatches, not a substitute for the broader contract refactor. Start with the highest-signal drift cases, such as yazi.plugins default mismatch, and fix them in a way that improves current behavior even before the canonical contract work is finished.
acceptance_criteria: Known live drift cases uncovered by the metadata audit are fixed or explicitly triaged. Current defaults and parser fallbacks are no longer silently inconsistent for the tracked cases, with yazi.plugins being the first defended example.
close_reason: Completed: fixed the tracked yazi.plugins default drift and added regression coverage for the missing-field fallback path.

title: Detached Yazelix sessions can accumulate and keep consuming CPU after windows close
design: Investigate why Yazelix/Zellij sessions can remain alive after their terminal windows are closed and continue consuming CPU while detached. Use the observed failure mode as the anchor: many long-lived zellij --server processes under /run/user/1000/zellij/contract_version_1 with zero attached clients, each burning nontrivial CPU. Determine whether the bug is in Yazelix launch/restart/session naming policy, desktop/current-terminal ownership, Zellij client detach behavior, or plugin/session lifecycle. Prefer a real lifecycle fix over cleanup-on-start band-aids.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Reproduce or explain how detached Yazelix sessions survive window closure and keep consuming CPU. 2. Identify whether the root cause is Yazelix-managed session lifecycle, Zellij behavior, or both. 3. If a Yazelix fix is made, closing a window or otherwise ending the last client for a non-persistent Yazelix session should not leave a hot detached server behind. 4. The fix must not regress intentional persistent-session behavior. 5. Verification should include a concrete check on session count or attached-client state, not just a code-path unit test.
notes:
Cross-window/session-semantics anchor:
- In the default non-persistent contract, closing the last client for a Yazelix window should not leave a hot detached Zellij server behind.
- In persistent-session mode, detached-server survival may be intentional depending on the named-session contract.
- Evaluate fixes against `yazelix-1zti` and `yazelix-vxrb` so cleanup logic does not erase intentional persistence.

Root cause found:
- Yazelix enables `session_serialization true`, strips user `on_force_close` from the merged Zellij base config, and previously failed to inject its own replacement.
- That left default non-persistent sessions inheriting Zellij's detach-on-terminal-close behavior, which matches the observed zero-client detached servers.

Fix direction:
- Generate `on_force_close "quit"` for default non-persistent sessions.
- Generate `on_force_close "detach"` for persistent sessions.

Verification:
- generated-config regression for both session modes
- concrete runtime probe: with a PTY-backed `zellij attach -c <session>`, `on_force_close "quit"` yielded `before=1 after=0`, while `on_force_close "detach"` yielded `before=1 after=1`
close_reason: Fixed: non-persistent sessions now generate on_force_close=quit while persistent sessions keep detach semantics, eliminating the observed default-mode zero-client session leak.

title: Convert workspace and session live specs to indexed contract items
description: After the first high-value contract-item batch lands under yazelix-7upo, convert the next live specs that still gate default or maintainer tests: workspace_session_contract.md, shell_opened_editors.md, persistent_window_session_contract.md, and nonpersistent_window_session_contract.md. Use indexed items to unblock future test cleanup and integration-wrapper deletion without promoting historical notes into live contracts.
close_reason: Completed

title: Move workspace request/response shaping into private module
description: Isolate the pure workspace request/response layer after the Zellij/workspace command boundary split. The goal is to keep runtime env, Yazi emit-to, zoxide, popup cwd policy, and Home Manager/Yazelix config policy in Yazelix adapters while moving payload shaping, typed responses, and pane-orchestrator workspace command helpers behind a private module.
design: Private module only. Candidate owner should sit near workspace_session.rs or a new workspace_* module and expose typed helpers for retarget, active-tab workspace root, open-file/open-terminal payloads, sidebar identity, and response parsing. It must not know about user-facing command text, runtime directory layout, Home Manager state, status cache, or popup configuration.
acceptance_criteria: Workspace request/response shaping is private and typed; Yazelix product adapters still own runtime env, Yazi emit-to, zoxide/path resolution, popup cwd policy, and user-facing messages; public behavior is unchanged; workspace extraction contract is updated if the boundary changes; focused tests pass; LOC/boundary impact is recorded.
notes: Implementation 2026-05-08: Extended workspace_session.rs from response parsing into the private workspace request/response owner. Added SidebarYaziRegistration, workspace_tab_name, workspace_retarget_payload, managed_editor_open_payload, and open_terminal_in_cwd_payload. zellij_commands/workspace.rs now delegates retarget/open-file/open-terminal JSON shaping to that module while keeping runtime env, Yazi sync, path resolution, user-facing output, and pane creation local. Updated the workspace extraction contract and Rust inventory. Rejected candidates: did not move runtime env construction, zoxide/path resolution, Yazi emit-to, popup cwd policy, or user messages into workspace_session because those remain Yazelix product adapters; did not create a public child repo. LOC: 79 insertions / 75 deletions, net +4 raw Rust lines; tokei Rust code 66,627, up 1; accepted as small organization debt for a typed private seam.
close_reason: Completed: moved pure workspace request payload shaping and sidebar registration into workspace_session.rs, kept Yazelix product adapters local, updated contract/budget docs, and verified workspace/session gates.

title: Give boids stable visual identity in yzx screen
description: The new yzx screen boids renderer makes every boid look like the same 2-cell block and only pulses colors over time, so users cannot track individual units. Improve the boid renderer so individual boids have stable visual identity and direction-readable silhouettes while preserving the scaled no-row-gap terminal footprint.
design: Keep the in-house boids simulation. Fix only the visual encoding: make color identity stable per boid instead of tick-pulsed, and vary the 2-column cell pattern by boid identity or direction so units are distinguishable at terminal scale. Do not shrink to tiny braille dots or reintroduce skipped rows.
acceptance_criteria: yzx screen boids renders distinguishable boid units with stable identity over time; direction or identity is visible without relying on global pulsing; full_block and dotted modes keep the same frame dimensions and no inserted rows; focused tests defend stable per-boid rendering and frame footprint.
notes: Implemented: removed time-pulsed boid colors, expanded the stable boid palette, and made each boid render as a 2-cell directional silhouette. Full-block boids now distinguish east/west/north/south with ▐█, █▌, ▀▀, and ▄▄; dotted boids use matching scaled braille signatures. Verification: cargo fmt --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml --all -- --check; cargo check --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core -p yazelix_screen; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_screen; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core front_door_render.
close_reason: Completed: boid units now have stable color identity and direction-readable scaled silhouettes.
