description: Support a separate user-managed devenv file as a power-user override path without replacing yazelix.toml or the managed runtime model as the default story.
design: This should be a deliberate extension point, not a hole punched through the configuration model. Define what a user-managed devenv overlay can change, how it composes with Yazelix-managed files, and how support boundaries are documented so power users get flexibility without making the default runtime impossible to reason about.
acceptance_criteria: Power users can provide a separate devenv file through a documented, supported path, and the default yazelix.toml flow remains the primary story. Add unit tests for path-resolution and overlay-selection helpers, config and path-resolution integration tests, and end-to-end validation that the override file is discovered and composed correctly, with detailed logs of the final devenv inputs used.
close_reason: Stale v14-era issue. v15 dropped devenv entirely — no yazelix_packs.toml, no runtime-local devenv, no yzx packs. This feature request is no longer applicable.

title: Notification system
description: Design and add a notification system that surfaces important Yazelix events without turning the workspace into a noisy status carnival.
design: Do not start with implementation detail. First define the event model: which events deserve a notification, what channel they should use, when they should be silent, and how users can dismiss or tune them. The system should reinforce existing flows such as rebuilds, permission issues, and long-running actions instead of compensating for unclear product behavior with spam.
acceptance_criteria: Yazelix has a documented notification model and an implementation that surfaces the right events with restrained UX. Add unit tests for event routing and suppression rules plus end-to-end scripts that trigger representative events and log delivery channel, payload, and user-visible outcome.

title: Always-on maintenance lane
description: Always-on maintenance lane for cross-cutting bug fixes, cleanup, and reliability improvements that should continue even while the larger roadmap moves forward.
design: This is the place for real ongoing maintenance work, not an excuse to bury urgent bugs under a generic label. Use it for issues that improve the product continuously without pretending they are the next big milestone. Keep the children concrete and user-impactful so the lane stays actionable.
acceptance_criteria: Maintenance-lane children are clearly described, independently actionable, and not hidden behind fake sequence blockers. The lane remains useful for triage and does not become a miscellaneous junk drawer.
close_reason: Obsolete grouping container; replaced by labels and direct dependencies

title: Continue removing unnecessary environment variables
description: Reduce the Yazelix launch/runtime environment contract so only true cross-process handoff variables and explicit override or test escape hatches remain. This lane should keep deleting dead or redundant Yazelix-owned env exports, especially where launch state currently mirrors config into shell env even though consumers can read config or state directly.
design:
This is not a purity contest. The job is to make environment variables stop pretending to be a second source of truth when config, runtime state, or explicit process arguments already exist.

Work from the real seam in the code:
- `nushell/scripts/utils/launch_state.nu`
- `nushell/scripts/utils/environment_bootstrap.nu`
- `nushell/scripts/utils/common.nu`
- `nushell/scripts/integrations/zellij.nu`
- `nushell/scripts/integrations/yazi.nu`
- `nushell/scripts/utils/detect_terminal.nu`
- `nushell/scripts/utils/helix_mode.nu`
- `nushell/scripts/utils/terminal_launcher.nu`
- `nushell/scripts/core/launch_yazelix.nu`
- `nushell/scripts/yzx/desktop.nu`
- `nushell/scripts/utils/constants.nu`

Classify remaining Yazelix-owned env vars into three buckets before deleting anything:
1. Keep:
- true cross-process contract or runtime identity (`YAZELIX_RUNTIME_DIR`, `YAZELIX_NU_BIN`, `IN_YAZELIX_SHELL`, `DEVENV_PROFILE`)
- explicit user or maintainer override/test surfaces (`YAZELIX_CONFIG_DIR`, `YAZELIX_CONFIG_OVERRIDE`, `YAZELIX_LAYOUT_OVERRIDE`, sweep/popup/bootstrap temp vars)
- process-selection state that is genuinely chosen at launch time (`YAZELIX_TERMINAL`, and likely the managed-editor handoff vars until those callers are redesigned)
2. Delete now:
- dead or effectively dead launch exports whose only remaining role is being exported and re-propagated
- current likely first-slice targets from the code are `YAZELIX_DEBUG_MODE`, `YAZELIX_ENABLE_SIDEBAR`, `YAZELIX_WELCOME_STYLE`, plus the stale `YAZELIX_ENV_VARS` constant inventory
3. Evaluate next:
- redundant aliases or config mirrors with live consumers
- current likely candidates are `YAZELIX_DIR` versus `YAZELIX_RUNTIME_DIR`, `YAZELIX_PREFERRED_TERMINAL`, and `YAZELIX_TERMINAL_CONFIG_MODE`

Delete-first rules:
- remove dead exports before touching real overrides
- prefer direct config/state reads for same-process consumers
- do not break real cross-process handoff into wrappers, popup flows, desktop launch, or sweep/test harnesses
- when a variable stays, make sure it stays for a defensible reason rather than habit
acceptance_criteria:
1. The lane produces a clearer keep/delete/evaluate contract for Yazelix-owned environment variables, grounded in the actual launch/runtime call graph.
2. Dead launch exports are removed from launch env generation and wrapper propagation instead of continuing as unused config mirrors.
3. Stale environment inventories such as `YAZELIX_ENV_VARS` stop pretending to be a maintained source of truth if they are no longer used.
4. Redundant aliases or config-mirror vars are only removed when call sites switch to direct config/state reads or a narrower explicit handoff.
5. Changes ship with focused regressions around the affected launch, wrapper, desktop, terminal, or editor flows rather than generic command-list tests.
notes: Code read for this refinement shows that the remaining env-var problem is concentrated in launch-state export and wrapper propagation, not spread evenly across the repo. The most suspicious current exports are `YAZELIX_DEBUG_MODE`, `YAZELIX_ENABLE_SIDEBAR`, and `YAZELIX_WELCOME_STYLE`, which are still added to launch env and Zellij wrapper env but do not appear to drive meaningful runtime behavior anymore. `YAZELIX_DIR` is now mostly a compatibility alias beside `YAZELIX_RUNTIME_DIR`, and `YAZELIX_PREFERRED_TERMINAL` and `YAZELIX_TERMINAL_CONFIG_MODE` look like later cleanup candidates once direct config/state reads are made explicit. Keep this bead focused on shrinking the real runtime contract, not on removing every env var on principle.
close_reason: Completed

title: [Bug]: The desktop launcher doesn't work with fish
description: Make the desktop launcher work reliably when the user's default shell is fish instead of depending on shell-specific assumptions that only happen to work for Bash-like setups.
design: Treat the desktop entry as a real supported entrypoint, not a maintainer-only convenience. Verify what environment and shell state the desktop launch path actually has, remove assumptions that leak from interactive shell startup, and make the launcher robust regardless of whether the user prefers fish or another shell. Keep the fix aligned with the broader push away from stale env dependencies.
acceptance_criteria: Launching Yazelix from the desktop entry works for fish users without requiring a manual desktop-file patch. Add unit tests for launcher command-construction helpers where they exist, a launcher smoke test or reproducible script that simulates the desktop-entry environment, and logs that make shell-specific regressions easy to diagnose.
close_reason: Replaced copied desktop asset flow with generated desktop install/uninstall commands, aligned Home Manager on the POSIX launcher, and verified the supported launcher contracts.

title: [Bug]: Starting Yazelix with yzx launch --here --path doesn't work
description: Fix the yzx launch --here --path flow so it opens the intended directory and does not break keybindings or leave the resulting session unresponsive.
design: This is a real multi-symptom bug: wrong startup path, bad session responsiveness, and confusing interactions with user remaps and non-default shells. The fix should trace how the here-path flow sets cwd, initializes the session, and wires the managed plugins so that the path override does not corrupt the normal control layer.
acceptance_criteria: Launching with --here --path opens the requested location and keeps the session responsive. Add unit tests for the command builder and path-handling helpers, integration coverage for the launch argument plumbing, and end-to-end tests that launch into a custom path under at least one non-default shell and capture detailed logs for cwd, plugin readiness, and keybinding responsiveness.
close_reason: Closed after fresh verification showed non-persistent --here --path launches already pass the requested cwd through to Zellij, while existing persistent sessions intentionally reattach and ignore --path with a clear warning.

title: [Bug]: Yazi user configuration not taken into account
description: Honor user-supplied Yazi configuration when generating the merged runtime config instead of silently dropping the user customization path.
design: Decide what the supported merge boundary is for Yazi user config, then implement it consistently. The generated config path should combine Yazelix-managed defaults with the supported user-owned overrides in a way that is predictable and documented, without pretending to merge unsupported pieces silently.
acceptance_criteria: Supported user Yazi configuration is present in the final generated runtime config. Add unit tests for the merge logic and end-to-end validation that inspects the final Yazi config output, with logs showing the user source path, merge inputs, and resulting generated file.
close_reason: Completed

title: yazi opening full white
description: Always-on maintenance lane item that is already closed on GitHub.
close_reason: Already resolved

title: Keep the Git Yazi plugins state from getting stale
description: Keep the Git-related Yazi plugin state and the related Starship Git status from going stale when repo state changes inside the same directory. Prefer narrow event-driven refreshes so users do not need to leave and re-enter a directory just to get current repo information, and keep any added Git styling subtle enough to preserve normal sidebar readability.
design: Treat this as an event-driven freshness contract, not a generic polling problem. First verify which Git-related Yazi surfaces go stale while staying in the same directory, then prefer narrow refresh triggers when Yazelix returns attention to Yazi: sidebar focus regain and popup close back into Yazi are the first-class candidates. Do not add background polling or a global heartbeat unless a clean event-driven refresh primitive proves insufficient. If Yazi exposes a real refresh or file-list invalidation action, use that directly; otherwise stop before adding a fragile keystroke-injection hack. If Git state is tinted in the sidebar, prefer foreground text styling over row-background fills so hover and selection states remain legible.
acceptance_criteria: Git-related Yazi indicators no longer require a directory change to become fresh again after repo state changes inside the same directory. The initial fix is limited to high-signal events: entering the sidebar pane and closing the popup pane back into Yazi. Defend the trigger contract with focused tests where practical, and only proceed if the Yazi-side refresh mechanism stays simple and maintainable. Reject background polling, broad layout or menu triggers, or input-injection hacks unless later evidence proves they are necessary. Any Git tinting should remain a foreground text treatment that coexists cleanly with existing hover and selection visuals.
close_reason: Completed: added event-driven Yazi sidebar refresh on popup close, sidebar focus, initial open, and Yazi navigation, plus distinct Git styling for staged versus unstaged changes and Starship refresh on sidebar open.

title: Fix status bar overflow on narrow layouts
description: Fix status-bar overflow and fit problems so the bar remains usable on narrower screens instead of clipping or breaking layout.
design: Solve this as a responsive-layout problem, not by blindly truncating everything. Audit which widgets or segments dominate width, define a graceful compaction or hiding strategy, and make sure the status surface still communicates the important state at common narrow widths and split sizes.
acceptance_criteria: The status bar fits and remains readable on supported narrow layouts. Add unit tests for width-budget and widget-selection helpers, rendering or layout regression checks, and end-to-end validation in multiple terminal widths with detailed logs of the computed widget set, widths, and any truncation or hiding decisions.
close_reason: Cancelled: this is no longer a product direction Yazelix intends to pursue.

title: Completed roadmap items
description: Migrated from docs_explore/roadmap.md. Historical roadmap items that are already shipped or otherwise complete.
close_reason: Historical grouping only

title: Sidebar toggle/orchestration shipped in v13
description: Completed roadmap item. Deterministic sidebar toggle, editor/sidebar focus, and managed editor targeting are shipped in v13.
close_reason: Shipped in v13

title: Update static preview assets (screenshots/gifs)
description: Refresh Yazelix's static preview assets so screenshots and GIFs reflect the current product instead of stale layouts, widgets, and onboarding states.
design: Treat this as a content-quality pass tied to the current runtime, not just regenerating everything blindly. Inventory where preview assets are used across README, docs, and the website, replace the stale ones, and prefer smaller, more focused captures that survive UI evolution better.
acceptance_criteria: The main docs and marketing surfaces show current Yazelix visuals, stale captures are removed, and the asset set is small enough to maintain. Include a logged capture checklist so the asset generation process is reproducible instead of ad hoc.
close_reason: Reduced repo preview assets to one maintained README screenshot, removed stale head demos, and documented the lightweight capture policy

title: Design configurable Yazelix/Zellij keybinding remap layer
description: Yazelix needs a first-class keybinding remap model instead of relying on users to edit native Zellij KDL or on Yazelix to append hard-coded overrides. The model should cover Yazelix-owned actions like popup, menu, sidebar focus, native Zellij actions that users still want to customize, and conflict handling across Helix, Yazi, shells, and terminal emulators. It should avoid the ambiguity exposed by clear-defaults: users need to know which layer owns each binding and how to intentionally replace it.
design: Define a semantic keymap surface in settings.jsonc or a dedicated contract-backed config section. Map action ids to keys, render them into Zellij config deterministically, validate conflicts before launch, and preserve an explicit escape hatch for fully native managed zellij.kdl ownership. Keep native ~/.config/zellij/config.kdl as borrowed base input, not an implicit Yazelix remap API. Include migration guidance from current hard-coded defaults.
acceptance_criteria: Users can remap Yazelix-owned Zellij actions without copying the whole Zellij keybind block. Generated config reflects the semantic remaps and fails fast on unsupported or conflicting bindings. Docs explain precedence between semantic Yazelix remaps, managed ~/.config/yazelix/zellij.kdl, and native fallback ~/.config/zellij/config.kdl. Regression tests cover remapping popup, menu, sidebar, and focus actions plus conflict behavior. Existing defaults remain unchanged when no remaps are configured.
notes: Implemented first slice: added semantic zellij.keybindings action map for Yazelix-owned Zellij actions, with defaults for popup/menu/config/sidebar/focus/workspace terminal/layout-family actions. Materialization now generates pane-orchestrator binds from the semantic map, supports partial remaps, supports [] to disable one action, rejects duplicate semantic keys, filters matching static unbind lines to avoid dead replacement keys, and bumps the generation fingerprint. Updated default config, main config contract, settings schema, config UI metadata, Home Manager option typing/rendering, Zellij docs, and workspace-session validator ownership. Local generated Zellij config regenerated with reused=false and verified Alt m, Alt Shift M, Alt h/l sidebar-aware binds are present. Verification: yzx dev rust fmt --check; yzx dev rust check; cargo check -p yazelix_maintainer; yzx dev rust test zellij_materialization; yzx dev rust test config_normalize; yzx dev rust test config_ui_metadata_covers_visible_fields_and_tabs; validate-config-surface-contract; validate-workspace-session-contract; nix eval --raw .#homeManagerModules.default --apply 'f: "ok"'; git diff --check.
close_reason: Implemented semantic Zellij action remap layer, docs, validation, and tests

title: Fix Home Manager desktop-entry module recursion
description: Home Manager switch can hit infinite recursion because the Yazelix module uses optionalAttrs with pkgs.stdenv.hostPlatform.isLinux while the module system is still resolving module arguments. Delay the platform condition with mkIf so pkgs is not forced during _module.freeformType construction.
close_reason: Completed: delayed the Linux desktop-entry platform check with mkIf so pkgs is not forced during _module.freeformType construction. Verified by refreshing /home/lucca/.config/home-manager's yazelix input to the fixed local commit and running home-manager switch --flake .#lucca@loqness successfully.

title: Add a first-run Helix import notice for managed Helix sessions
close_reason: Completed

title: Improve yazelix_bar extensibility without losing zjstatus features
description: Improve the standalone yazelix_bar preset so users keep most useful zjstatus capabilities while still getting a Yazelix-branded default and an easy path to configure or create their own widgets.
design: Start from the standalone preset contract and zjstatus capabilities. Decide whether this is documentation-only, a generated preset family, a small renderer/CLI, or config fragments. Keep generic standalone use independent from full Yazelix runtime helpers, while making optional command widgets and AI/provider widgets straightforward.
acceptance_criteria: Users can keep or enable common zjstatus features such as mode/tabs/session/datetime/command widgets, configure colors/labels/widget order without copying a fragile whole file where possible, and add custom widgets with clear examples. The design distinguishes generic zjstatus widgets, provider/AI widgets, and Yazelix-only cached widgets; it includes verification for generated preset output and docs.
close_reason: Completed standalone bar extensibility pass: kept the default generic, packaged copyable command/Yazelix-runtime widget examples, documented customization knobs and the generator follow-up, and verified the bar crate, contracts, and Nix package output.

title: Add structured yazelix_bar preset generator
description: Add a small generator for standalone yazelix_bar presets so users can set brand label, colors, widget order, generic command widgets, and optional provider presets from structured options instead of editing copied KDL directly. The generator should consume or share the existing yazelix_bar renderer rather than maintaining another hand-copied preset family.
acceptance_criteria: A maintainer/user-facing generator emits the generic standalone preset from structured options; generated output is covered by tests; docs explain when to use the generator versus raw zjstatus KDL; packaging exposes the generated preset path without requiring full Yazelix runtime helpers.
close_reason: Implemented a structured standalone yazelix_bar generator in the shared Rust crate, installed it in the flake package, exposed a generated preset path, documented generator usage, and verified the crate, contracts, traceability, generator smoke output, and Nix package build.

title: Shrink Zellij materialization deletion-first
description: Split zellij_materialization.rs ownership just enough to expose duplicate KDL/render paths, then delete duplicate or stale generated-config materialization logic where current render-plan contracts cover behavior.
design: Delete-first Zellij materialization pass. Preserve generated Zellij config output, keybinding rule behavior, layout family contracts, and integrated bar behavior. Split only around behavior ownership that enables deletion.
acceptance_criteria: zellij_materialization.rs is smaller or has a clearer private boundary; at least one duplicate/stale KDL/render path is deleted or explicitly rejected with evidence; focused Zellij render/materialization validators/tests pass; Rust budget and inventory are updated; LOC delta and rejected candidates are recorded.
notes: Implementation: deleted duplicated Zellij relevant-config fingerprint modeling and now hashes the typed ZellijRenderPlanData plus semantic keybindings. build_render_plan_request now deserializes the normalized config through the typed render-plan request instead of re-declaring render defaults in zellij_materialization.rs. Bumped generation fingerprint schema to 4 so cached generated configs refresh against the new fingerprint payload. Rejected: moving layout rendering to another file in this slice because it would add file-count organization debt without deleting more behavior. Verification: cargo check -p yazelix_core --all-targets passed; cargo test -p yazelix_core zellij_materialization passed; cargo test -p yazelix_core --test yzx_core_zellij_render_plan passed; validate-rust-ownership-budget passed with existing hard-target warning. LOC: zellij_materialization.rs 2792 -> 2738 raw lines; budget total 72730 -> 72676.
close_reason: Completed: removed duplicated Zellij fingerprint/render-plan config modeling, ratcheted Rust budget, and verified focused Zellij tests.

title: Broader yzx public CLI rewrite in clap
description: Track any later expansion of the Rust-owned public yzx root beyond the already landed control-family and status cuts. This epic is no longer about proving the first Rust root path; qsb5 and x3u2 already did that. It now exists to track only those additional public command families that can move to Rust with a real deletion budget for the surviving Nushell public or internal owners.
design: Follow docs/specs/v16_rust_cli_rewrite_evaluation.md. Do not add children here for generic clap migration or argv cleanup. New work only belongs under this epic when a family can name the exact Nushell owners that disappear or materially shrink. Current audit outcome: doctor is the closest plausible next mixed family; launch/enter/restart remain a no-go while they are shell/process heavy; interactive/info/distribution families remain likely Nushell survivors. Structural cleanup like splitting core/yazelix.nu is in scope when it makes later family ownership honest and smaller.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Every child under this epic names one concrete public family or one structural cleanup that clarifies later public-family ownership. 2. No child is allowed to add a Rust root above the same Nushell command tree. 3. The epic remains open only while at least one later family-level deletion budget or required structural cleanup is still unresolved.
notes:
2026-04-20 verification refresh:
- qsb5 is fully landed and should be treated as the first completed gate for this epic, not as future work
- the public Rust root already owns help/completion/dispatch for env, run, update, and status via yzx.rs plus yzx_control
- nushell_externs.nu is already demoted to startup compatibility glue; it is no longer command-discovery authority
- remaining public families fall into three groups:
  1. closest plausible next mixed Rust family: doctor
  2. explicit no-go for now: launch/enter/restart because the surviving owners are still shell/process heavy
  3. likely Nushell survivors: config/edit/import, menu/popup/screen/keys/tutor/whats_new, desktop/home_manager/dev, and small info commands like why/sponsor
- the better near-term Rust deletion lanes are still outside this epic: the runtime materialization owner seam and the fragmented terminal/Helix materialization family
- core/yazelix.nu is still a mixed internal lump for doctor, restart, cwd, reveal, why, and sponsor; splitting that lump will make later public-owner decisions much cleaner
close_reason: all steps complete

title: Audit remaining public yzx families after the first Rust root cut
description: Verify yazelix-2jkb against the actual post-qsb5 codebase. Audit the public Rust root, internal Nu dispatcher, and the remaining command-family owners so later public CLI work is driven by real deletion budgets instead of stale rewrite language.
design: Use the live code, not old planning assumptions. Read rust_core/yazelix_core/src/bin/yzx.rs, nushell/scripts/core/yzx_internal_dispatch.nu, core/yazelix.nu, and the doctor/launch families. Classify each remaining public family as either: 1) plausible next mixed Rust cut, 2) explicit no-go for now, or 3) likely Nushell survivor. Also name the better near-term Rust deletion lanes outside the public CLI epic when those are stronger than another root cut.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The audit names the remaining public families after qsb5 and x3u2 using the current code, not stale registry assumptions. 2. It identifies at least one plausible next mixed Rust candidate or states explicitly that none are ready. 3. It records the major no-go families and the better non-CLI Rust deletion lanes if they are stronger next moves.
notes:
2026-04-20 audit outcome:
- Confirmed that qsb5 is complete and should be treated as landed prerequisite work, not a still-open gate
- Confirmed that the public Rust root now owns env/run/update/status via yzx.rs + yzx_control, and nushell_externs.nu is only startup compatibility glue
- Remaining public family classification:
  - Closest plausible next mixed Rust cut: doctor
  - Explicit no-go for now: launch/enter/restart
  - Likely Nushell survivors: config/edit/import, menu/popup/screen/keys/tutor/whats_new, desktop/home_manager/dev, why/sponsor
- Structural cleanup before later public cuts: split the remaining core/yazelix.nu owner lump so doctor/restart/cwd/reveal stop riding together
- Better near-term Rust deletion lanes outside this epic remain generated runtime materialization and terminal/Helix materialization
close_reason: Completed: audited the live post-qsb5 public command surface, identified doctor as the only plausible next mixed Rust family, marked launch and interactive UX families as no-go or Nushell survivors, and redirected near-term Rust effort toward stronger non-CLI deletion lanes where appropriate.

title: Split the remaining core/yazelix.nu owner lump into explicit internal families
description: core/yazelix.nu is no longer the public yzx registry, but it still owns a mixed internal cluster: doctor, restart, cwd, reveal, why, and sponsor. That lump makes later public Rust family cuts fuzzier than they need to be. Split the remaining internal command owners so future CLI decisions act on honest families instead of one catch-all module.
design: Keep this delete-first. Do not add new Rust ownership here. Move the surviving internal command bodies behind explicit modules or thin family entrypoints so yzx_internal_dispatch.nu no longer relies on core/yazelix.nu as a mixed owner bucket. The goal is clearer command-family boundaries, not framework churn.
acceptance_criteria: 1. core/yazelix.nu no longer serves as a mixed internal owner for unrelated command families. 2. yzx_internal_dispatch.nu routes the surviving Nushell-owned families through explicit family modules or clearly bounded entrypoints. 3. Later public Rust family beads can name exact internal Nu owners without referring to the old catch-all module.
notes:
Split the remaining mixed core/yazelix.nu owner into explicit internal families: core/yzx_support.nu (why/sponsor), core/yzx_workspace.nu (cwd/reveal), core/yzx_session.nu (restart), and core/yzx_doctor.nu (doctor). Updated yzx_internal_dispatch.nu to route those families directly instead of importing the old catch-all module. Rewrote the CLI rewrite and migration docs so later Rust beads name the real surviving Nu owners. Also updated the stale reveal regression to defend the new internal-dispatch contract instead of the pre-Rust-root helper path.

Validation: nix develop -c nu nushell/scripts/dev/validate_syntax.nu; nix develop -c nu nushell/scripts/dev/validate_specs.nu; nix develop -c nu -c 'source nushell/scripts/dev/test_yzx_workspace_commands.nu; test_yzx_cwd_requires_zellij'; nix develop -c nu -c 'source nushell/scripts/dev/test_yzx_workspace_commands.nu; test_doctor_fix_repairs_missing_managed_generated_layout'; nix develop -c nu -c 'source nushell/scripts/dev/test_yzx_maintainer.nu; test_home_manager_profile_restart_uses_owner_wrapper_without_manual_surfaces'; nix develop -c nu -c 'source nushell/scripts/dev/test_yzx_workspace_commands.nu; test_yzx_cli_reveal_routes_through_internal_dispatch'; nix develop -c nu -c 'use "nushell/scripts/core/yazelix.nu" *; yzx why'; git diff --check
close_reason: Completed: split the mixed core/yazelix.nu owner into explicit internal Nu families and updated the ownership docs/tests to match.

title: Decide whether yzx doctor can be the next public Rust owner cut
description: After env/run/update/status, doctor is the only remaining public family that looks even plausibly close to a real Rust public-owner cut. Verify whether doctor can move without carrying the current Nushell doctor owner cluster forward in parallel.
design: Audit the surviving doctor owners in core/yazelix.nu, utils/doctor.nu, doctor_helix_report.nu, doctor_runtime_report.nu, install_ownership_report.nu, and any fix or repair helpers they call. A good outcome is either: 1) a concrete deletion budget for a future public Rust doctor cut, or 2) an explicit no-go decision that doctor is still too mixed. Do not count a Rust parser above the same doctor.nu tree as progress.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The bead names the exact Nushell doctor owners that would have to disappear or materially shrink for a public Rust doctor cut to count. 2. It states clearly whether doctor is actually the next viable mixed public family or not. 3. If viable, it names the parity harness and remaining Nushell rendering/fix roles. If not viable, it records the blocker owners explicitly.
notes:
2026-04-21 audit outcome:
- No-go for v15.x as the next public Rust owner cut.
- The public wrapper at nushell/scripts/core/yzx_doctor.nu is only 25 lines, so a Rust-root doctor move would mostly delete a tiny parser shim rather than a real family owner.
- The meaningful surviving doctor ownership is still Nushell-side: nushell/scripts/utils/doctor.nu (365 lines) owns family assembly, summary building, human rendering, and the interactive --fix flow; doctor_helix_report.nu (80 lines), doctor_runtime_report.nu (96 lines), and install_ownership_report.nu (89 lines) still shape requests and package Rust findings for the family.
- Rust already owns most typed evaluation behind the family: doctor-config.evaluate, doctor-helix.evaluate, doctor-runtime.evaluate, and install-ownership.evaluate. The remaining deletion budget is therefore not "move yzx doctor to Rust" but "delete or split the remaining doctor.nu report/fix owner cluster."
- A future public Rust doctor cut is only worth reopening if the family is first split into: 1) a read-only structured report owner that materially shrinks or deletes utils/doctor.nu and the report shims, and 2) an explicitly Nushell-owned interactive repair surface for --fix and human rendering. Until then, moving the public parser would add another layer above the same mixed family.
- Parity harness if reopened later: keep nushell/scripts/dev/test_yzx_doctor_commands.nu and the helix/runtime doctor contract tests as the family-level regression suite.
close_reason: Completed: audited doctor ownership and recorded a v15.x no-go decision because the real family remains a mixed Nushell report and fix owner cluster.

title: Fix installed runtime missing split internal dispatcher modules
description: The packaged yzx runtime can fail at desktop launch and doctor because the installed runtime includes yzx_internal_dispatch.nu but not the split core/yzx_*.nu family modules introduced by the internal-family split. The result is a shipped parse-time module_not_found failure in the installed package even though repo-local tests pass.
design: Fix the shipped runtime, not the desktop entry. Make sure the split Nushell core modules are part of the tracked package source, then tighten installed-runtime validation so the built flake runtime/package proves that yzx_internal_dispatch.nu can see its sibling core/yzx_*.nu modules. Reject any fix that only rewrites the desktop file or papers over the parser failure at launch time.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The built installed runtime includes the split core/yzx_*.nu modules required by yzx_internal_dispatch.nu. 2. The installed yzx desktop path no longer fails with module_not_found for the split internal-family modules. 3. Installed-runtime validation catches this class of missing shipped Nushell module before release.
close_reason: Completed: removed the internal Nu dispatcher bridge, routed Rust yzx directly to concrete Nu modules, restored installed-runtime validation, and refreshed the local profile so the shipped CLI no longer fails on missing split modules.

title: Prepare Steel-mode support for Helix
description: Prepare Yazelix to support Helix's emerging Steel-based plugin system once it is merged upstream or otherwise stable enough to justify first-class support.
design:
Updated decision, 2026-05-30: Yazelix should treat Helix Steel as the intended editor base, not vanilla Helix plus optional Steel mode. The normal in-Yazelix editor should be a bundled Yazelix-owned Helix Steel fork once packaged.

Product contract:
- Inside Yazelix, users get the bundled Yazelix Helix Steel editor.
- Yazelix-owned Helix files live under ~/.config/yazelix/helix/.
- Native ~/.config/helix remains untouched and is ignored by Yazelix-managed editor sessions.
- Do not introduce a user-facing helix.mode or backend selector. The product should not ask users to choose managed/native/steel/vanilla modes.

Fork contract:
- First Yazelix patch should be a small config-dir override, preferably --config-dir or an equivalent loader override, so config.toml, languages.toml, themes, ignore, runtime overlays, and Steel init files all resolve from the Yazelix Helix root without scoped XDG.
- The patch must be small enough to carry across Steel rebases and upstream landing.
- The current scoped-XDG stock-Helix path is only a temporary bridge, not the destination.

Advanced escape hatch:
- Plenty of users run custom Helix forks, so support an explicit external Helix binary/runtime pair at the user's risk.
- The pair, not the binary alone, is the unit: binary and runtime_path must both be set or both omitted.
- External binaries still launch under the Yazelix Helix config contract. If they support --config-dir, use it; otherwise use the scoped-XDG adapter where possible and fail fast when the contract cannot be honored.

Release/migration note:
- v17.1 is the pre-Steel escape-hatch tag for users who are not ready to move to the bundled Yazelix Helix Steel line.
acceptance_criteria:
1. A chosen source branch/repo for the Yazelix Helix Steel fork is recorded, including how it tracks the Steel branch before and after upstream merge.
2. The fork carries a minimal config-dir override that removes the need for scoped XDG in the default bundled path.
3. Yazelix has no public helix.mode or backend selector; the bundled Steel fork is the normal editor path.
4. The only custom-editor escape hatch is an explicit external binary/runtime pair, with both fields required together.
5. External Helix launch validation verifies binary existence, runtime_path existence, and whether the binary can satisfy the Yazelix config-dir contract.
6. v17.1 is documented as the pre-Steel pin for users who need the previous editor/runtime shape.
notes:
Repo naming decision, 2026-05-30: the Yazelix-owned Helix Steel fork repository should be named `yazelix-helix` when it is created. Do not use `yazelix-terminal`-style generic naming here; the fork is specifically the editor base.

Steel bundling decision, 2026-05-30: Yazelix should ship Steel already installed in the bundled `yazelix-helix` editor path. Normal users should not have to install Steel or manually wire the Steel runtime/plugin loader. The bundled editor runtime should include matching Helix binary, Helix runtime, Steel support, and the Yazelix-owned Steel config/init path.

Plugin bundle decision, 2026-05-30: do not ship first-party Yazelix Steel plugins initially. Start with a small optional/default bundle of existing cool Steel cogs/plugins from the Steel ecosystem after packaging proves the config-dir path. The relevant upstream Steel branch `mattwparas/steel-event-system` ships the Steel engine and examples, while its `STEEL.md` points to `mattwparas/helix-config` for concrete cogs such as recentf, file-tree, keymap helpers, splash, labelled buffers, git-status-picker, and themes. Treat those as candidates to evaluate.

Command rename, 2026-05-31: renamed the Rust-owned Helix Steel action command from `yazelix-open-shell-here` to `yzx-new-shell` before the Steel work was pushed. No compatibility alias is kept because this command surface is still local/unreleased.

Verification for rename:
- `cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core helix_materialization::tests`
- `cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core doctor_helix_report::tests`
- `nix build .#runtime --override-input yazelixHelix ../yazelix-helix --no-write-lock-file -o result_yazelix_helix_runtime`
- packaged `helix-materialization.generate` smoke shows `yzx-new-shell` as public and no stale `yazelix-open-shell-here` references

title: Bundle Yazelix Helix Steel fork as the owned editor runtime
description: Package a Yazelix-owned Helix Steel fork as the normal in-Yazelix editor runtime. This replaces the earlier idea of offering user-facing helix.mode/backend choices.
design:
The bundled editor should be a Yazelix-owned Helix Steel fork, not a user-visible choice between vanilla/steel/native modes. Vanilla Helix from source can exist temporarily only as a maintainer/package fallback before the Steel merge if needed; it should not become a stable user-facing mode.

The fork should carry as little Yazelix-specific delta as possible at first: config-dir override, package identity, and any runtime patches needed for reliable Yazelix launch. Additional editor-native Yazelix features should wait until the fork/package path is validated.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Fork source, branch, and update policy are recorded.
2. The packaged default editor is the Yazelix Helix Steel fork.
3. Native ~/.config/helix is ignored by bundled Yazelix editor sessions.
4. The package exposes enough version/runtime metadata for yzx doctor and release notes.
5. A temporary vanilla-from-source fallback, if kept, is maintainer/internal and not presented as a normal user mode.
notes:
Repo naming decision, 2026-05-30: create the owned Helix Steel fork as `yazelix-helix`.

Steel bundling decision, 2026-05-30: the packaged default editor should come with Steel installed and ready to load Steel cogs/plugins. Do not make users install Steel separately for the default path; package the matching binary/runtime/Steel support together.

Initial plugin policy, 2026-05-30: do not bundle Yazelix-specific Steel plugins yet. The first bundle, if enabled by default at all, should use existing cool Steel ecosystem cogs/plugins after review. Current candidates from `mattwparas/helix-config` are `recentf.scm`, `file-tree.scm`, `keymaps.scm`, `labelled-buffers.scm`, `git-status-picker.scm`, `splash.scm`, and `cogs/themes/spacemacs.scm`. Higher-risk pieces like embedded terminal, file watcher, and Vim-motion emulation should remain opt-in or deferred until their runtime and UX impact are tested.

Live test finding, 2026-05-30: `nix path-info .#runtime` currently packages `libexec/hx -> ...-helix-25.07.1/bin/hx`, and `hx --version` reports vanilla `helix 25.07.1`. Therefore the already-implemented vendored Steel plugin materialization cannot show splash/recentf in a real editor session yet; it only proves that Yazelix generates and packages the Steel config files. The visible Steel plugin test is blocked until this bead replaces the default runtime Helix with the Yazelix Steel fork.

Implementation progress, 2026-05-30: local child repo `/home/lucca/pjs/yazelix-helix` is based on Steel commit `4d86612df48447088ef4190bf503fd54a7562aa9` and has local commit `745e2bb6` adding `hx --config-dir`. Main repo wiring adds `packaging/yazelix_helix.nix`, a `yazelixHelix` flake input, a `yazelix_helix` package output, default runtime package routing through the Yazelix Helix package, and `yazelix_hx.sh --config-dir <generated helix dir>`.

Local validation, 2026-05-30: `nix build .#yazelix_helix --override-input yazelixHelix ../yazelix-helix --no-write-lock-file --no-link --print-out-paths` produced `/nix/store/ip75l236c5sc17wlpmiz57ajr9wc2jp2-helix-25.07.1`; that `hx --help` includes `--config-dir`. `nix build .#runtime --override-input yazelixHelix ../yazelix-helix --no-write-lock-file --no-link --print-out-paths` produced `/nix/store/8byqxxssfn0cz7kwqjnzyn5r7nfkb9p6-yazelix-runtime`; its `libexec/hx` resolves to the Yazelix Helix package and `runtime_tools.json` records the Steel fork note. A clean-env wrapper smoke with temp HOME/config/state reports `Config file: <tmp>/state/yazelix/configs/helix/config.toml`, proving native `~/.config/helix/config.toml` does not win for the bundled path.

Release boundary, 2026-05-30: do not close this bead yet. The GitHub repo `luccahuguet/yazelix-helix` does not exist/publish the child commit yet, and `flake.lock` is intentionally not updated. Cross-repo transaction still requires explicit approval to create/push the child repo, update the main lock to the published child revision, and rerun the main runtime validation without local overrides.

Live test fix, 2026-05-30: manual testing found that Steel itself worked, but Yazelix's generated Helix Steel module did not expose `evalp`/`eval-buffer` as Helix typed commands and the splash plugin never appeared because the wrapper-added `--config-dir`/`-c` arguments made `(command-line) == '("hx")` false. Local patch imports/provides `evalp` and `eval-buffer` from `helix/ext.scm`; the wrapper exports `YAZELIX_HELIX_SHOW_SPLASH=1` only for no-argument launches and generated `init.scm` uses that signal for splash. Validation: focused cargo tests pass, override runtime rebuild passes, generated `helix.scm`/`init.scm` contain the expected forms, and clean-env wrapper `--health` succeeds.

Follow-up live test fix, 2026-05-30: `evalp` still did not appear when only `helix.scm` imported/provided it; Steel typed-command discovery needs names imported by `init.scm`, which runs in the global engine environment. The generated `init.scm` now imports `evalp`/`eval-buffer` from `helix/ext.scm`, and recentf imports `recentf-open-files` there as well. Splash eligibility also changed from no-args-only to no args or directory-only args, because Yazelix editor-pane flows can launch Helix with the workspace directory as an argument. Validation: focused cargo tests pass, override runtime rebuild refreshed `result_yazelix_helix_runtime`, generated `init.scm` contains the command imports, clean-env wrapper `--health` succeeds, and a fake-Helix wrapper smoke with a directory arg sets `YAZELIX_HELIX_SHOW_SPLASH=1`.

Correction after Steel-enabled live test, 2026-05-30: the real reason `evalp` was missing in the runtime build was that the Nix package did not enable Helix-Term’s `steel` Cargo feature. `packaging/yazelix_helix.nix` now adds `steel` to `cargoBuildFeatures` and `cargoCheckFeatures`. The `os/getenv` splash approach was also invalid in Helix embedded Steel and has been removed; the POSIX wrapper now passes `--show-splash true|false` into `yzx_core`, and Rust materialization emits a literal `(show-splash)` only for workspace/directory launches. Validation: `cargo fmt --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml --all --check`, focused Rust tests for the wrapper and Steel materialization pass, `nix build .#runtime --override-input yazelixHelix ../yazelix-helix --no-write-lock-file -o result_yazelix_helix_runtime` succeeds, generated `init.scm` contains top-level `evalp`/`eval-buffer` wrappers and no `os/getenv`, and wrapper `--health` uses the generated config dir with the Steel-enabled Helix binary.

Manual validation, 2026-05-30: maintainer launched `YAZELIX_SKIP_STABLE_WRAPPER_REDIRECT=1 ./result_yazelix_helix_runtime/bin/yzx launch --path $env.PWD` and confirmed the Steel splash/file-picker UI appears, proving the Steel-enabled packaged Helix loads Yazelix-generated `init.scm` in a real editor session. Remaining visual polish: the borrowed splash component can visually coexist with the picker and should be treated as plugin polish, not the core packaging/config-dir blocker.
close_reason: Completed: Yazelix's default runtime packages the published Yazelix Helix Steel fork from luccahuguet/yazelix-helix commit 745e2bb6, routes public hx through the Yazelix wrapper, ignores native ~/.config/helix for managed sessions, exposes runtime metadata, and passes no-override runtime build plus locked Steel command smoke checks. Remaining child-owned package/cache/check-speed work stays tracked as follow-up beads.

comments.author: lucca
comments.text: Follow-up bare-hx validation, 2026-05-30: maintainer found that files opened through Yazi loaded Steel, but running `hx` from a Yazelix shell pane bypassed Steel because runtime `toolbin/hx` still pointed directly at raw Helix. Local fix keeps raw Steel Helix at `libexec/hx`, rewrites public `toolbin/hx` to `shells/posix/yazelix_hx.sh`, and sets `YAZELIX_MANAGED_HELIX_BINARY` to `libexec/hx` in both computed runtime env and sourced shell env. Validation: focused Rust tests pass, override runtime rebuild succeeds, `toolbin/hx` resolves to the wrapper, clean-env `hx --health` reports Yazelix-generated Helix config, and generated `init.scm` still exposes `evalp`/`eval-buffer` with literal splash emission.

comments.author: lucca
comments.text: Follow-up command namespace cleanup, 2026-05-30: live command completion showed prefixed helper globals such as `yazelix.recentf.*` because generated `init.scm` imported plugin modules at top level with `prefix-in`. Local fix moves plugin imports and startup side effects into module-scoped `helix.scm`; only `eval-buffer`, `evalp`, and `recentf-open-files` are provided as user-facing Steel commands. `show-splash` and `recentf-snapshot` remain internal startup calls. Validation: formatter and focused Rust tests pass, override runtime rebuild succeeds, generated `init.scm` has no `prefix-in`/`yazelix.*` entries, and clean-env `hx --health` still uses the generated Helix config.

comments.author: lucca
comments.text: Follow-up splash policy fix, 2026-05-30: live testing showed the borrowed Steel splash component is transparent and can sit over the file picker/editor when normal Yazelix workspace/file flows open Helix. Treat that as a Yazelix integration bug, not a Helix core bug. Local fix makes `helix.plugins.splash` default false, keeps the splash plugin opt-in, and changes the wrapper so `(show-splash)` is emitted only for a truly bare `hx` invocation. Directory and file launches now materialize Steel without showing splash. The wrapper also now prefers its own runtime `libexec/yzx_core` before inherited `YAZELIX_YZX_CORE_BIN`, preventing old Yazelix shell env from poisoning a local override runtime. Validation: focused Rust tests pass, override runtime rebuild passes, default clean-env `hx --health` materializes only recentf, opt-in bare `hx` emits `(show-splash)`, and opt-in `hx <dir>` imports splash without calling it.

comments.author: lucca
comments.text: Follow-up Steel action/doctor implementation, 2026-05-30: generated Helix Steel commands now come from a Rust command registry with public/internal visibility metadata. Public commands are eval-buffer, evalp, yazelix-open-shell-here, and recentf-open-files when recentf is enabled; recentf-snapshot and show-splash remain internal. Added :yazelix-open-shell-here, which resolves the current Helix file or workspace and shells through the packaged yzx_control terminal opener with POSIX path quoting. Added yzx doctor helix-steel plus managed Steel entrypoint checks for missing raw hx, missing public commands, internal command leaks, stale init.scm command bindings, and broken open-shell wiring. Deliberately did not add Yazelix-aware recentf tracking because the plugin already snapshots open Helix documents. Validation: focused cargo tests for Steel materialization, Helix doctor, wrapper env handling, and doctor arg parsing pass; override runtime build to result_yazelix_helix_runtime passes; clean-env hx --health materializes the expected Steel module and yzx doctor helix-steel --json reports healthy.

title: Export Yazelix Helix as a child-owned package
description: Move the Helix fork package definition into the yazelix_helix child repository so main Yazelix consumes a package output instead of overriding nixpkgs Helix from the main flake.
design: The child repository should own its Nix packaging, including source hash/vendor hash policy, checked package output, and any fast developer output. Main Yazelix should treat the editor as a child package artifact, matching the existing child-owned package pattern used by other Yazelix runtime components.
acceptance_criteria:
1. The yazelix_helix child repo exports packages for supported systems.
2. The exported package builds hx from the Yazelix Helix Steel fork and exposes --config-dir.
3. Main Yazelix no longer carries the Helix override logic or cargo vendor hash for the fork.
4. Main Yazelix consumes the child package output through the flake input.
5. Local override validation works with --override-input yazelixHelix ../yazelix-helix.
close_reason: Exported yazelix-helix as child-owned package bfc7bac, updated main flake.lock to the published GitHub revision, removed main-owned packaging/yazelix_helix.nix, and verified .#yazelix_helix plus local override builds expose --config-dir.

title: Add binary cache for Yazelix Helix builds
description: Add CI and a binary-cache publication path for the Yazelix Helix child package so users and main Yazelix builds download the fork instead of compiling Helix locally.
design: Prefer a child-repo cache pipeline after the child owns its package output. The full checked Helix package should build in child CI and publish to the configured binary cache. Main Yazelix should benefit from that cache by consuming the locked child package, not by rebuilding Helix during routine main-repo work.
acceptance_criteria:
1. The yazelix_helix child repo has CI that builds the checked package for supported systems or the explicitly supported launch systems.
2. Successful CI publishes the package to the chosen binary cache.
3. Maintainer setup documentation records the cache substituter and public key.
4. A fresh main Yazelix runtime build with the child input locked can substitute the Helix package from cache when available.
5. Cache absence degrades to a normal source build, not to a broken runtime.
close_reason: Completed. Child yazelix-helix now has Publish Yazelix Helix Cache CI, Cachix auth is configured, run 26724797718 passed for child commit 516580ae, and the current child output /nix/store/ach0klldfmm0brq930h8imf5l8pngir6-yazelix-helix-25.7.1 resolves from https://yazelix.cachix.org with narinfo-cache-negative-ttl disabled. Main commit 44993625 adds the Helix package to the Yazelix cache workflow and docs; main CI run 26724044258 and Publish Nix Cache run 26724044263 passed. Main locked Helix output /nix/store/s8gj86gy2a7n8n3670is1mv1sxsi4ygn-yazelix-helix-25.7.1 resolves from the Yazelix cache. Cache absence remains normal Nix source-build behavior and is documented.

title: Stop re-running Helix checks from main runtime builds
description: Make main Yazelix runtime builds consume an already-tested Yazelix Helix package without duplicating the expensive Helix checkPhase during routine main-repo builds.
design: Once yazelix_helix owns the full checked package, main Yazelix should avoid paying for Helix tests in every local runtime build. The main repo may consume the child checked output directly, or the child may expose a fast package for local smoke builds while CI/release use the checked package. Do not weaken release validation unless child CI has already proved the Helix package.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Main Yazelix routine runtime builds do not rerun the full Helix test/check phase when the child package is already built or substitutable.
2. Release/CI still has one authoritative checked Helix build before publication.
3. The fast path is clearly maintainer/local if it skips checks.
4. Main runtime validation still proves the packaged hx exposes --config-dir and the wrapper uses the generated config directory.
5. The package boundary is documented in Beads or release notes so future agents do not reintroduce duplicate check ownership.

title: Lock Yazelix to published Yazelix Helix commits
description: Make the main Yazelix flake consume only published yazelix_helix commits for normal builds and releases, keeping local overrides as smoke tests rather than durable integration state.
design: Treat a main-repo flake.lock update that consumes yazelix_helix as a cross-repo release transaction. Local --override-input builds are development smoke tests only. Before landing main integration, push the child repo, update main flake.lock to the published child revision, then validate the main runtime without overrides.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Main flake.lock points at a published yazelix_helix commit, not a local path-only revision.
2. The child commit is pushed before the main lock update is committed.
3. Main runtime validation passes without --override-input.
4. Beads close only after the no-override validation passes.
5. Documentation or Beads notes state that local overrides are smoke tests and not release evidence.
close_reason: Completed: created/published luccahuguet/yazelix-helix at 745e2bb6, updated flake.lock to that GitHub revision, built .#runtime without --override-input, verified child-release transaction, and smoke-checked the locked runtime exposes hx --config-dir plus public Steel commands evalp, yzx-new-shell, and recentf-open-files.

title: Model external Helix as a binary/runtime pair
description: Keep support for users who run custom Helix forks, but model the escape hatch as a matched binary/runtime pair rather than a bare binary. The runtime path mismatch has already caused real user issues.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Config/schema/Home Manager expose one nullable external Helix object with required binary and runtime_path when present.
2. Normal bundled Yazelix Steel Helix remains the default when the object is null or omitted.
3. Bare binary-only configuration is rejected with a clear error.
4. Bare runtime_path-only configuration is rejected with a clear error.
5. Doctor reports the external pair, validates both paths, and warns that mismatched binary/runtime versions are user-owned risk.
6. Runtime-env and the managed hx wrapper pass HELIX_RUNTIME from the external pair and still enforce the Yazelix Helix config root.
close_reason: Implemented helix.external as the supported external Helix binary/runtime pair across schema, config normalization, Home Manager, runtime-env, session facts, status/workspace launch, doctor reporting, docs, onboarding, and maintainer shell. Added validation/tests for incomplete pairs, old helix.runtime_path, non-Helix editor conflicts, runtime-env bare custom binaries, and doctor path/risk reporting.

title: Patch Helix Steel with a config-dir override
description: Add the minimal Helix fork patch Yazelix needs: a config-dir override that makes config.toml, languages.toml, themes, ignore, runtime overlays, and Steel init files resolve from ~/.config/yazelix/helix without process-wide scoped XDG.
design:
Implement a small Helix loader/CLI patch on top of the Steel branch: initialize a config-dir override before runtime_dirs or theme/language loading can observe the default config_dir. Prefer --config-dir for explicit CLI behavior, with an internal/env equivalent only if needed by wrappers.

The override must feed the existing helix_loader::config_dir() function so all config-dir consumers move together: languages.toml, themes, ignore, runtime overlay, workspace trust where appropriate, and Steel's init.scm/helix.scm lookup. Avoid patching each call site independently.
acceptance_criteria:
1. hx --config-dir <dir> --health reports Language file under <dir>/languages.toml when present.
2. Custom themes under <dir>/themes win over native ~/.config/helix/themes without setting XDG_CONFIG_HOME.
3. HELIX_RUNTIME remains available for explicit external runtime pairing and does not conflict with the config-dir override.
4. Steel init.scm/helix.scm lookup respects the same config-dir root unless an explicit Steel-only override is intentionally documented.
5. Tests cover default config_dir, CLI override, and early runtime_dirs initialization order.
notes:
Rust dependency decision, 2026-05-30: no new production crates and no new dev-only crates. Build the config-dir override in-house with the Steel branch's existing CLI parser, `once_cell`, and loader/runtime plumbing. Rejected alternatives: process-wide XDG override, patching each config/runtime call site independently, and using unrelated Helix forks. Packaging impact: the child `yazelix-helix` fork adds `hx --config-dir`; the main Yazelix wrapper should pass that flag and the main runtime package should consume the owned fork after publication.

Local implementation, 2026-05-30: `/home/lucca/pjs/yazelix-helix` commit `745e2bb6` adds `--config-dir <path>` to `helix-term`, initializes `helix_loader` before config/log/runtime setup, and makes `helix_loader::config_dir()` return the initialized override.

Local validation, 2026-05-30: `rustfmt --edition 2021 --check helix-term/src/args.rs helix-loader/src/lib.rs helix-term/src/main.rs` passed; `cargo test -p helix-term args::tests::` passed; direct `target/debug/hx --config-dir <tmp> --health` reported config and languages paths under `<tmp>`; Nix package probes built the fork when derivation `version` stayed aligned to upstream `helix 25.07.1`.

Publication boundary, 2026-05-30: do not close this bead yet because `yazelix-2k3.1` still needs the child repo pushed and the main flake locked to the published child revision before the dependency is durable for other machines.
close_reason: Completed: published the Yazelix Helix Steel fork with hx --config-dir at luccahuguet/yazelix-helix commit 745e2bb6. Locked main runtime now builds without --override-input and the locked hx --help exposes --config-dir.

title: Add declarative Helix Steel plugin config
notes: Crate-vs-in-house decision, 2026-05-30: production crates added: none. Dev-only crates added: none. In-house/std: reuse the existing config normalizer and managed Helix materialization path; render the generated Steel helix.scm/init.scm entrypoints directly; copy vendored Scheme files with the existing atomic managed-write helper. Rejected alternatives: a new plugin-manager crate, remote plugin fetching at runtime, process-wide XDG overrides, arbitrary user plugin path execution, and exposing keymaps/labelled-buffers as user toggles before they are actual user-facing plugins. Packaging impact: curated Steel Scheme files are vendored under configs/helix/steel_plugins and included in the runtime closure; yazelix_hx exports HELIX_STEEL_CONFIG to the generated Yazelix state directory for the Steel fork.
close_reason: Implemented declarative helix.plugins toggles for bundled Steel plugins, vendored the initial Scheme files into the runtime, generated Steel helix.scm/init.scm under Yazelix state, exported HELIX_STEEL_CONFIG from the hx wrapper, and set the managed Helix theme to ayu_evolve. Verified with cargo fmt --all --check, cargo test -p yazelix_core helix_materialization, cargo check, config surface validation, nix build .#runtime --no-link, and a runtime helix-materialization.generate smoke that confirmed vendored files are packaged.

title: Add Helix Steel plugin manifest surface
description: Let users declare arbitrary Yazelix-managed Helix Steel plugins in settings.jsonc instead of only toggling the curated built-ins. The manifest should copy user-owned plugin files from ~/.config/yazelix/helix/steel_plugins, expose only declared public commands, keep internal/startup commands out of completion, and validate bad manifests early.
acceptance_criteria:
1. settings.jsonc, schema, config contract, config UI metadata, and Home Manager expose helix.steel_plugins as { enabled = [...], extra = [...] }.
2. Bundled plugin ids are loaded dynamically from configs/helix/steel_plugins/manifest.toml instead of Rust hardcoding curated plugin metadata.
3. A custom extra manifest can load a user-owned .scm file from helix/steel_plugins, import declared commands, expose only public commands, and run declared startup commands.
4. Invalid manifest shape, duplicate ids/commands, unsafe paths, unknown bundled ids, file path collisions, or missing source files fail fast with actionable errors.
5. Generated helix.scm includes custom plugin command metadata without leaking internal commands.
6. Focused Rust tests and packaged runtime smoke cover bundled defaults, support files, custom plugin loading, and invalid manifest rejection.
notes:
Implemented the repository-backed Helix Steel plugin surface. Bundled plugin metadata now lives in configs/helix/steel_plugins/manifest.toml, settings use helix.steel_plugins.enabled for bundled ids plus helix.steel_plugins.extra for user-owned manifests, and materialization loads both through the same parser. Only Rust-owned base commands remain hardcoded: eval-buffer, evalp, and yzx-new-shell. Splash startup uses manifest startup_condition = "show_splash".

Validation run: cargo fmt --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml --all; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core helix_materialization_; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core config_normalize; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core config_ui; jq empty config_metadata/yazelix_settings.schema.json; yzx_repo_validator validate-config-surface-contract; yzx_repo_validator validate-docs-experience; nix build .#runtime --override-input yazelixHelix ../yazelix-helix --no-write-lock-file -o result_yazelix_helix_runtime; packaged yzx_core helix-materialization.generate smoke with a custom extra plugin; Home Manager eval smoke for helix_steel_plugins with enabled + extra.
close_reason: Completed repository-backed Helix Steel plugin manifest surface with bundled enabled ids, custom extra manifests, validation, docs, and packaged runtime smoke.

title: Add direct Helix action bridge for Yazelix
description: Replace Helix-targeted simulated typing with a first-class action bridge between Yazelix control commands and the bundled Steel-enabled Helix fork.
design:
Steel gives Yazelix an in-process Helix extension surface, but not an external remote-control surface by itself. The right direction is to keep Zellij as the pane/layout authority while adding a structured Helix action bridge for editor state changes that currently require simulated typing.

The bridge should be explicit and narrow at first:
- external callers use `yzx_control helix ...` or an equivalent internal control route
- the Helix side lives in the bundled `yazelix-helix` fork and schedules work on the Helix main context
- payloads are structured data, not terminal keystrokes
- unsupported actions fail fast instead of falling back to `zellij write-chars`

This is not a reason to move Zellij pane orchestration into Helix. Actions like opening/focusing panes, workspace retargeting, and tab layout changes remain owned by Zellij/pane-orchestrator.
acceptance_criteria:
1. The boundary between Zellij-owned pane orchestration and Helix-owned editor actions is documented.
2. A minimal Helix action bridge architecture is chosen, including transport, addressing, security/scope, lifecycle, and failure modes.
3. The first implementation exposes at least one editor-native action without using simulated terminal typing.
4. External commands fail clearly when no compatible Yazelix-managed Helix instance is available.
5. No legacy write-chars fallback is added for newly migrated action surfaces.
6. Doctor or validation can detect when the bridge is unavailable or mismatched with the bundled Helix runtime.
close_reason: Completed direct Helix action bridge epic. Added documented bridge contract, published yazelix-helix bridge implementation with helix.get_context/helix.set_cwd/helix.open_files, added main yzx_control helix action/status client, enabled managed Helix wrapper bridge env, migrated Yazi-to-Helix file opens and open-editor-cwd cwd sync away from terminal text injection, and published pane-orchestrator cleanup rejecting direct Helix :open/:cd command-mode pipes. Zellij remains pane/layout owner; Helix-owned buffer/cwd actions use structured bridge requests and fail clearly when no managed bridge is available. Validation across child/main: helix child fmt/tests/build/live socket smoke; main cargo fmt/tests for helix bridge and workspace surface; pane-orchestrator child fmt/tests/package build; runtime override build; no-override runtime build; workspace-session and child-release validators.

title: Design Helix action bridge contract
description: Define the supported contract for Yazelix-to-Helix actions before adding IPC or migrating write-chars call sites.
design:
Start from first principles: Helix can safely own editor state, selections, buffers, typed commands, and Steel/plugin execution; Zellij owns panes, tabs, focus, and layout. The bridge contract should encode that split instead of exposing a generic "send keys" API under a new name.

The design should choose whether the first bridge uses a Unix socket, a state-dir action queue, Zellij pipe routing, or a Helix-native IPC hook in `yazelix-helix`. It must account for multiple Helix panes, stale sessions, restart behavior, action authorization, and how `yzx doctor helix-steel` reports mismatches.
acceptance_criteria:
1. A contract or Bead notes define transport, addressing, payload format, response format, lifecycle, and timeout behavior.
2. The contract names which actions are in scope for the first slice and which remain Zellij-owned.
3. The design rejects simulated keystroke fallback for migrated actions.
4. The design explains how multiple Helix instances in one Yazelix session are addressed.
5. The design lists required doctor/validation checks before implementation starts.
notes: Designing the Helix action bridge as a contract first. No Rust dependency changes expected for this decision bead; implementation beads will record their own crate-vs-in-house decisions.
close_reason: Completed: added docs/contracts/helix_action_bridge_contract.md and wired it into the contracts inventory plus architecture map. The contract chooses a Helix-native per-instance Unix socket bridge in yazelix-helix, defines addressing, payloads, responses, lifecycle, timeouts, first-slice actions, Zellij-owned actions, no-keystroke-fallback policy, and doctor/validation requirements. Validated with git diff --check and yzx_repo_validator validate-contracts.

title: Add Helix-side action bridge to yazelix-helix
description: Implement the Helix-side receiver/dispatcher in the bundled Steel-enabled Yazelix Helix fork so external Yazelix commands can request structured editor actions.
design:
This is child-repo work in `yazelix-helix`, with main-repo integration only after the fork-side capability exists. Prefer a thin Helix patch that exposes an action receiver and dispatches onto the Helix main context, reusing Steel APIs where they are the cleanest way to invoke editor behavior.

The first slice should be intentionally small: prove one non-trivial editor-native action can be invoked from outside Helix without terminal keystrokes. Avoid building a broad plugin framework before the transport, lifecycle, and addressing are proven.
acceptance_criteria:
1. `yazelix-helix` exposes a runtime action receiver compatible with the contract from yazelix-2k3.6.1.
2. Actions run on the correct Helix context/main thread and do not block the UI indefinitely.
3. The receiver has clear startup, shutdown, timeout, and stale-session behavior.
4. At least one editor-native action is implemented and manually testable.
5. The fork delta remains small and documented so it can be carried across Steel rebases.
6. Main-repo integration waits for the child commit to be published or explicitly overridden for local testing.
notes: Crate-vs-in-house decision before implementation: production crates in child repo should be avoided unless existing dependencies already cover JSON, sockets, or async. Prefer in-house/std plus dependencies already present in Helix for Unix socket handling, JSON serialization, random ids, and action dispatch. Rejected alternatives for the first slice: broad IPC frameworks, network-visible transports, Zellij pipe as editor transport, state-file polling, and generic terminal-input APIs. Packaging impact: the bridge is part of the bundled yazelix-helix fork and must be consumed through a published child commit or explicit flake override before main integration is claimed.
close_reason: Completed in yazelix-helix commit 1c89347f and pushed to origin/main. Added opt-in local Unix-socket Helix bridge with owner-only registry/token files under YAZELIX_STATE_DIR, auth-token validation, request timeouts, stale-instance errors, and first-slice actions helix.get_context, helix.set_cwd, and helix.open_files. Validation: cargo fmt --all --check; cargo test -p helix-term yazelix_bridge --features steel; cargo build -p helix-term --features "steel integration" --bin hx with no warnings; live socket smoke for context, cwd, and open-files.

title: Add yzx_control helix action client
description: Add the main-repo control-plane command that sends structured actions to the managed Helix bridge and reports precise failures.
design:
Expose a narrow main-repo control route such as `yzx_control helix action <name> --payload <json>` or a more ergonomic equivalent after the contract is written. The command should discover only Yazelix-managed Helix instances for the active session and refuse ambiguous or stale targets.

This should not become a generic terminal input API. It is a structured editor-action client with explicit action names, JSON payloads, JSON-ish responses where useful, and clear error classes for no session, ambiguous session, bridge unavailable, unsupported action, timeout, and action failure.
acceptance_criteria:
1. A `yzx_control helix ...` command family or equivalent internal route sends structured actions to the managed Helix bridge.
2. The client validates payloads before sending when the action schema is known.
3. Errors are fail-fast and actionable: no compatible Helix, ambiguous target, stale bridge, timeout, unsupported action, and action failure are distinguishable.
4. `yzx doctor helix-steel` or a related doctor check can report bridge availability.
5. Tests cover argument parsing, target discovery errors, payload validation, and response handling without needing a live TUI.
6. A local runtime smoke proves the client can invoke the first bridge action with the overridden `yazelix-helix` fork.
notes: Crate-vs-in-house decision before implementation: use std UnixStream plus existing serde_json/serde stack in yazelix_core. Do not add production crates for IPC, CLI parsing, globbing, async, or discovery; the protocol is one newline-delimited JSON request over a local Unix socket. Rejected alternatives: shelling to socat/nc, Zellij pipe transport, state-file polling, generic terminal input APIs, and broad IPC frameworks. Packaging impact: no new runtime packages expected; main runtime must consume a published yazelix-helix commit before no-override validation is claimed.
close_reason: Completed main-repo Helix bridge client. Added internal yzx_control helix action/status route, typed target discovery under YAZELIX_STATE_DIR/helix_bridge/<session>, payload validation for helix.get_context/set_cwd/open_files, JSON request/response handling over Unix sockets, bridge availability status, wrapper env generation for managed Helix sessions, and flake.lock update to yazelix-helix 1c89347f. Validation: cargo fmt --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml --all --check; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core helix_bridge; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core --test yzx_control_helix_bridge; live yzx_control helix.get_context smoke against /home/lucca/pjs/yazelix-helix/target/debug/hx bridge.

title: Migrate Helix-targeted simulated typing to action bridge
description: Inventory Helix-focused zellij write-chars usage and migrate suitable actions to the structured Helix bridge instead of terminal keystroke emulation.
design:
After the bridge exists, audit every place Yazelix simulates Helix input through Zellij or terminal keystrokes. Migrate only actions that are truly editor-owned. Leave pane creation/focus/layout behavior in Zellij.

Use a delete-first approach: remove obsolete keystroke helpers and tests as each migrated behavior gets a structured action. Do not keep compatibility aliases or fallback write-chars paths for local/unreleased surfaces.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Existing Helix-targeted simulated typing call sites are inventoried and classified as editor-owned or Zellij-owned.
2. At least one real user workflow is migrated to the Helix action bridge.
3. Migrated workflows no longer depend on terminal focus timing or typed command text.
4. Obsolete write-chars helpers/tests are deleted when they no longer serve remaining behavior.
5. Verification includes focused tests and a manual Yazelix session smoke for the migrated workflow.
6. Remaining write-chars usage, if any, is documented as intentionally Zellij/terminal-owned or deferred with follow-up beads.
notes: Crate-vs-in-house decision before implementation: do not add new crates. Use existing serde_json/control-plane code in the main repo and existing std/serde_json surfaces in child code. Migration should delete or bypass Helix command-mode string injection for migrated actions instead of introducing another transport crate. Rejected alternatives: keeping write-chars as fallback, shelling from the pane orchestrator through ad-hoc quoted scripts, adding a generic terminal input API, or making Zellij own editor buffer actions.
close_reason: Completed first migration slice: Yazi-to-Helix file opens through yzx_control zellij open-editor now discover the managed editor pane from pane-orchestrator state, focus it through Zellij, and call helix.open_files through the Helix bridge instead of sending :open/:cd command-mode text. Existing missing-editor behavior still creates a managed editor pane through Zellij. Remaining simulated Helix cwd and legacy direct pane-orchestrator Helix pipe paths are documented as follow-ups yazelix-2k3.6.5 and yazelix-2k3.6.6. Validation: cargo fmt --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml --all --check; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core helix_bridge; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core --test yzx_control_helix_bridge; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core --test yzx_control_workspace_surface.

title: Migrate Helix cwd retargeting to action bridge
description: Move the Helix cwd-sync part of workspace retarget/open-editor-cwd from pane-orchestrator command-mode text to the structured helix.set_cwd bridge action. Zellij should continue to own the workspace retarget and pane lifecycle; only the editor-local cwd update should move to the bridge.
acceptance_criteria: 1. zellij open-editor-cwd and workspace retarget no longer cause the pane orchestrator to send Helix :cd command text. 2. Existing Neovim behavior remains unchanged until it has its own bridge contract. 3. Missing editor panes still create a managed editor pane through Zellij. 4. Bridge-missing/stale/timeout errors are surfaced directly for existing Helix panes. 5. Tests cover existing-pane, missing-pane, and bridge failure paths.
notes: Crate-vs-in-house decision before implementation: no new crates. Reuse the existing Helix bridge client and pane-orchestrator status-bus parsing introduced in yazelix-2k3.6.3/.4. Keep Zellij as workspace owner and call helix.set_cwd only for the editor-local cwd update.
close_reason: Completed Helix cwd bridge migration. zellij open-editor-cwd now sends retarget_workspace with editor=null for Helix so the pane orchestrator does not send Helix :cd text, then targets the active managed Helix pane by zellij pane id and sends helix.set_cwd through the action bridge. Missing/not-ready Helix editor panes still create a managed pane through Zellij, bridge-missing failures surface directly, and Neovim stays on the existing pane-orchestrator path. Validation: cargo fmt --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml --all --check; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core helix_bridge; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core --test yzx_control_helix_bridge; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core --test yzx_control_workspace_surface.

title: Retire pane-orchestrator Helix command-mode open/cd paths
description: After main-repo Helix open/cwd workflows move to the action bridge, remove or reject the pane-orchestrator Helix-specific :open/:cd command text path so direct open_file/set_managed_editor_cwd pipes cannot silently reintroduce simulated typing for Helix.
acceptance_criteria: 1. pane-orchestrator open_file no longer builds or sends Helix :open command text. 2. pane-orchestrator set_managed_editor_cwd no longer builds or sends Helix :cd command text. 3. Neovim command-mode behavior is either retained intentionally or split into its own editor bridge planning bead. 4. Tests prove direct Helix pipe requests fail clearly instead of injecting terminal input. 5. Main-repo callers do not depend on the retired Helix pipe paths.
notes: Crate-vs-in-house decision before implementation: no new crates. This is a child-repo cleanup in yazelix-zellij-pane-orchestrator; remove Helix command-mode text construction in existing Rust modules and tests.
