title: Continue Rust rewrite through renderer-adjacent render-plan data
description: The next Rust-forward phase should move more deterministic setup and generated-config decision logic into `yzx_core` without porting text renderers, shell/process orchestration, or the public `yzx` UX. After config/state/materialization/runtime-env work, the best remaining v15.x seam is renderer-adjacent plan data for generated Zellij and Yazi surfaces: Rust computes typed render-plan records, Nushell keeps KDL/TOML/Lua assembly, file writes, user-facing comments, and host integration glue.
design:
Keep the current ownership boundary quality at least as strong as the completed v15 Rust slices.

Scope principles:
1. Rust owns structured, deterministic plan data only.
2. Nushell stays the renderer owner for generated Zellij KDL, Yazi TOML/Lua, plugin syncing/copying, semantic block extraction/merge, header comments, and user-facing progress text.
3. Do not revisit launch/bootstrap/process execution as part of this phase; that track is intentionally stopped for v15.x unless a new tiny seam appears.
4. Prefer one helper command family at a time, with parity tests before deleting the Nushell decision logic it replaces.
5. Each implementation bead must start by recording the crate-vs-in-house decision per the Rust dependency gate.

Recommended sequence:
- First: Zellij render-plan data, because it has the clearer concentration of reusable geometry/layout/settings decisions.
- Second: Yazi render-plan data, only after the Zellij shape proves the architecture stays clean.
acceptance_criteria:
1. There is a concrete Rust-forward follow-up sequence after the completed config/state/materialization/runtime-env slices.
2. Each child bead targets a renderer-adjacent deterministic decision seam that can shrink Nushell without weakening architecture.
3. Renderer/template ownership remains Nushell-owned throughout this phase.
4. The phase explicitly avoids regressing into shell/process-orchestration rewrites.
notes:
Suggested primary files for this phase:
- `nushell/scripts/setup/zellij_config_merger.nu`
- `nushell/scripts/utils/layout_generator.nu`
- `nushell/scripts/setup/yazi_config_merger.nu`
- `rust_core/yazelix_core/src/runtime_materialization.rs`
- `docs/specs/rust_migration_matrix.md`
- `docs/specs/launch_bootstrap_rust_migration.md`

Guardrails:
- Reject additive Rust plus unchanged Nushell decision logic for the same plan data.
- Keep renderer/template ownership in Nushell.
- Do not move shell quoting, detached process launching, startup profile wiring, or public command prose into Rust.
- Do not reopen automatic migration machinery or broad CLI rewrite work under this epic.
close_reason: all steps complete

title: Move Zellij render-plan decision data into yzx_core while keeping KDL rendering in Nushell
description: `zellij_config_merger.nu` and `layout_generator.nu` still mix real decision logic with renderer work. This bead should move the deterministic Zellij render-plan data into `yzx_core` so Nushell becomes a thin consumer for geometry/layout/settings decisions while still owning KDL assembly, semantic block merging, wasm syncing, file writes, and user-facing progress text.
design:
Start with the Rust dependency gate: record crate-vs-in-house before code changes. Default posture should remain in-house/std unless a narrowly scoped crate clearly reduces risk.

Implementation target:
- Add one private helper command that computes a typed Zellij render plan (name it to fit current bridge conventions, e.g. `zellij-render-plan.compute`, unless extending an existing command is truly cleaner).
- Feed it explicit inputs only: normalized config fields and explicit runtime/layout/plugin path inputs as needed.
- Keep the output purely data-oriented. No prose, no file writes, no shell execution.

Rust should own plan data such as:
- default layout family / managed layout choice
- sidebar geometry percentages now computed in `layout_generator.nu`
- validated/normalized widget tray data
- structured dynamic/enforced owned-setting values that are currently re-derived before rendering
- any small plugin/layout metadata records that are deterministic and reused across generated config + layouts

Nushell must continue to own:
- semantic block extraction and merge against user/native Zellij config
- KDL rendering and placeholder substitution
- plugin wasm sync / runtime path wiring
- file writes, generation fingerprints, progress labels, and comments/headers
- any host/runtime probing that is not just typed plan computation

Delete-first rules:
- Remove the duplicated Nushell plan logic you replace; do not add a Rust plan and keep the same Nu computations beside it.
- If any Nu decision branch survives, document exactly why and open a follow-up instead of silently keeping duplicate ownership.

Non-goals:
- porting KDL parsing/rendering wholesale to Rust
- moving startup/launch orchestration into Rust
- changing user-facing generated file format beyond what naturally falls out of the same renderer consuming new plan data
acceptance_criteria:
1. One Rust helper returns the canonical Zellij render-plan data consumed by Nushell renderers.
2. Nushell no longer independently computes the same layout/geometry/widget-tray/owned-setting decisions in parallel.
3. KDL rendering, semantic block merge, plugin syncing, file writes, and user-facing reporting remain Nushell-owned.
4. Verification commands in the notes pass.
notes:
Primary files to inspect first:
- `nushell/scripts/setup/zellij_config_merger.nu`
- `nushell/scripts/utils/layout_generator.nu`
- `nushell/scripts/setup/zellij_generation_state.nu`
- `rust_core/yazelix_core/src/runtime_materialization.rs`
- `rust_core/yazelix_core/src/bin/yzx_core.rs`

Concrete seams most worth deleting:
- `compute_sidebar_layout_percentages`
- layout family / default layout-name choice still derived in Nushell renderers
- validated widget-tray shaping done immediately before placeholder rendering
- owned-setting classification/data shaping that does not require KDL assembly

Suggested verification:
- `nix develop -c cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml`
- `nix develop -c nu -c 'source nushell/scripts/dev/test_yzx_generated_configs.nu; run_generated_config_canonical_tests'`
- `nix develop -c nu -c 'source nushell/scripts/dev/test_yzx_workspace_commands.nu; run_workspace_canonical_tests'`
- `nix develop -c nu -c 'source nushell/scripts/dev/test_zellij_plugin_contracts.nu; main'`
- `nix develop -c nu -c 'source nushell/scripts/dev/validate_specs.nu; main'`
- `git diff --check`
close_reason: Implemented zellij-render-plan.compute in yzx_core; Nushell merger and layout_generator consume the plan; removed duplicate owned-settings and geometry logic.

title: Move Yazi render-plan decision data into yzx_core while keeping TOML/Lua rendering in Nushell
description: After the Zellij render-plan slice proves the pattern, move the remaining deterministic Yazi render-plan logic into `yzx_core` while keeping TOML/Lua rendering, user override merge, bundled asset syncing, and generated file writes in Nushell. The goal is to delete real Yazi decision logic from Nushell without turning Rust into a second renderer or reviving broad startup optimization machinery.
design:
Start with the Rust dependency gate: record crate-vs-in-house before code changes. Prefer in-house/std unless a narrowly scoped crate clearly improves safety.

Implementation target:
- Add one private helper command that returns a typed Yazi render plan, or extend an immediately adjacent helper only if that keeps the command surface cleaner without mixing unrelated concerns.
- Keep inputs explicit and machine-readable.
- Keep outputs data-only: no prose, no file writes, no TOML/Lua text generation.

Rust should own plan data such as:
- normalized managed plugin set / enabled plugin classification
- deterministic plugin-dependent decisions such as whether git fetcher config is present
- theme/sort render-plan fields that are still re-derived locally before rendering
- any deterministic managed-opener or bundled-asset expectation data that is currently recomputed in Yazi generation helpers

Nushell must continue to own:
- TOML merge with user-managed Yazi config and keymap files
- `init.lua` assembly and runtime-root placeholder rendering
- bundled asset copying/sync and user override discovery
- file writes, comments/headers, and user-facing progress text

Delete-first rules:
- Do not add a Rust plan while leaving equivalent plugin/theme/sort/asset decisions alive in Nushell.
- Keep this bead separate from warm-start optimization experiments; do not absorb deferred optimization bead `yazelix-8h9y` unless the user explicitly retargets it.

Non-goals:
- full Yazi config rendering in Rust
- caching/reuse machinery as a substitute for deleting decision duplication
- rewriting user override merge semantics or bundled asset syncing as part of this slice
acceptance_criteria:
1. One Rust helper returns the canonical Yazi render-plan data consumed by Nushell renderers.
2. Nushell no longer independently computes the same plugin/theme/sort/asset-plan decisions in parallel.
3. TOML/Lua rendering, user override merge, bundled asset syncing, file writes, and user-facing reporting remain Nushell-owned.
4. Verification commands in the notes pass.
notes:
Primary files to inspect first:
- `nushell/scripts/setup/yazi_config_merger.nu`
- `nushell/scripts/setup/yazi_bundled_assets.nu`
- `nushell/scripts/setup/yazi_user_overrides.nu`
- `rust_core/yazelix_core/src/runtime_materialization.rs`
- `rust_core/yazelix_core/src/bin/yzx_core.rs`
- deferred adjacent bead: `yazelix-8h9y`

Concrete seams most worth deleting:
- plugin-set classification and plugin-dependent config branches
- theme/sort render-plan shaping immediately before TOML/Lua rendering
- deterministic managed asset expectation/manifests that are not actually text-rendering work

Suggested verification:
- `nix develop -c cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml`
- `nix develop -c nu -c 'source nushell/scripts/dev/test_yzx_generated_configs.nu; run_generated_config_canonical_tests'`
- `nix develop -c nu -c 'source nushell/scripts/dev/test_helix_managed_config_contracts.nu; run_helix_managed_config_contract_tests'`
- `nix develop -c nu -c 'source nushell/scripts/dev/validate_specs.nu; main'`
- `git diff --check`
close_reason: Added yazi-render-plan.compute (Rust) + yazi_render_plan.nu bridge; yazi_config_merger consumes plan for theme/sort/git/init load order; unit + integration tests; verification passed.

title: Restore Rust test traceability for zellij-render-plan tests
description:
Post-implementation review of `yazelix-3qs8.1` found that the new Rust test files for the Zellij render-plan helper violate the repo's governed test metadata contract. `validate_rust_test_traceability.nu` currently fails because `rust_core/yazelix_core/src/zellij_render_plan.rs` and `rust_core/yazelix_core/tests/yzx_core_zellij_render_plan.rs` are missing the required `// Test lane:` declaration, and the integration test file also needs the normal nearby justification/strength markers for each test.

This is a process/CI failure even though the Rust and generated-config behavior tests are green.
design:
Keep this bead narrow and delete-first: do not rewrite the actual helper behavior or expand test scope unless needed for metadata locality.

Implementation target:
- Add the required `// Test lane:` declaration to both governed Rust test files.
- Add nearby `// Defends:`, `// Regression:`, or `// Invariant:` markers plus structured `// Strength:` lines for every governed test in those files.
- Keep the justification text specific to the actual contract these tests cover; do not paste boilerplate.
- Run the Rust traceability validator directly and keep the existing Rust test suites green.

Non-goals:
- changing helper behavior
- broad Rust test reorganization outside the two files above
acceptance_criteria:
1. `nix develop -c nu -c 'source nushell/scripts/dev/validate_rust_test_traceability.nu; main'` passes.
2. The governed tests in `rust_core/yazelix_core/src/zellij_render_plan.rs` and `rust_core/yazelix_core/tests/yzx_core_zellij_render_plan.rs` have valid lane + justification + strength metadata.
3. Relevant Rust tests for `yzx_core` still pass after the metadata-only cleanup.
notes: Review context: created from the post-implementation review of `yazelix-3qs8.1` after reproducing the validator failure locally.
close_reason: Added maintainer-lane test metadata to zellij_render_plan unit and integration tests; validate_rust_test_traceability passes.

title: Align Zellij render-plan defaults with the config contract and collapse split ownership
description:
The Zellij render-plan slice still encodes duplicated defaults across the Rust helper, the Nushell bridge request builder, and the Zellij generation fingerprint path. The sharpest example is `support_kitty_keyboard_protocol`: the canonical config contract and shipped default say `false`, but the helper-facing fallback path in the new slice still assumes `true`.

Today the normalized-config path usually masks this, so the problem is more contract drift and architecture debt than a fully reproduced startup bug. But it is exactly the kind of split ownership that `yazelix-3qs8` is supposed to delete before the same pattern gets copied into the Yazi render-plan work.
design:
Start with the Rust dependency gate note as usual (likely still in-house/std). Then make one owner responsible for helper-visible defaults and validation semantics instead of re-encoding them in multiple layers.

Implementation target:
- Audit the helper input defaults currently duplicated in: `rust_core/yazelix_core/src/zellij_render_plan.rs`, `nushell/scripts/utils/zellij_render_plan.nu`, and `nushell/scripts/setup/zellij_generation_state.nu`.
- Align `support_kitty_keyboard_protocol` with the canonical config contract/default surface.
- Remove duplicated helper-facing fallbacks where possible; prefer passing normalized values explicitly from Nushell and/or deriving defaults from the canonical contract seam instead of copy-pasting literals.
- Add focused tests for the omitted-field/default path so future helper slices cannot silently drift from `config_metadata/main_config_contract.toml`.
- Capture any remaining unavoidable split ownership in bead notes or a linked spec instead of leaving it implicit.

Delete-first rules:
- Do not just patch the Kitty default and leave the same pattern unexamined everywhere else.
- Do not widen this into Yazi implementation work; the goal is to fix the seam quality before `yazelix-3qs8.2` proceeds.

Non-goals:
- broad Zellij renderer rewrites
- changing user-facing generated config format unless required by contract alignment
acceptance_criteria:
1. The helper-visible default for `support_kitty_keyboard_protocol` matches `config_metadata/main_config_contract.toml` and `yazelix_default.toml`.
2. The Zellij render-plan path no longer relies on silently duplicated fallback literals across Rust/Nushell without an explicit ownership decision.
3. There is at least one focused regression test that would fail if helper defaults drift from the canonical config contract again.
4. `yazelix-3qs8.2` can build on the cleaned seam instead of copying the same split-default pattern.
notes: Created from the post-implementation review of `yazelix-3qs8.1`. This should land before or alongside the early planning for `yazelix-3qs8.2`.
close_reason: Aligned support_kitty_keyboard_protocol defaults with config contract (Rust serde, Nu bridge, generation fingerprint); regression test for omitted JSON field; layout renderer fails on unknown tray token.

title: Replace RAM status widget scraping with Nushell sys mem
description: The RAM status widget currently shells through macchina, suppresses stderr, and scrapes human-formatted output with grep. That can work on macOS because macchina supports macOS and memory percentages, but it is brittle and less portable than using Nushell's structured sys mem data already available to Yazelix.
design: Add a small configs/zellij/scripts/ram_usage.nu script that computes used / total memory from sys mem and prints a clamped percentage. Point the zjstatus RAM command at the packaged Nu binary and runtime script path, matching the CPU widget pattern. Do not broaden this into CPU changes or macchina removal; macchina still backs the optional welcome system-info surface.
acceptance_criteria: The RAM widget no longer invokes bash, grep, or macchina for percentage output; it uses a Yazelix-owned Nushell script based on sys mem. The script prints a percent value or ??% on structured data failure. The shipped zjstatus template and sweep test layouts reference the new script, and focused smoke checks prove the script returns a percentage on the local host.
close_reason: Completed: RAM status widget now uses a Yazelix-owned Nushell sys mem script instead of scraping macchina through bash/grep; zjstatus templates and sweep layouts reference ram_usage.nu; workspace asset contract tracks the new script. Verified with nu ram_usage smoke test, nu source syntax check, yzx dev rust fmt --check, yzx dev rust check, yzx dev rust test zellij_materialization, yzx_repo_validator validate-workspace-session-contract, and validate-nushell-syntax.

title: Add combined Codex 5h/week usage and quota widget
description: Implement codex_usage as one compact status-bar block that combines local Codex token totals and official quota remaining for the 5-hour active block and weekly windows. The widget must read only cached facts; provider/network work belongs in a shared cross-window background cache, not zjstatus. Use tokenusage structured JSON for Codex only: active block tokens from `tu blocks --active --json --offline --no-claude --no-antigravity`, weekly tokens from `tu weekly --json --offline --no-claude --no-antigravity --order desc`, and official quota from `tu blocks --active --json --official-limits --no-claude --no-antigravity`. Drop Codex day/month period configuration for this surface. `codex_usage_display` selects `token`, `quota`, or `both`.
acceptance_criteria:
- `codex_usage` renders a combined 5h/week Codex block, not day/month rows
- `codex_usage_display` supports exactly `token`, `quota`, and `both`
- zjstatus reads cached Codex facts only and never invokes `tu` directly
- quota/token cache is shared across Yazelix windows with locking, atomic writes, freshness, and backoff
- Codex refresh runs only when `codex_usage` is enabled and `tu` is available
- On success, widget renders compact remaining-oriented text such as `[codex 5h|138M|49% wk|1.34B|80%]`
- On missing/unauthorized/rate-limited official quota data, widget can still render token windows without noisy bar errors
- Focused tests cover parsing active-block tokens, weekly tokens, official_codex quota, cache freshness/backoff, and rendering
notes:
Decision: Codex is no longer part of the generic grouped day/month agent-usage path. The unpushed `codex_usage_periods` surface was removed instead of preserved.
Display contract: `codex_usage_display = "token"` renders tokens only, `"quota"` renders remaining quota percentages only, and `"both"` renders token plus quota windows. Full example: `[codex 5h|138M|49% wk|1.34B|80%]`.
Runtime contract: zjstatus runs only cache reads for `codex_usage`; the pane orchestrator schedules the shared refresh command and the cache uses a lock, atomic writes, freshness, and error backoff so multiple Yazelix windows do not stampede tokenusage.
close_reason: Completed: implemented combined Codex 5h/week token/quota status widget with token|quota|both display modes, shared cache refresh, docs/config surface, tests, and synced pane-orchestrator wasm.

title: Fix config edit live Zellij reload integration test
description: The focused config-edit integration test for live Zellij pane-orchestrator runtime config reload is failing. Diagnose whether the failure is product behavior or stale test fixture shape, fix the real contract, validate the exact test, and commit the fix.
acceptance_criteria: The failing integration test passes without weakening assertions. The fix preserves the live Zellij reload contract and avoids help-output or implementation-trivia tests. Commit the bead and code changes together.
close_reason: Fixed the failing config-edit integration test by aligning it with the maintained apply-mode contract. The test now exercises zellij.screen_saver_idle_seconds, a real live_with_pane_refresh field, instead of zellij.popup_width_percent, which is contract-owned as generated_runtime_refresh. This preserves the pane-orchestrator reload behavior assertion without changing product semantics. Validation: cargo fmt -p yazelix_core --check passed; cargo test -p yazelix_core --test yzx_core_config_edit passed, 2 tests.

title: yzx update <any existing package> for standalone updates
description: Imported GitHub issue #459.
close_reason: Not planned: generic imperative single-package updates do not fit Yazelix's current declarative runtime model. The supported update surfaces remain repo/devenv/nix and maintainer-scoped input updates rather than arbitrary package mutation.

title: Compress Game of Life screen rows to avoid line-gap rendering artifacts
notes:
Rust dependency gate: no dependency changes. Implement in-house in front_door_render.rs by changing the Game of Life render contract to vertically pack two logical rows into one terminal row with half-block glyphs. Rejected alternatives: terminal-specific line-height detection and host-specific config fixes, because those are unreliable and violate the repo's environment-independent behavior goal.
Implementation: changed the Game of Life screen renderer in rust_core/yazelix_core/src/front_door_render.rs to vertically pack two logical rows into one terminal row using half-block glyphs (▀/▄/█), avoiding row-gap artifacts without terminal-specific fallbacks. Verification: nix develop -c cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core front_door_render::tests -- --nocapture, plus a PTY cargo-run probe of yzx screen game_of_life_gliders showing consecutive packed rows instead of visually skipped lines. Status: committed locally for user testing; not pushed.
Follow-up implementation: replaced the lossy 1x2 half-block Game of Life screen packing with 2x2 quadrant packing and moved the logical Game of Life grid to packed dimensions so glider silhouettes survive rendering instead of collapsing into horizontal bars. Verification: focused front_door_render Rust tests passed before commit after the quadrant-render change; final dead-code cleanup is non-behavioral.
close_reason: Completed: yazelix_screen frame output now addresses rows directly without newline insertion, and Game of Life packed/dotted render tests preserve row shape without line-gap artifacts. Verified with the yazelix_screen test suite.

title: Restore canonical editor env for yzpp popups
description: Issue #595 follow-up: after routing managed popups through yzpp, default bottom popup commands such as lazygit can launch without Yazelix's canonical runtime env, so nested editor flows ignore settings.jsonc editor.command. Restore the old popup env contract without making yzpp own Yazelix-specific config. Acceptance: generated yzpp popup specs launch external popup commands through the Yazelix runtime wrapper so EDITOR and VISUAL are canonical; explicit yzx popup command overrides get the same runtime wrapper treatment; yzx-owned commands still route through yzx_cli.sh directly; focused regressions defend lazygit-style nested editor env behavior.
close_reason: Completed: generated yzpp popup specs and explicit yzx popup command overrides now route external tools through yzx_cli.sh run so popup-launched TUIs inherit canonical EDITOR/VISUAL; Yazelix-owned yzx commands still route directly through yzx_cli.sh; regressions cover generated specs, explicit raw requests, and the shared wrapper helper.

title: Resolve yzx edit cursors docs validator mismatch
description: The broad docs experience validator currently fails with: docs/yzx_cli.md is missing current command marker `yzx edit cursors`. This should be resolved by making the public command surface and docs agree. If `yzx edit cursors` / cursor reset commands are intentionally removed, remove the command-surface marker and validator expectation instead of documenting stale commands.
acceptance_criteria: validate-docs-experience passes; the public command surface and docs agree on whether cursor edit/reset commands exist; no stale cursor command aliases remain unless explicitly chosen.
close_reason: Completed: removed stale yzx edit cursors docs validator marker while keeping removed-command regressions for cursor edit/reset surfaces.

title: Delete pane-orchestrator copied-wasm sync guardrails after package migration
description: Once Yazelix consumes pane-orchestrator wasm from a locked child package, remove the transitional copied-wasm source ownership surface from the main repo and retarget validators/docs to the package-consumption contract.
design: Delete-first cleanup for the transitional guardrail. The cleanup should shrink main-repo artifact ownership and validation code rather than add compatibility shims around both old and new provenance models.
acceptance_criteria: configs/zellij/plugins/yazelix_pane_orchestrator.wasm and yazelix_pane_orchestrator.sync_stamp are no longer durable main-repo source artifacts when the runtime can consume the child package artifact directly. validate-pane-orchestrator-sync is deleted or narrowed to only the remaining local-development workflow. Workspace asset validation checks packaged/runtime artifacts rather than copied source binaries. Contracts/docs no longer describe copied-wasm sync stamps as the active normal path, while any local override path remains documented.
notes:
Follow-up from yazelix-n7j5. Do this only after the main runtime package consumes the locked child package successfully.
2026-05-20: Cleanup is staged locally as part of the package-consumption migration: copied pane-orchestrator wasm/sync stamp and repo_plugin_build guardrails are deleted, with validators/docs retargeted to package/runtime artifact checks. Not claimed or closed yet because this bead depends on closing yazelix-hion after the child package commit is pushed and main flake.lock is updated.
2026-05-20: Guardrail-deletion WIP is parked in stash@{0}; do not close until the child package commit is published, the stash is reapplied, and normal runtime build succeeds without overrides.
close_reason: Completed: deleted copied pane-orchestrator wasm and sync stamp from durable main-repo source, removed repo_plugin_build and copied-wasm sync validator/command guardrails, and retargeted docs/contracts/workspace asset validation to package/runtime artifact ownership after locked package consumption was verified.

title: Fix upgrade-note CI guard for cursor rename
close_reason: Added the missing unreleased upgrade-note guard for home_manager/module.nix and replaced the placeholder unreleased summary/changelog with the cursor-repo rename note. Verified cargo fmt --all --check, cargo check -p yazelix_maintainer, and yzx_repo_validator validate-upgrade-contract --ci.

title: Add editable config UI controls
close_reason: Completed editable config UI controls using the JSONC patcher.

title: Implement disableable cursor component for Home Manager and mkYazelix
description: Implement the accepted cursor component toggle from the Home Manager optional-component evaluation. Advanced users should be able to disable full Yazelix Ghostty cursor effects/config generation without breaking the default integrated install or the standalone #yazelix_cursors package output.
design: Start from packaging/runtime_component_registry.nix, home_manager/module.nix, ghostty materialization, cursor config ownership, runtime_components.json, and the yazelix_cursors crate linkage. The hard part is making the package truly omit something: either feature-gate cursor-dependent Rust paths and/or filter Ghostty cursor shader assets from the runtime source. Disabled mode should skip Ghostty cursor shader/config materialization, reject Home Manager cursor config ownership, and publish n/a cursor launch facts rather than silently generating stale shader references. Defaults remain enabled.
acceptance_criteria: Home Manager and lib.mkYazelix expose a typed cursor component setting whose default preserves current behavior. Disabling the component removes real cursor-related package/source/runtime surface, generated terminal configs no longer reference disabled cursor assets, manage_cursor_config is rejected or ignored with clear validation, yzx doctor/status reports the component as intentionally disabled, and focused tests cover enabled/default and disabled behavior.
close_reason: Implemented optional component toggles for cursors/screen and off mode for optional runtime helper tools, with runtime manifests, diagnostics, docs, and focused verification.

title: Step 4: verify the simpler contract end to end
description: For candidate refactors under `yazelix-790`, verify the simplified behavior with focused command, regression, or contract checks before considering the refactor done. The goal is to defend the smaller contract, not just the new implementation shape.
notes:
2026-04-08 verification guidance:
- Prefer one focused regression or contract validator per surviving seam over broad command-smoke checks.
- For the current shortlist, verification should stay close to the user-visible contract: doctor output families, maintainer command routing, migration-plan behavior, generated-config semantics, and runtime/path resolution.

2026-04-08 concrete verification example:

2026-04-08 second verification example:

close_reason: Completed

title: Replace Mandelbrot reset loop with self-similar seam
description: The Mandelbrot screen still does not read as an infinite zoom loop: the current implementation forces the final frame back to frame 0, so byte equality passes while the penultimate deep frame has no visual connection to the restart. Rework the animation around an approximate self-similar Mandelbrot transform and add a seam-continuity test so the last visible frame is structurally close to the first before the exact loop-boundary frame repeats.
notes: Implemented a self-similar Mandelbrot loop using the miniature-copy transform from the researched construction: estimate the period-3 baby Mandelbrot complex size r, center the view at c/(1-r), and animate the complex multiplier r^t instead of easing between unrelated camera points. Chose the period-3 nucleus near -1.754877666246693 because it gives a visibly continuous terminal seam; the previous Seahorse/period-18 target was deeper and prettier mid-zoom but too distorted for the loop boundary at terminal resolution. Added tests for exact cycle-boundary repeat at frame N, penultimate-frame structural similarity without a reset hack, monotonic zoom into the miniature copy, and the copy-center invariant.
close_reason: Implemented

title: Simplify Yazelix to a package-runtime-first distribution model
description: Shift Yazelix away from acting as its own installer/distribution manager and toward a flake-package-first product model. The main supported install and update paths should be the flake package surface and Home Manager, not an installer-owned mutable runtime identity. Yazelix should keep owning config semantics, backend/environment behavior if retained, and workspace UX. This lane should delete installer-owned runtime/current and stable-launcher assumptions where they are no longer justified, make `#yazelix` and Home Manager the primary product surfaces, and leave behind cleaner user and maintainer flows without treating upstream nixpkgs submission as a target.
notes:
2026-04-11 plan-space review of follow-up ownership beads:
- `yazelix-4buc.12` is the shared stable external `yzx` launcher-resolution seam, not a Home Manager-specific restart patch. The surviving contract is that restart must prefer an owner-provided external wrapper over the current session runtime-pinned command path.
- `yazelix-4buc.14` preserves cleanup behavior while refusing `yzx desktop install` in Home Manager mode. Refusal must not remove the ability to clean up a stale manual entry; `yzx desktop uninstall` remains part of the recovery path.
- `yazelix-4buc.15` stays narrow: repair only an already-existing Yazelix-managed manual desktop entry, never recreate desktop integration after `yzx desktop uninstall`, and never materialize a manual desktop entry in Home Manager-managed mode. It depends on both the shared stable-launcher seam from `yazelix-4buc.12` and the Home Manager desktop ownership decision from `yazelix-4buc.14`.
- `yazelix-4buc.13` remains one non-default Home Manager smoke lane. Its purpose is to validate the narrowed owner contract without relying on any user-local compatibility launcher or desktop entry.
- `yazelix-4buc.16` documents the final stale-live-session contract only after `yazelix-4buc.12`, `yazelix-4buc.13`, `yazelix-4buc.14`, and `yazelix-4buc.15` are settled.
close_reason: Completed: the package-runtime-first ownership cleanup is finished across update flows, launcher resolution, Home Manager desktop ownership, installer desktop behavior, and stale-live-session docs.

title: Define the package-runtime-first user and maintainer UX
description: Document the canonical user and maintainer flow for package-runtime-first Yazelix. Be explicit that the normal surfaces should be package-first commands such as `nix run .#yazelix -- ...`, `nix profile install ...#yazelix`, profile/system/Home Manager updates, and packaged-runtime dogfooding. Clarify what happens to `#install`, what remains true about `yazelix.toml` and `yazelix_packs.toml`, and how maintainer development differs from packaged-runtime validation.
close_reason: Documented the package-runtime-first user and maintainer flow and clarified the installer-first phase-1 doc

title: Document the supported upstream and Home Manager update ownership paths
description: Update the README, home_manager/README.md, and relevant command-surface help/docs so the supported update ownership story is explicit. Upstream/manual users should use yzx update upstream. Home Manager users should use yzx update home_manager. The docs should warn users not to mix both update owners for the same installed Yazelix runtime, and should stop implying that Yazelix still owns a generic runtime-update surface.
close_reason: Completed: documented the supported upstream/manual and Home Manager update owner paths in the README, Home Manager docs, CLI reference, and Home Manager command help.

title: Purge stale update and uninstall ownership references after the package-runtime cleanup
description: Audit and delete any remaining docs, tests, help text, menu descriptions, validators, or ownership wording that still assumes yzx uninstall, yzx update runtime, or the older installer-owned runtime identity model. Keep this delete-first: remove stale references where possible instead of layering more compatibility wording on top.
close_reason: Completed: removed stale live references to the deleted generic update/uninstall surface from current-contract specs and low-value command-surface tests, and deleted dead update-guidance data from the runtime distribution helper.

title: Extract stable external yzx launcher resolution and use it for restart across manual and Home Manager installs
description:
yzx restart currently only treats ~/.local/bin/yzx as the stable relaunch surface. In a pure Home Manager install that path normally does not exist, so restart falls back to yzx launch from the currently running session. After home-manager switch, that can relaunch the old runtime instead of the freshly updated Home Manager-owned one.

Extract one install-owner-aware stable external yzx launcher resolver and use it for restart. Manual installs should continue to relaunch through ~/.local/bin/yzx; Home Manager installs should relaunch through the profile-owned yzx wrapper. Keep the fix delete-first: do not recreate manual installer artifacts in Home Manager mode and do not add another compatibility alias surface.
design:
Reason from first principles:
- Restart should relaunch through the install owner's stable external wrapper, not through whatever runtime the current session happened to come from.
- Manual installs and Home Manager installs have different stable launcher surfaces, but they solve the same problem: they point at the currently owned runtime after an update.
- Do not special-case Home Manager by recreating ~/.local/bin/yzx; that would violate the current ownership contract.
- Prefer one shared stable-launcher resolution seam that can later be reused by desktop/install/restart flows instead of duplicating install-owner heuristics in each command.
- The surviving contract should be simple: resolve a stable external yzx wrapper when one exists, use it for restart, and fall back to in-session yzx launch only when there truly is no owner-provided stable wrapper.
acceptance_criteria: yzx restart relaunches through a stable owner-provided external wrapper for both manual installs and pure Home Manager installs. Restart must no longer trust the current session's runtime-pinned yzx command path when a stable owner wrapper exists. After a Home Manager update/switch, invoking restart from an older live session opens the newer runtime instead of relaunching the stale one. The fix must not recreate ~/.local/bin/yzx in Home Manager mode, and regression coverage must defend both the manual-install and Home Manager restart paths.
notes: Completed: extracted shared launcher_resolution.nu, made yzx restart prefer the stable owner wrapper for manual and Home Manager installs, reused the seam for desktop launch resolution, and covered Home Manager profile preference in default tests.
close_reason: Completed: restart and desktop launcher resolution now use the stable owner wrapper, with Home Manager profile ownership covered by default and maintainer tests.

title: Add a real Home Manager activation and update smoke lane for launch and restart ownership flows
description:
Home Manager is a first-class supported install owner, but the current regression surface still leans heavily on fixture tests and manual validation. Add one real non-default smoke lane that covers the actual owner-managed update path: Home Manager activation, profile-owned `yzx`, profile desktop entry, update/switch to a newer runtime, fresh launch, and `yzx restart` from an older live session.

Keep this lane high-signal and end-to-end. The point is to catch install-owner transition bugs that narrow fixtures can miss, not to build a second giant matrix.
design:
Reason from first principles:
- Home Manager is not a compatibility edge path; it is one of the two real install owners.
- Narrow fixture tests are still useful, but they do not fully defend owner transitions where profile wrappers, desktop entries, and already-open sessions interact.
- This should be one focused smoke lane, not a broad matrix explosion. Cover the exact surfaces users rely on after `home-manager switch`: profile `yzx`, profile desktop entry, fresh launch, and restart from an older live session.
- Keep it out of the default lane. The goal is to defend a high-value integration seam without turning common maintainer feedback loops into noise.
acceptance_criteria: There is a non-default verification lane that exercises a real Home Manager-owned update path. The lane proves that after a Home Manager activation/update, fresh launch uses the newer runtime, and `yzx restart` from an older live session also transitions onto the newer runtime. The lane also verifies the profile `yzx` path and Home Manager desktop-entry ownership without requiring `~/.local/bin/yzx` or user-local desktop integration, and it proves the flow does not silently depend on a recreated user-local compatibility launcher or desktop entry.
notes: Completed: maintainer lane now covers Home Manager input refresh + home-manager switch command construction and a profile-owned restart smoke test that proves yzx restart uses the Home Manager profile yzx without recreating manual ~/.local/bin/yzx or user-local desktop entries.
close_reason: Completed: non-default maintainer smoke coverage now defends Home Manager activation plus profile-owned restart/desktop ownership without user-local compatibility surfaces.

title: Refuse yzx desktop install in Home Manager-managed installs while preserving manual desktop cleanup paths
description:
Home Manager already owns the profile desktop entry, but `yzx desktop install` can still materialize a user-local launcher that shadows the Home Manager-owned one. That keeps desktop ownership ambiguous and makes it too easy to replace the correct owner with a stale manual surface.

Harden the command surface so Home Manager-managed installs do not quietly create or refresh a user-local Yazelix desktop entry. The surviving contract should point users back to the Home Manager owner and reserve manual desktop install for manual/upstream installs only.
design:
Reason from first principles:
- Desktop integration should have one clear owner. In Home Manager mode that owner is the Home Manager profile, not a user-local compatibility command.
- A warning is too soft here because it still permits writing the wrong owner surface and creating a shadowing problem that users then have to debug later.
- Do not solve this by trying to merge or auto-repair both surfaces at once. The right delete-first move is to narrow the command contract: manual desktop install is for manual/upstream installs; Home Manager owns desktop integration in Home Manager mode.
- Preserve the cleanup path explicitly. Refusing install must not trap users with stale manual entries they can no longer remove.
acceptance_criteria: When Yazelix detects a Home Manager-managed install, yzx desktop install refuses to create or refresh a user-local desktop entry and prints clear owner-aware guidance instead. The guidance points users at the Home Manager-owned desktop surface and the cleanup path for stale manual entries. Home Manager mode must still preserve manual desktop cleanup, including yzx desktop uninstall for removing a stale user-local entry that shadows the Home Manager launcher. Regression coverage defends that Home Manager mode no longer materializes a shadowing manual desktop entry and still permits the cleanup flow.
notes: Completed: yzx desktop install now refuses Home Manager-owned installs while yzx desktop uninstall remains the stale manual-entry cleanup path; default tests cover both.
close_reason: Completed: Home Manager desktop install refusal and manual cleanup preservation are implemented and covered.

title: Keep desktop integration explicit by repairing only existing managed manual entries during compatibility install
description:
The compatibility installer currently has a tempting path toward quietly re-owning desktop integration on every run. That muddies the surviving contract, because user-local desktop integration was supposed to stay explicit through `yzx desktop install`, not become a hidden installer side effect again.

Narrow the installer behavior so it only repairs or refreshes an already-existing Yazelix-managed user-local desktop entry when that exact surface exists. If the user never opted into manual desktop integration, the installer should leave desktop integration alone.
design:
Reason from first principles:
- The compatibility installer should not quietly take ownership of optional desktop integration for every user.
- Some users want no desktop entry at all, and `yzx desktop uninstall` should remain a respected choice instead of being reversed by the next installer run.
- The delete-first compromise is narrow: if a Yazelix-managed user-local desktop entry already exists, the installer may repair that exact managed surface; otherwise it should not create one.
- The repair path should ideally reuse the shared stable-launcher resolution seam from `yazelix-4buc.12` instead of inventing a second owner-lookup path.
- Home Manager-managed installs are out of scope for manual-entry repair; the installer should not recreate a user-local desktop entry there.
acceptance_criteria: The compatibility installer no longer creates a user-local desktop entry as a default side effect. If a Yazelix-managed manual desktop entry already exists for a manual/upstream install, the installer may repair or refresh that exact surface to the current expected launcher path. If manual desktop integration is absent, or if the user previously removed it with `yzx desktop uninstall`, it stays absent until the user explicitly runs `yzx desktop install`. Home Manager-managed installs do not get a recreated user-local desktop entry from the installer. Regression coverage defends all three cases.
notes: Completed: compatibility install now refreshes only an existing Yazelix-managed user-local desktop entry and otherwise leaves desktop integration absent; flake install smoke validates the default no-desktop-entry contract.
close_reason: Completed: installer-owned desktop integration is now explicit and repair-only, with flake install smoke coverage.

title: Document the stale-live-session contract after runtime updates and owner-managed relaunches
description:
Users can update the on-disk Yazelix runtime correctly and still keep an older live Ghostty/Zellij session open. That is a reasonable live-session contract, but it is easy to misread as a broken update when the product does not say it plainly.

Add one concise owner-aware note explaining that already-open Yazelix windows continue running the old runtime until they are explicitly relaunched or restarted. Keep the wording tight and factual in the relevant update and troubleshooting surfaces.
design:
Reason from first principles:
- Updating the runtime on disk and transitioning already-open live sessions are different operations.
- That distinction is normal, but users will read it as a failed update unless the product says so plainly.
- Keep this narrow and factual. Do not add noisy UI; just document the contract in the places where users already think about updates and recovery.
- The wording should stay owner-aware: fresh launch paths use the owner-provided wrapper or desktop surface, while already-open sessions stay on their current live runtime until explicit relaunch or restart.
acceptance_criteria: The relevant update and troubleshooting docs state clearly that already-open Yazelix windows keep running their current live runtime until they are relaunched or restarted. The wording matches the surviving manual-install and Home Manager owner contracts, including the owner-resolved restart behavior from yazelix-4buc.12 and the Home Manager smoke lane from yazelix-4buc.13. The docs must not promise invisible hot-swapping of already-open sessions.
notes: Completed: README, installation docs, troubleshooting docs, yzx CLI docs, and Home Manager README now state that already-open windows keep the current live runtime until relaunch or yzx restart.
close_reason: Completed: stale-live-session update ownership contract is documented across the relevant user-facing surfaces.

title: Make the flake package surface primary and demote the installer app
description: Promote the flake package and Home Manager surfaces as the canonical product entrypoints. Ground this in the real code: today `packages.default` still points at the bare runtime package, `apps` only exposes `install`, and the README/install docs still center `#install`. Normal usage should instead center the package output such as `#yazelix`, profile or Home Manager installation, and package-manager updates. `#install` should become legacy/bootstrap-only or disappear. Update docs, flake outputs, smoke checks, and entrypoint assumptions so the product no longer centers a mutable installer-managed runtime by default.
close_reason: Completed

title: Remove runtime_current and stable-launcher ownership from shared runtime logic
description: Remove `runtime/current` and stable-launcher ownership from the shared runtime model after the package-first and Home Manager runtime-identity work is in place. Ground this in the real code paths: `common.nu` still has installed-runtime and runtime-reference helpers, `install_ownership.nu` still treats manual runtime/current and `~/.local/bin/yzx` as central ownership surfaces, and multiple commands still rely on those helpers. Simplify the common runtime identity story so package runtime roots and explicit `YAZELIX_RUNTIME_DIR` become the normal path instead of installed-runtime indirection.
notes: This bead should focus on common helpers and CLI/runtime callers after the Home Manager-specific runtime_current cleanup is settled. Callers that currently hard-code installed-runtime assumptions include `common.nu`, `install_ownership.nu`, `desktop.nu`, and adjacent doctor/update helpers.
close_reason: Completed

title: Delete yzx update runtime and collapse installer-artifact doctoring
description: Delete `yzx update runtime` and collapse installer-artifact doctoring after the runtime identity model no longer depends on installed-runtime ownership. Ground this in the current code cluster: `core/yazelix.nu`, `runtime_distribution_capabilities.nu`, `doctor.nu`, and `doctor_install_artifacts.nu` still spend real surface area on installer-managed, Home Manager-managed, package-runtime, and runtime-root-only branching. Replace that with a thinner story centered on package-manager updates, Home Manager reapply flows, and command checks that defend real runtime/backend prerequisites instead of installer artifact ownership.
close_reason: Completed

title: Re-scope uninstall and desktop integration after runtime_current identity removal
description: Re-scope `yzx uninstall`, manual desktop-install logic, and related ownership assumptions after installer-owned and Home Manager-owned runtime-current identity is removed from the main product model. Keep only the user-local integration surfaces that still make sense for a flake-package-first and Home Manager-first product. Ground this in the real command code: `desktop.nu`, `uninstall.nu`, and `home_manager.nu` currently preserve or clean up paths whose meaning depends on the older installer-managed runtime model.
close_reason: Completed

title: Classify Yazelix config surfaces by backend and devenv dependence
description: Audit `yazelix.toml` and `yazelix_packs.toml` by ownership and runtime dependence. Classify which settings remain meaningful in a package-runtime-only model, which require backend materialization or `devenv`, which are rebuild-relevant, and which would need to narrow, move, or disappear in a backend-free workspace-only slice. The first output should be a clear matrix and guidance for future simplification work, not an immediate config-file split unless the audit proves that split is actually justified.
notes: This is still useful, but it is no longer the immediate next move now that upstream nixpkgs submission is off the table. Do it after the flake-package-first surface and installer demotion work is concrete enough that the config audit is answering the right product boundary.
close_reason: Completed

title: Stop Home Manager from materializing runtime_current as product identity
description: Rework the Home Manager path so it no longer treats `~/.local/share/yazelix/runtime/current` as the primary runtime identity. The current module still creates `xdg.dataFile."yazelix/runtime/current"`, exports that path during generated-config activation, and documents it as a first-class managed surface. Move Home Manager toward using the profile/package runtime directly where possible, keep generated config/state semantics honest, and remove the extra runtime-current indirection from the Home Manager-first product model.
close_reason: Completed

title: Add honest update wrappers for upstream installs and Home Manager

The surface should stay symmetric, explicit, and avoid reviving installer-owned runtime identity semantics.

title: Yazi boundary pass phase 2
description: Continue thinning the private Yazi writer/adapter boundary. Keep public extraction deferred until yazi_materialization/writer.rs is generic and Yazelix-specific opener/sidebar/runtime policy is isolated.
design: Private boundary only. Keep Yazelix paths, action ids, editor/sidebar orchestration, legacy override diagnostics, and runtime placeholder ownership local. Move or delete only generic writer behavior that does not need Yazelix policy. Prefer deletion of duplicated generated-file or opener handling over new abstraction.
acceptance_criteria: The Yazi writer/adapter boundary is thinner; generic writer behavior is isolated from Yazelix-specific policy where safe; duplicate or stale materialization logic is deleted or explicitly rejected with evidence; focused Yazi materialization/render-plan tests pass; Rust budget and inventory are updated; LOC delta and rejected candidates are recorded.
close_reason: Completed: collapsed repeated generated Yazi write/status construction into one private writer helper. yazi_materialization/writer.rs dropped 948 -> 939 raw lines; total Rust budget dropped by 9 raw lines. Rejected moving opener preservation, runtime placeholders, and legacy override diagnostics because they remain Yazelix-specific policy rather than generic writer behavior.

title: Render magician GIF through Kitty graphics
description: Fix the magician welcome style quality regression where the attributed 1mposter GIF was downsampled into a 96x96 ANSI block renderer. Use the GIF-derived bitmap frames directly through the existing Kitty graphics passthrough path so the original ASCII-art image remains crisp in supported Yazelix terminals.\n\nDependency decision: no new production crates and no new dev-only crates. Runtime uses pre-generated PNG frames committed as assets plus a small in-house base64 encoder for Kitty file-path payloads. Packaging impact: move the source GIF to runtime assets, add sampled PNG frame assets, remove the embedded RGB pack from the Rust binary.
close_reason: Completed: replaced the lossy embedded 96x96 RGB magician renderer with runtime GIF-derived PNG frame assets rendered through Kitty graphics. The source GIF now lives in runtime assets, the Rust binary addresses sampled PNG frames by path, unsupported graphics/missing assets fail clearly, and docs/contracts describe the runtime graphics boundary. Verification: cargo fmt --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml --all -- --check; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core front_door_render::tests; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core front_door_commands::tests; cargo check --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core; short Kitty-output smoke for internal welcome emitted frame placements and cleanup; git diff --check. Validator commands yzx_repo_validator validate-contracts and validate-rust-test-traceability were unavailable on PATH and through yzx run in this shell.

title: Remove the visible Ctrl+y helper pane while preserving sidebar refresh correctness
description: `Ctrl y` still routes through a transient `Run "nu" ... close_on_exit true` helper pane so Yazelix can first ask the pane orchestrator to toggle focus and then, when the target becomes the sidebar, call `refresh_active_sidebar_yazi`. The recent focus-context fix restored correctness, but the visible helper-pane flash and remaining latency are still user-visible. Track the next-stage cleanup here: remove the visible helper pane from the `Ctrl y` path without regressing sidebar freshness or editor/sidebar focus behavior.
design: Keep this delete-first. First verify whether `Ctrl y` still needs an explicit post-focus Yazi refresh at all, given the current Yazi-side event hooks and the event-driven freshness work already landed. If the explicit refresh has become redundant, delete the wrapper flow entirely and make `Ctrl y` a pure pane-orchestrator `MessagePlugin` action. If explicit refresh is still required, delete only the visible helper pane: keep the focus toggle inside the pane orchestrator and move the refresh trigger onto a silent path such as a plugin-owned background command or equivalent worker-mediated bridge, rather than a foreground `Run "nu"` pane. Do not add polling, more sleeps, or another visible helper pane. Also do not treat the current sidebar-state cache as a stronger correctness dependency just to make `Ctrl y` faster; if the chosen fix still leans on current Yazi identity seams, record that explicitly as a temporary boundary and keep the longer-term cleanup aligned with `yazelix-0hm3`.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Pressing `Ctrl y` no longer creates a visible transient helper pane or equivalent UI flash. 2. `Ctrl y` still toggles focus correctly between the managed editor and managed sidebar, including the case where the sidebar must be opened first. 3. Sidebar freshness on `Ctrl y` focus into Yazi remains correct for the maintained Git/starship surfaces, or the work records concrete evidence that an extra explicit refresh became unnecessary and was safely deleted. 4. The chosen implementation does not add polling or broaden the sidebar cache's correctness role. 5. Focus and refresh behavior is defended by a real regression or contract test rather than a help-text or command-list assertion.
notes:
Evidence gathered for the plan:
- Current local path is `Ctrl y` in `configs/zellij/yazelix_overrides.kdl` -> `configs/zellij/scripts/toggle_editor_sidebar_focus.nu` -> `nushell/scripts/zellij_wrappers/toggle_editor_sidebar_focus.nu` -> pane-orchestrator `toggle_editor_sidebar_focus` plus `refresh_active_sidebar_yazi` when the target becomes the sidebar.
- The pane orchestrator already owns the focus decision cleanly in `rust_plugins/zellij_pane_orchestrator/src/panes.rs`; the visible glitch comes from the extra foreground wrapper pane, not from the focus policy itself.
- Zellij's plugin API supports silent background host commands via `run_command` / `run_command_with_env_variables_and_cwd`, but that requires the `RunCommand` permission. This makes a no-flash background bridge plausible without a visible `Run "nu"` pane.
- Yazi's documented plugin pub/sub surface exposes named DDS kinds such as `cd` and `tab` through `ps.sub()`. The current `sidebar-state.yazi` plugin subscribes only to `cd` and `tab`, and local Yazi source did not show a built-in DDS `focus` kind alongside those message kinds. So do not assume there is a clean Yazi focus-gain hook that can simply replace the explicit refresh.
- Because `refresh_active_sidebar_yazi` still depends on current Yazi identity lookup and `ya emit-to`, a full cleanup may overlap with `yazelix-0hm3`, but this bead should first try to delete only the visible helper pane and keep the wider ownership refactor out of scope unless it proves necessary.
close_reason: Completed: Ctrl+y now binds directly to the pane orchestrator, the transient helper-pane path is deleted, and the popup-close refresh remains the only extra Yazi refresh path.

title: Add cargo-llvm-cov and cargo-mutants to maintainer shell
description: Add cargo-llvm-cov and cargo-mutants to the Yazelix maintainer shell. cargo-llvm-cov should provide the LCOV input that makes cargo-crap meaningful; cargo-mutants should be available for manual or sweep mutation-testing audits. Both tools must stay out of user runtime packages.
design: Use nixpkgs packages directly because both tools are available as `pkgs.cargo-llvm-cov` and `pkgs.cargo-mutants`; do not add custom package definitions. Wire them into `maintainer_shell.nix` only. Document `cargo-llvm-cov` as the coverage producer for `cargo-crap --lcov`, and document `cargo-mutants` as manual/sweep-only because it is intentionally heavier than default checks.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The maintainer shell exposes `cargo llvm-cov` and `cargo mutants`. 2. Neither tool is referenced by runtime package wiring. 3. Documentation says `cargo-mutants` is manual/sweep-only, not a default package-time or user-runtime gate. 4. A focused dev shell build or command smoke test verifies both tools are available.
close_reason: Completed: added pkgs.cargo-llvm-cov and pkgs.cargo-mutants to the maintainer shell only, documented cargo-llvm-cov as the LCOV producer for cargo-crap and cargo-mutants as manual/sweep-only, built the default dev shell, and smoke-tested cargo llvm-cov --version plus cargo mutants --version.

title: Make yzx_core the single owner of managed layout and materialization metadata
description: The first Rust materialization slice moved runtime-materialization planning/apply into yzx_core, but Nushell still re-derives parts of managed layout/materialization metadata across startup, generated-state, and shared runtime checks. This bead makes yzx_core the single decision owner for that metadata so Nushell becomes a thin consumer and renderer-only layer.
design:
Scope boundaries:
- Keep all text/template renderers in Nushell (Yazi, Zellij, terminal, Helix, shell initializers)
- Keep public profile labels and doctor/launch user-facing messaging in Nushell
- Move only structured decision metadata to Rust and consume it from one canonical helper output

Implementation instructions:
1) Inventory and remove duplicated Nushell decision seams:
   - expected layout resolution in runtime/startup checks
   - expected artifact/managed-artifact metadata derivation outside Rust helper output
   - any repeated materialization decision branches that duplicate runtime-materialization.plan data
2) Extend yzx_core output shape only as needed for shared metadata consumption (do not add a second command family unless required)
3) Collapse Nushell callers to consume Rust metadata:
   - generated_runtime_state.nu
   - runtime_contract_checker.nu (where materialization/layout metadata is currently re-derived)
   - startup glue that still computes expected layout/artifact metadata locally
4) Keep renderer invocation in Nushell unchanged; this bead is about decision ownership, not renderer migration

Delete-first enforcement:
- Reject additive Rust + unchanged Nu duplication
- If any Nu decision branch must survive, document exactly why and open explicit follow-up beads
acceptance_criteria:
1) Startup and generated-state paths consume one Rust-owned source for managed layout/materialization metadata
2) Nushell no longer independently computes the same expected-layout or expected-artifact decisions in parallel
3) Renderer/template ownership remains in Nushell and unchanged by this bead
4) Existing startup profile labels remain comparable before/after
5) Verification commands in the bead notes pass
notes:
Concrete targets and constraints:
- Primary files to inspect first:
  - nushell/scripts/utils/generated_runtime_state.nu
  - nushell/scripts/utils/runtime_contract_checker.nu
  - nushell/scripts/core/start_yazelix_inner.nu
  - rust_core/yazelix_core/src/runtime_materialization.rs
- Preserve existing startup profile/report step names
- Do not broaden into launch/process orchestration or renderer ownership changes
- Do not introduce fallback behavior that masks metadata/ownership drift

Verification checklist (minimum):
- Rust helper tests: nix develop -c cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml
- Generated-config/materialization regressions: nix develop -c nu -c 'source nushell/scripts/dev/test_yzx_generated_configs.nu; run_generated_config_canonical_tests'
- Workspace/startup regression subset touching layout/materialization expectations: nix develop -c nu -c 'source nushell/scripts/dev/test_yzx_workspace_commands.nu; run_workspace_canonical_tests'
- Spec consistency: nix develop -c nu -c 'source nushell/scripts/dev/validate_specs.nu; main'
- git diff --check
close_reason: Startup and doctor now take managed Zellij layout path from runtime-materialization.plan (yzx_core); removed parallel Nushell resolve_expected_layout_path. Exported compute_runtime_materialization_plan for shared consumption. Verification: cargo test, run_generated_config_canonical_tests, run_workspace_canonical_tests, validate_specs, doctor subset.

title: make the zellij popup faster
close_reason: Completed already: user confirmed this work was already done and it should be removed from the ready queue.

title: Bump the Nushell version Yazelix uses
description: Upgrade the Nushell version Yazelix pins and ships, then update the affected install/runtime/bootstrap surfaces and verification so the newer Nushell version is consistently used across flake installs, runtime-owned tools, and maintainer flows.
design: Treat this as a managed-runtime version bump, not just a single pin edit. Update the authoritative Nushell source Yazelix ships and boots through, then verify the newer version is used consistently across the flake/runtime package, bootstrap wrappers, managed shell entrypoints, doctor/version reporting, and maintainer flows. The change should also make any real bootstrap floor or compatibility caveats explicit instead of leaving them implicit in scattered scripts.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The pinned/shipped Nushell version is intentionally bumped in the authoritative runtime source. 2. Flake installs, runtime-owned wrappers, and managed shell entrypoints all resolve to the new Nushell version coherently. 3. Doctor/version reporting and maintainer update flows remain accurate after the bump. 4. Any new bootstrap caveat or minimum-version expectation is documented explicitly instead of surfacing as a surprise failure.
notes:
2026-04-13 update: tried the Nushell bump path while refreshing runtime pins. Upstream Nushell latest is 0.112.1, published 2026-04-11 at https://github.com/nushell/nushell/releases/tag/0.112.1, but the locked nixos-unstable input now resolves pkgs.nushell.version to 0.111.0. Checked nixpkgs-unstable and nixpkgs master as well; they also still expose 0.111.0.

Implemented the prerequisite pin workflow instead: PINNED_NUSHELL_VERSION is now synced from the locked nixpkgs input alongside PINNED_NIX_VERSION, docs/specs describe that Nu is owned by Nixpkgs, and tests cover the maintainer sync behavior. Leave this bump deferred until Nixpkgs packages 0.112.x or Yazelix deliberately chooses a separate Nushell packaging owner. Do not add a bespoke overlay just to chase upstream Nu without an explicit product decision.
close_reason: Handled by locked-nixpkgs runtime pin workflow; reopen only when nixpkgs ships a newer Nushell that requires repo changes.

title: Clean Bead contract workflow action warnings
description: The Bead contract workflow is green but still emits hosted annotations from Node 20 actions and setup-go's implicit cache lookup without go.sum. Upgrade the GitHub actions used there and disable setup-go's built-in cache because the workflow owns a dedicated bd/Go cache step.
acceptance_criteria: Validate Bead Issue Contract uses checkout and setup-go versions that support Node 24; workflow opts into Node 24 consistently; setup-go cache is disabled so it does not warn about missing go.sum; final hosted workflow run is green without those annotations.
close_reason: Completed: upgraded Validate Bead Issue Contract checkout/setup-go actions to v6, opted the job into Node 24, and disabled setup-go's implicit cache because the workflow owns a dedicated bd tooling cache. Verified workflow YAML parses with python3/PyYAML.

title: Evaluate yzx screen VFX quality pass against Frankentui Mandelbrot
description: Evaluate a yzx screen / yazelix-screen visual-effects quality pass using Frankentui's VFX Mandelbrot demo as a quality reference, without copying code or adopting Frankentui.
design: The concrete reference is Frankentui's web VFX Mandelbrot demo. Frankentui's source also has ftui-demo-showcase visual_effects.rs and golden checksums for vfx_mandelbrot, but Yazelix must treat that as inspiration only because of the license rider and because Yazelix renders terminal-native frames inside Zellij. Evaluate from first principles: what can yzx screen improve while staying terminal-safe, deterministic, performant, and pleasant under Ghostty/WezTerm + Zellij? Compare possible improvements such as a dedicated Mandelbrot/fractal style, smoother zoom target selection, better smooth coloring mapped to ANSI/half-block glyphs, palette quality, dithering, motion pacing, resize behavior, and random/welcome inclusion policy.
acceptance_criteria: The decision states whether to add a Mandelbrot/fractal VFX style, improve existing yzx screen rendering primitives first, or reject the comparison as out of scope. It records the terminal constraints that prevent matching WebGPU/Web/WASM fidelity, chooses the smallest terminal-native quality target, and creates implementation beads only for accepted slices. Any accepted implementation must live in yazelix-screen first, avoid copied Frankentui code, include deterministic frame tests/render smoke checks, and measure terminal performance before entering random or welcome defaults.
notes:
Decision, 2026-05-22: accept a real Mandelbrot VFX quality pass, but scope it as a deep-zoom camera and terminal-rendering fix in yazelix-screen, not as adoption or copying of Frankentui.

The current yazelix-screen Mandelbrot implementation already has math, tests, and a packaged style, but user testing shows the visible result mostly spins/orbits and barely appears to zoom. That fails the intended VFX bar. Frankentui's VFX Mandelbrot screenshot is the right quality reference for feel: a large boundary at terminal scale, high contrast, dense colorful edge detail, and clear inward depth. Yazelix should match that intent within character-cell terminal constraints, not match Frankentui's web/WASM fidelity.

Terminal constraints recorded here:
- Yazelix renders inside terminals and Zellij, so the accepted target is character-cell/ANSI-native output, not WebGPU/canvas fidelity
- The implementation must live in the yazelix-screen child repo first
- Do not copy Frankentui code; use the screenshot/demo only as visual inspiration
- Do not put the style into random or welcome defaults until frame cost is measured on representative terminal sizes

Evidence gathered:
- /home/lucca/pjs/yazelix-screen/src/mandelbrot.rs already contains MandelbrotAnimation, Seahorse Valley camera constants, escape/depth/distance-estimate logic, palette mapping, and deterministic tests
- cargo test mandelbrot passed in the child repo
- render_once and play_style mandelbrot smoke runs produced terminal frames successfully
- The user's visual review remains authoritative: successful rendering is not enough because the motion does not yet feel like an awesome zoom

Accepted follow-up beads:
- yazelix-5pql8: replace the current orbit/spinner feel with a real deep-zoom Mandelbrot camera and terminal visual polish
- yazelix-yw1nn: add a lightweight visual evidence/performance harness so VFX quality changes can be reviewed and measured
close_reason: Completed: accepted a terminal-native Mandelbrot deep-zoom quality pass, rejected copying/adopting Frankentui, and created follow-up beads yazelix-5pql8 and yazelix-yw1nn.

title: Harden the flake install surface
description:
Problem:
The top-level flake install surface exists, but it is still easy to regress because the repo does not yet defend the full install path, some docs remain contradictory, and desktop-entry behavior is not yet an explicit flake-install responsibility.

Design:
Track the remaining flake-specific hardening work as a small focused subtree: smoke validation, docs reconciliation, and explicit desktop-entry installation policy.

Acceptance Criteria:
- The flake install surface has maintained regression coverage.
- User-facing docs describe the flake install path consistently.
- Desktop-entry installation/responsibility is explicitly decided and tracked.
close_reason: Completed initial flake hardening follow-up subtree

title: Add a maintained flake install smoke check
description:
Problem:
The repo can ship flake install regressions without detection. Recent failures affected `nix run github:luccahuguet/yazelix#install` directly.

Design:
Add a focused smoke check that validates the flake install surface end-to-end enough to catch installer/runtime regressions without turning it into a heavy full-session integration suite.

Acceptance Criteria:
- There is a maintained smoke path for the flake install surface.
- The smoke check exercises the installer and verifies the installed `yzx` entrypoint at minimum.
- The check is wired into the appropriate maintainer/CI lane.
close_reason: Implemented flake install smoke validator and wired it into CI

title: Reconcile docs around the current flake install story
description:
Problem:
Some docs still describe flake as removed or otherwise contradict the new `nix run ...#install` front door.

Design:
Audit and fix the remaining contradictory docs so the current install story is internally consistent.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Primary docs no longer contradict the existence of the flake install surface.
- User-facing installation guidance is internally consistent.
close_reason: Reconciled README and troubleshooting docs with the current flake install story

title: Decide desktop entry installation responsibility for the flake installer
description:
Problem:
The flake installer materializes runtime state, but desktop-entry installation is not yet an explicit part of the contract.

Design:
Decide whether the installer should install/update desktop-entry assets directly or whether that remains a separate setup responsibility.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Desktop-entry responsibility is explicitly decided.
- The decision is reflected in docs or implementation follow-up scope.
close_reason: Decided desktop-entry installation stays explicit via yzx desktop install and updated specs/docs accordingly

title: Make Yazelix spec driven
description: Imported GitHub issue #451. Define a spec-driven workflow for new Yazelix features so user-visible behavior has an explicit contract before and during implementation.
close_reason: Completed: the spec-driven workflow epic work is done; the remaining Rust rewrite planning work now continues as standalone bead yazelix-kt5 with matching GitHub issue #461.

title: Pilot the spec-driven workflow on one real Yazelix feature
description: Pick one concrete user-visible feature or bugfix and run it through the proposed spec-driven flow end-to-end: bead, spec, implementation, and regression checks. The goal is to prove the workflow is small enough to use and strong enough to prevent drift before expanding it further.
close_reason: Piloted the spec-driven workflow on shell-opened editors with a concrete spec, user-facing docs, an implementation-side diagnostic clarification, and regression coverage.

title: Define the Yazelix spec template and repository conventions
description: Create the minimal spec format for user-visible Yazelix work. Define where specs live, which changes need one, what sections are required, and how acceptance criteria should be written so specs are precise enough to guide implementation without turning into bureaucracy.
close_reason: Defined the spec-driven workflow, spec location, required sections, acceptance-case rules, and reusable template under docs/spec_driven_workflow.md and docs/specs/template.md

title: Add traceability between specs, beads, and regression tests
description: Make spec-driven development enforceable instead of aspirational. Define a lightweight traceability path from a bead to its spec and from the spec acceptance cases to the tests or checks that defend them. Prefer a small validator or convention check over a heavyweight process engine.
close_reason: Added lightweight traceability between specs, beads, and regression checks through a Traceability section convention plus validate_specs.nu.

title: Tie the default test suite more explicitly to specs and contracts
description: Strengthen the connection between Yazelix's default automated regression suite and the specs/contracts it is supposed to defend. The goal is not heavy process or one-spec-per-test bureaucracy. The goal is to make the small default suite legible: maintainers should be able to see which supported behavior or contract each default-suite entrypoint exists to defend.
design: Keep this lightweight and default-suite-focused. Treat the public default suite as the strongest candidate for explicit spec/contract traceability. At minimum, map the default suite entrypoints to documented specs or explicitly justified regression-only contracts, add a small validator for that relationship, and avoid expanding the requirement to every helper test or validator in the repo. Prefer file-level traceability over per-function bureaucracy. Cheap validators and sweep/manual lanes can keep lighter ownership rules.
acceptance_criteria: The default automated suite entrypoints are explicitly tied to specs, documented contracts, or a small justified regression-only allowlist. Add a lightweight validator that checks the default-suite traceability relationship. Update the relevant specs and/or nearby comments so a maintainer can tell what the default suite is defending without reverse-engineering the test files. The solution should stay lightweight: no requirement for one spec per test function and no heavy metadata system for the whole repo.
close_reason: Completed: added a lightweight validator tying the default test suite to specs or a tiny justified regression-only allowlist, updated specs/comments to make default-suite ownership legible, and verified the default suite plus validators.

title: Remove noisy config UI explicit column
description: Config UI rows now show every main setting as explicit after complete settings.jsonc. Remove the noisy explicit state column from normal rows and add clear column headers for remaining row columns.
acceptance_criteria: Config UI settings list no longer renders the normal explicit/defaulted value-state column as row noise. Remaining visible columns have concise headers. Exceptional diagnostics or invalid states remain visible where useful. Focused UI tests cover the new header/row shape.
close_reason: Removed noisy config UI state column, added settings/advanced column headers, and covered renderer shape with focused Rust tests.

title: Add disposable Yazelix canary session validator
description: Create a maintainer/manual command that launches a disposable Zellij session from the packaged runtime and verifies the live workspace contracts.
design: The command should use an isolated session name and log evidence for pane-orchestrator load, left Yazi sidebar presence, managed editor targeting, top/bottom popup toggles, and generated keybinding surface. It should clean up or clearly name any leftover session.
acceptance_criteria: The canary command fails with actionable diagnostics when the packaged workspace contract is broken, logs enough evidence for CI/manual triage, and avoids corrupting the user's active Yazelix session.
close_reason: Completed: added yzx_repo_maintainer canary-session with isolated HOME, named Zellij session, dry-run plan, packaged-runtime launch, doctor/generated-asset evidence, and generated workspace contract marker checks. Verified with repo_canary_session::tests and canary-session --dry-run.

title: Move README CLI docs link to section end
