title: Codify artifact-first child integration architecture
description: Document the final source/runtime boundary: users consume locked artifacts, maintainers and CI build source artifacts, wasm is used only where the runtime ABI is wasm, and native control-plane code stays native.
acceptance_criteria: A contract records the artifact-first policy, names accepted boundaries for Zellij plugin wasm, native yzx helpers, screen/cursor libraries, and zellij-bar integration, and rejects wasm-as-general-runtime as a non-goal.
close_reason: Completed: added artifact_first_child_integration contract and inventory entry documenting artifact-first child repository boundaries, native control-plane policy, Zellij plugin wasm scope, and zellij-bar adapter direction. Verified with yzx_repo_validator validate-contracts.

title: Remove zellij-bar Rust library from yazelix_core build graph
description: Stop compiling the yazelix_zellij_bar child crate inside the native yzx_core helper without copying the child renderer into the main repo. Keep the child package as the runtime artifact provider for zjstatus.wasm and yazelix_zellij_bar_widget. Add a small child-owned integration artifact or generator surface in yazelix-zellij-bar, then make Yazelix consume that artifact through the package/runtime boundary with minimal adapter code.
acceptance_criteria: yazelix-zellij-bar owns the status-bar rendering data or generator used by integrated Yazelix; yazelix_core no longer depends on the yazelix_zellij_bar Rust crate; the main repo does not duplicate the child renderer implementation; generated zjstatus layouts preserve widget tray, custom text, tab-label, and command-widget output; the runtime still links yazelix_zellij_bar_widget from the child package; focused Rust tests and repo validators pass.
notes: Rust dependency decision: production crates unchanged in the main repo; no new main-repo crates. The rejected approach was in-house duplication of child rendering logic inside Yazelix. The accepted approach requires a child-owned artifact/generator surface first, then a minimal main adapter. Packaging impact: rust_core_helper should eventually drop the yazelix_zellij_bar Cargo output hash, while the runtime still includes the child package as an artifact/tool provider.
close_reason: Completed. Child repo luccahuguet/yazelix-zellij-bar owns integrated status-bar replacement rendering via yazelix_zellij_bar_widget render-yazelix-runtime and was pushed at 7bddaf2. Yazelix core now calls that child command for a schema-1 placeholder replacement map, applies it as data, and no longer links the yazelix_zellij_bar Rust crate or Cargo output hash. Main flake.lock points at the pushed child revision; runtime build includes libexec/yazelix_zellij_bar_widget. Verification: cargo test in child repo; cargo check --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core; cargo tree --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core -e normal has no yazelix_zellij_bar; yzx_repo_validator validate-contracts; nix build .#runtime .#yazelix --no-link; built runtime renderer command returned schema_version 1 with widget and compact-tab replacements. LOC scorecard for landing commit HEAD^..HEAD: runtime +62, tests +22, docs 0, packaging -1, other -9, total code +81; created payback bead yazelix-6h1n.12 for adapter LOC trim.

title: Trim zellij bar replacement adapter LOC after command-boundary landing
description: Follow-up payback for the zellij-bar command-boundary landing. The current bead removes the native yazelix_zellij_bar crate from yazelix_core and replaces it with a child command/data boundary, but the LOC scorecard for the landing commit is runtime +62, tests +22, docs net 0, packaging -1, other -9, total code +81. Review the adapter and tests after the boundary settles and delete or consolidate any remaining main-repo scaffolding without moving renderer logic back into Yazelix.
acceptance_criteria: Main repo keeps consuming child-owned replacement data; no child renderer implementation is copied into Yazelix; a focused simplification commit reduces or at least does not increase main-repo runtime LOC on the scorecard; existing zellij materialization tests and Nix runtime package build pass.
close_reason: Completed: main now passes typed runtime bar config to yazelix_zellij_bar_widget and inserts the child-rendered zjstatus plugin block. Deleted the main-owned zjstatus tab template and stale replacement-map handling. Verification: cargo fmt --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml --all --check; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core zellij_materialization::tests::; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core --test yzx_core_config_normalize runtime_materialization; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core; cargo run --quiet --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_maintainer --bin yzx_repo_validator -- validate-contracts; cargo run --quiet --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_maintainer --bin yzx_repo_validator -- validate-workspace-session-contract; nix build .#runtime .#yazelix --no-link; runtime child render smoke test returned schema_version 2 and plugin_block. LOC scorecard HEAD^..HEAD: runtime -137, tests -4, docs 0, packaging 0, tokei code -103.

title: Drive integrated bar rendering from child KDL preset
description: Replace the hardcoded integrated zjstatus plugin-block renderer in yazelix-zellij-bar with a child-owned KDL runtime preset. Keep the standalone/vanilla user KDL path simple and unchanged; Yazelix should pass runtime facts and semantic settings, and the child should resolve strict placeholders into a complete plugin block.
acceptance_criteria: yazelix-zellij-bar keeps the vanilla preset and README flow simple for standalone users; a separate integrated runtime KDL preset owns the Yazelix plugin shape; the child renderer fills runtime/path/setting placeholders from typed config and fails on unresolved placeholders; main Yazelix still treats the rendered plugin block as opaque data; tests cover the preset-render path; main LOC does not increase.
close_reason: Completed: yazelix-zellij-bar now renders integrated Yazelix status bar output from a child-owned runtime KDL template while keeping the vanilla standalone preset separate and unchanged. Child commit 7b6d2c3 adds presets/yazelix_runtime_bar.template.kdl, installs it in the child package, removes hardcoded runtime command-definition/string-assembly ownership from Rust, and keeps render-yazelix-runtime schema 2 stable. Main commit pins that child and updates contracts/docs. Verification: child cargo fmt --all --check; child cargo test; child nix build .#yazelix_zellij_bar --no-link; child package render smoke returned schema_version 2 with no __YAZELIX_RUNTIME_ placeholders; main git diff --check; main validate-contracts; main nix build .#runtime .#yazelix --no-link; main forwarded #yazelix_zellij_bar smoke returned schema_version 2 with no runtime placeholders; main cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core zellij_materialization::tests::renders_zjstatus_plugin_block_with_child_renderer_command. LOC scorecard HEAD^..HEAD in main: runtime 0, tests 0, docs 0, packaging 0, tokei code 0.

title: Compress the first-maintainer architecture reading path
description: Reduce the number of documents a new maintainer or coding agent must read before making a safe small change. Keep AGENTS.md as workflow policy, but make the docs front door identify the minimal current-state reading path for architecture, runtime contract, config contract, validation, and Beads without forcing a tour through historical or planning-era docs.
acceptance_criteria: A maintainer can identify the minimal current-state reading set from the docs front door; historical/planning docs are clearly separated from live contracts; no broad prose duplication is introduced.
notes: Architecture rating weakness: the internal architecture is strong, but the human onboarding path has too many named surfaces and docs to load before safe work.
close_reason: Added a maintainer fast path to docs/README.md covering AGENTS, bd prime, architecture, runtime/config contracts, and test governance; validate-docs-experience passed.

title: Create a machine-readable runtime ownership graph
description: Make Yazelix runtime ownership discoverable from one machine-readable surface instead of requiring readers to mentally merge flake outputs, runtime tool registry, runtime component registry, command metadata, config contract, and runtime materialization code. The graph should identify command owners, config owners, generated-state owners, packaged tools, optional host/off tools, runtime components, and validation commands where available.
design: Prefer deriving from existing Rust/Nix metadata and validators. Avoid adding another manually maintained inventory unless it is explicitly validated against source-of-truth data.
acceptance_criteria: There is one canonical machine-readable ownership artifact or command output; it is generated or validated from existing sources of truth instead of hand-maintained duplication; docs point humans and agents to it.
notes: Architecture rating improvement: make the runtime ownership graph machine-renderable from one source to reduce coordination burden and improve agent usability.\n\nRust dependency decision before implementation: use in-house Rust with existing serde/serde_json and existing command/config/runtime metadata; add no production or dev crates. Logic built in-house because the graph is a small projection over local metadata and manifests. Rejected alternatives: a hand-maintained TOML inventory, because it would duplicate source-of-truth data; a Nix-only generator, because command routing and helper ownership already live in Rust. Packaging impact: no new dependencies, only an additional yzx_core helper command and tests.
close_reason: Added yzx_core runtime-ownership.graph to expose command owners, config/generated-state surfaces, validation commands, and packaged runtime tool/component manifests when present. Focused Rust tests, cargo check, and a source-checkout graph smoke passed.

title: Audit deterministic Nushell seams for Rust ownership or explicit retention
description: Review the remaining Nushell modules and classify each surviving behavior as either shell/UI core that should stay in Nushell or deterministic control-plane logic that should move to Rust. For any migration candidates, create scoped follow-up beads rather than doing a broad rewrite inside this bead.
design: Use delete-first reasoning: remove obsolete seams before porting, and prefer Rust only where it clarifies deterministic behavior without hurting shell/UI ergonomics.
acceptance_criteria: Each remaining Nushell owner has a documented keep-or-move decision; deterministic migration candidates have concrete child/follow-up beads; shell/UI fits are explicitly marked as retained so future agents do not reopen the same question casually.
notes: Architecture rating: continuing the Rust-forward cut is good, but it should stop before Nushell ergonomics are damaged.
close_reason: Updated subsystem_code_inventory.md with the current 12-file Nushell owner map, explicit retain/shrink decisions, and follow-up beads yazelix-6h1n.4.1-.4.3 for deterministic migration candidates. Docs, Nushell syntax, and contract validators passed.

title: Collapse yzx_core_bridge.nu to a minimal transport helper
description: Review nushell/scripts/utils/yzx_core_bridge.nu and remove deterministic policy/rendering that can be owned by Rust or caller-local code. Keep only the smallest honest helper transport needed by surviving Nushell shell/UI owners.
acceptance_criteria: yzx_core_bridge.nu no longer owns domain policy beyond helper execution, JSON envelope parsing, and unavoidable shell-facing error display; any moved behavior has focused Rust or caller-local coverage.
notes: Follow-up from the remaining Nushell seam audit. Do not port just to add another wrapper; delete or move behavior only when the Nu owner shrinks end-to-end.
close_reason: Completed: yzx_core_bridge.nu now keeps the narrow helper transport/envelope boundary; deleted the empty caller error-surface argument and unused Zellij wrapper exports. LOC scorecard 4d36bd3d..HEAD: runtime -71, docs -13, code -64.

title: Move source-checkout runtime script chmod repair out of shellhook
description: Evaluate whether setup/environment.nu should still chmod runtime scripts in mutable source checkouts during shellhook setup, or whether package-time executable validation plus repo metadata should own that invariant. Delete the shellhook repair path if a cleaner owner exists.
acceptance_criteria: The executable-bit invariant for runtime scripts has one owner; shellhook startup does not perform broad chmod repair unless a current contract proves it is still needed.
notes: Follow-up from the remaining Nushell seam audit. This is a deterministic setup repair path inside an otherwise shell-bound environment setup file.
close_reason: Completed: deleted source-checkout runtime script chmod repair from shellhook; tracked executable bits/package metadata own script executability. Verified targets are 100755. LOC scorecard 4d36bd3d..HEAD: runtime -71, docs -13, code -64.

title: Re-evaluate setup/environment.nu initializer generation ownership
description: Audit setup/environment.nu after the Rust runtime-env and command metadata cuts to see whether shell initializer generation should stay Nushell-owned, move into Rust, or split into a Rust plan plus shell writers. Only proceed if the surviving Nu owner shrinks without hurting shell ergonomics.
acceptance_criteria: Initializer generation has a recorded keep-or-move decision with a concrete deletion target if migration is chosen; no extra bridge layer is introduced.
notes:
Follow-up from the remaining Nushell seam audit. The current audit keeps environment.nu because it is shellhook-bound, but initializer generation remains the most deterministic part of that file.
Decision: keep initializer generation as a shellhook-timed call into yzx_control. Rust owns generation; Nu owns the selected shell list, quiet-mode env, and startup profile boundary. Moving the call elsewhere would add a layer without deleting a real shell-entry responsibility.
close_reason: Completed as decision: initializer generation stays as a shellhook-timed call into yzx_control; Rust owns generation, Nu owns selected shells/quiet mode/profile timing. Moving it would not delete a real owner. Documented in subsystem inventory. LOC scorecard 4d36bd3d..HEAD: runtime -71, docs -13, code -64.

title: Add one-command validation references beside major subsystem contracts
description: Make each major subsystem family point to the highest-signal command that defends its current contract. This should cover runtime control plane, workspace session orchestration, distribution and host integration, shipped runtime data/assets, and maintainer validation without bloating docs or adding weak tests.
acceptance_criteria: Each major architecture-map subsystem has at least one nearby validation command or an explicit note that no direct command exists yet; weak command-discovery tests are not added; missing validator gaps are captured as follow-up beads if needed.
notes: Architecture rating improvement: add more 'one command proves this contract' references near major subsystem descriptions.
close_reason: Added architecture_map.md validation map for all five subsystem families using existing high-signal validators only. Docs and contract validators passed.

title: Reduce pane-orchestrator source and tracked-wasm sync risk
description: Tighten the existing pane-orchestrator source-to-tracked-wasm workflow with a lightweight provenance stamp and dirty-source guard. Keep the current copied-wasm workflow for now, but make the source Git revision that produced the tracked runtime artifact visible and validated.
acceptance_criteria: The tracked pane-orchestrator sync stamp identifies the source Git commit and remote that produced configs/zellij/plugins/yazelix_pane_orchestrator.wasm; build_pane_orchestrator --sync refuses a dirty source checkout; validate-pane-orchestrator-sync compares the stamp to the sibling checkout when available and remains the documented live-plugin gate; docs still state cargo check/test alone is not sufficient for live plugin behavior.
notes: Re-scoped after maintainer review: do not add a broad sync-stamp schema. Add the minimum useful provenance: source_git_commit, source_git_remote, and build command; make build_pane_orchestrator --sync refuse dirty sibling source; keep validate-pane-orchestrator-sync as the single high-signal gate.
close_reason: Completed: pane-orchestrator sync stamp now records source Git commit, source remote, source hash, build command, and wasm hash; --sync refuses dirty source checkouts; validator compares stamped provenance to the sibling checkout. Verified with cargo test -p yazelix_maintainer pane_orchestrator, flake-shell build-pane-orchestrator --sync, validate-pane-orchestrator-sync, validate-rust-test-traceability, and validate-package-rust-test-purity.

title: Simplify the user-facing runtime config and update mental model
description: Review the main user docs for places where Yazelix asks users to understand too many internal surfaces: packaged runtime, generated state, config UI, Home Manager ownership, update owners, child repos, popup plugin paths, and cursor sidecar config. Tighten the explanation so everyday users see a small model first, with advanced ownership details clearly secondary.
acceptance_criteria: README and docs front doors present a compact everyday mental model before advanced ownership details; update-owner and config-owner rules remain accurate; child repo and sidecar details are still findable but not required for first comprehension.
notes: Architecture rating weakness: human UX clarity scored lower than architecture and agent usability because the project has many named surfaces.
close_reason: Completed: README now leads with the everyday runtime/config/update ownership model, child repos are moved to an advanced section, docs front door points to the model, and customization opens with the canonical settings/runtime-state model. Verified validate-docs-experience and validate-readme-version.

title: Add yzpp tracked-wasm provenance guard
description: Give the tracked yzpp runtime artifact the same lightweight source provenance protection as the pane orchestrator. yzpp.wasm is a first-party child-repo artifact consumed by Yazelix, so maintainers should be able to tell which yazelix-zellij-popup source revision produced configs/zellij/plugins/yzpp.wasm.
acceptance_criteria: A maintainer can quickly identify which yazelix-zellij-popup source commit produced configs/zellij/plugins/yzpp.wasm; the refresh/sync workflow refuses or clearly rejects dirty child source when stamping from a local checkout; a focused validator catches stale yzpp.wasm provenance; docs identify yzpp as a first-party copied wasm until the locked-package architecture replaces it.
notes: Scope this as a guardrail, not the final ownership model. Reuse the small pattern from pane-orchestrator provenance: source Git commit, source remote, source artifact hash, and a validator/update path. Avoid a large schema unless it removes real ambiguity.
close_reason: Completed: added yzx dev sync_yzpp_wasm to build the yazelix-zellij-popup child package from a clean checkout, copy packaged yzpp.wasm, and write configs/zellij/plugins/yzpp.sync_stamp with source commit, remote, source hash, build command, package wasm hash, and tracked wasm hash. Added validate-yzpp-sync and docs/contract references. Verified with repo_plugin_build tests, sync-yzpp-wasm, validate-yzpp-sync, validate-pane-orchestrator-sync, validate-contracts, validate-rust-test-traceability, validate-package-rust-test-purity, validate-workspace-session-contract, and command metadata test.

title: Consume first-party Zellij plugin wasm from locked child packages
description: Replace the main repo's first-party copied-wasm ownership model with locked child package inputs where practical. Pane-orchestrator and yzpp should be packaged by their source repositories, and Yazelix should consume their built wasm artifacts from locked Nix inputs instead of tracking manually copied binaries in configs/zellij/plugins/.
acceptance_criteria: A design or implementation path exists for pane-orchestrator and yzpp to come from locked child package inputs; flake.lock or an equivalent package lock is the source provenance for regular builds; tracked first-party wasm artifacts are removed or explicitly justified; local development still has a documented override path; the validator story no longer depends on manually stamped copied wasm for first-party plugins.
notes: Architecture direction: the child repo owns source and standalone package; flake.lock owns provenance; Yazelix package copies the child package's wasm into the runtime package. Keep a local override/dev path for active plugin work. Treat zjstatus separately because it is third-party vendoring, not a first-party child repository.
close_reason: Completed as the architecture path allowed by the bead: added a first-party Zellij plugin wasm ownership contract covering locked child package consumption, local override workflow, transition steps, and the temporary nature of copied-wasm stamps. Updated pane-orchestrator and yzpp contracts to point at that package boundary. Verified with validate-contracts.

title: Refine config UI header metadata styling
description: The config UI header should keep path, owner, mode, and diagnostics in one top metadata row and improve key coloring for both metadata labels and setting paths.
acceptance_criteria: Path appears on the same header row as owner/mode/diagnostics; metadata is right-aligned when space allows; metadata labels and setting keys use dedicated readable key colors.
close_reason: Completed: header metadata moved to one right-aligned row, path truncates from the left, and metadata/config keys use dedicated colors.

title: Evaluate immutable config snapshot storage architecture
description: Evaluate where Yazelix should store immutable per-window config state now that live panes should not reread mutable yazelix.toml. Compare a Yazelix-owned session_facts file, Zellij-owned/cache-backed state, pane-orchestrator/session-state storage, environment-carried pointers, and generated config embedding.
design:
Decision: store immutable live-window config in Yazelix-owned per-window snapshots, not in mutable user config, global generated state, Zellij-owned cache, or pane-orchestrator state.

First-principles requirement: a running Yazelix window needs a stable configuration contract after launch. The user may edit `user_configs/yazelix.toml`, upgrade the package, or repair generated state while old windows remain open. Live pane commands must keep working against the configuration that created their session, and config/schema drift must not make an older window's popup/sidebar/status commands fail.

Rejected architectures:
- Reread `yazelix.toml` from live panes: mutable, schema-version sensitive, and the source of the stale-config breakage this bead exists to prevent.
- Keep a global `state/session_facts.json`: last-writer-wins across simultaneous windows and too narrow because it stores only selected facts.
- Put the canonical config in Zellij cache/session state: too coupled to one backend, unclear lifecycle/cleanup ownership, and bad for future non-Zellij surfaces. Zellij can carry inherited environment and status facts, but it should not own the canonical Yazelix config snapshot.
- Put the canonical config in the pane orchestrator/status bus: useful for live facts, but not reliable as the source of truth before plugins load or when plugin state is corrupt.
- Inline the config in process environment: leaks a large blob into child environments and process inspection, makes diagnostics worse, and has size/quoting risks.
- Embed this in generated Zellij/Yazi artifacts: those are shared materialized outputs for launches, not per-window immutable state.

Chosen architecture:
- At launch, write a versioned JSON snapshot under the Yazelix state tree, scoped to one live window/session instance: `state/sessions/<session_instance_id>/config_snapshot.json`.
- Export a pointer such as `YAZELIX_SESSION_CONFIG_PATH` into the Zellij session/pane environment. Zellij carries the pointer; Yazelix owns the file and schema.
- The snapshot stores `schema_version`, a snapshot/session instance id, created timestamp, source config path and config hash, runtime identity/hash/version, full normalized config, and a derived facts projection for hot live consumers.
- Live session-local commands read the snapshot API. The facts projection remains a convenience, not the canonical data model.
- Startup/config/edit/doctor/materialization/update commands may read current mutable config. Popup/menu/sidebar/reveal/runtime-editor/status fallback paths should not.
- `yzx restart` is the explicit way to apply a changed config to an already-open window. New windows get new snapshots. Existing windows keep their snapshot.
- Keep a short compatibility path for currently-open sessions that still export `YAZELIX_SESSION_FACTS_PATH`, but the final architecture should remove lossy direct config fallback for live panes and replace missing snapshot with a clear restart/relaunch diagnostic.

Migration plan:
1. Add the versioned snapshot schema and typed loader.
2. Create per-window snapshots directly during startup handoff rather than copying global `state/session_facts.json`.
3. Route live command surfaces through `YAZELIX_SESSION_CONFIG_PATH` and the snapshot API.
4. Decide/remove persistent-session semantics before finalizing attach behavior, because existing persistent attaches cannot safely adopt a fresh per-window snapshot.
5. Update docs/status/doctor to explain that config edits apply to the next window or `yzx restart`.
acceptance_criteria: Decision reasons from first principles about config immutability, multiple simultaneous Yazelix windows, persistent-session attach semantics, crash/restart recovery, security/privacy, stale runtime upgrades, Zellij cache lifecycle, and compatibility with non-Zellij future surfaces; records the chosen architecture and migration plan before changing the current session_facts implementation
notes: Code investigation 2026-04-27: startup-facts.compute and runtime-materialization.materialize are the strict startup-time config readers; start_yazelix_inner.nu then copies state/session_facts.json into state/sessions/<launch_id>/session_facts.json and exports YAZELIX_SESSION_FACTS_PATH. Live popup/workspace/Zellij/front-door helpers already depend on compute_session_facts_from_env, but that API returns only selected derived facts, keeps a global state/session_facts.json fallback, and still has a lossy direct-config fallback for pre-snapshot sessions. The implementation should replace this with a versioned per-window config_snapshot.json containing full normalized config plus derived facts, expose a snapshot path env var, and keep mutable yazelix.toml reads at startup/config/doctor/materialization boundaries only.
close_reason: Decision recorded: use Yazelix-owned per-window config snapshots with an environment pointer, keep mutable config reads at startup/config/doctor/materialization boundaries only, and migrate live panes away from session_facts/global config fallbacks.

title: Document Alt+m cwd regression spiral after refactor
description: Record the regression sequence where Alt+m pane creation was refactored away from the known-good orchestrator path and reintroduced the Zellij cwd bug, leading to panes opening in HOME, stale sidebar-state confusion, and crashing helper-pane experiments.
design: Keep this as a postmortem-style regression bead linked conceptually to yazelix-2ex.1.7.5. The key invariant is that Yazelix must resolve the live sidebar cwd in Nushell and delegate pane creation to the pane orchestrator via open_terminal_in_cwd. Refactors must not replace that path with zellij new-pane/run --cwd helpers, explicit shell launches, or command panes. Preserve the later good fixes from this incident: immediate sidebar-state cache refresh after open-from-Yazi sync, pruning stale sidebar session files, and avoiding launcher cwd poisoning from ~/.config/yazelix.
acceptance_criteria: The bead notes capture the failure chain, the known-good contract, and the forbidden fallback paths. Future work touching Alt+m or pane cwd should be able to use this bead to avoid repeating the regression.
notes: Regression chain on 2026-03-15: a refactor replaced the orchestrator-owned Alt+m pane-open flow from a7b5716/yazelix-2ex.1.7.5 with helper-driven zellij new-pane --cwd and later zellij run --cwd shell launches. Symptoms included panes opening in /home/lucca or ~/.config/yazelix despite correct target resolution, stale sidebar-state files masking the real issue, helper crashes, and a wider session cwd spiral after layout churn. Confirmed root cause remained the upstream Zellij cwd bug already documented in zellij-org/zellij#3068. Final recovery: restore open_workspace_terminal.nu to call run_pane_orchestrator_command_raw "open_terminal_in_cwd" with the resolved sidebar/workspace cwd, keep sidebar-state freshness fixes, and treat the orchestrator path as the only supported Alt+m implementation.
close_reason: Documented the Alt+m regression spiral, the repeated Zellij cwd bug trigger, and the required orchestrator-owned recovery path.

title: Define the Yazelix backend contract before evaluating alternative runtime backends
description:
Define the Yazelix backend capability contract before doing deeper backend experiments. The point is to stop using “backend” as a vague synonym for “env manager” and instead write down the concrete capabilities Yazelix currently needs from its runtime/environment layer.

This contract should classify at least these capability families:
- runtime materialization of the shipped/default Yazelix tool stack
- stable install/update ownership and user-facing entrypoint identity
- env/run/shell entrypoints for commands like `yzx env`, `yzx run`, and launch re-entry
- cached profile or reusable activation semantics, including refresh/invalidation rules
- runtime-owned binary and asset resolution, not just generic PATH lookup
- generated runtime/state ownership and how it relates to the backend
- platform/package availability expectations and which current conveniences are actually optional

The goal is not to bless a new backend yet. The goal is to produce a contract concrete enough to evaluate current full Yazelix and a future Nix-only `Yazelix Core` flake candidate without redefining the criteria midstream. Later optional backend experiments should be able to reuse the same contract instead of shaping it.
design:
Treat this as contract/spec-first work, not implementation work. Build directly on `docs/architecture_map.md` plus the existing runtime/install/config contracts: `runtime_root_contract.md`, `flake_interface_contract.md`, `one_command_install_ux.md`, and `config_surface_and_launch_profile_contract.md`.

Ground the contract in the real code seams that currently depend on the backend layer:
- `nushell/scripts/utils/devenv_cli.nu`
- `nushell/scripts/utils/environment_bootstrap.nu`
- `nushell/scripts/utils/launch_state.nu`
- `nushell/scripts/utils/config_state.nu`
- `nushell/scripts/yzx/env.nu`
- `nushell/scripts/yzx/run.nu`
- `nushell/scripts/yzx/launch.nu`
- `nushell/scripts/yzx/refresh.nu`

The written contract should produce:
1. a capability matrix with at least these columns:
   - full Yazelix today
   - possible Nix-only Core backend
2. a required vs optional vs explicitly-out-of-scope classification for each capability bucket
3. a clear boundary between backend capability requirements and launch-time dependency/preflight checks
4. a clear boundary between backend capability requirements and the eventual product/support boundary for `Yazelix Core`

Optional later backend experiments, such as the Pixi lane, should be evaluated against this contract after it exists instead of influencing the contract definition itself.

Important constraint: this bead is not where launch-time “is Helix/Zellij/Ghostty installed?” checks are defined. That belongs to `yazelix-j4qv`. This bead is about what a backend must make possible for Yazelix as a product at all.
acceptance_criteria:
There is a written backend contract for Yazelix that groups the real backend surface into explicit capability buckets instead of an abstract “backend” label. Each bucket is marked as required, optional, or out of scope for backend evaluation.

The contract is concrete enough to evaluate at least:
- current `devenv`-backed full Yazelix
- a future Nix-only, no-`devenv`, opinionated flake candidate for `Yazelix Core`

The contract explicitly covers the current hard seams visible in code today:
- runtime-owned CLI/tool resolution
- install/update ownership and stable entrypoint identity
- env/run/rebuild/re-entry entrypoints
- cached profile reuse and invalidation semantics
- relationship between backend materialization and generated runtime/state artifacts

The contract also explicitly states what it does *not* own:
- user-facing launch preflight / dependency checker behavior (`yazelix-j4qv`)
- the final `Yazelix Core` product boundary (`yazelix-qv8c`)
- implementation of a new backend

A later bead should be able to use this contract to evaluate candidate backends without moving the goalposts or rediscovering requirements from scattered code.
notes:
Evidence already points to a broader backend surface than “can spawn a shell”:
- `devenv_cli.nu` and `environment_bootstrap.nu` show that Yazelix currently expects preferred runtime-owned CLI resolution plus rebuild/re-entry control.
- `config_state.nu` and `launch_state.nu` show that cached profile reuse and invalidation are part of the backend story, not just a UX optimization.
- `yzx env`, `yzx run`, `yzx launch`, and `yzx refresh` show that the backend currently owns both activation and rebuild semantics.
- `flake_interface_contract.md`, `one_command_install_ux.md`, and `flake.nix` show that install/update identity and stable runtime pointers are part of the backend boundary too.

The likely delete-first outcome is that some things people casually bundle into “backend” will turn out to be:
- launch preflight concerns
- product-boundary concerns
- or optional conveniences rather than hard backend requirements.
close_reason: Backend capability contract written, linked into architecture docs, and aligned with issue #538.

title: Investigate recurring pane-orchestrator busy stalls
description: Yazelix/Zellij interactions can stall or lag while the pane orchestrator is busy. Observed symptoms include horizontal pane walking problems, clicking Zellij tabs not responding normally, and popup pane invocation delays or failures.
acceptance_criteria: Collect and inspect relevant Yazelix/Zellij/pane-orchestrator logs for a stall window; identify whether the issue is command pressure, timer scheduling, plugin event-loop blocking, status-cache refresh work, Zellij focus/tab events, or another runtime path; document reproduction clues and add a concrete follow-up fix bead or close with evidence if no product fix is needed.
notes:
Live occurrence reported in Zellij session named didactic-cuckoo. Symptoms active during investigation: pane walking/tab clicks/popup invocation appear busy or delayed.
Severity update: user clarified this is not rare. Treat as recurring P0 runtime responsiveness bug until proven otherwise.
Live evidence: affected Zellij server logged repeated KeybindPipe/CliPipe 1s timeouts during the stall windows. The hot server thread was plugin-exec for the loaded pane orchestrator/status plugins. Zellij set_timeout creates independent Tokio sleeps that later emit generic Timer events, while the orchestrator currently handles each Timer by checking every refresh loop and rescheduling non-due loops. Hypothesis: multiple status-cache timers can multiply queued Timer events and starve pipe handling.
Follow-up architecture angle: this incident is also a good signal that the pane-orchestrator heartbeat should expose useful liveness facts, such as last timer tick, last pipe handled, last status refresh start/finish, and stale/busy indicators. Keep the immediate P0 patch focused on eliminating timer amplification, then evaluate heartbeat diagnostics as the next hardening step.
Implemented focused fix: pane-orchestrator timed work now shares a single central armed timeout. Status-cache and screen-saver paths only update deadline state; the central timer planner arms the earliest deadline and keeps earlier already-armed timers when later deadlines change. Added regression tests for central timer planning. Also created follow-up yazelix-liep for heartbeat/liveness diagnostics.
Verification: cargo test --manifest-path rust_plugins/zellij_pane_orchestrator/Cargo.toml passed 42 tests; cargo check --manifest-path rust_plugins/zellij_pane_orchestrator/Cargo.toml passed; yzx dev build_pane_orchestrator --sync rebuilt and synced the wasm; cargo run --quiet --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_maintainer --bin yzx_repo_validator -- validate-pane-orchestrator-sync passed; validate-rust-test-traceability passed after replacing a weak removal-only usage test with an active Claude usage target test.
close_reason: Completed: live stall traced to pane-orchestrator timer amplification risk and fixed by centralizing timer scheduling; wasm rebuilt/synced and validation passed.

title: Stop stacked-pane focus navigation from injecting Enter
description: Leaving and returning to a terminal pane inside a Zellij stack can appear to press Enter, producing repeated empty shell prompts. Investigate the interaction between vertical stack navigation, Yazelix keybind overrides, and the opt-in screen saver InputReceived path. The fix should ensure normal pane focus movement does not write newline/input into the focused terminal.
acceptance_criteria:
Alt+j/Alt+k and Alt+Down/Alt+Up stack navigation do not execute an empty shell prompt when returning to a terminal pane.
Screensaver disabled sessions do not subscribe to or act on InputReceived for screen saver behavior.
If screensaver is enabled, dismissing it must not replay or synthesize Enter into the previously focused terminal.
Managed Yazelix Nushell panes keep prompts on one physical terminal line so Zellij stack focus redraws do not add fake empty prompt entries.
notes:
Investigation: current generated config and settings have screen_saver_enabled=false, and the screen saver plugin path only subscribes InputReceived when enabled and never writes Enter. Live Zellij reproduction against the one-row stacked shell pane showed focus cycling added blank prompts to scrollback without any Yazelix input write. Disabling terminal focus events did not help. Replacing the managed Nu prompt with a single-line prompt stopped the symptom. Root cause is multiline Starship/Nushell prompt redraw when Zellij collapses inactive stack panes to one row.
Implementation: added nushell/config/stack_prompt_guard.nu and source it last from shells/posix/yazelix_nu.sh so managed Yazelix Nu prompts and right prompts are trimmed and flattened to one physical terminal line after user shell_nu.nu runs.
Verification: direct guard probe flattened a synthetic a\nb prompt to a b; yazelix_nu.sh generated config sources stack_prompt_guard.nu and leaves PROMPT_COMMAND as a closure; yzx dev rust fmt --check passed; yzx dev rust check passed; cargo run -p yazelix_maintainer --bin yzx_repo_validator -- validate-nushell-syntax --verbose passed.
close_reason: Completed: flattened managed Yazelix Nushell prompts in stacked panes so focus redraws do not look like injected Enter; screensaver path was ruled out for disabled sessions.

title: Split Helix materialization modules
description: Refactor the overgrown Helix materialization implementation into focused modules without changing generated Helix config, Steel plugin behavior, import notices, config schema, or public command output.
acceptance_criteria:
1. helix_materialization.rs keeps the public request/data orchestration surface and is meaningfully shorter.
2. Managed Helix TOML merging, Steel plugin materialization, and import notice logic move into focused modules without changing public output.
3. No config/schema/command behavior changes are introduced.
4. Focused Helix materialization tests and config validators pass.
5. Packaged helix-materialization.generate smoke confirms default and custom plugin behavior still works.
notes:
Rust dependency decision: no new production or dev crates. This is an isomorphic module-boundary refactor using only Rust modules and existing tests.

Isomorphism proof: public request/data structs, generated TOML/Scheme files, error codes/messages, command ordering, plugin copy paths, import notice behavior, and config schema remain unchanged. Byte-for-byte generated Helix output comparison passed for default plugins and a custom hello_yazelix plugin using the packaged runtime.

Verification:
- cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core helix_materialization::tests
- cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core config_normalize
- cargo run --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_maintainer --bin yzx_repo_validator -- validate-config-surface-contract
- nix build .#runtime --override-input yazelixHelix ../yazelix-helix --no-write-lock-file -o result_yazelix_helix_runtime
- packaged result_yazelix_helix_runtime/libexec/yzx_core helix-materialization.generate smoke for default and custom plugins
- compare_outputs.py MATCH for default and custom generated state/configs/helix directories
- shells/posix/yazelix_loc_scorecard.sh HEAD <staged-tree>: runtime net 0, code delta -2, total delta 0
close_reason: Completed: split Helix materialization into focused helix_config, steel, import_notice, and tests modules without changing generated output. Verification passed: focused Helix tests, config_normalize tests, config-surface validator, Nix runtime build with local yazelixHelix override, packaged default/custom helix-materialization.generate smoke, byte-for-byte compare_outputs MATCH for generated Helix directories, and LOC scorecard runtime net 0 / code delta -2.

title: Move user-owned config under user_configs with terminal subdir
description: Move user-owned Yazelix config into a dedicated user_configs boundary. End-state: all Yazelix-managed user inputs live under ~/.config/yazelix/user_configs/ (including yazelix.toml, yazelix_packs.toml, terminal/, yazi/, and zellij/), while generated runtime state lives under ~/.local/share/yazelix/. Keep tracked defaults at the repo/config root. Managed mode should not implicitly read external native tool config from unrelated XDG paths; native tool config should only participate as an explicit import/migration source or escape hatch. Keep the system self-contained, coherent for users, and friendly to a future first-party Yazelix flake. Do not auto-scaffold optional user-owned files by default. Add only one minimal guide file at user_configs/README.md rather than a README in every subfolder.
close_reason: Completed

title: Add a self-validating desktop-entry freshness contract
description: Define a Yazelix desktop-entry contract that carries enough metadata to detect when the installed desktop file is stale relative to the current installed runtime, instead of relying only on indirect path drift checks.
design: Prefer explicit desktop-entry metadata over heuristic-only staleness checks. Store a runtime-target identifier in the desktop file, such as the runtime/current target path or a derived install-generation token. Keep the contract readable and stable enough for both yzx doctor and the desktop launcher to consume.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The desktop entry format includes a Yazelix-owned freshness field. 2. The chosen field is strong enough to distinguish a current runtime from a stale one without relying only on version strings. 3. The contract is documented clearly enough for both doctor and launcher-side validation.

title: Refactor the stable CLI into a leaf-command dispatcher for latency-sensitive commands
description: Refactor the stable Yazelix command-entrypoint path so latency-sensitive leaf commands do not pay the full `core/yazelix.nu` import cost by default. Recent performance work already exposed the pattern twice: popup and reveal both became much faster once their hot path stopped importing the monolithic core command suite. This bead turns that from ad hoc fast paths into a cleaner command-dispatch boundary for small interactive commands.
design: Treat this as a narrow pre-Rust architecture cleanup, not a giant CLI rewrite. Keep `core/yazelix.nu` as an aggregator and rich command-development surface, but stop treating it as the default hot path for every small interactive command. The immediate work is to identify which leaf commands are genuinely latency-sensitive, route them through direct module entrypoints by default, and leave slower/heavier commands on the core path until there is evidence they need more. Current measured evidence already justifies this for `reveal`, `popup`, and likely `menu`: full core import is about 2.05s locally, while `yzx/popup.nu` imports around 0.09s, `yzx/menu.nu` around 0.11s, and `integrations/reveal_in_yazi.nu` around 0.06s. The right outcome is a small dispatcher boundary around the stable CLI and runtime wrappers, not a speculative generated registry system yet. This should make the later Rust rewrite cleaner by clarifying command-entrypoint ownership first instead of porting the current monolithic hot path as-is.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The stable CLI and relevant runtime wrappers have an explicit fast path for the small interactive commands that are proven latency-sensitive. 2. At least one remaining hot candidate beyond the already-fixed popup/reveal path is migrated through that cleaner dispatcher boundary and measured before/after. 3. The repo has one authoritative place to decide whether a command uses a leaf entrypoint or the full core command suite, instead of growing ad hoc wrapper exceptions forever. 4. Add focused wrapper-behavior regressions for the new dispatcher paths without adding weak implementation-string tests.
notes: Current local evidence: `use nushell/scripts/core/yazelix.nu *` imports in about 2.05s, `use nushell/scripts/yzx/menu.nu *` in about 0.11s, and the lightweight reveal wrapper import in about 0.06s. `configs/zellij/scripts/yzx_menu_popup.nu` is the clearest remaining hot-path candidate because it still imports `core/yazelix.nu` just to run `yzx menu`.
close_reason: Completed

title: Speed up desktop-entry launch by bypassing monolithic CLI and core launch imports
description: The desktop launcher path is still paying the full stable-CLI and core-launch bootstrap when opened from the generated desktop entry. Current measurements show `~/.local/bin/yzx desktop launch --help` takes about 2.07s, while importing `nushell/scripts/yzx/desktop.nu` directly takes about 0.025s and `nushell/scripts/core/desktop_launcher.nu` about 0.057s. The generated desktop file currently executes `~/.local/bin/yzx desktop launch`, which falls through the full `core/yazelix.nu` path, and `desktop_launcher.nu` then shells back into `use .../core/yazelix.nu *; yzx launch --home` instead of calling the leaf launch module directly.
design: Treat this as a narrow child of the leaf-dispatcher architecture bead, not a separate launcher subsystem rewrite. The desktop file should keep using the stable `yzx` binary, but the stable CLI should leaf-dispatch the `desktop` command family directly to `nushell/scripts/yzx/desktop.nu`. Inside that family, `yzx desktop launch` should keep using the installed runtime and desktop-launcher entrypoint, but `desktop_launcher.nu` should call `nushell/scripts/yzx/launch.nu` directly instead of re-importing `core/yazelix.nu`. Keep the shared launch semantics, preflight, and terminal dispatch owned by `yzx/launch.nu`; only remove redundant aggregator overhead.
acceptance_criteria:
1. `~/.local/bin/yzx desktop launch --help` no longer pays the full `core/yazelix.nu` import path by default.
2. `desktop_launcher.nu` launches through `yzx/launch.nu` directly rather than routing through `core/yazelix.nu`.
3. The installed desktop entry still works and preserves the current installed-runtime contract.
4. Add at least one focused regression for the lightweight desktop dispatcher path without adding weak implementation-string tests.
5. Measure before/after and keep the local evidence in the bead notes or commit context.
notes: Local evidence before starting: `~/.local/bin/yzx desktop launch --help` ~2.07s, `use nushell/scripts/yzx/desktop.nu *; help 'yzx desktop launch'` ~0.025s, `use nushell/scripts/core/desktop_launcher.nu *; help main` ~0.057s, and `use nushell/scripts/yzx/launch.nu *; help 'yzx launch'` ~0.424s.
close_reason: Completed

title: Speed up desktop-entry launch by bypassing monolithic CLI and core launch imports
description:
The desktop launcher path is still paying the full stable-CLI and core-launch bootstrap when opened from the generated desktop entry. Current measurements show Usage:
  > yzx desktop launch 

Flags:
  -h, --help: Display the help message for this command

Input/output types:
  ╭───┬───────┬────────╮
  │ # │ input │ output │

title: Document and validate Zellij session sharing with Yazelix
description: Investigate how Zellij session sharing works in practice for Yazelix users, including whether Yazelix makes the process easier, harder, or just different compared with vanilla Zellij. The main sharing paths to verify are Zellij's built-in web client and ordinary attach/share flows for existing sessions.
design: Start as a docs-and-validation task grounded in the official Zellij sharing model. Zellij now has a built-in web server and web client that can be started with `zellij web` or through the share plugin, and authenticated with one-time login tokens. The default local URL scheme and token flow should be verified in a Yazelix session. Also verify ordinary multi-client session attach flows and session resurrection/share behavior. Then check how Yazelix interacts with these flows: shared Yazelix environments, shared devenv profiles, plugin/runtime expectations, persistent session naming, and whether the current keybinding model makes the share plugin discoverable enough. If Yazelix creates friction, consider small helper behavior or targeted docs from the Yazelix side to smooth it out.
acceptance_criteria: There is accurate documentation for sharing Zellij sessions with Yazelix, grounded in verified behavior rather than guesswork. Any Yazelix-specific helper or caveat is documented clearly. Leave behind a repeatable validation script or checklist with detailed logs covering the tested sharing path, authentication or web-client steps, and any plugin or session caveats that users need to know.
notes:
Official Zellij sources to anchor this work: Web client tutorial, web client user guide, and the 0.43.0 release notes about browser sharing. The docs note that the web client is useful for remote access, demos, pair programming, and collaborative debugging. They also recommend HTTPS for anything beyond local-only use.

Discussion 565 wishlist reinforced this bead: a macOS/Home Manager tester specifically called out Zellij Web Client as potentially useful for browser, mobile, and external-equipment access and asked Yazelix to keep an eye on its ease of configuration. Treat this as validation pressure for whether Yazelix needs docs, helper commands, or discoverability around zellij web and share-plugin flows.

2026-05-22 idea triage: this survives as a concrete Zellij-native capability validation item, not a generic collaboration wishlist. Keep the scope to verified Yazelix behavior, docs, and any small helper only if validation shows Yazelix-specific friction.

title: Replace generic OpenCode usage widget with OpenCode Go usage
description: Remove the generic opencode_usage status widget and add an OpenCode Go-specific usage widget that can honestly mirror the Codex 5h/week token/quota shape.
acceptance_criteria: opencode_usage and zellij.opencode_usage_periods are removed from the supported config/docs/Home Manager/onboard/bar surfaces; opencode_go_usage is accepted as the replacement widget and renders with label go; OpenCode Go usage reads current OpenCode SQLite usage data from opencode*.db, computes 5h/week tokens, computes remaining quota percentages from OpenCode Go dollar limits for opencode-go rows, and uses the existing shared-cache/background-refresh pattern so zjstatus never scans provider data directly; tests cover rendering, parsing/current DB selection assumptions, cache behavior, and stale/missing data.
notes:
Dependency decision: build the OpenCode Go widget in-house for now. Current OpenCode stores token/cost facts in SQLite, which is a small, stable enough read surface for this status widget. Rejected continuing ccusage-opencode because it reads the older JSON storage path and missed current local usage on the maintainer machine. Rejected generic provider quota because OpenCode is provider-agnostic; only OpenCode Go has a clear 5h/week/month quota contract. Use no new crates unless the existing rust_core dependency set lacks a SQLite reader; prefer the repo's current dependencies if available.
Dependency update: added rusqlite 0.39 with bundled SQLite for the in-house OpenCode Go SQLite reader. Packaging impact is limited to the Rust core helper; the bundled feature avoids relying on a host sqlite3 binary or system SQLite runtime.
Implemented opencode_go_usage as the replacement for generic opencode_usage. Removed opencode_usage and zellij.opencode_usage_periods from the supported default config, Home Manager module, config contract, docs, onboarding picker, zjstatus template, runtime exports, and flake package surface. Added a direct OpenCode Go SQLite reader for opencode*.db files that filters assistant rows with providerID=opencode-go, computes 5h/week token totals, and derives remaining quota percentages from OpenCode Go $12/5h and $30/week limits. The widget renders as label go and supports zellij.opencode_go_usage_display = token|quota|both. Background refresh uses the existing per-window status-cache/shared-cache pattern with a shared lock/backoff so zjstatus only reads small cache files.
Verification: yzx dev rust fmt --check; yzx dev rust check; yzx dev rust test zellij_commands; cargo test -p yazelix_bar renders_agent_usage_widgets_as_cached_command_placeholders; cargo check for the pane orchestrator; yzx_repo_validator validate-pane-orchestrator-sync; yzx_repo_validator validate-workspace-session-contract; yzx_repo_validator validate-config-surface-contract; yzx_repo_validator validate-flake-interface; yzx_repo_validator validate-docs-experience. Manual local provider check wrote an opencode_go cache from the maintainer OpenCode DB and rendered: [go 5h|1.71M|99% wk|211M|57%].
close_reason: Completed: replaced the generic OpenCode usage widget with the OpenCode Go-specific cached SQLite reader and go 5h/week token/quota widget.

title: Fix popup/menu transient panes losing mode env after Rust launcher rewrite
description:
Why this issue exists and what needs to be done: the Rust pane orchestrator transient launcher currently passes popup/menu mode markers through the Zellij plugin context parameter instead of a real process environment seam. As a result, floating panes open but  and likely  do not detect transient mode inside the pane and can render empty or recurse into the outer launch path. title: Add desktop-entry staleness detection to yzx doctor
description: Teach yzx doctor to detect when an installed Yazelix desktop entry exists but points at a missing runtime or a runtime different from ~/.local/share/yazelix/runtime/current, and recommend the exact repair command.

title: Audit large files and identify worthwhile refactors
description: Inspect Yazelix's largest or most complex files and decide which ones deserve real refactoring versus which ones are large for legitimate reasons. The goal is to create a justified shortlist, not to split files reflexively.
design: Use delete-first reasoning rather than assuming every large file is bad. Consider file size, complexity, churn, ownership boundaries, and whether the file is hard to understand or hard to test. Prefer identifying a small number of high-value refactors with concrete boundaries over creating a blanket mandate to break up large files everywhere.
acceptance_criteria: There is a ranked shortlist of large files that are worth refactoring, with concrete reasons and suggested seams. Files that are large but acceptable are explicitly called out as such. Follow-up implementation beads are narrower than this audit umbrella.
notes:
2026-04-09 continued public-surface cleanup:
- Internalized file-local `ascii_art.nu` helpers that only supported the local welcome/screen rendering pipeline.
- Internalized `is_palette_eligible_command` in `yzx/command_palette_catalog.nu`; the real exported surface is `get_palette_menu_items`.
- Verification: screen canonical tests, workspace palette-eligibility regression, syntax validation, and traceability validation pass.

2026-04-09 completion:
- The large-file/refactor audit is now recorded in `docs/streamlining_audit_2026_04.md`.
- The doc names the acceptable large files, the remaining worthwhile seams, and the specific delete-first rationale for stopping the broad umbrella here.
close_reason: Completed

title: Split oversized ownership-heavy modules by subsystem contracts
description: Turn the large-file pain in the current production/control-plane hotspots into a contract-first decomposition plan. The goal is not to reduce line count cosmetically; it is to stop single files such as `doctor.nu`, `yzx/dev.nu`, `common.nu`, `config_migrations.nu`, `zellij_config_merger.nu`, and `integrations/yazi.nu` from carrying several subsystem owners at once.
design: Start from ownership boundaries, not helper extraction. Group code by stable subsystem contracts such as config-contract consumers, runtime launch state, terminal wrapper policy, plugin protocol client, and Zellij render model. Prefer deleting mixed-responsibility paths and moving responsibilities behind clearer modules over creating a wider helper jungle. The decomposition plan should identify which slices are real subsystem boundaries and which apparent splits would just shuffle complexity around.
acceptance_criteria: 1. There is a concrete split plan for the worst mixed-responsibility files. 2. The plan groups future slices by subsystem ownership rather than arbitrary helper extraction. 3. The plan identifies deletions/simplifications as well as extractions. 4. The result narrows future refactor beads into explicit contract-owned slices instead of generic 'break up big file' work.
notes:
2026-04-08 scope update after Nushell LOC review:
- The decomposition plan should explicitly target the biggest production Nushell concentration zones, not just the currently loudest files.
- `integrations/yazi.nu` is now clearly underrepresented in the shortlist: at 713 lines it mixes sidebar-state lookup, reveal/sync flows, managed-editor routing, and command-resolution concerns. Add a focused child bead for that seam instead of leaving it implicit.
- The plan should keep deprioritizing test-file breakup, because the repo-wide Nushell total is large enough that production/control-plane reduction matters more than cosmetic test splitting.

2026-04-08 shortlist refinement after the hotspot scan:
- `utils/common.nu` and `utils/config_migrations.nu` now deserve explicit owner beads alongside `doctor.nu`, `yzx/dev.nu`, `integrations/yazi.nu`, and `setup/zellij_config_merger.nu`.
- `setup/yazi_config_merger.nu` currently belongs on the explicit acceptable-for-now side unless defect history changes, so the decomposition plan should not treat all config generators as equally urgent.
- The decomposition plan should keep aiming at mixed owners and weak contracts, not at raw line count alone.
close_reason: Completed: the oversized mixed-owner control-plane files are now narrowed into explicit contract-owned slices. common.nu, config_migrations.nu, doctor.nu, yzx/dev.nu, integrations/yazi.nu, and setup/zellij_config_merger.nu all spawned or completed focused child beads, and the remaining live hotspots are now explicit subsystem seams rather than a generic "break up big files" backlog item.

title: Split common.nu by runtime identity, repo-root, and host utility ownership
description: `nushell/scripts/utils/common.nu` currently mixes runtime-root resolution, installed-vs-repo identity, config/state path lookup, repo-root discovery, Nushell binary resolution, runtime-project materialization, build-parallelism policy, and miscellaneous host utilities. At ~528 lines it is not the biggest file in the repo, but it is one of the clearest grab-bag modules in the production control plane.
design: Delete-first plan: do not build a second `common_helpers` junk drawer. First decide which exports are true shared runtime/path ownership, which are maintainer-only repo/build helpers, and which convenience aliases should disappear entirely. Prefer moving code toward the subsystem that already owns the contract, and delete generic aliases such as broad `*_dir` compatibility wrappers where they no longer add real value.
acceptance_criteria: 1. `common.nu` no longer acts as a generic utility dump for unrelated runtime, repo, build, and process helpers. 2. Runtime identity/path helpers are separated from maintainer-only repo/build helpers, or at least one of those unrelated families is removed from `common.nu`. 3. At least one generic alias or convenience export is deleted rather than merely moved. 4. Callers become more explicit about whether they depend on runtime identity, config/state paths, repo-root discovery, or build policy.
notes:
2026-04-08 first delete-first cut landed:
- Deleted the broad runtime alias exports `get_yazelix_dir` and `require_yazelix_dir`.
- Deleted dead exports `require_yazelix_config_dir` and `is_hx_running` after confirming there were no remaining repo callsites.
- Internalized `get_yazelix_runtime_project_dir` and `get_yazelix_repo_root`, which were only used inside `common.nu` itself.
- Updated callers to use the narrower real names `get_yazelix_runtime_dir` and `require_yazelix_runtime_dir` instead of the generic aliases.
- Verification: refresh canonical suite, workspace canonical suite, doctor canonical suite, `validate_syntax.nu`, `validate_default_test_traceability.nu`, and `validate_readme_version.nu` all pass.

2026-04-08 second cut landed:
- Moved build/Nix policy out of `common.nu` into `utils/build_policy.nu`:
  - `get_yazelix_nix_config`
  - `get_max_jobs`
  - `get_max_cores`
  - `describe_build_parallelism`
- Moved runtime-project ownership out of `common.nu` into `utils/runtime_project.nu`:
  - `get_existing_yazelix_runtime_project_dir`
  - `materialize_yazelix_runtime_project_dir`
- Deleted the now-dead runtime-project internals left behind in `common.nu` after that move.
- Updated live callers and the affected refresh/workspace/maintainer test fixtures to the new module boundaries.
- Verification: refresh canonical suite, workspace canonical suite, targeted maintainer runtime-project regression, doctor canonical suite, `validate_syntax.nu`, `validate_default_test_traceability.nu`, and `validate_readme_version.nu` all pass.

2026-04-08 third cut landed:
- Moved writable repo-checkout detection out of `common.nu` into `utils/repo_checkout.nu`.
- `common.nu` no longer owns maintainer checkout resolution; it now stays focused on runtime/config/state identity plus shared path helpers.
- Updated maintainer workflow owners and the defending maintainer regression to import `require_yazelix_repo_root` from `repo_checkout.nu` instead of `common.nu`.
- Measured reduction: `common.nu` dropped to 270 lines after the repo-checkout move.
- Verification: `test_maintainer_repo_root_prefers_checkout_over_installed_runtime_env`, the issue-sync/update maintainer regressions, `validate_syntax.nu`, and `validate_default_test_traceability.nu` all pass.
close_reason: common.nu now focuses on runtime/config/state identity while repo checkout, runtime-project, and build policy ownership live in dedicated modules

title: Separate config_migrations.nu rule registry from planning and apply surfaces
description: `nushell/scripts/utils/config_migrations.nu` is nearly 1k lines and currently mixes canonical migration rule data, per-rule planning logic, plan assembly, preview rendering, rule validation, and apply entrypoints. That makes it hard to reason about what is durable migration metadata versus executable migration behavior, and it muddies the later Rust-shim discussion.
design: Delete-first plan: do not invent a second migration engine or generic framework. First separate what is data contract from what is plan/apply behavior, and delete any avoidable overlap between preview rendering, plan assembly, and per-rule helper logic before extracting modules. Keep one coherent migration engine, but make the registry/metadata owner and the execution owner explicit.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Migration rule metadata has a clearer owner than the full planning/apply engine. 2. Planning, preview rendering, and apply behavior are separated enough that future work can change one without rediscovering the whole file. 3. The refactor does not introduce a second migration engine or duplicate registry. 4. Follow-up Rust or spec work has a narrower seam to target than the current monolith.
notes:
2026-04-08 first ownership cut landed:
- Moved preview/load ownership out of `config_migrations.nu` into `utils/config_migration_preview.nu`:
  - `get_config_migration_plan`
  - `render_config_migration_plan`
  - the release-context formatting helper used only by preview rendering
- Updated `entrypoint_config_migrations.nu` and `yzx/config.nu` to import preview/load behavior from the new module while keeping plan/apply behavior in `config_migrations.nu`.
- Verification: targeted `yzx config migrate` preview/apply regressions, the startup migration-warning regression, `validate_config_migration_rules.nu`, `validate_upgrade_contract.nu`, `validate_syntax.nu`, and `validate_default_test_traceability.nu` all pass.

2026-04-08 second ownership cut landed:
- Moved the canonical rule registry and metadata validator out of `config_migrations.nu` into `utils/config_migration_rules.nu`.
- Updated `config_migrations.nu` to import rule data instead of owning it, keeping the file focused on per-rule planning, plan assembly, and apply behavior.
- Updated `entrypoint_config_migrations.nu`, `yzx/config.nu`, `validate_config_migration_rules.nu`, and `validate_upgrade_contract.nu` to import validation/rule metadata from the new module.
- Result: the migration registry, preview surface, and execution engine now have distinct owners without introducing a second migration engine.
- Verification: targeted `yzx config migrate` preview/apply regressions, the startup migration-warning regression, `validate_config_migration_rules.nu`, `validate_upgrade_contract.nu`, `validate_syntax.nu`, and `validate_default_test_traceability.nu` all pass.
close_reason: Registry, preview, and execution ownership are now separated cleanly

title: Decide whether shell-hook v1-v3 migration support is still required
description: Question whether Yazelix still needs to detect and migrate v1/v2/v3 managed shell-hook sections, or whether support can be narrowed to the current v4 surface plus explicit manual recovery guidance.
design: Start from the requirement, not the implementation. Check what the repo actually promises about backward shell-hook compatibility, what upgrade horizon is still considered supported, and whether old hook generations are still likely enough to justify carrying marker constants, detection branches, and migration code. Prefer deleting unsupported generations entirely over rephrasing the same compatibility burden.
acceptance_criteria: 1. There is an explicit decision on whether shell-hook v1/v2/v3 migration remains supported. 2. If support is retained, the reason is documented clearly. 3. If support is dropped, the code and docs are simplified accordingly and the surviving repair path is explicit.
notes:
2026-04-08 step-1 evidence:
- `config_manager.nu`, `shell_hooks.nu`, and `constants.nu` still carry explicit v1/v2/v3 shell-hook markers and migration branches.
- Repo-local docs/specs currently talk about shell hooks in general, but the audit did not find a clear user-facing promise that v1/v2/v3 hook generations remain supported indefinitely.
- This makes old hook-generation support a legitimate requirement question instead of an assumed permanent contract.
close_reason: Current shell-hook support narrowed to v4 plus manual legacy cleanup

title: Split zellij.nu by pane-orchestrator client, workspace routing, and editor-pane launch ownership
description: The file nushell/scripts/integrations/zellij.nu still mixes several production owners: pane-orchestrator command transport, workspace-root/tab routing, floating-wrapper env assembly, and managed editor pane opening. The goal is not cosmetic file breakup; it is to stop one module from owning both the stable pane client contract and the editor/workspace policy that sits on top of it.
design: Delete-first plan: question which exports are actual external contracts first. Prefer deleting or internalizing wrapper-level helpers such as internal tab-name shaping before extracting modules. The likely stable seams are: pane-orchestrator client transport/response parsing, workspace routing, floating wrapper env assembly, and managed editor pane opening.
acceptance_criteria: 1. zellij.nu stops owning at least one unrelated subsystem family directly. 2. Any surviving exports map to clearer external contracts than the current grab-bag surface. 3. At least one wrapper-level or convenience surface is deleted or internalized rather than merely moved. 4. Workspace and generated-config regressions still defend the surviving behavior.
notes:
2026-04-09 completion notes:
- Split floating wrapper env assembly and popup/menu wrapper launching into `integrations/zellij_runtime_wrappers.nu`.
- `integrations/zellij.nu` now depends on that owner instead of exporting `build_floating_wrapper_env_args`, `get_floating_wrapper_env`, `get_new_editor_pane_launch_env`, and `open_floating_runtime_wrapper` itself.
- Deleted additional wrapper-only convenience surface from `integrations/zellij.nu`: `next_layout_family`, `previous_layout_family`, and `toggle_sidebar_layout`. The small zellij wrapper scripts now call `run_pane_orchestrator_command_raw` directly.
- Verification: popup canonical suite passed, workspace canonical suite passed, `validate_syntax.nu` passed, and `validate_default_test_traceability.nu` passed.
close_reason: Completed

title: Split yazi_config_merger.nu by renderers, bundled asset sync, and user-config ownership
description: `nushell/scripts/setup/yazi_config_merger.nu` is still a large mixed-owner production file. It currently owns at least four different concerns at once: user-config reconciliation and legacy-path migration, bundled runtime asset syncing (plugins, flavors, starship config), generated-file rendering (`yazi.toml`, `theme.toml`, `keymap.toml`, `init.lua`), and generic merge policy helpers such as deep TOML merge and keymap append semantics. The goal is not cosmetic file breakup; it is to question whether those requirements belong under one owner at all and then delete or narrow cross-responsibility coupling before any extraction.
notes:
2026-04-09 user-override owner split landed:
- Moved Yazi user override resolution and merge policy into `setup/yazi_user_overrides.nu`.
- `yazi_config_merger.nu` now imports `resolve_yazi_user_file`, `merge_yazi_toml_config`, and `merge_yazi_keymap` instead of owning duplicate user-config reconciliation and merge semantics itself.
- Measured reduction: `yazi_config_merger.nu` is now 374 lines, down from 659 before the current bead started.
- Verification: generated-config canonical tests pass, `validate_syntax.nu` passes, and `validate_default_test_traceability.nu` passes.
close_reason: Renderer coordination now separated from Yazi asset sync and user override ownership

title: Split terminal_configs.nu by shared terminal policy, Ghostty-specific assets, and terminal renderers
description: `nushell/scripts/utils/terminal_configs.nu` is still a mixed-owner production file. It currently bundles shared terminal policy (branding, transparency, user override paths), Ghostty-specific cursor/shader/effect logic, concrete per-terminal renderers, and the top-level generation workflow that copies shader assets and writes generated files. The goal is not to port everything or explode it into helpers; it is to question which of those responsibilities should really share one owner and to delete convenience exports before any larger split.
notes:
2026-04-09 delete-first cut landed:
- Removed backup writes for files under `~/.local/share/yazelix/configs/terminal_emulators/*`.
- Generated terminal configs are Yazelix-owned outputs, so rewriting them in place is the simpler contract; user-owned terminal tweaks still live under `~/.config/yazelix/user_configs/terminal/*`.
- Verification: the bundled terminal generation regression now runs twice and asserts there is no `.yazelix-backup` noise in the generated terminal tree; syntax and traceability validators pass.
close_reason: Completed

title: Add Mandelbrot Seahorse camera tour
description: The current Mandelbrot loop has good color but keeps the self-similar point centered, so the visible motion reads as one centered spinning spiral. Replace the fixed-center camera with a periodic local-coordinate tour while preserving the Seahorse/Misiurewicz loop backbone and a clean seam.
design: Use in-house/std only. Keep the Misiurewicz multiplier as the backbone. Add a deterministic local tour offset applied before the multiplier: point = anchor + multiplier * (base + tour_offset(progress)). The offset must be zero at loop boundaries so frame 0 and loop frame remain exact. Prefer smooth piecewise interpolation through named Seahorse-region waypoints over random drift. Remove or sharply reduce hash seam blending because it causes visual teleport/noise.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Mandelbrot view center is no longer static over the loop. 2. Camera moves through valley entrance, side tendrils, bulb boundary, spiral arm, and dense filament field via local tour offsets. 3. Frame 0 and frame MANDELBROT_LOOP_FRAMES remain identical. 4. Hash-based seam blend is removed or reduced enough not to create teleport artifacts. 5. Add behavior tests proving the view center moves and visible mass crosses pane regions over the loop. 6. Existing mandelbrot screen tests pass.
close_reason: Implemented periodic Seahorse camera tour with clean ordered seam and behavior tests

title: Evaluate cursor status widget ownership with yazelix-cursors
description: The status-bar cursor widget renders mono and split cursor previews from Yazelix launch facts inside zellij_commands/status.rs. Decide whether any generic cursor preview formatting belongs in yazelix-cursors, or whether it should remain integrated because it depends on Yazelix launch-scoped facts and zjstatus command output constraints.
design: Start from status_bar_ownership.md, standalone_ghostty_cursor_distribution.md, and cursor widget tests. Classify pure cursor display primitives versus Yazelix-specific cache/env/session facts. Prefer keeping this integrated unless moving a small pure formatter to yazelix-cursors reduces duplication without forcing zjstatus or Yazelix cache concepts into the cursor child repo.
acceptance_criteria: A decision records keep/move/reject for cursor status preview formatting, expected main-repo LOC impact, child-repo API risk, and verification. If moving is accepted, create an implementation bead with exact files and tests.
notes: Decision: keep the cursor status widget in Yazelix core. The widget renders launch-scoped cursor/session/cache facts for zjstatus command output, while yazelix-cursors should remain responsible for cursor schemes/assets and non-Zellij cursor distribution. Moving this now would push Yazelix cache and zjstatus semantics into the child repo for little LOC benefit. Recorded as SBO-006 in docs/contracts/status_bar_ownership.md.
close_reason: Completed

title: Fix Home Manager option-doc store-path warning
description: Home Manager option documentation can warn that options.json references the Yazelix flake source store path without proper context. Stabilize the Yazelix module declaration path so third-party option metadata does not leak /nix/store/...-source into generated option docs.
notes: Follow-up after local Home Manager switch: the visible options.json warning path was the root Home Manager flake's nixpkgs source (/nix/store/...-source), not the Yazelix source. The repo change still stabilizes Yazelix module declaration metadata, but the remaining warning is from Home Manager's generated manual/options docs. Local setup workaround applied in /home/lucca/.config/home-manager/home.nix: manual.manpages.enable = false.
close_reason: Completed: Home Manager module now sets a stable logical _file declaration path, and the config-surface validator defends that programs.yazelix option metadata no longer leaks source paths into options.json.
