title: Fix upgrade contract CI guarded-change rule
description: CI can fail a guarded config change even when the unreleased upgrade-notes entry already acknowledges the guarded path. Make the validator enforce the documented source-of-truth rule: missing acknowledgement fails; acknowledged guarded paths should not force a changelog edit.
notes: Follow-up: split the runtime integration lane from validate so the two CI lanes run in parallel on runtime-impacting changes, removed Magic Nix Cache from routine integration, and reused the built maintainer validator in integration/cold_install.
close_reason: Completed: removed Magic Nix Cache from the always-on validate lane, built the maintainer validator once, reused the binary across separate steps, and capped validate at 12 minutes.

title: Scope Zellij clear-defaults ownership to managed sidecar
description:
A regression from the clear-defaults implementation: if ~/.config/yazelix/zellij.kdl is absent and Yazelix reads ~/.config/zellij/config.kdl as the read-only native fallback, a native `keybinds clear-defaults=true` header is treated as a full Yazelix keybinding-ownership opt-in. That drops Yazelix integration keybindings such as Alt+Shift+M and reverts side walking to native MoveFocus/MoveFocusOrTab, which can focus hidden managed sidebar panes.

Expected: `keybinds clear-defaults=true` disables Yazelix override keybinding injection only when declared in the explicit Yazelix-managed sidecar ~/.config/yazelix/zellij.kdl. Native fallback config remains a read-only base convenience and still receives Yazelix integration keybindings.
acceptance_criteria: When the base source is managed zellij.kdl and it declares keybinds clear-defaults=true, generated config preserves clear-defaults and skips Yazelix keybind overrides. When the base source is native ~/.config/zellij/config.kdl and it declares keybinds clear-defaults=true, generated config emits ordinary keybinds with Yazelix overrides appended, including toggle_transient_pane and sidebar-aware focus bindings. Docs explain the source distinction. The local generated runtime config is regenerated so Alt+Shift+M is present again after a fresh session.
notes: Implemented fix: native fallback ~/.config/zellij/config.kdl clear-defaults no longer suppresses Yazelix integration keybindings; only explicit managed ~/.config/yazelix/zellij.kdl clear-defaults preserves full user-owned keybinding mode. Added regression coverage for native fallback vs managed sidecar behavior, bumped Zellij generation fingerprint schema to force regeneration, and regenerated local runtime config. Verification: yzx dev rust fmt --check; yzx dev rust test zellij_materialization; yzx dev rust check; local zellij-materialization.generate reported reused=false and generated Alt Shift M plus sidebar-aware Alt h/l handlers.
close_reason: Fixed and verified native fallback clear-defaults keybinding regression

title: Split shared terminal cursor materialization from Ghostty config
description: Shared cursor shader state is now consumed by yzxterm as well as Ghostty. Move reusable cursor state/path/sync logic out of ghostty_materialization.rs so the module name no longer implies yzxterm depends on Ghostty terminal config ownership.
close_reason: Completed: split shared cursor shader state/path/sync logic into terminal_cursor_materialization.rs, left ghostty_materialization.rs focused on Ghostty config, and moved yzxterm materialization to the shared cursor path. Verified with cargo fmt --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml --all --check, cargo check --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core, and focused yzxterm/Ghostty terminal materialization tests.

title: README: document popup toggle-close refreshes Yazi git state
description: Update README user-facing popup documentation so it explains that the managed popup, usually lazygit, refreshes the Yazi sidebar git state when it closes, including the toggle-close path triggered by Alt+t.
close_reason: Completed: README now documents that the managed popup, usually lazygit, refreshes Yazi sidebar git state when it closes, including the Alt+t toggle-close path.

title: Make canonical contracts replace bloated specs folder
description: Turn the current docs/specs surface into a canonical Yazelix contracts surface. The folder should contain durable product, runtime, subsystem, integration, and validation contracts only, not research notes, implementation diaries, migration plans, or Bead-driven planning history.
design: Preferred direction: rename docs/specs to docs/contracts unless implementation discovers a stronger existing naming constraint. Contracts should be normative and current: what Yazelix promises, which subsystem owns it, and which checks defend it. Beads should own decisions, investigations, rejected alternatives, implementation sequencing, and closure evidence. Contract files should not mention Bead ids or follow-up beads except for contracts whose subject is Beads/GitHub/planning architecture. Update AGENTS.md, spec-driven workflow docs, inventories, validators, and references so the source-of-truth rule is enforced mechanically where practical.
acceptance_criteria: docs/specs is renamed or replaced by a canonical contracts surface; live contract files are rewritten to remove research/prototype/history/follow-up content; weak planning/evaluation markdown is moved into Bead notes, normal docs, or deleted; canonical contracts no longer reference Bead ids except Beads-specific contracts; AGENTS.md and spec workflow guidance reflect the new Beads-to-contracts direction; validators/tests/docs reference the new path; spec_inventory is replaced with a contracts inventory that contains only canonical contracts; validation passes without stale docs/specs references.
close_reason: Completed: canonical contracts now replace the bloated specs folder with a delete-first docs architecture, workflow, validation gate, and cleaned inventory.

title: Delete-first docs information architecture boundary
description: Define the smallest durable Yazelix documentation architecture after the canonical contracts cleanup. The goal is to delete or demote ambiguous documentation categories before renaming, reorganizing, or adding navigation layers.
design: Delete-first scope: question whether each current docs category deserves to exist as a source of truth. Eliminate duplicate source-of-truth surfaces before moving files. The surviving taxonomy should separate README/front door, user guides, maintainer guides, canonical contracts, architecture maps/inventories, historical archives, and generated/reference data. Prefer fewer top-level concepts over preserving every current folder or document type.
acceptance_criteria: A short docs information-architecture decision exists in the bead notes or a surviving docs architecture file; duplicate categories are identified for deletion or demotion; docs/contracts ownership is distinguished from user guides, maintainer docs, history, and inventories; no new docs category is introduced unless an existing ambiguous category is removed or merged; follow-up implementation work can use the taxonomy without re-litigating the folder model.
close_reason: Completed delete-first docs information architecture boundary in docs/documentation_architecture.md and linked it from the architecture map

title: Add delete-first canonical contracts validation gate
description: Add a validator that keeps the canonical contracts surface from accumulating planning, research, migration, and Bead-traceability content again.
design: Delete-first scope: remove the old permissive spec validator assumptions before adding new checks. Replace the requirement that every spec names a Bead with the inverse rule for canonical contracts: contracts should not mention Beads unless the contract is specifically about Beads/GitHub/planning architecture. Reject or quarantine planning terms such as Follow-Up Beads, Prototype Outcome, Evaluation, Historical planning note, and broad research diary sections in docs/contracts. Prefer a small denylist plus explicit allowlist over a complicated classifier.
acceptance_criteria: The validator no longer requires Bead traceability for canonical contracts; docs/contracts files are checked for stale docs/specs references, Bead IDs, follow-up bead sections, and planning/evaluation language unless allowlisted; Beads-specific contracts can be exempted explicitly; existing tests are updated to defend the new rule; validation fails clearly when a weak planning file is added to the canonical surface.
close_reason: Completed: validate-contracts now checks docs/contracts for stale docs/specs paths, Bead ids, planning markers, and contract verification hygiene; focused Rust tests defend the new failure modes.

title: Delete stale spec workflow guidance in favor of contracts workflow
description: Collapse the old spec-driven workflow guidance into a smaller contracts workflow that matches the canonical contracts direction.
design: Delete-first scope: remove or merge docs/spec_driven_workflow.md and docs/contract_driven_development.md instead of maintaining parallel guidance. Update AGENTS.md so Beads point to contracts, while contracts stay planning-system-independent. Delete instructions that say canonical files must include Bead IDs. Keep only the surviving guidance needed to decide when a durable contract is warranted, where it lives, and how it is defended.
acceptance_criteria: There is one surviving workflow entrypoint for canonical contracts; stale spec-driven wording is removed or redirected; AGENTS.md no longer instructs agents to put Bead IDs in canonical contracts; docs and validators agree on the new docs/contracts path; no duplicate workflow document keeps old docs/specs semantics alive.
close_reason: Completed: deleted the stale spec workflow entrypoint, made contract_driven_development.md the canonical workflow, and updated AGENTS, README, CI, and docs references to the contracts surface.

title: Delete live-contract pollution from historical audits and evaluations
description: Remove historical audits, evaluations, migration diaries, and planning notes from the canonical contracts surface so only current Yazelix contracts remain.
design: Delete-first scope: classify current docs/specs files by whether they are live contracts, duplicate docs, historical audits, evaluation records, migration plans, or Bead-owned implementation notes. Delete weak files when they have no live contract value. Move only genuinely useful historical prose into an explicit non-canonical archive or Bead notes. Rewrite surviving files into small current-tense contracts instead of preserving chronology.
acceptance_criteria: Historical/pre-trim/evaluation/audit files are no longer present in the canonical contracts folder; weak files are deleted rather than renamed when their content is already captured by Beads or current docs; any archive location is explicitly non-canonical; surviving contracts use current normative language and omit implementation diary content; docs/contracts inventory lists only live contracts.
close_reason: Completed: removed planning, evaluation, audit, template, and migration diary files from the canonical surface; renamed surviving live contracts into docs/contracts and rebuilt the inventory.

title: Canonicalize welcome and screen renderer ownership
description: Define the surviving front-door welcome and yzx screen product surface, then delete stale renderer ownership in nushell/scripts/setup/welcome.nu and nushell/scripts/utils/ascii_art.nu without losing real UX behavior.
acceptance_criteria: There is one explicit surviving welcome/screen style contract, random semantics are honest, stale renderer surfaces are deleted or demoted, and the surviving owner is smaller than the current welcome.nu plus ascii_art.nu stack while preserving yzx screen, width-aware rendering, interactive skip behavior, and no implicit user-config takeover.
notes: Deletion class: full_owner_migration. Created from yazelix-rdn7.5.5 setup/shellhook/welcome/terminal audit. Stop if the work only ports 1000 lines of renderer code into Rust without deleting stale styles, dead exports, or ambiguous random-style semantics.
close_reason: all steps complete

title: Define the retained welcome and yzx screen style contract and deletion budget
description: Decide which welcome and yzx screen styles actually survive, what random means, which behaviors are contract-critical, and how much of ascii_art.nu and welcome.nu can honestly be deleted instead of preserved behind a rewrite.
acceptance_criteria: The retained style surface is explicit, random semantics are either restored or deleted honestly, the executable defenses and spec gaps are named, and the deletion budget states exactly which ascii_art.nu and welcome.nu owners must disappear before the lane counts as success.
notes:
Deletion class: full_owner_migration. Preserve interactive skip, width-aware rendering, game_of_life screen continuity, and clear welcome gating. Question whether logo, boids, and the current random aliases deserve to survive as product features.
Retained the explicit style surface: static welcome only, logo and boids as opt-in live styles, three named Game of Life variants, and random constrained to the Game of Life pool. Defined the deletion budget for 7krc.2: remove welcome config reparsing and collapse the duplicated ascii_art style-policy owner cluster into one canonical style table.
close_reason: Completed

title: Delete stale welcome renderer surfaces or move the surviving renderer to the canonical owner
description: After the style contract lands, delete stale welcome/screen renderer surfaces end to end and keep only the canonical surviving owner for front-door rendering.
acceptance_criteria: The retained welcome/screen behavior survives with stronger direct tests, stale styles or dead exports are deleted or demoted, and the surviving renderer/data owner is materially smaller and clearer than the current Nushell stack.
notes:
Deletion class: full_owner_migration. Success is measured by deleting real Nushell owner logic from ascii_art.nu and/or welcome.nu, not by introducing a second renderer stack or preserving stale styles as compatibility aliases.
Completed the owner cut from docs/specs/welcome_screen_style_contract.md: welcome.nu no longer imports config_parser.nu or reparses config for session/terminal facts; ascii_art.nu now derives welcome/screen/random style policy from one STYLE_CATALOG; the stale get_animated_ascii_art export is deleted; direct FRONT-005 coverage now defends explicit startup facts.
close_reason: Completed

title: Evaluate Rust ownership for yzx cwd and yzx reveal
description: Define whether core/yzx_workspace.nu is the next honest public-family deletion cut after the 5ewl Clap no-go, with a delete-first budget that only counts if Rust becomes the single public owner for both workspace commands.
notes:
Decision on 2026-04-21:
- go for a real public-owner deletion lane
- yzx cwd and yzx reveal are the best remaining public-family cut because pane-orchestrator session truth already owns the live workspace/sidebar identity they depend on
- this only counts if Rust becomes the single public owner for both commands and core/yzx_workspace.nu is deleted
- do not count a thin Rust shim that shells back into integrations/zellij.nu, integrations/yazi.nu, or the old workspace module
- preserve current behavior: clear outside-Zellij failures, zoxide-or-path target resolution for yzx cwd, managed editor retarget status, sidebar Yazi sync/focus behavior, and direct public-root routing for both commands
- preferred implementation posture is in-house/std plus existing serde_json; no broad new crates are justified for this cut
- this is a better delete-first continuation than Clap because it removes two surviving INTERNAL_NU_FAMILIES roots instead of re-encoding the same routing table
close_reason: Completed: evaluated the workspace command owner seam, recorded a go decision for a real Rust public-owner cut, and created follow-up beads for the yzx cwd / yzx reveal deletion lane.

title: Extract yazelix_bar into a child repository
description: Turn the existing standalone yazelix_bar package surface into a real first-party child repository so non-Yazelix users can consume the bar without adopting the full Yazelix workspace, while Yazelix continues to integrate it by default.
design: Build on the completed in-repo standalone bar preset work. The extraction should preserve zjstatus-native capabilities instead of flattening everything into a Yazelix-only generator. Keep generic zjstatus widgets and AI/tool widgets reusable, keep Yazelix-specific workspace/status-bus widgets behind clear optional integration, and make the main repo consume the child repo through normal flake/package inputs rather than copied generated artifacts. The extraction only counts as successful if the main Yazelix repo loses meaningful ownership or generated clutter, not merely if a second repo exists.
acceptance_criteria: The child repo exists with build/test/docs sufficient for standalone Zellij users; the main repo consumes it as an external first-party input; default Yazelix behavior remains integrated; non-Yazelix users get a minimal install/config path; generic, AI, and Yazelix-specific widget boundaries are documented; and the LOC scorecard reports main-repo deletion or explicitly states any deferred deletion debt.
close_reason: Completed: extracted yazelix_bar into public child repo luccahuguet/yazelix-bar at c71d39ba91826d5c1406c95cc12872a31a81d62d, forwarded the main flake package to the child, switched yazelix_core to the pinned child crate, removed local bar package/source/presets, and updated docs/contracts. Verified child cargo test and nix build; main cargo check, focused Zellij tests, contracts/docs/flake validators, runtime build, and forwarded bar package build. Follow-ups: yazelix-zc6q and yazelix-dlkg.

title: General movement should skip the managed sidebar
description: Adjust general movement/navigation so the managed sidebar is skipped by default. Users should reach the sidebar explicitly through sidebar-focused commands like Ctrl+y, not by incidental workspace movement.
design: After sidebar focus behavior is made correct, consider making general movement treat the sidebar as skippable by default, but only if it falls out naturally from the current Zellij/plugin model. The sidebar should still be reachable through explicit sidebar commands, but ordinary pane movement should prioritize the main workspace panes. If this requires brittle special cases, layout-specific hacks, or substantial new focus state, it is not worth the complexity.
acceptance_criteria: General movement no longer lands on the managed sidebar unless the user uses an explicit sidebar-targeting action, but only if the implementation is straightforward and low-complexity. Add focused tests for the affected movement bindings if implemented; otherwise keep the current explicit-access model and close the issue as not worth the added complexity.
close_reason: Alt+h and Alt+l now use pane-orchestrator walking that skips the managed sidebar when it is closed.

title: Remove README Historical Context section
description: Remove the long Historical Context section from the main README so the repository front door stays focused on current-state install, update, usage, compatibility, and command surfaces.
acceptance_criteria: README no longer contains the Historical Context heading or the long Classic/v15/v16 narrative block. Deeper historical docs remain available from docs/README.md unless separately requested. README validators pass.
close_reason: Completed: removed the long Historical Context section from README while leaving deeper historical docs reachable from docs/README.md. Verified the section text is absent from README, git diff --check, validate-docs-experience, and validate-readme-version.

title: Make Yazi-to-Helix open robust to command-mode key remaps
description:
## Steps to Reproduce
1. In a Yazelix-managed Helix setup, remap Helix command mode away from ':' such as mapping ';' to command_mode and ':' to collapse_selection.
2. Open a file from the Yazi sidebar into the managed Helix editor.
3. Repeat the Yazi-to-Helix open flow.
4. Observe whether Yazelix's literal ':open ...' command enters command mode or gets typed into the buffer.

## Expected Behavior
Managed Yazi-to-Helix open either uses the configured command-mode entry contract or fails visibly with a doctor/config warning.

## Actual Behavior
The command text can be inserted into or corrupt the current editor buffer when ':' no longer enters command mode.

## Existing Context
A tester reported that remapping Helix ';' to command_mode and ':' to collapse_selection breaks repeated Yazi Enter opens: Yazelix's managed editor handoff sends literal Helix commands like ':open ...', so ':' must still enter command mode. With that remap, the command text can be inserted/corrupt the current buffer instead of opening the selected file.
acceptance_criteria: The decision/fix documents the supported contract for Helix command-mode keybindings in Yazelix-managed panes. Either Yazelix defends ':' as command_mode for managed Helix sessions, exposes an explicit configured command-mode key for pane-orchestrator open/cd commands, or rejects unsupported remaps with a clear doctor/config warning. Regression coverage demonstrates that Yazi-to-Helix file open cannot silently type command text into the editor buffer when the command-mode key contract is violated.
notes:
Zed lesson link 2026-05-05: docs/zed_architecture_lessons.md connects this bug to the future action registry. The robust fix should consume semantic Yazelix actions and backend diagnostics rather than depending on fragile editor command-mode key sequences.
Rust dependency decision before implementation: no new production or dev crates. Use existing toml/serde_json and in-house Helix config materialization logic. The fix is a managed-config contract change: after merging user Helix overrides, Yazelix should enforce the normal-mode `:` binding as `command_mode` because the Yazi-to-Helix opener sends command text through that key. Rejected alternatives: add a broad Helix remote-control dependency, parse arbitrary native Helix keymaps for conflict resolution, or introduce a new backend command transport before one exists upstream. Packaging impact: no new dependency; no pane-orchestrator wasm source change unless the command transport itself changes.
Implemented fix: managed Helix materialization now enforces `[keys.normal] ":" = "command_mode"` after merging `~/.config/yazelix/helix.toml`, alongside the existing `A-r` reveal binding. This defends the current Yazi-to-Helix transport, which sends Escape and then literal `:cd`/`:open` commands to the running Helix pane. Users can add another command-mode key, but managed Helix sessions cannot repurpose `:` without breaking Yazelix-owned open/cwd flows.

Doctor now treats generated Helix configs as stale when either the reveal binding or the `:` command-mode binding is missing or wrong. Updated the keybinding ownership contract and Helix keybinding docs to record this supported boundary. No pane-orchestrator wasm source changed.

Verification: yzx dev rust fmt --check; yzx dev rust check; yzx dev rust test helix_materialization; yzx dev rust test doctor_helix_report; cargo run --quiet --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_maintainer --bin yzx_repo_validator -- validate-contracts; git diff --check.
close_reason: Completed: managed Helix now enforces ':' as command_mode and doctor flags stale generated configs missing that binding.

title: Investigate Zed as a Rust/editor architecture reference
description: Investigate what Yazelix can directly use from, adapt from, or be inspired by in Zed, the open-source Rust IDE. This is a research bead, not approval to vendor Zed code or rebuild Yazelix as an IDE. The outcome should identify practical, Yazelix-shaped lessons from Zed's architecture, settings UX, command/action model, project/workspace model, terminal/task integration, editor/LSP integration, extension model, responsiveness model, and Rust code organization.
design:
Approach from first principles: Yazelix is a terminal workspace built around Zellij, Yazi, editors, shells, and generated config; Zed is a full GUI IDE. Compare only the parts that solve similar product problems.

Evaluate at least:
- Settings UX and schema-backed discoverability
- Command palette/action registry/keybinding resolution
- Project/workspace/session model
- Terminal/task integration and environment handling
- Editor/LSP integration boundaries and diagnostics UX
- Extension/plugin architecture
- Performance/responsiveness patterns in Rust async/UI code
- Testing and contributor workflow patterns
- Any reusable crates or small code patterns, subject to license and packaging review

Classify findings as: use directly, adapt conceptually, inspire only, reject, or defer. Be explicit about license compatibility, Nix/package impact, complexity cost, and whether each idea belongs in Yazelix core, pane orchestrator, config UI, docs, or a future standalone component.
acceptance_criteria: There is a written recommendation with a concise table of Zed areas reviewed, what Yazelix should learn from each, and why. The recommendation must separate direct code reuse from architectural inspiration, include license and packaging risk, identify 3-7 high-value follow-up beads when applicable, and reject ideas that would make Yazelix less terminal-native or too IDE-like. If no direct reuse is worthwhile, the bead still closes with concrete design lessons for the config UI, command surface, workspace/session model, or Rust architecture.
notes:
Research checked 2026-05-05 against current Zed public docs/blog and upstream repo metadata: zed.dev docs for settings/keybindings, Zed's settings UI rebuild blog, and zed-industries/zed root license files. Zed is Rust-first but is a GUI IDE; direct product or architecture copying would make Yazelix less terminal-native. Root repo carries GPL/AGPL/Apache license files, so direct code reuse should be rejected unless a specific Apache-licensed subcomponent is separately reviewed. Treat Zed primarily as product/architecture inspiration.

Findings:
1. Settings UI: Zed explicitly moved from JSON-only discoverability to a settings editor while keeping the settings file as a power-user surface. This strongly supports Yazelix's ryx4 direction: settings.jsonc remains the source of truth, but the UI should show defaults, absent/new settings, descriptions, and validation states.
2. Settings model: Zed's post describes consolidating scattered settings into strong types around UserSettings/ProjectSettings and treating files as the organizing principle. Yazelix should keep semantic settings centralized in settings.jsonc/schema/contract metadata rather than scattering UI metadata through runtime code.
3. UI architecture: Zed rejected macro-heavy UI metadata that tangled UI and core crates. Yazelix should avoid putting Ratatui widget details into config-normalization code. Keep a pure config inventory model first, then render it with Ratatui.
4. Keybindings: Zed has base_keymap choices, a separate keymap file, contextual bindings, explicit null unbinds, and a key context view for debugging. This maps cleanly to yazelix-v4rp: Yazelix should distinguish default/merge/user-owned modes and should eventually provide a binding/context diagnostic instead of forcing users to infer why Ctrl-g or Alt-z behaves a certain way.
5. Actions: Zed's command palette and keymap editor revolve around action ids. Yazelix should not treat keybindings as raw KDL snippets forever; the friendly future surface should be action ids with generated Zellij/Yazi/Helix bindings where possible.
6. Project/workspace settings: Zed separates global and project settings. Yazelix should defer project-local settings until the main config UI and XDG/native ownership story are stable; introducing project overlays now would complicate session snapshots and Home Manager ownership.
7. Terminal/task/editor internals: Zed's terminal/task/editor architecture is not directly reusable because Yazelix deliberately composes Zellij/Yazi/editors/shells instead of replacing them.

Recommendation: no direct Zed code reuse now. Use Zed as a reference for settings UI, action/keymap diagnostics, and file-scoped config architecture. Concrete follow-ups are already represented by yazelix-ryx4.1, yazelix-ryx4.2, yazelix-ryx4.3, yazelix-v4rp, and yazelix-hg3a; a future diagnostic bead should be created after v4rp if the ownership-mode design accepts a key context/debug view.
close_reason: Completed investigation: Zed should inspire Yazelix's settings UI, centralized config inventory, action/keymap model, and keybinding diagnostics, but direct code reuse is rejected for now due product mismatch, licensing review burden, and terminal-native scope.

title: Convert live prose specs to indexed contract items
description: Audit outcome: only a small subset of live specs currently use indexed contract items, while many live contracts still exist as prose plus broad Defended by lines. Convert the highest-value live specs to item IDs before more default-suite tests are deleted or demoted.
design: Start with specs that defend default-suite tests or major owner seams: runtime_root_contract.md, runtime_dependency_preflight_contract.md, floating_tui_panes.md, status_doctor_machine_readable_reports.md, startup_profile_scenarios.md, rust_nushell_bridge_contract.md, pane_orchestrator_component.md, and test_suite_governance.md. Do not index rationale or history. Each new item needs Type, Status, Owner, Statement, Verification, and a migration-safe test/validator mapping.
acceptance_criteria: 1. A ranked migration list exists for live specs without item IDs. 2. The first batch of high-value live specs gets stable item IDs without changing behavior. 3. Default-suite tests that already defend those behaviors can be mapped to concrete Contract markers. 4. validate_specs, validate_default_test_traceability, and validate_rust_test_traceability pass. 5. Historical/planning specs are not accidentally promoted to live contracts.
notes: Completed 2026-04-22 by indexing the first high-value live spec batch: runtime_root_contract, runtime_dependency_preflight_contract, floating_tui_panes, status_doctor_machine_readable_reports, startup_profile_scenarios, rust_nushell_bridge_contract, pane_orchestrator_component, and test_suite_governance. Default-suite quarantine is drained, and follow-up bead yazelix-0qxa was created for the next workspace/session spec batch.
close_reason: Completed

title: Fix Yazelix launch hanging after Zellij config generation
description: Yazelix sometimes hangs during startup after the welcome prompt and after successfully regenerating the Zellij config. The visible stall point is after the final 'Config will auto-regenerate when source files change' line, before the Zellij UI takes over. This should be tracked separately from the stale zjstatus layout-label bug.
design: Treat this as a startup handoff bug, not a status-bar naming bug. The key boundary is the transition from Yazelix's launch/setup flow into the actual Zellij UI. Investigate whether the hang is in the included terminal path, start_yazelix_inner handoff, wrapper interaction, plugin permission state, or Zellij startup state after config generation. Avoid bundling unrelated fixes. Keep the bug scoped to restoring reliable startup first; layout-label correctness is a separate issue.
acceptance_criteria: Yazelix no longer hangs after printing that Zellij configuration was generated successfully. From a normal launch, the UI reliably transitions into the Zellij session after that point. Add regression coverage or a defended diagnostic contract around the launch handoff, and document the exact failure boundary that was fixed.
notes: March 27 fresh-state evidence: after clearing /tmp/zellij-$UID, /run/user/$UID/zellij, ~/.cache/zellij, ~/.local/share/zellij, and the permission cache, a new launch still recreated the bad state. Zellij restored only the cached zjstatus permission block; neither yazelix_pane_orchestrator.wasm nor yazelix_popup_runner.wasm reached the permission-prompt stage. The launch also spawned two separate Zellij 0.44.0 servers from a clean slate, each with its own launch_sidebar_yazi/set_workspace_root path. This means the missing Yazelix plugin permission popup is a downstream symptom, not the root cause. Current strongest boundary: duplicate server/session creation or earlier Zellij 0.44 startup corruption before Yazelix plugins can finish loading.
close_reason: Completed by the March 27 startup-handoff fixes. Commit 5043aeb (Harden Zellij startup handoff) removed the startup-time sidebar launch path that was implicated in the duplicate-server / pre-UI stall boundary, and commit 142e4c8 added an explicit permission recovery path for already-corrupted Zellij plugin state. The observed hang after successful Zellij config generation is no longer the active failure mode; any future recurrence should come back as a fresh regression with new evidence.

title: README: refresh the project front door after recent architecture and packaging changes
description:
Update the main README so it matches the current v15 Yazelix product instead of older branch-era or Classic-era assumptions. The front page should describe one live supported line, the current workspace-focused scope, the first-party Ghostty path, layouts, and the fast popup-pane and command-menu experience.

This bead also owns the README-side release-surface cleanup: the README should stop implying that v14 remains supported, that dynamic runtime management is still in scope, or that the front page should duplicate release history.
design:
Treat this as a front-door documentation pass, not a changelog dump. The README should explain what Yazelix is today, how the core workspace UX hangs together, and where to go for deeper docs. It should present v15 as the only supported line and explain the surviving shape honestly: workspace entry, layouts, first-party Ghostty, Yazi/Helix/Zellij integration, fast toggleable popup and menu panes, and the trimmed runtime surface behind them.

Do not preserve the old two-lines story, the recommendation to stay on v14, or wording that treats dynamic runtime management as a feature Yazelix still owns. The README should not act like a second changelog; release history belongs in CHANGELOG.md, docs/upgrade_notes.toml, and docs/history.md. It should also avoid overexposing maintainer-only internals on the front page while still linking to architecture docs for readers who want them.
acceptance_criteria: The README presents v15 as the live supported Yazelix line, accurately explains the current feature set and architecture at a front-door level, includes the fast popup/menu and first-party Ghostty story where relevant, and stops implying ongoing v14 support or dynamic runtime-management scope. The README points cleanly to deeper docs instead of duplicating release notes or long historical prose, and the main commands/features referenced there are rechecked against the live v15 surface.
close_reason: Completed: README now presents v15 as the live supported line, moves the tagged-v14 block into historical context, and documents Ghostty, layouts, popup/menu, and the trimmed command surface.

title: README: remove stale v14/devenv-era wording from the v15 front door
description: The README still contains stale wording from the earlier two-lines plan, including language that treats v14/Classic as the recommended line and v15 as a future branch. Refresh the main README so it presents v15.0 as the live trimmed non-Rust Yazelix product, treats v14 as historical context only, explains that config migrations are no longer part of the live contract, and aligns the front door with the trimmed runtime contract, the built-in Ghostty path, and the fast popup/menu-centered workspace UX. Rust should be described as a later v15.x/v16 roadmap item, not as a prerequisite or headline for v15.0.
design: Keep the README as a front door, not a history dump. Preserve useful historical context where it matters, but keep that context explicitly historical. The main README should explain the current product honestly: one live line, one current command/runtime/config contract, one current recommendation to users, and a clear release framing where v15.0 is the trimmed reboot while Rust is a gradual later path toward v16.
acceptance_criteria: The main README presents v15.0 as the live trimmed Yazelix product, treats v14 as historical rather than recommended or maintained, stops implying removed runtime-local ownership, stale command surfaces, or config-migration support, and still points readers to the right deeper docs for history, architecture, and the later Rust roadmap.
close_reason: Completed: refreshed the README front door for the live v15 branch, removed stale v14-recommendation wording, clarified v15.0 as the trimmed non-Rust reboot, and aligned terminal/config migration wording.

title: Add an optional modern_js pack for Bun, Deno, and future Vite+ workflows
description: Add a dedicated optional modern_js pack instead of widening the default ts pack. The pack owns JS/TS runtime and workflow tooling, while ts stays focused on editor-facing language tooling. Vite+ remains a follow-up once it is packaged in nixpkgs.
design: Keep the boundary explicit: ts is for language servers, linting, and formatting; modern_js is for runtime and workflow tools. Implement modern_js with tools that already exist in nixpkgs and keep the pack small enough to remain an optional add-on. Document the Vite+ gap directly instead of pretending the package exists.
acceptance_criteria: Yazelix ships a documented optional modern_js pack in both yazelix_default.toml and home_manager/module.nix, the ts pack no longer owns runtime tooling that belongs in the new pack, docs explain the split, and tests defend parity across both configuration paths. If Vite+ is not yet packaged in nixpkgs, the docs explicitly say so and the bead still closes with that defended boundary.
notes: Implemented as the optional modern_js pack with bun and deno. bun was removed from the default ts pack so ts stays editor-focused, docs now explain the split, and parity tests cover both ts and modern_js. Vite+ is intentionally deferred until it is packaged in nixpkgs.
close_reason: Completed

title: Replace popup Claude examples with Codex examples
close_reason: Completed

title: Bundle optional Steel authoring tools in the runtime
design: Default-bundle pkgs.steel as a runtime authoring tool for the first-class Helix Steel surface. Export steel, steel-language-server, forge, cargo-steel-lib, and repl-connect from the bundled package. Keep plugin execution independent from these CLIs. Support runtime_tool_sources.steel = host or off for Home Manager and mkYazelix users who want host-owned tools or a smaller closure.
acceptance_criteria: Yazelix runtime includes Steel authoring commands by default. runtime_tool_sources.steel = "off" evaluates and omits the commands. runtime_tool_sources.steel = "host" evaluates and doctor can validate host requirements. Documentation names the default bundle and opt-out.
close_reason: Bundled pkgs.steel as an optional default runtime tool, exported Steel authoring commands, documented host/off controls, and added Nix customization validator coverage. Verified default runtime build and Steel command availability.

title: Prevent Mandelbrot uniform-frame regressions
description: The Seahorse valley-crop attempt made yzx screen mandelbrot spend long stretches as uniform full-block or low-detail frames. Restore the known-correct self-similar loop and add regression coverage that sampled frames retain visible variation instead of becoming mostly one glyph class.
notes: Implemented by reverting the bad Seahorse valley-crop constants back to the known-correct period-3 full-set self-similar loop. Added a regression test that samples the Mandelbrot loop and rejects frames that collapse to a single dominant visible glyph above 96%, which catches the all-full-block/all-low-detail failure from the attempted valley crop. Verification: focused Mandelbrot tests, full yazelix_screen tests, Rust test traceability validator, git diff --check.
close_reason: Reverted bad valley crop and added uniform-frame guard

title: Fix stale Nix customization component validator
description: CI validate-nix-customization-api still expects components.screen = false to be rejected, but screen and cursors are now supported component toggles. Update the validator so it rejects an actually unsupported component toggle while preserving the customization API contract.
acceptance_criteria: validate-nix-customization-api passes locally and in GitHub CI; the validator still defends rejection of unsupported component toggles; change is committed with Beads state.
close_reason: Completed: updated validate-nix-customization-api to reject an actually unsupported component toggle, components.status_bar = false, instead of the now-supported components.screen = false. Verified cargo fmt --check and direct validate-nix-customization-api.

title: Add local startup profile comparison and baseline tooling
description:
The new structured startup profiler makes local evidence possible, but there is still no first-class way to compare two runs, summarize phase deltas, or keep stable local baselines without relying on noisy hosted CI timing. That limits the practical value of the new reports for ongoing performance work.

Design:
Build on the existing structured JSONL report format rather than adding a separate metrics store. Provide a local-only surface that can diff two startup profile reports or compare a fresh run against a named baseline, highlighting phase deltas and major regressions. Keep this explicitly local and deterministic; do not turn it into a flaky GitHub Actions wall-clock gate.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. There is a user-facing local command or helper to compare two startup profile reports. 2. The comparison output highlights total wall-time delta plus per-step or per-phase deltas. 3. The tooling can work from saved reports under the Yazelix state dir without rerunning startup. 4. Docs explain the intended local workflow and explicitly avoid promising a noisy hosted-CI timing gate.
notes: Implemented local startup profile comparison tooling. Added yzx_control profile compare-reports/save-baseline/compare-baseline, user-facing yzx dev profile compare/save-baseline/compare-baseline wrappers, docs/spec updates, and a Rust regression test for total/per-step deltas. Verified focused Rust test, rust check, fmt check, spec validation, Nu syntax validation, rust test traceability, and command smoke checks against saved JSONL reports.
close_reason: Completed

title: Collapse install-ownership and launcher-resolution bridge owners
description: Delete the surviving Nushell install-ownership request-shaping and launcher-resolution seam now that Rust already owns install-ownership evaluation.
design: Keep this delete-first. Rust should become the single owner for install-ownership request construction and computed fields like stable_yzx_wrapper and desktop_launcher_path. Nushell should keep only desktop-entry writing and restart UX, not XDG/HOME/PATH inference or field projection.
acceptance_criteria: Rust becomes the single owner for env-derived install-ownership evaluation and launcher facts; nushell/scripts/utils/install_ownership.nu and nushell/scripts/utils/launcher_resolution.nu are deleted; desktop, restart, doctor, update, and home_manager behavior is preserved.
close_reason: all steps complete

title: Define the deletion budget for install-ownership and launcher-resolution bridge collapse
description: Decide the exact Rust owner shape for env-derived install-ownership evaluation and list the surviving callers that must switch cleanly.
design: Prefer one Rust env-request owner reused by doctor, update, home_manager, desktop, and restart. Reuse in-house install_ownership_env.rs and install_ownership_report.rs unless a narrower helper proves insufficient. Reject any plan that leaves duplicate HOME/XDG/PATH/'which yzx' inference in Nushell or in multiple Rust call sites.
acceptance_criteria: The lane records the exact owner cut, preserved callers, and the crate-vs-in-house decision; the implementation target is explicit enough to delete the Nu wrappers without adding a second bridge layer.
notes:
2026-04-21 deletion-budget decision:
- Production crates: none. Reuse the existing in-house Rust modules `install_ownership_report.rs`, `install_ownership_env.rs`, and existing serde/serde_json plumbing already in the crate.
- Dev-only crates: none beyond existing Rust/Nu test dependencies.
- Build in-house: add a Rust-owned `install-ownership.evaluate --from-env` path in `yzx_core`, plus an optional runtime-dir override for Nu callers that already resolved the helper runtime. Keep env-derived HOME/XDG/state/PATH/invoked-yzx request construction in `install_ownership_env.rs`.
- Rejected alternatives: keep `install_ownership.nu` as a domain bridge; move launcher-resolution policy into `yzx_core_bridge.nu`; add Clap or a formatting/process helper crate. Those would preserve or widen the bridge instead of deleting it.
- Packaging impact: no new dependencies. The runtime must continue shipping `yzx_core`; deleted Nu wrappers must be removed from routed caller expectations.
- Owner cut: Rust owns env-derived install-ownership evaluation and fields such as `stable_yzx_wrapper` and `desktop_launcher_path`. Nushell keeps only desktop-entry writes, icon installation, desktop launch UX, and restart UX.
close_reason: Recorded the delete-first owner cut and Rust dependency gate: no new crates, Rust owns env-derived install-ownership requests, and Nu keeps only desktop/restart UX.

title: Rust-own install-ownership env request construction and dedupe Rust callers
description: Make Rust the single owner for env-derived install-ownership requests and stop duplicating request construction across Rust callers.
design: Add or refine a Rust-owned from-env install-ownership path and reuse install_ownership_env.rs from doctor/update/home_manager instead of rebuilding the request shape in multiple places. Do not introduce a second request-construction seam.
acceptance_criteria: One Rust owner builds install-ownership requests from env; doctor, update, and home_manager reuse it; duplicate install-ownership request construction is removed without behavior drift.
close_reason: Added Rust-owned install-ownership.evaluate --from-env, reused install_ownership_env.rs from doctor, and removed duplicated doctor request construction.

title: Delete install_ownership.nu and launcher_resolution.nu by switching Nu callers to the Rust owner
description: Retire the remaining Nushell install-ownership wrappers after the Rust env owner exists.
design: Switch desktop install/launch and restart wrapper-resolution callers to the Rust-owned install-ownership surface. Keep Nushell only for desktop-entry writing, desktop fast-path UX, and restart UX.
acceptance_criteria: nushell/scripts/utils/install_ownership.nu and nushell/scripts/utils/launcher_resolution.nu are deleted; desktop and restart behavior is preserved; focused tests and docs/spec inventory are updated.
close_reason: Deleted install_ownership.nu and launcher_resolution.nu; desktop and restart now call the Rust-owned install-ownership.evaluate --from-env path directly.

title: Implement repeatable typed one-shot config patches
description: Implement repeatable --with key=value launch/session patches for yzx launch, yzx enter, and yzx restart. The implementation must stay schema-aware, fail hard on unknown or invalid settings, avoid creating a second config language, and represent temporary overlays as session-local config snapshots.
notes: Rust dependency gate: implement in-house/std only. Use existing serde_json/jsonc/config-normalization stack already in yazelix_core; add no new production crates. The patch surface is small path=value parsing plus JSON value coercion, and adding a new parser crate would make CLI semantics harder to control. Rejected alternatives: shell-level string rewriting, because it bypasses schema validation; a broad CLI framework, because the current Rust command owner already parses these command families.
close_reason: Implemented repeatable --with key=value session-only config overrides for launch, enter, and restart; overrides are contract-typed, materialized to ephemeral settings.jsonc files, validated through the normal config loader, documented, and covered by parser/materialization tests.

title: Fix config-surface contract validator Home Manager assertion eval
description: validate-config-surface-contract currently fails while evaluating home_manager/module.nix because the standalone Nix eval fixture does not provide the Home Manager assertions option. The validator should evaluate the module through the right Home Manager module stack or otherwise provide the required option so config-surface drift checks can run again.
acceptance_criteria: cargo run --quiet --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_maintainer --bin yzx_repo_validator -- validate-config-surface-contract succeeds in the normal maintainer environment, or the validator is explicitly narrowed with a documented reason. The fix should not weaken Home Manager module assertions for real users.
notes: 2026-05-07 implementation: fixed the standalone Home Manager validator harness by declaring the minimal Home Manager assertions option it evaluates through, and centralized the repeated stub module used by option, desktop-entry, and Darwin checks. Rust dependency gate: no new crates; in-house validator string fixture only; packaging impact none. Verification: cargo fmt --all --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml --check; cargo check --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_maintainer --all-targets; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_maintainer repo_contract_validation; cargo run --quiet --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_maintainer --bin yzx_repo_validator -- validate-config-surface-contract; git diff --check.
close_reason: Completed: standalone config-surface validator fixture now declares Home Manager assertions and the validator passes.

title: Investigate touchpad three-finger gestures for Zellij pane navigation
description: Investigate whether Yazelix can support touchpad three-finger gestures or similar gesture-driven input to move between Zellij panes without compromising portability or making the product depend on one desktop stack.
design: Treat this as an input-stack feasibility question spanning terminal, OS gesture system, desktop environment, and Zellij. First determine what signal, if any, can reach Yazelix reliably on the supported platforms. If gesture support requires desktop-specific helpers or fragile remapping layers, capture that explicitly instead of pretending it is a clean cross-platform feature.
acceptance_criteria: There is a clear feasibility decision on touchpad gesture support for pane navigation, including which platforms or terminal stacks could support it and what extra components would be required. If the idea is viable, leave behind a narrower implementation bead with explicit platform scope.

title: Optimize Yazi config generation on warm startup
description:
The structured startup profiler now shows warm current-terminal startup is dominated by Yazi config generation after the Zellij warm-state reuse fix. This should be the next delete-first warm-start optimization slice.

Design:
Keep this delete-first. First identify which parts of managed Yazi config generation are unnecessarily redone on every warm startup and which generated artifacts can be safely reused without weakening config correctness. Prefer deleting redundant regeneration or overly broad invalidation before micro-optimizing string processing. Preserve fail-fast behavior and managed-config correctness.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. The current bottleneck inside inner.generate_yazi_config is broken down enough to show which substeps dominate.
2. Warm startup avoids clearly redundant Yazi config work when inputs have not changed.
3. The change does not make generated Yazi state stale across real config/runtime changes.
4. A focused regression or profiling-oriented check defends the intended reuse or invalidation boundary.
notes: Completed warm materialization optimization without broad Yazi surface caching. Startup now profiles and runs runtime-materialization.plan first; if the plan is true noop, start_yazelix_inner returns that plan and skips runtime-materialization.materialize, avoiding warm Yazi/Zellij regeneration. To keep correctness, plan_runtime_materialization now checks generated Yazi static asset targets when hashes and top-level artifacts are current, so missing plugin/flavor assets still produce repair_missing_artifacts instead of being skipped. Adjusted bundled asset detection so a missing optional source starship config is not misclassified as stale generated state on the no-op plan path; refresh/sync still fails fast if required source assets are missing. Added regression coverage for missing generated Yazi static assets and updated runtime materialization fixture for yzpp.wasm. Scorecard: raw text diff excluding Beads 149 insertions / 7 deletions, net +142 including Nu; tokei Rust code LOC 66621 -> 66741 (+120). Accepted as performance debt, not a Spartan deletion win. Verification: cargo fmt --all -- --check; cargo check -p yazelix_core --all-targets; cargo test -p yazelix_core runtime_materialization; cargo test -p yazelix_core --test yzx_core_config_normalize runtime_materialization; nu --no-config-file source check for start_yazelix_inner.nu; validate-rust-ownership-budget; validate-docs-experience; git diff --check.
close_reason: Completed: warm no-op startup skips runtime materialization while preserving Yazi static asset repair

title: Collapse maintainer and yzx dev Nushell toward the provable irreducible shell floor
description: The maintainer and yzx dev surfaces still carry over two thousand lines of Nu, and yzx/dev.nu is part of that public dev-orchestration remainder. Keep Nu only where the maintainer/dev path is provably direct shell, gh, git, bd, cargo, or Nix orchestration; move everything deterministic, metadata-shaped, or file-surface-owned out.
design: This is not a blanket rewrite, but it is Rust-first. Classify maintainer and yzx dev files into shell orchestration, deterministic surface editing, dispatch or metadata ownership, and mixed ownership, then force every surviving Nu remainder to justify itself.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The maintainer and yzx dev keep-versus-cut split is explicit. 2. Deterministic maintainer/dev owners get their own migration lanes. 3. File deletion is preferred where possible. 4. Any surviving Nu maintainer/dev surface has explicit proof of irreducibility instead of a casual stop condition.
close_reason: all steps complete

title: Define the deletion budget for maintainer Nushell surfaces
description: Audit test_runner.nu, update_workflow.nu, issue_sync.nu, issue_bead_contract.nu, version_bump.nu, readme_surface.nu, plugin_build.nu, repo_checkout.nu, and the maintainer-only nix_detector.nu surface and classify deterministic versus shell-bound ownership.
design: The useful boundary is not maintainership. The useful boundary is shell orchestration versus deterministic policy and file editing.
acceptance_criteria: 1. A keep-versus-cut table exists for the maintainer surface. 2. The next deterministic deletion lanes are explicit. 3. Shell-bound stop conditions are explicit.
close_reason: Completed

title: Rust-own deterministic release, version, and README surface logic and delete Nu owners
description: Move deterministic release-surface editing and validation logic out of version_bump.nu, readme_surface.nu, and the paired README-version validation path where it can have a single non-Nu owner.
design: Do not move git or release orchestration if it is still shell-bound. Move the deterministic file-surface logic.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The selected release-surface Nu owners and paired deterministic README/version validation logic shrink materially or disappear. 2. The surviving owner is explicit. 3. Release/version behavior stays defended.
notes: Rust now owns README version/latest-series validation and mutation through yzx_repo_validator validate-readme-version and yzx_repo_maintainer sync-readme-surface. Deleted dev/validate_readme_version.nu and maintainer/readme_surface.nu; release/update workflows call the Rust owner.
close_reason: Completed delete-first Rust/Nu owner cuts, documentation cleanup, and validation

title: Collapse test_runner.nu to fixed suite/orchestration ownership and eliminate dynamic policy drift
description: Reduce test_runner.nu so Nu keeps only honest shell-orchestration behavior for yzx dev test, while suite inventory, policy, and any validator-facing metadata move to a fixed canonical owner. The runner should stop treating governed Nu test files as a normal steady-state surface and should route first-party Rust tests through nextest by default, keeping cargo test only for doctests and any explicit unsupported exception buckets.
design: Prefer fixed argv, checked-in helpers, and explicit suite inventories over dynamic policy code in Nu. The end state of the migration is zero governed Nushell tests, so test_runner.nu should orchestrate nextest-first Rust suites and a narrow cargo-test exception path rather than preserve broad Nu or cargo-test-by-habit ownership.
acceptance_criteria: 1. test_runner.nu is materially smaller or its surviving shell responsibilities have explicit stop conditions. 2. Any suite inventory needed outside the runner has a canonical fixed owner. 3. No new dynamic shell-program assembly is added. 4. The runner no longer assumes governed Nu test files are a normal steady-state suite surface. 5. First-party Rust tests run through nextest by default, with cargo test reserved only for doctests and explicit exceptions.
close_reason: Completed: fixed suite inventory, nextest-first Rust dispatch, and no dynamic test-suite policy drift; remaining red transitional Nu tests reproduce outside the runner rewrite

title: Re-evaluate issue sync and repo-checkout for honest Rust ownership or explicit Nu stop conditions
description: Review issue_sync.nu, issue_bead_contract.nu, and repo_checkout.nu and decide whether any deterministic contract or path-selection logic should leave Nu, or whether those paths are still honestly gh/git/bd-heavy and should stop there.
design: Be aggressive about deleting deterministic policy, but do not fake Rust ownership over gh/git/bd orchestration.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The honest owner decision is explicit for issue sync and repo checkout. 2. Any implementation-worthy deterministic subcore gets a concrete follow-up lane. 3. Otherwise the Nu stop condition is recorded explicitly.
notes: Decision recorded in maintainer_and_validator_nushell_budget.md: broad issue_sync/repo_checkout Rust ownership is not honest yet because the live value is gh/bd/git/repo-shell orchestration. Future Rust cuts should extract deterministic mapping or helper-selection logic only when that deletes Nu policy.
close_reason: Completed delete-first Rust/Nu owner cuts, documentation cleanup, and validation

title: Re-evaluate plugin build and update workflow for honest Rust ownership or explicit Nu stop conditions
description: Review plugin_build.nu and update_workflow.nu and decide whether any deterministic build-surface logic can leave Nu cleanly, or whether those paths are still honestly cargo, wasm, and repo-shell orchestration.
design: Delete deterministic helper policy if it is only gluing together file shapes. Keep real build orchestration where it belongs.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The honest owner decision is explicit for plugin build and update workflow. 2. Any implementation-worthy deterministic subcore gets a concrete follow-up lane. 3. Otherwise the Nu stop condition is recorded explicitly.
notes: Decision recorded in maintainer_and_validator_nushell_budget.md: plugin build and update workflow remain fixed external-tool orchestration for now. Deterministic README/update surface logic moved to Rust; no fake Rust wrapper was added for cargo/Nix/git build orchestration.
close_reason: Completed delete-first Rust/Nu owner cuts, documentation cleanup, and validation

title: Re-evaluate nix_detector.nu for explicit Nu stop conditions or a narrower non-Nu owner
description: Review the maintainer-only nix_detector.nu surface and decide whether any deterministic installation-detection logic should leave Nu, or whether the whole path is still honestly interactive/operator-facing shell behavior.
design: Do not keep maintainer-only detection logic in runtime-helper beads. Either extract a real deterministic subcore or record an explicit Nu stop condition.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The honest owner decision is explicit for nix_detector.nu. 2. Any implementation-worthy deterministic subcore gets a concrete follow-up lane. 3. Otherwise the Nu stop condition is recorded explicitly.
notes: Deleted utils/nix_detector.nu. update_workflow.nu now has a small fixed fail-fast Nix availability check; interactive detector repair prompts and broad Nix policy are not part of the maintainer floor.
close_reason: Completed delete-first Rust/Nu owner cuts, documentation cleanup, and validation

title: Define the yzx dev entry and dev-orchestration irreducible shell floor
description: Review yzx/dev.nu and the remaining dev-command glue after the test, validator, and maintainer budget work to decide what the public dev entry should still own in Nu and what should collapse into Rust, fixed metadata, or fixed helper assets.
design: Treat yzx dev as a thin public entrypoint over tests, validators, build helpers, and maintainer actions. The goal is the smallest provable shell orchestrator, not a dynamic Nu router kept for convenience.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The retained yzx dev responsibilities are explicit. 2. The deletion budget names the exact command-routing, metadata, and helper seams to remove. 3. Expected file or branch deletion is explicit where possible. 4. Every retained Nu route has an irreducibility proof.
close_reason: Completed

title: Implement the yzx dev cut toward the irreducible shell floor
description: Apply the yzx dev cut by deleting dynamic dispatch, metadata, and helper glue that no longer needs to live in Nu, and leave only the minimal shell-orchestration surface.
design: Count progress only when yzx/dev.nu and adjacent dev-command glue materially shrink or disappear. Do not preserve a dynamic Nu router because it already exists.
acceptance_criteria: 1. yzx/dev.nu and adjacent dev-command glue materially shrink or disappear. 2. Surviving Nu code is limited to honest dev-shell orchestration. 3. Each surviving Nu surface has explicit proof of irreducibility. 4. The public dev behavior still works.
notes:
Implementation dependency decision: no production or dev crates added. The yzx dev cut uses existing Nushell fixed process orchestration plus in-house Rust repo-validation logic. Rejected adding a CLI/test framework crate because the deleted path was transitional Nu suite policy, not argument parsing or external process management.
Completed the yzx dev cut by deleting default-suite transitional Nu entrypoint handling from maintainer/test_runner.nu and tightening validate-default-test-traceability into a hard guard against governed nushell/scripts/dev/test_*.nu returning. yzx dev test now runs fixed Rust nextest suites plus explicit cargo-test exceptions only; no Nu default suite policy remains in the runner.
close_reason: Completed

title: add a ruby pack
description: Imported GitHub issue #537.
close_reason: Dropped as out of scope for the trimmed v15 line: this was a v14-era packs/runtime-management idea rather than current Yazelix product work.

title: Add cursor status widget
description: Add an optional Yazelix status-bar widget that surfaces the active cursor sidecar state so users can tell which Ghostty cursor palette/effects are currently selected, especially when settings.trail, settings.trail_effect, or settings.mode_effect use random.
design: Treat this as a compact status widget, not a second cursor configuration UI. The widget should read the same resolved cursor state already produced during terminal materialization and render a terse label in the existing zellij.widget_tray system. It should avoid scanning shader files or reparsing large config during every status refresh; prefer existing generated state/cache facts or add a small Yazelix-owned status fact if needed. The first slice should be display-only: active cursor name, trail effect, mode effect, or a compact combined form. Reroll/toggle actions can be follow-up work if the display proves useful.
acceptance_criteria: A user can add a cursor widget to zellij.widget_tray and see the active cursor palette/effect state in the Yazelix bar. The widget works when cursor selection is fixed, random, or none; it degrades clearly when the current terminal does not use Ghostty cursor shaders. Default widget_tray remains unchanged unless there is a defended product reason to include it. Tests cover rendering, config acceptance, none/random/fixed states, and stale or missing cursor-state facts without making zjstatus parse full cursor config on every refresh.
close_reason: Implemented optional cursor widget in zellij.widget_tray. The bar renders {command_cursor}; zjstatus reads yzx_control zellij status-cache-widget cursor; launch materialization captures the resolved Ghostty cursor name once and publishes YAZELIX_CURSOR_NAME into the session; the status cache stores that launch fact so bar refreshes do not parse cursor config or shaders. Ghostty disabled cursor renders none, non-Ghostty/user-managed cursor state renders n/a, and missing facts render no segment. Default widget_tray remains unchanged. Verified with focused Rust tests, manual yzx_control cursor widget output, fmt/check, and workspace/config/docs validators.

title: Replace string-built Zellij runtime wrapper commands with direct runtime script execution
description: The Zellij wrapper layer under configs/zellij/scripts currently relies on dynamic Nushell source strings plus `nu -c` to enter runtime-owned logic. This works, but it is stringly typed, harder to test, and duplicates wrapper boilerplate.
design: Prefer a delete-first refactor. Add a shared helper that executes runtime-owned Nushell scripts directly by relative runtime path, move wrapper logic into real runtime leaf scripts, and keep tracked/generated Zellij wrapper files thin. Scope this narrowly to wrappers under configs/zellij/scripts that currently build command strings through run_runtime_nu_command. Do not widen into unrelated command-surface changes.
acceptance_criteria: 1. At least one current string-built Zellij wrapper path is replaced with direct runtime script execution through a shared helper. 2. The new pattern is capable of covering wrappers that currently build `use ...; ...` strings for runtime entry. 3. The converted wrapper surface is materially thinner or fewer lines overall for the touched paths. 4. Existing high-signal wrapper regressions still pass, and no live plugin/runtime behavior regresses.
close_reason: Completed/verified: Zellij script wrappers now use the shared run_runtime_nu_script helper to execute runtime-owned wrapper scripts directly, and no run_runtime_nu_command or zellij-wrapper nu -c string entrypoints remain.

title: Use JSONC as the shipped main settings default
description: Replace the user-visible shipped main settings default artifact with a JSONC-shaped settings_default.jsonc so the runtime seed mirrors ~/.config/yazelix/settings.jsonc instead of leaking the old yazelix_default.toml implementation detail.
design: Keep main_config_contract.toml as the machine-readable semantic contract. Make settings_default.jsonc the packaged default seed and runtime sentinel. Update startup/config UI/config normalization/validators/Home Manager docs/packaging to treat the default seed as JSONC while preserving settings.jsonc as the canonical user config.
acceptance_criteria: Runtime packages include settings_default.jsonc and first-run settings.jsonc generation reads that JSONC seed. Config normalization, config UI defaults, config-state hashing, Home Manager parity validation, runtime path detection, and docs no longer require yazelix_default.toml as the main settings default. main_config_contract.toml remains the machine metadata source. Focused tests and validators pass.
notes:
Crate decision: no new crates; use existing jsonc_parser and serde_json/toml conversion helpers already present in yazelix_core for JSONC default parsing.
Implemented: replaced the shipped main default with settings_default.jsonc; first-run settings.jsonc generation now accepts a JSONC seed directly; default/runtime sentinel paths, packaging source filters, Home Manager/docs metadata, config-surface parity validation, config-state fixtures, and update canaries now consume the JSONC seed. Kept main_config_contract.toml as machine metadata and retained TOML parsing only for legacy/explicit TOML inputs. Verification: cargo check -p yazelix_core; cargo check -p yazelix_maintainer; cargo test -p yazelix_core settings_surface; cargo test -p yazelix_core active_config_surface; cargo test -p yazelix_core --test yzx_core_config_normalize; cargo test -p yazelix_core --test yzx_core_yazi_materialization; cargo test -p yazelix_core --test yzx_core_settings_jsonc_patch; cargo test -p yazelix_core --test yzx_core_config_ui; cargo test -p yazelix_core --test yzx_core_command_metadata; cargo test -p yazelix_maintainer --test repo_upgrade_contract; yzx_repo_validator validate-config-surface-contract; validate-workspace-session-contract; validate-contracts; validate-docs-experience; validate-upgrade-contract; validate-flake-interface; validate-nixpkgs-package; validate-nushell-syntax; validate-rust-test-traceability; git diff --check. LOC scorecard HEAD..candidate: raw 482 insertions/550 deletions excluding .beads; runtime net -303, maintainer net -1, tests net +5, docs 0, generated 0, packaging 0, total code delta -54.
close_reason: Completed JSONC default-seed migration. Runtime/packages now ship settings_default.jsonc; startup/config/default parsers consume JSONC seed; config-surface, Home Manager, docs, packaging, and validators are updated; focused Rust tests and maintainer validators pass.

title: Fix installed-runtime CI regression from Home Manager activation contract
description:

The installed-runtime CI smoke currently fails after Home Manager activation because the validator expects a generated Ghostty terminal config under the Yazelix state root, but the activation path does not create it.

What needs to be done:
Inspect the installed-runtime validator and the Home Manager activation/runtime materialization path, then either restore the missing terminal generation or narrow the validator expectation so it matches the real Home Manager contract.

title: Remove README atuin optional history bullet

acceptance_criteria: README no longer advertises the atuin optional-history bullet or removed shell.enable_atuin knob in the package/customization section. Historical and contract mentions may remain where they describe migrations or stale config diagnostics.
