notes: Completed decision: accepted staged explicit materialization. Created follow-up implementation bead yazelix-xz1z. Ratatui config editor evaluation remains separate under yazelix-ryx4 and should consume this policy instead of deciding it again.
close_reason: Decision recorded: use staged explicit main-config materialization with comment preservation; keep runtime parsing tolerant and sidecars opt-in; implementation follow-up is yazelix-xz1z

title: Remove README experiments section
description: Remove the README Experiments section so the front-door docs stay focused on supported current-state product information.
acceptance_criteria: README no longer contains the Experiments heading or Nixless/System Mode branch note, and the surrounding section flow remains valid.
close_reason: Completed: removed the README Experiments section and Nixless/System Mode branch note. Verified git diff --check, absence of the removed text, validate-docs-experience, and validate-readme-version.

title: Fix Ratty magician Kitty passthrough env
description:
Ratty sessions use the packaged runtime feature `runtime_features/zellij_kitty_passthrough`, but the environment marker `YAZELIX_ZELLIJ_KITTY_PASSTHROUGH=1` was only exported by the Ghostty-specific wrapper. As a result, `yzx screen magician` inside a Ratty-launched Zellij session can fail with `magician_requires_kitty_graphics` even though the Ratty runtime supports the required Zellij Kitty passthrough path.

Fix the shared runtime startup environment so both Ghostty and Ratty sessions inherit the passthrough marker when the runtime feature exists.
acceptance_criteria:
`runtime_env.sh` exports `YAZELIX_ZELLIJ_KITTY_PASSTHROUGH=1` when the active runtime has `runtime_features/zellij_kitty_passthrough`.
Ratty and Ghostty startup paths both inherit the marker through the shared runtime env helper.
Focused tests cover the startup env handoff.
`yzx screen magician` no longer fails the support guard inside a fresh packaged Ratty Yazelix session.
close_reason: Fixed: shared runtime_env.sh now exports YAZELIX_ZELLIJ_KITTY_PASSTHROUGH=1 when the active runtime has the zellij_kitty_passthrough feature, so Ratty and Ghostty sessions both satisfy the magician Kitty graphics guard. Verified with focused startup env and magician tests plus validate-contracts.

title: Full-owner Helix config materialization in Rust
description: Move Helix config generation from setup/helix_config_merger.nu to a Rust-owned helix-materialization.generate command, following the proven Yazi/Zellij full-owner pattern. Helix config generation is deterministic, self-contained, and has no shell-bound execution. Delete the Nushell owner end-to-end.
close_reason: Implemented helix-materialization.generate in Rust, owning template reading, user config deep-merge, reveal binding enforcement, generated config writing, and import notice logic. Replaced 156-line Nushell helix_config_merger.nu with a ~55-line thin wrapper. All existing tests pass.

title: Fix magician GIF derivative frame animation
description: The magician screen renders in Ghostty but appears static because the checked-in PNG derivative under assets/third_party/ascii_magician_1mposter_frames only has 66 frames and collapses to 6 unique images, while the source GIF has 198 unique coalesced frames. Regenerate the derivative from the source GIF and update the renderer frame count so the Kitty graphics animation advances. Acceptance: Ghostty yzx screen magician visibly advances through the GIF-derived frames; derivative frame count matches the source GIF frame count used by the renderer; Rust front-door tests cover the updated frame count/path contract.
acceptance_criteria: Ghostty yzx screen magician visibly advances through the GIF-derived frames. The packaged runtime contains all renderer-addressed derivative frames. The magician placement stays inset enough that Kitty graphics does not cover Zellij pane borders. Rust front-door tests cover the updated frame count/path and inset layout contracts.
notes: Manual Ghostty test confirmed the regenerated frames animate, but the large opaque Kitty placement can overlap Zellij pane borders because terminal graphics are painted by Ghostty outside Zellij's normal text-cell clipping. Keep the magician placement inset from pane edges.
close_reason: Fixed. Regenerated the magician GIF derivative as 198 unique coalesced PNG frames, updated the renderer frame count, inset the Kitty graphics placement so Zellij pane frames remain visible, and strengthened graphics cleanup on screen exit. Verified by focused Rust tests, contract validator, Nix Ghostty package build, and manual Ghostty runtime test.

title: Create a GitHub social preview image for Yazelix
description: Create and configure a repository social preview image that represents Yazelix clearly when the repo is shared on GitHub and social platforms.
design: Treat this as a branding and front-door asset, not just a generic screenshot upload. The image should reflect Yazelix's terminal-native identity, remain readable at social-card sizes, and avoid overloading the card with tiny UI details. Decide whether the right asset is an illustrated brand card, a polished product screenshot, or a hybrid, then configure the repo to use the chosen image.
acceptance_criteria: There is a final social preview image asset sized appropriately for GitHub, it matches Yazelix branding well enough for public sharing, and the repository is configured to use it. The bead records the chosen direction so future branding work knows why this asset exists.

title: Add yzpp on-close hook support for configured popups
description: Why: Yazelix lazygit only needs to refresh the managed Yazi sidebar when the popup is closed by the popup keybinding; process-exit close-on-success is intentionally out of scope. What: extend yazelix-zellij-popup popup specs with a small on_close command hook and run it when yzpp closes a popup through toggle/close.
acceptance_criteria: yzpp KDL supports an on_close command hook; toggle-close and explicit close run the hook; open/focus do not run it; tests cover parsing and close hook behavior without adding process-exit lifecycle semantics.
close_reason: Completed: yzpp supports optional KDL/raw on_close command hooks and Yazelix uses the hook for sidebar refresh when the popup key closes lazygit-style popups.

title: Surface desktop-entry prelaunch progress and failures before terminal handoff
description:
Desktop launch currently uses a non-terminal fast path. Config parsing, state computation, terminal selection, or environment preparation can fail before a real terminal window exists, so users can click the desktop entry and get no visible feedback. Terminal switches that require environment preparation have the same UX problem in a softer form: Yazelix may be working, but the launcher looks dead.

Treat this as the primary desktop-launch UX contract. Immediate visible failure feedback matters more than decorative progress. Users should either see a terminal or error surface right away, or some other immediate visible preparation signal while Yazelix gets to the point where the terminal window can take over.
design: Treat this as a launch UX problem, not a config-migration or packaging problem. Start from the actual desktop entry and fast-launch path, then decide the smallest reliable way to guarantee visible feedback before terminal handoff. The solution may be a terminal-backed launcher, an explicit prelaunch progress surface, or a similar narrow mechanism, but it must cover both waiting and failed-before-terminal-spawn cases. Preserve the managed desktop-entry ownership model and keep Home Manager and manual installs on the same user-visible contract.
acceptance_criteria:
1. If desktop launch fails before the terminal window is spawned, the user sees an actionable error instead of a silent no-op.
2. If desktop launch needs noticeable prelaunch preparation or rebuild work, the user gets immediate visible feedback that Yazelix is preparing the session.
3. Manual and Home Manager desktop-entry paths share the same visible-feedback contract.
4. Tests and doctor coverage defend the launcher path and the visible-feedback behavior.
notes:
opus 4.6 review:

Core implementation is correct and well-scoped. Follows the bead plan faithfully: Terminal=true on both manual and HM entries, prelaunch progress output, error display with user acknowledgement via try/input/catch, no fallback paths. The desktop.nu changes are clean.

Two test quality issues:

1. test_yzx_desktop_install_includes_terminal_true is redundant — the same diff already adds the Terminal=true assertion to test_yzx_desktop_install_writes_entry_and_icon_assets. The new dedicated test checks the exact same thing in isolation. Should remove per AGENTS.md low-value test policy. Rated 8/10 but defends zero contract surface the existing test does not already cover.

2. test_yzx_desktop_launch_prints_prelaunch_progress is thin — the probe exits 0 without simulating real fast-path behavior. It only proves print runs before complete, which is trivially true (sequential execution). Most of its value is already covered by test_yzx_desktop_launch_propagates_fast_path_failures_without_fallback which also asserts the Preparing session stdout line. 9/10 strength rating overstates it; closer to 6/10. Borderline keep/remove.

Architectural notes (not blockers):
- Terminal=true delegates bootstrap terminal selection to the DE (gnome-terminal, Konsole, xterm, etc.), causing a brief visual mismatch before the real Yazelix terminal opens. This is the boring-but-reliable approach the bead explicitly endorses.
- StartupWMClass=com.yazelix.Yazelix is misleading with Terminal=true since the WM class belongs to the bootstrap terminal, not the Yazelix session. Not functionally harmful.
- Error text appears in both stdout (acknowledge_desktop_failure print) and stderr (error make). Intentional but slightly noisy duplication.

Verdict: core implementation good, ship it. Remove redundant test_yzx_desktop_install_includes_terminal_true. Consider downgrading or removing test_yzx_desktop_launch_prints_prelaunch_progress.
2026-04-18 implementation cleanup: added the desktop-launch visible-feedback spec, removed redundant low-value tests flagged in review, added doctor coverage for correct-Exec desktop entries missing Terminal=true, and added a Home Manager desktop-entry contract check to the config-surface validator.
close_reason: Completed: desktop entries are terminal-backed for manual and Home Manager installs, yzx desktop launch surfaces pre-terminal progress and actionable failures, doctor reports stale non-terminal desktop entries, and the visible-feedback contract is documented in docs/specs/desktop_launch_visible_feedback.md.

title: Shrink status/cache command surface after Zellij split
description: After the Zellij/workspace command split, shrink the remaining status/cache command surface. Separate status widgets, cache IO, agent usage refresh, cursor/workspace widget rendering, and public command adapters so the main repo can delete weak tests and avoid status logic blocking workspace extraction.
design: Deletion before extraction. Do not extract status/cache publicly in this bead. Keep Yazelix-owned session/cache paths local. Identify duplicate tests or implementation-detail assertions from the old broad zellij_commands.rs surface and delete them when covered by stronger behavior checks.
acceptance_criteria: Status/cache responsibilities are mapped and smaller; agent usage, cursor/workspace widgets, cache IO, and public adapters have clearer private ownership; weak tests tied to old structure are deleted or strengthened; current integrated status-bar behavior is preserved; focused status/cache tests pass; LOC delta and rejected candidates are recorded.
close_reason: Completed: zellij_commands.rs is now an export shell; status/cache/widget regressions live in zellij_commands/status/tests.rs; Rust ownership inventory updated; focused Zellij/workspace checks and validators pass.

title: Make main settings.jsonc fields explicit and mandatory
description: Define and implement a stricter main settings.jsonc contract: every supported main config field is explicitly present in the canonical settings file, booleans are always true or false, and runtime code does not silently treat omitted fields as durable user intent. This comes from GitHub issue #582: "make every setting mandatory. either true or false (if bool)".
design:
## Implemented Design

- `settings_default.jsonc` is a complete main settings shape, including `helix.runtime_path = ""` as the on-disk sentinel that normalizes to internal null.
- Main settings contract fields are not omitted from the default template or Home Manager render unless a different config file owns that surface.
- Runtime, materialization, doctor, config state, config UI, and maintainer sweep normalization request missing-field diagnostics in the normal path.
- Missing main fields are blocking and fixable diagnostics, not silent defaults.
- Existing writable `settings.jsonc` files are additively repaired during canonical settings bootstrap by inserting only fields missing from `settings_default.jsonc`.
- Additive repair does not hide bad states: malformed JSONC, unknown fields, wrong types, existing nulls, read-only files, and Home Manager-owned symlinks still fail with explicit errors.
- `yzx config unset` and config UI unset reset main settings to the shipped default value; cursor sidecar unsets still remove cursor fields.
- The JSON schema enumerates required main sections/fields and rejects unknown main-section properties.
- Home Manager can keep Nix-level null convenience for nullable command/path options, but generated `settings.jsonc` emits explicit empty strings for `empty_string_to_null` fields.
acceptance_criteria: settings_default.jsonc contains every main-config contract field, including explicit empty-string sentinels for nullable command/path fields such as helix.runtime_path. config_metadata/main_config_contract.toml no longer marks main settings fields as omitted from the default template unless a separate file owns that surface. Runtime settings.jsonc validation treats missing main fields as an actionable config issue instead of silently defaulting them in normal startup/materialization paths. JSON schema required arrays and additionalProperties rules match the canonical main config shape closely enough to catch missing/unknown fields in editors and validators. Home Manager manage_config rendering emits a complete settings.jsonc when enabled, using explicit empty strings where parser_behavior=empty_string_to_null maps to internal null. Verification covers the default template, a config missing one bool, a config with a bool set to null or the wrong type, a config missing helix.runtime_path, Home Manager generated settings, and the relevant config surface validator.
close_reason: Implemented complete main settings.jsonc shape with explicit helix runtime_path, strict missing-field diagnostics across runtime paths, writable additive settings repair, main-setting reset-to-default unset behavior, schema required/additionalProperties rules, Home Manager complete rendering parity, and regression coverage. Verified with cargo fmt --check; cargo check -p yazelix_core; cargo test -p yazelix_core; validate-config-surface-contract; config.normalize --include-missing on settings_default.jsonc zero diagnostics; nix build .#runtime --no-link.

title: Evaluate whether Yazelix should drop Foot support because it is not on macOS
description: Evaluate whether Yazelix should stop treating Foot as a meaningful supported terminal option because it is not available on macOS, unlike the other first-class terminal candidates under consideration.
design: Reason from the actual support matrix and product goals rather than from terminal popularity alone. Compare Foot against the terminals Yazelix most plausibly wants to support across platforms, especially macOS-relevant options like WezTerm and Alacritty. The key decision is whether Foot still earns documentation, config generation, testing effort, and maintainer attention despite being Linux-only. If Foot remains supported, the bead should justify that cost explicitly. If not, it should define the de-support path cleanly.
acceptance_criteria: There is a clear decision to keep, demote, or drop Foot support. The decision records the platform and maintenance tradeoffs, how Foot compares to WezTerm and Alacritty for Yazelix's goals, and what documentation, config, or support-matrix changes would follow from the chosen direction.
close_reason: Closed on GitHub

title: Close displaced popup slots when switching named popups
description: Opening one named yzpp popup while another named popup exists can leave the displaced popup as a hidden floating pane. Repro: open top_popup, open bottom_popup, close bottom_popup. No popup is visible, but Zellij still marks the tab as having a floating pane and the next top_popup toggle first clears stale state instead of showing the popup. Desired behavior: named popup slots are mutually exclusive for the managed popup layer; opening or focusing one closes other configured popup panes, and closing one cleans hidden siblings.
notes: 2026-05-21: Implemented local yzpp child commit ab68e23. The plugin now closes other configured named popup panes before opening/focusing a requested slot and before closing the current slot, preventing hidden displaced popup panes from keeping Zellij's floating marker/state alive. Verified with cargo fmt --check, cargo test, nix build .#yzpp --no-link, main nix build .#runtime --override-input yazelixZellijPopup ../yazelix-zellij-popup --no-link, and override doctor --fix/--verbose.
close_reason: Fixed in yzpp commit ab68e23 and consumed by Yazelix flake.lock. Named popup slots now close displaced configured popups before opening/focusing/closing another slot, preventing hidden floating panes from keeping stale Zellij floating state. Verified cargo fmt/test, yzpp Nix build, main runtime build, workspace validator, and no-override doctor after generated-state repair.

title: Non-Ghostty terminal configs and orchestrator materialization in Rust
description: Move WezTerm, Kitty, Alacritty, and Foot config text generation plus the generate_selected_terminal_configs orchestrator from utils/terminal_renderers.nu and utils/terminal_configs.nu to Rust. This deletes the remaining non-Ghostty terminal config Nushell owners and the dispatcher surface. Only start after Ghostty materialization proves the pattern.
close_reason: Implemented terminal-materialization.generate in Rust, owning WezTerm, Kitty, Alacritty, and Foot config generation plus the unified orchestrator. Deleted terminal_renderers.nu entirely (-197 lines) and reduced terminal_configs.nu from 147 to 89 lines (-58). All targeted tests pass.

title: Capture yzxterm terminal lifetime and crash-exit evidence
description:
Problem:
The current yzxterm desktop launch log records launch argv/env and a terminal PID, then exits with exit_status=not_observed_after_probe_window once the terminal survives the short probe window. For the recurring slow-then-burst-render window disappearance, this still leaves no durable evidence of whether Rio exited normally, received a signal, panicked without stderr, was closed by the compositor, or remained alive while the visible surface disappeared. Rio file logs for the generated yzxterm config were also stale during the 2026-06-02 incident window.

Evidence from 2026-06-02 investigation:
- Latest desktop log: ~/.local/share/yazelix/logs/terminal_launch/yazelix_terminal_desktop_1780407336957.log, created 2026-06-02 10:35:37 -03, records terminal_or_wrapper_pid=3370988 and exit_status=not_observed_after_probe_window.
- That PID remained alive during the investigation, so the newest launch was not itself a crash postmortem.
- stdout/stderr stayed attached to the launch log, but the log had no additional Rio/runtime crash output.
- ~/.local/share/yazelix/configs/terminal_emulators/yzxterm/log/rio.log was stale at 2026-06-01 13:06:37 -03, and ~/.config/rio/log/rio.log was stale at 2026-06-01 13:07:06 -03.
- journalctl --user only showed COSMIC app launch scope lines for the relevant yzxterm launches.
- coredumpctl from the Nix store reported no coredumps since 2026-06-02 00:00:00.

Acceptance criteria:
- Desktop-launched yzxterm keeps a bounded, detached lifetime watcher or equivalent owner that waits on the terminal PID/process group and appends final exit status, signal, and timestamp to the per-launch log without blocking desktop startup.
- The log can distinguish early wrapper failure, normal Rio exit, signal termination, and still-running/no-exit-observed states.
- Fresh Rio logging is either wired to the per-launch/generated yzxterm log path for desktop launches or explicitly disabled with a documented reason that doctor reports.
- Doctor distinguishes launch metadata only from lifetime-exit evidence captured, so a log ending in not_observed_after_probe_window is not treated as sufficient crash observability.
- Log retention remains bounded and normal yzxterm startup latency is preserved.
- Add focused tests for the watcher/log classification behavior and a runtime smoke that proves a short-lived test command writes a final status.
close_reason: Implemented bounded yzxterm lifetime evidence: desktop helper now waits after startup probe and records final exit status or signal, doctor distinguishes lifetime captured/watching from metadata-only logs, contracts and focused tests updated, and manual POSIX smoke proved final status capture. Recorded fork-added Rio/Yazelix Terminal source paths for the next slow-then-burst crash investigation.

comments.author: lucca
comments.text:
Implementation evidence, 2026-06-02

Main runtime change:
- shells/posix/desktop_deferred_launch_probe.sh now keeps the detached helper alive after the short startup probe. It writes lifetime_status=watching, waits for the terminal-or-wrapper PID, and appends final_exit_status plus final_exit_kind=exit/final_exit_code or final_exit_kind=signal/final_signal.
- Early exits now record both early_exit_status and final_exit_status.
- The yzxterm child wrapper in /home/lucca/pjs/yazelix-terminal/misc/yazelix_terminal_desktop.sh ends with exec, including the graphics-wrapper path, so the watched process should be the terminal process boundary rather than a short-lived wrapper shell.
- Doctor now classifies yzxterm logs as lifetime_captured, lifetime_watching, metadata_only, or legacy_or_missing_metadata. Metadata-only logs such as the old not_observed_after_probe_window shape now warn instead of counting as sufficient crash observability.
- Contracts updated: docs/contracts/terminal_launch_contract.md, docs/contracts/active_runtime_identity.md, docs/contracts/desktop_launch_visible_feedback.md.

Important correction from the user:
- The crashed launch was the launch before the currently live 10:35 yzxterm process. The live process evidence should not be read as disproving the earlier disappearance.

Fork-added code focus for the next crash reproduction:
- Treat vanilla Rio as unlikely to share this exact bug until evidence says otherwise. Focus on code added after the fork.
- Primary candidate source paths in /home/lucca/pjs/yazelix-terminal:
  - frontends/rioterm/src/application.rs lines around Render, RenderRoute, TerminalDamaged, and PrepareRenderOnRoute scheduling. These fork changes gate event-mode redraws through focus/occlusion checks, route dirty state, and scheduler timers.
  - frontends/rioterm/src/screen/mod.rs lines around GhosttyShaderFrameState emission, ghostty_shader_needs_redraw, should_present, discard_frame, and last_render_needs_redraw dirty rearming. This is the central slow-then-burst suspect because shader and renderer animation can re-arm presentation without terminal-cell damage.
  - frontends/rioterm/src/renderer/trail_cursor.rs cursor-trail spring timing and snap logic, especially dt clamping and warp/one-row behavior.
  - sugarloaf/src/components/ghostty_shaders/mod.rs, especially SHADER_ANIMATION_WINDOW=650ms, update_frame_state setting animation_until, needs_redraw, per-frame uniforms, and in-place WGPU texture copy/render chain.
  - sugarloaf/src/sugarloaf.rs WGPU render_with_grids Ghostty shader hook.
- Relevant fork commits inspected: eacaa017ef Add Ghostty cursor shader runtime, 7be31e0479 Throttle shader game redraw loop, 6702381d48 Fix event-mode Wayland redraw scheduling, 433e2995ff Wire cursor shaders for event mode, dd5e277be1 Optimize Helix viewport repaint latency, 0bc85ba96d Clamp cursor trail redraw jumps, d070b37948 Preserve one-line cursor warp trails.

Validation:
- sh -n shells/posix/desktop_deferred_launch_probe.sh
- cargo fmt --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml --all -- --check
- cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core yzxterm_launch_log_finding -- --nocapture
- cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core desktop_deferred_launch_helper_records_lifetime_status -- --nocapture
- cargo run --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_maintainer --bin yzx_repo_validator -- validate-contracts
- git diff --check
- Manual POSIX smoke: desktop_deferred_launch_probe.sh with sh -c exit 7 produced early_exit_status=7, final_exit_status=7, final_exit_kind=exit, final_exit_code=7.

title: Keep runtime-project shell artifacts aligned with recorded launch state
description:
Problem:
The runtime-owned profile resolver now correctly prefers recorded launch state over the runtime project shell artifact, but ~/.local/share/yazelix/runtime/project/.devenv/gc/shell can still lag behind the latest built profile after refresh. That means user-facing behavior is fixed by precedence, while one materialized artifact remains stale and misleading.

Design:
Decide the clean contract for the runtime-project shell artifact instead of continuing to tolerate drift. Either keep the runtime-project shell artifact aligned with the latest built profile whenever refresh or rebuild succeeds, or explicitly demote that artifact to bootstrap-only/internal status and remove remaining resolver or helper dependence on it where possible. Prefer delete-first reasoning: if the stale artifact does not need to participate in runtime-owned truth, stop treating it as meaningful state rather than layering more precedence hacks on top.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. The repo has an explicit contract for how ~/.local/share/yazelix/runtime/project/.devenv/gc/shell relates to the latest recorded launch profile after refresh/build flows.
2. Refresh/build flows either keep that artifact aligned with the fresh built profile, or the code stops depending on it as runtime-owned truth outside the narrow place that truly needs it.
3. Runtime-owned profile resolution, refresh, and desktop launch no longer rely on stale runtime-project shell artifacts to behave correctly.
4. Regression coverage or validators defend the chosen contract so this drift does not silently return.
close_reason: Investigation showed runtime-project .devenv profile artifacts are optional helper state, not canonical launch truth. Spec and maintainer regression now defend build-shell output plus recorded launch state as the authoritative reusable-profile contract.

title: Add profile-install smoke validation
description: Validate the README first-install path with a disposable Nix profile or isolated profile directory: install Yazelix from the flake, verify yzx is available, and prove the installed runtime reports itself correctly.
design: Keep this as a maintainer or CI lane if the Nix cost is too high for default CI. The check should validate the profile-owned path separately from Home Manager so releases do not accidentally depend on a maintainer's local configuration.
acceptance_criteria: The validation proves a profile-installed Yazelix package exposes yzx, reports the installed runtime/version, and can reach the desktop-entry install path shape where practical. It does not enter the cheap default lane if it is too expensive.
close_reason: Completed: cold profile-install validation now exercises profile-owned desktop install output and rejects stale Exec ownership. Verified with repo_contract_validation::nix_package::tests; full validate-flake-profile-install was attempted but current dirty tree is blocked by unrelated untracked menu_commands.rs packaging state.

title: Run child release transaction validator in CI
description:
The child release transaction validator now catches published child pin, Cargo source hash, and first-party Zellij plugin wasm toolchain regressions. Keeping it manual still lets broken child lock updates land before users or reporters see them. Wire the validator into the appropriate CI validation job without turning it into a package build.

Acceptance criteria:
- CI runs `yzx_repo_validator validate-child-release-transaction` after building the maintainer validator binary.
- The workflow name or docs make clear this is a release/child-input gate, not a default package build.
- Test governance docs mention the gate so future agents understand why CI performs a networked child-release check.
- Verification includes a local validator run and a review of the workflow command shape.
close_reason: Implemented. The always-on CI validation job now runs yzx_repo_validator validate-child-release-transaction after building the validator binary, so child input publication, Cargo source hash, and first-party child wasm package toolchain regressions are checked before branch protection passes. Updated the test-suite governance contract to classify this as a CI-focused release validator rather than a package build. Verification: validate-child-release-transaction, validate-contracts, and git diff --check.

title: Define desktop launch behavior when terminal choice changes across rebuilds
description: Desktop launch now has a real contract question: if the user changes terminal.terminals in yazelix.toml and the newly preferred terminal only becomes launchable after a rebuild, the launcher cannot simultaneously guarantee fast first paint, one-window behavior, and exact-terminal correctness. Capture and decide the intended product behavior instead of letting desktop fast paths guess.
design: Reason from first principles. The competing goals are: fast first paint, one click equals one real session/window, exact adherence to the newly configured terminal, and a maintainable state model. Evaluate at least these options: 1. correctness-first hidden wait, then launch the exact new terminal; 2. one-window visible bootstrap in the last known launchable terminal, with the new terminal taking effect on next launch; 3. two-window/bootstrap-then-final handoff that launches an old or host terminal immediately, rebuilds visibly, then spawns the newly configured terminal and disposes of the bootstrap window. Prefer the simplest contract that is honest, predictable, and maintainable. The bead should end with an explicit decision and follow-on implementation path, not just a list of options.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The bead records an explicit desktop-launch contract for the case where terminal selection changes and the new terminal only becomes available after rebuild. 2. The chosen behavior states whether Yazelix prioritizes fast first paint, exact new-terminal correctness, or a two-stage handoff in this edge case. 3. The decision includes failure behavior and restart or next-launch semantics. 4. The result is specific enough to guide a concrete implementation without re-arguing the product tradeoff.
close_reason: Defined and implemented the desktop launch contract: use a visible one-window bootstrap path when a host-launchable terminal exists, let newly rebuilt managed terminals take effect on the next launch via runtime-owned wrapper reuse, and fall back to the hidden-wait desktop path only when no visible bootstrap terminal is available.

title: Fix macOS CPU status widget fallback
description: The Zellij CPU status widget renders ?? on macOS. The CPU script falls back to ps outside Linux, but Nushell external command output is a byte stream, so the current skip/where pipeline fails before parsing %CPU rows. Linux usually masks this because /proc/stat succeeds first.
acceptance_criteria: On hosts without /proc/stat, the CPU script decodes ps stdout into lines and renders a numeric 0-100% value instead of ?? when ps returns valid %CPU rows. The parser remains clamped/normalized by core count and still returns ?? for real command or parse failures.
close_reason: Fixed the macOS ps fallback by decoding external stdout through complete/lines before parsing CPU percentages; locally verified the normal script and ps fallback render numeric values.

title: Compact grouped agent usage widgets
description: Replace repeated provider-period status-bar usage segments with compact grouped provider widgets. codex_usage and opencode_usage render configurable period lists with better token units. Period-specific provider facts are internal cache entries, not public widgets.
design: Only grouped provider widgets are public. codex_usage/opencode_usage render configured period lists with short d/mon/last labels and compact k/M/B token units. Period-specific cache facts remain internal implementation details, not widget_tray entries or status-cache-widget names.
acceptance_criteria: The public widget tray accepts codex_usage and opencode_usage only for provider usage summaries. No codex_*_usage or opencode_*_usage per-period widget names remain in docs, templates, onboarding, bar registry, or config metadata. Grouped widgets respect configured period ordering and compact token units.
close_reason: Completed: codex_usage and opencode_usage are the only public provider usage widgets; grouped period lists render compact d/mon/last labels with k/M/B token units, with no public per-period widget surface.

title: Restore Nushell yzx completion via generated extern bridge
description: Generate a lightweight Nushell extern bridge for the external yzx CLI from the real Yazelix command tree during setup/refresh. Managed Nushell should source the generated bridge from Yazelix state so subcommand and flag completion return without re-importing the full command implementation or hand-maintaining a duplicate signature file.
close_reason: Generated Nushell extern bridge from the real yzx command tree, sourced it from Yazelix state, and verified completion/signature metadata in the installed runtime.

title: Add a shared migration engine and schema-aware yzx config migrate
description: Add the shared migration engine that knows about supported config removals, renames, moves, and value replacements, and expose it through yzx config migrate. This should preserve user intent whenever the mapping is deterministic, stay separate from blunt reset and replace flows, and always back up the original config before writing.
design: Treat this as the foundation for the whole upgrade UX rather than as a one off command. The migration engine should own structured rules with version and date metadata, path and value matchers, rationale, manual fix text, and whether a change is safe to rewrite automatically. Prefer one canonical Nushell-friendly data shape and API for these rules so startup diagnostics, yzx doctor, yzx config migrate, and the first-run upgrade summary all consume the same metadata. The rule schema should be rich enough for validators to prove that breaking config changes are either covered by a migration rule or explicitly marked as manual only in the upgrade-notes contract. yzx config migrate should default to a non destructive preview, support an explicit apply path with backup, preserve supported user settings, and leave ambiguous or lossy cases untouched but clearly explained. Prefer focused behavior tests over help text checks.
acceptance_criteria: A maintainer or user can run yzx config migrate against stale yazelix.toml files and get a detailed preview of safe rewrites, skipped ambiguous cases, and the exact backup path used on apply. Known migrations such as removed widget tray entries are expressed as explicit rules rather than hard coded one offs, and the same rule metadata can be consumed by diagnostics, validators, and upgrade-summary surfaces. Add comprehensive unit tests for rule matching, ordering, safe versus ambiguous classification, preview rendering, apply behavior, backup creation, and rule metadata completeness. Add end to end scripts with detailed logging that exercise dry run and apply flows in temp config roots, log before and after TOML plus command output, and cover at least one removed value case, one renamed or moved field case, and one ambiguous case that must stay manual.
notes:
Planning refinements:
- Create a focused spec such as docs/specs/config_migration_engine.md.
- Prefer a stable command surface like yzx config migrate [--apply] with an explicit preview-first default.
- Prefer a small migration helper module that exposes rule discovery, rule matching, preview rendering, and apply operations separately so tests can defend each boundary.
- Non-goals: silent startup rewrites, auto-deleting unsupported user settings without explanation, and conflating migrate with config reset.
- Add a cheap validator that checks migration-rule completeness and wire it into the same local and CI contract lane used by the upgrade-notes work.
- Verification plan: canonical unit coverage for rule matching and apply semantics, plus at least one high-signal end to end Nushell script that logs temp HOME setup, input TOML, detected migrations, preview output, backup path, output TOML, and manual-only skips.
close_reason: Completed: added the shared config migration engine, preview-first yzx config migrate command, migration-rule validator, spec, and comprehensive unit + e2e coverage for deterministic and manual-only config migrations.

title: Fix macOS startup hang from sidebar-state orchestrator registration
description: Home Manager/macOS testers can see Yazelix hang at startup with the first sidebar pane stuck at Loading... The report shows sidebar-state.yazi synchronously pipes register_sidebar_yazi_state to the pane orchestrator during Yazi startup; if the orchestrator or permissions are not ready, Zellij's CliPipe route times out and the client can wedge. Yazelix also wrote Zellij plugin permission grants to the Linux ~/.cache/zellij path instead of Zellij's ProjectDirs cache path on macOS.
notes:
Crate decision: add production dependency directories=5 to yazelix_core so Yazelix resolves Zellij's plugin permission cache path with the same ProjectDirs convention Zellij uses. Rejected in-house cfg(target_os) path reconstruction because macOS/Linux path derivation is exactly the kind of platform-specific behavior the crate owns; packaging impact is one small path-resolution crate already used by Zellij's dependency graph.
Implemented two defensive fixes locally: sidebar-state.yazi delays startup registration and sends zellij action pipe asynchronously via Yazi Command/ya.async instead of os.execute; zellij materialization and doctor now resolve permissions.kdl with directories::ProjectDirs matching Zellij's own ProjectDirs path, covering macOS ~/Library/Caches/org.Zellij-Contributors.Zellij/permissions.kdl.
close_reason: Implemented in commit a35204fb; macOS-safe permissions path and async sidebar-state registration landed, and maintainer manual testing confirmed startup works.

title: Render cached status widgets without literal zjstatus markup
description: Cached status widgets currently return strings such as '#[fg=#00ff88,bold][workspace ...]' through zjstatus command stdout. zjstatus prints command stdout literally, so the bar shows raw style markup. Acceptance: cached widget commands emit plain text, template command formats own colors, and regressions cover workspace/agent output without literal '#['.
close_reason: Fixed by returning plain cached widget stdout and moving color markup into zjstatus command formats; regressions cover workspace and agent usage output without literal style tags.

title: add the vertical_split and horizontal_split to the cursor builder
description: Imported GitHub issue #572.
notes: Final schema decision: replace simple_dual with canonical family="mono" using one required color, automatic accent derivation, and optional accent_color/cursor_color overrides. Replace axis_gradient with canonical family="split" using direction="vertical"|"horizontal", required colors=[first, second], required blend=true|false, and optional cursor_color. No compatibility aliases for simple_dual or axis_gradient; old sidecars should fail fast with clear supported-family errors. Inferno should become the horizontal split starting point and be renamed to magma or lava; one split preset should use blend=false. Intensify reef green. Rust dependency decision: no new production or dev crates; implement deterministic RGB/HSL color derivation in-house/std. Packaging impact: users with old cursor sidecars must edit family names/fields.
close_reason: Completed

title: Evaluate whether sesh adds meaningful value to Yazelix session workflows
description: Evaluate what sesh would actually add to Yazelix compared with the current Zellij and workspace flow, and decide whether it is worth integrating, documenting, or ignoring.
design: Compare sesh against the problems Yazelix already has or may soon have around session discovery, startup, switching, and saved workflows. Do not evaluate it as an abstract cool tool. The question is whether sesh solves a real user problem better than current Yazelix or Zellij surfaces, and if so whether integration should be first-party, optional, or purely documented.
acceptance_criteria: There is a go, no-go, or optional-integration decision on sesh, grounded in concrete session-workflow use cases. The bead records what sesh does better, what it duplicates, and what integration shape would make sense if it is worth adopting.
notes:
Investigation result, 2026-04-28:
- Decision: no first-party sesh integration for now. Sesh is useful, but it is a tmux-centered session manager and project switcher; its strongest current value is tmux session listing/creation, zoxide-backed project switching, configured session startup commands, and fzf-style switching.
- What sesh does better: polished fuzzy session/project selection for tmux users, configured startup commands/windows, and root/session helpers for tmux workflows.
- What it duplicates in Yazelix: zoxide project selection, multiplexer session switching, project-root entry, and startup command orchestration. Yazelix already has Zellij as the workspace layer, per-window config snapshots, pane-orchestrator tab-local state, and a better native next step in yzx warp.
- Integration shape if revisited: optional docs/inspiration only, or an adapter after Yazelix has explicit session save/restore. Do not add sesh to the runtime or make it a dependency before it solves a Zellij-native problem better than Zellij/Yazelix.
- Source checked: https://github.com/joshmedeski/sesh README describes sesh as a CLI for creating/managing tmux sessions quickly using zoxide.
close_reason: Completed evaluation. Decision: no first-party sesh dependency/integration now; revisit only as optional docs or adapter after explicit Yazelix resurrection exists.

title: Stop zjstatus widget polling from saturating CPU
description: After fixing zjstatus widget command paths, enabled workspace/AI/token widgets can aggressively spawn `yzx_control` and query the pane-orchestrator status bus from zjstatus. This polling path can consume excessive CPU and destabilize the machine. The immediate fix should make dynamic widgets safe by default: no 1-second status-bus polling, no repeated expensive Zellij pipe calls from the bar, and no crash-grade process churn.
acceptance_criteria: Generated zjstatus config no longer polls status-bus widgets at 1-second cadence; empty or unsupported dynamic widgets remain quiet; the bar cannot spawn a high-frequency yzx_control/zellij-pipe loop; focused tests defend the safe intervals or disabled dynamic polling behavior.
notes:
Log/code evidence 2026-04-29:
- `~/.config/yazelix/user_configs/yazelix.toml` has workspace, ai_activity, token_budget, and all agent usage widgets enabled.
- Generated layouts under `~/.local/share/yazelix/configs/zellij/layouts/` show `command_workspace_interval "1"`, `command_ai_activity_interval "1"`, and `command_token_budget_interval "5"` with resolved `libexec/yzx_control` paths.
- This means the previous fix changed the widgets from broken/empty placeholders into active high-frequency `yzx_control zellij status-bus-*` polling, which calls the pane-orchestrator status bus through Zellij.
Immediate repo mitigation: raise status-bus widget intervals to workspace=30s, ai_activity=30s, token_budget=60s and test that the shipped template cannot regress to 1s polling.
Reopened 2026-04-29: throttling was insufficient. Live run still showed the Zellij server climbing from ~29% to ~35% with no stuck yzx_control/ccusage children, and zjstatus rendered style markup literally in the workspace command output. Local recovery disabled dynamic widget entries in user_configs/yazelix.toml with a timestamped backup. Next repo fix should suppress dynamic zjstatus command placeholders until a cached/status-file widget architecture exists.
close_reason: Fixed with emergency suppression. Live validation showed the installed top zjstatus path still made the Zellij server climb with no stuck child process, so dynamic workspace/AI/token/agent usage widget names now remain config-valid but render nothing, and the shipped zjstatus template no longer defines their yzx_control command pollers. Local recovery disabled those entries in user_configs/yazelix.toml and stripped the current generated layout cache. Follow-up yazelix-d1i6.1 tracks restoring the widgets through cached facts. Verified with yzx dev rust fmt --check, yzx dev rust check, yzx dev rust test zjstatus, yzx dev rust test onboard, cargo test -p yazelix_bar, and yzx_repo_validator validate-contracts via cargo run.

title: Rebuild dynamic status-bar widgets on cached facts
description: The emergency CPU fix suppresses workspace, AI activity, token-budget, and agent-usage zjstatus command widgets because live polling through zjstatus/Zellij can make the Zellij server spin and can render style markup literally. Reintroduce these widgets only through a cached fact reader or equivalent architecture that does not run pane-orchestrator pipes or ccusage commands from each zjstatus plugin instance. Acceptance: dynamic widget names render real data again without direct zjstatus command polling, generated templates contain no per-widget yzx_control command definitions, and a regression check covers multiple tabs/templates without command fan-out.
acceptance_criteria: Dynamic widget names render from window-local cached facts without zjstatus opening pane-orchestrator pipes or running ccusage providers; generated templates use only cache-reader commands for dynamic widgets; tests cover cache round-trip, missing-cache silence, multi-window cache path derivation, and synced pane-orchestrator wasm.
notes: Implemented a launch-scoped status-bar cache. Startup exports YAZELIX_STATUS_BAR_CACHE_PATH beside the per-window config snapshot. The pane orchestrator now requests ReadSessionEnvironmentVariables, serializes active-tab status facts when they change, and invokes yzx_control zellij status-cache-write through Zellij's command runner. Zjstatus dynamic widgets call yzx_control zellij status-cache-widget, which only reads the local cache and never opens a Zellij pipe or runs ccusage. Agent usage widgets are cache-reader-ready and render precomputed summaries when present, with provider refresh intentionally left out of zjstatus polling.
close_reason: Implemented window-local cached status-bar facts. Dynamic widgets now render through cache readers, pane orchestrator writes active-tab status facts into the per-window cache, wasm is rebuilt/synced, and focused checks plus validators pass. Follow-up yazelix-l1q9 tracks a throttled agent-usage producer for real usage summaries.

title: Fix stale config-root runtime env leaking into Zellij helper scripts
close_reason: Completed: runtime/Nushell resolution now recovers from stale config-root runtime env, and the flake install smoke defends the exact regression path from issue #526.

title: Inventory every test file with lane, contract, score, and verdict
close_reason: Completed

title: Own KGP Zellij package boundary
description:
PR #596 fixed a downstream NixOS build failure where Yazelix replaced `pkgs.zellij.src` with `yazelixZellij` but inherited source-coupled Cargo vendor metadata from the consumer's nixpkgs package. That made invalid package states possible whenever the consumer's `pkgs.zellij` version/vendor tree did not match the KGP fork.

Architectural direction:
- Stop modeling KGP Zellij as a shallow `overrideAttrs { src = yazelixZellij; }` of arbitrary consumer `pkgs.zellij`.
- Introduce a Yazelix-owned KGP Zellij derivation helper whose `src`, `version`, and `cargoDeps` are constructed together.
- Have graphics runtime variants consume that helper instead of ad-hoc override metadata from `pkgs.zellij`.
- Keep ordinary non-graphics/runtime-host tool source modes explicit and fail fast if unsupported.

Acceptance criteria:
- There is a single helper/boundary that builds the pinned `yazelixZellij` package.
- It is impossible for the flake to override the KGP Zellij `src` without also providing matching Cargo vendor deps.
- A consumer-style validation evaluates or builds `lib.<system>.mkYazelix { pkgs = olderNixpkgs; }` for a graphics runtime variant, so repo CI catches mismatches not visible with the current flake lock.
- The fix avoids broad compatibility shims and does not add duplicate package logic.

Context: https://github.com/luccahuguet/yazelix/pull/596
notes: Release-note linkage: v17.1 should name this as one of the post-v17 commits that introduced a Yazelix-owned Zellij fork/package boundary for the Kitty Graphics Protocol passthrough path used to restore Yazi image previews through Zellij.
close_reason: Implemented owned KGP Zellij package boundary. `packaging/yazelix_kgp_zellij.nix` now couples the pinned fork `src`, version, and Cargo vendor hash; graphics runtimes call that helper instead of open-coding a `pkgs.zellij` source override. Added a narrow Nix customization validator check that poisons consumer `pkgs.zellij.cargoDeps` and proves the KGP helper still returns `zellij-0.44.3-vendor`, avoiding a broad source linter. Verification: `cargo fmt --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml --all --check`; `yzx_repo_validator validate-nix-customization-api`; `yzx_repo_validator validate-flake-interface`; poisoned helper `nix eval`; `nix build .#runtime_ghostty --no-link --no-write-lock-file`. LOC scorecard vs origin/main: +42 code LOC total, packaging +16 net, maintainer +28 net.

title: Reduce duplicate shellhook/setup chatter during yzx launch rebuilds
close_reason: Completed

title: Audit and classify yzx command families by backend coupling
description: Audit the exported `yzx` command surface from `core/yazelix.nu` and `yzx/*.nu` and classify each command family by backend coupling. The goal is not a flat "lite or not" list, but an honest map of which surfaces are backend-required control-plane commands, which are backend-agnostic workspace/config UX, which are runtime-owned distribution/install surfaces, and which are genuinely mixed seams that need refactoring before `Yazelix Core` decisions are honest. Cover canonical families such as env/run/refresh, launch/restart, doctor/status, desktop/update, menu/edit/config/import/open, popup/cwd/reveal, packs/gc, and the purely informational commands.
design:
Treat this as a command-family audit grounded in the actual exported defs, not in docs/help text. Use `core/yazelix.nu` plus `yzx/*.nu` as the source of truth, and fold aliases into canonical families instead of listing every leaf command separately. For each family, record: the canonical commands it covers, its primary owner bucket, why it belongs there, the current code evidence for that coupling, and whether a future `Yazelix Core` edition would plausibly keep it unchanged, narrow it, or drop it.

Do not force every command into a fake binary choice. The audit should allow a fourth subtype for backend-agnostic but runtime-owned/distribution surfaces so commands like `yzx desktop launch` or `yzx update runtime` do not get mislabeled as full environment-backend requirements. Be explicit about mixed seams instead of pretending the current architecture is already clean.

The code pass already suggests these likely families to verify:
- Backend-required control plane: `yzx env`, `yzx run`, `yzx refresh`, probably `yzx packs`, and probably `yzx gc`
- Backend-agnostic workspace/config UX: `yzx cwd`, `yzx reveal`, `yzx popup`, `yzx config`, `yzx edit`, `yzx import`, `yzx open`, `yzx keys`, `yzx tutor`, `yzx screen`, `yzx why`, `yzx sponsor`, `yzx whats_new`
- Runtime-owned distribution/install surfaces: `yzx desktop *`, `yzx update runtime`, possibly parts of `yzx update nix`
- Mixed/refactor-needed seams: `yzx launch`, `yzx restart`, `yzx status`, `yzx doctor`, and `yzx menu`

The output should leave behind a small ranked shortlist of the genuinely mixed command families that deserve refactoring next, rather than just a spreadsheet of labels.
acceptance_criteria: There is a concrete command-family matrix covering the exported `yzx` surface from `core/yazelix.nu` and `yzx/*.nu`, with aliases folded into canonical families. Each family is classified as one of: backend-required, backend-agnostic, runtime-owned/distribution, or mixed/refactor-needed, with a short reason grounded in the current code. The audit explicitly identifies the most important mixed seams to refactor next instead of pretending every command already fits cleanly. The result is precise enough to guide `yazelix-qv8c`, `yazelix-qow`, and later backend-adapter/Rust work without having to re-argue the command surface family by family.
notes:
Initial code-evidence pass:
- The exported surface is defined in `nushell/scripts/core/yazelix.nu` plus `nushell/scripts/yzx/*.nu`.
- The main mixed seam is not just `yzx launch`; it is the cluster of `launch`, `restart`, `status`, `doctor`, and `menu`, where backend state, runtime integrity, install/distribution surfaces, and workspace UX still overlap.
- `yzx desktop *` and `yzx update runtime` look different from `yzx env`/`run`/`refresh`: they are runtime/distribution ownership surfaces, not generic workspace UX, but they also do not need the full environment-control semantics of the current backend.
close_reason: Completed: added the command-family backend-coupling audit spec and linked it from the architecture map.

title: Delete data-heavy Nushell owners aggressively and prove any remaining renderer logic must stay
description: A large remaining slice of Nushell is not shell logic at all. Files like ascii_art.nu and constants.nu are mostly data, metadata, or presentation tables and should leave Nu aggressively unless a retained logic core can be proven irreducible.
design: Prefer non-Nu assets or Rust-owned data surfaces wherever that deletes Nu code cleanly. Data does not get to stay in Nu by default. If an engine remains, prove that it cannot cleanly move without worse complexity.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Data-heavy Nu files are classified into delete, asset, Rust-metadata, and irreducible-logic buckets. 2. The largest data-heavy Nu owner is targeted for file deletion or major collapse. 3. Any surviving renderer logic has explicit proof of irreducibility. 4. No extra wrapper layer is introduced.
close_reason: all steps complete

title: Define the deletion budget for data-heavy Nu files
description: Audit ascii_art.nu, constants.nu, and adjacent static-data-heavy Nu surfaces and classify what should become assets, extracted data tables, Rust-owned metadata, or surviving logic. Be explicit about constants.nu: separate large extractable tables from tiny irreducible boot/runtime constants.
design: Separate data from behavior. The point is to stop storing large tables and art blobs in Nu code.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The data-heavy keep-versus-extract split is explicit. 2. The first extraction lane is explicit. 3. Surviving logic is justified.
close_reason: Completed

title: Extract welcome and screen art data out of ascii_art.nu
description: Move the large welcome and screen art payloads out of ascii_art.nu into assets or another non-Nu data surface so Nushell only keeps the minimal rendering behavior.
design: This is a delete-first data extraction, not a UI rewrite.
acceptance_criteria: 1. ascii_art.nu is materially smaller. 2. The art data no longer lives as large Nu code blobs. 3. Existing screen and welcome behavior stays intact.
notes: Dependency decision: add no new crates. Keep front-door renderer logic in-house and extract only static frame/pattern data out of ascii_art.nu; do not introduce a fake Rust renderer in this bead.
close_reason: Completed

title: Extract large static tables from constants.nu and adjacent metadata surfaces
description: Move large static tables out of constants.nu and adjacent metadata-heavy Nu code where they do not need to be shell-owned, then delete or shrink the Nu owners while keeping tiny irreducible runtime constants where they honestly belong.
design: Prefer one canonical owner for large metadata tables instead of parallel Nu constant blobs. Do not churn tiny boot/runtime constants just for language purity.
acceptance_criteria: 1. At least one large static-table Nu owner such as constants.nu materially shrinks or disappears. 2. Tiny irreducible runtime constants are explicitly kept if they still belong in Nu. 3. The surviving owner for extracted tables is explicit. 4. No duplicate metadata tables remain by habit.
notes: Dependency decision: add no new crates. Extract only inert metadata/table payloads out of constants.nu and adjacent Nu surfaces; keep live shell logic and version ownership in place.
close_reason: Completed

title: Delete or demote redundant data-shaping Nu helpers after extraction
description: After the data-heavy extractions land, remove the Nu helper code that only existed to shape or carry those data blobs.
design: Cleanup only after ownership transfer. Keep the rendering code minimal.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Data-shaping helper code is smaller or deleted. 2. The surviving split between assets, Rust metadata, and Nu rendering is explicit.
notes: Data-shaping cleanup deleted utils/config_schema.nu and utils/upgrade_notes.nu after confirming the live schema diagnostics are Rust-owned and welcome release copy can use upgrade_summary.nu. Updated nushell_budget.toml and front-door/data specs to reflect the lower runtime-helper/data surface.
close_reason: Completed delete-first Rust/Nu owner cuts, documentation cleanup, and validation

title: Prove whether ascii_art.nu's deterministic screen and welcome engine truly must remain in Nu
description: Review the remaining deterministic engine logic inside ascii_art.nu after data extraction and decide whether that logic can move to Rust, assets, or a smaller owned surface, or whether a narrow remainder is truly irreducible.
design: Do not keep the engine in Nu because it feels UI-shaped. Require a concrete why-not-Rust, why-not-asset argument at the level of the surviving logic.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The deterministic engine remainder is classified as move, delete, or provably irreducible Nu. 2. Any move-worthy slice gets a concrete follow-up bead. 3. If a remainder stays, the proof is explicit and narrow enough to stop cargo-cult survival.
notes:
Decision pass after payload extraction: evaluate whether the remaining ascii_art.nu logic is still a real shell/front-door renderer owner or whether a later Rust cut would delete it honestly end to end.
Decision: after payload extraction, the remaining ascii_art.nu engine stays Nu-owned for now. The live surface is width-aware frame composition, ANSI rendering, and Game of Life stepping consumed directly by shell-owned welcome/screen playback. A Rust port now would mostly recreate the same renderer behind another bridge, so the honest stop condition is recorded in docs/specs/front_door_and_data_nushell_budget.md.
close_reason: Completed

title: Add behavior-backed boids style variants
description: The current boids renderer uses several colors for visual identity, but behavior has no matching classes. Add explicit boids variants so color/style communicates real behavior instead of arbitrary per-index coloring.
design: Implement three boids style variants: boids_predator, boids_schools, and boids_flow. Predator should be ambient predator/prey: mostly prey, 1-3 slow predators, no killing/population changes, predators disturb flock movement without making the welcome stressful. Schools should use 2-3 species where same-species boids align/cohere and all boids separate. Flow should use a single flock with a slow deterministic flow-field/wind bias for wave-like movement. Random selection inside the boids family should choose the three variants with equal chance. Keep the overall random welcome family split separate: Game of Life, Boids family, Mandelbrot.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Public screen/welcome style resolution accepts boids_predator, boids_schools, and boids_flow. 2. boids remains either an alias for the safest default variant or is intentionally kept as the legacy/basic style, with the choice documented. 3. Colors map to behavior classes/species/roles, not arbitrary boid index identity. 4. Random boids-family selection chooses predator, schools, and flow equally. 5. Tests defend each variant's behavioral contract and that random boids selection is balanced. 6. Docs/config comments list the new variants without implying unrelated package or renderer changes.
notes: Dependency decision before implementation: implement boids variants in-house with the existing yazelix_screen boids engine and std only. No production crates, no dev crates. Rejected alternatives: adding animation/physics crates would be disproportionate for deterministic terminal welcome behavior. Packaging impact: none.
close_reason: Completed: boids styles now include behavior-backed flow, predator, and schools variants with tests for variant resolution, motion, and color roles.

title: Clarify v15 'non-Rust reboot' wording in docs and specs
description:
Why this issue exists and what needs to be done:
Current v15 messaging uses phrases like "trimmed non-Rust reboot", but the live workspace layer still depends on Rust in an important way through the Zellij pane orchestrator and related workspace contracts. The intended meaning is that v15 narrowed Rust compared with the broader roadmap, not that Rust was eliminated.

What needs to happen:
Review the main wording surfaces and update them so they communicate the real product boundary: v15 trimmed the product and narrowed Rust usage, while future/selective Rust work remains in scope. Avoid wording that implies Yazelix v15 is Rust-free if that is not true.

Likely surfaces to review:
- README.md
- docs/specs/v15_trimmed_runtime_contract.md
- docs/architecture_map.md
- Any release/history/roadmap text that repeats the same framing

Acceptance criteria:
- User-facing and maintainer-facing wording no longer implies that v15 removed Rust entirely
- The docs describe the actual current state: Rust remains in the workspace orchestration layer, but the product was trimmed and Rust scope was narrowed
- The wording supports the longer-term plan to expand or rewrite pieces in Rust without contradicting current docs
close_reason: Completed: replaced the misleading 'non-Rust reboot' framing with wording that says v15 is a trimmed reboot with narrower Rust scope, while the pane orchestrator and workspace layer still rely on Rust.

title: Document Yazelix subsystem code inventory and LOC snapshot
close_reason: Documented subsystem LOC inventory and linked it from architecture map

title: Canonicalize devenv CLI resolution and PATH ordering
description: Yazelix currently conflates the standalone devenv CLI, the project devenv input, and whatever devenv happens to be first on PATH. Fix the contract by introducing one shared host-tool resolver for devenv, using it for internal invocations and version reporting, and repairing Nushell PATH restoration so stale initial PATH entries do not outrank the current shell PATH.
close_reason: Canonicalized remaining maintainer/internal devenv invocations onto the shared resolver and made Nushell PATH restoration explicitly preserve current-shell precedence over stale saved entries.

title: Disable 32-bit nixGL wrapper support in bundled Linux runtimes
description:
Yazelix currently imports nixGL with `enable32bits = true`, which pulls duplicate 32-bit Mesa/LLVM paths into the bundled Linux graphics wrapper closure. Yazelix terminal runtimes are 64-bit, so the default bundled graphics wrapper should not pay for 32-bit OpenGL/Vulkan support unless a future explicit mode asks for it.

Measurements from the current nixGL input:
- `nixGLMesa` with 32-bit support: 2,121,580,360 bytes closure
- `nixGLMesa` without 32-bit support: 1,092,916,560 bytes closure
- current GL+Vulkan wrappers together: 2,160,340,584 bytes unique closure
- GL+Vulkan wrappers without 32-bit support: 1,131,675,520 bytes unique closure

Acceptance criteria:
- Keep `nixGLMesa` as the bundled OpenGL wrapper.
- Import nixGL with 32-bit support disabled for Yazelix-owned Linux runtimes.
- Avoid switching to `nixGLIntel`; Yazelix has no Intel-specific media-driver requirement and Mesa is the clearer generic wrapper.
- Update package-size docs with the new bundled-wrapper policy and measured savings.
- Validate the Nix package/contract surface and measure the changed runtime closure.
- Leave broader `host`/`none` graphics-wrapper source mode work to `yazelix-linux-graphics-wrapper-source-mode-6nhcg`.
close_reason: Kept bundled nixGLMesa/Vulkan wrappers but disabled 32-bit nixGL support, updated package-size docs, measured default/lean/yzxterm closures, and passed package/docs/Nix validators.

comments.author: lucca
comments.text:
Implemented. `packaging/runtime_tool_registry.nix` now imports nixGL with `enable32bits = false` and `enableIntelX86Extensions = false`, while keeping bundled `nixGLMesa` and `nixVulkanMesa` package selection. Measurements after the change:
- default `.#packages.x86_64-linux.yazelix`: 3,294,782,160 bytes closure, 820 paths; direct `nixGLMesa` closure 1,092,916,560 bytes.
- lean package-builder profile: 2,353,775,928 bytes closure, 445 paths.
- `.#packages.x86_64-linux.yzxterm`: 2,872,668,472 bytes closure, 688 paths; direct 64-bit-only GL/Vulkan wrappers about 1,131,675,520 bytes unique.
- current publish workflow delta after the change: `yazelix` contributes 2,860,776,832 incremental unique NAR bytes after KGP/Helix outputs; `yazelix_wezterm` remains 242,610,464 incremental unique NAR bytes.
Validation: built default `yazelix`, `yzxterm`, lean package-builder profile, current workflow outputs, `.#checks.$system.kgp_package_contracts`; ran `validate-docs-experience`, `validate-nix-customization-api`, `validate-nixpkgs-package`, and `git diff --check`.

title: Fix workspace pipe protocol target
description: Workspace widget disappeared after switching from command_workspace to pipe_workspace because the pane orchestrator sends zjstatus pipe content to the pipe name workspace while the rendered zjstatus placeholder/config uses pipe_workspace. Align the pushed pipe protocol name with the actual placeholder and sync the runtime wasm.
acceptance_criteria: The pane orchestrator sends workspace labels to zjstatus pipe name pipe_workspace; child tests defend the protocol payload; pane-orchestrator wasm is rebuilt/synced into Yazelix; main repo closes the tracking bead and pushes the synced fix.
close_reason: Completed: corrected the pane-orchestrator workspace pipe protocol target from workspace to pipe_workspace so it matches the rendered {pipe_workspace} zjstatus placeholder. Rebuilt and synced the pane-orchestrator wasm. Verified cargo test in the pane-orchestrator child, focused main status/materialization tests, and validate-pane-orchestrator-sync.

title: Define zjstatus wasm refresh ownership for yazelix-bar
description: yazelix-bar now vendors its own assets/zjstatus.wasm while the main Yazelix runtime still vendors configs/zellij/plugins/zjstatus.wasm. Decide the supported maintainer refresh flow: update child first and bump the main flake input, teach yzx dev update to coordinate the child repo, or move to a shared artifact owner. Acceptance: no undocumented manual copy step is required to keep the standalone bar and integrated runtime on the intended zjstatus pin.
notes: Decision: yazelix-bar no longer stores assets/zjstatus.wasm. The child package installs zjstatus.wasm from its pinned zjstatus flake input. Main Yazelix forwards #yazelix_bar with yazelixBar.inputs.zjstatus.follows = zjstatus, so the forwarded package uses the main runtime pin. The integrated runtime still owns configs/zellij/plugins/zjstatus.wasm through yzx dev update. Verification: child cargo test, child nix build .#yazelix_bar, child nix flake show; main cargo check -p yazelix_core --all-targets, nix build .#yazelix_bar, nix build .#runtime, validate-contracts, validate-docs-experience, validate-flake-interface, git diff --check.
close_reason: Completed: child bar packages zjstatus from its own flake input, main forwarded package follows the main zjstatus pin, docs record refresh ownership, and package/runtime validations pass.

title: Document and validate a lean Home Manager runtime profile
description:
Yazelix already supports meaningful per-user storage reductions through Home Manager `runtime_tool_sources`, component toggles, and `agent_usage_programs`. Create a documented lean profile that uses existing supported knobs without changing default behavior.

Existing knobs from the current contract:
- Host-source leaf tools such as `mise`, `carapace`, `git`, `neovim`, `lazygit`, `bottom`, `tombi`, `fd`, `rg`, `jq`, `starship`, `zoxide`, `fzf`, `helix`, `yazi`, and `steel`.
- Disable optional tools such as `steel`, `macchina`, `p7zip`, `poppler`, and `resvg`.
- Set `agent_usage_programs = []` when Codex/Claude usage widgets are removed or intentionally host-provided.
- Disable `components.screen` only when welcome/screen behavior is also disabled.

Questions:
1. Which lean defaults are safe enough to recommend to advanced users?
2. How much closure reduction does each documented knob produce?
3. Which user-visible features are lost or degraded by the lean profile?
4. Should the profile remain docs-only, or become a Home Manager example/module preset later?

Acceptance criteria:
- Build and measure at least one lean Home Manager/package-builder runtime shape.
- Document the exact Nix snippet and tradeoffs in the appropriate docs surface.
- Include expected feature losses, especially host-sourced Yazi/Helix/Steel and disabled preview helpers.
- Keep default packaged Yazelix fully bundled.
- Add follow-up beads if a first-class preset is justified after measurement.
close_reason: Built and measured a lean package-builder shape, documented the Home Manager lean profile and tradeoffs, linked it from installation/package-size docs, and created follow-up bead for removing bundled mise/tombi.

comments.author: lucca
comments.text: Documented lean profile in `home_manager/README.md#lean-runtime-profile`, linked from `docs/installation.md`, and summarized in `docs/package_sizes.md`. Built and measured the lean package-builder shape through `git+file://`/`lib.${system}.mkYazelix`: closure 3,382,427,280 bytes, 509 paths. Measured default package through reporter `--build`: closure 4,323,440,624 bytes, 884 paths. The documented profile keeps terminal/Nushell/Zellij/Nix/POSIX/graphics wrappers bundled, host-sources large leaf tools, disables optional helpers, disables cursors/screen, removes Codex/Claude status widgets, and sets `agent_usage_programs = []` only with those widgets removed. Follow-up created for default runtime ownership of `mise` and `tombi`: `yazelix-remove-bundled-mise-6r7kp`.

title: Fix yzx cwd renaming the wrong Zellij tab
description: Sometimes tab renaming updates the wrong Zellij tab instead of the active tab. In the reported case on March 25, 2026, running yzx cwd in the current tab renamed the tab immediately to the left rather than the current tab. The same wrong-tab rename behavior was also observed when opening from Yazi into the editor. Treat this as a tab-targeting bug in the active-tab and cwd-sync flow: commands and editor-launch flows must resolve and rename only the current Zellij tab, never an adjacent tab or another tab in the session.
design: Start from first principles: yzx cwd should act on the active Zellij tab the user invoked it from, not on whichever tab state file or tab identifier was last touched elsewhere in the session. Verify how the command resolves the current tab identity, how that identity is translated into a tab rename target, and whether stale cross-tab state, pane-to-tab mapping, or tab-index math can redirect the rename one tab to the left. Prefer a fix that uses stable current-tab identifiers rather than inferred position or most-recently-updated state.
acceptance_criteria: There is a clear reproduction or at least instrumentation for the wrong-tab rename bug in a multi-tab session. Running yzx cwd from the active tab renames only that active tab, and opening from Yazi into the editor also renames only that same active tab. Neither flow may rename the tab to the left or any other tab in the session. Add focused validation for active-tab resolution and a regression check covering at least two or three tabs with distinct names and cwd values, with logs showing the invoked tab, resolved tab identifier, triggering action, and final renamed tab.
close_reason: Cannot reproduce

title: Make yzx keys layout match status visual family
notes:
Outcome
- removed the old boxed `yzx keys` table chrome and synthetic `#` column
- aligned the keys layout more closely with the grouped status visual family
- kept the dense reference data and section structure intact

Verification
- `nix develop -c cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/yazelix_core/Cargo.toml --lib keys_commands::tests::renders_table_style_root_discoverability_surface`
- `nix develop -c cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/yazelix_core/Cargo.toml --test yzx_control_public_commands`

title: Evaluate toggleable Zellij hints and bottom-bar replacement options
description:
Investigate whether Yazelix can reclaim the native Zellij bottom hint/status bar row while preserving useful key/mode hints on demand. Re-evaluate zjstatus-hints under this framing and compare other plugin/options, including any Zellij ghost-style plugin that can show transient hints or overlays.

Acceptance criteria: document whether the native bottom bar can be disabled/toggled cleanly, what plugin options exist, what tradeoffs each option has for screen real estate and maintenance, and the recommended Yazelix direction.
design:
Investigation result, 2026-04-28:

Problem framing:
- Yazelix currently spends one fixed row on the top zjstatus tab/status surface and one fixed row on Zellij's native bottom `zellij:status-bar` hint surface. The bottom row is useful for mode/key discoverability but permanently costs vertical space and visually clashes with the Yazelix surface.
- In the current tracked layouts, the native bottom row is a literal `pane size=1 borderless=true { plugin location="zellij:status-bar" }` in every main template. That means reclaiming the row is a layout/materialization decision, not a zjstatus styling tweak.

Native Zellij options:
- Built-in `status-bar`: good mode-aware hints, but it is a resident layout pane. I found no clean runtime config flag that toggles this pane on/off while preserving the row when hidden. The clean path is to omit it from generated layouts or replace it with a different resident pane.
- Built-in `compact-bar`: upstream has a useful floating tooltip sub-mode. It can subscribe to ModeUpdate/InitialKeybinds, create a floating tooltip instance, and close it when returning to base mode. However, the shipped plugin is still a compact tab/status bar first. Direct adoption duplicates Yazelix's top zjstatus row or still costs a resident row. Best use is as source/inspiration for a first-party Yazelix hints overlay, not as a drop-in default.

External plugin options:
- `b0o/zjstatus-hints`: directly solves mode-aware hints for zjstatus via named pipe and can hide hints in base mode. It still consumes top-bar width, adds another plugin/vendoring/permission/update path, and introduces pipe coordination. Keep the previous no-default-adopt decision unless we later want an explicit optional widget.
- `karimould/zellij-forgot`: on-demand floating plugin for remembering keybinds and other entries. This aligns with the screen-real-estate goal better than a resident bar, and it can be a UX reference. It is still a generic third-party plugin and duplicates Yazelix's existing `yzx keys` data surface.
- `vdbulcke/ghost`: this is the Zellij ghost plugin the user likely meant. It spawns floating command terminal panes, effectively an interactive zrf. It is not a key-hints/status-bar replacement and overlaps Yazelix's pane-orchestrator popup/menu capability.
- `zellij-cb` and `zj-status-bar`: compact/status bar replacements, not row-free hint surfaces. `zj-status-bar` is archived. Neither should be a default Yazelix dependency for this problem.

Recommendation:
- Implement the user-visible change under the existing bottom-bar bead, `yazelix-2ex.1.7.6`, as a bottom-row reclamation project: remove or default-disable the resident native `zellij:status-bar` row from generated Yazelix layouts, while preserving the top zjstatus surface.
- Add an explicit on-demand hints/help surface, preferably first-party: launch `yzx keys`/`yzx hints` in a managed floating popup through the pane orchestrator. This gives users discoverability without paying a permanent row.
- If we still want context-aware Zellij mode hints after that, prototype a small first-party overlay using the upstream compact-bar tooltip design as the model. Do not adopt ghost or generic status-bar replacements for the default path.
close_reason: Completed investigation and recorded implementation direction in yazelix-2ex.1.7.6

title: Explore full deep Seahorse Misiurewicz Mandelbrot zoom
description: Research and prototype a future Mandelbrot screen path that targets a full deep Seahorse/Misiurewicz zoom: between-bulb valleys, tentacle/plant-like spiral structures, and a convincing self-similar return. This should not replace the current correct period-3 valley-crop loop unless it preserves seam continuity, bounded terminal CPU cost, deterministic rendering, and user-visible quality. Likely requires better coordinate selection, possibly higher iteration budgeting, and maybe an optional renderer path if f64/terminal resolution becomes the limiting factor.
notes:
2026-04-26 Rust dependency gate: Production crates: none. Dev-only crates: none. In-house/std logic: keep the existing CPU escape-time renderer and local Complex64 math.
