Rejected alternatives: Bevy/Fyrox/Ratatui/bigfloat for the shipped terminal runtime. Packaging impact: no new dependencies or Nix changes.

2026-04-26 implementation: mandelbrot now uses the Seahorse Valley Misiurewicz path directly. Removed the separate public mandelbrot_seahorse mode and deleted the old period-3 minibrot branch because the user rejected the boring first path and did not want fragmentation.

2026-04-26 continuity correction: the first Seahorse attempt looked better but failed spatial continuity. The fix keeps the canonical Misiurewicz center and repelling-cycle multiplier, uses a shallower integer derivative-power loop, starts from a tighter Seahorse viewport, and raises the bounded iteration floor to resolve filaments across the full loop. Added regression tests for sampled-frame detail, exact cycle-boundary repeat, penultimate-to-first visible continuity, monotonic zoom, and bounded iteration cost.
2026-04-26 second manual runtime test: 10-power Seahorse loop still looked static. Fix direction: use 121 repelling-cycle powers because its accumulated rotation is within 0.001 turns of a whole number while giving >9000x zoom, switch exterior rendering to repeated smooth contour bands with zoom phase compensation, raise bounded iterations to 800..2500, and add seam blending for the last 10 percent of the loop so the reset is visually continuous.
2026-04-26 final implementation pass: committed to a 121-power loop (>9000x zoom, <0.001 turn orientation error), smooth contour-band coloring with zoom phase compensation, 800..2500 bounded iteration budget, and deterministic seam blending over the final 10 percent. Focused tests now require material mid-loop visual difference, sampled-frame variation, exact loop-boundary repeat, penultimate-to-first continuity, orientation alignment, and bounded work.
2026-04-26 third manual runtime test: 121-power contour loop is still visually wrong: gray, sparse dot texture, high-frequency color noise, and repeated contour wallpaper. Next pass replaces contour wallpaper with distance-estimate boundary rendering, filled glyph density, and coarse color regions.
2026-04-26 final visual correction after screenshot: contour bands were too gray/sparse/noisy/repetitive. Replaced single-pass contour wallpaper with a two-pass distance-estimate renderer: compute boundary scores for the whole frame, normalize into local filled density bands, render only shade/block glyphs instead of dot glyphs, and use coarse 256-color neon bands for vibrant color without per-cell noise. Added tests defending no dotted texture, sufficient filled density, vibrant 256-color ANSI, mid-loop visual change, and seam continuity.
close_reason: Reworked Mandelbrot into filled distance-estimate neon rendering after runtime screenshot feedback

title: Remove dead AI activity/token-budget status widgets
description: Remove token_budget from the supported status-bar widget surface and stop presenting ai_activity as a normal user-facing widget until Yazelix has reliable activity facts behind it.
acceptance_criteria: token_budget is no longer accepted or documented as a widget_tray entry; ai_activity is removed from default/onboard/docs public widget lists if it has no working product behavior; tests and config contracts are updated; stale implementation code is deleted where it no longer has a caller.
notes: Implemented delete-first cleanup: removed token_budget from the main config contract, default config/docs, bar registry, zjstatus template commands, yzx internal subcommands, cached status widget renderer, and pane-orchestrator session extension schema. Removed ai_activity from the public config/bar/yzx command surfaces because it has no working user-facing behavior today; retained only internal pane-orchestrator AI pane activity fact plumbing for future use.
close_reason: Completed: token_budget public/runtime surfaces removed; ai_activity removed from supported config/bar command surfaces until it has reliable product behavior; contracts, tests, and synced pane-orchestrator wasm updated.

title: Audit and refactor Yazelix Rust for idiomatic quality across repos
description: Run a repo-wide Rust quality pass across the Yazelix main repo and first-party child repos. Yazelix started as a Nushell-first project, and some Rust code still reflects migration pressure: oversized modules, bridge-shaped APIs, procedural style, unclear ownership boundaries, mixed runtime/maintainer duties, and tests that may defend implementation trivia instead of contracts. The goal is top-quality idiomatic Rust without changing product behavior accidentally.
design: Use delete-first refactoring. Start with an evidence-based inventory, then split the work into focused slices. Favor idiomatic Rust ownership, small cohesive modules, typed domain boundaries, clear errors, trait use only where it buys something real, and tests that defend behavior. Do not perform a broad rewrite in one commit. Include child repos such as the pane orchestrator and other first-party Rust crates where the same migration-era style issues exist.
acceptance_criteria: Inventory covers main Rust crates and first-party child repos with concrete findings. Follow-up child beads or implementation slices are created for each meaningful refactor area. Completed slices preserve user-visible behavior, run focused Rust checks/tests, avoid new dependencies unless justified, and include LOC scorecards where main-repo simplification is claimed. The pass identifies any remaining Nushell-shaped Rust boundaries that should be deleted, collapsed, moved, or explicitly kept.

title: Split yzx_repo_validator main into small validator command handlers
description: cargo-crap no-LCOV complexity triage reported `rust_core/yazelix_maintainer/src/bin/yzx_repo_validator.rs:19` `main` at CC 52. Refactor the validator binary entrypoint so argument parsing, command selection, and validator execution live in smaller named handlers instead of one large branching function.
design: Preserve the public `yzx_repo_validator` command surface. Extract command parsing/dispatch from validator behavior, prefer typed command enums or small handler functions that match existing maintainer crate style, and avoid introducing a framework dependency. Keep error messages explicit and unchanged unless the refactor reveals a clearer failure boundary.
acceptance_criteria: 1. `main` in `yzx_repo_validator.rs` is reduced to argument collection, dispatch, and error reporting. 2. Existing validator subcommands keep the same CLI behavior and diagnostics. 3. Focused tests or existing maintainer validation cover the moved dispatch paths. 4. `yzx dev rust check` or the nearest maintainer Rust check passes. 5. If this is claimed as simplification, include the Yazelix LOC scorecard result in the close reason.
notes: Isomorphism card: preserve yzx_repo_validator argv syntax, validator calls, stdout/stderr labels, and exit codes. Refactor only command parsing/dispatch/report rendering inside the bin target. No new crates; std + existing maintainer APIs only. Rejected deletion candidates: no validator command is proven dead because runtime ownership docs and maintainer command surfaces reference these names.
close_reason: Completed: main split to parse_invocation/run_validator_command/emit_report with simple validator metadata and argument-sensitive handlers. cargo-crap no-LCOV: target main CC 52 -> 1; remaining validator helpers run_validator_command/failure_label CC 6, emit_report CC 7. Verified cargo fmt --check, cargo test -p yazelix_maintainer --bin yzx_repo_validator, cargo check -p yazelix_maintainer --bins. LOC scorecard HEAD~1..HEAD: maintainer +161 total, delta_code +133; budget debt owner is parent yazelix-dse2 for follow-up command-table shrink after behavior-preserving slices.

title: Split yzx_control main command dispatch into smaller handlers
description: cargo-crap no-LCOV complexity triage reported `rust_core/yazelix_core/src/bin/yzx_control.rs:1565` `main` at CC 43. Refactor the `yzx_control` binary dispatch so the entrypoint does not accumulate every command branch directly.
design: Preserve the current bridge-oriented command surface. Move command-family routing into small named helpers or typed dispatch structures close to the owning command modules. Do not add clap or a broad command framework unless a separate dependency decision justifies it. Keep the binary as a thin adapter over `yazelix_core` behavior.
acceptance_criteria: 1. `yzx_control.rs` `main` no longer owns a large flat branch over unrelated command families. 2. Public command names, args, stdout/stderr behavior, and exit semantics are preserved. 3. Focused tests or existing CLI bridge tests defend at least representative moved command families. 4. Rust checks/tests for the touched crate pass. 5. Any follow-up command-family extraction beads are created before closing if the first slice intentionally leaves large handlers behind.

title: Decompose run_yzx_update into explicit update workflow phases
description: cargo-crap no-LCOV complexity triage reported `rust_core/yazelix_core/src/update_commands.rs:356` `run_yzx_update` at CC 42. Refactor the update command coordinator into smaller phases so runtime-source selection, dry-run/no-op behavior, command execution, and reporting are easier to test independently.
design: Start by identifying the stable user-visible update contract. Extract pure or near-pure planning decisions before command execution where possible. Keep updater behavior fail-fast and avoid adding fallback paths. Preserve the supported update surfaces and no-op messaging.
acceptance_criteria: 1. `run_yzx_update` is split into cohesive helper functions or typed phases with clear ownership boundaries. 2. Update command behavior is preserved for upstream/home_manager/no-op/error cases. 3. Focused regression tests cover the highest-risk branch moves. 4. Relevant Rust checks/tests pass. 5. Close notes include the before/after cargo-crap CC if measured, or explain why it was not remeasured.
notes: Isomorphism card: preserve yzx update command names, flag parsing, confirmation behavior, Nix/Home Manager safety checks, stdout/stderr messages, and return codes. Refactor only run_yzx_update orchestration into parse/dispatch/workflow phases. No new crates; std + existing command helpers only. Dependency decision: production crates none; dev-only crates none; in-house/std sufficient because this is small command parsing and process orchestration already owned by yazelix_core; rejected clap/structopt because yzx update public surface is already established and broad CLI framework migration is out of scope; packaging impact none.
close_reason: Completed: run_yzx_update is now parse_yzx_update_command(args)?.run(), with typed YzxUpdateCommand variants and separate help, Determinate Nix, upstream profile, and Home Manager workflow phases. cargo-crap no-LCOV: target run_yzx_update CC 42 -> 2; remaining update workflow helpers expose next complexity layer (run_upstream_update CC 10, run_home_manager_update CC 8, run_nix_update CC 7). Verified cargo fmt --check, cargo test -p yazelix_core update_commands --lib, cargo check -p yazelix_core. LOC scorecard HEAD~1..HEAD: runtime +124 total, delta_code +96; budget debt owner is parent yazelix-dse2 for follow-up helper extraction if these update phases are touched again.

title: Split yzx_core run into typed private subcommand dispatch
description: cargo-crap no-LCOV complexity triage reported `rust_core/yazelix_core/src/bin/yzx_core.rs:136` `run` at CC 41. Refactor the private `yzx_core` bridge dispatcher so top-level parsing and command execution are split into smaller typed handlers.
design: Keep `yzx_core` private and bridge-shaped; this is not a public CLI redesign. Extract command parsing into a typed enum or small parser helpers, route to existing library functions, and leave behavior-owned modules in `yazelix_core` rather than moving domain logic into the bin target.
acceptance_criteria: 1. `yzx_core.rs` `run` is reduced to parsing, dispatch, and error conversion. 2. Private bridge subcommands keep their existing invocation syntax and output contracts. 3. Tests cover representative dispatch paths or existing bridge contract tests are updated to cover the refactor. 4. Rust checks/tests pass for the touched crate. 5. Any remaining high-complexity command families are captured as child follow-up beads instead of being hidden in the close reason.
notes: Isomorphism card: preserve private yzx_core subcommand names, argv parsing, stdout/stderr behavior, and exit/error conversion. Refactor only top-level command recognition and dispatch in the bin target. No new crates; std + existing lexopt/core APIs only. Rejected deletion candidates: bridge subcommands are internal but still consumed by runtime/Nushell wrappers, so command removal is out of scope.
close_reason: Completed: yzx_core run now parses helper command into typed HelperCommand and dispatches via a standard handler table, keeping runtime-materialization.repair as the special summary-aware path. cargo-crap no-LCOV: target run CC 41 -> 2; parse_helper_command/classify_helper_command CC 2, dispatch_helper_command CC 4. Remaining high-complexity command-family parsers captured as yazelix-dse2.4.1, yazelix-dse2.4.2, and yazelix-dse2.4.3. Verified cargo fmt --check, cargo test -p yazelix_core --bin yzx_core, cargo check -p yazelix_core --bins. LOC scorecard HEAD~1..HEAD: runtime +42 total, delta_code +21; budget debt owner is child follow-up parser beads.

title: Decompose yzx_core status.compute request parser
description: cargo-crap no-LCOV after the top-level yzx_core dispatcher split reports run_status_compute at CC 37. Extract status.compute argument parsing and request construction into typed helper phases while preserving the private bridge command contract. Acceptance: run_status_compute no longer owns one large option-parsing branch; status.compute flags, defaults, output envelope, and errors are preserved; focused tests cover representative explicit and missing-argument paths; relevant yazelix_core Rust checks pass.

title: Decompose yzx_core materialization helper parsers
description: cargo-crap no-LCOV after the top-level yzx_core dispatcher split reports high CC in terminal, ghostty, yazi, zellij, helix, launch, and runtime-materialization helper parsing. Extract shared typed parsing/request-builder phases where they reduce duplication without changing private bridge command syntax. Acceptance: high-complexity materialization parser functions are split into cohesive helpers; command flags, env-vs-explicit validation, output envelopes, and error messages are preserved; focused tests cover at least one env-only and one explicit-path branch; relevant yazelix_core Rust checks pass.

title: Decompose yzx_core config state and env helper parsers
description: cargo-crap no-LCOV after the top-level yzx_core dispatcher split reports high CC in config.normalize, config-surface.resolve, config-state.compute, config-state.record, runtime-env.compute, install-ownership.evaluate, and related runtime request parsers. Extract typed parser/request-builder helpers while preserving private bridge behavior. Acceptance: the largest config/state/env helper functions no longer own full option parsing plus request construction; supported flags, from-env exclusivity checks, output envelopes, and diagnostics are preserved; focused tests cover representative request-json, from-env, and explicit-path validation paths; relevant yazelix_core Rust checks pass.

title: Make pane orchestrator Rust unit tests hermetic again
description: `cargo test --manifest-path rust_plugins/zellij_pane_orchestrator/Cargo.toml --lib` is currently not hermetic in this repo and can fail on maintainer machines because a transitive OpenSSL-dependent dependency path leaks host/header assumptions into the test lane. This is maintainer debt, not a current release blocker: the live pane orchestrator wasm still rebuilds and syncs cleanly, and higher-level popup/menu behavior remains defended by the existing Nushell and generated-config tests.
design: Keep the current trust model in place until this is fixed: `yzx dev build_pane_orchestrator --sync` plus the higher-level popup/menu contract tests remain the authoritative verification path for live behavior. Then make the Rust unit-test lane hermetic again by fixing the maintainer-shell/toolchain boundary or otherwise eliminating the host OpenSSL mismatch from the supported test path. Prefer a fix that improves the supported maintainer workflow instead of adding more fallback verification paths.
acceptance_criteria: A supported maintainer path can run `cargo test --manifest-path rust_plugins/zellij_pane_orchestrator/Cargo.toml --lib` successfully without depending on host OpenSSL header state. The bead documents the temporary verification policy that keeps trusting `yzx dev build_pane_orchestrator --sync` plus higher-level popup/menu tests until the hermetic Rust test lane is restored. The fix does not weaken the current live-behavior verification story.
close_reason: Fixed by adding pkgs.openssl to maintainer shell and setting OPENSSL_DIR, OPENSSL_LIB_DIR, OPENSSL_INCLUDE_DIR. All 18 pane orchestrator unit tests now pass hermetically in nix develop.

title: Deduplicate Zellij popup wrapper bootstrap
description: The Zellij popup wrapper scripts still duplicate the same runtime-script resolution and wrapper bootstrap pattern. Replace that duplication with one shared runtime helper so popup/menu wrappers stay aligned with the canonical floating-wrapper contract.
close_reason: Completed

title: DRY shared runtime tree assembly across flake and nixpkgs package outputs
description:
The Yazelix packaging layer still duplicates the common runtime tree assembly across `yazelix_runtime_package.nix` and `mk_yazelix_package.nix`. Both build nearly the same runtime content: linked repo directories, top-level config/docs files, `bin/nu`, and the generated `bin/yzx` launcher, then diverge only in the final wrapper and package-shape details.

That duplication is now one of the clearest remaining packaging cleanup targets. It increases maintenance cost and makes authority changes, runtime-file additions, and install-path fixes easier to apply inconsistently.
design:
Keep the current top-level split: one flake/runtime artifact and one nixpkgs-style wrapped package. Do not try to collapse those two products into a single generic builder. Instead, extract one small shared helper for the common runtime tree assembly and let the final package files keep only the wrapper-specific behavior.

The shared helper should own the repeated linked directories/files, the common `bin/nu`, and the common `bin/yzx` launcher surface. Wrapper-specific PATH setup and any final package-shape differences should remain local to the callers. Prefer a narrow helper such as `mk_runtime_tree.nix` over a mini-framework.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The common runtime tree assembly no longer has to be edited in two places. 2. `yazelix_runtime_package.nix` and `mk_yazelix_package.nix` remain separate at the top level but share one maintained implementation for the repeated runtime tree contents. 3. Existing flake install, nixpkgs package, and nixpkgs submission validators still pass after the refactor. 4. The refactor does not reintroduce a second authority for the runtime-owned `devenv` path or weaken the current package/install contract.
close_reason: Completed: extracted shared runtime tree assembly into mk_runtime_tree.nix and kept only wrapper-specific behavior in the package callers.

title: Trim devenv GC ownership from yzx gc
description:
v15 no longer ships or owns runtime-local devenv, but yzx gc still resolves a devenv CLI and runs devenv gc before nix-collect-garbage. Delete that stale owner surface, keep yzx gc as a Nix store cleanup command, update tests/docs, and remove devenv_cli.nu if no live users remain.

Acceptance: yzx gc no longer resolves or invokes devenv; yzx gc still reports phase progress, handles du/nix-collect-garbage behavior, and preserves deep/deeper modes; stale devenv-specific gc tests/docs are rewritten or deleted; no live imports of devenv_cli.nu remain.
notes: Implementation: removed the devenv gc phase from yzx gc, deleted devenv_cli.nu after clearing live imports, rewrote gc maintainer tests for the Nix-only contract, and updated CLI/nixpkgs/backend-contract docs.
close_reason: Completed: yzx gc is now Nix-only, stale devenv CLI resolver and GC docs/tests were removed or rewritten, and maintainer GC tests pass.

title: Investigate zjstatus-hints
notes:
2026-04-26 subsystem review: evaluate zjstatus-hints as part of the yazelix_bar backend/options surface, not as a separate scattered layout tweak.
Investigation decision, 2026-04-28:
- Reviewed upstream b0o/zjstatus-hints as of 2026-04-28: it is a small Rust Zellij plugin that extends zjstatus with context-aware mode keybinding hints through a named pipe (`pipe_zjstatus_hints`); latest visible release is v0.1.4 from 2025-06-08. Source: https://github.com/b0o/zjstatus-hints
- Current Yazelix bar architecture already centralizes widget rendering through rust_core/yazelix_bar, config_metadata/main_config_contract.toml, and the single vendored zjstatus template. Recent agent/status widgets use zjstatus command slots, not extra wasm plugins.
- Product fit: hints would help onboarding and mode discoverability, but they duplicate part of `yzx keys`, consume scarce status-bar width, and would require a second plugin permission/vendoring/update path plus named-pipe coordination. That is too much surface for a default or immediate optional widget.
- Decision: do not adopt zjstatus-hints now. If hints become a priority, implement them as a first-party `yazelix_bar`/pane-orchestrator status fact or as an explicitly packaged optional `hints` widget with a full vendored-plugin contract, not as an ad-hoc layout tweak.
- No code change needed for this bead.
close_reason: Recorded no-adopt decision for zjstatus-hints

title: First desktop launch after HM update fails silently
description: First Yazelix launch after a Home Manager or flake version update can fail without feedback when started through the desktop entry, while subsequent launches work. Diagnose startup refresh ordering and make the first launch deterministic with visible errors.
close_reason: Completed: desktop launch now forces terminal config generation when generated-state inputs changed, preventing the first launch after a Home Manager/flake runtime update from opening with stale terminal configs.

title: Audit zellij_commands/status.rs for bar ownership and deletion
description: Audit the large status command surface after the yazelix_bar child-repo path is defined. Decide what belongs in standalone bar code, what remains Yazelix-specific status integration, what belongs in zjstatus config, and what can be deleted from Yazelix core.
design: Treat rust_core/yazelix_core/src/zellij_commands/status.rs as a deletion target, not a file to preserve. Classify every major responsibility: status-bus cached facts, zjstatus command rendering, bar preset generation, agent usage widgets, workspace widgets, CPU/RAM helpers, user customization, and Yazelix-only session facts. Move reusable bar behavior to the child repo where appropriate, keep only the integration adapter in Yazelix core, and delete duplicated generated KDL/status rendering paths that are better represented by structured bar config. Prefer deleting custom machinery that zjstatus already owns.
acceptance_criteria: The audit produces an ownership matrix and implementation follow-ups or a direct patch. Main-repo status LOC decreases or the remaining integration adapter is justified. The result states which status behaviors are generic bar package behavior, Yazelix-only behavior, zjstatus-native behavior, and deleted behavior. Existing status-bar/widget behavior remains covered by focused contract tests or validators.
notes: Audit result: status-bar ownership is now recorded in docs/contracts/status_bar_ownership.md. Deleted the stale direct zellij status-bus-workspace command path because generated zjstatus templates already use status-cache-widget commands and tests assert the direct path does not return. status.rs dropped from 3,516 to 3,465 lines; tracked Rust budget ratcheted from 75,562 to 75,507 raw lines. Follow-ups created: yazelix-ff4n for agent usage/provider status refresher split, yazelix-vmay for typed integrated zjstatus command-definition rendering, and yazelix-7ace for cursor widget ownership with yazelix-cursors. Verification: cargo fmt --all --check, cargo test -p yazelix_core status_cache, zellij_materialization, status_bus, yzx_control; validate-contracts; validate-docs-experience; validate-rust-ownership-budget; git diff --check.
close_reason: Completed: recorded status-bar ownership matrix, deleted stale direct status-bus-workspace command path, ratcheted Rust LOC budget after the deletion, and created focused follow-up beads for larger status reductions.

title: Verify whether the LiteLLM PyPI compromise affected Yazelix
description: Audit whether the malicious LiteLLM PyPI releases affected Yazelix directly or through any shipped dependency or package surface.
notes: Assessment: no litellm or LiteLLM references were found in the repo, shipped config, or package declarations. Yazelix does not appear to ship or pin LiteLLM directly in its maintained runtime surface, so this issue is treated as not affected.
close_reason: Not affected: no litellm or LiteLLM references were found in the repo, shipped config, or maintained package declarations, so Yazelix does not appear to ship the compromised PyPI package in its supported runtime surface.

title: Codify cross-repo release transaction policy
description: Implement the decision from yazelix-qni2 by adding a concise durable workflow rule for coupled child-repo/main-repo release transactions. The goal is to prevent local override-input validation from masking unpublished child commits without adding heavyweight process for trivial child-only changes.
design: Prefer a short AGENTS.md subsection or a tightly scoped maintainer-doc note. Keep the existing pane-orchestrator warning, but generalize the release gate to any child repo consumed by main flake.lock. Do not add scripts or validators unless manual repetition proves the prose rule is insufficient.
acceptance_criteria: AGENTS.md or the selected maintainer workflow doc states the coupled release transaction rule: classify trivial child-only versus coupled runtime changes; push child commits before main lock updates; update main flake.lock to the GitHub revision; run main validation without --override-input; close Beads and push Dolt only with the published main change. The note stays concise and does not create new payback, compatibility, or process-heavy follow-up work.
close_reason: Completed: added a concise Cross-Repo Release Transactions rule to AGENTS.md, generalized the child-publish/main-lock/no-overrides validation gate, and narrowed the pane-orchestrator-specific note to point at the general rule.

title: Evaluate nh for Home Manager and Nix switch UX
design: Evaluate nh as a maintainer/user workflow improvement for Home Manager switches and Nix rebuild visibility. Focus on whether nh home switch improves progress output, diff visibility, stale/competing switch diagnosis, and cleanup ergonomics compared with raw home-manager switch. Do not replace Yazelix runtime update semantics unless the UX and failure modes are clearly better.
acceptance_criteria: The evaluation compares raw home-manager switch with nh home switch for the local Yazelix/Home Manager setup. It records command examples, required Home Manager configuration if any, failure/lock behavior, and whether nh should be added to personal Home Manager, maintainer shell, Yazelix docs, or not adopted. If adopted, create follow-up implementation beads rather than mixing adoption into the evaluation.

title: Use Node 24 cache action in CI workflows
description: The new cache steps used actions/cache@v4, which passes but emits a hosted annotation because v4 targets deprecated Node 20. Move Yazelix cache steps to actions/cache@v5 so the CI optimization does not add avoidable warning noise.
acceptance_criteria: All actions/cache uses in touched CI workflows reference v5; workflow YAML parses locally; hosted CI no longer reports the cache Node 20 deprecation annotation.
close_reason: Completed: moved both new actions/cache steps from v4 to v5 after hosted CI showed the Node 20 deprecation annotation. Verified touched workflow YAML parses with python3/PyYAML.

title: Make Rust dev commands use the loaded runtime toolchain
description: Fast Rust developer commands such as yzx dev rust fmt should not require re-entering nix develop for normal agent and maintainer loops. They should prefer the already loaded Yazelix/Home Manager/Nix runtime toolchain and avoid silently falling through to a broken host rustup toolchain. Acceptance: yzx dev rust fmt --check and yzx dev rust check use the loaded runtime toolchain when available; missing toolchain errors are explicit and actionable; no routine fast command shells out to nix develop; tests or validators cover the command/toolchain selection behavior.
notes: Implemented the narrow fix in nushell/scripts/yzx/dev.nu: fast Cargo commands still run directly from the loaded environment, and fmt now exports RUSTFMT to the resolved rustfmt on PATH so Cargo cannot fall through to a broken rustup default. Deliberately did not add Rust to the default Yazelix runtime/Home Manager package because normal users should not pay for a maintainer toolchain; maintainers can keep cargo/rustc/rustfmt in their loaded profile/runtime.
close_reason: Fixed: yzx dev rust fmt pins RUSTFMT to the loaded PATH rustfmt and fast Rust commands keep using direct loaded cargo/rustc instead of nix develop.

title: Add a misc pack and place tokei there
description: Imported GitHub issue #519.
close_reason: Completed: the misc pack with tokei is already in the catalog, default packs file, Home Manager module, and docs, and GitHub issue #519 is closed.

title: Fix config UI terminal picker test expectation
description: The broad yazelix_core config_ui test filter fails because enum_string_list_picker_toggles_subvalues_with_space expects alacritty while the scripted navigation currently toggles kitty from the terminal.terminals allowed-value order. Decide whether the test or picker behavior is stale and fix it.
close_reason: Fixed stale config UI test expectations. The terminal multi-choice test now expects kitty, matching the current terminal.terminals allowed-value order and scripted key path. The metadata contract test now accepts object_list, which is used by helix.steel_plugins. Validation: cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core config_ui; cargo fmt --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml --all --check; git diff --check.

title: Add logo-forward welcome style
design: Add the branded baseline welcome style that should work as the strongest static final frame and as a short animated logo-forward reveal. It should adapt to narrow, medium, and wide layouts and define the overall visual language the other styles should respect. The implementation may use lightweight live frames or pre-rendered playback, but it must land on a clear final branded frame.
acceptance_criteria: Selecting welcome_style = logo produces a branded welcome sequence that fits narrow layouts, ends in a readable resting frame, and is defended by width-aware layout tests plus startup render checks.
close_reason: Completed

title: Refresh first-party child repo docs
description: Update README and related docs so the First-Party Child Repositories section reflects the current child repo set: reusable flake packages, the pane-orchestrator wasm child, the ratconfig Cargo child, Yazi assets, and the temporary Zellij/Yazi Kitty graphics forks.
close_reason: Updated README and docs/yazelix_collection.md so first-party child repo docs include the current reusable children, pane-orchestrator wasm child, ratconfig Cargo child, forwarded popup package output, and temporary Zellij/Yazi Kitty graphics forks. Verified docs validator, source references, and git diff --check.

title: Evaluate dedicated desktop prelaunch surface after terminal-backed launcher
description:
## Decision
Pending. Evaluate whether Yazelix needs a dedicated desktop prelaunch surface after the terminal-backed launcher is proven.

## Rationale
Terminal-backed launchers make failures visible, but can show a bootstrap terminal/window-class mismatch. The decision should balance polish against the requirement that launch failures remain visible and actionable.

## Alternatives Considered
Keep Terminal=true only; add a small prelaunch dialog; use terminal-specific launch wrappers; or defer until repeated user reports justify the extra surface.

## Existing Context
Terminal-backed desktop entries make pre-terminal launch progress and failures visible, but they can briefly show a desktop-environment bootstrap terminal with a different window class before the managed Yazelix terminal appears. After yazelix-c1kw ships, evaluate whether the boring Terminal=true contract is good enough or whether a small dedicated prelaunch wrapper/dialog would reduce flicker and WM-class mismatch without reintroducing invisible failures or fallback launch paths.

title: Add VISUAL to POSIX bootstrap runtime env
description: Follow-up cleanup: the Nushell canonical runtime env now exports VISUAL alongside EDITOR, but shells/posix/runtime_env.sh still does not seed VISUAL during POSIX bootstrap. That leaves the bootstrap layer slightly behind the runtime env contract even though managed popup and editor paths already normalize it later.
design: Keep this small. Audit whether POSIX bootstrap should export VISUAL with the same resolved value as EDITOR, and only do it if that preserves the trimmed bootstrap boundary without duplicating too much editor-resolution logic. The goal is contract consistency across bootstrap and managed runtime entrypoints, not app-specific behavior.
acceptance_criteria: If POSIX bootstrap is meant to participate in the stable runtime editor contract, shells/posix/runtime_env.sh exports VISUAL consistently with EDITOR; bootstrap and Nushell-managed entrypoints no longer disagree about the base editor env surface; validation covers the intended contract without expanding bootstrap complexity unnecessarily.
close_reason: Not needed: editor env ownership belongs to the Nu-side canonical runtime env, while POSIX bootstrap stays minimal and only bootstraps runtime location, PATH, and YAZELIX_NU_BIN. Reopen only if a concrete pre-Nu bootstrap path actually needs VISUAL.

title: Harden first Yazi file open against transient orchestrator readiness
description:
## Problem
The macOS discussion behind yazelix-tjc7 suggests one remaining failure family after the keybinding ownership fix: the first Yazi open from a sidebar-only `yzx enter` session may race pane-orchestrator readiness. `yzx_control zellij open-editor` currently asks the orchestrator to open an existing managed editor before creating a new editor pane; a transient pipe timeout/not-ready response can make the first Enter look like a no-op.

## Expected Behavior
The first Yazi file open should either target an existing managed editor or create the initial editor pane without requiring a second keypress or Esc delay. Transient pane-orchestrator startup readiness should be retried briefly or produce a clear diagnostic instead of silently consuming the user action.

## Scope
Investigate the `open-editor` first-open path after `yzx enter`, especially on macOS/Home Manager reports. Consider a bounded retry, not-ready handling, or missing-editor fallback only when it cannot corrupt an existing editor pane. Keep this separate from Zellij keybind ownership work in yazelix-583h.

## Acceptance Criteria
- The first `yzx_control zellij open-editor <file>` from a sidebar-only Yazelix tab is robust when the pane orchestrator is still settling.
- The fix distinguishes a true managed-editor failure from a transient missing/not-ready first-open state.
- Regression coverage defends the chosen retry/fallback behavior.
- The result documents whether the macOS Esc report was fully addressed by keybinding ownership, first-open hardening, or both.
close_reason: Completed: open-editor retries transient pane-orchestrator timeout/not_ready responses and creates the first editor pane when readiness still cannot prove a managed editor; added workspace-surface regression test.

title: Delete shared config-state and runtime-env request shims
description: Delete the shared Nushell request-building shims around runtime-env and config-state so Rust owns request construction and Nushell keeps only explicit shell execution or caller-local presentation where that boundary is real.
acceptance_criteria: runtime_env.nu and config_state.nu stop being owner seams, the surviving shell boundary is explicit, and runtime-env plus generated-state parity checks still pass.
notes: Created from docs/specs/config_runtime_control_plane_canonicalization_audit.md. Do not revive ambient host inference or hide materialized-state drift behind new wrappers.
close_reason: all steps complete

title: Define the deletion budget for the runtime-env and config-state shim collapse
description: Decide whether runtime-env and config-state can move together honestly, name every caller that still depends on Nu shell/process behavior, and define the exact deletion budget before implementation.
acceptance_criteria: The bead records the retained runtime-env and generated-state contracts, chooses one combined or split implementation lane explicitly, and names the stop condition if either shim still carries irreducible shell state.
notes:
Key invariants: no ambient host inference, no drift in rebuild-hash semantics, no loss of popup/editor/runtime env behavior.
Completed 2026-04-22: produced docs/specs/runtime_env_config_state_shim_collapse_budget.md. Decision: split the implementation lane. Revise yazelix-ekfc.2 to the config-state owner cut first, then follow with yazelix-ekfc.3 for the runtime-env request cut and explicit exec seam.
close_reason: Completed

title: Rust-own config-state request construction and delete config_state.nu
description: Move config-state request construction and managed-surface resolution behind Rust-owned inputs so Nushell no longer owns argv/path assembly for config-state.compute or config-state.record.
acceptance_criteria: config_state.nu is deleted, environment/bootstrap and launch callers consume the surviving Rust-owned path, and generated-state hash/record invariants still pass.
notes: Split decision from docs/specs/runtime_env_config_state_shim_collapse_budget.md: config-state is the first clean deletion lane because it no longer carries a real shell/process boundary.
close_reason: Completed

title: Rust-own runtime-env request construction and narrow runtime_env.nu to an explicit exec seam
description: Delete the request-building half of runtime_env.nu by moving explicit runtime-env input construction behind Rust-owned code, while keeping any surviving Nu behavior limited to shell env application and argv execution.
acceptance_criteria: Nu no longer reconstructs runtime-env requests as an owner seam, the surviving shell-exec boundary is explicit, and the defended popup/editor/runtime-env tests still pass.
notes: Split decision from docs/specs/runtime_env_config_state_shim_collapse_budget.md: runtime_env.nu still carries a real shell/process seam, so this cut must stop at the explicit exec boundary if full deletion is not honest.
close_reason: Completed

title: Enforce default suite shape and budget guards
description: Add validators that prevent default-suite test creep: no dead tests in default-lane files, canonical tests must stay spec-backed, canonical count budget, and default-suite time budget.
close_reason: Implemented default-suite shape/count and runtime-budget guards, wired into CI, and validated them against the current suite.

title: add a zellij plugin for fullscreen yzx screen
description:
Imported GitHub issue #530.

possibly add the feature for it to run after some minutes of inactivity
design: Use the future yazelix_screen API as the renderer source of truth. If a Zellij plugin is still useful, it should embed or call the screen subsystem and focus on inactivity/session integration rather than reimplementing frame packing, cursor addressing, or animation state.
acceptance_criteria: A fullscreen yzx screen path can run after inactivity without duplicating renderer logic; it uses the same animation/framebuffer contract as yzx screen; current welcome/screen behavior is unchanged; inactivity configuration is explicit and opt-in.
notes: 2026-04-26 subsystem review: fullscreen yzx screen should target the yazelix_screen extension seam after renderer/style extraction, not duplicate terminal output or animation logic in a Zellij plugin.
close_reason: Completed: added opt-in pane-orchestrator idle screen saver config that opens a full-tab yzx screen command pane after inactivity, using the existing yzx screen renderer rather than duplicating animation logic; docs, Home Manager metadata, tests, and synced plugin wasm were updated.

title: Investigate whether a Yazelix-specific zjstatus fork is needed for stable pane frames
description: Imported GitHub issue #521.
design: Treat this as a fork-worthiness investigation, not as an immediate implementation commitment. The purpose is to determine whether Yazelix can support real pane frames without severe flicker while keeping the current zjstatus-style top bar, or whether a Yazelix-specific zjstatus fork is the only honest path. Compare the realistic options: keep pane_frames false, prototype upstream zjframes, or fork zjstatus. The outcome should be a concrete recommendation with explicit reasoning and, if a fork is justified, a clearly scoped follow-up rather than a vague aspiration.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The repo has a concrete written recommendation on whether Yazelix should fork zjstatus for stable pane-frame support. 2. The recommendation explains why the non-fork alternatives are sufficient or insufficient. 3. If a fork is recommended, the issue leaves behind a clear next-step scope rather than a vague idea.
notes: Planning refinement: keep this investigation in three explicit phases: reproduce and classify the current flicker behavior, evaluate the upstream zjframes path under the same scenarios, then make a concrete fork-versus-nonfork recommendation. This should avoid a vague all-at-once investigation.
close_reason: Obsolete: the pane-frame fork investigation was superseded by the simpler direct pane_frames path without zjframes.

title: Reproduce and classify pane-frame flicker with the current zjstatus stack
description: Create a concrete behavior matrix for the current pane-frame problem so the fork decision is grounded in observed failure modes rather than memory or vague impressions.
design: Keep this as a focused reproduction and classification pass. Verify the current behavior across the scenarios that matter: pane_frames off or on, single or multiple panes, search or scroll modes, fullscreen, and any obvious transparency interactions. The result should identify which combinations actually flicker or redraw badly.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The current pane-frame behavior is reproduced in a small concrete scenario matrix. 2. The investigation records which combinations are stable, unstable, or visually unacceptable. 3. Later fork or non-fork decisions can point at this matrix instead of anecdotes.
close_reason: Obsolete: pane-frame flicker investigation was superseded by the simpler direct pane_frames path without zjframes or plugin-driven frame toggling.

title: Evaluate the upstream zjframes path against the same pane-frame matrix
description: Spike the upstream zjframes-based alternative under the same scenarios as the baseline reproduction so Yazelix can compare real behavior instead of guessing whether it helps.
design: Do not treat zjframes as automatically good or bad. Evaluate it under the same matrix as the baseline so the comparison is honest. The point is to learn whether the upstream non-fork path actually produces a stable usable pane-frame experience for Yazelix, not just whether it technically toggles frames.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The upstream zjframes path is tested against the same scenario matrix as the current baseline. 2. The repo records whether zjframes materially improves, fails to improve, or worsens the pane-frame experience. 3. The comparison is concrete enough to inform the final fork recommendation.
close_reason: Obsolete: the upstream zjframes evaluation is no longer relevant after adopting direct pane_frames without zjframes.

title: Decide whether a Yazelix-specific zjstatus fork is warranted for pane frames
description: Use the baseline reproduction and the upstream zjframes evaluation to make a concrete recommendation on whether Yazelix should keep pane frames off, rely on upstream alternatives, or pursue a fork.
design: This is the decision bead, not another open-ended investigation. Compare the baseline and zjframes results, explain the user-experience tradeoffs clearly, and recommend either keeping frames off, pursuing an upstream path, or creating a scoped fork follow-up. If a fork is recommended, define the first concrete scope instead of leaving a vague aspiration.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The repo has a concrete recommendation for pane-frame support based on observed evidence, not guesswork. 2. The recommendation explains why non-fork paths are sufficient or insufficient. 3. If a fork is recommended, the next implementation scope is named clearly.
close_reason: Obsolete: the fork-versus-zjframes decision is no longer the active problem after moving to direct pane_frames without zjframes.

title: Add semantic Yazi integration keybinding remaps
description: Expose semantic remaps for Yazelix-owned Yazi integration actions without taking ownership of arbitrary Yazi-native keymaps.
design: Use the scoped action registry and owner-scoped config model. Candidate actions include opening selected files through Yazelix's managed editor opener, direct zoxide-to-editor retarget, and opening selected directories as workspace panes. Native Yazi behavior remains in ~/.config/yazelix/yazi_keymap.toml.
acceptance_criteria: A yazi-owned semantic action map exists only for Yazelix-owned integration actions; defaults match current generated behavior; settings schema, main config contract, Home Manager option parity, docs, config UI metadata, and behavior tests cover the surface; user yazi_keymap.toml remains the native escape hatch; duplicate or malformed semantic keys fail clearly.
notes: Rust dependency decision for implementation: no new crates. Use the existing action_registry, config contract/schema generation, TOML/JSON parsing already in yazelix_core, and generated Yazi materialization helpers. Build the Yazi semantic action map in-house because the action ids and generated integration commands are Yazelix-owned. Rejected alternatives: adopting a Yazi keymap parser crate, exposing arbitrary native Yazi keymaps through settings.jsonc, or duplicating native keymap ownership. Packaging impact: user runtime gains one settings/config/Home Manager surface for Yazelix-owned Yazi integration keybindings only; native yazi_keymap.toml remains the escape hatch.
close_reason: Completed: added scoped Yazi action registry entries and yazi.keybindings config/Home Manager/schema/UI metadata; generated Yazi keymap now materializes Yazelix-owned integration bindings from the semantic map with duplicate/malformed validation; docs and focused behavior tests cover remap and disable behavior. Filed yazelix-ep4s.1 for the separate open-selected/native-default boundary.

title: Design true Yazi open-selected semantic remap boundary
description: Decide whether Yazelix can safely expose yazi.open_selected_in_editor as a semantic settings.jsonc remap without misleading users, given Yazi's native Enter/o open bindings still exist unless users take native keymap ownership.
acceptance_criteria: The decision documents whether open_selected_in_editor belongs in yazi.keybindings or must remain a native yazi_keymap.toml concern; if supported, it defines exact generated keymap behavior, disable semantics, duplicate validation, and docs/config UI updates; if rejected, docs explain why the native sidecar is the correct ownership surface.
notes: Decision: reject yazi.open_selected_in_editor as a semantic settings.jsonc remap. Yazelix owns the generated [opener].edit target, but Yazi owns the native open keymap entries such as <Enter> and o. A semantic remap would either leave native defaults active or require Yazelix to shadow/no-op native bindings, which violates the owner-scoped keybinding contract. Users should remap native open in ~/.config/yazelix/yazi_keymap.toml; editable files still route through Yazelix's managed editor opener.
close_reason: Decision documented: do not expose yazi.open_selected_in_editor as semantic settings.jsonc config. The native Yazi open keymap owns <Enter>/o; Yazelix owns only the generated edit opener target. Users should remap native open in ~/.config/yazelix/yazi_keymap.toml, where editable files still route through the managed Yazelix editor opener.

title: Split maintainer contract validators by domain
description: Split rust_core/yazelix_maintainer/src/repo_contract_validation.rs by validator domain and delete low-value validator trivia while preserving current contract gates. Keep the maintainer crate in-repo; the goal is smaller, clearer ownership, not a child repo.
acceptance_criteria: repo_contract_validation.rs no longer owns every contract validator directly; at least one coherent domain moves behind a private maintainer module or stale trivia is deleted with proof. Existing relevant validator commands still pass, docs/rust_code_inventory.md is updated if ownership changes, and the LOC scorecard records before/after impact.
notes: 2026-05-07 completed first maintainer validator split. Rust dependency gate kept: no new crates. Moved README/latest-release validation and sync helpers from repo_contract_validation.rs into private repo_contract_validation/readme_surface.rs with public re-exports preserved for yzx_repo_validator, yzx_repo_maintainer, version bump, and update workflow callers. Delete-first result: removed duplicate README latest-series sub-heading checks because the full generated-block comparison already defends those headings. LOC scorecard: repo_contract_validation.rs 4,149 -> 3,856 raw lines; new readme_surface.rs 275 raw lines; net -18 raw Rust lines and -19 tokei Rust code LOC, with one accepted private module file. Budget ratcheted to 73,882 raw Rust lines across 142 files. Verification: cargo fmt --all --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml --check; cargo check --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_maintainer --all-targets; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_maintainer; validate-readme-version; validate-contracts; validate-docs-experience; validate-rust-ownership-budget; git diff --check.
close_reason: Completed first maintainer validator domain split and trivia deletion

title: Home Manager prepare leaves standalone yazelix profile install in place
notes: Detect standalone default-profile yazelix entries from the default profile manifest, surface them as explicit home_manager prepare blockers, remove them during --apply, and expose the mixed-ownership warning through yzx doctor.
close_reason: Completed

title: Split demo GIFs into smaller pieces
description: Split the large demo GIF story into several smaller focused demos so users can understand specific Yazelix workflows without one heavyweight clip doing all the work.
design: The goal is not just smaller files. Each demo should communicate one idea clearly: onboarding, Yazi plus Helix flow, workspace switching, and so on. Coordinate with the preview-asset and git-history cleanup beads so the final asset set is coherent and lightweight.
acceptance_criteria: The docs and website use smaller focused demo assets instead of a single oversized catch-all GIF. Asset file sizes, placement, and narrative purpose are documented in a capture checklist or logged plan so the set remains maintainable.
close_reason: Deferred to the website. GIFs in the repo bloat git history, the README already has a static screenshot at the right weight, and creating feature GIFs twice (README then website) is wasted effort. The asset creation work belongs to the website project.

title: Separate environment-backend concerns from workspace UX and prepare a future Yazelix Core edition
description: Drive the next modularity pass around a clearer separation between Yazelix's environment backend concerns and its workspace/runtime UX layer. The immediate goal is to improve main while keeping devenv: define backend-facing contracts, add a shared runtime preflight checker, reduce hardcoded backend assumptions in the command surface, and classify what would remain viable in a future no-package-management edition. The longer-term motivation is to make a future Yazelix Core edition possible without turning main into a confused multi-backend product.
design: Build on the already-closed modularity framing work in docs/architecture_map.md and docs/workspace_session_contract.md instead of reopening subsystem-boundary debate from scratch. This epic is about making main cleaner while still using devenv: clarify backend-facing contracts, reduce ad hoc backend coupling in the command/bootstrap layer, and make a future Core edition a consequence of better boundaries rather than a speculative fork. Adjacent existing beads such as yazelix-2ex.2.1, yazelix-b16x.1, and yazelix-uwz5 should be treated as parallel cleanup inputs, not displaced by this epic.
acceptance_criteria: There is a coherent bead sequence that sharpens the environment-backend boundary, introduces a shared runtime/preflight dependency story, classifies the current CLI surface by backend reliance, and leaves behind an evidence-based recommendation for or against a future Yazelix Core edition. Main gets cleaner without promising multi-backend support in place.
close_reason: Completed as epic housekeeping: the concrete backend-boundary, runtime-contract, command-coupling, cross-language ownership, and Core-edition decision beads are all closed, and the remaining conclusions now live in durable specs/docs rather than open epic planning state.

title: Define and reduce cross-language ownership boundaries in the runtime/workspace stack
description: Map the current hot-path behavior that crosses POSIX shell, Nushell, Rust plugins, Lua Yazi plugins, and Zellij CLI behavior, then define which language/runtime should own which category of logic going forward. Use that map to identify which seams deserve consolidation rather than letting correctness continue to span too many runtimes.
design: Treat this as an ownership-and-simplification bead, not a rewrite-everything proposal. The current stack is defensible but expensive to debug because too much user-visible behavior crosses language boundaries. Identify the long-lived stateful logic, the thin adapter logic, and the incidental bootstrap glue. Prefer moving hot-path correctness and session ownership into fewer layers while keeping the CLI UX and integration adapters intentionally thin.
acceptance_criteria: 1. There is an explicit language/runtime ownership map for CLI UX, runtime orchestration, live workspace/session state, and integration adapters. 2. The map identifies at least one high-value seam that should collapse into a stronger single owner. 3. Follow-up implementation beads become narrower and aligned with that ownership model. 4. The work informs the broader backend/Core discussion instead of competing with it.
notes:
2026-04-08 streamlining alignment:
- Keep this bead focused on cross-language hot paths, not as a blocker for every local Nushell reduction.
- Immediate Nushell-surface reduction can proceed in `doctor.nu`, `yzx/dev.nu`, `common.nu`, `config_migrations.nu`, `yazi.nu`, and `zellij_config_merger.nu` without waiting for a full language/runtime ownership map.
- The map becomes most important where correctness still crosses Nushell, Rust plugins, Lua Yazi plugins, and Zellij CLI behavior in one user-visible flow.

2026-04-09 completion:
- Added `docs/specs/cross_language_runtime_ownership.md` to map ownership across POSIX shell, Nushell, Rust plugins, Lua Yazi code, and Zellij transport.
- The map identifies the pane orchestrator as the durable owner for live workspace/session truth and keeps Lua/Yazi as an adapter layer.
close_reason: Completed

title: Postmortem: session-global Yazi sidebar state caused cross-tab cwd leaks
description: Symptom: yzx cwd appeared to change the cwd of other tabs, and the same class of issue was suspected around Yazi-driven path changes. Initial suspicion was a pane-orchestrator tab-isolation leak. Actual cause: read_active_sidebar_state in nushell/scripts/integrations/yazi.nu selected the most recently modified Yazi state file for the whole session, so sidebar sync could target another tab Yazi pane. Because every tab starts with Yazi, this produced a real cross-tab leak in normal usage. Fix: resolve the current tab sidebar_pane_id via debug_editor_state, then read that exact sidebar state file. Verified in a disposable two-tab Yazelix session.
design: Keep this as a regression postmortem rather than implementation work. The value is preserving the causal chain: session-global sidebar state looked convenient, but it violated tab locality and produced real cross-tab cwd leaks. Capture the invariant that any sidebar or path-sync state must be keyed to the current tab or pane identity rather than global recency.
acceptance_criteria: The bead clearly documents the regression cause, the violated tab-locality invariant, the final fix, and the guardrail future work should preserve. A maintainer touching sidebar state later can use this bead to avoid reintroducing session-global leaks.

title: Fix Ghostty cursor effect shader path mapping
description: Ghostty cursor color changes still apply, but the cursor effect shaders do not render. Investigation shows the generated Ghostty config references effect shader paths like `./shaders/generated_effects/tail.glsl`, while the shader builder currently emits files named `cursor_trail.glsl`, `cursor_ripple_rectangle.glsl`, and similar. That mismatch leaves Ghostty with a flat colored cursor and no trail or mode-change effect.
close_reason: Completed: Ghostty cursor effect shaders were broken by a generated filename mismatch; the builder now emits the user-facing effect filenames that the generated Ghostty config references.

title: Continue private ratconfig boundary pass
description: Continue moving generic list/detail/editor behavior from config_ui.rs into the private yazelix_ratconfig boundary while keeping Yazelix settings, JSONC patching, Home Manager ownership, native status, cursor config, and runtime apply local.
design: Private boundary only. Do not create a public child repo. Prefer moving generic editor/list/detail behavior and deleting wrapper duplication; reject moves that would drag Yazelix product policy into ratconfig.
acceptance_criteria: config_ui.rs is smaller and delegates more reusable generic behavior to yazelix_ratconfig; product policy remains local; focused config UI tests pass; Rust budget and inventory are updated; LOC delta and rejected candidates are recorded.
notes: Moved generic config UI visible-row filtering and search matching into the private yazelix_ratconfig model boundary. Kept Zellij action-registry detail rendering, JSONC patching, Home Manager/read-only ownership, cursor config, native status, and runtime apply behavior in config_ui.rs. config_ui.rs dropped from 2,837 to 2,749 raw lines; raw tracked Rust budget ratcheted from 72,664 to 72,663. Rejected moving Zellij keybinding detail rendering into ratconfig because it depends on Yazelix action-registry product policy.
close_reason: Completed: row filtering/search moved into private ratconfig model boundary; config UI tests and Rust budget validator pass.

title: Design wallpaper image integration paths
description: Parent planning bead for GitHub #501. Decide how a curated wallpaper image pack should enter Yazelix without conflating the delivery paths. The three current tracks are terminal background integration, yzx screen walls slideshow, and wallpaper-backed static welcome random pool.
design: Keep real bitmap backgrounds at the terminal config layer, not inside Zellij panes. For welcome and yzx screen paths, the near-term contract is terminal-safe rendering derived from the curated source images. Prefer ANSI half-block rendering first because it preserves more color and shape than pure ASCII while still behaving as normal terminal text. Pure ASCII can remain a later optional style if it looks good. Real bitmap rendering inside Zellij is deferred until upstream graphics protocol support is reliable; do not make the wallpaper work WezTerm-only just to get high-fidelity images sooner. Prefer the wallpaper-backed static welcome pool as the low-hanging first slice.
acceptance_criteria: Child beads exist for terminal backgrounds, yzx screen walls slideshow, and wallpaper-backed static welcome random pool. The first implementation records the asset naming convention, manifest or registry shape, licensing requirements, size budget, terminal-safe rendering pipeline, and verification path. The plan explicitly distinguishes terminal-safe in-Zellij rendering from true terminal-emulator background images. Close this parent only when the selected image-pack strategy is implemented or explicitly rejected.
notes: 2026-05-01: Chose terminal-safe rendering as the baseline for welcome and yzx screen wallpaper features while Zellij Kitty graphics/image passthrough remains unresolved upstream. ANSI half-block is the preferred first renderer; pure ASCII is optional later. True bitmap backgrounds remain a separate terminal-emulator config path.

title: Curate initial Yazelix wallpaper image set
description:
## Decision
Pending. Choose the initial legally usable, visually coherent wallpaper set before any wallpaper-backed runtime integration is implemented.

## Rationale
Wallpaper work depends on source assets that survive both normal image use and terminal-safe rendering. Curation first prevents implementation from anchoring on assets that are too large, legally unclear, or visually weak in ANSI half-block form.

## Alternatives Considered
Ship no wallpapers; ship one canonical image; ship a small curated pack; or make wallpapers fully user-supplied.

## Existing Context
Choose the first small wallpaper set before implementation. Start from a larger candidate pool of about 20 strong images, then select 5 to 10 that are visually coherent, legally usable, and small enough to ship without bloating Yazelix.
design: Treat curation as a prerequisite to all runtime integration paths. Prefer a tight first set over shipping every good candidate. Record stable wallpaper IDs, source or authorship, license and attribution status, source format, dimensions, compression budget, dominant palette notes, and any rejected candidates worth revisiting later. Also evaluate whether each candidate survives terminal-safe rendering, with ANSI half-block as the first target and pure ASCII as an optional later variant.
acceptance_criteria: A curated first set of 5 to 10 wallpaper candidates is recorded with stable IDs, source or authorship, license status, source file format, approximate source size budget, expected derived terminal-safe rendering format, and notes on why each image belongs. Each chosen image has at least a qualitative check that it remains recognizable and attractive under ANSI half-block rendering. The decision also states which larger candidate images are deferred or rejected. No implementation bead should assume the wallpaper pack exists until this curation bead is complete.
notes: 2026-05-01: Curation now includes terminal-safe viability. Images should be selected not only as wallpapers but as sources that survive ANSI half-block rendering; pure ASCII is optional later, not the first baseline.

title: Add terminal-safe wallpaper-backed static welcome style
description: Add the low-hanging wallpaper path: a static welcome style that draws from the curated wallpaper image set for users who prefer static welcome screens over the existing animated styles.
design: Use the curated wallpaper manifest as the source of truth. Render through a terminal-safe derived representation in the Rust-owned welcome renderer, with ANSI half-block as the preferred first implementation. Do not depend on Kitty graphics, Sixel, WezTerm-only behavior, or any real bitmap passthrough for the baseline. The welcome content must remain readable in normal, narrow, and split layouts. Avoid making this the default until the visual result is tested.
acceptance_criteria: A static wallpaper welcome style can select one curated image or a random image from the curated pool. The baseline rendering is terminal-safe, preferably ANSI half-block, and works inside normal Yazelix Zellij sessions without relying on upstream graphics protocol support. The style works through the existing welcome_style configuration surface, preserves skip and duration behavior, keeps welcome text readable, and has behavior-backed verification for selection and constrained layout rendering. Documentation or config comments explain that this is a terminal-safe static welcome style, not a true bitmap background.
notes: 2026-05-01: Baseline is terminal-safe rendering while Zellij image passthrough is unresolved upstream. Do not make this WezTerm-only just for real images.

title: Add terminal-safe yzx screen walls slideshow mode
description: Add a yzx screen mode that uses the curated wallpaper image set as a full-screen terminal-safe visual slideshow, changing rendered images on an interval around every 5 seconds.
design: This is a standalone screen/runtime mode, not the startup welcome screen and not a terminal-emulator background. It should reuse the same terminal-safe wallpaper rendering pipeline as the static welcome style, with ANSI half-block as the preferred first renderer and pure ASCII as a possible later style. It should fit the existing Rust-owned yazelix_screen style architecture and exit cleanly on keypress. The mode can later serve as the visual payload for an idle screensaver workflow, but idle detection itself remains separate.
acceptance_criteria: A command such as yzx screen walls can cycle through the curated wallpaper set on a configurable or documented default interval near 5 seconds using terminal-safe derived renderings. It does not require Kitty graphics, Sixel, or WezTerm-only behavior for the baseline. It handles terminal resize, exits cleanly on keypress, avoids excessive CPU use, and has focused verification for image selection, timing boundaries, and terminal cleanup.
notes: 2026-05-01: Baseline slideshow rendering is terminal-safe. High-fidelity bitmap output can be considered later when Zellij graphics protocol support is reliable.

title: Integrate wallpaper pack into terminal backgrounds
description: Use the curated wallpaper image set as optional real bitmap backgrounds in Yazelix-owned terminal emulator configuration where the terminal supports that feature.
design: This is the only track that should treat the images as true terminal-emulator wallpapers. The owner is terminal config materialization, not Zellij panes, welcome rendering, or yzx screen output. This path is not blocked by Zellij image protocol support because the terminal emulator owns the background. Verify support and exact config syntax for Ghostty, WezTerm, and Kitty before implementation. Keep it opt-in until visual quality, platform behavior, and package size are proven acceptable.
acceptance_criteria: Yazelix can configure a selected or random curated wallpaper as a terminal background for supported generated terminal configs, with an explicit disabled state and opacity or equivalent visual-strength control where the terminal supports it. Unsupported terminals are documented explicitly rather than silently pretending to work. The implementation does not imply real bitmap rendering inside Zellij panes. Verification covers generated config output and at least one manually tested supported terminal.
notes: 2026-05-01: Keep this separate from terminal-safe welcome and yzx screen rendering. Terminal backgrounds can use real images because they are configured below Zellij at the terminal-emulator layer.

title: Delete core/yazelix.nu compatibility registry after Rust-root parity
description: Audit outcome: the Rust yzx root already owns public routing, help, metadata, and generated externs, but tests, sweeps, and a few legacy module paths still source nushell/scripts/core/yazelix.nu as a compatibility registry. Delete or demote that file only after replacing those uses with the Rust yzx binary or direct internal modules.
design: Retained behavior: root help/version parity, all surviving internal Nu families, test/sweep entrypoints, and maintainer restart coverage. Candidate surviving owner: rust_core/yazelix_core/src/bin/yzx.rs for public routing plus direct Nu modules for intentionally Nu-owned families. Stop condition: if any runtime-supported path still requires source-able re-export semantics rather than a real yzx binary or direct module entrypoint, record the no-go and narrow the registry instead of deleting it.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Every tracked use of core/yazelix.nu is classified as public compatibility, test fixture, sweep harness, or direct internal module need. 2. Public routing continues through the Rust yzx root without reviving generic Nu fallback dispatch. 3. Tests and sweep helpers no longer require a re-export registry. 4. The file is deleted or explicitly demoted with a precise remaining owner reason. 5. Verification covers public root routing, exported command metadata, and any migrated test/sweep path.
close_reason: Completed

title: Remove legacy no-sidebar startup mode
close_reason: Removed editor.enable_sidebar and built-in yzx_no_side layouts; startup now always uses yzx_side unless an explicit layout override is supplied.

title: Add bottom-terminal family to the no-side layout cycle
description: GitHub issue #491 asks for the no-sidebar layout family to include the same bottom-terminal variant that exists in the sidebar-aware family cycle. The change should add the missing bottom_terminal swap layout to yzx_no_side.swap.kdl and keep it in generated layouts.
close_reason: Added the missing bottom_terminal family to yzx_no_side.swap.kdl and verified generated no-side layouts keep it.

title: Trim deprecated --skip-devenv compatibility from Nix preflight helpers
description: v15 no longer has a normal-user devenv contract, but nix_detector.nu still exports a deprecated --skip-devenv option on check_nix_installation and ensure_nix_available. Remove that dead compatibility surface, simplify the call path, and keep only the real Nix/flakes preflight behavior that still matters for current Yazelix startup and install flows.
close_reason: Completed: removed the dead --skip-devenv option from nix_detector preflight helpers and kept only the real Nix/flakes availability path.

title: Split agent usage status refreshers out of zellij_commands/status.rs
description: After the status-bar ownership audit, zellij_commands/status.rs still owns Claude/Codex/OpenCode Go refresh commands, shared-cache locking, provider parsing, quota formatting, and widget rendering. Split provider-specific refresh/persistence logic into focused Yazelix-core modules without moving it to yazelix_bar, because provider polling and Yazelix session cache paths are integrated-runtime behavior.
design: Keep public yzx_control command names stable. Prefer modules such as zellij_commands/status_cache.rs, zellij_commands/agent_usage.rs, or provider-specific files if that reduces coupling. Do not create a child repo. Preserve status-cache-widget behavior and the first-paint cache behavior. Delete weak tests if they only defend function placement; keep behavior tests around cache freshness, backoff, lock staleness, and plain-text widget output.
acceptance_criteria: status.rs LOC decreases substantially and remaining status.rs responsibilities are status-bus/cache adapter orchestration rather than provider-specific logic. Provider refresh behavior still passes focused cargo tests for status_cache, codex_usage, claude_usage, and opencode_go_usage, and the LOC scorecard records before/after impact.
notes: Implemented by moving provider usage cache paths, hydration, shared-cache readers, refresh/backoff/lock logic, tokenusage parsing, opencode-go DB aggregation, and agent usage widget formatting from zellij_commands/status.rs into zellij_commands/status/agent_usage.rs. status.rs dropped from 3,465 raw lines to 1,548; the extracted module is 1,933 raw lines, so the split itself is roughly +16 raw lines while removing provider-specific ownership from the status adapter. Focused cargo tests passed for status_cache, codex_usage, claude_usage, and opencode_go_usage.
close_reason: Completed

title: Collapse maintainer sweep and test-runner dynamic Nu dispatch seams
description: Audit outcome: maintainer/test_runner.nu still builds dynamic nu -c module strings for sweep lanes, and the sweep path is more fragile than the default test path. The audit also found that sweep dispatch should be checked for missing imports and stale runtime-root assumptions before the ranked deletion budget treats harness Nu as healthy.
design: Retained behavior: yzx dev test default, lint-only, sweep, visual, all, new-window, and profiling behavior. Candidate surviving owner: one explicit test runner with direct script/module invocation and no interpolated command program bodies for sweep dispatch. Stop condition: keep external Nu process execution if needed for isolation, but pass arguments structurally or through checked-in helper entrypoints instead of constructing code strings.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The sweep and visual dispatch paths are verified from the public yzx dev test surface. 2. Dynamic nu -c strings in the runner are removed or justified with a fixed-body/argv boundary. 3. Missing imports or runtime-root assumptions are fixed if confirmed. 4. The default suite, sweep-only lane, and visual lane entrypoints remain clear and separately documented. 5. Verification covers validate_syntax, the default suite, and at least a dry or targeted sweep dispatch check that does not open uncontrolled windows.
notes: Verification 2026-04-22: validate_syntax passes, the maintainer runner now dispatches sweep lanes through direct script argv execution, and public 'yzx dev test --sweep' exercised the new path. The suite still fails on an unrelated Ghostty config validation error for missing terminal.ghostty_mode_effect, which is not caused by the dispatch rewrite.
close_reason: Completed

title: Design user-declared Zellij layout profiles
description:
## Decision
Pending. Decide whether Yazelix should expose user-declared Zellij layout profiles and how Home Manager should render them.

## Rationale
Users want layout customization without forking generated KDL. The design needs to preserve Yazelix's layout-family/session contract and pane-orchestrator ownership semantics.

## Alternatives Considered
Expose only custom layout file paths; expose declarative layout profiles; expose curated presets; or document native Zellij layouts without new Yazelix config.

## Existing Context
A macOS/Home Manager tester asked for a simple way to configure the default and alternative Zellij layouts, ideally through Yazelix/Home Manager options. Evaluate a supported user-declared layout surface that can express workflows such as sidebar/editor/aux panes without requiring users to fork generated layout KDL files.
acceptance_criteria: Decision defines whether Yazelix should expose layout presets, declarative layout profiles, custom layout file paths, or no new layout surface. It must preserve the layout-family/session contract, cover Home Manager option rendering, explain how pane names such as sidebar/editor/aux map to pane-orchestrator ownership, and create implementation follow-ups for any accepted surface.
notes:
2026-05-07 decision start: resolving as part of yazelix-pomi layout ownership gate. Scope is ownership decision for user-declared layout profiles, not implementation.
2026-05-07 decision: defer declarative Yazelix layout profiles. The supported customization boundary remains explicit KDL files plus editor.sidebar_command/editor.sidebar_args. Home Manager should not render a second layout-profile language now. Zellij cleanup should consolidate around config_metadata/zellij_layout_families.toml and copied top-level KDL files rather than preserving duplicate paths for unaccepted profile rendering.

title: Move startup orchestration out of generated_runtime_state.nu into a dedicated startup module
description: generated_runtime_state.nu currently mixes two concerns: thin bridge transport for runtime-materialization.plan/repair-evaluate/apply, and heavy startup orchestration (regenerate_runtime_configs, repair_generated_runtime_state) that coordinates between Rust plan evaluation and Nushell-side config generators. Split the orchestration into a startup-specific module so generated_runtime_state.nu becomes pure thin transport, deleting the mixed-owner seam.
close_reason: Split generated_runtime_state.nu into pure bridge transport and dedicated orchestration. New core/materialization_orchestrator.nu owns regenerate_runtime_configs, record_current_materialized_state, and repair_generated_runtime_state. generated_runtime_state.nu reduced from 224 to 115 lines (-109). Exported apply_runtime_materialization for cross-module use. Updated all 4 callers: start_yazelix_inner.nu, doctor.nu, update_workflow.nu, test_yzx_generated_configs.nu. All targeted tests pass.

title: Fix Alt+z Helix picker and Yazi sync semantics
description:
Alt+z from Yazi currently focuses/retargets Helix but does not open the Helix directory picker, and the sidebar Yazi cwd can remain stale. Preserve the git-root workspace rule: inside a Git repo, the workspace/root cwd should resolve to the repository root, while the Helix picker should open at the selected subfolder context.

Acceptance criteria:
- Alt+z with Helix sends helix.open_directory for the selected directory, not only helix.set_cwd.
- Workspace root retargeting uses the git root when the selected directory is inside a Git repo.
- Helix picker opens at the selected directory/subfolder even when the workspace root is the repo root.
- Sidebar Yazi is synchronized to the selected directory using the current-tab sidebar identity returned by retarget_workspace.
- Existing Neovim and missing-editor paths keep their current behavior.
- Add focused regression coverage for the git-root/subfolder split and picker action.
notes:
Implemented across main Yazelix and sibling yazelix-helix.

Main Yazelix changes:
- yzx_control zellij open-editor-cwd resolves target_dir separately from workspace_root.
