- workspace_root uses the existing git-root resolver, so selecting a subdirectory inside a Git repo retargets the tab/editor cwd to the repository root.
- Helix path sends helix.open_directory with working_dir=workspace_root and picker_dir=target_dir instead of only helix.set_cwd.
- Sidebar Yazi sync uses the current-tab sidebar identity returned by retarget_workspace and cd targets the selected directory.

Yazelix-helix changes:
- helix.open_directory accepts optional picker_dir.
- working_dir remains the Helix cwd.
- picker_dir, when present, is the file-picker root; absent picker_dir keeps legacy behavior.
- Published child commit: luccahuguet/yazelix-helix@173a8328bce9b60f9f2d2c1114b6a6ce7b7752a1.

Validation passed:
- cargo fmt --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml --all -- --check
- cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core --test yzx_control_workspace_surface
- cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core helix_bridge_client
- cargo fmt -- --check in yazelix-helix
- cargo check -p helix-term in yazelix-helix
- nix build .#yazelix_helix --no-link -L
- nix build .#runtime_yzxterm --no-link -L
- git diff --check in both repos

Current gate: local runtime update in progress for maintainer manual testing. Keep bead open until Alt+z is manually verified in a fresh Yazelix session.

title: Best-effort macOS child wasm target build fixes
description:
GitHub issue 604 reports a v17.3 aarch64-darwin Home Manager build failure in the pane-orchestrator child package with rustc missing wasm32-wasip1 core. Static verification shows v17.3 declares the wasm std target, so try a best-effort hardening fix that forces the child buildPhase to use the combined Fenix cargo/rustc and checks the wasm target libdir before cargo build.

Acceptance criteria:
- Child package buildPhase explicitly uses the combined Fenix toolchain that includes wasm32-wasip1 rust-std.
- Linux child package build still passes.
- aarch64-darwin derivation metadata shows the explicit toolchain use/check.
- Main runtime validates with a local override.
- If the child change is accepted, follow the cross-repo release transaction before updating main flake.lock.
notes:
Best-effort fix implemented in sibling child repo ../yazelix-zellij-pane-orchestrator: buildPhase now exports CARGO/RUSTC/PATH from the combined Fenix rustToolchain and checks rustc --print target-libdir --target wasm32-wasip1 before cargo build.

Verification on 2026-06-02 from Linux:
- nix build .#yazelix_zellij_pane_orchestrator --no-link passed in the child repo.
- nix eval --raw .#packages.aarch64-darwin.yazelix_zellij_pane_orchestrator.drvPath instantiated the Darwin derivation.
- nix derivation show for that Darwin drv confirmed the explicit CARGO/RUSTC exports and wasm target-libdir check.
- nix build .#runtime_ghostty --override-input yazelixZellijPaneOrchestrator ../yazelix-zellij-pane-orchestrator --no-link --no-write-lock-file passed in the main repo.
- Child fix published as luccahuguet/yazelix-zellij-pane-orchestrator@b2b1cc4c28518bcf2aff1eb0d1c4e2e9c4b9eab9.
- Main flake.lock updated to that published revision.
- nix build .#runtime_ghostty --no-link --no-write-lock-file --print-out-paths passed without override inputs.
- cargo run --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_maintainer --bin yzx_repo_validator -- validate-child-release-transaction passed.

Follow-up from GitHub issue 604, 2026-06-02: reporter confirmed the pane-orchestrator fix is picked up at main ac7d6c3a and found the same missing wasm32-wasip1 target failure in yazelix-zellij-popup. Implemented the same toolchain hardening in sibling child repo ../yazelix-zellij-popup.

Popup verification on 2026-06-02 from Linux:
- nix build .#yazelix_zellij_popup --no-link passed in the child repo.
- nix eval --raw .#packages.aarch64-darwin.yazelix_zellij_popup.drvPath instantiated the Darwin derivation.
- nix derivation show for that Darwin drv confirmed explicit CARGO/RUSTC exports and the wasm target-libdir check.
- nix build .#runtime_ghostty --override-input yazelixZellijPopup ../yazelix-zellij-popup --no-link --no-write-lock-file --accept-flake-config passed in the main repo.
- Popup child fix published as luccahuguet/yazelix-zellij-popup@75164c89e3303b3abf8a6394fd88aa833fbf1133.
- Main flake.lock updated to that published revision.
- nix build .#runtime_ghostty --no-link --no-write-lock-file --accept-flake-config --print-out-paths passed without override inputs, producing /nix/store/h0bdjcg18ssxh7ql5cl5r7yjb9dyilbm-yazelix-runtime.
- cargo run --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_maintainer --bin yzx_repo_validator -- validate-child-release-transaction passed.

Current situation: both known wasm child package failures from issue 604 have best-effort toolchain hardening fixes published and consumed by main. Exact aarch64-darwin build still needs reporter or macOS maintainer validation before closing GitHub issue 604.

title: Audit launch/startup wrappers for one more Rust-ownable data seam
notes:
2026-04-19 audit result:
- Reviewed launch_yazelix.nu, terminal_launcher.nu, start_yazelix.nu, start_yazelix_inner.nu, yzx/launch.nu, yzx/env.nu, yzx/run.nu, shells/posix/start_yazelix.sh, and shells/posix/runtime_env.sh
- working-directory and runtime-script validation already live under runtime-contract.evaluate
- canonical runtime env planning already lives under runtime-env.compute
- remaining launch/startup logic is mostly host detection, shell quoting, detached process execution, generated-state refresh, startup-profile wiring, and Zellij handoff
- decision: do not start another v15.x Rust helper here; defer further launch/startup rewrite unless a later bug exposes one more narrow deterministic seam worth deleting from Nushell
- recorded this stop condition in docs/specs/launch_bootstrap_rust_migration.md
close_reason: Completed: audit confirmed no further v15.x launch/startup Rust seam is worth moving after runtime-env.compute; stop condition recorded in launch_bootstrap_rust_migration.md

title: Pilot cargo-nextest for deterministic Rust-owned test buckets
description: After the Rust test hardening decision lands, pilot cargo-nextest only on deterministic Rust-owned suites such as config normalization, public route planning/metadata, materialization render plans, and pane-orchestrator contract tests. Do not change runtime packaging or default-suite ownership.
close_reason: Completed

title: Consume yzpp wasm from a locked child package
description: Migrate the main Yazelix repo from the interim copied yzpp.wasm artifact to the package output already published by yazelix-zellij-popup. This follows the local child-repo integration decision from yazelix-50at and the first-party Zellij plugin wasm ownership contract.
acceptance_criteria: Add a locked yazelixZellijPopup flake input or equivalent package dependency; place the package artifact at the runtime plugin path expected by generated Zellij config; document or preserve the local override smoke path using --override-input yazelixZellijPopup ../yazelix-zellij-popup; update validators so packaged/runtime artifacts are checked instead of copied-stamp provenance; delete or narrow sync_yzpp_wasm and the tracked yzpp.wasm sync stamp once package consumption is authoritative; verify with the child package build, nix build .#runtime, workspace/session validation, and a fresh-session manual smoke when live popup behavior changed.
notes:
2026-05-20: Local main-repo implementation is staged alongside the pane-orchestrator migration: flake input/package artifact plumbing consumes yazelix-zellij-popup, runtime tree materializes configs/zellij/plugins/yzpp.wasm from the child package, and copied yzpp sync tooling/validator paths were removed. Verified with the same override runtime build, cargo fmt/check/test, workspace/session validator, and contract validator. Not closed yet because the combined main lock update is blocked on publishing the pane-orchestrator child package commit first.
2026-05-20: Main-repo package-consumption WIP is currently parked in stash@{0} with the pane-orchestrator migration so local Home Manager flake updates stay lockable until the pane child package commit is published.
close_reason: Completed: main flake now consumes yazelix-zellij-popup as a locked child package at 0ed6aa7; runtime packaging materializes yzpp.wasm from the package artifact; copied yzpp wasm/sync-stamp provenance and sync validator surfaces were removed; verified with nix build .#runtime --no-link without override inputs plus Rust checks/tests and workspace/session contract validation.

title: Replace local pi_agent_rust packaging with upstream flake
description:
Track the follow-on work for removing Yazelix's local `pi_agent_rust` derivation once upstream ships a real flake surface. Codex does not need a matching bead because `openai/codex` already exposes `flake.nix` and `flake.lock`.

Scope:
- monitor upstream `pi_agent_rust` flake support
- when a flake exists, replace the local `buildRustPackage` path in `devenv.nix`
- prefer consuming upstream flake outputs instead of carrying custom packaging in Yazelix
- keep the current `pi_rust` user-facing alias stable while changing only the packaging source under the hood

Success means Yazelix no longer needs to maintain a local Nix package definition for `pi_agent_rust`
close_reason: Obsolete after removing pi_rust support from Yazelix

title: teste issue
description: Imported GitHub issue #454.
close_reason: Closed on GitHub

title: Fix yzxterm runtime selection boot failure
description: Selecting yzxterm in settings fails when the installed Home Manager package is still the default Ghostty runtime: terminal materialization cannot read share/yazelix-terminal/config.toml. Validate and document the correct yzxterm runtime selection path, update the local integration, and capture any code/package follow-up needed. Acceptance: Home Manager uses the yzxterm runtime variant when Yazelix is configured to launch yzxterm; installed runtime contains share/yazelix-terminal/config.toml and yazelix-terminal-desktop; yzx launch materialization no longer fails with missing packaged Yazelix Terminal config.
close_reason: Fixed local yzxterm boot failure by switching Home Manager to programs.yazelix.runtime_variant = "yzxterm" and updating yazelix-terminal to main. Verified runtime_yzxterm contains share/yazelix-terminal/config.toml and toolbin/yazelix-terminal-desktop, yzx launch --terminal yzxterm generated the config and sent the launch request, doctor reports first configured terminal yzxterm with Kitty passthrough active, and maintainer manually confirmed it is working.

title: Audit and clean stale in-progress tracker state after the Rust rewrite commit burst
description: Recent Rust/Nushell migration work landed as a large local commit burst with many beads closed correctly, but the tracker still shows multiple simultaneous in-progress issues and the worktree currently mixes distinct lanes \(finished pane-orchestrator cleanup plus active status migration and unrelated local changes\). Audit the remaining in-progress claims and bring Beads/worktree state back into an honest shape.
design: Keep this delete-first. Do not create more umbrella planning. First identify which in-progress beads are genuinely active versus stale claims, then either close them, unclaim/update them with explicit remaining work, or create a narrow follow-up bead if the current scope has split. Separate finished-but-uncommitted code from unrelated active lanes so future commits map cleanly back to Beads.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Every current in-progress bead is either confirmed active with current notes or moved out of in-progress. 2. Finished local work and active follow-up work are split into clear commit lanes or explicitly documented as intentionally mixed. 3. The cleanup leaves a trustworthy next-work picture in Beads for the Rust rewrite sequence.
notes: Cleanup result: reopened stale in-progress claims `yazelix-0dra`, `yazelix-ueaf`, `yazelix-tlh0`, `yazelix-kgb`, and `yazelix-2k9` with explicit notes about what remains. Confirmed `yazelix-x3u2` as the only currently active non-cleanup lane in the dirty tree. Current worktree mapping is now documented honestly: pane-orchestrator/session-truth cleanup files are finished-but-uncommitted, status-migration files belong to `x3u2`, and unrelated local changes still need separation before the next commit.
close_reason: Completed: stale in-progress claims were reopened with explicit remaining work, x3u2 was confirmed as the only active implementation lane, and the mixed worktree state is now documented honestly

title: Evaluate side-by-side Yazelix terminal variants
description:
Evaluate whether Yazelix should support installing and launching Ghostty and Ratty variants side by side instead of requiring one active default-profile yzx.

Questions to answer:
- Should variants expose distinct binary names, distinct desktop entries, or both?
- Should side-by-side variants share ~/.config/yazelix and ~/.local/share/yazelix, or should they get variant-scoped generated state?
- How should Home Manager model this: one runtime_variant, multiple named variants, or separate module instances?
- How should profile installs avoid bin/yzx collisions while keeping the existing single-variant UX simple?
- What is the migration story for existing profile and desktop-entry installs?

Constraints:
- Do not make Ratty the default.
- Preserve the current simple one-active-runtime path.
- Avoid legacy aliases unless the command/desktop surface has already shipped and needs an explicit transition.
- Do not duplicate runtime implementation logic between variants.
acceptance_criteria:
Document a recommended support shape for side-by-side Ghostty/Ratty usage.
Decide whether implementation should be package-only, launcher-only, desktop-entry-only, Home Manager-only, or some combination.
Identify state/config isolation requirements and risks.
Create follow-up implementation beads if the evaluation recommends doing the work.
close_reason: Completed by the packaged runtime-variant model: one primary runtime_variant plus Home Manager extra_terminal_variants for side-by-side terminal packages without duplicate yzx wrappers.

title: Audit Yazelix tests and validators for signal over clutter
description: Make a deliberate pass over Yazelix tests, validators, and quality gates to keep the important coverage and remove or demote low-signal clutter.
design: Inventory default, maintainer, sweep, and manual lanes plus repo validators. Keep tests that defend real user-visible contracts, regressions, or source-of-truth invariants; delete or demote tests that only assert command discovery, implementation trivia, duplicate config sync, or cosmetic constants. Preserve the governed strength rules but use them to reduce noise rather than justify weak tests.
acceptance_criteria: Produces a ranked keep/delete/demote list for tests and validators; removes or demotes agreed low-signal coverage; updates docs/contracts/test_suite_governance.md or quarantine metadata if the policy changes; records before/after test counts and gate runtime; verifies the surviving default gates still protect key contracts without excess clutter.
notes:
2026-05-06 cleanup pass inventory and decision:
- Inventory baseline before deletion: 482 first-party Rust tests containing #[test], 0 Nushell test_*.nu files, 19 yzx_repo_validator commands in the visible validator surface, and default-suite ownership declared as two nextest suites in nushell/scripts/maintainer/test_suite_inventory.toml.
- Deleted six low-signal tests: yzx_control_why_prints_elevator_pitch, yzx_control_tutor_root_keeps_guided_overview, command_metadata_help_prints_public_yzx_surface, yzx_control_edit_cursors_shape_is_removed, yzx_control_reset_cursor_shape_is_removed, and yzx_control_config_reset_shape_is_removed.
- Rationale: these tests mainly froze prose/help lists or proved stale command aliases still fail. Surviving tests still cover sponsor fallback, keybinding views, onboarding/restart help safety, cursor output generation, reset safety, tutor begin/list, whats_new state, metadata extern generation, and metadata sync.
- After deletion: 476 Rust tests containing #[test]. Code diff is 101 deleted lines and no replacement abstraction.
- Gates run: yzx dev rust fmt --check; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core --test yzx_control_public_commands; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core --test yzx_control_front_door; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core --test yzx_core_command_metadata; yzx_repo_validator validate-rust-test-traceability; yzx_repo_validator validate-default-test-traceability; yzx dev rust check.
- Ranked follow-up backlog: first prune exact-copy/UI rendering tests, then resolve the yzx dev package boundary and collapse redundant command-surface tests, then assess validator command pruning and canonical fast-gate ownership.
2026-05-06 verification follow-up: yzx dev test initially exposed an unrelated default-lane failure in launch_commands::tests::session_config_overrides_materialize_valid_ephemeral_settings. Root cause was the one-shot --with session config path rejecting the new string_list_map contract kind for zellij.keybindings. Fixed that parser gap under child yazelix-ft4k.7 and verified the focused session_config tests pass.
2026-05-06 final verification: after fixing yazelix-ft4k.7, yzx dev test passed both default nextest suites. Final count remains 476 first-party Rust tests containing #[test], down from 482 at the start of the cleanup pass.
close_reason: Completed the first canonical cleanup pass: inventoried suite and validator surfaces, removed six low-signal tests, recorded before/after counts and gates, repaired the dev-tool extraction bead, and opened focused follow-up beads for deeper validator/UI/command cleanup.

title: Inventory Yazelix test and validator surfaces
description: Produce the first concrete inventory for the ft4k cleanup pass: Rust tests, Nu tests, shell/manual test lanes, repo validators, Nix/build gates, and their rough runtime/ownership. The output should separate canonical gates from maintainer-only or sweep gates.
design: Prefer generated counts and direct source evidence over subjective impressions. Record which surfaces defend product contracts, which are convenience checks, and which are likely deletion/demotion candidates.
acceptance_criteria: A durable inventory exists in the ft4k bead notes or docs; it includes before counts for Rust/Nu tests and validator commands, identifies high-signal gates, and names the first cleanup candidates with evidence.
close_reason: Inventory completed for first ft4k cleanup pass: baseline recorded as 482 Rust tests, 0 Nushell test files, 19 validator commands, and two default nextest suites. Detailed notes are on parent yazelix-ft4k.

title: Delete weak command-discovery and implementation-trivia tests
description: Remove or demote tests that mostly assert command names, help-list trivia, exact implementation strings, or duplicate command-discovery surfaces without defending user-visible behavior.
design: Use the governed strength rubric strictly. A test is not strong just because it has a marker; it should defend a real regression, failure mode, or contract. Prefer deletion over rewriting when the behavior is already covered by stronger tests.
acceptance_criteria: A targeted patch deletes or demotes low-signal tests, updates any affected traceability markers, and verifies the surviving tests still cover the meaningful behavior. Closure evidence names deleted tests and why they were safe to remove.
close_reason: Completed first deletion slice: removed six weak copy/help/stale-alias tests, reducing first-party Rust tests from 482 to 476 with focused Rust tests and traceability validators passing.

title: Prune or merge redundant validators
description: Audit repo validators and dev quality gates for redundant source-of-truth checks, overly broad packaging/config sync checks, or validators that duplicate stronger behavior tests.
design: Keep validators that defend durable contracts cheaply. Delete, merge, or demote validators that mainly enforce implementation trivia or duplicate another stronger gate. Preserve final package and workspace gates where they defend real release safety.
acceptance_criteria: Validator cleanup candidates are ranked; at least one redundant validator or validator subcheck is removed, merged, or explicitly kept with rationale; validator docs or command metadata are updated when behavior changes.
notes:
2026-05-06 inventory note: visible yzx_repo_validator surface has 19 command names. Do not prune mechanically; start with validators that duplicate existing contract/runtime tests or only enforce packaging trivia. Keep cheap validators that enforce ownership, traceability, pane-orchestrator sync, config surface source of truth, and contracts unless a stronger replacement exists.
2026-05-07 cleanup result: removed the command-reference marker subcheck from validate-docs-experience. Ranking: delete command marker freezing from docs validator because it duplicated public command/docs review and failed TEST-002 command-discovery guidance; keep docs front-door route validation because it catches dead route targets cheaply; keep stale current-doc marker checks because they defend known removed command names; keep contract, config-surface, traceability, pane-orchestrator sync, and workspace-session validators as high-signal. Verification: repo_docs_validation::tests, validate-docs-experience, validate-contracts, validate-rust-test-traceability, validate-default-test-traceability, and yzx dev test passed.
close_reason: Completed: pruned redundant command-reference marker checks from validate-docs-experience while keeping docs route and stale-doc validators; recorded validator keep/delete ranking and verified focused tests plus default gate.

title: Define the canonical fast gate set
description: Define the small canonical gate set that agents and maintainers should run for ordinary changes, distinct from sweep/manual/release gates.
design: Measure current gate runtime where cheap, classify gates by default/maintainer/sweep/manual/release, and prefer high-signal behavior checks over broad all-tests reflexes. The result should make the project easier to verify without lowering release safety.
acceptance_criteria: The canonical gate set is documented in Beads or project docs; default gate runtime before/after is recorded; commands map to the kinds of changes they defend; heavier gates remain available but are clearly not the default for every small patch.
notes:
2026-05-06 inventory note: current default suite is explicit nextest membership for yazelix_core and zellij_pane_orchestrator with no default cargo-test exceptions and no governed Nushell test files. A canonical fast-gate bead should decide whether this remains the only default lane and which validators belong in fast maintainer hooks versus release gates.
2026-05-07 canonical gate result: documented the canonical fast gate set in docs/contracts/test_suite_governance.md. Current guidance: use yzx dev rust fmt --check, yzx dev rust check, and focused Rust tests for inner-loop source edits; use traceability validators for governed test changes; use config/workspace/pane validators only when their owned surfaces changed; use yzx dev test as the canonical default regression completion gate for nontrivial runtime or governance changes, not as the only inner-loop command. Timings from this pass: yzx dev rust check all 1.90s; config_ui focused tests 4.62s; profile focused tests 0.10s; public_command_surface focused tests 0.10s; repo_docs_validation focused tests 3.21s; validate-rust-test-traceability 1.03s; validate-default-test-traceability 0.08s; validate-contracts 0.10s; yzx dev test 1:26.03 after Nix rebuild work.
close_reason: Completed: documented canonical fast gates, classified inner-loop versus completion/release gates, recorded measured runtimes, and verified the default gate.

title: Prune exact-copy and UI rendering tests from non-contract surfaces
description: Audit remaining Rust tests that mostly freeze wording, terminal copy, or exact presentation details in config UI, front-door, profile, and command surfaces. Keep only tests tied to a documented visual or interaction regression; delete or demote the rest.
design: Start from tests with contains assertions against prose, table labels, color/style fragments, or layout trivia. Compare each to docs/contracts/test_suite_governance.md TEST-002 and TEST-005. Prefer deletion over moving weak coverage to a secondary lane.
acceptance_criteria: A ranked list identifies exact files and tests to remove, keep, or demote. Implemented removals pass Rust test traceability and focused surviving tests. Any kept below-obvious test has a concrete contract or bug rationale, not inertia.
notes: 2026-05-07 cleanup result: deleted eight exact-copy/UI-adjacent tests: six config_ui tests that froze label spacing, sidecar colors, header metadata copy, tab drawing, key/detail colors, and footer hint text; plus two profile_commands tests that froze run-id prefix and summary table text. Kept stronger behavior tests that parse/edit config values, drive enum/list/bool UI controls, patch JSONC, validate profile report loading, and compare structured profile summaries. Rust test count after the full follow-up batch is 466, down from 476 at the start of this pass.
close_reason: Completed: deleted eight exact-copy/UI-adjacent tests, kept stronger config UI/profile behavior coverage, and verified focused tests plus traceability/default gates.

title: Collapse redundant command-surface tests after dev extraction is decided
description: After the yzx dev extraction decision, re-audit command-surface tests that mainly prove routing tables, command categories, or help discoverability for maintainer commands. Delete duplicate route assertions when one behavior test or validator already protects the same boundary.
design: Sequence this after yazelix-w032.10 so the test suite follows the new package boundary instead of defending commands that may leave the user runtime. Keep user-facing diagnostics and high-risk route planners; remove low-value command inventory checks.
acceptance_criteria: Command-surface coverage maps to the final runtime versus maintainer package split. Duplicate route or help assertions are deleted, surviving tests defend real user behavior, and focused Rust tests plus traceability validators pass.
notes: 2026-05-07 cleanup result: deleted two redundant yzx dev command-surface route tests, routes_nested_dev_rust_commands_to_control_plane and routes_dev_inspect_session_to_control_plane. Remaining coverage still proves dev-family routing generically through grouped help aliases and missing-subcommand routing, while actual dev behavior remains covered by yzx_control/maintainer command tests. The final runtime-vs-maintainer package split is explicitly left to yazelix-w032.10; created yazelix-ft4k.6.1 blocked by that decision for the post-extraction re-audit.
close_reason: Completed safe collapse: removed redundant yzx dev route tests and created blocked post-extraction re-audit yazelix-ft4k.6.1 for the final package-boundary pass after yazelix-w032.10.

title: Re-audit command-surface tests after yzx dev package split
description: After the yzx dev extraction decision is implemented, re-audit public command metadata and route tests so the suite no longer defends maintainer-only commands as if they were permanent user-runtime surface.
design: Use the final runtime versus maintainer package split from yazelix-w032.10. Keep tests for user-facing diagnostics and high-risk root routing; delete tests that only prove extracted maintainer commands remain in the public runtime package.
acceptance_criteria: Command-surface tests match the implemented package boundary. Any yzx dev commands removed from the user runtime are not preserved by default-suite command inventory tests. Focused Rust tests and traceability validators pass.
notes: Rust dependency decision for implementation: no new crates. This is a command-surface test audit using existing Rust tests, public command metadata helpers, and maintainer command tests. Rejected alternatives: adding a test framework, preserving legacy runtime aliases for extracted maintainer commands, or adding help-output trivia tests. Packaging impact: tests should defend that repo-only yzx dev commands stay out of the user runtime while maintainer-shell coverage remains separate.
close_reason: Completed audit: public runtime command metadata and extern tests retain only yzx dev inspect_session/profile and assert repo-only dev commands stay absent; runtime control helper returns maintainer-shell guidance for repo-only dev commands; maintainer crate tests cover moved rust/update parser behavior. No code changes needed beyond Beads state.

title: Fix one-shot config overrides for semantic keybinding maps
description: The default test lane exposed that yzx --with session config overrides could not load the current main config contract after zellij.keybindings introduced the string_list_map field kind.
design: Extend the session config override contract-kind parser to accept string_list_map and validate --with values as JSON objects whose values are arrays of strings. Keep the fix local to the session override path; do not introduce a broader config DSL.
acceptance_criteria: yzx --with can materialize an ephemeral settings.jsonc when the contract contains zellij.keybindings. The existing session_config_overrides_materialize_valid_ephemeral_settings test passes, and typed patch coverage rejects malformed string-list-map values.
close_reason: Fixed during ft4k verification: session config overrides now support string_list_map fields, typed patch coverage exercises zellij.keybindings maps, and the previously failing session_config tests pass.

title: Delete the installer-owned runtime/current compatibility contract from v15
description: Yazelix v15 still carries the old manual-install compatibility contract built around ~/.local/share/yazelix/runtime/current. The flake installer template, install validators, path docs, and wrapper checks still defend that indirection even though Home Manager and the packaged runtime now use more direct ownership. Delete this compatibility layer if possible, update the installer and validation surface to the slimmer surviving contract, and remove runtime/current wording from docs and tests. The result should be that v15 no longer treats runtime/current as a first-class install boundary.
close_reason: Completed

title: Improve yzx run argument pass-through for external commands
description: Improve yzx run so argument pass-through to external commands is predictable and does not silently reshape argv in surprising ways.
design: Treat this as a command-surface contract issue. Clarify exactly what yzx run should pass through, where Yazelix-specific parsing should stop, and how quoting, separators, and variadic arguments should behave for external commands. Avoid compatibility hacks that preserve ambiguous behavior; the end state should be a clear argv contract that maintainers can test directly.
acceptance_criteria: There is a defined argv pass-through contract for yzx run, including representative examples of external command invocation. The bead leaves behind either an implementation path or a documented decision on what ambiguous legacy behavior will not be preserved.
close_reason: Completed with wrapped argv passthrough contract and focused regression coverage

title: Evaluate deterministic Yazi client ids for managed sidebar
description: Follow-up option 3 from the sidebar-state startup-delay investigation. Evaluate launching the managed sidebar Yazi with a deterministic yazi --client-id derived from the Zellij sidebar pane/tab/session identity, so the pane orchestrator can know the sidebar Yazi id from managed pane state instead of relying on a startup register_sidebar_yazi_state pipe. Acceptance: decide whether deterministic ids are safe across tabs/sessions/restarts, document collision and lifecycle behavior, and either implement the launch/materialization contract or reject it with evidence.
acceptance_criteria: Decision states whether deterministic Yazi client ids are safe across tabs, sessions, restarts, and concurrent Yazelix windows. It documents the id derivation input, collision behavior, lifecycle cleanup behavior, and how pane-orchestrator state discovers or validates the active sidebar Yazi id. The bead either implements the launch/materialization contract with verification or rejects deterministic ids with evidence and keeps the register pipe path.
notes: Decision: reject deterministic managed-sidebar Yazi client ids. Upstream Yazi --client-id is a u64 that must be globally unique, and duplicate ids replace the prior DDS client entry. Hashing Zellij session/tab/pane identity would introduce a second ownership layer and still would not remove the sidebar registration seam because the pane orchestrator needs live Yazi cwd updates. Keep Yazi-owned YAZI_ID plus pane-bound register_sidebar_yazi_state validation; follow-up yazelix-g1ax.1 tracks bounded retry/ack if the fixed startup delay remains flaky.
close_reason: Rejected deterministic sidebar Yazi client ids with upstream evidence, documented the supported pane-bound registration model, and filed the safer retry/ack follow-up.

title: Replace fixed sidebar Yazi startup registration delay with bounded retry
description: The deterministic Yazi client-id path was rejected because Yazi client ids must be globally unique numeric ids and sidebar cwd still has to come from live Yazi state. If managed sidebar registration is still flaky, improve the existing pane-bound register_sidebar_yazi_state path instead of adding a parallel deterministic identity layer.
acceptance_criteria: The sidebar-state plugin or surrounding launch path retries registration only when needed, with bounded timing and no synchronous startup block; the pane orchestrator still validates pane_id against the current tab's managed sidebar; cwd updates on Yazi cd/tab events keep working; tests or manual validation cover startup registration without relying on a fixed one-shot delay only.
notes: Dependency decision: no new crates. Implement bounded retry in the bundled Lua sidebar-state plugin with existing Yazi Command output and ya.sleep APIs; any Rust changes are maintainer validation only. Packaging impact: none. Rejected deterministic Yazi ids and a new RPC acknowledgement protocol for this slice.
close_reason: Completed: sidebar-state now attempts registration immediately, retries bounded transient orchestrator readiness responses, and cancels stale retries after newer sidebar state publishes.

title: slow yzx env
description:
Imported GitHub issue #509.

just after: 🔧 Yazelix environment loaded! Launch the full interface in a separate terminal with 'yzx launch' or here with 'yzx launch --here'.

it takes a while to load
close_reason: Dropped as obsolete after user retest: yzx env is no longer slow enough to track as active work.

title: Simplify Yazelix popup panes through yzpp config ownership
description: Why: lazygit, the command/menu pane, and yazelix-ratconfig still route through Yazelix-specific transient pane adapter code even though popup open/focus/close behavior belongs in yazelix-zellij-popup. What: move the durable popup identity/config surface toward yzpp specs, isolate Yazelix-only sidebar refresh as an explicit close hook, and delete bespoke transient adapter behavior that no longer has to own child process lifecycle.
acceptance_criteria: Yazelix popup/menu/config panes can be represented as yzpp popup specs; lazygit/sidebar refresh is modeled as an explicit close hook rather than hidden popup lifecycle logic; remaining Yazelix-specific code is limited to actual Yazelix commands or helpers.
notes: Implementation slice landed locally: yzpp gained on_close hooks; Yazelix now packages yzpp.wasm and uses generated yzpp specs/raw requests for popup/menu/config UI; `yzx sidebar refresh` is explicit; pane-orchestrator transient open/toggle code and Nu popup wrappers were removed. Follow-ups remain for the maintainer-shell build wrapper and stale transient facts naming.
close_reason: all steps complete

title: Fix maintainer-shell pane-orchestrator build wrapper from repo checkout
description: The documented `nix develop -c yzx dev build_pane_orchestrator --sync` path currently fails in this checkout before Cargo is available because the maintainer shell hook calls `runtime-env.compute` with `YAZELIX_RUNTIME_DIR` pointed at the repo root, where `runtime_components.json` is absent. Direct rustup Cargo build/sync worked and `validate-pane-orchestrator-sync` passed, but the documented maintainer command should work.
acceptance_criteria: The documented `nix develop -c yzx dev build_pane_orchestrator --sync` path can build and sync the pane-orchestrator wasm from a repo checkout without requiring a packaged `runtime_components.json` at the repo root.
notes: Fixed the maintainer-shell build wrapper from a source checkout without packaged runtime_components.json. The shell hook now calls runtime-env.compute with an explicit lightweight request instead of --from-env, skips user-runtime environment setup when the checkout has no runtime component manifest, and pane-orchestrator wasm sync no longer regenerates Zellij materialization as a side effect. Verification: nix develop -c yzx dev build_pane_orchestrator --sync built and synced repo/runtime wasm; cargo fmt --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml --all --check; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_maintainer repo_plugin_build; cargo check --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_maintainer --all-targets; cargo run --quiet --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_maintainer --bin yzx_repo_validator -- validate-pane-orchestrator-sync.
close_reason: Completed: maintainer-shell pane-orchestrator build/sync now works from a source checkout without packaged runtime_components.json.

title: Rename stale transient-pane facts surface after yzpp ownership cut
description: After popup/menu/config pane lifecycle moved to yzpp, the remaining `transient-pane-facts.compute` command is only a launch/session facts helper for `yzx popup <command ...>` overrides. Rename or retire the stale transient naming if no compatibility boundary requires it.
acceptance_criteria: The internal facts command and Rust module names either use popup/session terminology or have a documented reason to keep the transient name. Tests and docs no longer imply that the pane orchestrator owns popup/menu/config pane lifecycle.
notes: Renamed the remaining internal popup facts surface from transient-pane terminology to popup/session terminology. The yzx_core helper command is now popup-session-facts.compute; the Rust module is popup_session_facts.rs with PopupSessionFactsData and compute_popup_session_facts_from_env. No compatibility alias was kept because no current caller requires the stale internal command. Also ratcheted the Rust ownership budget from 73,542 to 73,513 raw tracked lines after the yzpp cleanup tail. Verification: cargo fmt --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml --all --check; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core popup_session_facts; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core --test yzx_core_owned_facts; cargo check --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core --all-targets; cargo run --quiet --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_maintainer --bin yzx_repo_validator -- validate-rust-ownership-budget; git diff --check.
close_reason: Completed: stale transient-pane facts surface renamed to popup-session facts and budget ceiling ratcheted down.

title: Repair GitHub/Beads CI contract drift
description: The scheduled Bead/GitHub contract validator reports new GitHub issues without matching Beads and GitHub-open issues mapped to closed Beads. Run the local sync path, commit resulting Beads state, and verify .github/scripts/validate_issue_bead_contract.nu passes.
notes: Rust dependency decision: no new production or dev crates. Implement transient GitHub CLI retry in-house/std inside the maintainer sync path because the failure is narrow command-process retry behavior around gh HTTP 504/502/503/rate-limit responses; adding a general retry crate would increase package surface without meaningful benefit. Packaging impact: none.
close_reason: Completed: synced GitHub issue lifecycle/comments into Beads, added transient GitHub CLI retry for read-only contract validation, and verified with nu .github/scripts/validate_issue_bead_contract.nu.

title: Create a bead-to-issue contract with no lifecycle drift
description: Imported GitHub issue #452. Define and implement the shared-subset contract between GitHub issues and Beads so the mapping and open/closed lifecycle cannot silently drift.
close_reason: Closed on GitHub

title: Show progress while upstream update runs
description:
Requested update path: default Nix profile.

  Use this only when a Nix profile package owns the active Yazelix runtime.

Do not use both update paths for the same installed Yazelix runtime.

Running:
  nix profile upgrade --refresh yazelix prints the nix profile upgrade command and then appears silent while  is still running. Add visible progress/heartbeat output so users can tell the updater is alive during long Nix refresh/build/eval phases.
acceptance_criteria:
Requested update path: default Nix profile.

  Use this only when a Nix profile package owns the active Yazelix runtime.

Do not use both update paths for the same installed Yazelix runtime.

❌ `yzx update upstream` could not find the active Yazelix runtime in the default Nix profile.
   Current runtime: /nix/store/fyhvk7x9nhq45gibc0fq5bi5cq3gm9pg-yazelix
   This command now updates profile-installed Yazelix packages after the legacy flake installer was removed.
   Recovery: Reinstall with `nix profile add github:luccahuguet/yazelix#yazelix`, or use `yzx update home_manager` if Home Manager owns this install. emits a visible still-running progress message while the child update command is active; command exit status is still propagated; tests cover the silent-child behavior without making the test slow; docs or command output remain accurate.
close_reason: Completed: changed yzx update upstream/home_manager from buffered Command::output to inherited live stdio so Nix stdout/stderr appears while the child command runs. Added a regression test that proves fake Nix progress is visible before the update process exits. Verified targeted upstream tests, rustfmt check, rust test traceability, and git diff --check.

title: Add validator for config surface and launch-profile parity
description: Add a validator that enforces the canonical config-surface and launch-profile contract once that contract is defined, so drift is caught mechanically instead of by maintainer memory.
design: This bead should not invent the contract; it should defend the canonical config-surface and launch-profile ownership model from yazelix-b16x together with the machine-readable main-config contract introduced by yazelix-0oj1.1. Keep the validator high-signal: check the maintained parity and ownership rules that actually matter, and avoid noisy pseudo-invariants that mainly create churn. The right validator may be structural, config-based, or generation-based depending on what the canonical contract ultimately exposes.
acceptance_criteria: There is a validator for the canonical config-surface and launch-profile contract, and it checks the maintained source-of-truth invariants that the contract explicitly requires. The validator is justified by the contract rather than by general anxiety about drift.
close_reason: Completed: added config surface and launch-profile contract validator with CI and pre-commit integration

title: Add experimental Ratty terminal support
description:
## Goal
Add Ratty as an explicit experimental Yazelix terminal option only after the Ratty evaluation bead chooses a support shape.

## Intended Shape
The likely first implementation should be PATH-provided terminal support, not a default packaged runtime:
- allow `ratty` in `terminal.terminals` only if the evaluation accepts the risk
- add schema/config metadata/Home Manager support if Ratty becomes a real user-facing option
- generate only minimal Ratty config if Ratty's config format is stable enough
- document that Ratty's outer terminal presentation can run Zellij, but inline Ratty Graphics Protocol objects emitted by apps inside Zellij are not expected to work until Zellij has generic APC passthrough and reverse replies

## Constraints
- Do not make Ratty the default terminal.
- Do not add a packaged `#yazelix_ratty` runtime unless Ratty packaging is stable and the dependency/build cost is acceptable.
- Do not claim RGP works inside Zellij unless a live manual test proves it.
- Do not fork Zellij or Ratty in this bead.
- Keep default Ghostty and WezTerm behavior unchanged.

## Verification
- Ratty can be selected explicitly without affecting existing Ghostty/WezTerm/Kitty/Alacritty/Foot users.
- `yzx` terminal selection fails clearly when Ratty is configured but unavailable.
- A fresh Ratty-launched Yazelix session starts Zellij successfully.
- Manual notes capture whether Ratty outer 3D presentation, Kitty image support, and RGP-from-inside-Zellij work or fail.

## Dependency
Blocked by the Ratty terminal-lane evaluation and by any future Zellij generic passthrough decision if the scope expands beyond launch/config support.
notes: Implemented local experimental Ratty support: ratty is accepted in terminal.terminals, has generated ratty.toml materialization, launch argv support through --config-file/--title/-e, native user-mode lookup at XDG_CONFIG_HOME/ratty/ratty.toml, status/onboarding/config schema exposure, Linux-only #runtime_ratty/#yazelix_ratty flake outputs, and Home Manager runtime_variant = "ratty". Automated verification passed: cargo fmt --all --check, cargo check, focused ratty launch/materialization tests, validate-config-surface-contract, validate-flake-interface, validate-nix-customization-api, validate-contracts, validate-rust-test-traceability, nix build .#runtime_ratty, nix build .#yazelix_ratty. Manual Ratty session test still pending before push/closure.

title: Add data-driven Ghostty cursor builder
description: Add a constrained, data-driven Ghostty cursor builder that lets users choose a supported cursor family and supply 2-3 colors in ~/.config/yazelix/yazelix_cursors.toml while preserving all existing named presets. Generate runtime shader output from structured Yazelix-owned config rather than exposing raw GLSL or terminal-native shader editing.
design: Refined direction: this is not just a one-off custom color escape hatch. The goal is to migrate Yazelix's useful Ghostty cursor preset library and cursor behavior settings into a data-driven sidecar so presets and cursor effects can be edited without bloating yazelix_default.toml. Once this lands, yazelix.toml should lose every Ghostty cursor setting; cursor selection, enabled cursor list, global effects, duration, glow, Kitty cursor enablement, and cursor definitions live in user_configs/yazelix_cursors.toml or an equivalent explicitly owned cursor sidecar. The sidecar should separate selection/settings from definitions: enabled_cursors = ["blaze", "orchid"] is the explicit source of truth for what can be selected and what random can draw from; [settings] owns global trail, trail_effect, mode_effect, glow, duration, and kitty_enable_cursor; [[cursor]] tables are definitions users can edit or add. For Kitty, keep the model binary: kitty_enable_cursor = true/false. Do not expose a separate Kitty fallback cursor selector in the first version. Keep effects/duration/glow global; do not add per-cursor effect overrides yet. Keep the schema minimal and avoid overlay/replace registry modes, random include filters, tags, per-entry enabled flags, or raw GLSL DSL. Preserve the existing simple selector UX through the sidecar: settings.trail = "random", named presets, and none continue to work. Validate strictly: enabled names must resolve exactly once, cursor names must be unique, families must be supported, colors must have exact counts and valid hex values, global setting values must be in their allowed sets, and duration must be in range. Start with data-driven generation for simple_dual and axis_gradient presets. Remove party from the supported set unless a real purpose appears. Keep neon as a curated_template/hardcoded shader in the first slice to avoid overfitting the registry around the most complex effect.
acceptance_criteria: Evaluate and implement a cursor sidecar model that removes Ghostty cursor settings from yazelix.toml while keeping useful Ghostty cursor presets data-driven where practical. Existing user intent from ghostty_trail_color, ghostty_trail_effect, ghostty_mode_effect, ghostty_trail_glow, ghostty_trail_duration, and Kitty cursor fallback behavior must have a clear transition path into the sidecar. Existing named values keep working via settings.trail, including random and none; party is removed or intentionally excluded unless a product purpose is found. Neon may remain a curated hardcoded preset with registry metadata for random behavior. Users can enable/disable cursors through enabled_cursors, edit/add definitions through [[cursor]] tables, set global effects/duration/glow through [settings], and toggle Kitty cursor fallback with kitty_enable_cursor; generated runtime shader output remains Yazelix-owned. Invalid sidecar data fails fast with actionable errors. Home Manager either gets an explicit sidecar ownership story or clearly documents that cursor customization is direct-config/user-owned for the first slice. Tests cover sidecar parsing, enabled_cursors resolution, missing/duplicate enabled names, named-preset compatibility, random drawing from enabled_cursors, global settings validation, Kitty enable/disable behavior, color/family validation, generated shader output, no-sidecar default behavior, and legacy yazelix.toml cursor-field handling.
notes:
2026-04-27 refinement from user: the intended design is to migrate existing Ghostty cursor presets to a data-driven registry so users can change and disable/enable them. This makes a sidecar appropriate because the cursor preset data is too large for yazelix_default.toml. The main config should stay small and selector-oriented; the sidecar should own the larger cursor dataset.
2026-04-27 refinement from user: party can be killed because it is too wild and has no clear purpose. Neon can stay as a hardcoded/curated preset for now, which keeps the first data-driven migration focused on simpler families instead of forcing every shader into the same schema.
2026-04-27 refinement from user: do not use registry mode = overlay/replace or random_include_tags. The sidecar should be a simple list of enabled cursors. Defaults ship with the existing useful cursors, and users add, modify, comment, or uncomment cursor entries directly.
2026-04-27 refinement from user: tags are unnecessary. Keep the cursor sidecar schema small and direct.
2026-04-27 refinement from user: the enabled list should be explicit, e.g. enabled_cursors = ["blaze", "orchid"]. Cursor tables define available presets; enabled_cursors controls selection/random.
2026-04-27 refinement from user: yazelix.toml can lose every cursor setting. Move trail selection, effects, duration, glow, Kitty fallback, and cursor definitions into the cursor sidecar, with effects kept global for simplicity.
2026-04-27 refinement from user: Kitty should be simpler: kitty_enable_cursor = true/false. Do not expose a Kitty fallback cursor selector in the first schema.
Implemented data-driven cursor sidecar: shipped yazelix_cursors_default.toml, bootstraps user_configs/yazelix_cursors.toml, moved Ghostty cursor settings out of yazelix.toml/Home Manager, removed party from supported runtime presets, kept neon as curated_template, added strict Rust validation, generated data-driven Ghostty shaders, and made Kitty cursor fallback a sidecar boolean. Verified with rust check, focused Rust tests, config-surface/upgrade/flake validators, fmt, Taplo checks for main TOML surfaces, and git diff --check.
close_reason: Completed

title: Fix cached status widgets missing session cache env on startup
description: Cached status widgets can render empty because start_yazelix_inner.nu writes a per-window config snapshot but sets YAZELIX_SESSION_CONFIG_PATH and YAZELIX_STATUS_BAR_CACHE_PATH inside a plain Nu def. Those env mutations do not persist back to main, so the Zellij process and pane-orchestrator cannot discover the launch-scoped cache path. Acceptance: the startup helper preserves those env vars through the Zellij handoff, with a regression that catches plain-def env loss.
close_reason: Fixed by making the startup snapshot helper env-preserving and adding a config-surface regression for launch-scoped status cache env inheritance.

title: Make agent usage widgets available on first paint
description: Codex usage facts currently appear only after the background status-cache refresh completes, so the first status-bar paint can look empty or stale even when tokenusage is fast. Investigate and implement a faster first-paint path for agent usage widgets without reintroducing provider execution from zjstatus. Consider startup cache seeding, immediate background refresh before/with first status bus write, per-target freshness, and splitting fast daily facts from heavier monthly/session facts. Acceptance: a fresh Yazelix window shows configured usage widgets promptly from cache or explicit loading state; provider commands still run only in controlled background paths with timeouts; multiple windows remain safe.
close_reason: Completed: usage widgets can render from existing sibling/shared caches before the new window writes status_bar_cache.json; Codex partial token-only caches no longer suppress quota retries behind long backoff; added focused regressions.

title: Evaluate Frankentui for Yazelix TUI architecture
description: Evaluate whether Frankentui should influence Yazelix's TUI, ratconfig, config UI, and terminal UI correctness strategy.
design: Compare direct adoption, optional integration, idea borrowing, and rejection. Evaluate against the current Yazelix state: yzx config ui is already backed by the first-party yazelix-ratconfig child crate, Yazelix uses Ratatui/crossterm, Zellij owns workspace/popup placement, main-repo LOC growth is tightly constrained, and the config UI should remain a product-specific adapter over reusable child-owned mechanics.
acceptance_criteria: The evaluation records whether Frankentui should be adopted, integrated, mined for ideas, or rejected. It must cover license fit, packaging/build cost, overlap with Ratatui and yazelix-ratconfig, terminal cleanup/rendering/testing lessons, web/WASM relevance, accessibility relevance, and any follow-up beads for accepted narrow slices.
notes:
Decision, 2026-05-22: borrow ideas only. Do not adopt Frankentui directly, do not add it as an optional integration, and do not replace Ratatui/yazelix-ratconfig with it.

Evidence inspected:
- Repo metadata: https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/frankentui is Rust, recently active, and describes itself as a minimal high-performance terminal UI kernel with diff-based rendering, inline mode, and RAII terminal cleanup.
- README scope: claims 20 crates, 80+ widgets, 46 demo screens, inline mode, deterministic buffer diffing, one-writer terminal output, RAII cleanup, pane workspaces, web/WASM backend, Bayesian runtime decisions, snapshot/shadow-run validation, and accessibility infrastructure.
- Cargo workspace: 20 workspace members including ftui-core/render/runtime/widgets/layout/text/style/a11y/web/showcase/harness/doctor crates. This is a broad framework/runtime, not a small helper crate.
- License: LICENSE is "MIT License (with OpenAI/Anthropic Rider)" and explicitly grants no rights to OpenAI, Anthropic, affiliates, or people/entities acting on their behalf. That is incompatible with Yazelix taking it as a dependency, vendored source, or framework base.
- Packaging status: no flake.nix was visible in the inspected root; README emphasizes cloning/source and cargo run for the demo showcase. It is WIP/nightly-oriented rather than a stable Nix-friendly library dependency for Yazelix.
- Accessibility: ACCESSIBILITY.md documents an a11y tree/focus-management direction, but also says there is no platform accessibility bridge yet, web semantic proxy is static, and important widgets are still missing Accessible implementations.
- Terminal correctness docs: one-writer rule, inline-mode ADR, and definition-of-done material emphasize direct stdout hazards, PTY capture, RAII cleanup, mux-aware inline fallback, deterministic snapshots, and render budget discipline.

Recommendation:
Reject adoption and integration. Yazelix already made the right local choice: keep config UI on Ratatui/crossterm and extract reusable config-editor behavior into yazelix-ratconfig. Frankentui is much broader than Yazelix needs, overlaps with existing ratconfig/Ratatui architecture, would add massive dependency and build surface, and carries an unacceptable license rider.

What Yazelix should borrow:
- one-writer discipline for any future terminal UI that mixes logs/output and UI chrome
- explicit RAII cleanup checks for raw mode, alt-screen, mouse capture, panic/error exit, and popup exit
- deterministic UI snapshot fixtures for high-value config UI screens rather than weak help-text tests
- PTY or Zellij-popup smoke tests only where they defend real terminal lifecycle behavior
- mux-aware caution around inline mode; under Zellij, prefer conservative redraw/cleanup behavior over clever scroll-region tricks
- minimal focus/accessibility metadata as ratconfig grows, especially for focusable rows, popups, selects, and validation/status messages

What Yazelix should not borrow:
- broad framework/runtime replacement
- pane workspace system; Zellij owns workspace layout
- web/WASM backend for current Yazelix config UI
- Bayesian/statistical runtime machinery for simple config editing
- huge demo/showcase/snapshot apparatus beyond a few high-signal fixtures
- any code, dependency, or vendored material because of the rider and scope mismatch

Accepted follow-up:
Created yazelix-0ytzl to define a small ratconfig terminal UI hardening checklist. The follow-up should keep Yazelix/yazelix-ratconfig on their current architecture and only accept narrow correctness/test/a11y improvements.
close_reason: Completed: evaluated Frankentui and chose borrow-ideas-only. Rejected direct adoption and integration due to license rider incompatibility, large WIP framework scope, overlap with Ratatui/yazelix-ratconfig, packaging cost, and mismatch with Zellij-owned workspace layout. Created yazelix-0ytzl for narrow ratconfig terminal UI hardening follow-up.

title: Fix Yazelix-provided Ghostty runtime integration
description: The Yazelix-managed Ghostty path is still less reliable than it should be. Recent install and launch regressions showed that the packaged/default Ghostty flow can fail because of runtime-root propagation, wrapper assumptions, desktop-launch differences, or other Yazelix-managed integration bugs.
design: Treat this as the end-to-end Ghostty integration bug, not a single wrapper tweak. Audit the Yazelix-provided Ghostty path across install, yzx launch, restart, and desktop entry launch. Separate Ghostty-host problems from Yazelix-owned runtime/env/config bugs, then fix the Yazelix-owned path so the default managed Ghostty experience is reliable without local handholding.
acceptance_criteria: A normal installed Yazelix setup can launch through the managed Ghostty path from yzx launch and the desktop entry without clone-path assumptions or stale-wrapper failures. The remaining Ghostty-specific host limitations, if any, are explicit and documented rather than surfacing as silent Yazelix breakage.
notes: New evidence on 2026-04-01: plain nix-provided Ghostty also fails on the maintainer's hybrid Intel+NVIDIA Linux host outside the Yazelix wrapper path. Direct probe: devenv shell -- bash -lc 'timeout 8 ghostty --gtk-single-instance=false -e true' reproduced the OpenGL/EGL failure. Treat remaining Yazelix-owned wrapper/runtime issues separately from the possibility that nixpkgs Ghostty itself is not a safe default on some Linux hosts.
close_reason: Fixed by stabilizing the managed Ghostty launch path across install, yzx launch, and desktop launch, and removing stale-wrapper dependence from the installed runtime path.

title: Optional Codex / AI agent pane?
description:
Imported GitHub issue #587.

Hey Lucca!

First of all: I’m still happily using Yazelix after all these years :)

I think I started using it on NixOS around https://github.com/luccahuguet/yazelix/issues/50, and it has become one of my
favorite terminal tools. It’s amazing how nice the Yazi + Zellij + Helix workflow feels, especially over SSH.

I wanted to ask what you think about adding an optional extra pane for AI coding agents, like Codex, Claude Code,
OpenCode, Gemini CLI, etc.

Nothing too fancy or provider-specific. My rough idea is just:

- an optional managed `agent` pane
- configurable command, e.g. `codex`, `claude`, `opencode`
- easy focus/switching between Yazi, editor, and agent
- maybe later: reveal/open files changed by the agent in Yazi/Helix

The main pain point is that AI coding tools can already edit files, but the terminal workflow around them is still
awkward. After Codex changes something, I want to quickly browse the changed files in Yazi, open them in Helix, maybe
tweak them manually, then jump back to Codex, all inside the same Yazelix workspace.

I looked around a bit, and I don’t think there is a good TUI-native project that really does this yet. There are many AI
agent tools, but most are desktop/browser-first, or they don’t have the Yazi + Helix + Zellij workflow that Yazelix
already nails.

I think this could bring a lot of power users to Yazelix: the kind of people who live in terminals, SSH into servers, use
Nix, Helix/Neovim, Yazi, Zellij, and now want Codex/Claude/OpenCode without moving to a GUI IDE.

If you like the idea and think it fits Yazelix, I’d be very happy to work on it soon. And honestly, I’d be honored if I
could contribute this upstream :)
notes:
2026-05-10 maintainer direction: make Codex the first-class/default Yazelix agent integration. Do not make OpenCode the default solely because it has free models; instead document OpenCode as the democratic/free-model alternative and make agent.command easy to change. Runtime must not silently fall back from Codex to OpenCode. If Codex is configured but missing or unauthenticated, show an actionable error that tells the user to install/sign in to Codex or set agent.command to another tool such as opencode.
First implementation slice is tracked as yazelix-gxti.1: tab-local right_sidebar agent pane with host-installed Codex as the default command, no bundled Codex in the default flake package, and no automatic fallback chain.
close_reason: Completed v1 Codex/AI agent pane. Shipped host-installed Codex through yzx agent, managed right-sidebar lifecycle via the pane orchestrator, Alt Shift L toggle, Ctrl Shift Y editor/right-agent focus, docs/key surfaces, and Home Manager/runtime integration. Broader multi-agent command center work remains open under yazelix-bl3 / GitHub #419.

title: Implement right-sidebar Codex agent pane
description: Implement the first minimal validation slice: a hardcoded tab-local right_sidebar Codex agent pane. This deliberately skips the full workspace.roles/workspace.placements config surface so the pane behavior can be validated first. Mission-control and generalized role-placement config remain future work.
acceptance_criteria: A Yazelix-owned native binding opens a new right-side agent pane in the active tab; the pane launches host-installed codex through yzx agent with no bundled Codex in the default flake package and no fallback chain; missing codex fails with a clear message explaining that Codex is the default agent but host-installed for now; the implementation is intentionally hardcoded and does not introduce the full workspace.roles config surface; docs or key/help surfaces describe the validation behavior; focused validation covers generated binding/action metadata and runtime launch/failure behavior.
notes:
Implementation narrowed per maintainer direction: first validation slice is main-repo only. Add a hardcoded Codex agent launcher named yzx agent and a generated Zellij Run binding that opens it on the right. Do not add workspace.roles or workspace.placements yet, and do not change the pane-orchestrator child repo for this slice. This validates the right-side Codex pane UX before adding singleton or generalized role config behavior.
Startup regression found during manual test: generated native Zellij Run binding used inline sibling metadata direction plus name, and Zellij 0.44.3 rejected that KDL before session start. Fixed the validation slice by dropping the temporary pane name metadata and keeping only direction right; managed role naming should come back with the orchestrator-owned lifecycle layer.
Second startup regression check: Zellij also rejects inline Run child metadata even with only direction. Source fix now renders native actions with multiline child KDL for actions that carry child metadata, matching Zellij serializer shape. The current generated config validates with zellij setup --check after rendering Run yzx agent with a child direction block.
close_reason: Completed and manually validated. Hardcoded Codex right-pane validation slice works after rendering the native Run binding as multiline Zellij KDL; full managed right-sidebar lifecycle remains future work.

title: Implement managed right-agent sidebar lifecycle
description: Replace the hardcoded native right-pane validation slice with the first managed agent sidebar lifecycle. The slice stays hardcoded to host-installed Codex and must not add a user config surface yet.
design: Implement the smallest managed state model that validates the right-sidebar UX: agent pane existence is separate from content layout and left-sidebar visibility; new tabs do not auto-start Codex; opening files does not auto-start Codex; the agent command creates or reveals a right-side Codex pane and preserves left/right visibility through layout-family changes.
acceptance_criteria: Alt+Shift+A creates a named Codex agent pane on the right when absent; invoking it again can focus/reveal or hide the existing agent pane without spawning duplicates; new-tab and file-open flows keep Codex absent until requested; layout-family switching preserves both left sidebar state and agent-sidebar state; exact changed commands/tests are run; no new Rust crates are added unless the bead notes are updated first.
notes:
Rust dependency decision: production crates none; dev-only crates none; implement the small pane/layout state extension in-house with std and existing zellij_tile APIs. Rejected alternatives: broad state-machine/layout helper crates and configurable agent-provider crates. Packaging impact: pane-orchestrator child wasm changes require the normal child build plus main runtime override validation before this can land.
Local implementation complete for the managed Codex right sidebar slice. Validation passed: child cargo check/test/fmt, main yazelix_core focused tests, workspace/session contract validator, child nix build, main runtime nix build with local pane-orchestrator override, and generated Zellij config check. Pending manual Yazelix session test before close/push/lock update.
Manual test feedback: agent sidebar opens but layout swaps could place the editor in the right-agent slot. Follow-up local revision removes vertical split from the managed layout family, keeps only single stack and bottom-terminal families, hardcodes the agent-open width to 30%, and nudges the managed agent pane right after agent-open layout swaps. Validation passed: child cargo test, focused main zellij_render_plan/zellij_materialization tests, workspace/session contract validator, child nix build, main runtime override build, full yazelix package override build, and generated Zellij config setup check.
Design alternatives captured from manual testing before continuing implementation:

- O(1) layout override: would ideally remove glitch frames by jumping directly to a target layout instead of traversing swap layouts. Risk: previous attempts suggested the plugin/API path may still only expose next/previous swap traversal or otherwise make this brittle, so confidence is low.
- Accept glitch frames: technically works and sidebar toggles themselves are stable, but layout switching feels unstable and is probably not good enough for the default UX.
- Remove bottom-terminal: delete-first option. The new right agent sidebar, existing stack behavior, and upcoming top/bottom popup panes may make bottom-terminal less important. This gives the right-sidebar design a fair stability test with fewer states.
- Right popup instead of right sidebar: preserves vertical split and bottom-terminal layout families and can give the agent more width. It fits a dashboard/info-rich model where users want to see many surfaces at once.
- Right sidebar: persistent sidecar that lets editor and agent coexist on screen, including with both sidebars open. It fits a calmer workspace model, but layout state must stay simple enough to avoid pane reassignment/glitch frames.
- Sidebar coexistence: allow both sidebars open for now. With left 20% and agent 30%, center keeps about 50%. Auto-closing the other sidebar could preserve center width but may feel jumpy/noisy and should only be tested after the static behavior is stable.

Current next experiment candidate: remove bottom-terminal from the active managed layout family and test whether the right-sidebar model becomes stable enough. Keep right-popup as the main fallback design if the sidebar model continues to fight Zellij layout behavior.
Implemented the bottom-terminal removal experiment locally: active managed layout family now has only single stack variants (left sidebar open/closed x agent open/closed), and layout-family next/previous actions short-circuit as no-ops instead of traversing hidden families. Validation passed after the child commit was amended: child full cargo test, child nix build, main runtime nix build with local pane-orchestrator override, generated Zellij config plus zellij setup --check, and full .#yazelix package build with local child override. Pending manual session test before close, main lock update, commit, or push.
Rejected follow-up: do not use Zellij pane suppression/hide_pane_with_id for the closed agent sidebar. It actually hides the tiled pane from the layout and lets Zellij reflow or replace that slot with another existing pane, which breaks the persistent managed-right-sidebar model. Keep the current width-1 closed layout as the stable baseline unless a layout-native narrower visual treatment is found.
Manual design test approved by maintainer. Pane frames make the sidebar model look better than no-frame mode. Known minor visual artifact: closing the right agent sidebar can leave a small visual oddity on the left sidebar, but it is minimal and accepted for this slice. Right-popup remains a viable user-level alternative because the action surface can be rebound while the managed sidebar remains available.
Landing validation after published child commit e24fefe: main flake.lock now points at github:luccahuguet/yazelix-zellij-pane-orchestrator/e24fefe; no-override .#runtime build passed; generated Zellij config from the locked runtime passed zellij setup --check; workspace/session asset validator passed; no-override .#yazelix build passed; git diff --check passed.
close_reason: Completed managed Codex right sidebar slice. Manual design test approved; child pane-orchestrator commit e24fefe was pushed and locked in main flake.lock; no-override runtime/package builds, generated Zellij setup check, workspace/session validator, and diff check passed. Known minor visual artifact with pane frames accepted for this slice; right-popup remains available as a user-level keybinding alternative.

title: Keep pane-orchestrator messaging self-contained per Yazelix session
description:
Alt+m and other pane-orchestrator actions can sometimes materialize a visible broken pane named like yazelix_pane_orchestrator instead of handling the command through the already-loaded background plugin instance.

Live investigation showed that sending a pane-orchestrator message with the same plugin alias but a different runtime_dir configuration can cause Zellij to instantiate a second visible plugin pane. That breaks session isolation and is especially plausible when multiple Yazelix instances with different runtime roots are open or when repo/runtime command surfaces mix.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Pane-orchestrator keybind and CLI transport paths do not create a second plugin instance just because another Yazelix runtime root exists elsewhere
2. Alt+m continues to open a new terminal in the current tab workspace root
3. Existing session-local plugin behavior remains functional without per-message runtime_dir coupling
4. Regression coverage defends the no-per-message-runtime-coupling contract
close_reason: Completed

title: Tighten the test contract further and cut LOC, especially Nushell and tests
description: Investigate how to make the test contract stricter while reducing overall LOC. Focus especially on Nushell code size and the test suite footprint, since a significant chunk of the LOC growth is test code.
notes:
Progress on 2026-03-29:
- Extracted shared managed-config fixture and e2e log helpers into nushell/scripts/dev/test_yzx_helpers.nu.
- Reduced duplicated plumbing across test_yzx_core_commands.nu, test_config_migrate_e2e.nu, test_stale_config_diagnostics_e2e.nu, and test_upgrade_summary_e2e.nu.
- Reduced duplicated doctor/config fixture setup across test_yzx_doctor_commands.nu and test_yzx_generated_configs.nu.
- Fixed stale test setup assumptions that still wrote managed config to legacy root paths instead of user_configs/.
- Landed in commits c62577c and 8f1383e.
Remaining work:
- Continue scanning for honest shared-fixture reductions in remaining high-duplication Nushell tests.
- Keep removing only setup/plumbing duplication; preserve strong behavior coverage and avoid weak existence tests.
close_reason: Tightened the default-suite contract to spec-backed entrypoints only, removed regression-only sidecars, and pruned low-signal tests to cut default-suite time and LOC.

title: Clean up extra floating panes when popup or menu closes
description: Opening the managed popup or menu floating pane can bring other floating panes forward. When the popup/menu is toggled off, Yazelix currently hides only that managed pane and leaves the other floating panes visible. The close path should either isolate the popup/menu so unrelated floating panes are not brought forward, or at minimum hide/restore the extra floating panes when toggling the managed popup/menu off.
design: Investigate the Zellij popup/menu toggle path and pane-orchestrator floating-pane behavior. Prefer a precise contract that shows only the managed popup/menu if Zellij supports it cleanly. If not, capture the floating-pane set before opening and restore/hide unrelated floating panes when the managed popup/menu closes. Keep popup and menu behavior consistent, avoid disrupting normal non-floating workspace panes, and add behavior-backed tests around the toggle-off cleanup contract.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Toggling popup off does not leave unrelated floating panes visible because of the popup open action. 2. Toggling menu off follows the same cleanup behavior. 3. The fix does not close or retarget normal tiled editor/sidebar panes. 4. Behavior is defended by focused tests or a documented manual check if live Zellij behavior cannot be unit-tested directly.
close_reason: Completed: popup/menu close now hides the active tab floating layer after closing the managed transient; contract test added and pane-orchestrator wasm rebuilt/synced.

title: Evaluate role-based pane placement architecture
description: Evaluate a data-driven workspace model where semantic roles such as file_tree, editor, agent, and git_client can be assigned to placement slots such as left_sidebar, right_sidebar, top_popup, and bottom_popup. Keep role identity, placement, routing, lifecycle, and keybindings as separate decisions before implementation.
acceptance_criteria: Decision records the recommended architecture boundary between roles, placements, routing, and keybindings; identifies which implementation beads should follow; rejects or defers any ideas that add config surface without reducing real workspace complexity.
notes:
2026-05-10 current architecture shape to evaluate: file_tree -> left_sidebar, editor -> stack/main, agent -> right_sidebar, config_ui -> top_popup, git_client -> bottom_popup. Command_pane stays on the existing Alt+Shift+M command palette path and is not a placement default. The earlier editor -> top_popup experiment is demoted because editor state is durable while top_popup is better used for transient config UI. If git_client owns bottom_popup, Alt+t can be freed, deprecated, or retained as an alias after the command-surface decision.
Architecture decision set completed across docs/role_based_pane_placement_decision.md, docs/directional_placement_keymap_decision.md, and docs/role_placement_config_decision.md. Accepted boundary: semantic roles, placements, lifecycle/routing, keybindings, and user config remain separate. Implementation should now proceed as concrete slices for config metadata/HM/schema, generated actions/keybindings, runtime/orchestrator role commands, agent/git_client panes, docs, and terminal manual validation.
close_reason: Completed: all child decisions are closed and the architecture decision set is recorded in docs/role_based_pane_placement_decision.md, docs/directional_placement_keymap_decision.md, and docs/role_placement_config_decision.md. The accepted boundary separates semantic roles, placements, routing/lifecycle, keybinding policy, and user configuration, and identifies concrete implementation slices without adding unnecessary knobs.

title: Evaluate pane role identity and cross-role routing
description: Decide whether Yazelix panes should expose stable semantic roles independent of placement, and how role-to-role routing should work. Focus on file_tree targeting editor, and keep routing from depending on physical pane geometry.
acceptance_criteria: Decision defines whether roles are first-class, how a role target is identified, whether roles are singleton or repeatable by default, what happens when the target role is missing, and what failure messages or creation behavior are acceptable. It explicitly rejects any design where file opens depend only on a hard-coded placement.
