notes: Decision recorded in docs/role_based_pane_placement_decision.md: roles are first-class, singleton per tab by default, and routing targets semantic role ids rather than physical placement. file_tree routes to editor; missing editor may use the existing managed editor creation path, but unsupported/missing role commands fail clearly and shell-opened editors are not adopted.
close_reason: Completed. Role identity and routing decision recorded in docs/role_based_pane_placement_decision.md. Roles are first-class singleton-per-tab targets, routing is semantic rather than geometry-based, file_tree routes to editor, and missing roles either use an existing creation contract or fail clearly. Verified with validate-contracts and validate-docs-experience.

title: Evaluate editor-as-role versus fixed editor surface
description: Analyze the editor under the assumption that it can run in any pane or placement. Decide whether editor should still be modeled as a role, whether main editor is just a default placement, and what Yazi/file-tree targeting requires if the editor is hidden, moved, or absent.
acceptance_criteria: Decision states whether editor remains configurable as a role, whether it defaults to main, whether file_tree/yazi can target a non-visible editor placement, whether visibility is required for correct behavior, and what fallback or fail-fast behavior applies when no editor role exists.
notes:
2026-05-10 current direction: keep editor as a role, but default it to stack/main rather than top_popup. The top_popup editor idea remains useful as an opt-in experiment, but not as the current default candidate. Left_sidebar remains file_tree, config_ui moves to top_popup, command_pane remains the existing Alt+Shift+M palette/popup surface, git_client moves to bottom_popup, and agent likely remains right_sidebar. This bead should still prove that file_tree/Yazi targeting is role-based rather than hard-coded to stack, but the default should preserve editor durability and avoid making editor state feel disposable.
Decision recorded in docs/role_based_pane_placement_decision.md: editor remains a first-class role and defaults to main_stack. Visibility is not required for role routing, but adapters must fail clearly if they cannot accept the route. Default editor top_popup and transient editor popup routing are rejected.
close_reason: Completed. Editor-role decision recorded in docs/role_based_pane_placement_decision.md. Editor remains a first-class role with main_stack as default placement; routing does not require visibility; editor top_popup and transient editor popup defaults are rejected. Verified with validate-contracts and validate-docs-experience.

title: Evaluate placement slots and pane lifecycle
description: Evaluate the placement vocabulary separately from roles: left_sidebar, right_sidebar, top_popup, bottom_popup, and any retained main/stack surface. Decide which placements are persistent, temporary, toggleable, singleton, or compatible with each role.
acceptance_criteria: Decision defines the supported placement set, whether main/stack remains a placement, sidebar versus popup lifecycle rules, how repeated toggles behave, whether multiple roles can share a placement, and which role/placement combinations are unsupported or deferred.
notes:
2026-05-10 current placement shape: left_sidebar=file_tree, stack/main=editor, right_sidebar=agent, top_popup=config_ui, bottom_popup=git_client. Keep command_pane on the existing Alt+Shift+M command palette path, outside the four directional placement slots. This frees top_popup for config UI and avoids adding a vague utility-popup concept. It also intentionally frees Alt+t from being the default git-client toggle if git_client becomes bottom_popup. The placement decision should distinguish durable stack roles from transient popup roles, and should keep editor -> top_popup as an opt-in experiment rather than the default unless testing proves otherwise.

2026-05-10 right-sidebar sizing note: evaluate right_sidebar with a larger width ceiling, likely the current maximum sidebar width of 40%, because the default right_sidebar role is agent and agents need enough horizontal space for conversation, diffs, tool output, and status. This should be treated as a placement-specific sizing decision, not automatically applied to the left file_tree sidebar.

2026-05-10 sidebar exclusivity note: default to single-open-sidebar behavior. Opening left_sidebar should close right_sidebar first, opening right_sidebar should close left_sidebar first, and toggling the currently open sidebar should close it. Do not add an allow-both-sidebars config knob yet; the value is unproven and would multiply layout-family states while consuming too much editor screen real estate. Keep the internal model from precluding a future allow_both policy, but implement/evaluate single_open first.

2026-05-10 focus policy note: do not include top_popup or bottom_popup in sidebar/editor focus keys, because popups pull focus when opened. Use side-specific focus toggles instead of a three-pane focus cycle: Ctrl+y toggles focus between left_sidebar and editor/stack, while Ctrl+Shift+y toggles focus between right_sidebar and editor/stack. Directional placement keys show/hide surfaces; focus keys switch between the editor and a specific open sidebar.

2026-05-10 popup extensibility note: support the mental model that more named popup placements can be added data-driven, but do not implement extra always-on popups by default. The eventual config/docs should include a commented-out third popup example so users understand extension is possible without forking generated layouts. The example should remain disabled and should avoid conflicting with shipped bindings.
Decision recorded in docs/role_based_pane_placement_decision.md: accepted placements are main_stack, left_sidebar, right_sidebar, top_popup, and bottom_popup. Sidebars use single-open lifecycle; popups use managed-popup focus/close lifecycle; command_pane stays outside directional placement slots; placement sharing is deferred beyond one default role per placement.
close_reason: Completed. Placement/lifecycle decision recorded in docs/role_based_pane_placement_decision.md. Accepted placements are main_stack, left_sidebar, right_sidebar, top_popup, and bottom_popup; sidebars default to single-open; popups use managed-popup lifecycle; command_pane stays outside directional slots. Verified with validate-contracts and validate-docs-experience.

title: Evaluate directional placement keymap
description: Evaluate a split directional keymap for role placement and movement independently from the role model. Plain Alt+h/j/k/l remains the Zellij/Yazelix pane-walking layer. Current candidate: Alt+Shift+h/j/k/l summons or toggles placement slots, while Ctrl+Alt+h/l moves tabs left/right and Ctrl+Alt+j/k moves the current pane down/up. Account for Vim movement intuition, Zellij default bindings and action support, Yazelix overrides, first-class Ghostty and WezTerm terminal behavior, kitty keyboard protocol requirements, AltGr/international-keyboard risk, Helix/Yazi/Nushell conflicts, and discoverability.
acceptance_criteria: Decision records whether Alt+Shift+h, Alt+Shift+j, Alt+Shift+k, and Alt+Shift+l are viable default Yazelix-owned placement toggles for left_sidebar, bottom_popup, top_popup, and right_sidebar. It must also decide whether existing Alt+Shift+H/L tab movement should move to Ctrl+Alt+h/l and whether Ctrl+Alt+j/k should become direct current-pane movement. It must document checked conflicts against Yazelix generated bindings, configs/zellij/yazelix_overrides.kdl, upstream Zellij defaults/actions including MoveTab and MovePane, and terminal behavior in Ghostty and WezTerm with zellij.support_kitty_keyboard_protocol both false and true where relevant. It must state whether WezTerm needs enable_kitty_keyboard=true or equivalent setup for the Ctrl+Alt movement layer, what AltGr/international-keyboard caveats remain, and whether the accepted keymap is default-on, opt-in, or rejected.
notes:
2026-05-10 discussion update: Alt+h/j/k/l is not acceptable for placement because it is pane walking in Zellij and Yazelix. Earlier Ctrl+Alt+h/j/k/l placement proposal is superseded by a split-layer model after runtime feedback: with zellij.support_kitty_keyboard_protocol=false, Shift+Alt continued working normally, while Ctrl+Alt remains more exposed to old terminal control-character ambiguity on h/j/k/l. Proposed model: Alt+h/j/k/l = focus/walk panes; Alt+Shift+h/j/k/l = summon/toggle placement slots; Ctrl+Alt+h/l = move current tab left/right; Ctrl+Alt+j/k = move current pane down/up. This makes Alt+Shift the placement-surface layer and Ctrl+Alt the structural movement layer. It requires moving existing Alt+Shift+H/L tab movement off that layer and validating Ctrl+Alt+h/j/k/l in Ghostty and WezTerm, but tab/pane movement is lower-frequency than placement toggles, so it is a better place for the more technical chord.

2026-05-10 current mapping update: evaluate Alt+Shift+h=file_tree/left_sidebar, Alt+Shift+k=config_ui/top_popup, Alt+Shift+l=agent/right_sidebar, and Alt+Shift+j=git_client/bottom_popup. Editor remains stack/main by default, not a directional popup. Keep command_pane on Alt+Shift+M for now instead of assigning it to top_popup. If git_client owns bottom_popup, Alt+t no longer needs to be the git popup default and can be freed, deprecated, or kept as a compatibility alias depending on the final command-surface decision.

2026-05-10 focus binding update: evaluate Ctrl+y as left_sidebar <-> editor focus toggle and Ctrl+Shift+y as right_sidebar <-> editor focus toggle. Do not make these keys cycle through popups; top_popup and bottom_popup already pull focus when opened. This keeps right_sidebar O(1) without adding cycle-state uncertainty. The decision must validate Ctrl+Shift+y in first-class terminals and keep the semantic action remappable if a terminal/layout cannot send it reliably.
Decision recorded in docs/directional_placement_keymap_decision.md: Alt+Shift+h/j/k/l accepted as the default-on placement layer for the role-placement implementation; Ctrl+Alt+h/l tab move and Ctrl+Alt+j/k pane move accepted as the movement target with a first-class Ghostty/WezTerm manual gate before release; Ctrl+Shift+y rejected as an unconditional default while Zellij kitty keyboard protocol remains off by default; legacy aliases should not be kept by default.
close_reason: Completed: recorded docs/directional_placement_keymap_decision.md and linked it from docs/role_based_pane_placement_decision.md. Decision accepts Alt+Shift+h/j/k/l as the placement defaults for the next role-placement implementation, accepts Ctrl+Alt+h/l and Ctrl+Alt+j/k as movement targets behind Ghostty/WezTerm manual validation, and rejects Ctrl+Shift+y as an unconditional default while keyboard protocol support remains off by default. Validated with yzx_repo_validator validate-contracts, yzx_repo_validator validate-docs-experience, and git diff --check.

title: Evaluate data-driven role placement config
description: Design the user-facing configuration shape for assigning roles to placements without committing to implementation. Consider settings.jsonc schema, defaults, Home Manager parity, validation errors, future roles, and avoiding config knobs that preserve too many old/new systems at once.
acceptance_criteria: Decision proposes a concrete config shape or rejects configurability for now; it covers defaults, invalid role or placement names, Home Manager representation, schema/metadata updates, compatibility policy, and how future agent or git_client roles would fit without another breaking redesign.
notes:
2026-05-10 discussion update: keep role placement config separate from keybinding policy. The role/placement model should be data-driven, and keybindings should target placement/action ids rather than being part of role identity. Current keymap candidate from yazelix-h0kp.4 is split-layer: Alt+h/j/k/l walks/focuses panes, Alt+Shift+h/j/k/l summons or toggles placement slots, Ctrl+Alt+h/l moves tabs, and Ctrl+Alt+j/k moves panes. Because Ghostty and WezTerm are Yazelix first-class terminals, defaults and docs may optimize for those terminals, while preserving user-remappable keys for other terminals and international layouts. The config shape should therefore expose role-to-placement assignment and, if needed, action key remaps separately from role definitions.

2026-05-10 current default shape to evaluate in config terms: file_tree -> left_sidebar, editor -> stack/main, agent -> right_sidebar, config_ui -> top_popup, git_client -> bottom_popup. Command_pane remains the existing command palette/popup action, defaulting to Alt+Shift+M, and should not force top_popup ownership. The config decision should decide whether editor -> top_popup is an opt-in profile or merely an experiment, while keeping the default model clear and avoiding a vague top utility popup abstraction.

2026-05-10 sizing config note: evaluate placement-specific width settings rather than a single sidebar width for every side surface. In particular, right_sidebar may need a larger default or ceiling, likely up to 40%, for the agent role, while left_sidebar/file_tree can stay narrower.

2026-05-10 sidebar policy note: default to single_open sidebars. Do not expose allow_both sidebars yet unless evaluation proves real user value. The config shape may reserve a future placement.sidebar_policy concept, but the initial decision should avoid adding a knob that preserves multiplicative layout complexity before it is needed.

2026-05-10 focus config note: model focus actions separately from placement visibility actions. Defaults to evaluate: Ctrl+y = focus left_sidebar/editor, Ctrl+Shift+y = focus right_sidebar/editor. Popups are excluded from these focus actions because opening top_popup or bottom_popup already pulls focus. The config shape should keep these as semantic action bindings so users can remap them.

2026-05-10 role abstraction note: agent should be a real role with command configuration, defaulting to codex, because it is likely to need durable identity and routing behavior. Codex is the first-class Yazelix integration and should not be silently replaced by another tool. OpenCode should be documented as a free/democratic alternative command. If codex is missing or unauthenticated, the runtime should fail clearly and suggest installing/signing in to Codex or changing the agent command, for example to opencode. Git_client should start as a lightweight named role/default popup command, defaulting to lazygit in bottom_popup, without a rich git-specific subsystem until behavior beyond launch/focus/toggle exists. Avoid forcing two heavy new per-role config sections if role placement plus simple command fields cover the need.

2026-05-10 extensibility example note: when the role-placement config is documented or generated, include a commented-out extra popup example to show that users can add another popup data-driven rather than waiting for a hard-coded pane. The example must be disabled by default and must not create runtime behavior. It can show a candidate legacy/free key such as Alt+y only if that key has been vacated by the final keymap; otherwise use a clearly non-conflicting placeholder so docs do not teach a broken binding.
Decision recorded in docs/role_placement_config_decision.md: add a workspace-owned config section with workspace.roles and workspace.placements; keep keybindings separate in zellij.keybindings/native_keybindings; replace old editor.sidebar_*, editor.hide_sidebar_on_file_open, and zellij.popup_* settings during implementation without runtime aliases; mirror the nested JSON shape in Home Manager; use tab_session_restart apply mode; reject invalid role/placement names with explicit replacement guidance.
Refinement after review: file_tree should use a built-in launcher token, not expose the internal runtime script path. Accepted shape is workspace.roles.file_tree.launcher = "yazi_file_tree"; role entries may use launcher or command, not both. launcher is for Yazelix-owned integrations; command remains argv for command-launched roles.
Agent install policy refinement: Codex remains the first-class default agent command, but the default Yazelix flake package should not bundle Codex because pkgs.codex adds roughly 0.9-1.0 GiB of closure/storage. Codex is host-installed by default; Home Manager users can install/manage it declaratively, non-HM users install it however they prefer, and Yazelix must fail clearly if the configured command is missing or unauthenticated. No automatic fallback chain.
close_reason: Completed: recorded docs/role_placement_config_decision.md and linked it from docs/role_based_pane_placement_decision.md. Decision accepts workspace.roles plus workspace.placements as the user-facing shape, keeps keybindings separate, defines defaults/validation/Home Manager/apply-mode/compatibility policy, and rejects extra knobs such as allow_both_sidebars and fallback agent providers. Validated with yzx_repo_validator validate-contracts, yzx_repo_validator validate-docs-experience, and git diff --check.

title: Evaluate agent and git-client workspace role requirements
description: Evaluate future agent and git_client roles before the placement model overfits file_tree/editor. Identify each role's lifecycle, targeting needs, persistence, default placement, and whether it needs special routing to editor, shell, or selected pane.
acceptance_criteria: Decision records expected agent and git_client role behavior, likely default placements, routing targets, singleton/repeatable assumptions, minimum viable implementation boundaries, and which requirements should be deferred until those roles actually exist.
notes:
2026-05-10 trial shape: evaluate agent -> right_sidebar and git_client -> bottom_popup as the likely defaults. Agent should be persistent/visible enough to monitor while editing, but independent from the editor placement. Git_client in bottom_popup replaces the current mental model where Alt+t usually opens lazygit, which may free Alt+t for another action or leave it as a compatibility toggle. The decision should verify whether bottom_popup git feels better than the current generic popup, especially for status/diff/rebase flows, and whether agent needs focus, selected-text, cwd, or editor-target routing beyond placement.

2026-05-10 agent sizing note: evaluate right_sidebar at a wide default or ceiling, likely up to 40%, for the agent role. The agent pane has denser text and tool output than the file_tree, so it probably should not inherit the same narrow sidebar width assumption as left_sidebar. The decision should distinguish default width, max width, and user-configurable width.

2026-05-10 abstraction note: make agent a real first-class role abstraction because it is likely to need durable identity, focus behavior, workspace cwd, future selected-file/changed-file routing, and provider command configuration. Codex should be the first-class/default agent command because Yazelix intends to build a polished Codex workflow. OpenCode should be documented as the more democratic/free-model alternative, but Yazelix should not silently fall back from Codex to OpenCode. If codex is unavailable or unauthenticated, fail with a clear message telling users to install/sign in to Codex or set the agent command to another tool such as opencode. Treat git_client more lightly at first: default command lazygit and bottom_popup placement are useful, but do not grow a rich git_client abstraction until it needs behavior beyond launch/focus/toggle.
Decision recorded in docs/role_based_pane_placement_decision.md: agent is a first-class right_sidebar role, singleton per tab, workspace-root cwd, command-configurable, Codex default, no silent OpenCode fallback. git_client starts lightweight in bottom_popup with lazygit default and managed-popup lifecycle; rich Git/editor routing is deferred.
close_reason: Completed. Agent/git role decision recorded in docs/role_based_pane_placement_decision.md. Agent is a first-class right_sidebar role with Codex default and no silent fallback; git_client starts as a lightweight bottom_popup role with lazygit default and no rich Git subsystem yet. Verified with validate-contracts and validate-docs-experience.

title: Evaluate replacing yzx gc's du store-size probe with a modern Rust tool
description:
Investigate whether Yazelix should replace the current `du -sb /nix/store` size probe used by `yzx gc` with a modern Rust-based alternative such as `dust`, `dua`, or `gdu`.

Scope:
- identify which candidate tools are realistically available in the Yazelix environment or Nixpkgs
- verify whether any candidate provides a stable script-friendly single-path total size mode suitable for automation
- compare startup/runtime cost and output stability against `du -sb`
- avoid switching just for novelty; the replacement must be at least as robust for scripted GC summaries

Success means there is a clear decision: either adopt one concrete Rust tool for the probe with a clean contract, or document why `du -sb` should remain the implementation.
design: Judge replacement candidates by scriptability and robustness first, not by novelty or a Rust implementation. The current du probe may be ugly but acceptable if it is stable, available, and cheap enough. Any replacement must provide a clean single-path total-size contract, acceptable runtime, and predictable output that Yazelix can safely automate.
acceptance_criteria: There is a concrete keep or replace decision for the store-size probe. If replaced, one tool and invocation contract are chosen. If not, the bead records why the current du-based probe remains the better maintained option despite the appeal of modern tooling.
close_reason: Obsolete after deleting yzx gc from the v15 public surface.

title: Avoid stale repo-checkout yzx_core helper selection during profiling
description: Repo-checkout helper resolution can prefer a stale local rust_core/target/release/yzx_core even when that binary is older than the current helper contract. During the v15.3 startup-profile comparison, `yzx dev profile` from the repo checkout failed because the stale release helper rejected `runtime-materialization.materialize`; the same profiling run worked immediately when `YAZELIX_YZX_CORE_BIN` was forced to the current debug helper. This should not require tribal knowledge or a manual env override.
design: Delete first. Do not add another override layer or profiling-only special case. Fix helper resolution so repo-checkout flows choose the freshest compatible built helper instead of blindly preferring target/release when it exists. The likely seam is nushell/scripts/utils/config_parser.nu::get_source_checkout_yzx_core_helper_path / resolve_yzx_core_helper_path. Keep the packaged runtime helper first when it exists, keep YAZELIX_YZX_CORE_BIN as the explicit override, and then make the source-checkout fallback robust against stale debug-vs-release drift. A good solution either proves helper compatibility before choosing a candidate or prefers the newest built candidate rather than the first path in a fixed list.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Repo-checkout commands such as `yzx dev profile` do not fail just because rust_core/target/release/yzx_core is stale while another current local helper exists. 2. The source-checkout fallback no longer blindly prefers target/release over a newer compatible debug build. 3. The fix does not require users to set YAZELIX_YZX_CORE_BIN manually for normal repo work. 4. A focused regression defends the stale-release/current-debug failure mode.
notes:
Implemented 2026-04-21:
- Updated nushell/scripts/utils/config_parser.nu so source-checkout fallback chooses the freshest local yzx_core build by modification time instead of blindly preferring rust_core/target/release/yzx_core. This fixes the profiled repo-checkout failure mode where an older release binary rejected newer helper commands while a current debug build existed.
- Aligned nushell/scripts/dev/yzx_test_helpers.nu::resolve_test_yzx_core_bin with the same freshest-local-helper rule so maintainer tests do not keep selecting a stale release artifact.
- Added a focused regression in nushell/scripts/dev/test_yzx_generated_configs.nu that installs a stale failing release helper plus a newer working debug helper and proves both resolve_yzx_core_helper_path and run_yzx_core_request_json_command use the newer debug build without requiring YAZELIX_YZX_CORE_BIN.

Verification:
- nix develop -c nu -c source nushell/scripts/dev/test_yzx_generated_configs.nu; test_run_yzx_core_request_prefers_newer_source_checkout_helper_over_stale_release\n- nix develop -c nu nushell/scripts/dev/test_yzx_generated_configs.nu\n- nix develop -c nu nushell/scripts/dev/validate_syntax.nu\n- yzx dev profile\n- git diff --check
close_reason: Completed: source-checkout helper selection now prefers the freshest local yzx_core build and the stale release/current debug regression is covered.

title: Make Mandelbrot screen feel like a real fractal zoom
description: Replace the current Mandelbrot animation feel with a more authentic fractal zoom that uses a proper spatial loop rather than a shallow or fake-looking view interpolation.
design: Investigate and implement a real looping Mandelbrot camera path. The loop should preserve fractal continuity as much as practical in a terminal renderer: pick a visually rich target, use a smooth zoom trajectory, avoid obvious snap-back, and consider loop techniques such as zoom-out/zoom-in palindrome paths, crossfade-free cyclic paths, or known self-similar regions where the start/end relationship feels spatially justified. Keep it in-house/std and bounded for terminal runtime. Add tests for loop periodicity, bounded iteration cost, and structural frame progression, but also include a manual visual acceptance note because the core requirement is perceptual quality.
acceptance_criteria: yzx screen mandelbrot visibly reads as a real fractal zoom rather than color pulsing or fake panning; loop boundaries are not jarring; the selected camera path and rationale are documented in code or notes; CPU cost remains bounded on normal terminal sizes; tests defend deterministic output, loop periodicity, and nontrivial visible structure changes across the path; manual visual test notes are recorded before close.
notes: 2026-04-26 correction: the previous symmetric Seahorse Valley palindrome was rejected as the wrong model. The stricter follow-up bead yazelix-1vqn now owns the true Mandelbrot/minibrot loop contract: monotonic zoom into a baby Mandelbrot, then reset only when the mini copy visually matches the parent.
close_reason: Completed: Mandelbrot now uses a deterministic fractal zoom/tour with visible structural motion, bounded iteration detail, and loop continuity. Verified with the yazelix_screen and front-door render tests after the stricter Mandelbrot follow-up landed.

title: Upgrade yzx menu to use fzf for a prettier interactive palette
description: Replace Nushell input list --fuzzy with fzf in yzx menu for a visually polished command palette. fzf is already bundled in the runtime, so this is zero new deps. Use --border, --header, --color, --prompt, --pointer styling, and optionally --preview for command help text. Keep the same palette data pipeline (categories, descriptions, formatting) and preserve the popup/non-popup mode distinction.
close_reason: Closed

title: Audit remaining source-vs-installed runtime identity leaks after the launch-path cleanup
description: After the source-shell export cleanup lands, re-audit the remaining runtime identity surfaces. Check launch, doctor, generated shell hooks, popup/wrapper handoff, and maintainer probes for places where an installed runtime path can still shadow a source checkout or vice versa. Delete redundant aliases and document any remaining intentional split between inferred runtime and installed runtime reference.
notes:
2026-04-04 finding: desktop relaunch latency traced to runtime-identity hash churn, not desktop dispatch itself. The installed runtime package symlinks its nushell tree into a Nix -source path, so path-self inference inside common.nu could resolve the source tree even when YAZELIX_RUNTIME_DIR explicitly pointed at the installed runtime. Because compute_config_state hashes the runtime path, that heuristic made combined_hash oscillate between installed-runtime and source-tree identities and forced rebuilds on reopen. The fix direction is to trust an explicit valid YAZELIX_RUNTIME_DIR in installed flows, while keeping repo-shell DEVENV_ROOT as the user-facing source identity and using a separate setup-only runtime root for shellHook bookkeeping.

Deeper follow-on tightening from this audit is now split under yazelix-le0s: le0s.2 for fail-fast runtime root resolution, le0s.3 for explicit runtime-project materialization, le0s.4 for narrower profile resolvers, and le0s.6 for deprecating generic root aliases.
close_reason: Completed: audited remaining runtime-identity surfaces after the source-shell cleanup. Remaining explicit YAZELIX_RUNTIME_DIR exports are intentional installed-runtime, desktop, wrapper, Home Manager activation, or cross-process handoff boundaries rather than ambient source-checkout leaks. Repo-shell documentation now reflects the DEVENV_ROOT plus IN_YAZELIX_SHELL model. Broader generic alias cleanup remains tracked under yazelix-le0s.6.

title: Clean obsolete default-seed compatibility after JSONC migration
description: Delete compatibility and fixture code that only existed for the retired yazelix_default.toml default seed after settings_default.jsonc became the packaged main settings default.
design: Delete-first cleanup. Keep TOML parsing for explicit legacy/user TOML inputs where diagnostics still depend on it, but remove TOML-default fallback paths and unused default-seed parameters once call sites prove the shipped default seed is JSONC-only.
acceptance_criteria: No runtime/default-generation code path depends on yazelix_default.toml. render_default_settings_jsonc has no TOML default fallback or unused cursor-default argument. Existing legacy/user TOML diagnostics still work. Focused tests and validators pass; LOC scorecard recorded.
notes:
Crate decision: no new crates. Cleanup uses existing jsonc_parser/read_settings_jsonc_value helpers; retain existing TOML parser only for explicit non-JSONC config inputs and contracts.
Implemented cleanup: removed the TOML fallback from render_default_settings_jsonc, removed its unused cursor-default argument, deleted the now-dead settings_surface TOML-to-JSON render helper, removed obsolete cursor-default fixture setup from onboarding tests, made upgrade-contract fixture edits valid JSONC, and fixed the remaining live WezTerm generated config reference to settings_default.jsonc/settings.jsonc. Kept read_config_table TOML support because explicit legacy/user TOML diagnostics still require it. Kept historical docs/upgrade note mentions of yazelix_default.toml because they describe past releases rather than live runtime behavior. Verification: cargo check -p yazelix_core -p yazelix_maintainer; cargo test -p yazelix_core settings_surface; cargo test -p yazelix_core onboard_commands; cargo test -p yazelix_core --test yzx_core_config_normalize --test yzx_core_settings_jsonc_patch --test yzx_core_config_ui; cargo test -p yazelix_maintainer --test repo_upgrade_contract; validate-config-surface-contract; validate-workspace-session-contract; validate-upgrade-contract; validate-rust-test-traceability; git diff --check. Stale live reference scan: no yazelix_default.toml outside historical docs/upgrade validator/beads. LOC scorecard HEAD..candidate excluding .beads: raw 45 insertions/146 deletions; runtime net -89, tests net -12; tokei code delta -98, total delta -101.
close_reason: Completed cleanup of obsolete default-seed compatibility after the JSONC migration. Removed TOML default rendering fallback, unused cursor argument, stale fixtures, and stale live WezTerm reference; focused tests and validators pass.

title: Make generated Zellij layout validation detect stale or corrupt content
description: Startup now rerenders generated Zellij layouts before handoff, so runtime risk is lower, but doctor/workspace asset checks still treat generated layouts as valid when the expected files merely exist. A corrupt layout can be reported as fresh.
design: Decide the smallest honest validation surface: compare generated layouts against the current render output, add generated-layout markers, or record/check layout hashes in the existing generation metadata. Keep this as doctor/status honesty unless implementation shows startup still has a live risk.
acceptance_criteria: Doctor/workspace asset validation reports stale or corrupt managed generated Zellij layout files instead of accepting existence-only; repair remains `yzx doctor --fix` / runtime materialization; focused regression covers a corrupt existing managed layout; runtime startup behavior stays simple and does not add a second layout renderer.
close_reason: Completed: generated Zellij layouts now carry a Yazelix layout marker plus the current Zellij generation fingerprint. Workspace asset validation reads the existing generation metadata and reports generated layouts as stale/corrupt when the marker or fingerprint is missing or mismatched, with repair remaining yzx doctor --fix / runtime materialization. Verification: cargo fmt --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml --all --check; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core workspace_asset_contract --lib; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core zellij_materialization --lib; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core --test yzx_core_config_normalize runtime_materialization_materialize_writes_generated_artifacts_and_records_state; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core --test yzx_core_config_normalize runtime_materialization_materialize_replaces_plain_generated_zellij_config; cargo check --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core; cargo run --quiet --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_maintainer --bin yzx_repo_validator -- validate-workspace-session-contract; git diff --check.

title: Sweep remaining stale shell-hook wording after runtime setup ownership removal
close_reason: Completed

title: Remove the need to clone the Yazelix repo under ~/.config/yazelix
description: Eliminate any remaining requirement for end users to keep a full Yazelix git clone under ~/.config/yazelix in order to run the product normally.
design: Treat this as a runtime-boundary and packaging-quality issue. First identify which paths still assume a live repo checkout instead of a packaged or generated runtime layout. Then decide whether the correct fix is packaging changes, relocated runtime assets, generated config changes, or documentation cleanup. Avoid local-host workarounds that keep the clone requirement alive implicitly.
acceptance_criteria: Normal Yazelix usage no longer requires a git clone living under ~/.config/yazelix. Any remaining maintainer-only clone assumptions are explicit and documented. The final behavior works through the supported packaged or relocated runtime paths, not only from a source checkout.
close_reason: Runtime entrypoints and tracked terminal snapshots no longer require a repo clone under ~/.config/yazelix

title: Define the package-ready Yazelix runtime/config root contract
description: Make the config root versus runtime asset root split explicit for non-clone installs. Clarify which environment variables and helpers own user config under ~/.config/yazelix versus shipped/runtime assets under the installed runtime location.
close_reason: Superseded by completed child yazelix-hac.2

title: Define the package-ready Yazelix runtime/config root contract
description: Make the config root versus runtime asset root split explicit for non-clone installs. Clarify which environment variables and helpers own user config under ~/.config/yazelix versus shipped/runtime assets under the installed runtime location.
close_reason: Completed: added the runtime root contract spec and linked it from the architecture map.

title: Make shell, terminal, and desktop entrypoints resolve runtime assets without a repo clone
description: Remove launcher assumptions that Yazelix scripts live under ~/.config/yazelix. Terminal wrappers, desktop entry launchers, and startup entrypoints should resolve shipped runtime assets from the installed runtime root while keeping user config under the config root.
close_reason: Completed: POSIX launchers, devenv wrappers, and yzx launch now resolve shipped runtime assets through the runtime root instead of assuming ~/.config/yazelix is a repo checkout.

title: Remove bundled Yazi runtime path assumptions about ~/.config/yazelix
description: Fix shipped Yazi configs, plugins, and related command launchers so they invoke Yazelix integration scripts through the runtime root rather than assuming a source checkout under ~/.config/yazelix.

title: Update installation and user-facing docs for non-clone Yazelix runtime installs
description: Revise docs and help text so normal usage no longer assumes users cloned the repo into ~/.config/yazelix. Keep maintainer/source-checkout instructions explicit where they still apply.

title: Fix yzx update runtime output ordering
description: yzx update runtime currently prints captured stdout before stderr from nix run, which makes Nix's build/unpack lines appear after the install app says the runtime is already installed. Preserve the real event order or otherwise render the build phase clearly so the update flow does not look like it creates a second runtime after install already completed.
close_reason: Stream yzx update runtime output directly so build/install phases stay in order

title: add sections in troubleshooting explaining how to remove older repo based installs
description:
Imported GitHub issue #568.

tricks like checking config hooks in .bashrc and .config/nu (or similar)
close_reason: Closed on GitHub

title: Design Yazelix action keymap config
description:
## Decision
Yazelix-owned action remaps are implemented through the shared Rust action registry and owner-scoped config maps.

Zellij semantic actions live under `zellij.keybindings`; curated native Zellij policy lives under `zellij.native_keybindings`; Yazi-owned generated integrations live under `yazi.keybindings`. Arbitrary upstream tool keymaps remain in their native sidecars.

Default directional map:
- `Alt Shift H/J/K/L`: left sidebar, bottom popup, top popup, right Codex agent sidebar
- `Ctrl Shift H/L`: move current tab left/right
- `Ctrl Shift J/K`: move current pane down/up
- `Alt t`: unbound by default

`bottom_popup` and `top_popup` are semantic `yzpp` popup specs over the configured popup program and geometry. They are good remap defaults; they do not require distinct placement geometry yet.
acceptance_criteria: Decision defines the boundary between Yazelix-owned actions and native upstream keybinding surfaces. It proposes the config shape, names supported profiles if any, identifies required migration/docs behavior, and creates implementation follow-ups. It must account for the Helix command-mode remap failure and Zellij Ctrl-g ownership conflict from discussion 565.
notes:
Current-state evaluation: Yazi remapping is possible today via ~/.config/yazelix/user_configs/yazi/keymap.toml, yzx edit yazi-keymap, and yzx import yazi, but it is not a simple Yazelix action-remap surface; users must know Yazi keymap TOML and the merger only extends known sections while preserving Yazelix's managed editor opener. Zellij remapping is possible via ~/.config/yazelix/user_configs/zellij/config.kdl or yzx import zellij, but it is not easy for replacing Yazelix defaults because the materializer appends Yazelix override keybinds after the user config. This means users can add bindings, but suppressing defaults such as Ctrl-g requires a clearer ownership mode or generated keymap policy.
Maintainer direction: proceed with a Yazelix-owned action keymap layer for common integrations while preserving native deep config paths. Zellij keybinding ownership is the sharper gap; Yazi already has functional managed keymap/import paths but needs a friendlier action-remap layer. Arbitrary upstream remaps should remain in upstream tool config rather than becoming a broad Yazelix keybinding DSL.
Issue 578 comment 2026-05-04: `keybinds clear-defaults=true` should be accounted for as a low-level Zellij-native ownership mode under yazelix-v4rp. It helps users reuse one standalone Zellij config in Yazelix, but it does not replace the proposed Yazelix action-remap layer because it requires users to understand/recreate Yazelix integration bindings manually.
Zed lesson link 2026-05-05: docs/zed_architecture_lessons.md recommends designing a small Yazelix action registry before friendly remaps. Each Yazelix-owned action should have a stable id, label, description, owner subsystem, backend steps, default binding, optional arguments, and diagnostics for duplicate/shadowed keys, missing backend actions, context mismatch, and unsupported backends. Start with coarse contexts rather than cloning Zed full context-expression language.
2026-05-21: Added zellij.popup_commands for named yzpp popup surfaces: bottom_popup defaults to lazygit, top_popup defaults to yzx config ui, and menu defaults to yzx menu. Kept zellij.popup_program as the generic yzx popup command. Generated Zellij config resolves leading yzx through the runtime wrapper and includes popup command config in the generation fingerprint.
2026-05-21: Manual test approved the directional keymap plus right Codex sidebar design. Follow-up polish added Ctrl Shift Y as the semantic editor/right-agent focus action, updated user docs and contracts for the two-sidebar model, and consumed published child plugin commits for yzpp popup close hooks and pane-orchestrator right-agent focus.
close_reason: Implemented the directional Yazelix-owned action keymap: Alt Shift H/J/K/L for sidebar, bottom popup, top popup, and agent sidebar; Ctrl Shift H/L for tab moves; Ctrl Shift J/K for pane moves; Alt t freed; top/bottom popup specs generated as semantic yzpp defaults. Verified with Rust keybinding/materialization/config UI tests, config/workspace/contract/docs validators, and yzx_control keys output.

title: Make Yazelix modular
description: Imported GitHub issue #450. Create a concrete modularity plan for Yazelix so major subsystems can evolve with clearer boundaries instead of being coupled through ad-hoc shell glue and shared assumptions.
close_reason: Closing the modularity epic: the core modularity framing work has landed, and the remaining follow-up work now continues as standalone beads instead of staying under this parent epic.

title: Define Yazelix subsystem ownership boundaries
description: Define a concrete ownership map for Yazelix subsystems so runtime/source, user config, generated state, workspace/session state, and Linux/desktop integrations stop bleeding into each other through implicit conventions. The output should be an explicit boundary document grounded in the current codebase, not a vague modularity aspiration.
close_reason: Documented the four-subsystem architecture map, ownership rules, and product-layer model in docs/architecture_map.md and linked it from the README

title: Define the workspace/session contract between Nushell and the pane orchestrator
description: Make the workspace/session boundary explicit between Nushell scripts and the Rust pane orchestrator. Define which side owns tab workspace roots, managed-pane identity, sidebar state, tab naming, and cross-tab isolation so future fixes stop rediscovering the same seam through bugs.
close_reason: Documented the current Nushell/plugin/Yazi workspace-session contract, ownership rules, command contract, invariants, and boundary gaps in docs/workspace_session_contract.md

title: Evaluate embedding a Beads viewer in the Yazelix website
description: Evaluate whether the Yazelix website should expose a Beads-based project viewer, and if so what form it should take: embedded live viewer, static snapshot, or simple external link.
design: Treat this as a product and maintenance question, not just a flashy website widget. Clarify the value to users and contributors, the hosting/update burden, and whether the public project graph should be live, periodically generated, or linked out to an existing viewer. Prefer the simplest surface that still gives meaningful roadmap visibility.
acceptance_criteria: There is a clear decision on whether and how the Yazelix website should expose Beads data. If adopted, the bead leaves behind a concrete delivery shape such as embed, generated page, or external link. If rejected, it records why the maintenance cost or product value is not good enough.

title: Consume pane-orchestrator wasm from a locked child package
description: Teach the Yazelix main flake/runtime package to consume the pane-orchestrator wasm artifact from a locked yazelix-zellij-pane-orchestrator child package input instead of treating configs/zellij/plugins/yazelix_pane_orchestrator.wasm as a source-owned copied binary.
design: This is the main-repo migration from copied artifact provenance to lock-driven package provenance. Keep the generated Zellij alias, permissions repair, runtime plugin copy, and doctor freshness behavior unchanged from the user perspective. Do not duplicate the child plugin implementation in Yazelix.
acceptance_criteria: Yazelix has a locked child input or equivalent package lock for yazelix-zellij-pane-orchestrator; packaging/mk_runtime_tree.nix or the relevant runtime package layer places the child package wasm at configs/zellij/plugins/yazelix_pane_orchestrator.wasm in the runtime tree; local development has an explicit override-input/path workflow; nix build .#runtime and workspace/session validation cover the integrated artifact; regular users do not need adjacent child checkouts.
notes:
Follow-up from yazelix-n7j5. Requires the child package artifact from the package-output bead before implementation.
2026-05-20: Local main-repo implementation is staged: flake input/package artifact plumbing consumes yazelix-zellij-pane-orchestrator, runtime tree materializes configs/zellij/plugins/yazelix_pane_orchestrator.wasm from the child package, and copied-wasm validators/tooling were removed. Verified with nix build .#runtime --override-input yazelixZellijPaneOrchestrator ../yazelix-zellij-pane-orchestrator --override-input yazelixZellijPopup ../yazelix-zellij-popup --no-link --no-write-lock-file, cargo fmt/check/test, workspace/session validator, and contract validator. Not closed yet because the child package commit b489341 is local-only; normal nix build .#runtime --no-write-lock-file fails against remote 725f80f because flake.nix is absent. Remaining step: after maintainer manual test/approval, push child commit, update main flake.lock to the GitHub rev, rerun normal runtime build without overrides.
2026-05-20: Stashed the local main-repo migration as stash@{0} (message: wip child plugin package consumption before child publish) after it broke Home Manager flake updates while the pane-orchestrator child package commit remained unpublished. The child repo package commit b489341 remains committed locally; resume by pushing that child commit after maintainer approval, then reapply the stash and update the main lock.
close_reason: Completed: main flake now consumes yazelix-zellij-pane-orchestrator as a locked GitHub child package at b489341; runtime packaging materializes the child package wasm at configs/zellij/plugins/yazelix_pane_orchestrator.wasm; verified with nix build .#runtime --no-link without override inputs plus Rust checks/tests and workspace/session contract validation.

title: Package bd for Nix/Home Manager and update AGENTS.md workflow
description:
After migration tooling exists and bd feature parity is confirmed:
1. Add bd (gastownhall/beads) to the Nix flake or Home Manager config.
2. Rewrite all AGENTS.md sections that reference br/bv commands to use bd equivalents. Key sections: Beads Workflow, Beads Workflow Integration, Session Protocol, every command example.
3. Remove bv (beads_viewer) entirely — bd has no direct bv equivalent for robot-triage/robot-plan/robot-insights, and those features are not needed. Replace the graph-aware triage workflow with bd ready, bd prime, bd stale, bd blocked, and bd graph.
4. Switch .beads/ from br+fsqlite storage to bd+embeddeddolt storage. This means bd init --force in the repo and then bd import of the migrated JSONL.
5. Update .beads/.gitignore for bd conventions (embeddeddolt/ instead of beads.db/beads.db-wal).
6. Remove br from the Nix/Home Manager config and remove the manually-installed ~/.local/bin/br.
7. Remove beads-viewer from the Nix/Home Manager config since bv is not compatible with bd storage.
close_reason: Completed repo-side migration: AGENTS/docs/CI now use bd, live maintainer issue sync uses bd, the maintainer shell packages pinned Beads v1.0.0 from upstream with the local hash/ICU override, and br/bv references are removed from tracked live surfaces. Personal profile cleanup for old br/bv binaries was intentionally left outside the repo.

title: Investigate why test_yzx_commands.nu takes so long
description: Investigate why test_yzx_commands.nu takes so long now that lightweight profiling exists, and separate genuinely slow contracts from unnecessary default-suite cost.
design: Use the profiling work from yazelix-q9mj rather than guessing. Break the runtime down by major test segments, identify whether the cost comes from real high-signal behavior checks or from avoidable setup and churn, and decide whether the right fix is optimization, lane reclassification, or test deletion. Keep the test-governance spec in mind: slower tests are acceptable only if they defend important behavior.
acceptance_criteria: There is a clear explanation for where test_yzx_commands.nu time is going, including the dominant slow segments and whether they are justified. The bead leaves behind concrete next steps such as optimize, split into another lane, or keep as-is with explicit justification.
close_reason: Closed on GitHub

title: Eliminate inline quoted shell-script execution seams
description:
Yazelix has accumulated several places where Nushell assembles inline quoted shell programs and passes them to `bash -lc`, `sh -c`, or similar entrypoints. The pattern is brittle, hard to review, awkward to test, and easy to make worse when timing or quoting changes. It also spreads shell-policy decisions across Nushell string interpolation instead of keeping them at a clean shell boundary.

This bead exists to drive a repo pass that removes or refactors those seams into cleaner boundaries.
design:
Treat this as a code-shape and boundary-cleanup epic, not a blind search-and-replace.

Delete-first guidance:
- do not replace one awkward inline shell string with a different awkward inline shell string
- prefer checked-in helper scripts or structured argv execution over bigger `bash -lc` blobs
- if a shell trampoline is truly unavoidable, keep the script body fixed and pass dynamic values via argv or env instead of interpolating them into the script text

Known current surface:
- production/runtime helpers such as `launcher_resolution.nu`, `yzx/screen.nu`, and `setup/welcome.nu`
- the detached terminal launch probe in `terminal_launcher.nu` is the hottest production case, but its performance-sensitive cleanup is already owned by `yazelix-4xp1.1` and should not be duplicated here
- dev/maintainer/test seams such as `record_demo.nu` and test harnesses that shell through quoted inline scripts

The point is not to eliminate every shell invocation. The point is to eliminate the inline quoted-script style as a maintained pattern.
acceptance_criteria:
1. The major inline quoted shell-script seams in production/runtime and dev/test paths are tracked explicitly.
2. New refactors replace the pattern with helper scripts, structured argv execution, or fixed-body trampolines with argv/env inputs.
3. `terminal_launcher.nu` cleanup remains coordinated with `yazelix-4xp1.1` instead of being duplicated under a vague repo-wide churn pass.
notes:
Create child beads for the real slices instead of treating this as one giant mechanical refactor.

Expected outcome:
- new code follows the AGENTS shell-boundary rule
- the remaining shell seams become smaller, clearer, and easier to reason about
- the terminal-launch hot path cleanup stays coordinated with `yazelix-4xp1.1`
close_reason: all steps complete

title: Refactor non-launch runtime shell execution seams
description: Refactor the non-launch runtime/product shell execution seams away from inline quoted script bodies. This covers the smaller production helpers that still shell through brittle `bash -lc` snippets even though they are not the main startup hot path.
design:
Target the current known production/runtime seams:
- `nushell/scripts/utils/launcher_resolution.nu::path_is_symlink`
- `nushell/scripts/yzx/screen.nu::poll_for_screen_keypress`
- `nushell/scripts/setup/welcome.nu::poll_for_welcome_keypress`

Preferred shapes by seam:
- use direct Nushell or direct external argv execution when the logic is structured and does not rely on shell semantics
- otherwise use a dedicated checked-in POSIX helper script or a fixed shell trampoline with argv/env inputs

Do not absorb `terminal_launcher.nu` into this child. That performance-sensitive seam is already owned by `yazelix-4xp1.1`.
acceptance_criteria:
1. The identified runtime/product helpers no longer build inline quoted shell-script bodies for their current behavior.
2. Their replacements are smaller and easier to reason about than the previous shell-string construction.
3. The refactor does not regress the visible behavior of keypress polling or launcher-path resolution.
notes:
These seams are smaller than the launch probe, but they still normalize the wrong pattern if left behind. They are good candidates for a clean follow-up pass once the hot terminal-launch path is handled or in parallel if the write scopes stay disjoint.
2026-04-18 implementation guidance:
- Do not assume every inline shell seam should become Nushell, and do not assume every seam should stay shell.
- Preferred decision rule:
  1. If the behavior does not need POSIX shell semantics, rewrite it into direct Nushell logic or direct argv execution.
  2. If the seam is genuinely a shell boundary \(redirection, backgrounding, shell-entry trampolines, or POSIX runtime hooks\), keep it as shell but move it into a checked-in helper script or a fixed-body trampoline with argv/env inputs.
- Explicit non-goal: replacing inline `bash -lc` with a different large interpolated `bash -lc` string.
- Explicit non-goal: porting true shell-boundary behavior into more complex Nushell just because Nushell is already present.
2026-04-18 prioritization note:
- Promote this child ahead of the broader shell-boundary cleanup pass because it is Rust-rewrite-enabling boundary cleanup, not just repo hygiene.
- Recommended sequencing: complete kt5.1 / kt5.1.1 planning and bridge work, then use hm6n.1 as the small runtime-boundary cleanup pass before the first real Rust implementation slice.
- Keep hm6n.2 lower priority; the runtime/product seams here are the higher-leverage rewrite enabler.
close_reason: Replaced the launcher-resolution symlink shell probe with direct Nushell path-type checks, moved screen and welcome timed keypress polling to a shared Nushell helper, removed the dead welcome poll override seam, and added a regression covering broken Home Manager profile-wrapper symlinks.

title: Refactor dev and test shell execution seams
description: Refactor the dev, maintainer, and test harness shell execution seams away from inline quoted script bodies. These are less user-visible than runtime helpers, but they still spread the same brittle shell-boundary style through the repo and make test/debug code harder to trust.
design:
Target current known non-production seams such as:
- `nushell/scripts/dev/record_demo.nu`
- test harnesses that rely on inline `bash -lc` strings with embedded dynamic commands
- maintainer or validation helpers that shell through quoted program text when direct argv or helper scripts would be clearer

This child should prioritize the highest-signal seams first instead of churning every generated test fixture that happens to contain newline-joined file content. The smell to remove is inline shell-program construction for execution, not ordinary text generation.
acceptance_criteria:
1. The major dev/maintainer/test execution seams using inline quoted shell programs are identified and reduced.
2. Cleanup work distinguishes real shell-execution smells from ordinary text-generation helpers.
3. The remaining execution helpers are easier to review and less dependent on fragile shell quoting.
notes:
Useful heuristic:
- in scope: code that builds a shell program and then executes it via `bash -lc`, `sh -c`, or similar
- out of scope: plain text/config generation that happens to use `str join "\n"`
close_reason: Refactored all major dev/maintainer/test shell execution seams: record_demo.nu now invokes vhs directly via nix shell --command; update_workflow.nu replaced sh -c temp-file wrapper with direct nix profile add using .LAST_EXIT_CODE; test_shell_managed_config_contracts.nu replaced bash -lc test -L with direct test -L; test_yzx_maintainer.nu dropped unnecessary login shell flag; test_yzx_workspace_commands.nu replaced bash -lc env-prefix indirection with direct env -u invocation. All files pass syntax validation.

title: Keep Yazelix terminal runtime env from leaking into host Rio
description:
Problem:
Yazelix integration should be able to launch the experimental Rio-derived runtime without polluting the host session or breaking a user's normal Rio terminals. Current evidence from upstream Rio reproduction showed a Yazelix shell exporting RIO_CONFIG_HOME=/home/lucca/.local/share/yazelix/configs/terminal_emulators/yzxterm, which caused plain `rio` commands to pick up Yazelix Terminal config instead of vanilla host Rio config.

Practical implication today:
Users and agents must be able to install/run Yazelix and still reproduce upstream Rio bugs, launch host Rio from desktop entries, and compare vanilla Rio behavior without hidden Yazelix env contamination. This matters especially while yazelix-terminal remains experimental.

Scope:
- Audit Yazelix launch/materialization code that sets YAZELIX_TERMINAL, YAZELIX_TERMINAL_CONFIG, RIO_CONFIG_HOME, or terminal-wrapper env.
- Ensure Yazelix passes Yazelix-owned config only to the Yazelix Terminal process boundary, not as ambient shell state for user commands.
- Ensure generated yzxterm config remains under Yazelix-owned state/config paths and is never treated as host Rio config.
- Add diagnostics or a doctor/status check that warns when a Yazelix shell/session has ambient RIO_CONFIG_HOME or package-only loader env that would contaminate host terminal launches.
- Coordinate with the yazelix-terminal isolation bead yzt-host-rio-isolation-x2a for wrapper-side behavior.

Acceptance criteria:
- Starting a Yazelix session with the experimental yzxterm runtime does not leave RIO_CONFIG_HOME ambient in user shells unless the user explicitly configured that.
- Launching plain `rio` from inside a Yazelix-managed session uses host Rio defaults or the user's host Rio config, not Yazelix-generated yzxterm config.
- Existing yzxterm launch still receives the generated Yazelix config through an explicit process-local contract.
- A regression test, launch-env snapshot, or documented manual verification covers the host-Rio isolation case.
close_reason: Implemented parent-side yzxterm env isolation: Yazelix clears ambient RIO_CONFIG_HOME at the yzxterm process boundary, passes only YAZELIX_TERMINAL_CONFIG plus the child sanitization marker, documents the launch contract, and adds a doctor warning for contaminated host-Rio env. Verified with cargo fmt, focused cargo tests for launch env and doctor warning, and git diff --check.

title: Implement deterministic ratconfig JSONC migration primitives
description: Design and implement deterministic ratconfig migration primitives for JSONC settings surfaces. The first reusable operation set should cover simple config arithmetic: rename field, delete field, add missing default, and narrow value transforms. It should run atomically when a project opens ratconfig/config UI and should be generic enough for the child repo while leaving Yazelix-specific migration policy in the Yazelix adapter.
design: Keep the engine small and declarative. The child repo owns operation semantics and JSONC patching primitives; Yazelix owns which operations apply to its settings versions. Complex array transforms, broad type migrations, and TOML adapters are future work unless a simple typed primitive naturally covers them.
acceptance_criteria: Generic migration primitives can deterministically transform a JSONC settings document with atomic write support. Operations are tested for rename, delete, add-default, and at least one narrow transform. Yazelix can supply its own migration plan without embedding Yazelix-specific fields in the generic engine.
notes: Rust dependency gate: production crates are serde_json for document values and jsonc-parser for comment-preserving JSONC CST edits. No broad migration framework; operation semantics are in-house because the first surface is a small deterministic config arithmetic language. Rejected alternatives: whole-file JSON rewrite, TOML-first adapter, app-specific Yazelix migration code in the generic engine. Packaging impact: this lands in the child crate and is consumed by Yazelix as a normal Rust dependency during extraction.
close_reason: Completed in yazelix-ratconfig child repo commit 1ae35c9: added generic comment-preserving JSONC set/unset primitives plus deterministic migration operations for rename, delete, add default, and caller-supplied narrow transforms. Rename refuses destination collisions. Verification: cargo fmt; cargo test in /home/lucca/pjs/yazelix-ratconfig. The engine returns transformed text and mutation facts so Yazelix can own policy and atomic writes in its adapter.

design: Keep this delete-first and narrow. Do not add parser fallbacks or special-case sweep-only config tolerance. title: Implement a shared runtime contract checker and reuse it across launch and doctor
description: Implement a small shared runtime contract checker for the overlap between launch-preflight and `yzx doctor`, instead of scattering the same dependency reasoning across launch, startup, and doctor-specific helpers. The checker should stay lightweight enough for normal entrypoint use, return structured results, and give both launch and doctor one authoritative story for the checks they truly share. This is not a mega-doctor rewrite: the first slice should focus only on the runtime blockers and dependency conditions already called out by `yazelix-j4qv`.
design: Treat this as a narrow shared-evaluation layer plus separate presentation layers. The checker should probably live in a small runtime-validation module and return structured records with fields such as check id, status, severity, owner surface, and recovery hint. Ground the first slice directly in the current overlap seams instead of inventing a giant abstraction: `start_yazelix.nu` currently owns startup working-directory and generated-layout/runtime-script checks, `yzx/launch.nu` owns runtime launch-script checks before re-entry, `core/launch_yazelix.nu` owns new-window working-directory and terminal-availability checks, and `doctor.nu` owns richer rendering/reporting. The first implementation slice should cover only the checks that both launch and doctor can honestly share today: working-directory validity, required runtime entrypoint scripts, required generated layout presence, and configured terminal availability for new-window launch. Launch should consume only hard blockers from that result set and fail fast. `yzx doctor` should be able to render the same result set more fully, but doctor-only and install-only surfaces should remain separate for now. Do not try to absorb config-migration preflight, shell-hook freshness, desktop-entry freshness, version drift, Helix runtime conflicts, plugin/session-local health, or heavy install-smoke invariants into the first shared checker just because they are all “health” concerns. This bead should also standardize the result vocabulary enough that later Rust work (`yazelix-kt5.3`) has a clean target without forcing a daemon or giant abstraction now.
acceptance_criteria: Yazelix has one shared runtime contract checker used by both launch-facing code and `yzx doctor` for the overlapping runtime dependency checks covered by `yazelix-j4qv`. The checker returns structured results rather than only ad hoc error strings. The first shared slice covers at least: missing or invalid working directory, missing runtime entrypoint scripts, missing generated layout required for startup, and configured terminal availability for new-window launch. Startup and launch entrypoints consume the shared checker instead of keeping those checks split across `start_yazelix.nu`, `yzx/launch.nu`, and `core/launch_yazelix.nu`, while `yzx doctor` can surface the same shared results without reimplementing their core reasoning separately. Doctor-only diagnostics such as shell-hook freshness, desktop-entry freshness, version drift, Helix runtime conflicts, plugin health, and heavy install/package checks remain outside the first shared checker unless they clearly fit the same runtime contract result model. The implementation stays lightweight enough for normal launch use and does not regress startup into a slow audit path.
close_reason: Implemented shared runtime contract checker and reused it across launch and doctor

title: Collapse environment_bootstrap.nu onto one devenv shell-entry path
description: `nushell/scripts/utils/environment_bootstrap.nu` currently exports both `run_in_devenv_shell` and `run_in_devenv_shell_command`, but repo search shows only the command-args variant has real callers. The string-command variant appears to be dead convenience surface that still carries overlapping env-handling, refresh, and shell-entry logic. Delete-first goal: remove the unused shell-entry path instead of keeping two bootstrap APIs that do the same job slightly differently.
notes:
2026-04-08 implementation:
- Deleted the unused `run_in_devenv_shell` export from `nushell/scripts/utils/environment_bootstrap.nu`.
- Verification: repo-wide search now finds no remaining `run_in_devenv_shell` references; importing `run_in_devenv_shell_command` from the module still succeeds; `validate_default_test_traceability.nu` passes.
- This confirms the requirement cut from step 1: Yazelix only needed the argv-based shell-entry contract.
close_reason: Completed: deleted the unused string-command shell-entry export and kept the argv-based bootstrap path as the sole contract.

title: Fix CI Nushell syntax validator host-state dependency
description: GitHub CI validate-nushell-syntax fails on nushell/config/config.nu because the validator inherits a Yazelix shell environment but CI lacks generated files under ~/.local/share/yazelix/initializers/nushell. The validator should isolate HOME and provide generated initializer stubs so syntax validation does not depend on maintainer host state.
acceptance_criteria: CI validate-nushell-syntax passes in a clean or generated-file-free HOME; validator has focused regression coverage for config.nu generated source stubs; fix is committed with validation evidence.
close_reason: Completed: validate-nushell-syntax now uses an isolated temporary HOME with generated Nushell initializer stubs, so CI does not depend on maintainer host state. Added regression coverage for config.nu generated source stubs. Also repaired GitHub/Beads issue-contract drift with yzx dev sync_issues. Verified empty-HOME nix develop validate-nushell-syntax, focused cargo test, validate-rust-test-traceability, validate-package-rust-test-purity, and validate_issue_bead_contract.nu.

title: Investigate repo-root terminal_overrides regeneration
description: Investigate and fix why an untracked terminal_overrides/ directory is being recreated in the repository root during terminal config generation instead of staying inside the supported Yazelix config or runtime paths.
design: Treat this as a real path-resolution bug, not just local clutter. Identify the exact writer and the exact path-resolution mistake that creates repo-root terminal_overrides/. Then tighten the contract so override scaffolds and generated files can only land in supported locations such as ~/.config/yazelix/terminal_overrides or the runtime share tree. Avoid one-off local cleanup hacks; fix the path derivation at the source and document the intended destination. If tests or dev commands are responsible, make the fixture or runtime root explicit so they cannot accidentally write into the repository.
acceptance_criteria: No command or test path used by Yazelix recreates terminal_overrides/ in the repository root. The real source of the bug is identified and fixed, and the intended destination for override files is documented. Add focused unit or helper tests for the path-resolution function that was wrong. Add an end-to-end regression with detailed logging that runs the reproducing generation flow from the repo root and proves that only the supported config or runtime locations are written.
notes: Already fixed in current generated-config behavior. Repo inspection finds no terminal_overrides writer or directory in the repo root; the remaining references are focused tests. test_generate_all_terminal_configs_keeps_terminal_overrides_opt_in asserts generation does not scaffold user override directories, and test_terminal_override_imports_ignore_yazelix_dir_runtime_root proves terminal override imports stay under HOME/.config/yazelix/user_configs/terminal even when YAZELIX_DIR points at a runtime root. Full default yzx suite passed 90/90 after the current cleanup pass.
close_reason: Already fixed and defended by generated terminal config tests.

title: Fix config validation for optional TOML fields
description: Yazelix config validation currently infers the allowed schema from the parsed default TOML surface, which means optional fields that only exist as commented examples in yazelix_default.toml are treated as unknown. Comments should not affect validation in any way. Fix the validator so supported optional fields like helix.runtime_path are accepted based on a real schema/source-of-truth rather than whether the default TOML happens to materialize that key after parsing. The doctor and startup diagnostics should then report real config problems instead of false unknown-field errors for documented optional settings.
close_reason: GitHub issue #499 is closed; tracker synced.

title: yzx update devenv should also sync the devenv lock input
description:
## Problem

`yzx update devenv` only runs `nix profile install` to update the devenv CLI binary, but does not update the implicit `devenv` input in `devenv.lock`. This leaves the lock pinned to an older module revision, causing devenv to print a version-skew warning on every `devenv shell` / `yzx env`:

    ✨ devenv 2.0.7 is newer than devenv input (2.0.6) in devenv.lock. Run 'devenv update' to sync.

The devenv input (`github:cachix/devenv?dir=src/modules`) is mandatory — devenv injects it unconditionally in its generated flake. It cannot be removed. The maintainer-facing `yzx dev update devenv` already calls `devenv update devenv` to sync the lock, but the user-facing command does not.

## Proposed Fix

After `yzx update devenv` successfully installs/updates the CLI, also run `devenv update devenv` in the yazelix directory to re-lock the devenv modules input to match the new CLI version.

### Location

`nushell/scripts/core/yazelix.nu`, `yzx update devenv` command (lines ~399-476). After the `nix profile install` succeeds, add:

```nushell
# Sync the devenv lock input to match the new CLI
let yazelix_dir = (get_yazelix_runtime_dir)
cd $yazelix_dir
^(resolve_preferred_devenv_path) update devenv
```

### Alternatives considered

- `devenv.warnOnNewVersion = false` — suppresses the warning but hides legitimate drift. Not a real fix.
- Telling users to run `devenv update devenv` manually — bad UX, the whole point of `yzx update devenv` is a single command.
- Dropping the devenv input — impossible, devenv requires it for module evaluation.

## Acceptance Criteria

- After `yzx update devenv`, the devenv lock input matches the CLI version (no skew warning on next `yzx env`).
- If the lock sync fails, print a warning but don't fail the overall command (the CLI update already succeeded).
close_reason: Obsolete after the runtime-owned devenv cleanup removed yzx update devenv; the remaining version-skew architecture work is tracked in yazelix-15bx.

title: Reduce Yazelix Home Manager activation JSON noise
description:
Why this issue exists and what needs to be done:
Home Manager activation currently prints the full runtime-materialization repair JSON payload from Yazelix, which makes successful switches noisy and hard to scan.

What needs to be done:
Update the activation path to emit a concise success summary instead of dumping the full JSON payload while preserving actionable warnings/errors and verify the Home Manager path still succeeds.
close_reason: Completed

title: investigate session management
description: Imported GitHub issue #522.
notes:
Investigation result, 2026-04-28:
- Session management should stay Zellij-native and workspace-scoped instead of becoming a second Yazelix session database.
- The current durable owner is the pane-orchestrator active-tab session seam plus per-window config snapshots. That is the right source for tab-local workspace root, managed panes, focus/layout state, sidebar Yazi identity, and status-bus facts.
- Product command taxonomy should separate: launch/enter/restart/new window; tab/workspace navigation such as yzx warp; inspection such as yzx inspect and yzx dev inspect_session; explicit save/restore only after a resurrection contract exists.
- Do not bring back persistent_sessions as a user setting. It encourages implicit ownership across windows and conflicts with the new immutable per-window config snapshot direction.
- The next concrete session-management work is covered by yazelix-u66i for project/tab switching and by yazelix-p7qs/yazelix-l38n for explicit resurrection.
close_reason: Completed investigation. Decision: keep session management Zellij-native and workspace-scoped; route concrete work through yzx warp and explicit resurrection follow-ups.

title: Promote Ghostty KGP runtime to default
description: Make the tested Ghostty Kitty graphics passthrough runtime the normal Yazelix default, update docs, keep WezTerm behavior unchanged, and preserve rollback clarity for the temporary Zellij/Yazi forks.
acceptance_criteria: Default Yazelix and Ghostty outputs use the locked Yazelix Zellij/Yazi fork pair and set the passthrough marker; WezTerm output does not set it; user docs describe Ghostty image support and the temporary fork boundary; changes are committed, pushed, and CI is checked.
notes:
Promoted the tested Ghostty Kitty graphics path to the normal Yazelix default.

Implementation:
- Default `yazelix`, `yazelix_ghostty`, `runtime`, `runtime_ghostty`, `yazelix_agent_tools`, and `runtime_agent_tools` use the locked Yazelix Zellij/Yazi fork pair for Ghostty.
- WezTerm keeps upstream Zellij/Yazi and does not get the passthrough marker.
- Removed the short-lived `*_kgp_preview` flake package/app outputs instead of keeping stale canary aliases.
- Home Manager module defaults now get the same Ghostty forked runtime path through `mkYazelixPackage`; `programs.yazelix.package` remains only for selecting a specific prebuilt package.
- Docs describe Ghostty as the default image-preview path and document the temporary fork boundary. The forks should be dropped and archived once upstream Zellij supports the required Kitty graphics path directly enough for Yazelix to return to upstream packages.

Verification:
- `nix build .#yazelix .#yazelix_agent_tools .#yazelix_wezterm --print-out-paths --no-link`
