title: Shrink Zellij materialization without waiting on status-bar toggles
description: Shrink rust_core/yazelix_core/src/zellij_materialization.rs now that bar/layout ownership is clearer enough for deletion-first work. Do not wait on hideable bottom-bar or barless-status toggles; those are low-priority UX/storage options. Target duplicate KDL/layout/render paths and generated-layout ownership that can be deleted or moved behind clearer adapters without changing current integrated status-bar behavior.
design: Delete-first. Do not introduce a public child repo in this bead. Classify remaining zellij_materialization.rs responsibilities into keep-in-core, delete, move to yazelix_bar/layout adapter, and defer. Preserve the current integrated status-bar contract; do not implement or block on hideable/barless status-bar behavior. Prefer deleting duplicate render/materialization paths over adding more wrappers.
acceptance_criteria: zellij_materialization.rs has a documented responsibility map; duplicate KDL/layout/render paths are deleted where behavior is proven equivalent; current integrated status-bar behavior is preserved; hideable/barless status work is explicitly out of scope; the Rust ownership budget is updated; behavior-backed layout/materialization tests still pass; and the bead records raw/tokei LOC delta plus any rejected deletion candidates.
notes:
Roadmap correction 2026-05-08: removed blockers on hideable bottom bar and barless status-bar toggle because the maintainer is not prioritizing those UX/storage options. This bead should proceed as a deletion-first Zellij materialization shrink while preserving current status-bar behavior.
Implementation 2026-05-08: Responsibility map after shrink: keep in core: merged Zellij config source ordering, Yazelix semantic keybinding/remap ownership, plugin artifact wiring, permission seeding, runtime fingerprint/reuse, generated layout template materialization, Yazelix session/status/cache helper paths, and pane-orchestrator/yzpp integration. Delete/move completed: deleted stale no-op keybind layout fragment and placeholder, removed fragment validator/fixture references, collapsed duplicated yazelix-bar render-error mapping behind a local adapter, removed redundant control-plane env wrappers, and deleted weak tests that pinned implementation trivia rather than behavior. Defer/rejected: do not implement hideable/barless status work here; do not extract layout templates/workspace yet because session facts, launch/restart, Zellij commands, pane-orchestrator client, and status cache still share ownership; do not delete open/closed layout variants because their startup closed-sidebar semantics are still distinct; do not move status cache/provider paths to yazelix-bar because they depend on Yazelix runtime/session state. LOC result: diff excluding Beads is 44 insertions / 129 deletions total after inventory updates, net -85 text lines; implementation-only diff before inventory was 27 insertions / 113 deletions, net -86 text lines; tokei Rust code is 66,633, down 65 code LOC; budget raw Rust lines are 72,759, down 73 raw Rust lines.
close_reason: Completed: deleted stale no-op Zellij keybind fragment/placeholder, tightened zellij_materialization error/env/header paths, removed weak implementation-detail tests, updated rust LOC inventory/budget, and verified layout/materialization/contract gates.

title: Fix Nix vendoring hash for extracted cursor crate
description: Home Manager switch fails after extracting yazelix-cursors because rust_core/Cargo.lock now contains the git dependency yazelix_cursors-0.1.0 but packaging/rust_core_helper.nix only declares an outputHash for yazelix_screen.
acceptance_criteria: packaging/rust_core_helper.nix declares the yazelix_cursors-0.1.0 Cargo git output hash; a Yazelix Nix package build reaches past Cargo vendoring without the missing-hash error; the fix is committed with Beads state.
close_reason: Added the missing Cargo git output hash for yazelix_cursors and verified nix build .#yazelix

title: Optimize Zellij config generation on warm startup
description:
The new structured startup profiler shows that warm current-terminal startup is dominated by Zellij config generation in start_yazelix_inner.nu, typically around 2.9s to 3.3s on the maintainer machine. That makes this the highest-value warm-start performance follow-up after yazelix-3n1.

Design:
Keep this delete-first. First identify which parts of merged Zellij config generation are unnecessarily redone on every warm startup and which artifacts can be reused safely without weakening config correctness. Prefer deleting redundant regeneration or broad invalidation before micro-optimizing string processing. Preserve fail-fast behavior and managed-config correctness.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. The current bottleneck inside inner.generate_zellij_config is broken down enough to show which substeps dominate. 2. Warm startup avoids clearly redundant Zellij config work when inputs have not changed. 3. The change does not make generated Zellij state stale across real config/runtime changes. 4. A focused regression or profiling-oriented check defends the intended reuse or invalidation boundary.

title: Smoke-test Yazelix Zellij Popup in plain Zellij

title: Cull removal-only absence tests from governed suites
description:
Remove weak tests whose main value is confirming that a deliberately removed implementation detail or artifact is absent, and add a repo guard so this pattern does not keep reappearing

## Context
Example: shipped_default_registry_parses_without_party only verifies that a preset we intentionally removed is not present
This kind of test does not defend user-visible behavior, a real regression, config compatibility, or an integration boundary
It creates maintenance noise and can pass even when the actual behavior users care about is broken
design:
Use the existing governed-test strength model rather than adding a blanket ban on all negative assertions
Keep useful negative tests when they defend a real contract such as rejecting invalid config, preventing unsafe fallback, or blocking a known bug
Remove or rewrite tests that only assert the absence of old names, removed presets, deleted files, or implementation trivia
Add a validator or rubric check that flags removal-only absence tests by name/marker/content and requires a Defends/Regression/Invariant justification that points to behavior, not cleanup history
acceptance_criteria:
Inventory existing governed tests for removal-only absence checks, including shipped_default_registry_parses_without_party
Remove or rewrite each weak absence test so remaining coverage defends active behavior
Add a repo guard that catches new low-value removal-only tests without rejecting meaningful negative-contract tests
Document the distinction in the test-strength guidance or validator failure message
Run the affected test suites and validator checks
close_reason: Completed: removed weak removal-only absence assertions and added a rust traceability guard for cleanup-only absence tests with behavior-backed exceptions.

title: Improve Yazelix Terminal crash observability
description:
A Yazelix Terminal window appears to have exited silently while its Zellij server/session survived. Investigation of the recent incident found no coredump, no Rio journal entries, no kernel segfault/OOM/GPU fault entries, and no fresh generated Rio log for the affected desktop-launched sessions.

Incident evidence from 2026-06-01 night:
- Current local time during investigation: about 23:47 -03.
- COSMIC launched a Yazelix app scope at 22:52:51 and another at 23:43:23.
- `zellij list-sessions` showed `likable-muskrat` still active from the earlier launch and `loyal-apple` as the current new session, which matches terminal-window exit while Zellij server persisted.
- `coredumpctl list --since "2 hours ago"` through a temporary Nix systemd shell reported no coredumps.
- `journalctl -b` and `journalctl --user` over the incident window had no Rio/Yazelix segfault, panic, coredump, OOM, or GPU-fault entries.
- `~/.config/rio/log/rio.log` was stale at 13:07.
- `~/.local/share/yazelix/configs/terminal_emulators/yzxterm/log/rio.log` was stale at 13:06 despite later 22:52 and 23:43 desktop launches.
- The only nearby panic was an apparently unrelated COSMIC Files `not in a compio runtime` panic at 23:13:36.

Problem:
- When a desktop-launched Yazelix Terminal exits unexpectedly, the current runtime does not leave enough evidence to distinguish a Rio panic, normal exit, compositor close, GPU failure, wrapper failure, or child process exit.

Acceptance criteria:
- Identify whether yzxterm desktop launch is owned by main Yazelix runtime, the yazelix-terminal child wrapper, or both.
- Ensure desktop-launched yzxterm writes fresh stdout/stderr logs for each launch, including argv, config dir, Rio PID, child PID, exit status if observable, and timestamps.
- Ensure Rio file logging is either disabled intentionally with a documented reason or points at the generated yzxterm log directory and updates for each desktop launch.
- Add a diagnostic command or doctor finding that reports recent yzxterm launch logs and stale/missing log state.
- Preserve normal terminal startup latency and avoid unbounded log growth.
- If the terminal process cannot observe its own exit status because it is the exec target, document the owner that can capture it and create a child-repo follow-up if needed.
notes:
Additional user observation during incident: Codex was updating very quickly immediately before the terminal disappeared, "as if it was in fast mode."

Follow-up process evidence:
- The older surviving `likable-muskrat` Zellij server still has active Codex processes under Nushell panes, including `codex resume`.
- The newer `loyal-apple` session also has multiple active Codex processes.
- This supports a plausible stress trigger: Codex may have produced a high-rate stream of terminal updates shortly before the Rio/yazelix-terminal window exited.

Interpretation:
- Codex can be the trigger/workload, but a terminal should survive valid TUI output. Root ownership remains unresolved until we capture stdout/stderr, Rio logs, and ideally a replay or minimized escape stream.
- The next diagnostic path should capture per-launch logs and, if feasible, a TTY/ANSI transcript around high-rate Codex updates so we can distinguish invalid output, Zellij relay behavior, and Rio renderer/window failures.
close_reason: Preserved bounded desktop launch logs with argv/env/PID/status metadata, added scoped yzxterm doctor findings, documented the child-wrapper logging boundary, and validated with focused Rust tests plus runtime_yzxterm build.

title: Investigate recurring Yazelix crash after Home Manager runtime update
description:
Yazelix crashed again after the Home Manager runtime was updated on 2026-06-02. Create a later investigation path that starts from the newly available crash observability and runtime identity surfaces instead of relying on prompt memory.

Known context from the update session:
- Home Manager switched successfully after locking Yazelix to `ac7d6c3aa9609be9219e7221d8a86f98be35d8fa`.
- The active durable profile `~/.nix-profile/bin/yzx` reported runtime source short revision `ac7d6c3` via `yzx --version-full`.
- The runtime identity reported `runtime_variant = "yzxterm"`.
- The pane orchestrator input reported short revision `b2b1cc4`.
- `yzx doctor --json` reported runtime/distribution capability `home_manager_managed`, generated initializers current, and no yzxterm desktop launch logs captured yet.
- The long-lived shell still had an older store-path `yzx` before `~/.nix-profile/bin/yzx` in PATH, but that wrapper redirected to the current Home Manager runtime.

Investigation goals:
- Determine whether this is the same high-rate Codex output crash tracked separately, a desktop launch/logging gap, a yzxterm/Rio renderer exit, stale-shell PATH behavior, or a different runtime/update regression.
- Gather fresh per-launch logs, journal/coredump evidence, and `yzx --version-full` output from the incident window.
- Confirm whether the Zellij session survived when the terminal window disappeared.

Acceptance criteria:
- Collect `yzx --version-full`, `yzx doctor --json`, recent yzxterm launch logs, relevant `journalctl --user`/`coredumpctl` output, and `zellij list-sessions` evidence for a post-update crash.
- Decide whether this incident is covered by `yazelix-reproduce-yzxterm-codex-output-crash-1h717` or needs a new implementation/upstream issue.
- If logs are still absent, identify why the crash-observability path did not capture them and create a focused follow-up.
- Record whether refreshing the shell/PATH after Home Manager switch changes reproduction behavior.
notes:
Additional user observations on 2026-06-02:
- This is likely the same Yazelix Terminal behavior where output/rendering slows down first, then buffered updates appear to render very fast all at once, then the terminal crashes/disappears.
- Fast rendering is not limited to text flushes: the Zellij pane title spinner also spins very quickly during this state.
- One plausible trigger is background-window throttling: rendering may lag while the Yazelix Terminal window is not focused/visible, then accelerate sharply when returning to the window as yzxterm/Rio catches up.

Treat this as stronger evidence for a yzxterm/Rio rendering backpressure, event-loop stall, frame pacing, animation-timing, visibility/focus throttling, or buffer flush failure rather than a generic Home Manager update problem. Compare against the high-rate Codex output reproduction bead and capture logs/timing around any slow-then-burst-render transition, including whether UI animations accelerate alongside text output and whether the burst begins immediately after the terminal window regains focus.
close_reason: Investigated the June 2 recurring yzxterm disappearance with current runtime identity, doctor JSON, launch logs, process/session state, journal searches, coredumpctl, and Rio log freshness. Current evidence points away from a Home Manager update regression and toward the existing high-rate Codex/yzxterm rendering crash path; stale PATH did not change version identity. Created follow-up yazelix-capture-yzxterm-terminal-lifetime-exit-evidence-cew8f because launch logs still do not capture terminal lifetime exit/signal evidence or fresh Rio logs.

title: Investigate Yazelix runtime and Cachix closure size
description:
Yazelix public Cachix storage is near the free-tier limit while the local installed runtime closure is also large. Investigate what the current published/installable outputs include, identify the biggest closure contributors, and propose practical reductions without breaking the integrated runtime contract.

Initial evidence:
- Cachix UI shows `yazelix` cache at about 4.9 GiB of 5 GiB.
- Separate `luccahuguet-yazelix-terminal` cache is about 33.7 MiB, so the terminal package alone is not the main weight.
- Local current `yazelix` closure is about 3.6 GiB.
- Local current `yazelix-runtime` closure is about 3.6 GiB.
- Local current `yazelix-helix` closure is about 327.6 MiB.
- Local current `yazelix-terminal` closure is about 119.5 MiB.
- Local current packaged `zellij` closure is about 101.9 MiB.
- Suspected large contributors include Helix runtime/tree-sitter grammars, Yazi/KGP Yazi closure duplication, preview/media tools such as chafa, and cache workflow output selection.

Questions to answer:
1. Which exact outputs does `.github/workflows/publish_nix_cache.yml` build and push?
2. What are the top closure contributors for the current runtime?
3. Is Cachix storage high because of the current closure alone, multiple revisions, or accidental extra outputs?
4. Are there duplicated heavy dependencies or parallel package variants that can be removed?
5. Which reductions are safe now, which require opt-in feature flags, and which should be deferred?

Acceptance criteria:
- Record measured closure sizes for the current runtime and major components.
- Record whether the cache workflow pushes only intended runtime/package outputs.
- Identify the top actionable reduction candidates with risk and expected benefit.
- Create follow-up beads for concrete reductions if the investigation finds safe work.
- Do not change runtime packaging until the evidence supports a specific low-risk reduction.
notes:
2026-06-02 initial investigation:

Cache/workflow surface:
- `.github/workflows/publish_nix_cache.yml` publishes to the public `yazelix` Cachix on main/tag/workflow_dispatch.
- It builds these x86_64-linux outputs: `yazelix_kgp_yazi`, `yazelix_kgp_zellij`, `yazelix_helix`, `yazelix`, `yazelix_ghostty`, `yazelix_wezterm`, `yazelix_agent_tools`, `yazelix_screen`.
- Therefore the 4.9 GiB Cachix UI number is not just the default app wrapper. It includes multiple curated package outputs and whatever historical store paths remain retained in the cache.
- The separate `luccahuguet-yazelix-terminal` cache is small because it only contains the Rio-derived terminal package, not the full Yazelix runtime stack.

Installed-runtime measurement from current Home Manager runtime:
- Active package path: `/nix/store/lfg8x6cbxycgxzbb918b77jm76mniz0g-yazelix`.
- `nix path-info -SSh` reports `yazelix` closure about 3.6 GiB / 3725.6 MiB.
- Direct `yazelix-runtime` reference closure: about 3725.4 MiB.
- `yazelix-helix`: about 327.6 MiB closure, 41.5 MiB NAR.
- `yazelix-terminal`: about 119.5 MiB closure, 3.6 MiB wrapper NAR plus about 30.9 MiB terminal-unwrapped NAR.
- Packaged Zellij: about 101.9 MiB closure, 47.0 MiB NAR.

Largest individual NAR paths in the current closure:
- `llvm-21.1.8-lib`: about 540.2 MiB and another about 527.8 MiB.
- `mesa-26.1.1`: about 272.3 MiB and another about 263.9 MiB.
- `python3-3.13.13`: about 126.9 MiB and another about 74.0 MiB.
- `mise`: about 104.2 MiB NAR / 497.4 MiB closure.
- `carapace`: about 67.4 MiB NAR / 105.9 MiB closure.
- `nushell`: about 58.0 MiB NAR / 104.0 MiB closure.
- `git`: about 54.7 MiB NAR / 373.8 MiB closure.
- `steel`: about 52.4 MiB NAR / 111.4 MiB closure.
- `zellij`: about 47.0 MiB NAR / 101.9 MiB closure.
- `yazelix-helix`: about 41.5 MiB NAR / 327.6 MiB closure.
- `yazelix-terminal-unwrapped`: about 30.9 MiB NAR.
- `yazi`: about 27.6 MiB NAR, with wrapper closure about 503.2 MiB.

Ownership trace:
- The duplicated huge LLVM/Mesa paths are reached through runtime-owned `nixGLMesa` and `nixVulkanMesa` for the Linux graphics wrapper path, especially relevant to `runtime_variant = "yzxterm"` and other Kitty-graphics bridge terminals.
- `yazelix-terminal` itself is not the heavy item. It is a moderate terminal package; the graphics wrapper closure around it dominates the yzxterm runtime.
- Yazi appears as both wrapper and unwrapped paths; this needs follow-up to distinguish expected wrapper structure from removable duplicate closure.

Existing reduction surfaces already supported:
- `runtime_tool_sources` can host-source leaf tools such as `mise`, `carapace`, `git`, `neovim`, `lazygit`, `bottom`, `tombi`, `fd`, `rg`, `jq`, `starship`, `zoxide`, `fzf`, `helix`, `yazi`, and `steel`.
- `steel`, `macchina`, `p7zip`, `poppler`, and `resvg` support `off`.
- `agent_usage_programs = []` can omit `tokenusage` when Codex/Claude usage widgets are removed or intentionally host-provided.
- Graphics wrappers are explicitly treated as bootstrap/runtime-owned and are not currently hostable/disableable through `runtime_tool_sources`.

Early hypotheses:
1. Biggest potential reduction for Linux yzxterm is an explicit graphics-wrapper mode, probably `bundled | host | off/none`, but this needs a contract because desktop launch and GPU reliability currently rely on runtime-owned NixGL.
2. Medium safe reductions are already available per-user through Home Manager by host-sourcing heavy leaf tools (`mise`, `git`, `carapace`, `neovim`) and disabling Steel authoring/preview helpers/tokenusage when unused.
3. Cache workflow may be pushing some outputs that are mostly duplicate with `yazelix`, but `yazelix_wezterm` and other variants can add real incremental storage. Need Cachix-side or local output-difference measurement before changing CI.
4. Yazi wrapper/unwrapped closure should be audited before assuming duplication is waste. KGP graphics behavior may require the current shape.

Attempted but stopped:
- Tried to measure alternate package shapes with `nix build --expr` for full yzxterm, leaf-trimmed yzxterm, and no-nixgl yzxterm. It produced only evaluation warnings and no compile logs, but stayed silent for about two minutes, so the probe was killed to avoid an accidental heavyweight build during investigation. Follow-up should use a more controlled derivation/eval method or run when we intentionally budget for Nix measurement.

comments.author: lucca
comments.text:
2026-06-02 follow-up measurement summary:
- Added repeatable reporter at `shells/posix/yazelix_runtime_size_report.sh`.
- Installed maintainer `yzx` measured with `--top 1 --direct-top 1`: closure 3,906,525,120 bytes, 752 paths; largest duplicate family remains two LLVM libs totaling about 1.1 GiB.
- Current dirty-tree `git+file://...#packages.x86_64-linux.yazelix` measured through reporter `--build`: closure 4,323,440,624 bytes, 884 paths.
- Lean package-builder profile measured: closure 3,382,427,280 bytes, 509 paths, saving about 941,006,232 bytes versus the measured default shape.
- yzxterm graphics-wrapper evidence: direct runtime-owned NixGL/Vulkan wrapper closures together accounted for 2,160,340,584 bytes; approximate no-wrapper yzxterm closure would be about 1,740,980,504 bytes before reliability validation.
- Cache-output delta measured in workflow order and documented in `docs/package_sizes.md`; workflow now drops explicit zero-increment outputs `yazelix_ghostty`, `yazelix_agent_tools`, and `yazelix_screen`.
- Follow-up beads created: `yazelix-linux-graphics-wrapper-source-mode-6nhcg` and `yazelix-remove-bundled-mise-6r7kp`.
- Validation run: reporter help/path/--build checks, YAML parse, `validate-docs-experience`, `validate-config-surface-contract`, `validate-nix-customization-api`, and `validate-contracts`.

title: Manual-install to Home Manager takeover should not require manual cleanup of ~/.local/bin/yzx
description: A supported manual-install to Home Manager takeover still fails because Home Manager refuses to replace the existing ~/.local/bin/yzx launcher. During validation we had to move that file aside manually before home-manager switch could succeed. Track this as a real takeover bug, not just a docs gap.
design:
Delete-first plan for this takeover bug:
1. Reproduce the exact failure with the real manual-install launcher shape and `home-manager switch -b ...`, so the bead records whether `-b` is actually insufficient or whether the failure was shape-specific.
2. Prefer deleting Home Manager ownership of `~/.local/bin/yzx` entirely instead of teaching Home Manager and the installer to fight over the same stable path.
3. Expose `yzx` through the Home Manager package/profile path instead, and point the Home Manager desktop entry at that Home Manager-owned executable rather than `~/.local/bin/yzx`.
4. Teach `nushell/scripts/utils/doctor.nu` to accept the Home Manager-native profile launcher shape when `runtime/current` is Home Manager-owned.
5. Downgrade the `home_manager/README.md` claim that `home-manager switch -b hm-backup` is the solution. At most it is a takeover aid for the remaining `runtime/current` and generated-config collisions, not the product answer for the launcher path.
6. Only if removing Home Manager ownership of `~/.local/bin/yzx` proves too disruptive, fall back to `yazelix-x0v2.2`: an explicit migration helper layered on the uninstall ownership engine.

This gives a cleaner result than a helper-first plan: clean-room Home Manager installs work without launcher collisions, manual-to-Home-Manager takeover no longer fights over the same launcher path, and Yazelix stops depending on undocumented Home Manager overwrite behavior.
acceptance_criteria:
1. The bead records a real reproduction of the manual-install to Home Manager takeover failure with the actual manual launcher shape and `home-manager switch -b ...`.
2. The preferred fix removes Home Manager ownership of `~/.local/bin/yzx` and uses a Home Manager-owned profile/package executable instead.
3. A documented manual-install to Home Manager takeover succeeds without asking the user to manually move `~/.local/bin/yzx`.
4. `nushell/scripts/utils/doctor.nu` accepts the supported Home Manager profile launcher shape when `runtime/current` is Home Manager-owned.
5. `home_manager/README.md` no longer treats `home-manager switch -b hm-backup` as the main solution; it is documented only as a takeover aid for the remaining collisions.
6. If the direct ownership-boundary fix proves too disruptive, the bead records that clearly and hands off to `yazelix-x0v2.2` as the explicit helper path.
notes: Likely implementation files for the preferred path: `home_manager/module.nix`, `home_manager/README.md`, `nushell/scripts/utils/doctor.nu`, and `nushell/scripts/dev/test_yzx_doctor_commands.nu`. Upstream Home Manager collision refusal is expected behavior, so this bead should not assume Home Manager will grow a general overwrite mode for us.
close_reason: Completed: the Home Manager path no longer owns ~/.local/bin/yzx, the supported launcher is the HM profile executable, doctor/docs accept that shape, and the remaining takeover collisions are handled by the explicit yzx home_manager prepare path rather than manual launcher cleanup.

title: Update the Yazelix Collection
description: Refresh docs/yazelix_collection.md so the catalog matches the current Yazelix product surface and integrated tools.
design: Audit the current runtime/package/docs surfaces, then update the collection page as canonical catalog prose. Keep implementation decisions and historical notes out of the collection page.
acceptance_criteria: docs/yazelix_collection.md includes current major integrations and standalone/package surfaces, removes stale or misleading entries, and README links remain valid.
close_reason: Completed. Refreshed docs/yazelix_collection.md against the current flake package outputs, runtime dependency surface, terminal variants, Yazi plugin inventory, standalone screen/cursor packages, user config surfaces, and maintainer tooling. Removed stale catalog entries and verified docs routes plus local collection links.

title: Clean-room first-use Yazelix entries should not require pre-existing .bashrc and Nushell config
description: A real clean-room Home Manager activation can own runtime/current, ~/.local/bin/yzx, and the managed TOML surfaces correctly, but the first noninteractive entry paths still assume host shell config files already exist. In validation, `yzx run which uv` emitted a hard bash-config error until empty ~/.bashrc and ~/.config/nushell/config.nu files were seeded manually. This is not a Home Manager ownership problem; it is a first-use startup/materialization bug that still leaks host-dotfile assumptions into the user path.
close_reason: Completed

title: Evaluate initializers and environment for Rust materialization
description: Evaluate whether setup/initializers.nu and setup/environment.nu have a clear deterministic subcore that can be moved to Rust (e.g. a structured shell-hook render plan), or whether their shell-bound integration nature means they should stay Nushell-owned. Only attempt a Rust port if it deletes a real Nushell owner end-to-end without moving shell execution into Rust.
notes:
2026-04-20 audit outcome:
- Reviewed `setup/initializers.nu` and `setup/environment.nu` after the earlier `runtime-env.compute` cut and the landed terminal materialization work
- No further Rust port is recommended in v15.x for this family
- `runtime-env.compute` already captured the only obvious typed decision layer worth moving
- `initializers.nu` remains external-tool initializer generation plus shell-specific text ownership
- `environment.nu` remains shellhook and startup orchestration
- Updated the delete-first roadmap docs so this family is treated as a likely Nushell survivor unless a future full-owner cut can delete the surviving shellhook owner end-to-end
close_reason: Completed: runtime_env.compute already captured the only clear deterministic subcore; initializers.nu and environment.nu stay Nushell-owned in v15.x, and the roadmap now classifies them as likely Nushell survivors rather than an active Rust lane.

title: Evaluate tokenusage-lite packaging or fork for Codex usage
description: Decide how Yazelix should package the fast Codex usage provider after tokenusage proved fast but the shipped tu binary is broad. Evaluate upstream feature split, a local tokenusage-lite fork/wrapper, a Yazelix-owned narrow parser, and whether llm-agents.nix should own the package. Acceptance: record package closure/build impact, maintenance cost, licensing/upstream plan, and a recommended opt-in package surface for Codex usage widgets.
notes: Decision 2026-04-30: make tokenusage the supported provider for Claude/Codex usage widgets now, without a ccusage-codex fallback. Packaged tokenusage 1.5.2 as an opt-in runtime package and exported its tu binary through the Yazelix runtime tree. Kept ccusage-opencode as the OpenCode provider because tokenusage does not cover OpenCode. The package is the full upstream tu binary for now; a future strip/fork can be evaluated only if closure/build weight becomes unacceptable, but runtime semantics are no longer fallback based.
close_reason: Completed: tokenusage is packaged as the supported Claude/Codex provider; OpenCode remains on ccusage-opencode.

title: Fix stale AGENTS guidance
description: Refresh stale AGENTS.md references after pane-orchestrator publication and recent instruction pruning. Keep the user's deleted sections deleted while correcting current inaccurate lines.
close_reason: Fixed AGENTS.md stale references while preserving user-deleted sections

title: Collapse duplicate active config-surface owners between Nu and Rust
description: Collapse the duplicated active config-surface owner seam between nushell/scripts/utils/config_surfaces.nu and rust_core/yazelix_core/src/active_config_surface.rs without losing canonical user_configs ownership, default bootstrap, legacy-root fail-fast, Taplo sync, or Home Manager parity.
acceptance_criteria: The surviving owner is explicit, config_surfaces.nu is deleted or reduced to a non-owning caller-local shell seam, and validate_config_surface_contract.nu plus the cited config-surface regressions still pass.
notes: Created from docs/specs/config_runtime_control_plane_canonicalization_audit.md. Do not solve this by adding another wrapper or by taking ownership of user-managed external config files.
close_reason: all steps complete

title: Define the deletion budget for the active config-surface owner cut
description: Read every live Nu and Rust caller of active config-surface resolution, name the retained behavior and verification, and decide the narrowest surviving shell seam before any code deletion starts.
acceptance_criteria: The bead records the retained behavior, names the surviving owner, identifies all callers that still need shell-local behavior, and sets an explicit stop condition if config_surfaces.nu cannot yet collapse fully.
notes:
Key invariants: user_configs ownership, default bootstrap, legacy-root fail-fast, Taplo sync, and Home Manager parity.
Completed 2026-04-22: produced docs/specs/active_config_surface_owner_cut_budget.md with live caller inventory, surviving-owner decision, deletion budget for config_surfaces.nu, verification gates, and stop conditions for the active config-surface owner cut. Validated with nu nushell/scripts/dev/validate_specs.nu.
close_reason: Completed

title: Rust-own active config-surface resolution for Nu callers and collapse config_surfaces.nu
description: Switch the remaining Nu callers onto the Rust active-config-surface owner, collapse nushell/scripts/utils/config_surfaces.nu, and keep any surviving Nu behavior strictly caller-local shell orchestration instead of config ownership.
acceptance_criteria: Nu no longer duplicates active-config-surface rules, config_surfaces.nu is deleted or demoted to a non-owning shell seam, and validate_config_surface_contract.nu plus the defended command regressions still pass.
notes:
Do not drift Home Manager defaults from yazelix_default.toml and do not reintroduce root-level legacy migration guesses.
Completed 2026-04-22: added the Rust-owned config-surface.resolve helper route, switched Nu callers onto the Rust active-surface owner, split surviving non-owning helpers into config_paths.nu and config_files.nu, and deleted nushell/scripts/utils/config_surfaces.nu. Verified with nu nushell/scripts/dev/validate_syntax.nu, nu nushell/scripts/dev/validate_config_surface_contract.nu, targeted yzx config / yzx edit regressions from test_yzx_core_commands.nu, targeted Taplo/bootstrap and managed-state regressions from test_yzx_generated_configs.nu, and nix develop -c cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core active_config_surface.
close_reason: Completed

title: Cache Rust validation build artifacts in CI
description: The always-on CI lane rebuilds yzx_repo_validator and yzx_core from scratch on hosted runners. Add an actions/cache entry for Cargo registry/git data and rust_core/target keyed by Rust dependency inputs so repeat pushes avoid the full cold Rust build cost.
acceptance_criteria: CI restore/cache step covers ~/.cargo/registry, ~/.cargo/git, and rust_core/target; cache key changes when rust_core/Cargo.lock or flake.lock changes; workflow syntax is reviewed; bead is closed with the exact validation performed.
close_reason: Completed: added actions/cache for Cargo registry/git data and rust_core/target, keyed by rust_core/Cargo.lock plus flake.lock with OS restore fallback. Verified .github/workflows/ci.yml parses with python3/PyYAML; the cached build command itself was already validated in yazelix-j9hk.

title: Make desktop icon reinstall replace read-only copied icons
close_reason: Fixed desktop icon reinstall by removing existing copied icons before copying Nix-store icons; added regression coverage for read-only existing icons.

title: Trim stale lockfile ownership from yzx status --versions and make version_info work on v15 installs
description: The packaged v15 runtime no longer ships devenv.lock, but version_info.nu still treats a runtime-local lockfile as part of the normal status surface and currently crashes when it is absent. Delete that stale ownership from the normal user path. Make yzx status --versions produce a clean runtime-version table on installed v15 runtimes without requiring a runtime lockfile.
close_reason: Completed: yzx status --versions no longer depends on a runtime-local devenv.lock and version_info now reports runtime versions cleanly on v15 installs.

title: Evaluate a minimal schema-versioned Rust config migration core only if current diagnostics remain insufficient
description:
Past config churn caused real pain, but the current first-principles decision is not to reintroduce a broad automatic migration engine as a live v15 feature. The first cheaper fix is to make desktop-entry launch failures visible and keep the existing fail-fast config diagnostics and manual repair path honest. If that still proves insufficient after the launch UX work lands, then evaluate a much smaller migration approach.

The candidate scope is a Rust-first internal config core with an explicit schema_version in yazelix.toml and only deterministic structural migrations. This is an evaluation bead, not approval to rebuild the old Nushell migration subsystem.
design:
Keep the scope extremely small. Do not save per-user copies of the initial schema, do not log hashes for every user edit, and do not promise that every historical config shape will auto-repair forever. If revisited, prefer an internal monorepo Rust crate aligned with the surviving config core rather than a separate repo or product. Use existing seams such as the canonical config contract and config-state hashing, and consider small supporting crates like toml_edit only if they clearly reduce code.

Non-goals:
- reviving the broad v13/v14 migration rule engine
- a Nushell-first implementation that will immediately be rewritten
- automatic handling of ambiguous or lossy migrations
- inventing a second config product around migrations
acceptance_criteria:
1. There is a written recommendation to either drop this idea or proceed with a tiny Rust-first prototype.
2. Any approved prototype proves an explicit schema_version boundary and one deterministic migration on real Yazelix config data.
3. Ambiguous or lossy changes remain manual-only with clear diagnostics.
4. If the design cannot stay small and robust, the bead is closed rather than expanded into a large subsystem.
notes:
2026-04-19 Rust dependency gate: before any minimal migration-core prototype, record the crate-vs-in-house plan. Do not add toml_edit or migration-framework-like dependencies unless preserving user-authored TOML formatting becomes an explicit accepted requirement.
2026-04-19 guardrail:
- If this bead is ever revived, it must delete config-diagnostics duplication rather than layering a second migration engine beside the current Rust config core and Nushell error path.
- Any prototype must name the existing seams it replaces in config_parser.nu, config_state.nu, or related diagnostics, and the earliest point where transition-only migration parity code would be removed.
- If the prototype cannot stay smaller than the duplication it introduces, close the bead instead.
2026-04-19 roadmap update: keep this bead blocked on yazelix-vi8q so config.normalize becomes the canonical diagnostics source before any schema-versioned migration prototype is reconsidered.
close_reason: Current Rust-backed config diagnostics are sufficient for the live v15 contract: startup and doctor share one unsupported-config path, tests defend no-migration guidance, and there is no current backlog evidence that a schema-versioned migration core is justified. Closing instead of reviving migration machinery.

title: Define the runtime dependency contract and launch preflight checker scope
description:
Specify the runtime dependency contract that normal user-facing entrypoints actually rely on, and define the scope of a preflight checker that should validate that contract before launch. Cover required versus optional dependencies, config-conditioned requirements, runtime assets versus host tools, and the boundary between lightweight launch-preflight checks and heavier doctor or install-smoke flows.

The contract should explain at least:
- always-required launch/startup prerequisites
- config-conditioned requirements such as terminal availability
- the difference between runtime-owned assets and host/external tools
- what launch should check quickly
- what doctor may check more deeply
- what only install/package validation should defend
design:
Treat this as a contract-first task, but not as the generic backend contract. Start from the current launch, startup, doctor, and install-smoke behavior and write down what the runtime layer is actually promising when a user runs Yazelix.

Ground it in the current code seams:
- `nushell/scripts/core/start_yazelix.nu`
- `nushell/scripts/core/start_yazelix_inner.nu`
- `nushell/scripts/core/launch_yazelix.nu`
- `nushell/scripts/utils/terminal_launcher.nu`
- `nushell/scripts/utils/doctor.nu`
- `nushell/scripts/utils/entrypoint_config_migrations.nu`
- `nushell/scripts/dev/validate_installed_runtime_contract.nu`
- `nushell/scripts/dev/validate_flake_install.nu`

Important boundary: config migration preflight remains adjacent but separate. Launch should still run it, but stale-config/migration semantics are not the same thing as runtime dependency checking.

This bead should complement the backend contract from `yazelix-6lkw`, not reopen it. The output should also stay honest for current full Yazelix while being reusable for a future Core discussion.
acceptance_criteria:
There is an explicit runtime dependency contract for user-facing entrypoints, with clear required, optional, and config-conditioned classifications.

The contract clearly separates:
- lightweight launch-preflight checks
- richer `yzx doctor` diagnostics
- heavier install/package validation

It is concrete enough to explain at least these current cases:
- missing or invalid working directory as an immediate launch blocker
- missing runtime script or generated layout as an immediate startup/runtime blocker
- unavailable configured terminal as a config-conditioned launch blocker for new-window launches
- stale shell hooks, desktop entries, install artifacts, version drift, and deeper runtime health as doctor-first or install-validation concerns rather than mandatory launch-preflight checks

A later bead should be able to implement one shared runtime checker from this contract without rediscovering the scope ad hoc.
close_reason: Runtime dependency/preflight contract written, issue #532 synced, launch preflight tightened for runtime scripts/layouts, and doctor-side desktop-entry freshness diagnostic defended.

title: Continue gradual Rust insertion after the first yzx_core slices
description: The first helper-backed Rust slices are done, but Yazelix still has a few remaining Nushell decision seams that are good Rust candidates. This follow-up epic tracks only the next high-value v15.x moves: deleting duplicate config diagnostics, consolidating managed layout and materialization metadata, and extending shared typed classification only where it removes real Nushell logic.
design: Keep the current ownership boundary. Nushell remains the public yzx UX owner, renderer owner, and host-integration surface. The Rust pane orchestrator remains the owner of live workspace truth. New Rust work should stay behind the private yzx_core bridge and target deterministic control-plane logic that is reused across startup, generated-state, doctor, or status flows.
acceptance_criteria: There is a concrete follow-up sequence after the first Rust slices, with child beads for the remaining high-value control-plane seams and explicit guardrails against shell-heavy or text-heavy rewrites.
notes: Stop lines: do not reopen a broad launch/bootstrap rewrite, renderer-port effort, or public CLI rewrite under this epic. The goal is deletion of surviving Nu decision duplication, not additive Rust glue. This epic follows the completed kt5 planning sequence and the completed kt5.2/kt5.3/kt5.4/kt5.5 first slices.
close_reason: all steps complete

title: Detect stale host-shell yzx shadowing and make profile wrappers future-proof
close_reason: Completed

title: Evaluate Home Manager toggles for optional Yazelix components
description: Review which Yazelix modules, child-repo-backed packages, and runtime helper surfaces can be turned on or off through Home Manager without weakening the normal integrated Yazelix experience. The goal is to let regular Yazelix users keep the default fully integrated package, while advanced Home Manager users can disable optional child-repo modules or heavy subsystems when they do not need them and save closure size or runtime storage where possible.
design: Start from the current integrated product contract: defaults should keep the normal Yazelix experience complete. Evaluate optionality component by component instead of adding one broad toggle. Candidate surfaces include yazelix-screen, yazelix-cursors / Ghostty cursor shaders, yazelix-zellij-popup / yzpp, optional agent usage tools, terminal-specific runtime variants, bar/status helpers, welcome/screen styles, and other child-repo-backed packages. For each candidate, record whether it is required for default behavior, whether disabling it should remove a package, skip generated config, hide config options, change defaults, or only avoid exposing a flake app. Prefer fail-fast validation over silently generating config that references disabled assets. Avoid toggles whose only effect is conceptual complexity without meaningful storage/runtime savings.
acceptance_criteria: A decision matrix lists every plausible Home Manager/runtime component toggle with: default state, user-facing purpose, package/closure/storage impact, generated-config impact, runtime behavior when disabled, validation/error behavior, and whether the toggle should be implemented, rejected, or deferred. The accepted toggle set preserves the default fully integrated Yazelix install, documents how child-repo-backed modules are integrated by default, and creates implementation follow-up beads for any accepted Home Manager options.
notes: Decision recorded in docs/contracts/nix_customization_surfaces.md. Keep implemented surfaces: runtime_tool_sources host mode, agent_usage_programs opt-in, and runtime_variant. Accept follow-ups for real package-saving toggles only: cursor component disabling (yazelix-49s1), screen/welcome component disabling (yazelix-arpm), and optional helper-tool off mode (yazelix-koim). Reject toggles for forwarded standalone packages such as yazelix_bar and yzpp because they are not installed by default. Defer barless integrated status-bar disabling until Zellij layout ownership/materialization cleanup closes (yazelix-jhu5).
close_reason: Completed: optional component toggle matrix documented and implementation follow-up beads created

title: Fix Beads contract CI Rust toolchain
close_reason: Fixed the Beads contract workflow to install Rust 1.96 and made the Nu validator honor CARGO; repaired GitHub/Beads drift and verified the contract validator passes locally.

title: Use minimal CI shell and build Rust helpers together
description: Main CI pays a large fixed cost entering the full maintainer shell and then builds yzx_repo_validator and yzx_core in separate steps. Add a narrow CI dev shell and build both Rust helpers in one Cargo invocation so source-contract CI avoids maintainer/runtime shell overhead.
acceptance_criteria: CI workflow uses a minimal flake shell for source validators; yzx_repo_validator and yzx_core build in one step; YAZELIX_YZX_CORE_BIN is exported from that step; local validation runs the new build command and at least validate-nushell-syntax through the new shell.
close_reason: Completed: added a lightweight .#ci dev shell, built yzx_repo_validator and yzx_core in one CI step, exported YAZELIX_YZX_CORE_BIN there, and validated the shell, combined build, plus validate-nushell-syntax locally through .#ci. A broader local source-lane sweep reached validate-flake-interface and was stopped after the known dirty-tree nix eval slowdown exceeded eight minutes.

title: Show what changed since the installed runtime
description: Make yzx whats_new or a sibling command compare the active installed runtime/version/snapshot with release notes and show relevant changes since that runtime.
design: Prioritize keybindings, config migrations, install-owner changes, manual actions, and runtime/fork changes. Handle unknown, dirty, and dev runtimes explicitly rather than pretending they map cleanly to a release.
acceptance_criteria: The command reports meaningful changes for current installed version versus latest known release notes, handles unknown/dev runtimes clearly, and has tests for release-note selection without network access.
close_reason: Implemented yzx whats_new as an offline installed-runtime comparison: it now renders newer structured release notes and populated unreleased entries from the active runtime, falls back to the current-version summary when there are no newer notes, reports dirty/dev/unknown snapshots explicitly, and rejects non-release version strings for range selection. Verified with cargo fmt --check, cargo check -p yazelix_core, focused upgrade_summary and yzx_control whats_new tests, yzx_core_command_metadata integration tests, validate-contracts, validate-docs-experience, and git diff --check.

title: Thin config UI toward private ratconfig boundary
description: config_ui.rs remains a large product UI owner. Move generic ratatui config editing model/editor/render pieces behind the private yazelix_ratconfig boundary while keeping Yazelix settings, apply status, Home Manager state, JSONC patching, and runtime apply local.
design: Private boundary only. Do not create a public child repo. Use the existing yazelix_ratconfig module as the generic owner, but keep product policy and persistence adapters in config_ui.rs/apply_adapter. Delete speculative tests or wrappers that only defend old file structure.
acceptance_criteria: config_ui.rs is smaller and delegates reusable generic model/editor/render behavior to yazelix_ratconfig; Yazelix-specific settings/apply/Home Manager/JSONC behavior remains local; public config UI behavior is unchanged; focused config UI tests pass; LOC delta and rejected candidates are recorded.
notes:
Implemented private ratconfig render boundary pass.

Moved generic config UI row rendering, default field details, single-choice and multi-choice picker detail rendering, sidecar details, diagnostic details, and native status details into yazelix_ratconfig/render.rs. Kept Yazelix-specific Zellij action/keybinding detail rendering in config_ui.rs because it depends on the Yazelix action registry. Kept settings loading, JSONC patching, cursor config writes, Home Manager/read-only policy, native config classification, and runtime apply behavior local to config_ui.rs/apply_adapter.

Delete-first notes: collapsed duplicated set/unset post-write apply handling into finish_field_write. Did not move persistence or apply behavior into ratconfig; that would make the private boundary less generic and harder to extract later.

LOC/accounting: config_ui.rs shrank from 3,130 to 2,837 raw lines. yazelix_ratconfig/render.rs grew from 436 to 727 raw lines. The core config/materialization family moved from 20,730 to 20,728 raw lines. Full tracked Rust budget moved from 72,736 to 72,734 raw lines; tokei Rust code moved from 66,559 to 66,556 LOC.

Verification: cargo fmt --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml --all -- --check; cargo check --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core --all-targets; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core config_ui; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core --test yzx_core_config_ui; cargo run --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_maintainer --bin yzx_repo_validator -- validate-rust-ownership-budget; cargo run --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_maintainer --bin yzx_repo_validator -- validate-docs-experience; git diff --check.
close_reason: Completed private ratconfig render boundary pass; focused config UI tests and budget validators pass.

title: Spec refresh, runtime update, and restart semantics across open Yazelix windows
description: Specify how shared runtime/materialized-state updates interact with already-open Yazelix windows.
design:
Yazelix now has clearer runtime/materialized-state ownership, but the user-visible meaning of `yzx refresh`, `yzx update runtime`, and `yzx restart` across multiple open windows still needs to be codified.

This task should define the durable-state versus live-session contract:
- `yzx refresh` rebuilds shared materialized state and launch-profile state; it should not silently hot-swap unrelated open windows
- `yzx update runtime` updates the installed runtime on disk; it should affect future launches, not mutate already-open windows in place
- `yzx restart` should be defined as an explicit live-session transition rather than a vague refresh alias

The spec should cover both the normal non-persistent case and the persistent-session exception, where restart scope may be session-wide because multiple windows are clients of one logical session.
acceptance_criteria:
1. The docs state that `yzx refresh` updates shared materialized state but does not hot-replace unrelated already-open windows.
2. The docs state that `yzx update runtime` updates the installed runtime for future launches and does not silently upgrade already-open live sessions in place.
3. The docs state that `yzx restart` is the explicit command that moves a live session onto newer durable state.
4. The docs state how the `yzx restart` contract differs between default non-persistent windows and persistent-session mode.
5. The resulting contract is specific enough to guide future fixes in launch, desktop launch, and session cleanup without re-deriving the UX each time.
notes:
This task is about cross-window semantics, not the internal launch-state resolver mechanics already covered by the `le0s` family.

Spec delivered:
- `docs/specs/open_window_update_transition_contract.md`

Current defense:

title: Remove stale TODO noise from shipped Yazi flavor snapshots
description: Several shipped Yazi flavor snapshot files still contain low-value '# TODO: remove' markers that do not describe a real maintenance action anymore. Clean those comments up so the shipped asset surface reads like an intentional snapshot rather than a half-finished scratch area.
design: This is a tiny cleanup bead, not a style rewrite. Delete obviously stale TODO markers or replace them with a precise upstream-sync note only if the comment still carries real meaning. Do not change flavor behavior just to satisfy the cleanup.
acceptance_criteria: The stale TODO markers in shipped Yazi flavor snapshots are removed or replaced with precise durable comments, and the asset content is otherwise unchanged.
notes: Removed stale '# TODO: remove' marker comments from six shipped Yazi flavor snapshots while leaving all TOML rules and values unchanged. Verification: rg found no remaining TODO markers under configs/yazi/flavors; taplo check --no-auto-config --no-schema passed on the six changed flavor.toml files; git diff --check passed. Full taplo check with repo schema still fails on existing unrelated Yazi schema drift in ashen.yazi.
close_reason: Completed: stale TODO marker comments removed from shipped Yazi flavor snapshots without changing asset behavior.

title: Replace text-based Zellij config merging with a typed render pipeline
description: Replace the current block-splitting and string-surgery approach in zellij_config_merger.nu with a typed intermediate model and deterministic renderer for Yazelix-owned Zellij output. The goal is to make plugin insertion, keybind merging, and managed overrides easier to reason about and less fragile.
design: Do not assume a full typed KDL AST or a broad new render framework is required. First isolate the real owners: base-source resolution, generation-state/reuse, semantic user/native block extraction, Yazelix-owned settings, and the final deterministic assembly path. Only introduce a richer intermediate model for the remaining pain points that survive after those cuts. The target is explicit ownership and deterministic Yazelix-owned output, not abstraction for its own sake.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Zellij merging is driven by explicit ownership seams rather than one mixed text-surgery file. 2. Base-source selection, generation reuse, semantic block extraction, and Yazelix-owned rendering each live behind narrow owners. 3. Plugin/load_plugins handling and top-level Yazelix overrides are deterministic and defended by generated-config regressions. 4. The remaining merger/orchestrator path is materially smaller and easier to reason about than the original monolith.
notes:
2026-04-08 streamlining alignment:
- `setup/zellij_config_merger.nu` is ~726 lines and remains one of the clearest mixed-risk setup hotspots because it performs broad block splitting and KDL string surgery.
- By contrast, `setup/yazi_config_merger.nu` currently looks more cohesive and should not be used as justification for a generic “split all config generators” effort.
- This bead remains the higher-value generator refactor in the Nushell streamlining lane.

2026-04-09 step-1 narrowing:
- The actual surviving contract is narrower than the old helper story: base-source priority, generated layout output, dynamic/enforced overrides, and explicit semantic handling for `load_plugins`, `plugins`, and `keybinds`.
- The first delete-first cut landed: the generic top-level block split chain is gone, replaced by one explicit extractor for those three semantic block families only.
- Remaining work should keep questioning whether more of the broad text-surgery path can disappear before introducing a typed render model.

2026-04-09 delete-first progress:
- `zellij_config_merger.nu` no longer owns Yazelix scalar-setting resolution or rendering; that seam moved into `setup/zellij_owned_settings.nu`.
- `zellij_config_merger.nu` no longer owns semantic-block extraction or keybind/plugin block assembly; that seam moved into `setup/zellij_semantic_blocks.nu`.
- `zellij_config_merger.nu` also no longer owns generation fingerprinting, reuse validation, or tracked/runtime plugin artifact hashing; that seam moved into `setup/zellij_generation_state.nu`.
- The merger is now down to ~284 lines and is acting much more like an orchestration layer over narrower owners instead of a single text-surgery grab bag.
close_reason: Completed: Zellij merging now runs through narrow owners for base-source selection, generation state, semantic block extraction, and Yazelix-owned rendering; the merger/orchestrator path is materially smaller and defended by generated-config regressions.

title: Evaluate barless Zellij status layout component toggle
description: Evaluate whether advanced users should be able to disable the integrated zjstatus bar to save the runtime zjstatus.wasm asset and run a barless or native-Zellij status layout. This is deferred because the status bar is part of the current layout contract and layout ownership is still unsettled.
design: Do not implement until Zellij layout ownership and materialization shrinkage are resolved. The decision must compare: keep integrated zjstatus required, provide a barless layout family, provide a native Zellij status fallback, or only allow widget hiding via zellij.widget_tray. A real toggle must remove zjstatus.wasm and generated command widgets from the runtime shape; hiding widgets alone is not enough.
acceptance_criteria: Decision records whether a status_bar component toggle is accepted, rejected, or deferred again. It must quantify package/storage impact, define generated layout behavior, explain pane-orchestrator/status-cache implications, preserve default integrated Yazelix behavior, and create implementation beads only if a real package-saving barless/native path is accepted.
notes:
Roadmap correction 2026-05-08: maintainer is not prioritizing barless/status-bar component toggles in the LOC/extraction lane. Keep as low-priority storage/config decision; do not let it dominate the near-term refactor roadmap unless status-bar ownership directly blocks a proven deletion in zellij_materialization.rs.
2026-05-08 sp4.7 audit: still deferred. Do not treat widget-tray hiding as a package/storage toggle; revisit only after a barless/native layout can remove zjstatus.wasm and generated status-command wiring while keeping default Yazelix unchanged.

title: Make Mandelbrot screen use Seahorse Valley loop
description: The current Mandelbrot screen zooms along the real axis into a boring minibrot path. Rework it into a visually compelling Seahorse Valley zoom between the main cardioid/body and period-2 head, based on documented Seahorse Valley coordinates and correct Mandelbrot escape-time rendering. The animation must loop with the final frame exactly equal to the first frame, not merely frame_count wrapping after the last frame.
notes: 2026-04-26 implementation: replaced the real-axis period-3 minibrot route with a Seahorse Valley route. Manual testing showed this was not a real visual loop: it reached a deep frame with no structural connection to the restart, and byte equality was achieved by forcing the final frame back to frame 0. Superseded by yazelix-4b0c, which replaces the reset hack with an approximate self-similar miniature-copy seam and a penultimate-frame continuity test. Historical verification for this bead before supersession: cargo test -p yazelix_screen, focused mandelbrot tests, Rust traceability validator, Rust ownership budget validator, git diff --check.
close_reason: Implemented Seahorse Valley Mandelbrot loop with exact final-frame repeat

title: Make remaining Zellij native key policy data-driven
description: Move the remaining curated native Zellij key policy out of handwritten yazelix_overrides.kdl and into a user-facing zellij.native_keybindings map in settings.jsonc. The config should keep each bind/unbind pair consecutive and commented in the default TOML so users can remap ergonomic conflict policy without editing full KDL.
design: Add a native Zellij policy registry distinct from zellij.keybindings. Keep zellij.keybindings reserved for Yazelix-owned plugin/orchestrator actions. Generate native keybind KDL from zellij.native_keybindings defaults, including paired *_unbind entries where the policy remaps away from a conflicting upstream/default key. Leave arbitrary native Zellij ownership in ~/.config/yazelix/zellij.kdl.
acceptance_criteria: settings.jsonc/default TOML expose zellij.native_keybindings with bind/unbind pairs kept together and commented. Generated Zellij config preserves current remaining native policy by default. Users can set a native policy entry to [] to disable that bind/unbind. yazelix_overrides.kdl no longer hardcodes the remaining native key policy. Focused tests cover default generation and a remap/disable path.
notes:
Starting implementation. Crate decision: no new crates; use existing config contract string_list_map parsing and in-house static registry for native Zellij policy because the domain is small fixed KDL generation.
Verification: cargo fmt --check --all; cargo check -p yazelix_core; cargo check -p yazelix_maintainer; cargo test -p yazelix_core native_zellij; cargo test -p yazelix_core normalizes_zellij_native_keybinding_map; cargo test -p yazelix_core zellij_native_keybinding_details_use_policy_registry_metadata; cargo test -p yazelix_core managed_zellij_remap_can_remove_ctrl_n_resize_leader; cargo test -p yazelix_core normalizes_default_config_with_parser_keys_and_transforms; yzx_repo_validator validate-config-surface-contract; yzx_repo_validator validate-contracts; yzx_repo_validator validate-rust-test-traceability; yzx_repo_validator validate-docs-experience; git diff --check. LOC scorecard HEAD -> candidate: runtime +673, maintainer +12, docs +30, generated +59, packaging +10, tokei code +781. Opened yazelix-0m4d to evaluate/pay down the Rust LOC cost.
close_reason: Completed: zellij.native_keybindings now owns the remaining curated native Zellij key policy; default TOML keeps bind/unbind pairs together with comments; generated Zellij KDL preserves defaults, supports remap and [] disable paths, and yazelix_overrides.kdl no longer hardcodes the policy. Verified with focused Rust checks/tests, config-surface/docs/contract validators, rust test traceability, and git diff --check. LOC scorecard recorded in notes; follow-up yazelix-0m4d owns paydown evaluation.

title: Backfill upgrade notes for historical point releases
description: Retroactively add structured upgrade-note entries and concise changelog coverage for the historical point releases that shipped after Yazelix started doing point releases, so historical exact-version whats_new lookups can work from real note data instead of commit archaeology.
design: Build this on top of the existing upgrade-notes contract instead of inventing a second historical notes format. Prefer exact version entries in docs/upgrade_notes.toml for every supported historical point release, with matching concise sections in CHANGELOG.md. Keep the notes honest: if a release had no migration impact, say so; if migration or manual action cannot be reconstructed confidently, record the uncertainty conservatively rather than guessing. Define the practical historical floor for supported whats_new lookups so the CLI can fail clearly outside that window instead of pretending the archive is complete.
acceptance_criteria: docs/upgrade_notes.toml and CHANGELOG.md contain exact, validator-clean entries for the supported historical point releases in scope, with real dates, short headlines, and accurate upgrade-impact metadata. The historical entries are sufficient for a future exact-version yzx whats_new flow to render notes without scraping git history at runtime. Add focused verification for note integrity and at least one fixture-based or end-to-end check that historical exact-version lookup data can be loaded cleanly once version selection lands.
notes:
Planning refinements:
- Scope this to the era after Yazelix started shipping point releases; do not invent unsupported older history.
- Prefer reconstructing from real tags, commit ranges, release docs, and existing history pages.
- Keep the result narrow and high-signal: concise upgrade-facing notes, not full release essays.
- This bead should pair naturally with a future yzx whats_new <version> enhancement but should still be useful on its own as historical upgrade-note backfill.
close_reason: Completed: backfilled exact upgrade-note entries for the supported v12/v13 point-release floor, repaired the v13.7 structured-note baseline, generalized the upgrade-summary loader to accept explicit versions internally, and added default-suite plus detailed e2e verification for historical exact-version note loading.

title: Gate boids spatial hashing behind measured flock size need
description: Avoid adding spatial indexing to boids until the current O(n^2) implementation has a measured user-visible performance problem at the flock sizes Yazelix actually renders.
design: Keep the current simple Vec<Boid> model by default. If flock size or frame budget grows, add a tiny in-house uniform-grid spatial hash scoped to yazelix_screen. Do not add a crate for this unless the in-house implementation becomes nontrivial or risky.
acceptance_criteria: There is a benchmark or frame-budget check showing whether current O(n^2) boids is sufficient; if insufficient, an in-house spatial hash is added with tests for neighbor lookup correctness; if sufficient, the bead is closed with measured evidence and no code added.

title: Repo cleanup pass for legacy and unused artifacts
description: Do a deliberate cleanup pass across the repository to remove legacy, dead, or clearly unused files, commands, docs references, and compatibility paths that no longer serve the current Yazelix runtime model. Keep the pass conservative: prefer deleting things that are demonstrably stale over broad aesthetic churn.
design: Treat this as a conservative pruning pass, not an aesthetic refactor. Target legacy compatibility paths, stale docs references, dead commands, unused helper files, superseded repo-era assumptions, and external-tool artifacts that are no longer part of the intended Yazelix workflow. Every deletion should be justified by one of: no remaining callers, superseded by a newer path, no longer part of the supported public surface, or contradicted by the current architecture/runtime model. Prefer removal over replacement when behavior is no longer needed, and avoid broad style churn or speculative rewrites just to make the repo look cleaner.
acceptance_criteria: The repo leaves the pass with less unjustified surface area: at least one real stale, duplicated, superseded, or unused artifact is removed from the categories touched, any removed public surface is scrubbed from docs/help text, and the default test suite still passes. Leave behind a short inventory of what was removed versus intentionally kept. Net repo LOC should decrease unless a small amount of new code is explicitly justified as necessary to enable a larger cleanup or add a validator that supports future pruning.
notes:
Partial progress already landed in 5253b00, 591edee, 7aef715, and 4857fcb, but the cleanup lane is not exhausted. Keep this bead open as an ongoing high-priority repo-hardening pass for deleting legacy, dead, redundant, or stale artifacts that are still discoverable.

- Deleted the unused `build_current_upgrade_summary_report` export from `utils/upgrade_summary.nu`; the supported surface remains `build_upgrade_summary_report`, `maybe_show_first_run_upgrade_summary`, and `show_current_upgrade_summary`.

Re-home them under packaging/ and update imports, validators, and packaging docs accordingly. Prefer a narrow move with no behavior change.

Existing validators or packaging references that mention the old paths are updated. 4. The move reduces root-level clutter without changing the public install/package entrypoints. 5. Verification includes at least one package/validator check, not just path edits.

title: Evaluate flatter Yazelix config surfaces
description: The current primary config path ~/.config/yazelix/user_configs/yazelix.toml and adjacent managed override paths under user_configs/ are accurate to the old managed-config ownership model, but they read overnested for user-facing Yazelix configuration. Evaluate whether Yazelix-owned user-editable config should move to a flatter ~/.config/yazelix/ surface while keeping generated/runtime state out of config.
acceptance_criteria: Decision chooses keep nested user_configs/, move only the main config, or migrate all Yazelix-owned user-editable config surfaces to a flat ~/.config/yazelix/ directory. It evaluates user-facing clarity, current legacy-root conflict behavior, Home Manager manage_config behavior, yzx reset/config/edit/import/doctor surfaces, docs churn, migration safety, duplicate-config handling, auto-migration rules, native app config boundaries, and backward compatibility. If moving is accepted, follow-up implementation beads define the migration plan, warning behavior, exact target paths, and tests.
notes:
Initial product read: the flatter main config path is cleaner, while generated/runtime state should remain under XDG data. The risk is that ~/.config/yazelix/yazelix.toml is currently treated as a legacy root config, so this needs a deliberate contract/migration rather than a casual path edit.
Prime-beads 2026-05-04: this should be the first config-surface decision in the current cluster. It does not require implementing a move; it should explicitly decide whether flatter main config is worth migration cost before explicit/full config materialization or a config UI are designed around the current nested path.
Decision 2026-05-04: choose a staged migration to the flat main config path, not keep the nested path. The target canonical main config is $YAZELIX_CONFIG_DIR/yazelix.toml, normally ~/.config/yazelix/yazelix.toml. Tool-specific managed overrides stay under $YAZELIX_CONFIG_DIR/user_configs/ (Helix, Yazi, Zellij, terminal, shells, cursor sidecars for now).

Rationale: the main Yazelix config is the primary user-facing configuration surface, so ~/.config/yazelix/yazelix.toml is easier to explain, edit, document, and generate from Home Manager than ~/.config/yazelix/user_configs/yazelix.toml. The user_configs directory still makes sense as the boundary for managed per-tool override inputs, but it reads overnested for the main product config. Generated/runtime materialized state remains under XDG data, not config.

Migration constraints: this cannot be a casual path flip because current code treats root yazelix.toml as an unsupported legacy surface and hard-errors on duplicates. The implementation must update active_config_surface, bootstrap, yzx config/edit/reset/onboard/doctor, Home Manager manage_config, docs, and the runtime-root/config contracts together. Old nested-only configs should migrate visibly when safe; Home Manager/profile symlinks should not be moved by startup code; duplicate old/new configs with divergent content must fail with a clear remediation instead of silently guessing.

Follow-up: created yazelix-07ta to implement the root-path migration, warning behavior, Home Manager target update, docs/contracts, and tests.
Refined decision 2026-05-04 after first-principles review: the accepted target is not only a flat main config path; it is one flat Yazelix-owned user-config directory. All user-editable Yazelix-owned config should live directly in $YAZELIX_CONFIG_DIR, normally ~/.config/yazelix/. No user_configs/ layer. No blanket yzx_ prefixes because the parent directory already provides the Yazelix namespace. Use terminal_* and shell_* prefixes only where flat filenames need category context.

Accepted target paths: yazelix.toml, cursors.toml, helix.toml, zellij.kdl, yazi.toml, yazi_keymap.toml, yazi_init.lua, terminal_ghostty.conf, terminal_kitty.conf, terminal_alacritty.toml, terminal_foot.ini, shell_bash.sh, shell_zsh.zsh, shell_fish.fish, shell_nu.nu.

User experience rule: mutable old user_configs/ files should auto-migrate when unambiguous. Home Manager/Nix-store symlinks must not be moved by startup code. Divergent old/new duplicates must fail clearly. Implementation follow-up yazelix-07ta was updated to cover the full flat config migration rather than only the main yazelix.toml path.
close_reason: Completed: chose staged migration to flat main config path and created implementation follow-up yazelix-07ta.

The default Codex status widget renders both 5h and week quota windows when tokenusage data includes them. Users can configure the displayed Codex periods consistently with Claude/OpenCode Go. notes: Implemented the main repo integration for Codex usage periods and default 5h/week rendering. Child repo yazelix-zellij-bar was updated and published at 25cb9b8e45c2d3084068dac71b0d8c58b72b1f67 with --periods support for Codex/Claude/OpenCode usage widgets. Local main integration is committed and installed in the maintainer profile for manual testing. Main push and bead close are waiting for maintainer manual runtime test approval.

title: Make direct update subcommands describe owner scope neutrally
description:
Direct update subcommands currently print wording that can sound like Yazelix has detected the install owner, when it is really just confirming the command path the user invoked. Example: a Home Manager-owned install running `yzx update upstream` prints "Using the default-profile update path for this install" before correctly failing because the active runtime is not in the default Nix profile.

That is technically recoverable, but the first line is misleading for Home Manager users. The message should describe the command scope neutrally and, when ownership is detectable, point directly at the correct owner command before doing unnecessary profile-specific work.
design:
Improve wording without reintroducing a generic auto-updater. Keep the explicit owner commands: `yzx update upstream` for default Nix profile installs and `yzx update home_manager` for Home Manager installs.

For direct subcommands, prefer phrasing like "Requested update path: default Nix profile" rather than "Using ... for this install." If `has_home_manager_managed_install` or runtime/distribution capability detection can cheaply prove the current install is Home Manager-owned, `yzx update upstream` should fail earlier with a specific message: this runtime appears Home Manager-owned; run `yzx update home_manager` from the owning flake and then `home-manager switch`. The symmetric case can stay advisory unless profile ownership is cheaply and reliably detectable.

Do not make `yzx update upstream` silently call the Home Manager updater. The user should choose the owner path explicitly.
acceptance_criteria:
1. A Home Manager-owned install running `yzx update upstream` no longer sees wording implying the default-profile path owns this install.
2. If Home Manager ownership is detectable, the command fails early with an actionable `yzx update home_manager` / `home-manager switch` recovery path.
3. A profile-owned install still gets clear `yzx update upstream` behavior and does not regress exact-profile-entry resolution.
4. Tests cover the misleading Home Manager-owned `yzx update upstream` case and the normal profile-owned update path.
close_reason: Completed: direct update subcommands now describe the requested update path neutrally, and Home Manager-owned installs fail early from `yzx update upstream` with an actionable `yzx update home_manager` plus `home-manager switch` recovery path.

The stricter Mandelbrot renderer and screen tests are already landed.

description: Boids welcome frames were constrained to the tiny catalog body height, so the integrated welcome animation rendered only across the top few terminal rows even though the standalone/full-screen boids renderer fills the pane.
design: Use the existing terminal height already passed into the welcome renderer to size the boids welcome body. Keep the standalone yzx screen boids path unchanged. Rust dependency gate: no new production or dev crates; implement with existing std/Rust helper code only; packaging impact none.
acceptance_criteria: Boids welcome frames use most of the pane height instead of a fixed 5-7 row cap, remain unframed and broad, and focused Rust tests defend the sizing regression.
close_reason: Fixed boids welcome sizing by deriving the boids body height from the terminal pane height instead of the tiny catalog cap. Verification: cargo fmt --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml --all -- --check; cargo check --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core boids_welcome; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core front_door_render::tests; cargo run --quiet --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_maintainer --bin yzx_repo_validator -- validate-rust-test-traceability.

title: Add yzx dev bump for version and tag automation
description: Add a maintainer-facing yzx dev bump command that automates the release bump flow instead of doing version edits, commits, and tags by hand.
design: Implement a maintainer-only yzx dev bump command that computes or accepts the next version, updates nushell/scripts/utils/constants.nu so YAZELIX_VERSION matches the intended tag, creates a dedicated version bump commit, and creates the matching git tag. The command should fail fast on a dirty worktree, an existing conflicting tag, or a version mismatch between the file and the requested tag. The flow should be explicit and deterministic so maintainers do not need to remember the manual release steps.
acceptance_criteria: A maintainer can run yzx dev bump with an explicit version and the command updates nushell/scripts/utils/constants.nu to that version; the command creates a dedicated git commit for the version bump with a predictable message; the command creates the matching git tag after the commit succeeds; the command refuses to run when the git worktree is dirty; the command refuses to run when the requested tag already exists or conflicts with repository state; the command verifies that YAZELIX_VERSION matches the tag it creates; the command has automated tests covering the happy path and failure cases.
close_reason: Completed: added yzx dev bump as a clean-worktree maintainer release helper that rotates Unreleased release metadata into a real version, resets the Unreleased placeholder, updates YAZELIX_VERSION, updates the README version marker, creates a dedicated commit, and creates the matching git tag. Automated tests now cover the happy path plus dirty-worktree and existing-tag failures.

title: Include Nushell version in the pin/update workflow
