- In the active checkout, `nix build .#runtime --no-link` is currently blocked by unrelated in-flight ratconfig extraction changes: `packaging/rust_core_helper.nix` has `yazelix-ratconfig-0.1.0 = lib.fakeHash`, causing a fixed-output hash mismatch.
- To isolate this bead, I created a clean detached worktree at HEAD, applied only the nixpkgs-unstable/doc/example patch, and ran validation there.
- Clean-worktree `nix build .#runtime --no-link`: passed.
- Clean-worktree `nix build .#yazelix --no-link`: passed.
- Clean-worktree `nix build ./home_manager/examples/minimal_flake#homeConfigurations.demo.activationPackage --no-link`: passed.
- Built runtime symlinks show Yazi 26.5.6, Zellij 0.44.3, and Nushell 0.112.2.

`yazelix-q82i` is package-level addressed by the Yazi 26.5.6 bump, which includes the upstream Chafa `--probe off` fix. Keep q82i open until a fresh Ghostty+Zellij+Yazi image-directory manual test confirms no ghost keypresses.
Follow-up verification after the other in-flight worktree changes disappeared from `git status`:
- Active checkout `nix build .#runtime --no-link`: passed.
- Active checkout `nix build .#yazelix --no-link`: passed.

The earlier ratconfig fakeHash caveat was a transient dirty-worktree blocker, not a remaining blocker for this package-set migration.
close_reason: Completed. The flake now intentionally tracks `github:NixOS/nixpkgs/nixpkgs-unstable`, the lockfile moved to root nixpkgs rev 4a29d733e8a7d5b824c3d8c958a946a9867b3eb2, docs/examples record the policy, package version deltas were recorded in notes, and clean-worktree validation passed for `.#runtime`, `.#yazelix`, and the minimal Home Manager activation package. Active-checkout runtime build is currently blocked only by unrelated in-flight ratconfig fakeHash changes.

title: Collapse boids diagonal sprites to cardinal triangles
description: The filled diagonal triangle boids look bad in terminal cells because character height is roughly twice cell width. Keep diagonal movement internally, but render boids using only cardinal directional sprites. Predator should use a small ball body for east/west and a larger ball body for north/south.
design: Keep the existing simulation and 8-way direction classifier if useful, but map visual sprites to cardinal directions. Diagonal visual selection should choose the dominant/readable cardinal axis rather than corner triangles. Prey use ◀ ▶ ▲ ▼. Predator keeps body-plus-tip: east/west use a smaller ball body with horizontal triangles; north/south use a larger ball body with vertical triangles. Update tests to assert no diagonal filled-corner glyphs appear in boids output and that predator horizontal/vertical body sizes differ.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Boids output no longer uses ◤ ◥ ◢ ◣. 2. Prey render with only ◀ ▶ ▲ ▼. 3. Predator east/west use a small ball body. 4. Predator north/south use a larger ball body. 5. Movement remains deterministic and existing boids tests pass.
close_reason: Completed: boids now render only cardinal visual sprites; diagonal movement maps to horizontal cardinal glyphs, corner triangles are removed, and predator uses a small body for east/west plus a larger body for north/south. Tests defend the glyph set, body-size split, frame dimensions, and deterministic motion.

title: Define yzx restart layout retention contract
description: GitHub issue #576 asks whether yzx restart should save the live Zellij pane topology across restart, including each tab layout, while intentionally not preserving/rerunning pane commands. Investigation of upstream Zellij shows useful built-in support: DumpSessionLayout returns KDL plus layout metadata through the plugin API, DumpLayout exists as a Zellij action, SaveSession writes session info/layout to disk, and session serialization already captures tabs, tiled/floating panes, focus, names, sizes, cwd, viewport when enabled, and commands marked start_suspended. The decision should evaluate whether Yazelix can reuse that Zellij session-layout machinery and only add a sanitizing/adaptation layer instead of building its own topology serializer. If upstream/plugin surfaces cannot avoid resurrection prompt contamination or cannot expose enough safe layout metadata, evaluate a thin Zellij fork as a bounded fallback, with the fork limited to clean layout-skeleton export or non-command resurrection behavior rather than broad Yazelix-specific behavior.
design:
Investigation notes for avoiding wheel reinvention:

Upstream Zellij already has most of the layout machinery Yazelix needs. The useful surfaces are:
- Plugin API `dump_session_layout()` / `dump_session_layout_for_tab()`: returns live session layout KDL plus layout metadata. The default layout-manager plugin uses this for its "Save Layout of Current Session" flow.
- Plugin API `save_layout(name, layout_kdl, overwrite)`: validates the KDL with Zellij's own parser, sanitizes the file name, formats the document, and saves it under Zellij's layout directory.
- Plugin API `parse_layout(layout_string)`: validates layout KDL and returns metadata.
- Zellij action `DumpLayout`: dumps the current layout to the CLI client, but the plugin API is a cleaner fit for Yazelix because it returns data to the caller and removes the requesting plugin from the dump.
- Zellij action/API `SaveSession`: writes session info/layout for resurrection, but full resurrection is not the desired UX because command panes remain command-bearing and can trigger prompt/hold behavior.

Recommended product shape:
1. `yzx restart` asks the Yazelix pane orchestrator for a restart layout snapshot. The orchestrator should call Zellij's `dump_session_layout()` and return the raw KDL plus enough metadata/status to the CLI pipe caller.
2. `yzx restart` writes a temporary sanitized layout file and launches the fresh Yazelix session with that file instead of the configured default layout.
3. Sanitization preserves tab topology, tiled/floating geometry, stack/split structure, pane sizes, tab names, cwd, focus, pane titles, and Yazelix-owned pane identity where possible.
4. Sanitization strips command resurrection from user panes: remove `command`, `args`, and `start_suspended` for ordinary command panes. `start_suspended` must not remain after command removal because Zellij rejects it without a command.
5. Yazelix-owned panes should be rehydrated from current Yazelix config/runtime, not stale dumped commands. In particular, sidebar panes should run the current sidebar command normally instead of carrying a suspended resurrected command.
6. Built-in/Yazelix chrome plugins should be reconstructed from current generated layout/render plan. Unknown third-party plugin panes should be an explicit policy decision: either preserve only safe built-in `zellij:` plugins or convert unknown plugin panes to neutral shell panes for the first iteration.
7. Validate sanitized KDL through a structured parser and, where possible, Zellij's own parse/save surfaces. Do not use ad-hoc string edits for KDL.
8. If capture fails or sanitized KDL does not validate, restart should fall back to the configured default layout with a visible warning/log, not fail the restart.

Future layout saving shape:
- Reuse the same capture and sanitizer, then call Zellij's `save_layout()` rather than inventing a Yazelix layout database.
- A future `yzx layout save <name>` should save a sanitized layout skeleton, not process state.
- This aligns with Zellij layout-manager's existing "new layout from current session" model while preserving Yazelix's cleaner no-command-resurrection UX.

Thin fork fallback:
- Only evaluate a thin Zellij fork if the plugin/API path cannot expose enough safe layout data or cannot avoid command prompt contamination.
- A fork should be limited to a generic upstreamable feature such as exporting a command-free layout skeleton, not broad Yazelix-specific session behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Decision defines whether yzx restart should snapshot and restore live tab layout topology by default, expose a retain_layout setting, or keep returning to the configured default layout. It must cover every tab, tiled and floating panes, stacked panes, focus, pane sizes, active swap layout, sidebar collapsed/open state, tab names, cwd handling, and what is explicitly excluded from restoration, especially process commands and command resurrection. It must evaluate Zellij's dump-session-layout/session-serialization surfaces as inputs, define how command-bearing panes are stripped or converted into neutral shell/editor/sidebar panes, explain interaction with user-declared layout profiles from yazelix-fhf1, and create implementation follow-ups for the accepted scope.

title: Prototype Zellij session-layout capture for restart
description: Prototype the restart capture path by adding or validating a Yazelix pane-orchestrator pipe that calls Zellij's dump_session_layout() plugin API and returns live session layout KDL plus status/metadata to yzx restart.
acceptance_criteria: A maintainer can trigger the capture from inside a Yazelix session and receive current-session layout KDL without using Zellij resurrection. The result must document whether capture includes every tab, focused tab, tiled/floating panes, cwd/title/focus metadata, and whether the requesting pane-orchestrator plugin is removed from the dump as upstream documents. Failure modes must be visible and suitable for restart fallback.
notes: Plan repair 2026-05-03: parent decision yazelix-l18h is closed as the captured restart-layout product decision. This bead is the first actionable implementation slice: prove the Zellij dump_session_layout capture path before defining sanitizer policy or handoff implementation.

title: Define restart layout sanitization contract
description:
## Decision
Pending. Define the command-free restart layout sanitizer contract before implementing restart-layout handoff.

## Rationale
Yazelix wants some session-layout benefits without Zellij resurrection's command rerun prompts. A sanitizer policy is the safety boundary that decides exactly what live layout data may survive restart.

## Alternatives Considered
Use Zellij resurrection unchanged; use raw dumped layouts; build a structured KDL sanitizer; or keep restart at configured default layouts only.

## Existing Context
Define the command-free restart layout sanitizer for dumped Zellij session-layout KDL. This is the policy seam that keeps Yazelix from using full resurrection while still reusing Zellij's layout capture machinery.
acceptance_criteria: Decision specifies the structured KDL parser/serializer approach, crate-vs-in-house choice, and exact transformations for command panes, edit panes, args, start_suspended, contents_file, viewport/scrollback content, cwd, focus, floating panes, stacked panes, tab names, pane titles, built-in plugins, Yazelix-owned panes, and unknown plugin panes. It must state which data is preserved, stripped, rehydrated from current Yazelix config, or rejected, and define validation through Zellij parse/save surfaces where possible.

title: Implement sanitized restart layout handoff
description: Make yzx restart use a sanitized live-layout snapshot as the startup layout for the fresh Yazelix session, while falling back to the configured default layout when capture or validation fails.
acceptance_criteria: yzx restart preserves the current session layout skeleton across tabs after a runtime refresh without rerunning arbitrary pane commands. The implementation writes a bounded temporary restart layout, passes it to the fresh launch path, avoids stale command resurrection prompts, rehydrates Yazelix-owned panes from current config where defined by the sanitizer contract, and logs or prints a clear fallback reason when default layout is used instead. Tests cover success, capture failure, sanitizer failure, and no command/start_suspended leakage in the generated restart layout.

title: Evaluate named saved layouts from live session
description:
## Decision
Pending. Evaluate named saved layouts from live sessions only after the restart sanitizer contract is defined.

## Rationale
Named layout save can reuse the restart capture/sanitizer path, but should not create a separate Yazelix layout database unless Zellij's existing layout-manager surfaces are insufficient.

## Alternatives Considered
Only document upstream Zellij layout-manager; add yzx layout save/list/delete/open; store layouts in Home Manager/user config; or defer until restart layout retention works.

## Existing Context
Evaluate a future yzx layout save flow that reuses the same Zellij session-layout capture and Yazelix sanitizer to save named command-free layout skeletons.
acceptance_criteria: Decision defines whether Yazelix should expose yzx layout save/list/delete/open commands, delegate storage to Zellij's save_layout/delete_layout/layout-manager surfaces, or only document upstream layout-manager usage. It must reuse the restart capture/sanitizer where possible, avoid a separate Yazelix layout database unless justified, and define how saved layouts interact with user-declared layout profiles and Home Manager-managed layout files.

title: Implement structured KDL restart-layout sanitizer
description: Implement the Rust structured parser/serializer pass for command-free restart layout snapshots. This turns dumped Zellij session-layout KDL into a safe layout skeleton that preserves topology while preventing command resurrection.
design: Use the sanitizer contract from yazelix-l18h.2 as the policy source. Prefer a real KDL parser/serializer or a documented crate-vs-in-house decision before implementation. Avoid ad-hoc string edits. Strip or rewrite command-bearing fields such as command, args, edit/start_suspended, contents/viewport process state, and rehydrate Yazelix-owned panes from current config where the contract requires it.
acceptance_criteria: Given dumped Zellij layout KDL, the sanitizer emits valid KDL that preserves tab/pane topology, sizes, names, cwd/focus metadata where allowed, and contains no command/args/start_suspended leakage for ordinary command panes. Tests cover command stripping, start_suspended removal, Yazelix-owned pane rehydration policy, unknown plugin policy, malformed KDL errors, and validation against the accepted parser/serializer path.

title: Add throttled agent-usage producer for cached status-bar widgets
acceptance_criteria: Agent-usage status-bar widgets are populated by a throttled cache producer, not by zjstatus polling. Producer runs from the pane orchestrator on a startup delay and roughly every 120 seconds, uses short provider timeouts, preserves the window-local cache contract, and has regression coverage for precomputed summaries and hung providers.
notes: Implemented yzx_control zellij status-cache-refresh-agent-usage. The command reads the launch-scoped status-bar cache, skips refresh while cached usage is fresh, discovers opt-in ccusage provider binaries from PATH, runs each provider with a 1.5s timeout, and writes precomputed summaries back into agent_usage. The pane orchestrator now subscribes to Timer events, schedules an initial usage refresh after 5s, then repeats every 120s. Zjstatus usage widgets still only read the cache and never run providers directly.
close_reason: Completed throttled cached agent-usage producer with provider timeouts, pane-orchestrator scheduling, tests, synced wasm, and upgrade-contract CI fix docs.

title: Prototype explicit Yazelix session save and restore UX
description: Prototype the smallest explicit session save/restore surface that fits Yazelix: likely a yzx session family or a Zellij-session-manager handoff that calls Zellij save-session/restore primitives while preserving Yazelix per-window config snapshot expectations.
design: Do not reintroduce persistent_sessions as a config toggle. The first implementation should be explicit, inspectable, and compatible with multiple concurrent Yazelix windows.
acceptance_criteria: There is a working prototype or a documented no-go result for explicit save/restore, including how it handles restored workspace roots, managed panes, and config snapshot/version mismatch diagnostics.

title: Make Ghostty the default packaged runtime
description: Restore Ghostty as the default Yazelix packaged runtime so the cursor-trail identity is present by default, while keeping WezTerm available as an explicit runtime variant.
notes: Implementation: made Ghostty the default packaged runtime across flake/package/Home Manager/runtime helper defaults; changed the default terminal order to ghostty then wezterm; kept WezTerm available as #yazelix_wezterm and runtime_variant = "wezterm"; updated user-facing docs and README generated surface. Verification: yzx dev rust fmt --check; yzx dev rust check; focused Rust tests normalized_configured_terminals_defaults_to_ghostty_then_wezterm and full_launch_materialization_defaults_to_ghostty_then_wezterm; validate-config-surface-contract; validate-flake-interface; validate-readme-version; validate-upgrade-contract; validate-rust-test-traceability; nix derivation show confirmed #runtime writes runtime_variant ghostty and #runtime_wezterm writes wezterm. Note: taplo fmt --check reports existing whole-file TOML formatting drift, so no broad TOML reformat was applied.
close_reason: Completed: Ghostty is now the default packaged runtime and terminal order, with WezTerm retained as the explicit packaged variant.

title: Retire or hard-archive docs that cite deleted Nu owners as live examples
description: Audit outcome: several historical docs still mention deleted files such as launch_state.nu, yzx/env.nu, yzx/run.nu, config_migrations.nu, and config_migration_transactions.nu. Some are valid history, but they should not be easy to misread as current planning or live contract guidance.
design: Retained behavior: useful migration history and upgrade-note guarded-change records. Candidate surviving owner: spec_inventory.md plus explicit historical labels, or deletion for docs with no durable value. Stop condition: keep references in upgrade history or archived design notes when deleting them would erase useful release context; rewrite only when current docs present deleted files as current examples.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Every stale deleted-owner reference in docs is classified as history, guarded upgrade history, stale planning, or delete candidate. 2. Current planning docs no longer point at deleted Nu owners as active targets. 3. Historical docs that remain are clearly marked historical or archived. 4. Useless transition docs are deleted or removed from active inventories. 5. validate_specs passes after inventory updates.
close_reason: Completed

title: Do not shadow host Nix with runtime-local upstream Nix
description: Tracks GitHub issue 586: Yazelix must not expose runtime-private Nix ahead of the host Nix in normal interactive or terminal-launch PATHs. The immediate code leak is shells/posix/yazelix_ghostty.sh prepending runtime libexec, which can surface private helpers such as nix despite the curated toolbin contract.
notes: Implemented local fix: shells/posix/yazelix_ghostty.sh now prepends only runtime toolbin and bin, never runtime libexec, so private helpers such as libexec/nix are not exposed ahead of the host Nix through the Ghostty launch wrapper. Added regression test ghostty_wrapper_keeps_runtime_libexec_private_for_host_nix. Validation: preferred yzx dev rust test refused outside maintainer shell; direct fallback cargo test -p yazelix_core ghostty_wrapper_keeps_runtime_libexec_private_for_host_nix passed.
close_reason: Fixed the Ghostty launch wrapper so it prepends only runtime toolbin/bin and never runtime libexec, preventing private runtime helpers such as libexec/nix from shadowing host Nix. Added regression test ghostty_wrapper_keeps_runtime_libexec_private_for_host_nix. Validation: preferred yzx dev rust test refused outside maintainer shell; direct fallback cargo test -p yazelix_core ghostty_wrapper_keeps_runtime_libexec_private_for_host_nix passed.

title: evaluate splitting some yazelix builting zellij plugins to standalone plugins
description: Imported GitHub issue #485.
close_reason: Superseded by child issues yazelix-l968.1 / #503 and yazelix-l968.2 / #504

title: Harden popup as a standalone-capable pane-orchestrator capability
description: Internal-first roadmap for eventually extracting the managed popup flow into a standalone Zellij plugin. Today the popup command and geometry are already config-driven inside Yazelix, but popup identity and selector assumptions are still hardwired to Yazelix names and wrapper flow.
design: Grounded in current code: `yzx popup` already owns argv command resolution, workspace-root cwd selection, and popup geometry through config parsing and Home Manager parity. The remaining coupling lives in the popup identity contract: `yzx_popup`, `yzx_popup_program.nu`, and the popup-runner selector logic. Do not jump straight to a standalone plugin. First make popup identity explicit and configurable inside Yazelix, formalize the adapter contract around argv/cwd/geometry, and harden the managed single-instance tests. Extraction only makes sense if those steps leave behind a clean generic contract with little or no Yazelix-specific shell glue.
acceptance_criteria: Yazelix first has an explicit internal popup contract covering managed popup identity, argv command execution, cwd policy, and geometry. Popup identity is no longer implicitly tied to hardcoded wrapper names alone. Regression coverage defends the single-instance managed popup behavior. After those steps, there is enough evidence to decide whether standalone extraction is justified.
notes:
2026-04-26 subsystem review: keep popup inside yazelix_pane_orchestrator for now. The code still depends on Yazelix runtime wrappers and sidebar-refresh hooks, but popup has real external value. New architecture bead yazelix-subsys.4 tracks the capability/API hardening path before any standalone repo or plugin extraction.
2026-04-26 plan-space review: remaining popup work is now tracked by yazelix-subsys.4.1 and yazelix-subsys.4.2. Do not treat the closed child beads as proof that standalone extraction is ready; they completed internal identity/argv/geometry groundwork only.
close_reason: Completed: child beads l968.1.1-l968.1.3 and subsys.4 coverage establish the internal popup identity, argv/cwd/geometry adapter contract, and single-instance regression evidence; no standalone extraction is claimed.

title: Popup: make managed popup identity explicit and configurable inside Yazelix
description: Replace the remaining hardcoded managed popup identity assumptions with an explicit internal contract so popup selection is not implicitly coupled only to `yzx_popup` and `yzx_popup_program.nu`.
design: Today the popup identity contract is split across `yzx popup`, `yzx_popup_program.nu`, and the popup-runner selector. Make that identity explicit: pane title, wrapper marker, and any downstream selector inputs should be treated as a contract rather than incidental matching. Keep this internal to Yazelix first.
acceptance_criteria: Managed popup identity is represented explicitly in code rather than only as scattered hardcoded string assumptions. The popup-runner selector and wrapper flow agree on one clear identity contract. Existing popup behavior still works.
close_reason: Completed: managed transient popup identity is now explicit in shared Nushell/Rust contract surfaces, wrappers, and tests.

title: Popup: formalize the argv/cwd/geometry adapter contract
description: Turn the existing popup command, cwd, and geometry behavior into an explicit internal adapter contract that downstream extraction could reuse later.
design: The code already resolves popup argv, workspace-root cwd, and popup width/height through config parsing and Home Manager parity. Make that shape explicit as the internal adapter contract before any extraction discussion. Prefer documenting and defending the existing contract over inventing broader popup abstractions.
acceptance_criteria: The popup argv command, cwd policy, and geometry are treated as an explicit internal contract. The current config path and Home Manager path remain in sync. The behavior is documented or defended clearly enough that later extraction would not need to rediscover the contract.
close_reason: Completed: popup/menu open requests now use one explicit argv/cwd/runtime/geometry adapter contract with focused verification.

title: Popup: harden managed single-instance regression coverage
description: Add or strengthen focused tests around the managed popup contract so later refactors or extraction attempts do not regress single-instance behavior.
design: Keep this focused on the contracts that matter: one managed popup instance, configured argv command, workspace-root cwd preference, and configured geometry. Avoid generic command-discovery tests.
acceptance_criteria: Focused regression coverage defends the managed popup identity and single-instance behavior, the popup argv command contract, the cwd policy, and popup geometry behavior.
close_reason: Covered by popup command/wrapper tests and transient-pane contract coverage

title: Keep sidebar as a pane-orchestrator capability while isolating reusable contracts
description: Internal-first roadmap for eventually extracting a generic sidebar plugin with sidebar-aware motions. The reusable motion logic is already partially isolated, but the sidebar launcher and generated layouts are still tightly coupled to Yazelix-managed Yazi behavior.
design: Grounded in current code: sidebar width is already config-driven and the closed-sidebar-aware motion logic already lives in reusable Rust contracts. The main blocker is launcher and layout coupling: generated layouts still hardwire `launch_sidebar_yazi.nu` and a managed `sidebar` pane running Yazi. Do not jump straight to a standalone plugin. First make the sidebar launcher configurable inside Yazelix, isolate the reusable sidebar and motion contract from Yazelix-specific Yazi/editor assumptions, and keep plugin commands as the stable contract while treating keybindings as docs/examples only. Extraction only makes sense if those steps leave a generic sidebar contract behind.
acceptance_criteria: Yazelix first has an explicit internal sidebar contract covering configurable sidebar program, sidebar width, toggle/focus commands, and horizontal motions that ignore a closed sidebar. The launcher and layouts are no longer inherently tied to Yazi-only behavior. After those steps, there is enough evidence to decide whether standalone extraction is justified.
notes:
Terminology guardrail: keep `sidebar` as the generic side-surface term in architecture and plugin-contract work. Use `file tree` or `Yazi file tree` only when referring specifically to the current Yazi-backed surface in user-facing wording. This matters because future side surfaces may include agents or other tab-local tools, and the sidebar abstraction should stay broader than the current file-tree implementation.
2026-04-26 subsystem review: do not pursue a separate sidebar repo yet. Current sidebar behavior is tightly coupled to pane-orchestrator session truth, layout state, editor focus, workspace state, and the Yazi file-tree adapter. Continue the child beads as internal contract isolation work, and use yazelix-subsys.5 as the architecture decision bead.
close_reason: Completed by closed child beads: sidebar launcher is configurable, reusable sidebar/motion contracts are isolated, plugin commands are separated from keybinding policy, and the broader architecture decision is captured in yazelix-subsys.5.

title: Sidebar: make side-surface launcher configurable inside Yazelix
description: Remove the assumption that the managed sidebar surface is always launched through launch_sidebar_yazi.nu, while preserving the current Yazi file tree as the default side-surface adapter.
design: Generalize inside Yazelix first. The launcher command should become an explicit config/generation concern, but the pane-orchestrator still owns the sidebar role, identity, focus policy, and tab-local state. Keep the word sidebar for the generic architectural slot and Yazi file tree for the default adapter.
acceptance_criteria: Generated layouts no longer inherently depend on launch_sidebar_yazi.nu as the only sidebar launcher path; the default Yazi file tree behavior is unchanged; non-Yazi side-surface launchers have a clear config/generation path; the change does not create a second sidebar state owner.
notes: 2026-04-26 implementation start: no new crates. Keep the pane role/name/state owned by the orchestrator; make only the launched terminal command/args configurable in Rust Zellij materialization. Default remains the managed Yazi launcher.
close_reason: Completed: sidebar side-surface launcher is configurable via main config and Home Manager while preserving the default Yazi adapter

title: Sidebar: isolate the reusable motion and visibility contract
description: Preserve and sharpen the reusable Rust-side sidebar contract so closed-sidebar-aware motions and visibility toggles can stand on their own apart from Yazelix launcher assumptions.
design: The left/right motion and sidebar visibility logic is already partly isolated in pure Rust contracts. Strengthen that boundary and keep it generic. The reusable contract should not depend on Yazi-specific launcher details, and it should minimize editor-specific assumptions where possible.
acceptance_criteria: The reusable sidebar and motion contract is clearly isolated and defended. Horizontal motions ignore the sidebar when closed and treat it as a valid focus target when open. The contract is not entangled with Yazi-only launcher details. Sidebar state and path-sync state remain tab-local or pane-local and do not reintroduce session-global recency lookup.
notes:
2026-04-26 plan-space review: explicitly preserve the tab-local state invariant from yazelix-f0w. Any reusable sidebar contract must key path-sync and visibility state by current tab or pane identity, never by session-global recency.
2026-04-26 dependency gate: production crates unchanged; dev-only crates unchanged. Build in-house/std only because this is pure pane state/motion policy already inside pane-orchestrator. Rejected adding layout/state helper crates because the required contract is small, deterministic, and tied to Zellij pane metadata. Packaging impact: pane-orchestrator source/wasm rebuild only; no new runtime dependencies.
close_reason: Completed: sidebar motion now uses role-based panes instead of literal title matching; sidebar visibility returns action plus post-layout focus plan; sidebar-associated state retention/registration is isolated as a tab-local pane identity contract; pane-orchestrator wasm rebuilt and synced.

title: Sidebar: separate plugin commands from keybinding policy
description: Treat sidebar commands such as toggle and focus as the stable command surface and keep keybindings as docs/examples rather than plugin semantics.
design: The plugin command surface already wants to be the stable contract, while keybindings belong to generated config and user policy. Make that separation explicit before any extraction attempt so the later standalone story is about commands, not about copying Yazelix default keymaps.
acceptance_criteria: Sidebar command surfaces such as toggle and focus are documented or treated as the stable contract. Keybindings remain configurable policy in Zellij config, with examples rather than hard requirements.
notes: 2026-04-26 implementation start: documentation/contract slice only. No new crates. Preserve pane-orchestrator command names as the stable API and clarify that Ctrl+y/Alt+y are Yazelix keybinding policy examples, not plugin semantics.
close_reason: Completed: documented sidebar pane-orchestrator command names as the stable API, clarified Ctrl+y/Alt+y are default Zellij keybinding policy, and corrected Zellij customization docs away from layout-file keybind edits.

title: Tighten runtime/launch ownership boundaries after the recent state-ownership fixes
description: Carry the post-hardening runtime/launch architecture tightening lane after the recent fixes to runtime identity, launch-state ownership, desktop launch, refresh/build profile recording, and shell-vs-session profile resolution. The goal is not a rewrite. It is to align specs and code around a cleaner ownership model: dynamic user intent, deterministic runtime code, materialized/generated state, and explicit live session activation state, with narrower helpers and less split-brain between maintainer shells, runtime build artifacts, and recorded launch state.
design: Treat this as a delete-first architecture tightening pass, not a new abstraction project. Keep the current direction: split roots, deterministic runtime code, recorded launch state, and installed-runtime support. Tighten the remaining leaks by making runtime resolution fail fast, separating runtime-project materialization from read-only lookup, decomposing smart profile resolution into explicit layers, clarifying which flows own persistent state writes, and retiring generic root aliases where explicit helpers already exist.
acceptance_criteria: The follow-on child beads land a cleaner runtime/launch model without rewriting the product architecture. The specs name live session activation state explicitly, runtime-root resolution no longer falls back to config-root semantics, runtime-project materialization is not hidden behind read-style helpers, launch/profile resolution uses narrower explicit layers, persistent state writes have clearer ownership, and generic get_yazelix_dir or YAZELIX_DIR usage is reduced to intentional compatibility surfaces.
notes: Deeper review conclusion: the current runtime/launch architecture is directionally sound and does not need a rewrite, but it still needs tightening around runtime root resolution, runtime-project materialization, launch-profile resolution, persistent state ownership, and generic root aliases. The most important finding is that the docs should treat live session activation state as distinct from both materialized/generated state and deterministic runtime code. Follow-on work is now split into child beads le0s.1 through le0s.6 rather than leaving those concerns buried inside one review task.
close_reason: all steps complete

title: Define live session activation state as a first-class runtime layer
description: Extend the current runtime/launch specs beyond the three-layer model of dynamic user intent, deterministic runtime code, and materialized/generated state by naming live session activation state explicitly. Clarify how DEVENV_PROFILE, PATH activation, IN_YAZELIX_SHELL, YAZELIX_TERMINAL, and Zellij session markers relate to launch_state.json and the recorded built profile, so stale maintainer shells and live launched Yazelix sessions stop looking like the same thing in the architecture docs and helper contracts.
design: This is the spec-first slice. Extend the runtime/launch contracts and architecture map so they distinguish four layers instead of collapsing the last two: dynamic user intent, deterministic runtime code, materialized/generated state, and live session activation state. Be explicit that launch_state.json and rebuild_hash live in materialized state, while DEVENV_PROFILE, PATH activation, IN_YAZELIX_SHELL, YAZELIX_TERMINAL, and Zellij markers belong to live session activation. Keep this bead focused on naming and ownership, not broad helper refactors.
acceptance_criteria: The runtime-facing specs and architecture notes name live session activation state explicitly and distinguish it from both materialized/generated state and deterministic runtime code. The docs explain why a stale maintainer shell can coexist with a correct launch_state.json, and they clarify which session markers are activation-only rather than persisted runtime truth. The resulting terminology gives later helper refactors a cleaner contract to target instead of keeping activation state implicit.
close_reason: Completed

title: Fail fast when runtime root resolution cannot find a valid runtime
description: Remove the hidden fallback in get_yazelix_runtime_dir that currently returns the config root when no valid runtime root can be resolved. That fallback keeps config and runtime concerns conflated and violates the runtime-root contract. Replace it with fail-fast runtime resolution, harden the affected callers, and make startup/doctor/launch errors explain that the runtime root is missing or invalid instead of silently treating ~/.config/yazelix as runtime code.
design: Constrain this bead to runtime-root resolution only. Remove the config-root escape hatch from get_yazelix_runtime_dir, preserve explicit YAZELIX_RUNTIME_DIR or legacy YAZELIX_DIR only as runtime aliases, and harden callers that were accidentally surviving on the fallback. Error handling should say that the runtime root is missing or invalid; it should not silently reinterpret ~/.config/yazelix as shipped runtime code. Do not mix this bead with generic alias cleanup beyond what is strictly required for fail-fast behavior.
acceptance_criteria: No canonical runtime-root helper returns the config root as a fallback when runtime resolution fails. Launch, doctor, startup, and other affected user-facing entrypoints fail fast with a clear runtime-root error instead of drifting into config-owned paths. Existing compatibility surfaces for explicit runtime aliases remain intentional and documented rather than being reintroduced as hidden fallback behavior.
close_reason: Completed

title: Split runtime-project lookup from runtime-project materialization
description: Replace ensure_yazelix_runtime_project_dir with a cleaner split between read-only lookup and explicit materialization. The current helper rewrites the symlinked build workspace even when callers only want to inspect build artifacts, which hides write-heavy behavior behind a read-style name. Introduce a read-only get_existing_runtime_project_dir surface and an explicit materialize_runtime_project_dir surface, then move read callers such as launch-state and bootstrap logic onto the non-materializing path unless they really need to regenerate the project tree.
design: This bead is about helper honesty. Split ensure_yazelix_runtime_project_dir into an explicit materializer and a read-only existing-project resolver so callers stop paying write-heavy workspace regeneration when they only want to inspect build artifacts. Classify current callers into writers versus readers first, then move read paths such as launch-state/profile inspection and bootstrap path discovery onto the non-materializing helper unless they truly need to regenerate the symlinked runtime project.
acceptance_criteria: The codebase has separate read-only and materializing runtime-project helpers with names that match their behavior. Read-side callers no longer recreate the runtime project tree as a hidden side effect. The remaining writers are explicit about why they materialize the project workspace, and the repo is easier to reason about because project-workspace writes are no longer hidden behind lookup-style helpers.
close_reason: Completed

title: Decompose resolve_built_profile into explicit profile-resolution layers
description: Break resolve_built_profile into narrower resolvers instead of keeping live-session detection, runtime-project artifacts, recorded launch state, and ambient env fallback inside one smart resolver. The real goal is not arbitrary caller-defined precedence for every call site. It is to separate the two actual policy bundles in the code today: current live-session profile resolution versus runtime-owned reusable profile resolution, so stale maintainer shells stop leaking into external launch and doctor logic.
design: Refactor resolve_built_profile by extracting explicit evidence resolvers for the distinct sources it currently mixes: live launched session activation, runtime-project build artifacts, recorded launch-state, and ambient shell fallback. Then add small policy wrappers for the two real caller families that already exist in code. Current-session flows such as start_yazelix_inner and refresh should prefer live launched-session activation first, then narrower runtime-owned evidence if needed. External/runtime-owned flows such as yzx launch, doctor, and editor launch outside the session should prefer runtime-project build artifacts first, then recorded launch-state, with ambient shell fallback kept only as a narrow compatibility edge. Keep resolve_profile_candidate and shell-script parsing as low-level utilities. It is acceptable to keep resolve_built_profile temporarily as a thin compatibility wrapper, but its precedence must simply delegate to one explicit policy helper rather than remaining the smart policy center.
acceptance_criteria: Profile resolution is decomposed into narrower helpers that each represent one evidence source or one explicit policy bundle. Core callers stop all relying on one monolithic smart resolver for materially different contexts. At minimum, launch and doctor use a runtime-owned reusable-profile policy, while start/refresh use a current-session policy, and editor launch context picks intentionally between them. If a compatibility wrapper remains, it is thin, documented, and no longer the place where most precedence logic hides. Regression coverage defends both key precedence stories: outside a live Yazelix session, runtime-owned evidence beats stale ambient shell state; inside a live Yazelix session, the activated session profile still wins.
close_reason: Completed

title: Make materialized-state persistence use explicit recording helpers
description: Tighten persistent state ownership by replacing generic bootstrap-side mutation helpers with explicitly named recorders for materialized state and launch state. Today mark_config_state_applied and record_launch_state express two different ownership domains but still feel like generic state writes hanging off bootstrap/launch helpers. Refactor that surface so setup/install, launch/refresh, and live session activation each write only the state they actually own.
design: Clarify persistent-state ownership by naming the writes after the ownership domain they represent. The current split between mark_config_state_applied and record_launch_state is already better than before, but the mutation surface still hangs off generic bootstrap and launch helpers. Move toward explicit recorders for materialized state versus launch-state, and keep setup/install, refresh/build, and live launch flows from writing state they do not own.
acceptance_criteria: Persistent state writes are routed through explicitly named helpers that reflect ownership boundaries instead of generic bootstrap-side mutation. Setup or install flows only record the materialized state they own, while launch or refresh flows record launch-state/profile state they own. The resulting API makes it harder for future setup or shell-hook paths to overwrite launch-state accidentally.
close_reason: Completed

title: Deprecate generic get_yazelix_dir and YAZELIX_DIR in favor of explicit root helpers
description: Follow through on the split-root architecture by retiring generic runtime aliases where explicit config, runtime, repo, state, or runtime-reference helpers now exist. Reduce use of get_yazelix_dir and YAZELIX_DIR in code, tests, and docs so helpers stop hiding which ownership domain they actually mean. Keep compatibility only where it remains an intentional user-facing transition surface.
design: Treat this as the final cleanup slice after the explicit helper boundaries are in place. Replace get_yazelix_dir and YAZELIX_DIR usages with config-root, runtime-root, state-root, repo-root, or runtime-reference helpers where the ownership domain is now known. Keep compatibility only at intentional external boundaries, and avoid turning this bead into a search-and-replace churn pass before the sharper helper APIs from the earlier children exist.
acceptance_criteria: Generic get_yazelix_dir and YAZELIX_DIR usage is materially reduced in code, tests, and docs because call sites now use explicit root helpers that match the ownership domain they mean. Remaining compatibility uses are deliberate, documented, and narrow. New code in the affected areas no longer introduces fresh generic root alias usage by default.
notes: Pipeline stage 1 for the current Core-readiness lane. Explicit root ownership and runtime identity must stop leaking through generic helpers before the repo can prove a backend-free workspace slice honestly.
close_reason: Completed: removed the remaining live YAZELIX_DIR runtime-root fallback from shared runtime resolution and the managed Helix wrapper, narrowed docs/tests/helpers to explicit runtime roots, and added wrapper regression coverage for legacy-alias isolation.

title: Tighten duplicate refresh-state inspection between prelaunch and in-terminal startup
description: Yazelix no longer appears to do a true double rebuild for the recent desktop and launch paths, but there is still duplicated refresh-state inspection across the prelaunch path and the in-terminal startup path. Both sides reason about needs_refresh and related state before the real interactive session starts. Track the follow-up cleanup so the product keeps one real rebuild boundary while reducing duplicated state inspection and split-brain launch reasoning.
design: Keep this delete-first and narrow. The goal is not to merge every launch helper into one giant orchestrator. The goal is to stop both the external/prelaunch path and the in-terminal startup path from independently inspecting refresh state when only one side should own the authoritative rebuild decision. First map exactly which path should decide whether a rebuild is required and which path should simply consume the already-decided result. Then delete duplicate state checks, notices, and branching where they only restate the same decision. Preserve the current good property: one real rebuild boundary, visible when appropriate, without reintroducing hidden rebuilds or stale-session confusion.
acceptance_criteria: 1. There is one clearly owned rebuild-decision boundary for the affected launch flow, rather than parallel prelaunch and in-terminal inspection of the same refresh state. 2. Remaining startup code consumes an explicit rebuild decision or artifact instead of recomputing the same launch-state judgment ad hoc. 3. The cleanup does not regress the visible-rebuild desktop contract, reuse mode, or terminal-wrapper repair logic. 4. Focused regressions defend that the flow still rebuilds exactly once when needed and does not silently hide rebuild work behind the launcher.
notes: Implemented as a narrow delete-first cleanup: yzx launch and the in-terminal start_yazelix wrapper now parse config directly instead of calling prepare_environment, so they no longer recompute config_state/needs_refresh. launch_yazelix.nu remains the single prelaunch rebuild-decision boundary for this flow, while start_yazelix_inner.nu keeps materializing runtime configs inside the session. Updated the workspace forwarding fixture with a config_parser stub for the lighter launch dependency. Verified: validate_syntax.nu, default yzx suite 90/90, core canonical 17/17, workspace canonical 21/21, validate_default_test_count_budget.nu, validate_default_test_traceability.nu, git diff --check.
close_reason: Completed refresh-state ownership cleanup and verified affected yzx suites.

title: Test suite governance: keep every test relevant
description: Define how Yazelix decides whether a test belongs in the suite, which suite it belongs in, and when an existing test should be removed. The goal is to prevent the test surface from growing forever without clear relevance.
design: Start from first principles: every test should defend a real user-visible behavior, a regression that already happened, an integration boundary, a source-of-truth invariant, or an explicitly documented contract. This policy must apply to both new and existing tests. Define the actual Yazelix suite surfaces explicitly: the default yzx dev test path, optional heavier lanes such as sweep or visual integration checks, CI-only checks, and cheap local maintainer hooks like prek. Require a lightweight inventory of the current suites by test file or suite entrypoint, classify each bucket by the kind of contract it protects, and define what happens when a test cannot be tied to a living contract, regression, invariant, or accepted user-visible behavior: remove it, demote it out of the default suite, or explicitly grandfather and justify it. Prefer fewer higher-signal tests over broad shallow coverage.
acceptance_criteria: Yazelix has a written test-policy document or issue design note that defines: 1. the real suite categories and command entrypoints (yzx dev test, optional heavier lanes, CI-only checks, and prek); 2. admission criteria for new tests; 3. retention and pruning rules for existing tests; and 4. when a test belongs in the default suite versus an optional lane. Existing tests are inventoried at least at the suite or file level against that policy, and at least one low-value or redundant test is removed, demoted, or explicitly grandfathered with justification. Future testing work can point to this policy when deciding whether a test is worth adding and where it belongs.
notes: Final shape: public test model simplified again after the aggressive prune. yzx dev test now runs the normal non-sweep automated regression suite with explicit membership: test_yzx_commands.nu, test_yzx_extra_regressions.nu, and test_reuse_mode.nu. The public --extended flag was removed because only a tiny number of extra checks remained and the split no longer earned its UX cost. Internal organization remains: core yzx contracts plus a small extra-regression bundle. The default suite currently covers 20 core checks + 6 extra regressions + 1 reuse-mode regression. yzx dev test --all now means default suite plus sweep + visual. Verified: validate_syntax, yzx dev test, direct core/extra/reuse entrypoints, validate_specs, and validate_readme_version all pass.
close_reason: Completed: added test-suite governance spec, switched to explicit default-suite membership, aggressively pruned low-value tests, and simplified the public yzx dev test model.

title: Investigate pane interactivity delay before prompt becomes usable
description: Imported GitHub issue #517. Investigate why opening a new pane can leave it unresponsive for a couple of seconds before Starship renders and typing works. Trace the exact pane-open path and identify whether the wait is in Zellij pane creation, shell bootstrap, devenv shell entry, prompt initialization, or another Yazelix-managed hook.
close_reason: Completed: pane interactivity delay fixed by trimming managed Nushell startup, routing new panes through the managed Yazelix Nushell wrapper, and defending the startup contracts with regressions.

title: Investigate one-time post-welcome startup crash during generated-state repair
notes: User reported a single startup crash on 2026-04-24 after the welcome screen. Before reproduction, 'yzx status' reported generated-state refresh_required. After the failed first launch, status reported generated state up to date, and ~/.local/share/yazelix generated files under configs/yazi and configs/zellij updated at 2026-04-24 11:44. Subsequent launches worked. Working hypothesis: one-time failure in the repair-then-handoff seam after startup welcome, likely around generated-state materialization or the first Zellij handoff after repair. No steady-state startup failure currently reproduces.
close_reason: Completed: the concrete startup handoff context capture work was finished in yazelix-lh93.1, giving future repair-path crashes a durable handoff artifact for diagnosis.

title: Add startup handoff context capture for repair-path crashes
description:
Why this issue exists and what needs to be done

 points at a one-time post-welcome startup crash that likely lives in the repair-then-handoff seam. Right now the repo has runtime logs and profiling, but it does not leave behind one compact, durable startup-context artifact focused on the exact handoff inputs when generated-state repair just ran.

Add a targeted startup-context capture path for repair-driven launches so the next failure leaves behind the working directory, layout path, session mode, materialization outcome, and other handoff facts needed to diagnose the crash without guessing from partial logs.
close_reason: Completed: repair-driven startup now records a durable handoff JSON artifact with working dir, layout, shell, session, and materialization facts before the Zellij handoff.

title: Automate child-repo release transactions
description: Add maintainer tooling or a documented command sequence for coupled child-repo changes consumed by the main Yazelix flake.
design: Cover child clean-state checks, child push, main flake.lock update to the published revision, no-overrides validation, changelog or bead evidence, and refusal/diagnostics when main consumes unpublished child commits.
acceptance_criteria: The workflow prevents or clearly diagnoses unpublished child commits in main, records validation evidence before bead closure, and keeps child push plus main lock update as one explicit release transaction.
close_reason: Completed: added validate-child-release-transaction to verify first-party Yazelix flake pins are fetchable from GitHub and warn on dirty adjacent child checkouts. Verified with repo_child_release::tests and validate-child-release-transaction.

title: Add pane-orchestrator heartbeat and liveness diagnostics
description: The recurring pane-orchestrator busy-stall incident showed that Yazelix needs better live liveness facts for debugging plugin responsiveness. Evaluate and add a small heartbeat surface that records useful pane-orchestrator activity facts without creating new hot-path work.
acceptance_criteria: Expose or persist lightweight pane-orchestrator liveness facts such as last timer tick, last pipe handled, last status refresh start/finish, and stale/busy indicators; keep the heartbeat cheap enough for multiple Yazelix windows; document how maintainers inspect it during stalls; avoid adding high-frequency status-bar work.
notes:
Implemented follow-up heartbeat diagnostics: pane orchestrator records started_at, last event, last timer, last handled pipe, last status-cache write, and status-refresh starts. It flushes these facts every 30 seconds into the window-local status_bar_cache.json through a new internal status-cache-heartbeat command. The command can also read the cached heartbeat without piping into the plugin, so maintainers can inspect it during stalls. Status-refresh finish timestamps merge into the same heartbeat object from yzx_control without replacing plugin facts.
Verification: pane-orchestrator cargo test passed 43 tests; yazelix_core status_cache tests passed 17 tests; cargo check passed for pane_orchestrator and yazelix_core; validate-rust-test-traceability, validate-workspace-session-contract, and validate-pane-orchestrator-sync passed; yzx_control zellij status-cache-heartbeat --help runs through cargo.
Additional verification after preserving heartbeat across status-cache rewrites: yazelix_core status_cache tests passed 18 tests.
close_reason: Completed: added window-local pane-orchestrator heartbeat facts, cached read command, docs, tests, and synced wasm.

title: Implement explicit Linux graphics wrapper source mode
description:
Design follow-up from the NixGL closure audit. The current Linux runtime bundles `nixGLMesa` for Linux package variants and `nixVulkanMesa` for Ratty/Yazelix Terminal package variants. Measurements showed the combined direct graphics-wrapper closures can account for about 2.1 GiB of the yzxterm package closure.

Proposed surface:
- Add an explicit Linux graphics wrapper source option to Home Manager and `lib.${system}.mkYazelix`, defaulting to bundled.
- Candidate values: `bundled`, `host`, and `none`.
- Do not route this through `runtime_tool_sources`; graphics wrappers are terminal launch infrastructure, not leaf runtime tools.
- `host` should omit runtime-owned NixGL packages and rely on host `nixGL*`/`nixVulkan*` commands, using the existing host-wrapper lookup behavior where possible.
- `none` should omit wrappers and launch terminals directly, with clear doctor/runtime diagnostics about possible Linux graphics failures.

Acceptance criteria:
- Wire the option through `flake.nix`, `yazelix_package.nix`, `packaging/runtime_tool_registry.nix`, and `home_manager/module.nix` without changing the default bundled behavior.
- Update the terminal launch contract and Nix customization contract with the supported modes and per-terminal risks.
- Add focused tests for launch command rendering and doctor diagnostics for bundled, host, and none modes.
- Validate yzxterm, Ghostty, WezTerm, Kitty, and Ratty behavior expectations on Linux at the package/contract level.
- Measure before/after closure size with `shells/posix/yazelix_runtime_size_report.sh`.
- Run the focused package/runtime validation ladder and keep any full runtime build as the final gate.
close_reason: Grand audit decision: do not implement host/none Linux graphics-wrapper source modes now. Keep Linux graphics wrappers bundled for reliability and reproducible desktop launches; the separate 32-bit nixGL trimming already reduced bundled closure cost without adding a fragile host dependency mode. Reopen only if a future contract proves host/none graphics launching can be diagnosed and supported without weakening the default runtime.

comments.author: lucca
comments.text: 2026-06-02 partial reduction landed separately in `yazelix-disable-nixgl-32bit-wrappers-3tneo`: bundled mode now keeps `nixGLMesa` but disables nixGL 32-bit support. This cuts the default Linux bundled-wrapper cost while leaving the broader explicit `host`/`none` source-mode work open.

title: Drive Yazelix from 13.2k Nu LOC to the 4.2k hard floor
description: Second implementation tranche after the under-5k planning epic closed. The repo is at 13,232 tracked Nu LOC across 87 files, while the hard target is 4,200 LOC. Convert the current budget into concrete Rust-owned or deleted implementation lanes rather than preserving closed planning owners as excuses.
design: Every child must count success by Nu owner deletion, demotion out of the runtime path, or a lowered budget ceiling. Do not create Rust wrappers that merely hide the same Nu owner. Preserve real behavior through Rust tests, explicit specs, or documented stop conditions.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The next-wave budget map names exact file targets and updates owner beads away from closed epics. 2. Each large remaining Nu family has a concrete implementation lane or explicit no-go with a smaller surviving boundary. 3. The tracked Nu ceiling ratchets downward after each landed cut. 4. No feature or supported workflow is removed without explicit contract retirement. 5. The tranche ends with the budget materially closer to the 4,200 LOC hard floor.
notes: Created after closing yazelix-w6sz at 13,232 tracked Nu LOC / 87 files. Top remaining deletion targets by family: maintainer/dev 3,978 LOC, runtime_helpers 2,483 LOC, front_door_presentation 2,281 LOC, integration 1,328 LOC, setup/bootstrap 1,070 LOC, manual E2E/sweep 881 LOC, deterministic validators 639 LOC, session/desktop 572 LOC. First child lj7z.1 is the mandatory plan gate; implementation children lj7z.2-lj7z.10 are blocked by it so the next pass starts by resetting budgets and exact deletion targets.
close_reason: all steps complete

title: Define the second-wave Nu deletion map and reset closed budget owners
description: Plan-space gate for the 13.2k-to-4.2k implementation tranche. The current budget still points several families at closed owner epics, and the next cuts need exact file-level targets before implementation starts.
design: Inventory the 87 tracked Nu files from config_metadata/nushell_budget.toml. Classify each as delete, Rust-port, asset/static-data extraction, fixed shell survivor, or blocked. Update specs/budget notes so every family has a live owner bead and a measurable next ceiling.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The current 13,232 LOC budget is split into exact next-wave targets. 2. Closed owner beads in the budget/spec prose are replaced with live implementation owners or explicit historical references. 3. Each implementation child has a deletion budget and stop condition. 4. The cut order prioritizes largest honest deletion without losing runtime behavior.
notes:
Dependency decision for plan-space bead: no crates or code dependencies. This bead only resets budget ownership and names implementation deletion targets before code moves.
Completed the second-wave map. Added docs/specs/second_wave_nushell_deletion_map.md with file-level classifications for all 87 tracked Nu files and updated config_metadata/nushell_budget.toml so each budget family points at live lj7z implementation owners instead of closed epics. Updated the floor, ranked, runtime-shell, front-door, maintainer/validator, and spec inventory docs to use the new tranche.
close_reason: Completed: second-wave Nu deletion map and live budget owner reset are in place.

title: Split common.nu and constants.nu into Rust-owned facts, assets, and tiny shell survivors
description: common.nu and constants.nu remain broad catch-all modules used by many surfaces. Split the retained behavior into Rust-owned facts, static metadata/assets, or tiny shell primitives so new Nu code cannot keep importing a miscellaneous helper bag.
design: Treat catch-all helpers as architectural debt. Move platform/path/metadata constants to Rust or generated assets, inline one-off helpers where that deletes a shared dependency, and preserve only direct shell primitives that cannot move cleanly.
acceptance_criteria: 1. common.nu and constants.nu no longer act as general-purpose dependency hubs. 2. Callers use typed Rust facts/assets or local minimal helpers. 3. Any surviving helpers have explicit names and narrow ownership. 4. The runtime_helpers budget is lowered and future imports are discouraged by docs or validation.
close_reason: Completed

title: Rust-own the remaining validator and package-smoke probes
description: Delete or sharply demote the remaining 639 LOC deterministic-validator family: validate_flake_install.nu, validate_flake_interface.nu, validate_nixpkgs_package.nu, validate_nixpkgs_submission.nu, and validate_syntax.nu.
design: Port deterministic file parsing, flake interface checks, package metadata checks, and command construction to Rust. Keep only truly irreducible external Nix/Nu process probes, preferably as fixed argv execution owned by Rust or a tiny POSIX helper. Do not add broad wrapper debt.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The remaining validator family has a Rust-owned request/check surface or explicit per-file no-go. 2. Redundant Nu wrappers are deleted. 3. Any surviving Nu syntax/Nix smoke helper is fixed, tiny, and budgeted as shell execution only. 4. CI/pre-commit/docs point at the Rust owner or the narrowed survivor.
notes: Dependency decision before implementation: prefer std plus existing serde_json/toml in yzx_repo_validator. Add no crates unless flake/package parsing proves materially safer with a narrow parser crate. Keep Nix and Nu execution as fixed argv external probes owned by Rust; do not keep Nu wrapper owners.
close_reason: Completed: Rust-owned the flake interface, cold profile install, nixpkgs package/submission, and Nushell syntax validator surfaces; deleted the Nu validator/package-smoke cluster and moved CI/pre-commit/maintainer calls to yzx_repo_validator.

title: Replace the Nu maintainer test runner with a Rust nextest orchestrator
description: Delete the remaining Nu policy in maintainer/test_runner.nu and shrink yzx/dev.nu test dispatch. The default suite is already nextest-first and no longer has governed Nu tests, so Nu should not own suite orchestration.
design: Move fixed suite inventory loading, nextest argv construction, logging, profiling, and lint-only routing to a Rust maintainer command where it deletes Nu. Keep yzx dev as a thin public router only if it is still needed for shell entry.
acceptance_criteria: 1. maintainer/test_runner.nu is deleted or reduced to an explicitly budgeted shell trampoline. 2. yzx dev test continues to support default, lint-only, sweep, visual, all, profile, and new-window behavior or explicitly records a preserved split. 3. The default test path remains nextest-owned. 4. Tests verify the Rust command planning and failure reporting contracts.
notes: Dependency decision before implementation: use the existing Rust std/serde/toml stack in yazelix_core; add no new production crates. The runner should invoke fixed cargo/nextest/nix argv from a checked-in inventory and keep shell interaction at process-execution boundaries only. Reject Clap or a broad command framework for this private maintainer orchestrator until the public router migration has a separate go/no-go.
close_reason: Completed: deleted nushell/scripts/maintainer/test_runner.nu, moved default nextest orchestration/logging/profiling/syntax phase to yzx_repo_maintainer run-tests, and kept yzx dev test as a thin Nu handoff.

title: Rust-own maintainer release, update, version, and issue policy
description: Collapse the largest maintainer Nu policy cluster: update_workflow.nu, version_bump.nu, issue_sync.nu, issue_bead_contract.nu, repo_checkout.nu, plugin_build.nu, and adjacent yzx/dev.nu routing where Rust can own deterministic planning.
design: Separate external-tool execution from deterministic policy. Rust should own release/update plans, dirty-state checks, version edits, issue mapping validation, helper selection, and report formatting. Nu may temporarily retain fixed gh/bd/git/cargo/Nix process orchestration only when it is smaller than the Rust alternative.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Deterministic maintainer policy moves to Rust-owned commands with tests. 2. Large Nu maintainer files are deleted or demoted to fixed argv shell orchestration. 3. The public yzx dev commands keep behavior parity for update, bump, sync_issues, and build_pane_orchestrator. 4. The maintainer_and_dev_orchestration budget ceiling is ratcheted downward.
close_reason: Completed

title: Collapse the yzx_core bridge and runtime-helper Nu seam
description: Attack the runtime_helpers family around yzx_core_bridge.nu, common.nu, runtime_env.nu, startup_facts.nu, integration_facts.nu, transient_pane_facts.nu, config_files.nu, config_paths.nu, doctor_fix.nu, and terminal_launcher-adjacent helpers.
design: Do not preserve a general-purpose Nu bridge. Prefer direct Rust command invocations, typed request files, fixed argv helpers, or moving caller logic into Rust. Any surviving Nu helper must be a narrow shell/process primitive, not a config/state/report owner.
acceptance_criteria: 1. yzx_core_bridge.nu is deleted or reduced to the minimum transport seam with explicit callers. 2. Common/config/runtime facts helpers lose duplicated path, env, and report policy. 3. Rust owns deterministic config/state/request shaping. 4. The runtime_helpers budget ceiling drops materially.
close_reason: Completed

title: Rust-own terminal launch, startup profile, desktop, and session request assembly
description: Collapse launch/session Nu across terminal_launcher.nu, startup_profile.nu, core/launch_yazelix.nu, core/start_yazelix.nu, core/start_yazelix_inner.nu, yzx/desktop.nu, yzx/launch.nu, and core/yzx_session.nu where those files still assemble deterministic requests.
design: Keep direct shell entry, terminal process spawning, and visible host feedback only where genuinely irreducible. Move terminal request construction, desktop command planning, session-state request shaping, profile report formatting, and launch error envelopes to Rust when it deletes Nu owners.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Terminal/desktop/session request assembly is Rust-owned end to end. 2. Nu launch files shrink to fixed shell/process entrypoints or are deleted. 3. Startup profiling behavior and desktop launch visible feedback survive. 4. setup/session/runtime-helper budget ceilings ratchet downward.
close_reason: Completed

title: Collapse Zellij, Yazi, and managed-editor integration Nu into typed owners
description: Attack the 1,328 LOC integration family: integrations/zellij.nu, yazi.nu, managed_editor.nu, zellij_runtime_wrappers.nu, open_dir_in_pane.nu, zoxide_open_in_editor.nu, and zellij_wrappers/*.nu.
design: Prefer pane-orchestrator commands, Rust-owned workspace/session planning, and fixed wrapper entrypoints. Delete duplicate tab/session reads, sidebar/editor sync policy, transient pane contract shaping, and runtime-env glue from Nu. Preserve live pane behavior with direct regression coverage.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Integration state reads have one truth source. 2. Wrapper scripts are deleted or reduced to fixed argv entrypoints. 3. Zellij/Yazi/managed-editor behavior parity is defended by focused Rust or plugin tests. 4. The integration budget moves toward the 300 LOC target.
close_reason: Completed

title: Extract or Rust-own front-door presentation data and renderers
description: Collapse the 2,281 LOC front-door presentation family, especially ascii_art.nu, upgrade_summary.nu, setup/welcome.nu, yzx/screen.nu, yzx/menu.nu, yzx/popup.nu, yzx/edit.nu, yzx/import.nu, yzx/tutor.nu, and yzx/whats_new.nu.
design: Do not flatten the product experience. Move static art/copy/tables to assets or Rust data modules, move deterministic renderer selection and report shaping to Rust, and keep Nu only for direct terminal interaction or process handoff. Delete duplicate renderer branches before adding abstractions.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Large static data leaves Nu or is generated from Rust/assets. 2. ascii_art.nu is deleted or reduced to an irreducible terminal-renderer floor. 3. yzx screen/menu/popup/welcome behavior remains visually intentional. 4. The front_door_presentation budget ceiling drops materially.
close_reason: Completed

title: Port or delete shell-heavy E2E and sweep runners under a Rust harness
description: Collapse the manual_e2e_and_sweep_runners family plus adjacent dev/sweep helpers: config_sweep_runner.nu, upgrade_contract_e2e_runner.nu, upgrade_summary_e2e_runner.nu, stale_config_diagnostics_e2e_runner.nu, historical_upgrade_notes_e2e_runner.nu, and dev/sweep/*.nu.
design: Strong deterministic assertions should move to Rust nextest. Shell-heavy live probes should become small fixed external-runner requests or be demoted to manual tools with explicit non-governed status. Delete weak or duplicate runner coverage instead of porting noise.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Each E2E/sweep runner is classified as strong Rust-port, manual survivor, or delete. 2. Strong coverage lands in Rust nextest or a Rust-owned harness. 3. Weak duplicate runners are deleted. 4. Any surviving Nu runner has a narrow shell/TTY reason and a smaller budget.
close_reason: Completed

title: Split and trim status/cache tests
description: Split zellij_commands/status/tests.rs by cache IO, widget rendering, and agent usage refresh behavior, then delete weak tests that defend old broad structure instead of live status/cache contracts. After the split, thin status.rs adapters only where the test split makes behavior clear.
design: Delete-first status test diet. Keep tests that defend cache freshness, provider timeout/backoff, status bus decoding, and visible widget output. Delete command-surface or parser trivia covered by stronger behavior tests.
acceptance_criteria: status tests are split into domain modules; weak duplicate cases are deleted or explicitly rejected with evidence; status.rs adapters are thinner where safe; focused status tests pass; Rust budget and inventory are updated; LOC delta and rejected candidates are recorded.
notes: Implementation: replaced the single 1723-line status/tests.rs with domain modules tests/widgets.rs, tests/cache.rs, and tests/agent_usage.rs plus a small mod.rs. Deleted two weak duplicate cases: missing_status_cache_file_renders_no_widget_segment and status_bus_workspace_widget_formats_fixture_payload; stronger cache round-trip and zjstatus plain-text regressions still cover live behavior. Rejected status.rs adapter changes in this slice: after the split, the remaining adapter duplication is command parsing/help surface, and introducing a generic parser would be abstraction debt without deleting behavior. Verification: cargo test -p yazelix_core zellij_commands::status passed 30 tests; validate-rust-ownership-budget passed with existing hard-target warning; git diff --check passed. LOC: status test surface 1723 -> 1711 raw lines, budget total 72676 -> 72664 with +3 test-domain files.
close_reason: Completed: split status tests by domain, deleted weak duplicate cases, verified focused status tests, and updated Rust budget/inventory.

title: Measure long-running Yazelix battery and resource usage
description: Design and run a repeatable measurement pass for Yazelix battery impact over time on first-class terminals, especially Ghostty and WezTerm, under realistic idle and active workspace scenarios.
design: Measure from first principles: Yazelix is a terminal, Zellij, Yazi/sidebar, status bar, plugins, shell, and optional visual features running together. The first pass should separate idle cost from active use, compare against useful baselines such as plain terminal shell and plain Zellij, and record CPU, wakeups, process tree, memory, and available battery/power data. Treat this as a manual/laptop validation lane rather than default CI, because VM or plugged-in CI power data is not meaningful.
acceptance_criteria: A maintainer can reproduce the measurement with documented commands and scenarios. Results include at least plain terminal baseline, plain Zellij baseline, Yazelix idle, Yazelix with Yazi/sidebar visible, and a representative active workflow over a long enough window to smooth startup noise. The bead records Ghostty and WezTerm results or explains why one could not be measured. The output identifies any dominant power consumers and creates follow-up beads only for concrete regressions or high-impact optimizations.
notes: 2026-05-22 Frankenterm evaluation from yazelix-lytcv: include any future Yazelix agent watcher, command-center observer, or pane-output capture loop in this battery/resource measurement lane before making it default. Long-running observation can be valuable, but Yazelix should prove idle CPU, wakeups, memory, and battery impact against plain terminal and plain Zellij baselines first.

title: Collapse the remaining helper-transport and runtime-helper Nushell toward the provable irreducible floor
description: After the larger owner cuts, the remaining generic runtime Nu infrastructure is concentrated in yzx_core_bridge.nu plus shared runtime helpers like common.nu and version_info.nu. Re-cut this cluster so anything deterministic or data-shaped leaves Nu and only a provably irreducible shell/process remainder survives.
design: Classify each exported helper as delete, Rust-own, POSIX/helper-own, or provably irreducible shell/process code. The goal is to kill generic helper transport where possible, not merely document it.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The smallest surviving bridge/helper boundary is explicit. 2. yzx_core_bridge.nu is targeted for deletion or near-elimination, not passive shrinkage. 3. At least one shared helper disappears or materially shrinks. 4. Any surviving exported helper has explicit proof of irreducibility. 5. No new generic bridge layer is added.
close_reason: all steps complete

title: Define the minimum surviving transport and shared runtime-helper boundary
description: Audit yzx_core_bridge.nu, common.nu, and version_info.nu and classify which exports are honest shell/process owners and which are still deterministic helper policy that can move or die.
design: Be explicit about display-only and user-facing text helpers versus machine-owned request shaping, metadata, and path policy.
acceptance_criteria: 1. A keep-versus-cut table exists for the bridge/helper cluster. 2. The minimum surviving Nu seam is explicit. 3. At least one implementation-ready deletion lane falls out of the audit.
close_reason: Completed

title: Collapse yzx_core_bridge.nu to the minimum surviving transport seam
description: Delete redundant policy, error shaping, and per-command helper logic from yzx_core_bridge.nu so only the irreducible helper transport remains.
design: If the bridge cannot shrink further without moving shell/process work into Rust, record the explicit stop condition instead of hiding it.
acceptance_criteria: 1. yzx_core_bridge.nu materially shrinks or gets a precise stop note. 2. No per-domain policy is reintroduced around Rust helpers. 3. Existing helper callers stay correct.
notes: Dependency decision: add no new crates. Reuse existing yzx_core / yzx_control binaries and keep remaining transport in-house. Success means deleting duplicated Nu-side bridge logic, not adding another wrapper layer.
close_reason: Completed

title: Implement the shared runtime-helper cut or record explicit stop conditions
description: Apply the audited shared runtime-helper cuts to common.nu and version_info.nu where the logic is deterministic enough to leave Nushell, and record explicit no-go boundaries where the helper is still honestly shell-bound or display-only.
design: Delete first. If a helper is mostly data or deterministic path policy, it should not stay Nu by habit.
acceptance_criteria: 1. At least one shared runtime helper owner such as common.nu or version_info.nu shrinks materially or is deleted. 2. Surviving helpers have explicit stop conditions. 3. No fake Rust wrapper is added for shell-bound or display-only logic.
notes: Deleted dead common.nu helpers get_materialized_state_path plus extra Helix directory wrappers. Recorded explicit stop conditions in docs/specs/runtime_shell_floor_budgets.md: runtime/config/state root discovery, external command discovery, and resolve_yazelix_nu_bin stay Nu-owned only while startup, launch, and import still consume them as shell/process owners; do not add Rust wrappers for those seams.
close_reason: Completed

title: Delete stale version_info.nu and its stale inventory/docs surface if no live callers remain
description: version_info.nu appears to be stale now that status/version collection lives in yzx_control. If no live callers remain, delete the file and any stale README or inventory references instead of carrying dead Nu code.
design: This is a delete-first cleanup bead, not a rewrite. Verify live callers first, then delete the dead surface.
acceptance_criteria: 1. version_info.nu has no live runtime callers or a documented stop condition if one appears. 2. The stale Nu file is deleted when safe. 3. Any stale README or inventory references are cleaned up. 4. No feature-bearing version path regresses.
close_reason: Completed

title: Evaluate standalone Ghostty cursor preset distribution
description:
Evaluate whether Yazelix cursor presets should be packaged for standalone Ghostty users, with an eventual extraction path to a separate repo if the boundary is clean

## Context
The new yazelix_cursors.toml sidecar makes Ghostty cursor themes more product-like and less tied to the full Yazelix runtime
Standalone Ghostty users may benefit from the presets without adopting Yazelix
