The evaluation should decide whether this belongs as docs, an installable artifact, a flake output, or a separate repository
design:
Start delete-first: identify what parts are Yazelix-specific and what can be treated as generic Ghostty cursor assets
Prefer a small distribution contract before adding automation
Do not split a repository until maintenance ownership, versioning, and update flow are explicit
acceptance_criteria:
Decision records the target audience and supported install path
Decision compares in-repo export, flake output, and separate repository options
Decision defines which cursor assets/config are generic enough to publish
Decision names the maintenance and release boundary if extraction proceeds
notes: Decision recorded in docs/specs/standalone_ghostty_cursor_distribution.md. Recommendation: expose complete Ghostty cursor shader assets through an in-repo flake/package export first, keep yazelix_cursors.toml/random reroll/Kitty fallback/Yazelix materialization as Yazelix-owned, and defer a separate repository until the export boundary and release ownership prove stable. Follow-ups: yazelix-lo9h.1 and yazelix-lo9h.2.
close_reason: Completed

title: Add in-repo Ghostty cursor shader export package
description: Add the first supported standalone Ghostty cursor distribution path inside the Yazelix flake/package surface.
design: Package complete generated Ghostty cursor shader files plus example config snippets under a stable share path. Do not mutate user Ghostty configs and do not export Yazelix runtime reroll or yzx cursor sidecar behavior.
acceptance_criteria: A Nix user can install or build the cursor asset export; the installed output contains complete shader files and examples; docs show the custom-shader path; validation confirms exported shader files exist.
close_reason: Completed: added .#ghostty_cursor_shaders as an in-repo flake package that generates standalone Ghostty cursor shader assets from the Yazelix cursor materialization path, ships example custom-shader config, and documents the canonical contract. Verified with nix build .#ghostty_cursor_shaders and validate-contracts.

title: Extract yazelix_cursors for Ghostty users
description: Re-evaluate extracting Yazelix cursor presets into a standalone Ghostty-focused distribution. Selected name: yazelix_cursors. Alternatives: yazelix_ghostty_cursors, ghostty_yazelix_cursors.
design: Target users: Ghostty users who want Yazelix cursor trails/effects without the full Yazelix runtime. Initial scope is Ghostty only, because that is where the cursor value is concrete and shippable today. Included scope: cursor preset metadata, shader/assets, Ghostty config snippets, preview docs/screenshots, versioned releases, license/provenance notes, and a clean import path back into Yazelix. Excluded scope: multi-terminal cursor abstraction, shell integration, and editor/workspace behavior. Multi-terminal support can be a later follow-up only after Ghostty extraction proves demand and maintenance cost.
acceptance_criteria: Decision either keeps cursors in-repo or opens an extraction implementation with selected name plus alternatives, Ghostty-only support contract, release/version policy, install docs, asset provenance, Yazelix consumption boundary, migration plan, and criteria for adding non-Ghostty terminals later.
notes: Completed decision and first extraction surface: selected #yazelix_cursors as the primary standalone Ghostty cursor package, kept #ghostty_cursor_shaders as a compatibility flake attribute for the same output, moved packaging to packaging/yazelix_cursors.nix, updated the standalone cursor contract with release policy, provenance, Yazelix consumption boundary, and non-Ghostty criteria. Verification: nix build .#yazelix_cursors --no-link; nix build .#ghostty_cursor_shaders --no-link; nix eval both package names; yzx_repo_validator validate-contracts; git diff --check.
close_reason: Completed: selected and shipped #yazelix_cursors as the branded standalone Ghostty cursor package, preserved #ghostty_cursor_shaders compatibility, and documented the release/support boundary.

title: Split cursor registry and generator behind an extraction-ready boundary
description: Prepare the next step toward moving yazelix_cursors out of the main repo by separating the reusable cursor preset registry and Ghostty shader generation boundary from Yazelix settings/runtime integration. The current standalone #yazelix_cursors package is useful, but yzx cursors and the registry still read settings.jsonc, feed Ghostty materialization, and interact with Yazelix runtime/status-widget state. This bead should create a smaller internal boundary before any separate repository move.
design: Do not move code to another repo in this bead. First isolate the reusable cursor package concerns from Yazelix-specific integration. Candidate split: reusable cursor registry model, preset validation, shader/effect generation, exported package metadata, and Ghostty example generation on one side; settings.jsonc loading, config UI metadata, runtime random selection, status-widget facts, terminal materialization orchestration, and yzx cursors UX on the Yazelix side. Prefer an internal crate or sharply separated module only if it reduces coupling without forcing premature public API polish.
acceptance_criteria: The cursor registry/generator ownership boundary is documented and implemented or explicitly scoped as the next implementation slice. Reusable code needed by #yazelix_cursors is distinguishable from Yazelix-only config/runtime code. yzx cursors remains in the main repo unless a smaller stable command API is defined. Existing #yazelix_cursors and #ghostty_cursor_shaders builds still pass. The result records whether a separate repo is now justified or what coupling remains.
notes:
Rust dependency decision before implementation: no new production or dev crates. Use existing serde/serde_json/toml and std only. Build in-house because this is an ownership-boundary refactor around existing Yazelix cursor schema and Ghostty shader generation, not a new parsing or rendering domain. Rejected alternatives: introduce a public crate now, or add a shader/template crate; both would force API polish before the reusable/Yazelix-specific split is clear. Packaging impact should remain the same: #yazelix_cursors and #ghostty_cursor_shaders continue to build from the existing Rust helper.
Implemented internal extraction-ready boundary. Added rust_core/yazelix_core/src/yazelix_cursors.rs for reusable cursor registry parsing/validation/resolution and Ghostty palette shader generation. Reduced ghostty_cursor_registry.rs to the Yazelix-owned settings.jsonc/config-path loader and compatibility re-export. ghostty_materialization.rs now calls the reusable shader generator instead of owning data-driven cursor shader rendering. Updated the standalone cursor contract, Rust inventory, and ownership budget. Validation also required normalizing two existing Rust test traceability markers/attribute order so the traceability gate passes. Verification: cargo fmt --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml --all --check; cargo check --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core --all-targets; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core yazelix_cursors::tests; yzx_repo_validator validate-rust-ownership-budget; yzx_repo_validator validate-contracts; yzx_repo_validator validate-rust-test-traceability; nix build .#yazelix_cursors --no-link; nix build .#ghostty_cursor_shaders --no-link; git diff --check. Nix still emits the local FlakeHub 401 warning before continuing.
close_reason: Completed: split reusable cursor registry and Ghostty palette generator into yazelix_cursors.rs, leaving ghostty_cursor_registry.rs as the Yazelix settings adapter, with standalone cursor package builds verified.

title: Move cursor settings to shared yazelix_cursors config
description: Decide and implement the future config ownership split where standalone yzc/yazelix_cursors and integrated Yazelix share one cursor settings source at ~/.config/yazelix_cursors/settings.jsonc instead of keeping cursor settings embedded inside ~/.config/yazelix/settings.jsonc forever. The current config UI reduces the UX harm because Yazelix can still expose a cursors tab while editing a sidecar-owned config.
design: Target shape: standalone binary yzc owns ~/.config/yazelix_cursors/settings.jsonc and generates Ghostty include output; Yazelix keeps ~/.config/yazelix/settings.jsonc for workspace settings and treats the config UI cursors tab as an external/sidecar-owned editor for ~/.config/yazelix_cursors/settings.jsonc. New Yazelix installs should create/read the shared cursor config once the yzc surface exists. Existing settings.jsonc.cursors should auto-migrate once to the shared config, then become a hard error if it reappears after the migration window. Home Manager should be able to own Yazelix config, cursor config, or both explicitly. Do not make Yazelix silently depend on a missing external config: bootstrap it or fail with a direct repair action.
acceptance_criteria: Decision records whether yzc or Yazelix initializes ~/.config/yazelix_cursors/settings.jsonc, the exact migration path from settings.jsonc.cursors, hard-error behavior for stale embedded cursor settings, config UI ownership/status copy, Home Manager ownership mode, and how Yazelix consumes the shared cursor library or command API. Implementation, when done, preserves the cursors tab in yzx config ui, keeps first launch self-healing, validates #yazelix_cursors builds, and documents standalone Ghostty usage with a Ghostty config-file include snippet.
notes: Implemented decision: Yazelix and standalone yzc now share ~/.config/yazelix_cursors/settings.jsonc as the cursor source. Yazelix initializes it during settings bootstrap from old cursors.toml/user_configs/yazelix_cursors.toml or the shipped cursor defaults. Main ~/.config/yazelix/settings.jsonc no longer embeds cursors; existing embedded settings.jsonc.cursors migrates once into the shared cursor file and is removed from the main file. If embedded cursors reappear after the sidecar exists, startup hard-errors with embedded_cursor_settings_after_sidecar. Config UI keeps a cursors tab by composing the shared cursor file into the UI model and routes cursor edits to the sidecar. yzx config set/unset routes cursors.* paths to the same sidecar. Home Manager owns main config via manage_config and cursor config independently via manage_cursor_config. Yazelix consumes the shared library boundary directly through CursorRegistry/load/render helpers, while yzc uses the same renderer for standalone Ghostty config generation.
close_reason: Completed: moved cursor settings to shared yazelix_cursors settings.jsonc, preserved config UI cursors tab, added cursor-sidecar yzx config set/unset routing, split Home Manager ownership, updated docs, and verified Rust/config/Nix gates.

title: Add minimal yzc CLI for standalone Yazelix cursors
description: Define and implement the first standalone yzc command surface for Yazelix cursor presets. The CLI owns ~/.config/yazelix_cursors/settings.jsonc, can initialize/list/inspect the standalone cursor config, and can generate a Ghostty include file for users who want cursor presets without installing full Yazelix.
design: Command surface: yzc init, yzc list, yzc inspect, and yzc generate ghostty. Default config directory is $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/yazelix_cursors or ~/.config/yazelix_cursors. Default config path is settings.jsonc. Default generated Ghostty include is ghostty.conf in the same directory. The #yazelix_cursors package exposes bin/yzc and installs reusable shader assets under share/yazelix/yazelix_cursors. Local verification can use a temporary config directory rather than mutating the maintainer real config.
acceptance_criteria: Bead records crate-vs-in-house dependency decision before Rust work. yzc init creates settings.jsonc without overwriting an existing file. yzc list prints configured cursor presets. yzc inspect reports config/output/share paths and current selected cursor. yzc generate ghostty writes ~/.config/yazelix_cursors/ghostty.conf using the standalone config and packaged shader assets. #yazelix_cursors exposes bin/yzc. Docs mention the standalone Ghostty include flow. Focused Rust/package checks pass, then this bead is closed and committed.
notes:
Rust dependency decision: no new production or dev-only crates for this slice. Reuse the existing yazelix_core cursor registry/generator modules, existing serde/jsonc support already in the crate, and std filesystem/path handling. Build the CLI argument parser in-house because the first yzc command surface is small and adding clap or another framework would increase packaging surface before extraction. Packaging impact: #yazelix_cursors must copy bin/yzc into its output and keep shader assets in share/yazelix/yazelix_cursors for runtime generation.
Implemented minimal yzc CLI as rust_core/yazelix_core/src/bin/yzc.rs. Added #yzc and #yazelix_cursors flake apps, copied bin/yzc into the #yazelix_cursors package, and documented the standalone Ghostty include flow. Verification passed: yzx dev rust fmt --check, yzx dev rust check, cargo check --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core --all-targets, nix build .#yazelix_cursors, nix build .#ghostty_cursor_shaders, nix run .#yzc -- --help, package-installed yzc --config-dir <tmp> init, and package-installed yzc --config-dir <tmp> generate ghostty. validate-rust-ownership-budget passed with the existing long-term 60k LOC warning. validate-contracts passed. validate-flake-interface was stopped after hanging in nix eval; direct nix build/nix run covered the changed flake surfaces.
Additional focused verification: cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core yazelix_cursors passed 12 cursor-registry/shader tests.
close_reason: Completed minimal yzc CLI, package/app wiring, docs, and verification

title: Adopt Spartan LOC protocol for refactor and extraction beads
description: Yazelix child-repo extraction and refactor work kept producing adapter, validator, docs, fixture, and packaging growth in the main repo. Adopt a stricter protocol so extraction/refactor beads are judged by main-repo ownership reduction, not by movement or activity.
design: Record the operational rule in AGENTS.md so future agents see it, and the detailed measurement rule in docs/loc_extraction_scorecard.md. Require scorecard evidence, explicit budget debt for accepted growth, payback beads for meaningful growth, same-bead deletion of stale scaffolding where safe, and budget ceiling ratchets after shrinkage.
acceptance_criteria: AGENTS.md names the Spartan LOC protocol; docs/loc_extraction_scorecard.md defines labels and close criteria; rust_code_inventory no longer encourages delaying weak local test/fixture deletion after extraction; a payback bead exists for the current accepted Rust budget debt.
close_reason: Completed: added the Spartan LOC protocol to AGENTS.md and docs/loc_extraction_scorecard.md, tightened rust_code_inventory extraction guidance, and created yazelix-lopk.1 to pay down the accepted post-v16.3 Rust budget debt.

title: Pay down accepted post-v16.3 Rust budget debt
description: The optional runtime component toggle slice was accepted product behavior but raised the Rust budget by 601 raw Rust lines and +650 tokei code LOC over the previous commit. Under the Spartan LOC protocol, this growth needs an explicit payback owner instead of being normalized as the new baseline.
design: Start from the largest current main-repo Rust owners in docs/rust_code_inventory.md, especially config_ui.rs, launch_commands.rs, zellij_commands.rs, zellij_materialization.rs, and maintainer validators. Prefer deletion of stale scaffolding, generated mirrors, weak tests, duplicate validators, and wrappers before moving code. The target is a real main-repo reduction, not a child-repo move that leaves adapters or validators behind.
acceptance_criteria: Reduce main-repo Rust code LOC by at least the accepted +650 tokei-code debt, or record exactly why less was safe. Update config_metadata/rust_ownership_budget.toml and docs/rust_code_inventory.md to ratchet ceilings down after the reduction. Close with a LOC scorecard and validate-rust-ownership-budget.
close_reason: Completed: deleted the hidden moved-Ghostty cursor-field runtime repair migration and its repair tests; repair now uses the existing strict stale-config diagnostics. Removed structured strength-score parsing, minimum enforcement, removal-absence heuristics, and their fixture tests from the maintainer validator; strength scoring is now review guidance instead of mechanical metadata. Updated the Spartan LOC Protocol to treat transitional migrations/compatibility shims as deletion debt, refreshed test-governance docs, and ratcheted the Rust budget/inventory from 76,285 to 75,231 tracked raw Rust lines. LOC scorecard: raw diff excluding .beads is 37 insertions / 1,113 deletions; tokei Rust code went 69,599 -> 68,591 (-1,008), paying back the required 650 LOC debt. Verification: cargo fmt --all --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml --check; cargo check --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core -p yazelix_maintainer --all-targets; cargo test -p yazelix_core runtime_materialization_repair; cargo test -p yazelix_core config_normalize_reports_moved_ghostty_cursor_fields; cargo test -p yazelix_maintainer repo_validation; validate-rust-test-traceability; validate-rust-ownership-budget; validate-contracts; validate-docs-experience; validate-package-rust-test-purity.

title: Add fast Rust inner-loop check commands
description: Current Yazelix Rust edits are too often verified through `nix develop -c ...`, which can realize dev-shell/package derivations before running simple `cargo fmt` or focused tests. Add a maintainer workflow surface for fast Rust inner-loop checks that runs Cargo directly from an already-prepared environment, keeps Nix package validation as an explicit final gate, and documents when to use each path.
acceptance_criteria: A maintainer command or documented workflow exists for fast Rust fmt/check/test without re-entering `nix develop` each time; package/Home Manager validation remains a separate explicit gate; AGENTS/docs explain the intended inner-loop vs package-gate split; verification demonstrates the fast path on a focused Rust test.
notes: Local maintainer setup now installs cargo, rustc, rustfmt, clippy, and rust-analyzer through /home/lucca/.config/home-manager/home.nix. Direct cargo fmt --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml --all -- --check works without nix develop and completed in under a second on this host. Repo task still remains open: add a Yazelix-maintained fast command/workflow plus docs/AGENTS guidance, and keep nix build/Home Manager validation as the explicit package gate.
close_reason: Completed: added direct yzx dev rust fmt/check/test commands, documented fast inner-loop usage, and verified focused Rust tests/checks run without entering nix develop.

title: Simplify config UI takes-effect labels
description: Use the user-facing takes effect vocabulary in the config UI: now, after pane reopen, after Yazelix restart, and after Home Manager switch. Keep internal apply modes distinct, but stop exposing implementation labels like gen refresh/tab restart in normal rows.
acceptance_criteria: Settings list column is named takes effect. Normal row labels use the agreed option-1 vocabulary. Detail/save copy no longer promises a fresh tab when the real boundary is a fresh Yazelix window. Focused tests cover labels and headers.
close_reason: Implemented takes effect config UI vocabulary, mapped generated Yazi settings to pane reopen and Zellij/window-scoped settings to Yazelix restart, removed stale tab/session wording, and verified with full yazelix_core tests.

title: Evaluate Ghostty package source policy
description: Evaluate which Ghostty package source Yazelix should prefer for its managed/default terminal path once the current integration bugs are fixed.
design: Compare the three realistic Ghostty package sources separately instead of treating 'Ghostty' as one blob: nixpkgs Ghostty on Linux, the upstream Ghostty flake, and system Ghostty / Homebrew on macOS. Judge them on stability, platform coverage, integration burden, update cadence, and whether they improve or complicate Yazelix's managed launch path. Do not let this policy work blur into the current Ghostty runtime/shader bug fixes.
acceptance_criteria: There is a clear Ghostty package-source policy for Yazelix covering nixpkgs Ghostty, the upstream Ghostty flake, and system/Homebrew Ghostty on macOS. The chosen default(s) and any experimental/override path are explicit, with reasoning tied to platform support and maintenance cost.
notes: Decision on 2026-04-01: Yazelix should not switch its default managed Ghostty source from nixpkgs to the upstream Ghostty flake. The upstream flake is explicitly the tip/dev path, not the regular-user package path, and it carries the same non-NixOS OpenGL caveat. Recommended policy shape: macOS should continue to use system/Homebrew Ghostty; NixOS can continue to treat nixpkgs Ghostty as the normal managed package path; non-NixOS Linux should stop assuming nixpkgs Ghostty is a friction-free default and should likely prefer another default terminal or a system Ghostty path unless the user explicitly opts into the Nix-managed Ghostty path and its GPU/driver caveats.
close_reason: Completed

title: Step 2: delete unnecessary parts or processes first
description: For candidate refactors under `yazelix-790`, delete dead paths, redundant layers, convenience wrappers, or obsolete processes before introducing new abstractions. The goal is to remove what should not survive instead of reorganizing it.
notes:
2026-04-08 third concrete deletion landed:
- Deleted `doctor.nu`'s standalone shell-integration check after confirming the real install-artifact and shell-hook diagnostics already covered the supported contract.
- Step 2 now has three concrete wins: dead bootstrap path deleted, dead zellij debug exports deleted, and overlapping doctor warning deleted.

2026-04-08 next delete-first candidates from the hotspot scan:
- `common.nu`: generic aliases and unrelated maintainer/build helpers living beside runtime/path ownership.
- `config_migrations.nu`: overlap between rule metadata ownership and preview/render/apply behavior.
- `integrations/yazi.nu`: editor-open routing and file-backed sidebar identity scanning mixed into one integration layer.

2026-04-08 fourth concrete deletion landed:
- Removed `common.nu`'s generic runtime aliases (`get_yazelix_dir`, `require_yazelix_dir`), two dead exports (`require_yazelix_config_dir`, `is_hx_running`), and two exports that only served internal self-use (`get_yazelix_runtime_project_dir`, `get_yazelix_repo_root`).
- This confirms the common.nu bead should keep preferring surface deletion and caller explicitness before any new module extraction.

2026-04-08 fifth concrete delete-first result landed:
- After the first alias cut, `common.nu` no longer needed to own build policy or runtime-project ownership. Those families moved into `build_policy.nu` and `runtime_project.nu`, and the dead runtime-project internals were deleted from `common.nu`.
- This kept the refactor aligned with step 2: remove mixed ownership from the old grab-bag module instead of merely reorganizing it under a new generic helper layer.
close_reason: Completed

title: Evaluate Frankenterm for Yazelix agent orchestration
description: Evaluate whether Frankenterm should influence Yazelix's terminal, agent-pane, and orchestration strategy. Frankenterm is a Rust terminal hypervisor for AI agent swarms built around WezTerm pane capture, state-machine/pattern detection, policy-gated control, and JSON/robot APIs.
design: Do not treat this as an implementation bead. Compare four outcomes: adopt Frankenterm directly, add an optional Frankenterm integration, borrow specific design ideas, or reject it for Yazelix. Evaluate it against Yazelix's current product shape: Zellij remains the workspace/multiplexer, Ghostty and WezTerm are first-class terminals, the agent pane should be user-friendly rather than swarm-only, and Yazelix should avoid growing a huge orchestration subsystem unless the value is decisive. Pay special attention to the WezTerm-only mux boundary, interaction or conflict with Zellij, Ghostty implications, Nix packaging cost, license/rider fit, security of agent-to-agent control, resource and battery impact for long-running watchers, and whether any Frankenterm ideas should instead become small Yazelix-native diagnostics or agent APIs.
acceptance_criteria: The evaluation records a recommendation among: direct adoption, optional integration, idea borrowing, or rejection. It compares Frankenterm with Yazelix's existing Zellij-centered model, the yazelix-terminal research path, and the planned role/placement agent model. It identifies which Frankenterm capabilities are genuinely useful for Yazelix, which conflict with Zellij/Ghostty/default-user ergonomics, and which are too large or risky. It includes a packaging/license/security/resource assessment and creates narrower follow-up beads only for concrete accepted slices.
notes:
Decision, 2026-05-22: borrow ideas only. Do not adopt Frankenterm directly and do not add an optional Frankenterm integration for the default Yazelix product.

Evidence inspected:
- Repo metadata: https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/frankenterm is Rust, recently active, and describes itself as a terminal hypervisor for AI agent swarms across WezTerm.
- README claims: Frankenterm captures terminal output across panes, detects state transitions, runs workflows, exposes Robot Mode/MCP/TOON APIs, and is designed for dozens to hundreds of concurrent AI coding agents.
- Backend boundary: the README explicitly says pane/session operations require WezTerm CLI and a reachable mux/GUI, and its "Why a WezTerm Fork" section says it intentionally does not abstract over Zellij or other muxes because mux semantics are not interchangeable.
- Code/package shape: Cargo workspace lists 77 members, including many vendored WezTerm-derived crates, GUI/mux-server crates, policy/resource/search/replay/MCP crates, fuzz, and perf gates. There is no simple flake.nix at repo root in the inspected tree; installation is cargo-install or custom install.sh centered.
- License: LICENSE is "MIT License (with OpenAI/Anthropic Rider)" and explicitly grants no rights to OpenAI, Anthropic, affiliates, or people/entities acting on their behalf. That is a hard incompatibility for normal Yazelix dependency or vendoring decisions.
- Security model: SECURITY.md acknowledges repo-clone attacker risk, MCP/input trust boundaries, raw pane bytes in the SQLite store, workflow trigger risk, and a local-user trust model.
- Resource model: README claims idle watcher CPU below 1%, around 50 MB watcher memory for 100 panes, and about 50 microseconds per pane per tick when idle, but those claims are for Frankenterm's own fleet model and should not be imported into Yazelix without measurement.

Recommendation:
Reject direct adoption and optional runtime integration. Frankenterm solves a different problem: large WezTerm-centered agent fleets with full capture/search/workflow/control. Yazelix's current architecture is Zellij-centered, Ghostty and WezTerm are both first-class terminals, and Zellij owns panes/tabs/layouts. Adopting Frankenterm would add a second mux/control plane, conflict with the Ghostty-first daily-driver path, massively increase packaging/build surface, and pull in a license rider that is not acceptable for a general Yazelix dependency.

What is useful for Yazelix:
- passive-first observation before action
- structured JSON state surfaces for agents/tools
- condition-based waits instead of sleeps
- explicit policy gates for mutating agent actions
- redaction/privacy boundaries for any captured text
- resource/battery measurement before watchers become default

What Yazelix should not borrow now:
- full scrollback capture and FTS indexing across panes
- sending arbitrary input to agents as a default product feature
- MCP/Robot Mode fleet control before a smaller local command-center contract exists
- WezTerm-only mux assumptions
- vendored terminal/mux fork architecture
- asupersync/runtime-doctrine or huge subsystem import

Accepted follow-up:
Created yazelix-bl3.2 under yazelix-bl3 to define a minimal Yazelix-native agent command-center observation contract. That should borrow the passive-first and policy-gated ideas without adopting Frankenterm or introducing a terminal-specific observer daemon.

Effect on related beads:
- yazelix-zkxw: Frankenterm should not be treated as a strong yazelix-terminal base. It is a WezTerm fork plus fleet platform, not a small terminal base aligned with Zellij-as-mux.
- yazelix-lnhg7: any future watcher/agent-observation feature should be included in the battery/resource measurement lane before it becomes default.
close_reason: Completed: evaluated Frankenterm and chose borrow-ideas-only. Rejected direct adoption and optional integration due to WezTerm-only mux boundary, conflict with Yazelix's Zellij/Ghostty model, very large vendored/fleet-platform scope, packaging cost, and license rider incompatibility. Created yazelix-bl3.2 for the accepted minimal Yazelix-native agent observation contract.

title: Allow users to rebind native Zellij Ctrl+n resize key
description: GitHub issue #590 reports that remapping Zellij Resize from Ctrl+n to Ctrl+Alt+n still leaves Ctrl+n active inside Yazelix. Investigation found Yazelix reasserts native Zellij mode leader defaults in configs/zellij/yazelix_overrides.kdl after user keybinds, turning copied defaults into Yazelix-owned overrides.
design: Do not make native Zellij mode leaders semantic zellij.keybindings actions. Remove copied native Zellij default mode enter/exit binds from the Yazelix override layer so Zellij/user config owns them. Keep only intentional Yazelix conflict-remap policy.
acceptance_criteria: Yazelix overrides no longer inject Ctrl+n Resize/Normal bindings. Copied native mode defaults such as Ctrl+p/Ctrl+t/Ctrl+h/Ctrl+q are removed from the override layer. Intentional Ctrl+o/Ctrl+s/Ctrl+g conflict remaps still render. Focused regression covers the Ctrl+n materialization path.
notes: Implementation started after confirming root cause: Yazelix override file reasserts copied native Zellij mode leader defaults after user config, including Ctrl+n resize.
close_reason: Completed. Removed copied native Zellij mode leader binds from configs/zellij/yazelix_overrides.kdl so Ctrl+n/Ctrl+p/Ctrl+t/Ctrl+h/Ctrl+q stay Zellij/user-owned instead of Yazelix-reasserted. Simplified Ctrl+o/Ctrl+s remaps to the specific session/scroll mode blocks and kept intentional Ctrl+Alt conflict policy. Added focused materialization regression for the Ctrl+n remap path. Verification: cargo test -p yazelix_core managed_zellij_remap_can_remove_ctrl_n_resize_leader; cargo check -p yazelix_core; cargo fmt --check --all; git diff --check; rg absence check for copied native leaders. LOC scorecard HEAD..worktree: raw runtime net -1, tokei code +44 from regression test.

title: Managed popup pane lifecycle: toggle, focus, reuse, no duplicates
description: Move transient popup-pane lifecycle into a dedicated popup runner plus a small shell toggle wrapper so popup actions behave like managed session state rather than disposable launches. The popup key should toggle a single managed popup pane: open it if missing, focus it if present but unfocused, and close it if focused. Repeated presses must not spawn duplicate floating panes.
design: Keep this scoped to one managed popup identity first, backed by the existing configurable popup_program. Use the shell-side yzx popup path for creation because it is the proven reliable floating-pane launcher. Let the popup runner own discovery, focus, close, and reuse semantics so shell glue does not have to infer floating-pane state from raw Zellij output. Preserve the current configurable popup command surface, but make the lifecycle stateful and session-aware without prematurely generalizing to multiple popup types.
acceptance_criteria:
Pressing the popup key opens the configured popup program in one managed floating pane when none exists.
Pressing it again focuses the existing popup if it exists but is not focused, and closes it if it is focused.
Repeated presses do not create duplicate floating panes.
Add regression coverage at the popup-runner/plugin seam for popup discovery and toggle behavior.
Add an end-to-end Yazelix script test that verifies no duplicate popup panes are created across repeated toggles.
close_reason: Implemented managed popup lifecycle with hybrid shell/plugin toggle flow

title: Audit Yazi integration as a module boundary
description: Review the Yazi integration surface as a future module boundary, with the explicit goal of deciding what could become a reusable Yazi config/plugin pack and what must remain Yazelix editor/sidebar orchestration.
design: Start from yazi_materialization.rs, Yazi user config generation, bundled plugins, sidebar-state behavior, semantic keybindings, reveal/open flows, and editor retargeting. Classify each responsibility as generic Yazi config pack, Yazelix-managed sidebar/editor integration, pane-orchestrator protocol, Home Manager/runtime packaging, or obsolete glue. Do not extract Yazi before the higher-value bar/config/Zellij materialization passes unless this audit finds a low-risk deletion path. Prefer deleting duplicated config generation or transitional wrappers before designing a public child repo.
acceptance_criteria: The audit produces a boundary matrix for Yazi integration, identifies deletion candidates, and states whether a future child repo is justified, deferred, or rejected. It names the exact code/doc/test surfaces that would move or remain, and it records a LOC scorecard estimate for any accepted follow-up.
notes: 2026-05-07 completed audit: Yazi public extraction is deferred. Boundary matrix recorded in docs/contracts/yazi_integration_boundary.md. Classification: yazi_render_plan.rs and config_metadata/yazi_render_plan.toml are the generic config-pack candidates; bundled flavors and vendored upstream plugins are the large asset-pack candidates; sidebar-status/sidebar-state/zoxide-editor, opener.edit preservation, semantic Yazi action keymaps, flat ~/.config/yazelix sidecars, generated state dir writes, Home Manager/import vocabulary, and legacy override rejection stay in Yazelix. Delete-first result: removed stale configs/yazi/user override guide and stale source-template docs; updated Yazi README/flavor docs to settings.jsonc. LOC scorecard: Rust Yazi materializer 1,464 lines, render plan 276, Yazi tests 517, configs/yazi 6,513; next reduction should come from asset slimming before Rust extraction. Follow-ups: yazelix-lzlg.1 slim bundled Yazi config/plugin asset pack; yazelix-lzlg.2 split private writer/Yazelix adapter after .1. Verification: cargo test -p yazelix_core yazi_materialization; cargo test -p yazelix_core yazi_render_plan; cargo run -p yazelix_maintainer --bin yzx_repo_validator -- validate-contracts; validate-docs-experience; git diff --check.
close_reason: Completed Yazi boundary audit, stale docs deletion, and follow-up beads

title: Slim bundled Yazi config and plugin asset pack
description: Reduce the Yazi asset surface before any public extraction. Evaluate bundled flavors and vendored plugins as optional/runtime components, delete stale docs or copied metadata that does not serve runtime behavior, and record what must stay integrated for the managed sidebar/editor experience.
acceptance_criteria: The bead records a LOC scorecard for configs/yazi, identifies which assets stay default versus optional, deletes any stale or redundant shipped asset/docs that fail the proof gate, and avoids changing managed Yazi behavior without explicit follow-up.
notes:
2026-05-07 yazelix-wn52 audit ranking: top-three delete-only candidate. Expected main-repo impact is primarily runtime asset/storage and generated config ownership, not Rust LOC. Start by proving which bundled Yazi flavors/plugins are actually required for the managed sidebar/editor contract, then delete stale shipped docs/assets that fail that proof gate. Avoid a paper extraction that leaves the same asset pack in Yazelix plus a duplicate child package.
Implemented local extraction on 2026-05-07. Reusable Yazi flavors, auto-layout.yazi, git.yazi, lazygit.yazi, starship.yazi, yazelix_starship.toml, and pinned upstream metadata moved to sibling child repo /home/lucca/pjs/yazelix-yazi-assets. Main repo configs/yazi now keeps only base templates plus sidebar-state, sidebar-status, and zoxide-editor. Packaged runtime reassembles configs/yazi from main-owned files plus #yazelix_yazi_assets. Main configs/yazi scorecard: 6,513 -> 356 lines. Child repo package scorecard: 6,822 lines including flake/lock/docs/metadata. Deleted main repo vendored Yazi plugin refresh workflow and ratcheted Rust budget 73,513 -> 73,220 raw lines. Follow-up yazelix-lzlg.1.1 tracks publishing the child repo and replacing the temporary local git+file flake input before pushing main.
close_reason: Completed: extracted reusable Yazi flavor/plugin asset pack into local child repo /home/lucca/pjs/yazelix-yazi-assets, rewired packaged runtime to link #yazelix_yazi_assets while keeping Yazelix-only sidebar/editor plugins local, deleted main-repo vendored Yazi plugin refresh code, updated docs/contracts/LOC scorecards, and verified child package, runtime package, Rust checks/tests, and validators. Created yazelix-lzlg.1.1 to publish the child repo and replace the temporary local flake input before remote push.

title: Publish yazelix-yazi-assets and replace local flake input
description: The lzlg.1 local extraction creates /home/lucca/pjs/yazelix-yazi-assets and wires the main flake through a local git+file input so it can be tested before pushing. Before publishing the main Yazelix branch, create/push the luccahuguet/yazelix-yazi-assets GitHub repository and relock the main flake to the GitHub input so users never depend on a sibling checkout.
acceptance_criteria: The child repo exists on GitHub with the committed asset package, the main flake input uses github:luccahuguet/yazelix-yazi-assets instead of git+file:../yazelix-yazi-assets, flake.lock records the published commit, and nix build .#runtime proves the regular Yazelix package still links flavors and reusable Yazi plugins from the child package.
notes: Published child repo on 2026-05-08 at https://github.com/luccahuguet/yazelix-yazi-assets with commit 85f46698fd11cdef821f051da4966875de7e8ce8. Main flake input changed from git+file:../yazelix-yazi-assets to github:luccahuguet/yazelix-yazi-assets and flake.lock records the same published commit. Verification: nix build .#yazelix_yazi_assets --no-link; nix build .#runtime --no-link --print-out-paths; runtime yazi flavors/git plugin/sidebar-state plugin path checks; validate-flake-interface.
close_reason: Completed: published https://github.com/luccahuguet/yazelix-yazi-assets at commit 85f46698fd11cdef821f051da4966875de7e8ce8, switched the main flake input to github:luccahuguet/yazelix-yazi-assets, relocked flake.lock to the published commit, and verified the runtime links flavors/reusable plugins from the child package.

title: Split Yazi materializer into private writer and Yazelix adapter
description: After the Yazi asset pack is slimmer, split yazi_materialization.rs internally so generic render/config-pack writing is separated from Yazelix-owned settings normalization, managed sidecar paths, semantic action registry keybindings, runtime state writes, and legacy ownership errors. Keep the boundary private unless the Yazelix adapter becomes thin.
acceptance_criteria: yazi_materialization.rs no longer mixes generic Yazi config writing with Yazelix adapter policy; behavior tests for generated yazi.toml, keymap.toml, init.lua, asset sync, and legacy override rejection still pass; the bead records whether public extraction remains deferred or becomes justified.
notes: Implementation: split yazi_materialization.rs into a private Yazelix adapter plus yazi_materialization/writer.rs. The adapter keeps settings normalization, managed flat sidecar paths, semantic Yazi action keymap expansion, and legacy override rejection. The writer owns generated yazi.toml/theme.toml/keymap.toml/init.lua output, asset sync, runtime placeholder rendering, atomic writes, and permission normalization from already-resolved adapter inputs. Public extraction remains deferred: the writer still contains Yazelix runtime-placeholder and opener-preservation policy and receives managed output roots. LOC accounting: yazi_materialization.rs 1464 -> 567, new writer.rs 957, net +60 raw Rust lines/+54 tokei code LOC accepted as organization debt after the prior yazelix-00nz extraction net reduction. Verification: cargo fmt --all -- --check; cargo check --workspace; cargo test -p yazelix_core yazi; yzx_repo_validator validate-rust-ownership-budget; validate-contracts; validate-docs-experience; git diff --check.
close_reason: Completed: split Yazi materialization into a private adapter/writer boundary, updated the Yazi boundary contract and Rust inventory/budget, and verified generated Yazi behavior plus validators.

title: Evaluate Zellij fork path for Yazi image previews via Kitty graphics passthrough
description:
## Decision
Pending. Decide whether Yazelix should pursue a Zellij fork/patch path for Yazi image previews through Kitty graphics passthrough.

## Rationale
The user value is Yazi previews working inside Yazelix/Zellij, but full Kitty graphics support is a large upstream-quality project. The plan needs to decide whether a narrowly scoped passthrough POC is worth the fork cost.

## Alternatives Considered
No fork and document limitations; Yazelix-only workaround; narrow Zellij passthrough POC; or full upstream-quality Kitty graphics model.

## Existing Context
Context:
Yazelix wants better image-preview behavior inside Zellij, especially for Ghostty/Kitty/WezTerm users and Yazi-style previews. Upstream Zellij has open requests for Kitty graphics support and passthrough:
- https://github.com/zellij-org/zellij/issues/2814
- https://github.com/zellij-org/zellij/issues/4500
- https://github.com/zellij-org/zellij/issues/4336

Local Zellij code read:
- `zellij-server/src/panes/terminal_pane.rs`: PTY bytes enter `TerminalPane::handle_pty_bytes` and are advanced byte-by-byte through `vte::Parser`.
- `zellij-server/src/panes/grid.rs`: `Grid` implements `vte::Perform`; Sixel is handled via DCS `hook`/`put`/`unhook`.
- `zellij-server/src/panes/sixel.rs`: Zellij already has a full Sixel image model with image locations, pixel rect clipping, image ids, and an image store.
- `zellij-server/src/output/mod.rs`: output has per-client chunks and pre/post VTE instructions; Sixel chunks are clipped and serialized at the end with cursor save/restore.
- `zellij-client/src/stdin_ansi_parser.rs`: client startup already queries text-area and character-cell pixel dimensions, which would be needed for any graphics clipping/placement path.

Architectural conclusion:
Do not start with a full Kitty graphics implementation. A full model would need to emulate image ids, placements, deletes, queries, z-index, Unicode placeholders, scrollback interactions, pane clipping, floating panes, and multiple attached clients with different terminal capabilities. That is a large upstream-quality project.

Recommended fork direction:
Start with a Zellij-owned Kitty graphics passthrough POC, not a tmux-compat illusion and not a full Kitty parser. Add an explicit capability / contract such as `zellij-kitty-graphics-passthrough`, capture graphics payloads before normal VTE parsing, avoid feeding image-transfer bytes into the text grid, let Unicode placeholders flow as normal text where possible, and serialize raw graphics instructions only to clients known or configured to support Kitty graphics.

Proposed POC scope:
- One attached client first
- Active pane or fullscreen pane first
- Ghostty/Kitty/WezTerm support gated behind explicit client capability or config
- No perfect scrollback guarantee
- No promise of tmux-compatible passthrough semantics
- Test with Yazi/Kitty protocol placeholder mode and a direct `kitten icat`/minimal KGP fixture

Estimated Zellij patch size:
- Tactical passthrough POC: roughly +350 to +650 net new LOC, with about 120 to 250 existing LOC touched across 6 to 10 files.
- Upstreamable first-class passthrough contract: roughly +900 to +1,600 net new LOC, with about 250 to 500 existing LOC touched across 10 to 18 files.
- Full Kitty graphics model comparable to or beyond Sixel support: roughly +2,500 to +5,000 net new LOC, with about 600 to 1,200 existing LOC touched, plus a much larger terminal compatibility test burden.

Likely files for the POC:
- `zellij-server/src/panes/terminal_pane.rs` for pre-VTE capture or parser integration
- `zellij-server/src/panes/grid.rs` if VTE performer hooks are needed
- `zellij-server/src/output/mod.rs` for per-client graphics chunks / raw passthrough serialization
- `zellij-client/src/stdin_ansi_parser.rs` or adjacent capability code for terminal graphics probing
- server/client protocol types if capability negotiation needs to cross the client/server boundary
- unit fixtures around VTE capture and output serialization

Open design questions:
- Whether to create a new Zellij passthrough escape wrapper or support raw Kitty APC in an experimental mode
- How to detect client graphics support without corrupting unsupported terminals
- Whether image ids need pane/session namespacing in the POC
- Whether placeholders should be the only supported placement mechanism initially
- Whether any part of this can be upstreamed without coordination from Kitty/Yazi maintainers
acceptance_criteria: Decision is complete when we have chosen one of: no fork, Yazelix-only workaround, Zellij passthrough POC, or full upstream-quality graphics model. The chosen scope must explicitly answer whether Yazi image previews can render inside Yazelix/Zellij, what Yazi configuration/protocol mode it depends on, which terminals are supported first, what behavior is out of scope, the estimated Zellij LOC impact, integration points, and verification plan.
notes:
User framing update 2026-04-28:
The primary product goal is Yazi image previews working well inside Yazelix/Zellij. Kitty graphics passthrough is a means to that end, not the end itself. Evaluate fork scope around the smallest path that makes Yazi previews usable in Ghostty/Kitty/WezTerm-backed Yazelix sessions. Generic terminal graphics support, full Kitty protocol coverage, and tmux-compatible behavior are secondary unless they are required for Yazi.
Secondary goal update 2026-04-28:
A successful graphics path could also make `yazelix_screen` richer by enabling real image/graphics surfaces inside Zellij-backed Yazelix sessions. This is explicitly secondary to Yazi previews: do not expand the first fork/POC scope unless the same passthrough/capability work naturally supports both. Treat `yazelix_screen` as an upside case and a later demo target, not as the acceptance gate for the initial Yazi-focused decision.
Related 2026-05-22 investigation: `yazelix-q82i` tracks a separate, concrete Yazi/Chafa ghost-keypress regression in the pinned runtime (`yazi=26.1.22` plus `chafa=1.18.1`). Fixing that bead should stop random typed-character actions while navigating previews, but it does not solve the high-quality Ghostty+Zellij image-preview gap. Keep the Zellij passthrough/fork decision focused on graphics capability after the Chafa regression is eliminated.
Related Ratty research 2026-05-22: Ratty's inline 3D path uses Ratty Graphics Protocol over APC (`ESC _ ratty;g;... ESC \`). Running Zellij inside Ratty should mostly work as ordinary terminal output, but RGP emitted by apps inside Zellij needs the same generic passthrough/reverse-reply class of work as Kitty graphics support. See `yazelix-ar71` for the Ratty terminal-lane evaluation and `yazelix-gdox` for possible experimental support.
Research update 2026-05-22: do not make upstream-generic passthrough the first Yazelix implementation goal. Generic/protocol-agnostic passthrough remains the likely upstream-preferred architecture, but Yazelix does not need to solve that first if the goal is a controlled first-party runtime that makes Yazi previews work.

Relevant upstream/fork evidence:
- zellij-org/zellij#3383 already merged base Kitty keyboard protocol support and multi-modifier keybindings. Keyboard protocol is not the same problem as graphics passthrough.
- Follow-up keyboard PRs such as #3942, #4334, #5058, #5149 show that Zellij has real parser/query complexity around Kitty keyboard bytes, but the current `nixpkgs-unstable` runtime has Zellij 0.44.3, which includes the 0.44.x host-query stall fixes.
- zellij-org/zellij#4851 is the best narrow graphics starting point: about +352 LOC across 3 files, forward-only Kitty graphics APC passthrough, no image decoding, tested in Ghostty, and intentionally limited. It was closed because upstream wants a more generic/protocol-agnostic long-term solution, not because the narrow path is useless for Yazelix.
- misaelaguayo/zellij#1 proves a heavier fork can display images, but it grew to about +2165 LOC across 13 files with new image/flate dependencies and reported lifecycle bugs: junk escape output, images stuck across tabs, clear/Ctrl-l not clearing, wrong pane after resize, zoom/fullscreen leakage, scroll/cut issues, and image.nvim lag. Do not use that as the first Yazelix base unless #4851 cannot satisfy Yazi.
- zellij-org/zellij#4876 is open Kitty multi-cursor passthrough work. It is not image support, but it confirms the same pressure: terminal applications increasingly need richer protocol passthrough through the multiplexer.

Revised Yazelix recommendation:
Start with a Yazelix-scoped Kitty graphics passthrough spike, not a generic passthrough framework and not a full Kitty graphics model. Use #4851 as the conceptual base. Optimize for Ghostty + Zellij + Yazi and WezTerm + Zellij + Yazi. Treat upstreamability as a later cleanup path, not the first acceptance gate.

First spike contract:
- forward-only Kitty graphics APC passthrough is acceptable for v1
- no reverse APC/query response support in v1 unless Yazi requires it
- no image decoding inside Zellij
- no tmux-compat illusion
- Unicode placeholder mode is the preferred supported placement mechanism
- unsupported terminals must fail cleanly or stay on existing Sixel/Chafa paths

Practical acceptance gates for a fork spike:
1. Yazi image preview works in Ghostty + Zellij in a fresh Yazelix session.
2. Yazi image preview works in WezTerm + Zellij if WezTerm supports the needed Kitty graphics mode for that path.
3. Images clear when leaving Yazi or clearing the preview.
4. Images do not leak across tabs.
5. Images do not leak across pane focus, resize, zoom/fullscreen, or common Yazelix sidebar/popup layouts.
6. Known unsupported behavior, especially reverse APC responses and non-placeholder positioning, is documented rather than silently half-working.

Decision bias after this research: choose "Zellij passthrough POC" over "full upstream-quality graphics model" as the next concrete evaluation step.
close_reason: Completed decision and implementation verification. Chosen path is the Ghostty-scoped Zellij/Yazi KGP passthrough adoption, not a generic upstream-quality graphics model. Evidence: implementation child yazelix-m0c0.1 closed with patched Zellij c10aad0d; Yazi policy child yazelix-owh3 and adoption child yazelix-owh3.1 closed with patched Yazi f6db84c and published Yazelix lock wiring. Verified locally on 2026-05-23: flake metadata points at luccahuguet/yazelix-zellij c10aad0dd657d7df0e2c39762df0daba404a564b and luccahuguet/yazelix-yazi f6db84cf6e8e1da531377c4f382c531c9c03aca8; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml start_yazelix_scrubs_gui_loader_env_before_control_handoff passed; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml graphics_strategy passed; nix build .#runtime_ghostty --no-link passed; built Ghostty runtime has runtime_features/zellij_kitty_passthrough plus zellij/yazi commands; built WezTerm runtime omits the passthrough marker. Follow-up yazelix-m0c0.2 remains open for the upstream removal/archive gate.

title: Prototype thin Zellij Kitty graphics passthrough for Yazelix
description:
Build an isolated proof-of-adoption for a thin patched Zellij that can make Yazelix Yazi image previews work through Kitty graphics passthrough.

This is not an upstream-quality generic terminal protocol framework. The target is a small, reviewable fork/spike that proves whether Yazelix can adopt a patched Zellij runtime for Ghostty/WezTerm + Yazi without owning a large graphics subsystem.
design:
Implementation direction:
- Work in isolated git worktrees so the main Yazelix checkout and parallel ratconfig work are not disturbed.
- Start from current Zellij v0.44.3 / current upstream shape, because Yazelix now consumes nixpkgs-unstable and gets Zellij 0.44.3.
- Use zellij-org/zellij#4851 as the preferred conceptual base: forward-only Kitty graphics APC passthrough, no image decoding, small patch surface.
- Do not start from misaelaguayo/zellij#1 unless #4851-style code cannot satisfy Yazi; that fork has too much LOC, dependencies, and reported lifecycle bugs for a first Yazelix adoption candidate.
- Keep reverse APC/query responses out of v1 unless Yazi requires them for the tested mode.
- Keep upstreamability as a future improvement path, not the first success condition.
acceptance_criteria:
Ready for Yazelix adoption evaluation when:
- a patched Zellij builds from the isolated worktree
- a Yazelix integration worktree can build/run with the patched Zellij through an explicit local override or package injection
- Ghostty + patched Zellij + Yazi can display image previews without falling back to Chafa/Sixel for the tested path, or the remaining blocker is precisely identified
- WezTerm behavior is tested or explicitly deferred with a reason
- images clear when Yazi exits or preview changes
- images do not visibly leak across tab switch, pane resize, pane zoom/fullscreen, or common Yazelix sidebar/popup layouts in the tested scenario
- unsupported behavior such as reverse APC responses, non-placeholder positioning, or multi-client differences is documented
- the resulting patch size and touched-file surface are recorded so Yazelix can decide whether fork maintenance is acceptable
notes: Final spike evidence: current Yazi 26.5.6 intentionally forces Adapter::Sixel whenever ZELLIJ_SESSION_NAME exists (yazi-adapter/src/adapter.rs retains only Sixel), even though Ghostty normally maps to Adapter::Kgp. Therefore normal Yazelix-in-Zellij image previews in Ghostty/WezTerm do not exercise Kitty graphics passthrough; they use Sixel. Visual evidence: /tmp/yazelix_screens/Screenshot_2026-05-22_03-16-56.png shows default patched Ghostty and WezTerm Yazi image previews rendering via the normal Zellij/Sixel path; /tmp/yazelix_screens/Screenshot_2026-05-22_03-17-29.png shows moving off logo.png clears both images. Control comparison for Kitty/KGP path: stock Zellij /nix/store/43rwvg45zss7cpijzdrdr9s4za9kl46v-zellij-0.44.3 plus forced KGP by unsetting only ZELLIJ_SESSION_NAME selected logo.png but left the preview area blank (/tmp/yazelix_screens/Screenshot_2026-05-22_03-21-41.png context). Patched Zellij /nix/store/iyypnxc307l8k4mwn90cvsz49h0vigg2-zellij-0.44.3 plus the same forced-KGP setup rendered logo.png (/tmp/yazelix_screens/Screenshot_2026-05-22_03-24-01.png), and moving selection to the tapes directory cleared the image (/tmp/yazelix_screens/Screenshot_2026-05-22_03-25-01.png). Conclusion: the thin Zellij Kitty APC passthrough patch works for the KGP path, but adopting a patched Zellij does not improve Yazelix's current default Yazi behavior unless we also change Yazi/Yazelix to permit KGP under patched Zellij. Near-term recommendation: do not ship a Zellij fork just for image preview; keep default Sixel, fix/avoid chafa 1.18.1, and create a separate evaluation for a coordinated Yazi adapter policy plus Zellij passthrough if we want Ghostty's Kitty path long term.
close_reason: Completed spike. Zellij branch yazelix_kitty_passthrough has commit c10aad0d with a thin Kitty APC passthrough patch; Yazelix spike branch yazelix_zellij_passthrough has commit 76384d3c fixing the Yazi 26.5.6 fetcher schema blocker. Validation passed: cargo fmt/check/tests for Zellij server, patched Nix builds for Ghostty and WezTerm variants, focused Yazelix yazi_materialization tests, and live Ghostty/WezTerm screenshots. Decision evidence: default Yazi-in-Zellij uses Sixel because Yazi forces Adapter::Sixel when ZELLIJ_SESSION_NAME exists, so the fork does not improve current default image preview. Forced-Kitty comparison shows stock Zellij stays blank while patched Zellij renders and clears correctly. Recommendation recorded: do not adopt a Zellij fork just for current image preview; treat Kitty/KGP passthrough as a future coordinated Yazi-adapter-policy + Zellij patch evaluation.

title: Define upstream-removal gate for temporary Zellij and Yazi graphics forks
description: Define the exact gate for dropping and archiving yazelix-zellij and yazelix-yazi once upstream support is sufficient.
design: Name the required upstream Zellij graphics or passthrough behavior, Yazi policy requirements, Ghostty and WezTerm manual tests, remaining fork-local patches that must disappear, and the rollback path if upstream support regresses.
acceptance_criteria: There is a documented keep/drop/archive checklist with concrete upstream versions or PRs, runtime tests, and a rollback plan. The forks are not treated as permanent without revisiting this gate.

title: add yzx import zellij/yazi to import native configs into yazelix-managed overrides
description: Add a short import flow that brings native Zellij and Yazi config files under Yazelix management instead of making users manually copy files into the managed override paths.
design:
Keep the command surface short and product-shaped: use `yzx import zellij` and `yzx import yazi` instead of a generic `yzx cp` command or a 4-word `yzx config import ...` flow. Treat this as an adoption/import path, not a reset path: the source is the native config location and the destination is Yazelix's managed override location.

For Zellij, import only `~/.config/zellij/config.kdl` into `~/.config/yazelix/user_configs/zellij/config.kdl`. For Yazi, import only the override files Yazelix already has a clear contract for: `yazi.toml`, `keymap.toml`, and `init.lua` from `~/.config/yazi/` into `~/.config/yazelix/user_configs/yazi/`. Do not try to import plugin directories, flavors, caches, or other broader state in v1.

Fail fast when the managed destination already exists so users do not silently clobber their Yazelix-managed files. Allow an explicit `--force` path, but back up any overwritten managed file first and keep the messaging clear about what was imported, skipped, backed up, or overwritten.
acceptance_criteria: `yzx import zellij` imports `~/.config/zellij/config.kdl` into `~/.config/yazelix/user_configs/zellij/config.kdl`. `yzx import yazi` imports `yazi.toml`, `keymap.toml`, and `init.lua` from `~/.config/yazi/` into `~/.config/yazelix/user_configs/yazi/`. Missing native source files fail clearly instead of silently succeeding. Existing managed destination files are not overwritten unless `--force` is passed, and the `--force` path writes backups before replacing managed files. The docs point users to the import commands as the supported way to adopt native Zellij or Yazi config into Yazelix-managed overrides. Add focused command-level regression coverage for the happy path and overwrite protection.
close_reason: Completed

title: Migrate legacy [packs] out of yazelix.toml and make legacy pack placement fail fast
close_reason: Completed

title: Evaluate statix for Nix linting
design: Evaluate statix as an advisory Nix linter for Yazelix, not an immediate hard CI gate. Run it over flake.nix, packaging/*.nix, home_manager/*.nix, examples, and related Nix files. Classify findings into real bugs, style-only churn, and false positives. Decide whether statix belongs in the maintainer shell, a manual/sweep lane, docs, or nowhere.
acceptance_criteria: A maintainer note or bead notes summarize statix findings with examples. The evaluation records whether to adopt statix, and if so where it should live. Any accepted fixes are either committed in a small follow-up or converted into beads. The decision explicitly avoids noisy style churn as a default gate unless the findings defend real correctness.

title: Extract a backend adapter boundary around env, run, refresh, and launch bootstrap
description: Refactor the command/bootstrap layer so the current devenv integration sits behind a clearer backend adapter boundary instead of bleeding directly into user-facing command semantics. Focus on the shared paths now rooted in environment_bootstrap.nu and the yzx env/run/refresh/launch surface. The immediate goal is not to support a second backend in main; it is to make current devenv-specific assumptions explicit, shrink accidental coupling, and leave behind a clearer seam for later experiments or a Core edition.
design: Treat this as main-branch architecture cleanup, not speculative abstraction. The important move is to identify what the command layer expects from the environment backend and to keep the rest of the command semantics outside that adapter. Likely hot spots include get_devenv_base_command, run_in_devenv_shell_command, resolve_preferred_devenv_path, and the launch/bootstrap state that currently assumes devenv-shaped profiles. Delete direct ad hoc devenv assumptions where possible, but do not build a pretend generic backend framework unless the resulting seam is materially clearer. This should complement ongoing env-var cleanup in yazelix-2ex.2.1 rather than duplicating it.
acceptance_criteria: The env/run/refresh/launch bootstrap path has a clearer backend boundary, with fewer direct devenv-specific assumptions bleeding into user-facing command semantics. The repo shape is simpler to reason about than before, not more abstract. Future backend experiments or a Core edition would have a concrete seam to target instead of rediscovering environment_bootstrap.nu as a monolith.
notes:
2026-04-08 streamlining alignment:
- This remains a high-value architecture seam, but it should not absorb simpler delete-first cuts that can land independently inside `common.nu`, `doctor.nu`, or `yzx/dev.nu`.
- Use this bead for the shared backend boundary only: env/run/refresh/launch bootstrap, backend command resolution, and profile-shaped state assumptions.
- As the refactor lane sharpens, treat `common.nu`, `environment_bootstrap.nu`, `launch_state.nu`, and the relevant command entrypoints as the core input set.

2026-04-09 backend adapter extraction progress:
- Moved the shared devenv-backed command/build/reuse functions into `utils/devenv_backend.nu` and repointed the production env/run/refresh/launch entrypoints at that narrower owner.
- Deleted the temporary `environment_bootstrap.nu` re-export bridge so environment preparation now owns only prep/state checks while backend execution helpers live only under the backend adapter.
- Tightened the refresh regressions to model the real split: `environment_bootstrap.nu` now stubs only `prepare_environment`, while backend-specific tests source `devenv_backend.nu` directly.
- Deleted the duplicate `devenv build shell` runner from `yzx/refresh.nu`; refresh and rebuild now share `run_devenv_build_shell` in the backend adapter instead of maintaining separate execution logic, unfree handling, and profile-resolution plumbing.
- Centralized the standard reuse/skip-refresh/requested-rebuild classification and guidance in `devenv_backend.nu`, so env/startup/launch/restart stop hand-rolling the same stale-environment branch logic and user messaging.
- Tightened the workspace/startup fixtures to mirror the real split as well: test stubs now separate `environment_bootstrap.nu` prep ownership from `devenv_backend.nu` execution ownership instead of saving the same fake module to both paths.

2026-04-09 completion:
- Finished the backend adapter boundary by moving the remaining shared runtime-entry context and recorded-profile selection rules behind `devenv_backend.nu` and `launch_state.nu`.
- `env`, `run`, `launch`, and `start_yazelix` now consume the shared adapter context instead of rebuilding the same backend-state logic locally.
close_reason: Completed

title: Model launch, refresh, and profile reuse as an explicit runtime state machine
description: Make the runtime-control plane explicit instead of continuing to spread launch, refresh, reuse, and activation decisions across bootstrap helpers and command entrypoints. The goal is a single state model for when Yazelix can reuse a built profile, when it must rebuild, and how live activation state differs from persisted launch truth.
design: Treat this as a product-state-model bead, not just a refactor. Enumerate the meaningful runtime states and transitions first, then make yzx launch, enter, env, run, refresh, and restart consume that model instead of hand-rolling overlapping checks. The design should make rebuild-vs-reuse-vs-force-reenter decisions legible and should keep the distinction between materialized state and stale ambient shell activation explicit. Decide whether the resulting state engine stays in Nushell or deserves a smaller compiled supervisor, but make the transition model explicit either way.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The runtime launch model has explicit states and transitions for rebuild, reuse, refresh, force-reenter, and live activation. 2. The main user-facing entrypoints share one transition model instead of re-encoding should-refresh/profile-selection logic independently. 3. The model preserves the current runtime/config/state ownership contract. 4. Follow-up implementation work can target explicit transitions instead of scattered shell-condition cleanup.
notes:
2026-04-09 explicit state-model slice:
- added resolve_runtime_entry_state plus command-specific transitions in utils/devenv_backend.nu
- launch/env/startup now consume explicit activation_surface, refresh_transition, and profile_request state instead of re-deriving those branches locally
- removed duplicate rebuild handoff in yzx env and yzx launch after explicit rebuilds by driving force-refresh from the resolved transition rather than the original needs-refresh bit
- added direct workspace tests for runtime state, startup transition, launch transition, and env transition contracts
close_reason: Completed: launch/env/startup/run now consume an explicit shared runtime state and transition model in devenv_backend.nu, including activation surface, refresh transition, profile request, and backend-shell vs profile activation outcomes. Duplicate rebuild handoff logic was removed from env/launch, direct workspace tests now defend the transition contracts, and the runtime activation spec now states that yzx refresh is the materialization boundary rather than another activation selector.

title: Investigate missing generated Zellij keybinds after permission repair
description: Deleting Zellij's cached permissions.kdl can leave Yazelix plugins without permissions and without a visible prompt. Sidebar key actions such as Ctrl+y and Alt+y then stop working, and users do not have a clear supported way to re-grant permissions.
acceptance_criteria: Yazelix rejects keybinds blocks in managed ~/.config/yazelix/zellij.kdl with a clear config error; read-only native ~/.config/zellij/config.kdl fallback still cannot suppress Yazelix-generated keybindings; docs direct users to settings.jsonc for Yazelix keybinding remaps and to zellij.kdl only for native non-keybinding settings; GitHub issue #595 is updated with the strict final contract and current supported defaults.
notes:
2026-05-19 implementation: startup/runtime materialization plan now treats a missing Zellij plugin permissions cache as a repairable generated artifact, so deleting permissions.kdl makes the next Yazelix launch reach Zellij materialization and re-seed bundled plugin permissions before Zellij starts. Added regression coverage for the missing permissions cache plan path and updated troubleshooting. Verification: cargo fmt --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml --all --check; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core plan_marks_missing_zellij_plugin_permissions_without_forcing_refresh; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core runtime_materialization; cargo run --quiet --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_maintainer --bin yzx_repo_validator -- validate-docs-experience; git diff --check.

2026-05-20 follow-up investigation: permissions.kdl repair is fixed, but reporter confirmed Alt y/Ctrl y/Alt t still fail because ~/.local/share/yazelix/configs/zellij/config.kdl can contain only the native ~/.config/zellij/config.kdl. Reproduced against installed v16.4: overwriting generated config.kdl with native config leaves runtime-materialization.plan and repair at noop because the gate checks file existence/hashes, not generated config content.

2026-05-20 implementation follow-up: simplified launch convergence for generated Zellij state. Startup now calls runtime-materialization.materialize directly instead of skipping on a preliminary noop plan. Zellij materialization no longer reuses generated text config/layout state from .yazelix_generation.json; it re-renders config.kdl and layouts and atomically replaces them. Plugin wasm sync now hashes the runtime copy before copying, and Zellij permissions.kdl remains preserved/upserted rather than deleted. Added shared generated-config marker validation so runtime repair planning, apply validation, and doctor reject a plain native config in ~/.local/share/yazelix/configs/zellij/config.kdl. Fixed yzx status logs_dir to point at the state logs directory derived from YAZELIX_STATE_DIR, not runtime_dir/logs. Verification: cargo fmt --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml --all --check; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core runtime_materialization --lib; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core runtime_materialization_materialize --test yzx_core_config_normalize; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core workspace_asset_report --lib; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core logs_dir_comes_from_yazelix_state_path --lib; cargo check --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core; cargo run --quiet --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_maintainer --bin yzx_repo_validator -- validate-nushell-syntax; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core; git diff --check. LOC scorecard against temporary current-worktree tree: runtime +75, tests +42, total code +98; accepted because the bead adds real repair/doctor/status coverage while deleting the unsafe generated-state reuse path.
2026-05-21: GitHub #595 latest state is no longer only plugin permissions. Reporter says permissions.kdl returns, but Alt y/Ctrl y/Alt t are absent from final ~/.local/share/yazelix/configs/zellij/config.kdl and the generated file appears to be just ~/.config/zellij/config.kdl. Current main has changed default keys: Alt y is replaced by Alt Shift H, Alt t is unbound by default, bottom popup is Alt Shift J, and Ctrl y remains editor/left-sidebar focus. Current code also has regression coverage that read-only native ~/.config/zellij/config.kdl clear-defaults must not suppress Yazelix overrides, while managed ~/.config/yazelix/zellij.kdl clear-defaults intentionally does. Managed ~/.config/yazelix/zellij.kdl remains an advanced native Zellij settings sidecar only; Yazelix keybinding behavior is configured through settings.jsonc zellij.keybindings and zellij.native_keybindings. Managed zellij.kdl must not contain keybinds blocks, including keybinds clear-defaults=true, because that creates a second owner for workspace controls and can bypass generated Yazelix bindings. Read-only native ~/.config/zellij/config.kdl fallback still appends Yazelix generated keybindings and strips clear-defaults semantics. yzx import zellij now refuses native configs containing keybinds blocks so it cannot create an invalid managed sidecar. title: Measure incremental Cachix output storage and trim publish workflow
description:
The public `yazelix` Cachix cache is near the free-tier limit. The publish workflow pushes multiple x86_64-linux package outputs, not just `#yazelix`. Measure which outputs add unique storage and decide whether the workflow should publish fewer outputs, split caches, or use Cachix retention/pruning.

Known workflow outputs:
- `yazelix_kgp_yazi`
- `yazelix_kgp_zellij`
- `yazelix_helix`
- `yazelix`
- `yazelix_ghostty`
- `yazelix_wezterm`
- `yazelix_agent_tools`
- `yazelix_screen`

Questions:
1. Which outputs are genuinely needed for user install/update speed?
2. Which outputs are mostly duplicate once `#yazelix` is cached?
3. Does Cachix storage include stale revisions that should be pruned by retention settings?
4. Should terminal/runtime-specific outputs be split into separate caches or stop publishing by default?

Acceptance criteria:
- Measure output closures and incremental unique store paths for each published output.
- Verify Cachix-side retention/pruning options from current Cachix docs or UI before recommending a policy.
- Propose a publish workflow change or explicitly keep current workflow with evidence.
- If a workflow change is accepted, create or implement a small CI change with before/after expected storage impact.
- Avoid reducing cache coverage for the main supported install path without explicit tradeoff documentation.
close_reason: Measured incremental unique NAR for every published output, verified current Cachix GC/pins behavior from docs, documented the policy, and trimmed zero-increment outputs from publish_nix_cache.yml while keeping main and alternate runtime coverage.

comments.author: lucca
comments.text: Measured publish workflow outputs in order. Incremental unique local NAR bytes: `yazelix_kgp_yazi` 80,149,152; `yazelix_kgp_zellij` 58,608,496; `yazelix_helix` 295,247,680; `yazelix` 3,889,428,184; `yazelix_ghostty` 0; `yazelix_wezterm` 242,610,464; `yazelix_agent_tools` 0; `yazelix_screen` 0. Verified current Cachix docs: pushes check upstream NixOS cache before upload; garbage collection removes oldest entries by last access/creation at the storage limit; pins protect release paths with bounded retention. Implemented conservative workflow trim removing explicit zero-increment outputs while preserving `yazelix`, KGP child packages, Helix, and WezTerm cache coverage.

title: Re-evaluate migrating Yazelix planning back to beads_rust
description: beads_rust was retired because the old br workflow was unstable for Yazelix, especially around SQLite corruption, dotted IDs, dependency writes, and rebuilds from JSONL. The project now hears that beads_rust is working much better. Re-evaluate whether Yazelix should migrate planning back from gastownhall/beads to beads_rust, stay on bd, or keep watching without moving.
design: Treat this as a toolchain decision, not a nostalgic rollback. Compare current bd and current beads_rust against Yazelix's real workflow: hierarchical IDs, dependencies, external_ref/GitHub sync contract, embedded storage safety, JSONL interchange, single-writer behavior, Nix/Home Manager packaging, speed, recoverability, and agent ergonomics. No migration should begin until the decision proves the replacement is better for this repository now.
acceptance_criteria: Decision records the current tested beads_rust version, the current bd version, a parity/risk matrix, migration cost, data backup/export path, rollback path, and recommendation. If migration back is chosen, create implementation beads for tooling, data conversion, AGENTS updates, validation, and cleanup. If rejected or deferred, record the concrete blockers that would need to change.
notes: Counterpart to closed yazelix-nasl, which migrated away from beads_rust when br was unreliable.
close_reason: Completed by implementation: current beads_rust 0.2.11 was tested against Yazelix's tracked JSONL, packaged in the maintainer shell, wired through yzx dev sync_issues and CI, and adopted as the default planning tracker.
