title: Collapse launch-time terminal and Ghostty request assembly toward the provable irreducible Nu floor
description: Surviving launch-time Nushell in core/launch_yazelix.nu still assembles terminal-materialization and Ghostty request data around Rust owners. Count progress only if this deletes launch-time Nu logic end-to-end instead of adding a fake Rust launcher wrapper.
design: Keep only the final human-facing launch summary text and final process execution in Nu or POSIX if they are truly irreducible. Push every deterministic request, reroll input, and launch-plan decision out.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The exact deletable launch-time request-assembly slice is recorded. 2. core/launch_yazelix.nu is targeted for deletion or near-elimination, not passive shrinkage. 3. Any surviving Nu launch logic has explicit proof of irreducibility. 4. Terminal launch behavior and Ghostty reroll behavior stay intact.
close_reason: all steps complete

title: Define the deletion budget for launch-time terminal and Ghostty request assembly
description: Audit core/launch_yazelix.nu line by line and classify which launch-time terminal and Ghostty request-shaping logic can move to the existing Rust owners without creating a fake launcher wrapper.
design: Be explicit about what stays Nu-owned: terminal selection, process exec, and user-facing launch errors. Everything else must justify its survival.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The deletable request-assembly slice is explicit. 2. The surviving Nu responsibilities are explicit. 3. The stop condition against a fake Rust launcher wrapper is recorded.
close_reason: Completed

title: Implement the launch-time terminal and Ghostty request-assembly collapse
description: Implement the launch-time owner cut from yazelix-nuj1. Delete the request-shaping logic in core/launch_yazelix.nu that no longer needs to stay in Nushell now that Rust owns terminal materialization and Ghostty generation.
design: Do not move process execution into Rust. Keep Nu only for the irreducible launch path.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The launch-time Nu request assembly is materially smaller or deleted. 2. No new generic launcher wrapper is added. 3. The launch contract stays defended by focused runtime checks.
notes: Dependency decision: add no new crates. Reuse existing yazelix_core terminal_materialization, ghostty_materialization, control_plane, and serde_json support. Keep launch preparation logic in-house because it is small domain policy around existing Rust owners; reject a broader CLI framework or wrapper crate because the goal is deletion of Nu request assembly, not another generic abstraction layer.
close_reason: Completed

title: Redo Rust code inventory
description: Refresh docs/rust_code_inventory.md and the Rust ownership budget manifest after the Ghostty cursor rename and latest extraction work. Recalculate current Rust LOC/file counts, ratchet no-growth ceilings where appropriate, validate the ownership budget, and commit.
notes: Commit 35fa198a LOC scorecard vs HEAD^: runtime net 0, maintainer net 0, tests net 0, docs net +2, generated net -2, packaging net 0, tokei code delta -2, total delta 0. Inventory now records tokei Rust code LOC 57,888, Rust lines 63,119, and budget raw lines 63,217 across 136 Rust files.
close_reason: Completed: refreshed Rust code inventory and ownership budget for the current post-SP11/post-yazelix-ghostty-cursors state. Recalculated tokei rust_core rust_plugins --exclude target: 57,888 Rust code LOC, 63,119 Rust lines, 136 Rust files. Ratcheted rust_ownership_budget.toml to 63,217 raw Rust lines across 136 files, removed stale status agent refresh/test paths, and updated family counts/largest-file/extraction-target inventory. Validation: validate-rust-ownership-budget passes with only the expected warning that 63,217 remains above hard_target_loc 60,000. LOC scorecard will be recorded after commit.

title: Keep repo workspace root when opening files inside a repo
description: Opening a file from within a repository can incorrectly retarget the Yazelix workspace to that file's containing folder. The expected behavior is that file-open flows inside the current repo keep the workspace rooted at the repo/project root, while explicit project/directory switch flows can still retarget intentionally.
acceptance_criteria: Reproduce the file-open path that retargets workspace root to a file's parent directory; identify whether it comes from Yazi open, yzx open-editor, workspace retarget, sidebar sync, or another file-open path; preserve the current repo/workspace root when the opened file is inside it; only retarget to a file parent when there is no known workspace root or the user explicitly switches workspace; add behavior-backed regression coverage for opening files from nested repo paths.
close_reason: Duplicate of closed yazelix-znx7; Yazi-to-Helix nested file opens now preserve Git/workspace roots with regression coverage.

title: Scaffold managed Helix Steel plugin input directory
description: After a Home Manager runtime update, bundled Steel plugins are still present in the package and generated state, but the user-facing custom plugin input directory ~/.config/yazelix/helix/steel_plugins can be absent. This makes the documented Steel plugin surface look gone even though generated commands are healthy. Scaffold a non-behavior-owning README/directory for the managed Helix Steel plugin input surface without creating live Helix config files or deleting user content.
close_reason: Completed: managed Helix materialization now scaffolds ~/.config/yazelix/helix/steel_plugins/README.md as a non-behavior-owning input surface, preserving missing helix.toml/config.toml behavior while making the documented custom Steel plugin directory visible. Verified focused managed_user_config_stubs tests, rust fmt --check, rust check core, and git diff --check.

title: Fix Alt-( and Alt-) keybindings in Helix under Zellij
description: Fix the Alt-( and Alt-) Helix keybindings when running under Zellij so these editor shortcuts behave reliably instead of getting lost or mangled by the terminal and Zellij layer.
design: Treat this as an input-stack bug spanning terminal, Zellij, and Helix. First confirm what escape sequences are emitted in the supported terminal set, then decide whether the fix belongs in Zellij keybindings, terminal keyboard protocol settings, Helix mappings, or some narrower compatibility layer. Avoid a fix that only works in one terminal if Yazelix claims broader support.
acceptance_criteria: The affected Alt bindings work in the supported Yazelix terminal stack or the docs state the exact caveat when they cannot. Add unit tests for escape-sequence normalization or mapping helpers where they exist, regression tests or scripted input capture, and end-to-end validation with detailed logs of the emitted sequences and the final behavior inside Helix.
close_reason: Closed after fresh manual verification confirmed Alt-( and Alt-) currently work in the supported Yazelix stack.

title: Add yzx edit cursors sidecar target
description: Add a yzx edit cursors command target so users can open ~/.config/yazelix/user_configs/yazelix_cursors.toml directly after cursor settings moved out of yazelix.toml
design: Follow the existing yzx edit command pattern and route cursors to the active cursor sidecar path. Bootstrap/resolve the active config surface as needed so the sidecar exists before opening. Keep existing yzx edit config behavior unchanged.
acceptance_criteria:
yzx edit cursors opens the active user cursor sidecar path
Existing yzx edit targets keep working
Help or command metadata exposes the cursors target where edit targets are listed
Focused tests cover the target path resolution without launching an interactive editor
notes: Implementation: added cursors as a first-class yzx edit target resolving to user_configs/yazelix_cursors.toml. The target table now bootstraps through active config surface resolution when available, command metadata exposes yzx edit cursors, README and CLI docs mention it, and a public yzx_control regression defends yzx edit cursors --print without launching an editor. Verification: yzx dev rust fmt --check; yzx dev rust check; yzx dev rust test edit_commands; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core --test yzx_control_public_commands yzx_control_edit_cursors_prints_cursor_sidecar_path; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core --test yzx_core_command_metadata; yzx_repo_validator validate-rust-test-traceability; git diff --check.
close_reason: Completed: yzx edit cursors now opens or prints the active cursor sidecar.

title: Make Mandelbrot loop use valley crop
description: Keep the self-similar Mandelbrot loop transform, but make frame 0 a visually interesting Seahorse/valley crop instead of the full-set/tip view. Preserve seam continuity, bounded CPU cost, and deterministic terminal dimensions.
notes: 2026-04-26 implementation attempted to make the Mandelbrot loop more interesting by changing the base viewport to a Seahorse-valley crop centered at -0.77 + 0.09i with scale 0.06 while preserving the period-3 self-similar transform. Manual runtime testing showed this was not acceptable: it produced long stretches of uniform full-block/red and low-detail frames. Superseded by yazelix-82wi, which reverted the runtime constants back to the known-correct loop and added a sampled-frame uniformity guard. Keep future valley/deep Seahorse work in yazelix-dryz with explicit frame-quality acceptance.
close_reason: Implemented valley-crop Mandelbrot loop

title: Evaluate yazelix_workspace extraction after launch and Zellij shrink
description: Evaluate whether yazelix_workspace is ready to become a child repository after launch fallback flow and Zellij materialization are smaller. The decision should prevent workspace extraction from dragging launch, restart, status, layout, and pane-orchestrator ownership into a bloated child repo.
design: Treat this as an extraction gate, not implementation. The decision must compare: keep private modules in main, extract a private internal crate/module, create a public child repo, or defer. Require a thin adapter proof before approving a public repo.
acceptance_criteria: Decision records current launch/Zellij/workspace ownership, expected main-repo LOC movement, child-repo API boundary if accepted, package/runtime impact, verification path, and explicit blockers if extraction remains deferred. Implementation beads are created only if the adapter is thin enough.
notes: Decision 2026-05-08: Public yazelix_workspace extraction remains deferred after the launch and Zellij shrink. Current ownership: workspace_session.rs and pane_orchestrator_client.rs are small reusable candidates, but zellij_commands.rs still mixes Zellij pipe diagnostics, workspace retarget, Yazi-to-editor open, editor pane creation, terminal pane opening, sidebar hiding, runtime editor env construction, and status/cache test/export surface; workspace_commands.rs still mixes public cwd/reveal/popup/sidebar commands, zoxide/path resolution, managed editor kind detection, Yazi emit-to, and popup cwd policy; launch/restart adapters still provide runtime/session env consumed by workspace commands; zellij_materialization.rs still owns generated layouts, plugin paths, permission seeding, keybindings, and status-bar widget wiring. Expected LOC movement: extracting only the small reusable parsers/client would be minor and not useful for non-Yazelix users; extracting the larger command surface now would pull thousands of lines of Yazelix runtime policy into a child repo. Package/runtime impact: no child repo, no new package, no wasm/source split. Verification: validate-contracts and validate-docs-experience passed after updating docs/contracts/yazelix_workspace_extraction.md. Explicit blockers: split the Zellij/workspace command boundary first; keep status/cache widgets, popup commands, Yazi emit-to, and runtime env construction local; isolate request/response shaping and pane-orchestrator calls behind a measurable private module before reevaluating. No public extraction implementation bead was created because the adapter is not thin enough.
close_reason: Completed: evaluated the post-shrink workspace extraction gate, kept public extraction deferred, updated the durable contract with the current coupling snapshot, and recorded explicit blockers.

title: Restore Starship Yazi plugin prompt integration
description: GitHub issue #529 reports that the Starship Yazi plugin has regressed and Yazi is showing the normal prompt instead of the plugin-driven one. Investigate the current Yazi shell/prompt wiring, identify where the plugin path stopped taking effect, and restore the intended prompt integration without depending on host-local prompt setup.
close_reason: Closed on GitHub

title: Audit child repo ownership boundaries
description: Evaluate whether each current first-party child repo has a justified standalone ownership boundary, or whether any repo split is creating more integration risk than value. This should be a product/architecture audit, not an implementation plan.
acceptance_criteria: Produce an evaluation-only matrix for the current child repos, including yazelix-zellij-bar, yazelix-zellij-pane-orchestrator, yazelix-screen, yazelix-ghostty-cursors, and yazelix-yazi-assets; score standalone user value, coupling to Yazelix runtime, artifact ownership clarity, duplicate-code risk, release friction, and local testability; recommend keep/separate, revise boundary, or consider merge-back for each; do not implement repository moves.
close_reason: Completed: added docs/child_repo_ownership_boundaries.md with scored evaluation matrix for screen, ghostty-cursors, zellij-bar, pane-orchestrator, zellij-popup, and yazi-assets. Recommendation: keep all current child repos separate; revise discipline for pane-orchestrator boundary; no merge-back now. Verified validate-contracts and validate-docs-experience.

title: Popup command should respect editor.command
description: GitHub #513: yzx_popup flows such as jjui editing a commit description should respect the configured editor.command instead of falling back to the wrong editor binary.
close_reason: Completed

title: Define LOC budget and extraction scorecard
description: Create the canonical measurement policy for the next delete-first extraction cycle so Yazelix can tell whether refactors and child-repo extractions actually shrink the main project instead of only moving complexity around while net LOC grows.
design: Define the counted surfaces explicitly: user runtime code, maintainer tooling, tests, docs, contracts, generated fixtures, Nix packaging, plugin wasm/assets, child-repo vendored inputs, and generated output snapshots. Prefer a small repeatable script or maintainer command over ad hoc one-off counts. Separate raw diff churn from net tracked LOC, code LOC, runtime closure/storage impact, and main-repo ownership reduction. The scorecard should be used before and after major extraction beads, and it should make it clear when an extraction adds a public repo but does not yet reduce the Yazelix main repo.
acceptance_criteria: The bead is complete when Yazelix has a documented LOC/extraction scorecard with: baseline ref, counted and excluded paths, tokei or equivalent commands, runtime-vs-maintainer-vs-doc/test categories, wasm/assets treatment, child-repo treatment, closure/storage measurement guidance where practical, and a short template for reporting before/after deletion and extraction results.
close_reason: Completed: added docs/loc_extraction_scorecard.md with counted surfaces, exclusions, baseline, child-repo accounting, closure/storage guidance, and report template; added shells/posix/yazelix_loc_scorecard.sh repeatable helper; linked it from docs front door. Verified sh -n, validate-docs-experience, and scorecard run.

title: clone yazi zellij and helix near yazelix so it's easier for AI to read their code
description: Imported GitHub issue #486.
close_reason: Imported closed GitHub issue #486.

title: [Bug]: yzx update runtime fails
description: GitHub #555: yzx update runtime failed while rebuilding generated Yazi runtime configs because bundled plugin directories copied from the store were not writable before placeholder rendering or later recursive cleanup.
close_reason: Completed: fixed on main in 21998b8; bundled Yazi plugin refresh now relaxes copied store permissions before placeholder rendering and recursive managed cleanup, so yzx update runtime succeeds again.

title: Filter flake source so Home Manager updates do not snapshot local build artifacts
description: Home Manager updates currently copy the entire local Yazelix repo into the Nix store because the flake/runtime path uses the repo root as a path input. On this machine that means hashing and copying a ~2.0G tree, dominated by rust_core/target (~1.6G), .beads (~275M), and .git (~120M), before evaluation/build. The repo already filters Rust helper sources in packaging/rust_core_helper.nix, but the top-level flake/runtime/Home Manager path still snapshots the whole repo. Add a filtered top-level source for package/runtime/Home Manager use so local artifacts like target/, .beads/, logs/, and similar state are excluded.
notes: Refined scope after investigation: the performance problem has two distinct owners. (1) User-facing Home Manager input UX: explicit local path inputs like url = "path:/home/.../yazelix" force full path snapshots and should be detected with clear git+file migration guidance. (2) Internal packaging hygiene: package/runtime builders should consume a filtered repo source rather than the whole checkout. Implement both; do not auto-edit user-managed Home Manager files outside Yazelix-owned paths.
close_reason: Implemented and verified locally: Home Manager path-input guidance plus filtered package/runtime source defaults.

title: Detect local path-pinned Home Manager Yazelix inputs and print git+file migration guidance
description: Improve the Home Manager update UX by detecting when the current flake pins Yazelix as a local path input and printing explicit migration guidance to git+file so users do not keep paying for full-repo path snapshots.
notes: This is the user-facing half of yazelix-odan. The goal is not to rewrite user-managed Home Manager files automatically, but to fail or warn clearly with the exact safer replacement when the current flake uses a local path input that points at a git checkout. Preference order: detect from flake.lock or flake metadata during yzx update home_manager, recognize local git repos, and print exact git+file:///... guidance.
close_reason: Implemented and verified locally: Home Manager path-input guidance plus filtered package/runtime source defaults.

title: Filter Yazelix package/runtime source trees before Nix packaging
description: Reduce Nix packaging overhead and shrink the package/runtime source surface by filtering the Yazelix repo before passing it into package/runtime builders. Exclude local build artifacts and planner state that are not part of the packaged runtime contract.
notes: This is the packaging half of yazelix-odan. Implement a shared source filter for package/runtime/HM-owned packaging that keeps only the shipped runtime surface plus the Rust core inputs it needs, while excluding rust_core/target, plugin target trees, logs, transient local state, and non-runtime repo baggage. This does not replace the separate local path-input UX fix.
close_reason: Implemented and verified locally: Home Manager path-input guidance plus filtered package/runtime source defaults.

title: Popup pane open latency is too high
description: Track the managed popup path taking around five seconds to open while closing remains fast. This is a popup-open-path performance bug, not a generic pane-slow issue. The work should identify where the latency actually lives: popup launch orchestration, Zellij pane creation, plugin coordination, shell/env setup, or the launched popup program itself, then remove unnecessary work or cache the expensive step.
design: Treat this as a measurement-first bug. Reproduce the latency with a concrete popup command and instrument the open path from /Alt+t through pane creation to first usable frame. Keep the scope on open latency only; close is already fast and should remain unchanged. Prefer deleting work or reusing existing state over adding more control layers.
acceptance_criteria: 1. There is a reproducible measurement of popup open latency before and after the fix. 2. The main source of open-path cost is identified and documented. 3. The managed popup opens substantially faster in normal use without regressing close/focus/toggle behavior. 4. Add one focused regression or benchmark guard at the seam that actually caused the latency.
close_reason: Popup wrappers now avoid importing the full core command suite on popup open, removing the main measured bootstrap cost.

title: Audit refresh and restart flows against cached launch state
description: Verify the real effects of yzx refresh and yzx restart against the cached launch profile, generated configs, and runtime state. Catch cases where Yazelix reports success but still reuses an environment that lacks the newly configured terminal or other runtime changes.
close_reason: Audit completed: launch and restart now reject stale cached/current environments that lack the configured terminal and re-enter a fresh environment.

title: Delete weak validation and generated clutter after v16.3
description: Run a delete-first pass over large validators, tests, generated fixtures, and documentation snapshots that make the main repo look canonical while defending implementation trivia or stale generation details. The goal is not fewer tests for its own sake; it is fewer weak tests and generated artifacts so the remaining validation budget protects real contracts.
design: Start from the LOC scorecard and the largest current validation/test/fixture surfaces. Review repo_contract_validation.rs, repo_validation.rs, large Rust test files, generated config examples, and any generated fixture snapshots. Keep tests that defend user-visible behavior, integration boundaries, failure modes, schema/source-of-truth invariants, or regressions that actually happened. Delete or demote checks that only assert command names, help trivia, string implementation details, palette constants, stale generated text, or duplicated validation already covered by a stronger contract. Do not weaken fail-fast behavior; replace broad clutter with narrower validators where needed.
acceptance_criteria: A pass records the largest validation/test/generated-clutter contributors, deletes or simplifies weak surfaces, and leaves behind a short result summary using the LOC scorecard. Existing governed test-strength rules still pass, docs/config validators still pass, and any deleted validation surface is either redundant with a stronger check or explicitly rejected as low-value clutter.
close_reason: Completed: deleted stale generated Zellij config snapshot and unreferenced v15 post-trim test coverage matrix, and removed the stale history pointer. Scorecard from 20b15246..HEAD: 680 text LOC deleted, generated -545, docs -135, tokei code LOC unchanged because the deleted generated KDL was not counted as code. Verified validate-docs-experience, validate-contracts, and reference scan.

title: Export a stable Yazelix config as a generated flake/package
description: Add an optional export path that compiles a stable Yazelix config (`yazelix.toml` + `yazelix_packs.toml`) into a generated flake/package for store-backed reuse.
design: Treat this as an optional compiled/export mode for stable configs, not as a blanket "remove devenv" rewrite. The generated output should be derived from the canonical Yazelix config contract, disposable/regenerable, and aligned with the store-backed package/runtime direction. Avoid a second hand-maintained config system or ad-hoc TOML-to-Nix duplication.
acceptance_criteria:
1. The issue is scoped as an optional export/compile path for stable configs, not a global removal of `devenv`.
2. The generated result derives from the canonical config contract rather than a second hand-maintained config system.
3. The exported output targets a store-backed flake/package shape that aligns with the existing nixpkgs package contract.
4. The generated flake/package is disposable derived output; TOML remains the editable source of truth unless a later design explicitly changes that.
5. The normal dynamic Yazelix runtime remains supported.
notes: Reframed from the vague "use the flake without devenv" wording into a narrower generator/export concept. This likely wants the config single-source work first and should stay aligned with the nixpkgs/store-backed package direction instead of inventing a third runtime model. Likely future command shape: `yzx export flake` or `yzx compile config`.
close_reason: The existing nix profile install flow already provides store-backed immutability. The acceptance criteria describe the existing runtime behavior. No compelling user story distinguishes this from normal Yazelix updates.

title: Investigate popup failure from config drift in stale Yazelix sessions
description:
Investigate why popup/menu panes fail hard when an already-running older Yazelix session sees a user config that has changed under a newer Yazelix version

## Context
Observed in an older Yazelix window after updating Yazelix and changing config schema
Opening a yzx_popup pane reported unknown config fields in ~/.config/yazelix/user_configs/yazelix.toml and blocked the popup entirely
Example fields: editor.initial_sidebar_state, terminal.ghostty_trail_duration, zellij.screen_saver_enabled, zellij.screen_saver_idle_seconds, zellij.screen_saver_style
The popup pane should not become unusable just because the user config has fields from a newer or older Yazelix schema, especially when the current session was already running before the update
design:
Separate startup/session-critical config validation from popup/menu rendering requirements
Determine whether popup panes can use already-materialized session facts, a tolerant read path, or a smaller config subset instead of full strict config normalization
Keep strict validation for commands that actually mutate or regenerate runtime state
Avoid silently accepting bad config for normal startup; the goal is stale-session resilience for transient popup/menu panes
acceptance_criteria:
Reproduce or model the stale-session popup failure path
Identify which popup/menu code path performs full config validation and why
Decision or fix defines whether popup panes should tolerate unknown config fields, use a cached session contract, or show a nonblocking warning
Add a behavior-backed regression test or validator case if a fix is implemented
notes:
Rust dependency decision: no new production or dev crates. This is a control-plane/config-read behavior fix using existing in-house config loading and test fixtures. If implementation stays in Nushell or POSIX, the same no-new-dependency decision applies. Packaging impact: none expected.
Implementation: transient-pane facts now use a tiny tolerant TOML subset reader for zellij.popup_program and popup geometry, so unrelated stale/unknown config fields do not block popup/menu pane construction. Popup panes already running in a Yazelix session no longer recompute the full runtime env from strict config before launching their program; they reuse the current process runtime environment. Strict config validation remains on startup/materialization/doctor-style paths. Verification: yzx dev rust fmt --check; yzx dev rust check; yzx dev rust test transient_pane_facts_compute_ignores_unrelated_unknown_fields; yzx dev rust test yzx_control_popup_pane_tolerates_unrelated_unknown_config_fields; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core --test yzx_core_owned_facts --test yzx_control_workspace_surface; validate-rust-test-traceability; git diff --check.
close_reason: Completed: popup/menu transient facts tolerate unrelated stale config fields, and in-pane popup launch no longer depends on full strict config normalization.

title: Audit and delete the remaining nushell_externs compatibility wrapper
description: Generated command metadata already moved to Rust, but nushell_externs.nu still survives as a compatibility wrapper. Track whether it can be deleted honestly or whether an explicit blocker still remains.
design: Do not assume this should disappear. First name the remaining real owner role, if any. Then either delete nushell_externs.nu or record a concrete stop condition instead of leaving it as vague background debt.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The first child records whether nushell_externs.nu still has a real blocker. 2. The second child either deletes it or records a precise stop. 3. The result keeps the public command-surface migration honest.
close_reason: all steps complete

title: Define whether nushell_externs.nu still has a real blocker
description: Audit the remaining owner role of nushell_externs.nu and decide whether it still blocks deletion.
design: Inventory every live caller and decide whether nushell_externs.nu is still authoritative for anything user-visible, or whether it is now just compatibility glue that can be deleted. Record the exact blocker if one remains.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The bead records every live caller of nushell_externs.nu. 2. It records a clear delete-or-stop decision basis. 3. If a blocker remains, it is named precisely.
notes: Caller inventory: product caller is nushell/scripts/setup/environment.nu shellhook step sync_yzx_extern_bridge; regression callers are nushell/scripts/dev/test_shell_managed_config_contracts.nu extern bridge tests; docs/specs still name nushell_externs.nu as a compatibility wrapper. Decision: no public command-metadata blocker remains. Rust already owns yzx command metadata and extern content through command_metadata.rs and yzx_core yzx-command-metadata.externs. The remaining real blocker is generated-file lifecycle ownership: path derivation, fingerprinting/reuse, atomic writes, and failed-refresh preservation still live in nushell/scripts/utils/nushell_externs.nu. Delete path: move that lifecycle into a private Rust helper command and keep environment.nu as a thin caller.
close_reason: Completed: no command-metadata blocker remains; nushell_externs.nu only owned generated extern lifecycle, so deletion path is Rust-owned sync command.

title: Delete nushell_externs.nu or record the explicit stop condition
description: Execute the nushell_externs decision: either delete the wrapper or record the explicit stop condition that keeps it alive.
design: If the decision bead says delete, remove nushell_externs.nu and move the remaining callers to the authoritative Rust-owned metadata surface. If it says stop, update the migration docs so the surviving seam is explicit and honest.
acceptance_criteria: 1. nushell_externs.nu is deleted or its stop condition is recorded in code/docs. 2. The public command-surface migration docs match reality. 3. Relevant command metadata/help/extern regressions still pass.
notes:
Rust dependency gate: production crates remain existing serde, serde_json, sha2, lexopt, and std only; no new crates. Dev-only crates unchanged. In-house logic: extern bridge path derivation, source fingerprinting, current-state check, atomic text writes, and failed-refresh preservation. Rejected alternatives: keeping nushell_externs.nu as compatibility glue, or inlining its cache lifecycle into setup/environment.nu. Packaging impact: none beyond the existing yzx_core helper binary.
Implemented delete path: removed nushell/scripts/utils/nushell_externs.nu; added yzx_core yzx-command-metadata.sync-externs for generated extern bridge cache lifecycle; setup/environment.nu now only calls the Rust sync command inside the existing shellhook profile step; test helpers call yzx_core directly. Verification: nu validate_syntax; nix develop -c cargo fmt --check; nix develop -c cargo test -p yazelix_core command_metadata; focused managed-shell extern tests; temp-home setup/environment.nu shellhook exercise; nu validate_specs; git diff --check.
close_reason: Completed: deleted nushell_externs.nu and moved generated yzx extern bridge lifecycle to yzx_core sync command.

title: v16.4 release notes
description: Add v16.4 release notes to CHANGELOG.md and docs/upgrade_notes.toml, and keep the README version marker aligned with YAZELIX_VERSION.
close_reason: Completed: added v16.4 release notes to CHANGELOG.md and docs/upgrade_notes.toml, reset unreleased to post-v16.4, and synced README version marker.

title: Fix Yazi 26.5.6 git fetcher schema
description: Yazi 26.5.6 requires plugin fetchers to use url plus required group. Yazelix still emits the old git fetcher shape with name and implicit group, causing Yazi to abort at startup with: missing field group. Apply the verified spike fix to the main repo.
close_reason: Fixed generated Yazi git fetchers for Yazi 26.5.6 by emitting url/run/group instead of the old name-based shape. Verified cargo fmt --check, git diff --check, and cargo test -p yazelix_core yazi_materialization.

title: Make adding custom Zellij layouts straightforward
description: Imported GitHub issue #438 after verifying that custom Zellij layouts are only easy in a narrow no-sidebar case and are otherwise blocked by hardcoded layout generation, hardcoded sidebar-family knowledge in the pane orchestrator, and stale docs.
design: Treat this as a structural customization problem rather than a docs-only paper cut. The supported path for adding layouts should be explicit: layout files must be discoverable or registered through one clear mechanism, sidebar layout families must expose enough metadata for the pane orchestrator to switch and toggle them without hardcoded family names, and unsupported layouts must fail fast with clear errors instead of silently disappearing. The documentation should reflect the actual current filenames and the real extension workflow.
acceptance_criteria: A maintainer can add a supported custom layout without touching multiple unrelated code paths by hand; generated Zellij config picks up the new layout through the documented mechanism; sidebar-aware layouts either work through a documented manifest or naming scheme or fail fast with a clear error; docs describe the current layout files and the actual extension workflow; automated tests cover the supported custom-layout path.
close_reason: Made top-level Zellij layout discovery automatic, documented the supported customization boundary, and added regression coverage for custom layout generation.

title: Use a floating Yazi picker from Helix when the sidebar is disabled
description: Narrower slice of the broader floating Yazi picker feature: provide a picker flow that remains useful when the normal sidebar is disabled, likely using a Zellij floating pane instead of depending on the persistent side surface.
design: Keep this aligned with the broader floating-picker direction instead of creating a second unrelated Helix-Yazi picker implementation. The key question is how the picker should behave when the file-tree sidebar is unavailable: invocation path, floating-pane UX, selected-path return, and cancellation behavior. This should remain compatible with a future general floating picker from Helix.
acceptance_criteria: There is a clear design or implementation path for using a floating Yazi picker from Helix when the sidebar is disabled, and it remains aligned with the broader picker feature rather than diverging into a duplicate flow.
close_reason: Closed on GitHub

title: Implement native config integration status report
description: Expose the native/managed/imported/read-only/generated config status model to doctor and the config UI using the native_config_integration_status contract.
design: Implement a shared classifier instead of separate doctor/UI wording. Preserve settings.jsonc as the semantic settings source; report per-tool status for Zellij, Yazi, Helix, terminals, and shell hooks; never mutate native files during status collection.
acceptance_criteria: Doctor and config UI use the same status labels from docs/contracts/native_config_integration_status.md; Zellij native read-only fallback, terminal.config_mode=user missing native config, Home Manager read-only settings, generated runtime outputs, native-available imports, and managed sidecars are classified with tests; import remains an explicit user action.
notes:
Rust dependency decision before implementation: no new production or dev crates. Build the native config status classifier in-house with std path checks, existing serde serialization, and existing config model data. Rejected alternatives: add platform/path discovery crates, infer imports by content comparison, or let doctor and config UI each maintain separate wording. Packaging impact: none; status collection must be read-only and must not mutate native files.
Implemented shared native config status classifier in rust_core/yazelix_core/src/native_config_status.rs. It reports contract status codes/labels for settings.jsonc, Zellij managed/native/generated surfaces, Yazi managed/native/generated surfaces, Helix managed/native/generated surfaces, terminal managed/native/generated surfaces including terminal.config_mode=user required-missing cases, and Yazelix shell hooks without inspecting native rc files.

Doctor now emits one Native config integration status finding with the shared entries, using the highest severity from the same classifier. Config UI model now carries the same status entries and shows them on the Advanced tab with shared labels, paths, allowed action, and read-only reasons. Status collection is read-only and does not infer imported_override without provenance.

Verification: yzx dev rust fmt --check; yzx dev rust check; yzx dev rust test native_config_status; yzx dev rust test config_ui; yzx dev rust test doctor_commands; cargo run --quiet --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_maintainer --bin yzx_repo_validator -- validate-contracts; git diff --check.
close_reason: Completed: added shared native config status classifier and wired doctor/config UI to it with focused coverage.

title: Evaluate Helix Lua fork versus upstream Steel plugin path
description:
Evaluate the editor-side fork in the road for Yazelix:

1. Adopt or optionally support a Helix fork that already has a functional Lua plugin system.
2. Stay aligned with upstream Helix, which is pursuing Scheme/Steel through the long-running plugin PR.
3. Support both, if Yazelix can keep the runtime/user contract clean without multiplying integration code.

This is an evaluation bead, not an implementation bead. The goal is to decide which editor extension path best serves Yazelix before adding config or runtime complexity.
design:
Research summary, 2026-05-22:
- Helix upstream did not publish a clean Lua rejection RFC. Lua appears to have lost mostly because maintainers chose Scheme/Steel instead.
- The most direct Lua-specific comment found is kirawi saying they did not recall the exact reason, but thought Blaž/archseer did not enjoy writing Lua: https://github.com/helix-editor/helix/discussions/3806#discussioncomment-3629659
- Upstream maintainer rationale for Scheme/Steel is stronger than anti-Lua rationale:
  - Helix is maintainer-preference-led, not popularity-led.
  - Scheme is small enough to own, parse, and maintain long term.
  - Scheme macros are a real attraction and support an Emacs-like programmable-editor story.
  - A single language/runtime avoids ecosystem fragmentation.
  - Large dependency/runtime choices are treated as long-term maintenance liabilities.
  - Prior Wasm attempts ran into ABI, memory-layout, interface maturity, and large-document-copying problems.
- Key upstream references:
  - Plugin system discussion: https://github.com/helix-editor/helix/discussions/3806
  - archseer Scheme/Steel rationale and Wasm rejection: https://github.com/helix-editor/helix/discussions/3806#discussioncomment-6686976
  - Wasm PR concerns and shift toward Scheme: https://github.com/helix-editor/helix/pull/2949#issuecomment-1179686877
  - Wasm maturity summary from implementation attempt: https://github.com/helix-editor/helix/pull/2949#issuecomment-1186449110
  - Steel PR: https://github.com/helix-editor/helix/pull/8675

Yazelix-specific framing:
- Lua is pragmatically attractive because Yazi and WezTerm already use Lua, a Helix Lua fork exists, many terminal/editor users know Lua from Neovim, and Lua is easier for AI agents to generate than Scheme.
- Steel/Scheme is upstream-aligned and may eventually become the official Helix extension language, but it has schedule and ecosystem risk.
- The decision should focus on Yazelix user value and integration cost, not abstract language purity.

Possible outcomes:
- Prefer upstream Helix + Steel: safest upstream alignment, least fork risk, slower plugin maturity, weaker cross-tool Lua unification.
- Prefer Helix Lua fork: better Yazelix stack consistency and faster Lua plugin experimentation, higher fork maintenance and divergence risk.
- Support both: maximizes optionality but risks multiplying editor integration paths unless Yazelix defines one semantic action API and keeps Lua/Steel as adapters only.

Important constraint:
If both paths are supported, Yazelix must not duplicate editor behavior in two unrelated implementations. Shared editor actions should live behind a Yazelix semantic API, with Lua/Steel/native Helix adapters calling the same contract.
acceptance_criteria:
Decision is ready when it answers:
- Which Helix runtime should Yazelix default to today: upstream Helix, Lua fork, or configurable choice?
- What user-visible value does the Lua fork unlock that upstream Helix cannot yet provide?
- What maintenance risk does adopting the Lua fork create?
- Can Yazelix support both without duplicate editor integration code?
- What minimal spike would prove the chosen path?

The decision should explicitly name the default, optional supported variants, and whether editor extension support belongs in the broader Yazelix action/event contract from yazelix-mgl1.
notes: Initial take: do not make Lua-vs-Steel a purity fight. For Yazelix, Lua is compelling as the common scripting layer across Yazi, WezTerm, and a Helix fork. Upstream Steel is compelling as the official Helix trajectory. Supporting both may be viable only if Yazelix keeps one semantic editor/workspace contract and treats Lua/Steel as thin adapters.

comments.author: lucca
comments.text:
Lua fork audit note, 2026-05-30:

Audited the local gj1118/helix reference clone only as a Lua-plugin design reference. Verdict: do not base Yazelix on this fork right now.

Evidence:
- The clone was clean at 2b833dcf and was broadly divergent from upstream Helix.
- `cargo test -p helix-plugin` failed before tests due to a cfg mismatch around `ClipboardProvider::Termcode`: the variant is gated behind the term feature, but match arms reference it in a build path that does not enable that feature.
- Plugin config is not wired through the normal Helix config surface; the terminal app constructs `PluginConfig::default()` directly.
- The Lua editor bridge uses a thread-local raw editor pointer and unsafe static mutable access.
- The sandbox is shallow: it removes a few globals such as process/file-loading entrypoints, but it is not a mature capability model.
- Several APIs appear placeholder-level or incomplete, while the repo docs describe the implementation more confidently than the current source supports.

Good ideas to keep as references:
- Lua ergonomics and familiarity are still attractive for users and AI agents.
- Manifest-style plugin packaging is worth studying.
- Explicit command registration and host callbacks are the right shape.

Decision implication: stay on the Yazelix-owned Steel fork for now, keep Lua as a possible future adapter only behind the broader Yazelix action/event contract, and do not multiply editor integrations unless the fork becomes build-clean and semantically mature.

Verified validate-docs-experience and git diff --check.

title: Make yzx doctor eligible for an honest public Rust owner cut
description: Track the delete-first prep work needed before any future public Rust or Clap-owned `yzx doctor` lane is reconsidered. The current doctor surface is still too mixed to justify another public Rust owner cut, but the surviving structured-report bridge cluster can be collapsed so the later go/no-go decision is based on real remaining owners instead of stale wrapper shape.
design: Do not reopen the generic public-CLI rewrite track. Keep this focused on the specific deletion work that could make `yzx doctor` honestly eligible later. First collapse the structured report bridge around `doctor.nu`, `doctor_helix_report.nu`, `doctor_runtime_report.nu`, and `install_ownership_report.nu`. Keep human rendering, remediation prose, and fix actions in Nushell unless a whole family actually disappears. Only after that bridge collapse should Yazelix re-evaluate whether a public Rust doctor cut has a real deletion budget.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The doctor bridge-collapse child names or lands the exact report-transport owners that shrink or disappear. 2. A follow-up decision child records a concrete go/no-go call for any future public Rust `yzx doctor` cut based on the surviving Nu owners after the collapse. 3. This epic does not become a generic Clap migration bucket or a thin Rust parser over the same Nushell doctor tree.
close_reason: all steps complete

title: Collapse the doctor and install report bridge cluster
description: The current doctor family still bounces already-structured Rust findings through several Nushell owners: `doctor.nu`, `doctor_helix_report.nu`, `doctor_runtime_report.nu`, and `install_ownership_report.nu`. Collapse that bridge layer so one shared report-transport seam carries the structured data and the remaining Nu code only owns the human-facing rendering and repair logic that still adds product value.
design: Delete first. Do not port prose or fix actions into Rust by habit. Start by inventorying which request shaping, response shaping, and shared classification branches are duplicated across the doctor report files today. Prefer deleting or centralizing those transport seams before extracting more helpers. A good outcome is one clear structured-report transport seam plus thinner Nu renderers, not one more wrapper layer.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The surviving doctor/install report files no longer each own parallel request/response shaping for already structured Rust data. 2. At least one real Nu bridge owner disappears or materially shrinks. 3. Human rendering and fix actions stay in Nushell unless a whole family actually moves. 4. Focused doctor regressions still defend the user-visible report contracts.
notes: Collapsed the doctor/install report bridge cluster into `nushell/scripts/utils/doctor_report_bridge.nu`. Deleted `doctor_helix_report.nu`, `doctor_runtime_report.nu`, and `install_ownership_report.nu`; `doctor.nu` now only owns rendering, summary, Zellij plugin health, and fix actions. Repointed install/launcher/desktop/home-manager helpers at the shared bridge and updated the migration docs/specs to describe the new single seam.
close_reason: Completed

title: Re-evaluate yzx doctor as a public Rust owner cut after the bridge collapse
description: After the doctor/install report bridge cluster is collapsed, decide whether `yzx doctor` has become an honest next public Rust family or whether it should remain Nushell-owned. This is explicitly a post-cleanup go/no-go decision, not permission to add a Rust parser above the same surviving doctor tree.
design: Audit the surviving owners in `core/yzx_doctor.nu`, `utils/doctor.nu`, `doctor_helix_report.nu`, `doctor_runtime_report.nu`, `install_ownership_report.nu`, and any remaining fix helpers after the bridge collapse lands. The only acceptable positive outcome is a real deletion budget for a future public Rust doctor cut. If the surviving Nu rendering, remediation, and fix ownership still dominate, close this as a no-go instead of reviving the generic Clap rewrite lane.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The bead names the exact surviving Nushell owners after the bridge collapse. 2. It records a clear go/no-go decision for any future public Rust `yzx doctor` cut. 3. If go, it names the deletion budget and parity harness; if no-go, it records the blocker owners explicitly.
notes: Decision: no-go for a public Rust `yzx doctor` cut after `yazelix-osco.1`. The post-collapse family still has three meaningful Nushell owners: `core/yzx_doctor.nu` keeps the public command surface and `--json`/`--fix` split, `utils/doctor.nu` keeps the human renderer, summary logic, Zellij pane-orchestrator live health checks, and all current fix actions, and `utils/doctor_report_bridge.nu` still shapes multi-source install/runtime/Helix/config requests before the Rust helpers run. That is not an honest deletion budget for a public Rust or Clap-owned doctor family yet.
close_reason: No-go decision recorded

title: Make Helix reveal integration self-contained in Yazelix

The goal is for Yazelix-managed Helix sessions to get the reveal binding and related Yazelix-specific config from Yazelix-owned state instead of depending on the user's personal ~/.config/helix/config.toml.

title: Desktop relaunch rebuild must stay visible instead of happening invisibly

Track the remaining desktop-entry UX regression so rebuild/setup work happens in the visible Yazelix window rather than invisibly behind the launcher.
close_reason: Completed

title: Evaluate Yazi Kitty/KGP policy under patched Zellij
description: Current Yazi 26.5.6 forces Sixel whenever ZELLIJ_SESSION_NAME exists, so normal Yazelix-in-Zellij image previews do not use Kitty/KGP even under Ghostty. The Zellij Kitty APC passthrough spike proves patched Zellij can render the forced-KGP path, but adoption would also require a deliberate Yazi/Yazelix adapter-policy decision. Evaluate whether Yazelix should keep Sixel as the default, patch/configure Yazi to allow KGP under a known patched Zellij, or pursue an upstream-friendly capability signal.
design: Inputs from yazelix-m0c0.1: stock Zellij + forced KGP stayed blank; patched Zellij + forced KGP rendered and cleared; default Yazi-in-Zellij rendered through Sixel. Evaluate fork maintenance cost, Yazi patch surface, Ghostty/WezTerm behavior, fallback semantics, and whether the user-visible gain beats the cost.
notes:
Starting evaluation with the smallest possible Yazi fork patch. Proposed contract: keep upstream behavior by default, but if Yazelix launches Yazi under a patched Zellij and sets an explicit env flag, Yazi should not discard Ghostty's KGP adapter just because ZELLIJ_SESSION_NAME exists. No new crates expected; patch should be isolated to adapter policy and tests if the source has a suitable test seam.
Dual-fork experiment evidence (2026-05-22): created Yazi worktree /home/lucca/pjs/worktrees/yazi_kgp_policy at f6db84c with a minimal adapter-policy env gate. Yazi keeps upstream behavior by default: ZELLIJ_SESSION_NAME still forces Sixel unless YAZELIX_ZELLIJ_KITTY_PASSTHROUGH is present. With the flag, Ghostty maps to Adapter::Kgp via yazi-adapter/src/adapters.rs. Built a real Yazelix runtime at /nix/store/yrwmz749y0jhmpmvy2gf41pdmzlk92h6-yazelix using patched Zellij /nix/store/iyypnxc307l8k4mwn90cvsz49h0vigg2-zellij-0.44.3 and patched Yazi /nix/store/l33s33wh59030rvs59ib3iza4jnc33q9-yazi-26.5.6. Live Ghostty test inside Zellij session with both ZELLIJ_SESSION_NAME and YAZELIX_ZELLIJ_KITTY_PASSTHROUGH=1 rendered assets/logo.png and cleared after moving off the image. Screenshots: /tmp/yazelix_screens/Screenshot_2026-05-22_03-59-51.png and /tmp/yazelix_screens/Screenshot_2026-05-22_04-00-53.png. Verification: cargo test -p yazi-adapter zellij; git diff --check; nix build of dual-fork Yazelix package.
Final recommendation: keep current upstream/Sixel behavior as the default until Yazelix deliberately consumes both patched forks. The dual-fork path is viable and strategically attractive for Ghostty because Ghostty maps to Yazi Adapter::Kgp and the patched Zellij forwards Kitty graphics APC frames. The adoption gate should be package/runtime-owned, not user-config-owned: only a Ghostty runtime with both patched packages should export YAZELIX_ZELLIJ_KITTY_PASSTHROUGH=1. Do not apply the same signal to WezTerm; Yazi maps WezTerm to IIP before Sixel, and the current Zellij patch does not implement iTerm2 image passthrough. Created follow-up yazelix-owh3.1 for the adoption path.
close_reason: Completed decision: a Ghostty-scoped dual-fork KGP path is viable. Verified patched Yazi policy, patched Zellij passthrough, real dual-fork Yazelix build, live Ghostty render/clear behavior, and normal sidebar env inheritance. Follow-up adoption bead: yazelix-owh3.1.

title: Adopt Ghostty-scoped dual-fork KGP preview path
description: Implement the adoption path only if Yazelix decides to ship the patched Zellij+Yazi pair: consume/lock the patched Zellij and Yazi forks, enable YAZELIX_ZELLIJ_KITTY_PASSTHROUGH only for the Ghostty runtime that uses those patched packages, and keep WezTerm/Sixel behavior unchanged until an explicit WezTerm image-protocol bridge exists.
design: Use the decision evidence from yazelix-owh3. The env signal is safe only when both sides are true: patched Zellij forwards Kitty graphics APC frames, and patched Yazi treats the signal as permission to keep Ghostty's KGP adapter inside Zellij. Do not set the signal for WezTerm, because Yazi's WezTerm adapter order prefers iTerm2 inline images before Sixel and this Zellij patch does not bridge IIP. Prefer package/runtime gating over a user config knob.
acceptance_criteria: A Ghostty Yazelix runtime built from locked patched forks launches the normal managed Yazi sidebar with YAZELIX_ZELLIJ_KITTY_PASSTHROUGH=1 in its environment; selecting an image renders and moving away clears it; WezTerm runtime does not receive the env signal and retains current Sixel behavior; docs/bead notes explain the fork boundary and rollback path.
notes:
Adoption completed as an experimental Ghostty-only runtime path.

Published forks:
- Zellij: https://github.com/luccahuguet/yazelix-zellij tag yazelix-kgp-preview-0, branch yazelix_kgp_preview, commit c10aad0d Add Yazelix Kitty graphics passthrough spike
- Yazi: https://github.com/luccahuguet/yazelix-yazi tag yazelix-kgp-preview-0, branch yazelix_kgp_preview, commit f6db84c Allow Yazelix patched Zellij Kitty passthrough

Main integration:
- Yazelix commit 08d14a0f Add Ghostty KGP preview runtime
- Added locked flake inputs yazelixZellij and yazelixYazi, both pinned to yazelix-kgp-preview-0
- Added preview outputs runtime_ghostty_kgp_preview, runtime_ghostty_kgp_preview_agent_tools, yazelix_ghostty_kgp_preview, and yazelix_ghostty_kgp_preview_agent_tools
- Added a runtime marker at runtime_features/zellij_kitty_passthrough; shells/posix/yazelix_ghostty.sh exports YAZELIX_ZELLIJ_KITTY_PASSTHROUGH=1 only when that marker is present
- Stable Ghostty and WezTerm runtime builds keep the marker absent and do not receive the env signal
- Home Manager supports programs.yazelix.package so a user can select the prebuilt preview package instead of rebuilding from module options

Local daily-driver switch:
- /home/lucca/.config/home-manager commit 7a6c294 Use Yazelix KGP preview runtime
- programs.yazelix.package now points to inputs.yazelix.packages..yazelix_ghostty_kgp_preview_agent_tools
- home-manager switch succeeded and ~/.nix-profile/bin/yzx resolves to /nix/store/lmdmwiivr9w29j9jfsvp09fvchwk7znf-yazelix/bin/yzx
- Preview runtime marker is present and toolbin/tu is present

Verification:
- nix flake lock
- nix eval --raw .#packages.x86_64-linux.yazelix_ghostty_kgp_preview.drvPath
- nix eval --raw .#packages.x86_64-linux.runtime_ghostty_kgp_preview.drvPath
- git diff --check
- nix build .#yazelix_ghostty_kgp_preview --print-out-paths
- nix build .#yazelix_ghostty --print-out-paths --no-link
- nix build .#runtime_wezterm --no-link --print-out-paths
- nix build .#yazelix_ghostty_kgp_preview_agent_tools --print-out-paths
- nix build .#homeConfigurations.lucca@loqness.activationPackage --print-out-paths in /home/lucca/.config/home-manager
- home-manager switch --flake .#lucca@loqness

Rollback path: switch Home Manager back to the normal Yazelix package or remove programs.yazelix.package; stable outputs are unchanged and omit the passthrough marker.
close_reason: Published Yazelix Zellij/Yazi KGP preview forks, added locked Ghostty-only preview runtime outputs, switched local Home Manager runtime to the preview package, and verified stable Ghostty/WezTerm paths remain unmarked.

title: Delete inline detached-launch probe shell assembly and prove the remaining terminal-launch Nu floor
description: terminal_launcher.nu still owns a fixed detached-launch probe shell program shape. That shell body should live in one checked-in POSIX helper, and any remaining Nu around terminal launch must justify itself against fixed helpers or Rust-owned planning.
design: Keep dynamic terminal argv assembly in Nu only if it is still genuinely caller-specific and irreducible. Move every fixed shell body and repeated probe path into checked-in helpers.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The detached-launch probe contract is explicit. 2. The fixed shell body lives in one checked-in POSIX helper. 3. terminal_launcher.nu no longer assembles that shell program inline. 4. Any surviving Nu terminal-launch logic has explicit proof of irreducibility.
close_reason: all steps complete

title: Define the fixed POSIX helper contract for detached launch probing
description: Define the exact argv, environment, log, and exit-status contract for the detached-launch probe helper so the fixed probe body can leave terminal_launcher.nu cleanly.
design: Separate the fixed probe program from caller-local launch command assembly. The helper should be dumb and stable.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Helper argv and env inputs are explicit. 2. Success/failure reporting is explicit. 3. The remaining caller-local Nu responsibilities are explicit.
close_reason: Completed

title: Implement the detached launch probe helper cut
description: Move the fixed detached-launch probe body into a checked-in POSIX helper and delete the inline shell-program assembly from terminal_launcher.nu.
design: No new Nushell trampoline. Keep one fixed helper plus caller-local argv passing.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The fixed probe body is no longer assembled inline in Nu. 2. terminal_launcher.nu is materially smaller. 3. Launch-probe regressions stay covered.
notes: Dependency decision: add no new crates. Keep the launch probe as a fixed POSIX helper plus in-house Rust/Nu argv wiring; do not introduce shell-generation crates or additional helper frameworks.
close_reason: Completed

title: Own KGP Yazi package boundary
description:
The KGP Yazi override has the same structural risk PR #596 exposed for Zellij: Yazelix swaps the pinned `yazelixYazi` code source through `yazi-unwrapped.overrideAttrs`, but still inherits source-coupled Cargo vendor metadata from the consumer's `pkgs.yazi-unwrapped`. A poisoned consumer probe confirms `cargoDeps` leaks through the current override.

Scope:
- Add a Yazelix-owned KGP Yazi package helper that couples `srcs`, `sourceRoot`, version, and Cargo vendor deps.
- Have graphics runtimes consume that helper instead of open-coding the Yazi source override in `flake.nix`.
- Add a narrow validator poison check for Yazi cargo deps, matching the Zellij boundary check.
- Avoid a generic Nix source linter or broad cache policy.

Acceptance criteria:
- `flake.nix` has no inline KGP Yazi `overrideAttrs` source/vendor coupling logic.
- Poisoning consumer `pkgs.yazi-unwrapped.cargoDeps` does not affect the Yazelix KGP Yazi helper.
- `nix build .#runtime_ghostty --no-link --no-write-lock-file` passes.
notes: Release-note linkage: v17.1 should name this as one of the post-v17 commits that introduced a Yazelix-owned Yazi fork/package boundary paired with the Yazelix Zellij KGP passthrough fork for Yazi image previews through Zellij.
close_reason: Implemented owned KGP Yazi package boundary. `packaging/yazelix_kgp_yazi.nix` now couples the pinned `yazelixYazi` code source, upstream manpage source, sourceRoot, version, and Cargo vendor hash; graphics runtimes call that helper instead of open-coding a `yazi-unwrapped` source override in `flake.nix`. Added a narrow Nix customization validator poison check that makes consumer `pkgs.yazi-unwrapped.cargoDeps` throw and proves the helper still returns `yazi-26.5.6-vendor`. Verification: `cargo fmt --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml --all --check`; `yzx_repo_validator validate-nix-customization-api`; `yzx_repo_validator validate-flake-interface`; poisoned Yazi helper `nix eval`; `nix build .#runtime_ghostty --no-link --no-write-lock-file`. LOC scorecard vs origin/main: +45 code LOC total, packaging +26 net, maintainer +22 net.

title: Split yazi.nu by sidebar-state, sync, and editor-open ownership
description: `nushell/scripts/integrations/yazi.nu` is a large production module that currently mixes several distinct owners at once: sidebar state discovery, reveal and refresh flows, cwd sync behavior, editor-kind resolution, and file-open routing into managed editors. At roughly 713 lines, it is now large enough that leaving it implicit would undercut the broader Nushell-streamlining goal.
notes:
2026-04-08 creation rationale:
- `integrations/yazi.nu` is currently one of the largest production Nushell modules and was not modeled explicitly enough in the earlier shortlist.
- It mixes at least five concerns: command resolution (`yazi`/`ya`), sidebar state lookup and pane-id mapping, reveal/refresh/sync flows, managed editor cwd sync, and editor-open routing.
- Delete-first direction: do not start with helper extraction. First identify which flows should disappear or move behind narrower contracts, especially any overlap with `integrations/zellij.nu` or future sidebar-ownership work under `yazelix-ewjz.1`.

2026-04-08 refinement after direct code read:
- Current file size is ~713 lines.
- Likely stable survivors are the narrow command-resolution and `ya emit-to` bridge pieces.
- Likely seams to question first are file-backed sidebar identity scanning, managed-editor cwd sync, and editor-open routing mixed into the Yazi integration layer.
- `open_file_with_editor` may belong closer to editor-integration ownership than sidebar-sync ownership.

2026-04-08 first ownership cut landed:
- Moved the managed-editor family out of `integrations/yazi.nu` into `integrations/managed_editor.nu`:
  - `get_managed_editor_kind`
  - `sync_managed_editor_cwd`
  - `resolve_managed_editor_open_strategy`
  - `open_file_with_editor` and its helper openers
- Updated `yzx cwd`, `open_file.nu`, `zoxide_open_in_editor.nu`, and the defending tests to depend on that narrower owner instead of pulling editor policy through `yazi.nu`.
- Result: `integrations/yazi.nu` dropped from 713 lines to 457 lines after the editor-family move.
- Verification: `run_yazi_canonical_tests`, `run_workspace_canonical_tests`, `validate_syntax.nu`, and `validate_default_test_traceability.nu` all pass.

2026-04-08 second ownership cut landed:
- Moved sidebar-state discovery and cache lookup out of `integrations/yazi.nu` into `integrations/yazi_sidebar_state.nu`:
  - sidebar state-path derivation
  - active-session/pane lookup
  - active sidebar state/id/cwd resolution
- Kept `integrations/yazi.nu` focused on Yazi command resolution, bootstrap cwd consumption, and active Yazi actions (`sync`, `refresh`, `reveal`).
- Result: `integrations/yazi.nu` now owns command/config plus action behavior, while `managed_editor.nu` owns editor policy and `yazi_sidebar_state.nu` owns sidebar-state discovery.
- Verification: `run_yazi_canonical_tests`, `run_workspace_canonical_tests`, `validate_syntax.nu`, and `validate_default_test_traceability.nu` all pass.
close_reason: yazi command/action ownership is now separated from managed-editor policy and sidebar-state discovery

title: Add boids welcome style
design: Add a boids-inspired welcome style that stays cheap enough for startup, runs for a short bounded time, and resolves into a readable final frame. The implementation may be deterministic lightweight live rendering or pre-rendered frame playback, whichever better preserves the startup budget and layout fit.
acceptance_criteria: Selecting welcome_style = boids renders the boids sequence within the startup budget, preserves readability in narrow layouts, and is defended by tests for bounded frame generation and layout fit.
close_reason: Completed

title: Make the installed runtime own devenv so yzx update runtime can subsume yzx update devenv
description: Today the installed runtime still depends on a separate profile-installed devenv CLI. Runtime-localize devenv so the flake-installed Yazelix runtime carries the exact devenv surface it needs, bootstrap prefers that runtime-local path, and the user-facing update model can collapse toward one runtime update instead of separate runtime and devenv update commands.
design: Keep this delete-first. Do not add another compatibility layer around the existing split if the real fix is to stop depending on a separately managed profile CLI. First prove the runtime can ship or otherwise deterministically resolve the exact devenv it needs. Then switch bootstrap and launch helpers to prefer that runtime-local devenv path. Only after that should Yazelix reconsider whether yzx update devenv still needs to exist as a first-class user command.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The installed/flaked Yazelix runtime no longer requires a separate user-profile devenv install for normal launch, refresh, restart, and desktop-launch flows. 2. Bootstrap and launch helpers resolve a runtime-local or otherwise runtime-owned devenv path first. 3. The user-facing update contract is reevaluated afterward, with a clear keep/remove decision for yzx update devenv. 4. The result simplifies, rather than multiplies, update surfaces and bootstrap state.
close_reason: Completed

title: Expose Ghostty trail duration as a supported yazelix.toml setting
description:
Yazelix already exposes Ghostty cursor palette, trail effect, mode effect, and glow through the main terminal config surface, but it does not expose trail duration as a supported setting. Users who want a shorter or longer Ghostty cursor trail currently need terminal-native overrides or unsupported edits instead of one clear Yazelix-owned knob in yazelix.toml.

Add a first-class trail-duration setting to the main terminal config surface so users can tune the Ghostty cursor trail lifetime through the same supported path as the other Ghostty cursor options.
design:
Keep this narrow and consistent with the existing main-config contract. Prefer a field name that matches the current terminal.ghostty_trail_* family, likely terminal.ghostty_trail_duration. Define one supported numeric range and fail fast on invalid values rather than silently clamping or ignoring them. Update all owned config surfaces together: yazelix_default.toml, config_metadata/main_config_contract.toml, home_manager/module.nix, parser/schema behavior, and the Ghostty config renderer that emits the final terminal config.

Do not turn this into a general Ghostty raw-config escape hatch. The goal is one maintained, documented setting with the same direct-config and Home Manager behavior as the rest of the main Ghostty cursor surface.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Users can set Ghostty trail duration through the main Yazelix config surface without resorting to terminal-native overrides.
2. The setting is present and synchronized across yazelix_default.toml, the canonical config contract, and the Home Manager module.
3. Invalid values fail clearly during config validation instead of being silently ignored.
4. Generated Ghostty config output reflects the configured trail duration, with regression coverage for direct-config and Home Manager paths.
notes:
Dependency decision for Ghostty trail duration: use no new Rust crates. Production and dev-only crate sets stay unchanged. Build validation and rendering in-house because this is a small typed numeric config field wired through the existing contract/schema/materialization helpers. Rejected alternatives: a raw Ghostty config escape hatch or generic numeric parsing crate, both broader than the maintained knob requested. Packaging impact: none beyond normal Rust/core validation.
Implemented terminal.ghostty_trail_duration as a supported duration multiplier with default 1.0 and range 0.25..=4.0. Wired the field through yazelix_default.toml, main_config_contract.toml, Home Manager, config normalization, yzx_core explicit/from-env Ghostty materialization, terminal materialization, launch rerolls, and the Ghostty shader builder. Generated Ghostty config now records the multiplier, color trail shaders scale their tuned DURATION constants, movement effect shaders scale DURATION, and mode-change effects keep their tuned timing. Verification: full yzx_core_config_normalize test file, focused Ghostty materialization/range tests, yzx dev rust fmt --check, yzx dev rust check, validate-config-surface-contract, validate-specs, validate-rust-test-traceability, validate-nushell-syntax, validate-flake-interface, git diff --check.
close_reason: Completed: exposed terminal.ghostty_trail_duration as a validated config/Home Manager setting and wired it into Ghostty shader generation with regression coverage.

title: Define explicit Yazelix session resurrection contract
description:
## Decision
Pending. Define the explicit user-facing Yazelix session resurrection contract separately from restart-layout capture.

## Rationale
Zellij owns low-level session serialization, while Yazelix owns product coherence, config snapshots, diagnostics, and avoiding automatic command resurrection surprises. The contract must be explicit before any save/restore UX.

## Alternatives Considered
Use Zellij resurrection directly; implement Yazelix explicit restore on top of Zellij state; save only command-free layouts; or reject session resurrection beyond restart skeletons.

## Existing Context
Define the canonical user-facing and subsystem contract for explicit Yazelix session resurrection. The contract should state which restored state is supported, which state is intentionally unsupported, how per-window config snapshots interact with restored Zellij sessions, and which diagnostics users/maintainers get when a restored session is incomplete or stale.
design: Reason from first principles: Zellij owns low-level session serialization; Yazelix owns product coherence and diagnostics. Keep restore explicit because users may keep many Yazelix windows open concurrently.
acceptance_criteria: A canonical contract or bead decision defines supported/unsupported restored state, explicit restore entrypoints, config-snapshot/runtime compatibility behavior, and at least one concrete diagnostic/verification path.
notes: Prime-beads 2026-05-04: keep this contract distinct from yazelix-l18h. The current restart path is command-free layout skeleton capture/sanitization for yzx restart; this bead remains about explicit full-session resurrection/save-restore semantics where Zellij may preserve commands/process state. Do not use this bead to reintroduce automatic default resurrection or resurrection prompt contamination into new tabs.

title: for every comment that is automated or similar prefix it with something like "Automated: <message>"
description:
Imported GitHub issue #458.

could be only for new comments
close_reason: Completed: canonical GitHub issue comments created by the Beads sync flow are now prefixed with Automated:, legacy unprefixed Beads comments are still detected for update, and the maintainer contract test plus AGENTS guidance were updated

title: Make generated Yazi bundled static assets content-correct
description: Generated Yazi TOML/Lua files are rewritten atomically during materialization, but bundled static plugin/flavor assets are only checked for missing target paths. Existing but stale or corrupt generated asset files can be treated as healthy.
design:
Keep bundled generated Yazi static assets disposable, but do not regress the known warm-start performance boundary from yazelix-stb6 / yazelix-8h9y. Full delete-and-recreate is acceptable for explicit repair, runtime/input changes, install/update refresh, or a detected stale/corrupt generated asset tree. It is not acceptable as the default unchanged warm-start behavior.

Preferred shape: add a simple content-correctness guard for Yazelix-owned bundled plugin/flavor/starship assets, such as a runtime-owned manifest, source fingerprint, or direct hash comparison for the generated asset tree. The guard should be cheap on the no-op path and should drive one existing asset-sync implementation when repair is needed. Avoid duplicate copy logic and avoid broad cache machinery.

Correctness rule: a stale or corrupt existing generated bundled asset must be repaired; user-managed Yazi config inputs under ~/.config/yazelix/yazi remain outside this static-asset ownership path.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Materialization repairs stale or corrupt bundled generated Yazi plugin/flavor/starship assets, not only missing files.
2. Unchanged warm startup does not unconditionally delete, recreate, or recopy the bundled Yazi static asset tree.
3. Runtime/input changes, install/update refresh, explicit repair, or detected stale/corrupt generated assets still trigger a full enough resync to restore correctness.
4. User-managed Yazi config paths under ~/.config/yazelix/yazi remain untouched except through existing managed merge rules.
5. Focused regression covers an existing stale bundled asset being corrected and an unchanged warm path preserving a sentinel file or equivalent proof that static assets were not recopied.
6. Implementation reuses one asset-sync path and avoids duplicate copy logic or broad cache machinery.
notes: 2026-05-20 planning update: historical evidence shows Yazi static asset recopy was a real warm-start risk. yazelix-stb6 moved bundled Yazi asset sync out of warm startup, commit 1efa6e4e was titled Skip warm Yazi static asset recopy, yazelix-8h9y records Yazi config generation dominating warm startup, and yazelix-4xp1.2 records runtime materialization around 500ms on desktop and managed launch. This bead should therefore repair stale/corrupt bundled assets without restoring unconditional warm-path recopy.
close_reason: Completed: generated Yazi bundled static asset checks now compare source-rendered content, not just target existence. Warm unchanged materialization preserves existing generated asset trees when content is current, while stale/corrupt bundled plugin/flavor/starship files trigger the existing static asset sync path. Runtime planning marks stale generated Yazi static assets as repair_missing_artifacts so warm no-op startup still reaches repair. Verification: cargo fmt --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml --all --check; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core yazi_materialization --lib; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core --test yzx_core_yazi_materialization yazi_materialization_repairs_stale_bundled_assets_without_warm_recopy; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core runtime_materialization --lib; cargo check --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core; git diff --check.

title: Revisit override-layout via owned explicit-run pane creation
description: Capture the future path for returning to Zellij override-layout transitions after the current rollback to layout-family swapping. The experiment showed that override_layout only becomes viable if Yazelix owns the relevant pane-creation surfaces and opens panes with stable explicit run instructions instead of default-shell panes that Zellij normalizes to invoked_with = None. Scope this as design + implementation exploration, not an immediate bugfix. Evaluate which pane creation surfaces Yazelix can realistically own (Alt+m, pane-mode creation, split flows), whether built-in Zellij Run/NewTiledPane/NewStackedPane actions are enough, and what exact contract would let Alt y / Alt [ ] preserve live panes under override_layout without duplicating panes or sidebar state.
design: Treat this as the current explicit-run path back toward override-layout after the earlier rollback. The core question is whether Yazelix can own enough pane-creation surfaces to preserve live panes under override_layout by using stable explicit run instructions instead of anonymous shell panes. This should remain a design and contract bead first, not a promise that override-layout is immediately ready to ship again.
acceptance_criteria: There is a concrete contract for what Yazelix would need to own in order to make override-layout viable again, including pane creation surfaces, run-instruction identity, and layout-transition invariants. If the path is still too costly or constrained, that is captured explicitly instead of leaving override-layout in limbo.
notes:
2026-05-07 decision start: resolving as part of yazelix-pomi layout ownership gate. Rust dependency gate: no Rust implementation planned; documentation/ownership decision only; no new crates or packaging impact.
2026-05-07 decision: rejected for this pass. Override-layout only becomes reliable if Yazelix owns enough pane-creation surfaces to give panes stable explicit run instructions, but that would require replacing too many native Zellij split/new-pane flows. The current supported layout owner remains built-in layout families plus swap layouts, with pane-orchestrator owning live sidebar/layout state after startup.
close_reason: Rejected for this pass: override-layout requires broad Yazelix ownership of Zellij pane creation and is not a deletion enabler now.

title: evaluate adding a little hash to the yazelix bar so each user knows their exact version
description: Imported GitHub issue #571.
close_reason: Not planned: the exact-version/debugging use case belongs in yzx status and yzx status --versions, not in the always-visible Zellij bar. Keeping the bar clean is more valuable than adding a commit hash there; a future narrower build-id improvement can target yzx status if needed.

title: Validate docs links, keybinding examples, and config examples against source
description: Extend docs validation to catch stale internal links, source-backed keybinding rows/examples, and config snippets that no longer parse or map to supported settings.
design: Avoid brittle prose assertions. Validate structured examples, fenced config snippets, link targets, action-registry-backed keybindings, and settings/default snippets where the docs intentionally mirror source-of-truth data.
acceptance_criteria: The validator rejects stale source-backed values and broken links while leaving prose flexible. It covers at least one keybinding example, one JSONC config example, and internal README/docs links.
close_reason: Completed: validate-docs-experience now checks internal Markdown links, source-backed right-sidebar keybinding docs, and JSONC config snippets against main_config_contract.toml; CI runs it. Verified with repo_docs_validation::tests and validate-docs-experience.

title: Split maintainer contract validators by domain
