Caution: Hyprland has one relatively coherent config domain; Yazelix has multiple native sidecars, so Advanced should expose sidecar status/actions before full sidecar editing.

2. spotatui - compact in-app settings. Source: https://docs.rs/crate/spotatui/0.33.1 and https://github.com/LargeModGames/spotatui. It exposes an in-app Settings screen via Alt-, categories Behavior/Keybindings/Theme, theme presets, navigation tabs, save hotkey, and immediate apply on save. Useful for Yazelix: first mutable settings UI can start with common product settings and theme/cursor-like presets; keybindings can initially be view-only while design matures.

3. Bridle - profile/config manager for many external tools. Source: https://docs.rs/bridle/0.2.4 and https://github.com/neiii/bridle. It manages profiles across Claude Code, OpenCode, Goose, Amp, Copilot CLI, Crush, and Droid, translates paths/schemas, launches a TUI, provides status/create/switch/edit/diff flows, and stores its own config in ~/.config/bridle/config.toml. Useful for Yazelix: native-integration status, import/profile thinking, and explicit ownership display. Caution: Yazelix must not silently activate or overwrite native app configs; import/export/adopt must stay explicit.

4. Purple - focused config manager/editor for ~/.ssh/config. Source: https://github.com/erickochen/purple and https://docs.rs/crate/purple-ssh/0.1.0/source/Cargo.toml.orig. It is an SSH manager and SSH config editor built with Ratatui, with fuzzy search and one-domain editing. Useful for Yazelix: focused domain editors can be powerful; fuzzy search over many config records matters. Caution: this supports a single well-known grammar, unlike Yazelix native sidecars.

5. BlueTUI - app settings and keybindings via TOML. Source: https://github.com/pythops/bluetui and https://deepwiki.com/pythops/bluetui. It uses Ratatui for Bluetooth management and supports TOML keybinding/layout config at ~/.config/bluetui/config.toml. Useful for Yazelix: simple action-key config is enough when actions are clear; config should be discoverable and overrideable without editing source.

6. hypr-showkey - read-only config-derived keybinding browser. Source: https://github.com/cubismod/hypr-showkey and https://lib.rs/crates/hypr-showkey. It parses configured Hyprland keybinding files and displays a fuzzy-searchable TUI. Useful for Yazelix: before a full keymap editor, build read-only diagnostics that show effective bindings, source files, conflicts, and unresolved native actions.

7. Ratatui official config guidance - source: https://ratatui.rs/templates/component/config-rs/ and https://github.com/ratatui/ratatui/discussions/220. The template and discussion point toward XDG config paths, typed config structs, config-rs-like loading, and key events mapped to actions/messages instead of hardcoded key handlers. Useful for Yazelix: keep the settings inventory typed, treat keymaps as action mappings, and keep XDG/default/user layering explicit.

Recommendation for Yazelix: keep the current ryx4 sequence. First expand schema/UI metadata, then implement comment-preserving JSONC patching, then ship a read-only Ratatui browser with search and explicit/defaulted/read-only/sidecar status. Do not jump straight to a full mutable sidecar editor. The only mature close analog is r-hyprconfig, and even it works because it owns one domain. Yazelix should copy the product patterns, not the sidecar-editing scope.

Follow-up impact: reinforces yazelix-ryx4.1 metadata, yazelix-ryx4.2 JSONC patcher, yazelix-ryx4.3 read-only browser, and yazelix-hg3a action registry/keymap diagnostics.
close_reason: Completed: documented existing Ratatui config/settings UI references and extracted Yazelix lessons in the bead notes.

title: Add editable controls to Ratatui config UI
description: Use the JSONC patcher and config edit command path to make the Ratatui config UI mutate supported settings directly.
design: Start with explicit single-field edits only. Booleans can toggle, enum strings can cycle/pick, numbers and strings can use a one-line editor, string lists can use a simple list editor. Home Manager-owned or read-only settings remain inspect-only. Saving must reuse the comment-preserving settings.jsonc patcher and the same config-contract validation path used by yzx config set/unset.
acceptance_criteria: The config UI can edit supported scalar values and string lists, save through the comment-preserving settings.jsonc patcher, reject or explain unsupported fields, keep Home Manager/read-only owners inspect-only, refresh explicit/defaulted state after save, and has tests for toggling a boolean, editing an enum/string value, validation failure, and read-only ownership.
close_reason: Completed by yazelix-49rl and follow-up direct choice work: config UI now edits through the JSONC patcher, handles read-only/Home Manager ownership, and includes direct enum/multi-choice controls. Remaining metadata depth is tracked separately in yazelix-ryx4.1.

title: Evaluate extracting config UI into yazelix_ratconfig
description:
## Decision
Select yazelix_ratconfig as the future extraction name, but do not publish a standalone crate or repository yet. The current readiness state is internal_split_ready: split the Yazelix config UI into reusable model/editor/render/validation seams plus a Yazelix adapter before any public extraction.

## Rationale
The config UI has real reusable value for Rust/Ratatui projects, but the current implementation still bundles Yazelix settings.jsonc, JSONC patching, Home Manager read-only behavior, native config status, generated runtime refreshes, and apply-mode wording. Publishing that boundary now would make a product adapter look like a stable toolkit API.

## Alternatives Considered
Rejected yazelix_config_ui because it is too product-specific, ratconfig because it is too generic and collision-prone, and keeping the surface internal indefinitely because the reusable schema-backed editor model is worth preserving after the internal split.

## Durable Record
See docs/contracts/yazelix_ratconfig_extraction.md.
design: Crate/repo shape: keep the first split in-repo under yazelix_core or a private workspace crate. Reusable boundary: field/section model, editor actions, rendering helpers, diagnostics, theming hooks, and write-plan traits. Yazelix adapter boundary: settings.jsonc loading, main_config_contract/schema metadata mapping, JSONC comment-preserving patching, Home Manager ownership, native config status, generated runtime refresh, and apply-mode reporting. Follow-up: yazelix-ryx4.6.1 splits config_ui.rs before a public yazelix_ratconfig extraction.
acceptance_criteria: Decision records extraction readiness, selected name plus alternatives, crate/repo shape, reusable API boundary, Yazelix adapter boundary, config format assumptions, editing and validation guarantees, maintenance cost, migration plan, and one proof plan using Yazelix as the first consumer.
close_reason: Completed: documented yazelix_ratconfig extraction decision in docs/contracts/yazelix_ratconfig_extraction.md, recorded internal_split_ready readiness, and created follow-up yazelix-ryx4.6.1 for the internal model/editor/render/apply adapter split.

title: Split config UI into model/editor/render/apply adapter before ratconfig extraction
description: Split the current Yazelix config UI implementation into focused internal boundaries before any public yazelix_ratconfig extraction.
design: Keep Yazelix as the only shipped consumer. Separate reusable field/section model, editor state/actions, rendering, validation diagnostics, and persistence/write-plan seams from Yazelix-specific settings.jsonc, Home Manager ownership, native config status, generated runtime refresh, and apply-mode policy.
acceptance_criteria: config_ui.rs responsibilities are split into focused modules without changing user behavior; reusable model/action/render code does not depend on Yazelix paths or command text; Yazelix-specific JSONC patching, native status, Home Manager read-only state, and apply-mode refresh stay behind adapter-owned code; existing config UI and JSONC patch tests pass; a small non-Yazelix fixture or test proves the reusable model is not hardcoded to Yazelix settings.
notes: Rust dependency decision: no new crates. Keep this as an internal module split in yazelix_core using existing ratatui/crossterm/serde_json/toml dependencies already owned by config_ui. Packaging impact: none. Rejected a public yazelix_ratconfig crate split for this slice; this only prepares model/editor/render/apply adapter boundaries.
close_reason: Completed: split config UI internals into model, editor, render, and apply-adapter modules while preserving the existing Yazelix adapter behavior and adding a non-Yazelix namespace model test.

title: Evaluate runtime-applied settings
description:
## Decision
Adopt explicit apply-mode classification instead of broad live apply. Yazelix should save semantic settings first, then report whether each changed value is active now, pending pane refresh, pending generated config refresh, pending tab/session restart, pending terminal/shell restart, pending package/Home Manager activation, or not live-applicable.

## Outcome
The first safe live slice is intentionally narrow: bounded pane-orchestrator runtime values for popup geometry and screen saver behavior. Command surfaces, keybindings, generated sidecars, terminal/shell behavior, and Home Manager-owned settings stay restart/activation scoped until each owner has an explicit refresh contract.

## Contract
See docs/contracts/runtime_applied_settings.md.
design:
Apply-mode vocabulary: live, live_with_pane_refresh, generated_runtime_refresh, tab_session_restart, shell_terminal_restart, package_home_manager_activation, and never_live.

Inventory: core welcome/debug settings are launch/session scoped; Helix/editor/sidebar command and layout settings are tab/session scoped; shell.default_shell and terminal settings are terminal/shell restart scoped unless Home Manager owns the source; Zellij chrome/theme/default-mode/keybindings are tab/session scoped; Zellij status bar fields are generated-runtime scoped until the bar has an explicit refresh owner; Yazi command/plugin/theme/sort settings are generated-runtime refresh scoped; native/import/generated boundaries are never mutated as live apply side effects.

First live slice: zellij.popup_width_percent, zellij.popup_height_percent, zellij.screen_saver_enabled, zellij.screen_saver_idle_seconds, and zellij.screen_saver_style. These are bounded scalar/enum values already owned by Yazelix pane runtime behavior.

Runtime mechanism: yzx_control validates and writes settings, computes changed field apply modes, then sends versioned live_with_pane_refresh updates to the pane orchestrator through an explicit pipe/control message. The pane orchestrator validates session/config generation and returns explicit success/failure. Generated-runtime settings use materializers and targeted restart guidance instead of plugin state mutation.

Failure behavior: save succeeds independently from live refresh; live refresh failures become visible pending statuses rather than silent rollback. Stale snapshots, missing permissions, missing pipes, and version mismatches fail closed with restart guidance. Mixed apply results are reported per field.

UI copy: Applies now; Applies after pane refresh; Saved, refresh generated config; Saved, restart this tab/session; Saved, restart terminal/shell; Saved, activate package/Home Manager; Not live-applicable.

Risks recorded: stale session snapshots, plugin permission mismatch, generated sidecar drift, partial apply failures, and unsafe broad command/keybinding reloads.
acceptance_criteria: Completed: inventoried schema-known settings into apply modes, selected the first live-with-pane-refresh slice, defined yzx_control plus pane-orchestrator propagation and failure behavior, defined config UI status copy, recorded stale snapshot/plugin permission/sidecar drift/partial apply risks, and created implementation follow-ups.
close_reason: Completed runtime-applied settings decision, contract, apply-mode inventory, first live slice, failure behavior, UI copy, risks, and implementation follow-ups.

title: Add apply-mode metadata to main config contract and config UI model
description: Implement docs/contracts/runtime_applied_settings.md as machine-readable field metadata. Every schema-known main settings field should declare one apply mode, and the Rust config/UI loaders should expose the mode and user label from the same source.
acceptance_criteria: main_config_contract.toml declares an apply mode for every fields entry; unknown or missing modes fail validation; Rust config metadata exposes apply mode and label; config UI model includes the metadata; tests defend the first live slice and representative restart/activation fields.
notes: Rust dependency decision for implementation: use existing std/toml/serde_json paths already in yazelix_core; no new production or dev crates. Build apply-mode parsing and labels in-house because the domain is a small closed vocabulary from docs/contracts/runtime_applied_settings.md. Rejected alternatives: broad schema/metadata framework or duplicating labels in the UI. Packaging impact: none.
close_reason: Completed apply-mode metadata in the main config contract, shared Rust apply-mode vocabulary, config UI model exposure, Home Manager activation override, and config-surface validation for missing/unknown modes.

title: Implement pane-orchestrator runtime reload for first live settings slice
description: Add the first live-with-pane-refresh path for bounded pane-orchestrator settings: popup geometry and screen saver behavior. The implementation should use an explicit versioned control/pipe message rather than rewriting plugin state files directly.
acceptance_criteria: yzx_control validates and saves changed values; pane-orchestrator accepts a versioned runtime-config reload for zellij.popup_width_percent, zellij.popup_height_percent, zellij.screen_saver_enabled, zellij.screen_saver_idle_seconds, and zellij.screen_saver_style; stale generation, missing pipe, permission, and version mismatch failures return visible per-field pending statuses; pane-orchestrator wasm is rebuilt and synced; targeted Rust/plugin tests and pane-orchestrator sync validator pass.
notes: Rust dependency decision: use existing production crates serde/serde_json for the versioned pane-orchestrator reload payload; build the small reload protocol, generation check, and response classification in-house. Rejected extra IPC/config crates because the transport is already Zellij pipes and the schema is a tiny bounded command. Packaging impact: no new crates; pane-orchestrator wasm must be rebuilt and synced after plugin edits.
close_reason: Implemented the first live pane-orchestrator refresh slice: config saves now send a versioned reload_runtime_config pipe with generated Zellij generation, popup geometry, and screen saver values; the plugin validates schema/generation and applies in-memory runtime config; stale generation, missing pipe, permission, version, and payload failures are visible as saved-but-pending apply errors; wasm rebuilt and synced.

title: Show saved-versus-active apply status in config UI
description: Teach the config UI to show whether a saved setting is active or pending a pane refresh, generated config refresh, session restart, terminal/shell restart, Home Manager activation, or non-live boundary.
acceptance_criteria: After save, the UI displays per-field apply status using the runtime-applied-settings contract copy; failed live refreshes remain saved but visibly pending; Home Manager read-only settings use native-config status remediation; tests cover success, pending restart, and failed refresh presentation without claiming inactive settings are active.
close_reason: Config UI now shows saved-versus-active apply status from apply-mode metadata, including generated-refresh and restart-pending states, and keeps save-time refresh failures visible as pending apply work.

title: Evaluate status bar live refresh for Yazelix bar settings
description: Decide whether status-bar settings can move from generated-runtime refresh to live-with-pane-refresh after the yzx_bar/zjstatus boundary is clearer.
acceptance_criteria: Inventory zellij.widget_tray, zellij.tab_label_mode, zellij.*usage*_display, zellij.*usage*_periods, and zellij.custom_text; identify the owning bar component and refresh mechanism; define failure behavior and user copy; either create an implementation bead for live refresh or explicitly keep the fields generated-runtime scoped with rationale.
notes: Decision: keep status-bar settings generated_runtime_refresh. widget_tray, tab_label_mode, and custom_text are rendered into zjstatus plugin KDL before plugin load. usage display/period settings are read from the launch session config snapshot by yzx_control command widgets. Current zjstatus pipe protocol can rerun commands/notify/update pipe widget content, but cannot replace loaded module config, widget map, tab formats, or command definitions. Future live apply requires a dedicated Yazelix status-bar owner or upstream zjstatus config reload protocol; pane-orchestrator proxying is rejected.
close_reason: Completed evaluation and documented the decision to keep Yazelix bar settings generated-runtime scoped until a real status-bar reload owner exists.

title: Add generated config refresh flow for settings save
description: Add an explicit save-time path for generated-runtime-refresh settings that regenerates managed Yazelix runtime files and reports the exact tool restart or reload needed.
acceptance_criteria: yzx_control can regenerate targeted managed outputs for representative generated-runtime fields without editing native files; output drift and materialization errors are visible per field; UI/doctor guidance names the affected tool and restart/reload action; tests cover Yazi, Helix, and Zellij generated-output examples.
close_reason: Completed: config set/unset and config UI saves now consult apply-mode metadata, refresh generated runtime config for generated-runtime fields, report tool-specific guidance, and surface materialization failures as saved-but-not-applied errors.

title: Remove dangling packaged rust_plugins runtime symlink
description: The built Yazelix runtime contains a dangling rust_plugins symlink because mk_runtime_tree links /rust_plugins even though the packaged source no longer includes that ignored maintainer source tree. Nix profile upgrades then warn about skipping the dangling symlink. Remove the stale runtime symlink and defend the package contract.
acceptance_criteria: Built .#yazelix and .#runtime outputs do not contain a rust_plugins symlink; configs/zellij/plugins wasm assets remain present; the nixpkgs package contract no longer requires shipping rust_plugins source; a validator catches any reintroduced dangling/legacy rust_plugins package path; local package build or validator verifies the warning is gone.
close_reason: Completed: removed the stale packaged runtime rust_plugins symlink and dropped rust_plugins from the package source root. Updated the nixpkgs package contract to state that first-party wasm ships through configs/zellij/plugins, not maintainer Rust plugin source trees. Added validator coverage that rejects a packaged rust_plugins path even when it is a broken symlink. Verified .#yazelix builds without the skipping dangling symlink warning, validate-contracts, validate-nixpkgs-package, rustfmt, git diff --check, and direct checks that .#runtime/.#yazelix lack rust_plugins while wasm assets remain present.

title: Investigate Helix directory picker handoff latency
description:
Opening a directory from Yazi through the managed Helix bridge works, but the handoff feels slower than desired. Current flow: discover managed editor pane, focus editor, send helix.open_directory to Yazelix Helix, Helix sets cwd and opens native file picker, then Yazelix retargets workspace/sidebar. Likely costs include Zellij pipe round-trips, editor focus timing, retarget/sidebar sync after picker open, and Helix file picker directory walking.

Scope: document/profile the latency before changing behavior. Do not reintroduce terminal write-chars. Preserve the contract that directory targets use Helix native picker rather than helix.open_files.

Acceptance criteria:
- Capture a rough timing breakdown for directory open: pane discovery, focus_editor, bridge request, Helix picker creation, retarget_workspace, sidebar sync.
- Identify whether perceived delay is dominated by Helix picker walking or Yazelix/Zellij IPC.
- Evaluate low-risk sequencing changes, such as moving focus after the bridge action or retargeting before picker open, with tradeoffs.
- If deeper Helix picker changes are needed, document whether async/streaming picker improvements belong in yazelix-helix rather than main Yazelix.
- Keep existing no-write-chars bridge contract intact.

title: Extract flake installer shell block into a maintained script or helper
description:
The `install` app in `flake.nix` still embeds a long shell script inline. That block now carries most of the flake-specific install behavior: runtime symlink setup, seeded config creation, shell hook refresh, generated config rebuilds, and user-facing install messaging.

It works, but it is the largest remaining behavior-heavy chunk in the flake and is harder to review, test, and evolve than the surrounding flake output wiring.
design:
Keep the current install contract and app surface, but move the behavior-heavy shell block out of `flake.nix` into one maintained checked-in script or a small helper derivation that generates the script. Prefer a real script file if possible so install behavior can be reviewed and evolved without editing large inline shell strings.

The goal is not to redesign installation. It is to separate flake output plumbing from installer behavior and make future install-path changes less brittle.
acceptance_criteria: 1. `flake.nix` no longer contains the long inline installer shell block. 2. The install behavior remains the same from the user perspective: `nix run .#install` still installs the runtime, seeds config files, refreshes shell hooks, rebuilds generated configs, and prints the same next-step guidance. 3. Flake install smoke validation still passes after the extraction. 4. The extraction does not reintroduce runtime-path staleness or split install logic across multiple ad hoc shell snippets.
close_reason: Completed: moved the flake installer shell logic into a maintained checked-in script template and kept the nix run install contract unchanged.

title: Fix zoxide editor when sidebar hidden state is unknown
description: The Yazi zoxide-editor path can fail before opening the editor when editor.hide_sidebar_on_file_open is true and get_active_tab_session_state does not include layout.sidebar_collapsed. The hide-sidebar preflight should not abort the whole editor-open action when this optional layout fact is missing or temporarily unknown.
acceptance_criteria: Alt+z / yzx_control zellij open-editor-cwd still opens or retargets the managed editor when layout.sidebar_collapsed is missing or null. When the state explicitly says the sidebar is open, Yazelix still sends hide_sidebar before retargeting. When the state explicitly says it is already hidden, Yazelix skips hide_sidebar. Tests cover the missing/null sidebar-collapsed state regression.
notes:
Manual follow-up found the remaining failure only when the managed editor pane is missing. Root cause: pane-orchestrator hide_sidebar closed the focused sidebar but did not nudge focus to a non-sidebar fallback before yzx_control opened a new editor pane with zellij run. Patched hide_sidebar to use a new resolve_sidebar_hide contract and synced the tracked/runtime wasm.
Second root cause fixed: initial startup BASE layout needs two next-swap steps to close the sidebar, because the first next-swap reaches single_open and still keeps the sidebar visible. Programmatic hide and manual close now use the same BASE-aware close-step helper.
close_reason: Implemented across commits 5b93dfc6, a35204fb, and 651011b; sidebar-hidden-state unknown handling, focused-sidebar fallback, BASE close-step behavior, and first-pane post-create hide are covered by workspace surface regressions.

title: Package pane-orchestrator wasm from the child repo
description: Add a durable package output to yazelix-zellij-pane-orchestrator so the child repository owns building and exposing the pane-orchestrator wasm artifact instead of requiring the Yazelix main repo to track a copied binary. This is the prerequisite for replacing the current sync-stamp copied-wasm workflow with lock-driven package consumption.
design: Source owner: yazelix-zellij-pane-orchestrator child repo. Runtime form: wasm package artifact. Rejected approach: continue treating the main repo copied wasm as durable provenance. Packaging impact: introduces the package surface that the main flake can later consume through a lock file.
acceptance_criteria: The child repo exposes a stable package artifact path for the Yazelix runtime wasm name, such as share/yazelix_zellij_pane_orchestrator/yazelix_pane_orchestrator.wasm or an equivalent documented path. The child package builds from source for wasm32-wasip1, includes focused package/build verification, documents the artifact path, and does not require the Yazelix main repo to copy source or duplicate plugin implementation logic.
notes: Follow-up from yazelix-n7j5. Keep current copied-wasm sync stamp in Yazelix until the child package exists and main runtime consumption is implemented.
close_reason: Completed: added a Nix flake package to yazelix-zellij-pane-orchestrator that builds wasm32-wasip1 and exposes share/yazelix_zellij_pane_orchestrator/yazelix_pane_orchestrator.wasm; documented the package artifact path; verified with nix build .#yazelix_zellij_pane_orchestrator --no-link; committed child repo change as b489341.

title: Keep magician Kitty frames inside pane border
description:
The magician Kitty GIF can visually overlap Zellij pane borders because the image payload is not pane-clipped. It also uses palette black for the surrounding terminal cells, which can differ from the GIF's true black background. Keep the frame placement within a safer pane gutter, preserve centered placement, and use true RGB black for the wrapper background when Yazelix surrounds the animation.

Acceptance criteria:
- Magician Kitty frame layout leaves a larger safety gutter around pane borders while remaining centered.
- Existing full-pane and wide-pane layout tests are updated to defend the new centered geometry.
- Yazelix wrapper uses true black, not palette black, for the surrounding background.
- Main Yazelix consumes a published yazelix-screen child revision for the layout change.
close_reason: Fixed by publishing yazelix-screen 1501763 with an 8-cell magician frame gutter, updating main Yazelix to consume that child revision, switching the wrapper background to RGB true black, and validating the no-overrides runtime build.

title: Validate macOS Home Manager install path from discussion 565
description: A macOS nix-darwin/Home Manager tester reported that the Home Manager module needed Linux-only desktop-entry guarding and described their normal install shape: declarative Home Manager, custom Helix package/runtime, terminal_config_mode = "user", and terminal-first launch. Use this as the macOS Home Manager smoke target for v16+ rather than reasoning only from Linux.
acceptance_criteria: A macOS Home Manager smoke checklist or manual run verifies that importing the module succeeds on Darwin, yzx is present in the profile, yzx launch/doctor run from Terminal, and Linux desktop-entry options are not required on Darwin.
notes:
2026-05-05 local validation: applied the reported Home Manager desktop-entry guard in home_manager/module.nix. The final guard uses optionalAttrs with both pkgs.stdenv.hostPlatform.isLinux and the option-presence check, so non-Linux and missing-option eval contexts receive no xdg.desktopEntries definition at all. This is stricter than a plain mkIf guard because validate-config-surface-contract showed mkIf can still leave a definition for a missing option in minimal eval contexts.

Verification passed:
- cargo run --quiet --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_maintainer --bin yzx_repo_validator -- validate-contracts
- cargo run --quiet --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_maintainer --bin yzx_repo_validator -- validate-config-surface-contract

Public GitHub issue remains open because its thread says only a macOS user can close it after manual Home Manager testing.

title: Evaluate integrating sahaj-b/ghostty-cursor-shaders into Yazelix-managed cursor effects
description: Evaluate adding curated support for the MIT-licensed ghostty-cursor-shaders repo to Yazelix so Ghostty cursor effects remain fully Yazelix-managed instead of requiring users to clone and wire shader files manually. The goal is to extend the current Ghostty cursor-trail system with upstream trail and pulse/boom patterns while preserving Yazelix generation, docs, defaults, and random selection.
design: Current state: Yazelix already generates Ghostty shader config, vendors local shader files under configs/terminal_emulators/ghostty/shaders, and defaults terminal.cursor_trail to random. The upstream repo is a good fit because Ghostty supports multiple custom-shader lines and the repo is simple standalone GLSL under MIT. Important caveat: these upstream effects are not only color variants. They include trail shaders like cursor_warp, cursor_sweep, and cursor_tail, plus mode-change shaders like ripple_cursor, sonic_boom_cursor, rectangle_boom_cursor, and ripple_rectangle_cursor. That means this should be treated as a broader cursor-effects surface, not just more colors. Preferred direction: keep Yazelix as the builder and orchestrator, vendor or generate a curated subset of upstream shaders locally, and expose them through a structured selector that supports both existing color presets and new pattern effects. A robust design is to support an ordered list for Ghostty shader selection and composition, with backward-compatible string aliases for existing cursor_trail values. If random remains the default, define a curated random pool explicitly instead of sampling every shader, because pulse and boom effects are behaviorally different from trails and may need separate inclusion rules. Also account for Ghostty-specific caveats such as custom-shader-animation = always for some line-cursor effects, and avoid regressing Kitty fallback behavior that currently maps random to snow.
acceptance_criteria: There is a clear implementation decision and scope for integrating ghostty-cursor-shaders into Yazelix. The chosen design keeps shader management inside Yazelix, documents the upstream source and license, and specifies whether the config surface stays terminal.cursor_trail, becomes truly list-capable, or is renamed to a broader cursor-effects concept. The plan identifies which upstream shaders are included, how random selection works, what Ghostty-only caveats must be emitted in generated config, and how existing cursor_trail users remain backward-compatible. Verification includes generating Ghostty config with a fixed preset, a composed or list-based preset if adopted, and the default random path.
close_reason: Implemented Ghostty cursor effect integration with vendored shaders, new Ghostty effect config, generated shader build, and collection/docs updates

title: Pane orchestrator module diet
description: Identify and remove redundant or dead Rust plugin submodules, then document minimal module boundary cleanup sequence
notes: Pane orchestrator module diet audit: no dead Rust plugin submodule is safe to delete as a module boundary. Runtime modules are the main.rs state/action adapters: ai_pane_activity, editor, heartbeat, layout, panes, runtime_config, screen_saver, sidebar_yazi, status_bar_cache, workspace. title: [EPIC] Empower standalone yazelix-bar widget ownership
description: Move every status-bar widget that does not fundamentally depend on Yazelix-only session state into the standalone yazelix-bar child repo, so non-Yazelix Zellij users can use the bar as-is and Yazelix stops owning generic/widget formatting code.
acceptance_criteria: All child beads either move widget ownership into yazelix-bar or record a defended no-go for genuinely Yazelix-only behavior. Non-Yazelix users must be able to use yazelix-bar with plain Zellij config/env/cache inputs. Yazelix keeps integration plumbing only: launch-scoped cache paths, provider refresh scheduling, session snapshot hydration, yzx_control commands, and Yazelix-specific generated layout policy. Workspace may remain Yazelix-owned initially unless a generic cwd/env fallback is defined. Cursor can be standalone when fed yazelix-cursors facts/cache. Agent widgets should move display/rendering to yazelix-bar while Yazelix keeps refresh/cache plumbing until standalone helper commands are designed.

Yazelix consumes bar-owned renderers and keeps integration-only responsibilities: runtime paths, generated layout integration, status-cache transport, provider refresh/probing, locks, cache location, session config hydration, workspace facts, and CPU/RAM scripts. Workspace remains Yazelix-only until a generic fallback is designed. Closeout validation and scorecard recorded on sp8.9.

title: Delete the remaining product-side full-config Nushell owner seam
description: utils/config_parser.nu is still imported by startup, launch, setup, menu, popup, popup wrappers, and editor-launch helper code. Rust already owns config normalization, active-config surface decisions, runtime env computation, materialization planning, and integration facts. Collapse the remaining full-config Nushell owner seam so live product code stops reparsing the full Yazelix config in Nu.
design: Do not move shell execution into Rust just to claim a migration. The honest target is Rust-owned retained facts or request construction, with Nu keeping shell or process orchestration and user-facing UX where that is still the real owner. Count progress only when a live product Nu consumer stops depending on parse_yazelix_config end to end.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The remaining live parse_yazelix_config product consumers are grouped into explicit deletion lanes. 2. Each lane names the retained behavior, candidate Rust owner, verification path, and stop condition. 3. The final lane aims to delete or demote utils/config_parser.nu from product ownership rather than adding another bridge.
close_reason: all steps complete

title: Define the deletion budget for the remaining full-config Nushell owner seam
description: Inventory the remaining product-side parse_yazelix_config consumers across menu, popup, popup wrappers, startup, launch, setup, and adjacent utilities. Record what behavior each consumer still owns, what facts are actually needed, and where Rust can become the single retained owner.
design: Separate front-door facts, popup or wrapper facts, and startup or launch facts. Be explicit about no-go boundaries where Nu still owns shell or process behavior. Do not treat tests as product owners in this budget.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Every live product parse_yazelix_config consumer is classified. 2. The budget separates front-door, popup or wrapper, and startup or launch lanes. 3. The budget records explicit stop conditions so later work does not wrap shell behavior in Rust.
close_reason: Completed

title: Rust-own front-door config facts and delete menu and popup full-config reads
description: Move the retained config facts for yzx menu and yzx popup off full Nu config parsing and onto a narrower Rust-owned facts or request surface. Target nushell/scripts/yzx/menu.nu and nushell/scripts/yzx/popup.nu.
design: Keep this narrow. Rust should own the retained config facts; Nu should keep front-door UX, process launch, and menu or popup rendering. Do not create a second generic config bridge.
acceptance_criteria: 1. yzx menu and yzx popup no longer call parse_yazelix_config. 2. The retained facts come from one narrower Rust-owned seam. 3. UX behavior, exclusions, launch behavior, and popup geometry remain intact. 4. No broader fake Rust UI wrapper is introduced.

title: Rust-own startup and launch retained config facts and collapse full-config reads
description: Collapse the remaining full-config Nu reads in nushell/scripts/core/start_yazelix.nu, core/start_yazelix_inner.nu, core/launch_yazelix.nu, yzx/launch.nu, and setup/environment.nu into narrower Rust-owned retained facts or request construction.
design: Keep Nu as the shell and process orchestrator. Move only deterministic config facts and request shaping into Rust where that deletes a real Nu owner seam. Do not rebuild a second startup wrapper stack in Rust.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Startup, launch, and setup stop depending on full-config Nu parsing where only retained facts are needed. 2. Shell or process orchestration remains Nu or POSIX owned. 3. Materialization, launch selection, and environment behavior stay intact. 4. The surviving Nu code is visibly narrower after the cut.

description: After the remaining live product consumers stop needing full-config Nu parsing, delete nushell/scripts/utils/config_parser.nu or demote it to a clearly bounded non-product role.
design: This bead only counts as done if config_parser.nu stops being a live product owner. Do not keep it around as a generic convenience layer if Rust already owns the retained config semantics.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Live product code no longer depends on utils/config_parser.nu. 2. The file is deleted or explicitly demoted with a precise remaining owner reason. 3. Verification covers the surviving startup, launch, popup, menu, and wrapper paths.
close_reason: Completed

title: Move bundled Yazi asset sync out of warm startup
description:
Bundled Yazi plugins, flavors, and the managed sidebar Starship config are currently refreshed during startup-managed Yazi generation. Those files are largely static for a given runtime, so paying that copy/sync cost on the common warm path is likely the wrong boundary.

This follow-up should evaluate moving bundled Yazi asset materialization to install/refresh/runtime-change ownership instead of launch-time ownership.
design:
Reason from first principles. The common warm path should only do work that is truly dynamic for that launch. Runtime-owned Yazi assets are mostly static for a given installed runtime, so they should not be recopied on every startup unless there is a strong correctness reason. Prefer a design that deletes launch-time bundled-asset sync instead of adding more cache metadata or broad invalidation logic.

Preserve fail-fast behavior and clear ownership boundaries between runtime-owned assets and user-owned Yazi config.
acceptance_criteria:
1. The chosen design makes explicit which Yazi files are runtime-owned static assets versus per-launch dynamic generated config.
2. Warm startup no longer recopies unchanged bundled Yazi assets on the common path.
3. Runtime updates, install, or explicit refresh still materialize the needed bundled Yazi assets correctly.
4. A focused regression defends the new ownership boundary without relying on broad cache metadata.
notes: Implemented the warm-start boundary as a guarded static-asset sync instead of a new cache layer. generate_merged_yazi_config now accepts --sync-static-assets; regenerate_runtime_configs forces that flag when config/runtime materialized state is stale, while warm current-state regeneration skips static Yazi plugin/flavor/starship recopy unless the copied asset tree is missing required runtime-owned files. The same wrapper now passes the active get_yazelix_state_dir()/configs/zellij path into generate_merged_zellij_config so generated-state repair honors explicit Yazelix state roots. Extended the existing bundled-plugin regression to prove runtime placeholders still render, warm materialization does not recopy static Yazi assets, and missing bundled plugin files self-heal. Verified: validate_syntax.nu, generated-config canonical lane, full yzx default suite 90/90, validate_default_test_count_budget.nu, validate_default_test_traceability.nu, git diff --check.
close_reason: Moved warm startup to guarded static Yazi asset sync and verified default suite.

title: Subsystemize Yazelix internals before repo extraction
description: Define and execute the internal subsystem shape for Yazelix before extracting any standalone repositories. The intended shape is one repo with hard boundaries: yazelix_core, yazelix_maintainer, yazelix_screen, yazelix_pane_orchestrator, and yazelix_bar. External repos should happen only after a subsystem has a stable API, independent tests, and clear value outside Yazelix.
design: Use internal crates/packages and versioned contracts first. Keep live Zellij session truth in the pane orchestrator; let the bar consume session/status facts; make screen rendering a self-contained terminal animation crate; keep popup/sidebar as pane-orchestrator capabilities until their generic API is proven. Avoid separate repos while subsystem APIs are still changing quickly.
acceptance_criteria: There are concrete Beads for the maintainer crate split, screen renderer/style/extension seam, session/status schema and transport, bar widget/rendering subsystem, AI activity/widget extension, popup capability hardening, and sidebar capability decision. Each extraction candidate states whether it is in-repo only, external-facing API, or future standalone repo candidate. Existing features must be preserved unless a separate bead explicitly decides to remove them.
notes:
Created after reviewing the current pane-orchestrator, zjstatus/bar, popup/sidebar, and screen renderer code. This epic supersedes ad hoc repo-splitting discussion; code movement still happens in child implementation beads.
Current related work: yazelix-9opk.5.1 covers the maintainer crate split; yazelix-subsys.1 covers yazelix_screen extraction; yazelix-subsys.2 and .3 cover the pane-orchestrator status bus and yazelix_bar; yazelix-subsys.4 retargets popup toward a standalone-capable pane-orchestrator capability; yazelix-subsys.5 records that sidebar remains a pane-orchestrator capability for now.
2026-04-26 plan-space review: keep the subsystem plan feature-preserving and split by stable seams, not by premature repo boundaries. New child beads now separate screen renderer/style/extension work, status schema/transport/AI facts, bar static extraction/dynamic widgets/AI widgets, and popup adapter isolation/extraction gate.
close_reason: all steps complete

title: Extract yzx screen into an internal yazelix_screen crate
description: Move the terminal animation/framebuffer code out of front_door_render.rs into a self-contained in-repo Rust crate or module package named yazelix_screen. The screen subsystem should own terminal-frame construction, Game of Life state, boids state, future Mandelbrot rendering, and reusable frame playback primitives.
design: Do this before adding major new screen simulations. Keep crossterm as the backend and avoid ratatui unless the screen surface gains real widget/app requirements. The first split should be mechanical: preserve current welcome/screen behavior, expose a small API consumed by yazelix_core front-door commands, and move renderer tests with the subsystem.
acceptance_criteria: front_door_render.rs stops owning the full screen engine; yazelix_screen has focused tests for terminal output, row addressing, Game of Life full_block and dotted styles, boids-ready framebuffer behavior, and narrow-layout fit; existing yzx welcome/screen behavior is unchanged; follow-on visual beads implement against the new screen extension seam.
notes:
This is the strongest internal extraction candidate because the current front_door_render.rs is already a cohesive terminal animation engine. Future external extraction is plausible after the API stabilizes.
2026-04-26 plan-space review: implementation should happen through child beads .1.1 renderer/backend, .1.2 Game of Life styles/settings, and .1.3 future animation seam. This avoids mixing mechanical extraction with new boids or Mandelbrot behavior.
Implemented through children .1.1-.1.3: created internal yazelix_screen crate, moved terminal framebuffer/output primitives, moved Game of Life state/styles/rendering/tests, and added a frame-producer seam for future animations. Existing logo/boids welcome composition remains in front_door_render until those visual features get their own implementation beads; boids/Mandelbrot should now target yazelix_screen rather than expanding front_door_render.
close_reason: Completed: internal yazelix_screen crate now owns terminal screen primitives, Game of Life rendering, and future animation frame-producer seam.

title: Screen: extract terminal framebuffer and output backend
description: Move ScreenFrame, ScreenCell, terminal sizing, alternate-screen output, cursor addressing, autowrap handling, and frame flushing into the internal yazelix_screen subsystem without changing rendered behavior.
design: Keep this slice mechanical. The renderer contract should make terminal rows explicit and forbid newline-driven animation output. Preserve the current crossterm backend, absolute row cursor addressing, no-autowrap screen mode, and clear failure surfaces. Do not add ratatui or a second renderer abstraction unless a later screen feature proves it needs widget-style layout.
acceptance_criteria: The front-door code delegates framebuffer construction and terminal output to yazelix_screen; yzx screen and welcome paths preserve their current frame placement; tests defend no inserted renderer rows, absolute row addressing, and stable visible footprint; no user-facing screen style or setting is removed.
notes:
Rust dependency gate: production crates for this slice are crossterm only, moved into the new internal yazelix_screen crate for terminal size/mode/output primitives. Dev-only crates: none. In-house logic: ScreenCell, ScreenFrame, visible line rendering helpers, terminal width/height env override, absolute-row frame output, and screen flush primitives. Rejected alternatives: ratatui is too broad for a terminal animation surface; adding unicode-width is unnecessary because the current visible-width contract already counts rendered scalar values and this slice preserves behavior; adding an error crate is unnecessary because yazelix_screen can return io::Result and yazelix_core keeps user-facing CoreError mapping. Packaging impact: rust_core workspace gains an in-repo yazelix_screen crate and yazelix_core depends on it by path; no external repository and no new third-party dependency.
Implemented: added internal rust_core/yazelix_screen crate for ScreenCell, ScreenFrame, terminal width/height, visible-width centering helpers, raw-mode guard, screen-mode enter/leave, absolute-row frame output, and stdout flush. yazelix_core front_door_render now delegates framebuffer construction and terminal output to yazelix_screen while keeping Game of Life simulation/style behavior for the next bead. Verification: cargo fmt --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml --all -- --check; cargo check --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core -p yazelix_screen; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_screen; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core front_door_render.
close_reason: Completed: terminal framebuffer and output backend extracted into internal yazelix_screen crate with focused renderer regressions passing.

title: Screen: move Game of Life styles and settings into yazelix_screen
description: Move Game of Life state, style selection, full_block rendering, dotted scale-4 braille texture behavior, and related welcome/screen adapters into yazelix_screen.
design: Preserve the current UX exactly before adding new visuals. full_block remains the default. dotted remains a supported style with the same two-terminal-columns by one-terminal-row footprint, not a tiny packed-cell variant. Keep config parsing and Home Manager parity in the existing config surface while the screen subsystem owns the rendering semantics.
acceptance_criteria: core.game_of_life_cell_style continues to support full_block and dotted; both direct config and Home Manager paths still normalize the option; glider footprint, no skipped rows, and narrow-layout fit are defended by focused tests; no old welcome_style behavior is removed.
notes:
Rust dependency gate: production crates remain crossterm only through yazelix_screen and serde only where existing asset/config parsing in yazelix_core already needs it; no new production crates. Dev-only crates: none. In-house logic: GameOfLifeCellStyle parse/glyph contract, screen state, grid sizing, seed placement, stepping, and framebuffer rendering. Rejected alternatives: moving the whole ascii_art_data catalog into yazelix_screen would mix logo/boids welcome assets into the screen simulation crate; keeping Game of Life entirely in yazelix_core would leave the next boids/Mandelbrot work without a clean simulation/rendering owner. Packaging impact: no new external dependencies; this extends the internal path crate boundary created by yazelix-subsys.1.1.
Implemented: moved GameOfLifeCellStyle, parse error type, GameOfLifeScreenState, fixed Game of Life specs, seed placement, stepping, full_block/dotted glyph rendering, and Game of Life screen-frame tests into yazelix_screen. yazelix_core now keeps only style catalog resolution, welcome composition, final logo frame composition, and CoreError mapping for invalid config values. Verification: cargo fmt --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml --all -- --check; cargo check --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core -p yazelix_screen; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_screen; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core front_door_render; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core --test yzx_control_front_door.
close_reason: Completed: Game of Life styles, state, stepping, and rendering moved into yazelix_screen with footprint regressions preserved.

title: Screen: define future animation extension seam
description: Define the yazelix_screen extension seam for boids, Mandelbrot, fullscreen screen plugin use, and future animation families after the renderer and Game of Life contracts are stable.
design: Use a small frame-producer boundary rather than a broad TUI framework. A simulation should own deterministic state updates and emit cells into the shared framebuffer. The renderer should stay style-agnostic and terminal-safe. Avoid losing current welcome features while making new animations addable without editing terminal-output internals.
acceptance_criteria: A new animation can be added by implementing the screen frame-producer contract; boids and Mandelbrot can share framebuffer/output code; fullscreen yzx screen plugin work has a stable renderer API to target; startup cost and narrow-layout behavior remain explicit acceptance checks.
notes:
Rust dependency gate: no new production or dev-only crates. In-house logic: a small ScreenFrameProducer trait, ScreenAnimationContext, and a GameOfLifeAnimation implementation that wraps the existing Game of Life state/render/resize behavior. Rejected alternatives: a broad animation framework, async runtime, ratatui app model, or plugin-level abstraction; the next useful seam is only deterministic frame production into the existing framebuffer/output path. Packaging impact: no external dependencies and no new binaries; this extends the internal yazelix_screen API so boids and Mandelbrot can target it later.
Implemented: added ScreenAnimationContext and ScreenFrameProducer in yazelix_screen, plus GameOfLifeAnimation as the first concrete frame producer. yzx screen now drives Game of Life through render_frame, advance_frame, and resize instead of hand-rolling state calls in front_door_render. This gives boids and Mandelbrot a small concrete API to target without changing terminal output internals. Verification: cargo fmt --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml --all -- --check; cargo check --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core -p yazelix_screen; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_screen; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core front_door_render; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core --test yzx_control_front_door.
close_reason: Completed: future animation frame-producer seam added and Game of Life now uses it.

title: Define pane-orchestrator session/status bus
description: Extend the pane-orchestrator's current active-tab session snapshot into a stable versioned status bus that can feed Yazelix bar widgets and future external integrations. The bus should expose live session facts without making the bar rediscover Zellij truth on its own.
design: Start from get_active_tab_session_state and active_tab_session_state.rs. Add only facts that are already owned by the pane orchestrator or can be derived from PaneUpdate/TabUpdate: active tab, workspace root/source, managed pane IDs, focus context, layout/sidebar collapsed state, sidebar Yazi state, transient popup/menu state, and eventually AI pane activity. Keep schema_version explicit and keep renderer/presentation out of this layer.
acceptance_criteria: A versioned status/session schema and transport exist with documented fields and tests; pane-orchestrator remains the single owner of live Zellij session truth; zellij/workspace commands and bar code can consume the same payload; AI pane activity can be represented as facts; no bar-specific formatting leaks into the pane-orchestrator.
notes:
This is the key seam between yazelix_pane_orchestrator and yazelix_bar.
2026-04-26 plan-space review: split into schema, transport, and AI activity facts so the bar can consume stable facts without turning the pane orchestrator into a presentation layer.
close_reason: Completed by closed child beads: the pane-orchestrator status bus has a versioned schema, producer/consumer transport, and AI pane activity facts without bar-specific formatting in the producer.

title: Status bus: define versioned session schema
description: Define the versioned pane-orchestrator status/session payload before adding more consumers.
design: Start from active tab session state and explicitly classify fields as owned live state, derived state, or adapter state. Include schema_version, active tab, workspace root/source, managed pane IDs, focus context, layout/sidebar collapsed state, sidebar Yazi state, transient pane state, and extension slots for AI pane activity. Keep presentation and widget formatting out of the schema.
acceptance_criteria: A documented schema exists with stable field names, ownership notes, and compatibility policy; tests defend serialization and representative payloads; the schema does not make the bar rediscover Zellij truth or embed bar-specific formatting.
notes: Rust dependency gate: no new production or dev-only crates. The status bus schema should use existing serde support already present in the pane-orchestrator crate; the schema itself is plain data and should stay in-house/std. Rejected alternatives: adding a schema-validation framework, JSON schema generation crate, or event-bus framework before the producer/transport contract exists.
close_reason: Completed: expanded the active-tab status bus v1 schema with ownership/compatibility notes, transient pane facts, AI activity extension slots, and serialization/back-compat tests.

title: Status bus: expose producer and consumer transport
description: Expose a reliable way for Yazelix consumers to request or receive the pane-orchestrator status payload.
design: Prefer the smallest useful transport first: an explicit pipe command or snapshot command backed by the pane orchestrator state. Do not add a long-running second state owner. If subscription is needed later, it must still treat the pane orchestrator as the only live Zellij truth source.
acceptance_criteria: A consumer can obtain the current versioned status payload without parsing ad hoc logs, KDL, or generated files; stale or unsupported schema versions fail clearly; tests cover request handling and a representative consumer path.
notes: 2026-04-26 implementation start: no new crates. Transport should reuse existing zellij pipe mechanics and pane-orchestrator state; no second long-running state owner.
close_reason: Completed: added zellij status-bus consumer command backed by the existing pane-orchestrator active-tab snapshot pipe, with schema-version validation and focused tests.

title: Status bus: model AI pane activity facts
description: Add a clean status-bus fact model for AI panes so the bar can show activity such as a thinking spinner for Codex, Claude, or other agent panes in the current tab.
design: Keep this as status facts, not provider UI. Detect or accept explicit pane activity signals through pane-orchestrator-owned state and avoid brittle scraping where possible. The schema should support provider name, activity state, pane association, and freshness without coupling to one CLI.
acceptance_criteria: The status payload can represent inactive, active/thinking, stale, and unknown AI pane states; tab-local association is explicit; no token or provider UI formatting leaks into the pane orchestrator; future bar widgets can consume these facts directly.
notes: Dependency decision before implementation: model AI activity facts in-house using existing pane-orchestrator/status-bus structs and serde/serde_json already present. No new production crates or dev crates. Rejected alternatives: provider scraping libraries or provider-specific SDKs; this bead is fact schema/state only. Packaging impact: none.
close_reason: Completed: status-bus extensions now model tab-local AI pane activity facts with normalized unknown, inactive, active, thinking, and stale states.

title: Make yazelix_bar a first-class in-repo subsystem
description: Promote the current zjstatus-backed bar from scattered generated template/config snippets into a first-class Yazelix subsystem. The bar should own status/tabline presentation, widget rendering, CPU/RAM widgets, custom badges, and future AI usage/activity widgets while consuming session facts from the pane-orchestrator bus.
design: Keep zjstatus as the first backend. Move bar-specific rendering decisions out of generic Zellij materialization where practical: widget registry, allowed widget names, command widget templates, colors, compact/full display decisions, and custom text formatting. Do not make the bar own pane/session discovery; consume the versioned pane-orchestrator status bus instead.
acceptance_criteria: There is a clear yazelix_bar-owned source boundary for widget definitions and zjstatus template rendering; existing editor/shell/term/cpu/ram/custom text behavior is preserved; dynamic widgets consume the pane-orchestrator status bus; AI usage and activity widgets have explicit extension points; bar tests cover widget rendering without requiring a live Zellij session.
notes:
The current bar is in configs/zellij/layouts/fragments/zjstatus_tab_template.kdl plus zellij_materialization.rs render_widget_tray_segment/render_custom_text_segment and zellij.widget_tray config.
2026-04-26 plan-space review: do not block all bar work on the status bus. Static widget registry and zjstatus rendering can be extracted first; only dynamic widgets and AI activity depend on the bus.
close_reason: Completed by closed child beads: yazelix_bar now owns widget registry/rendering, consumes pane-orchestrator status facts for dynamic widgets, and exposes AI activity/token-budget extension points with tests.

title: Bar: extract widget registry and zjstatus renderer boundary
description: Move bar-specific widget definitions, static/custom text rendering, command widget templates, colors, and zjstatus template decisions behind a yazelix_bar-owned boundary.
design: This should not wait for the live status bus. Preserve the current zjstatus backend and existing editor, shell, term, cpu, ram, and custom text behavior. The split should reduce generic Zellij materialization knowledge of bar presentation while keeping package/layout generation stable.
acceptance_criteria: A yazelix_bar-owned source boundary exists for widget registry and zjstatus rendering; existing bar widgets render the same as before; CPU and RAM widgets keep their current behavior; tests cover widget rendering and narrow or empty tray cases without a live Zellij session.
notes: Rust dependency gate: no new production or dev-only crates. Build the bar widget registry and zjstatus segment renderer in-house/std because the domain is small string rendering with a fixed widget set. Rejected alternatives: a KDL parser dependency for the existing template, a full TUI/status framework, or serde-backed schema work in this bead; all add packaging surface without improving the current renderer boundary.
close_reason: Completed: extracted yazelix_bar crate with zjstatus widget registry and renderer boundary; kept existing widget output and added focused renderer tests.

title: Bar: consume pane-orchestrator status bus for dynamic widgets
description: Connect yazelix_bar to the versioned pane-orchestrator status bus for dynamic session-aware widgets.
design: The bar may format facts, but it must not rediscover live Zellij state by parsing panes, KDL, logs, or generated files. Start with facts that are already reliable in the bus: focus context, active tab/workspace, sidebar state, popup/menu state, and layout state.
acceptance_criteria: At least one dynamic widget consumes the status payload through the supported transport; unsupported schema versions fail visibly; bar rendering remains deterministic in tests with fixture payloads; no pane-orchestrator presentation formatting leaks into bar code.
notes: 2026-04-26 implementation start: no new crates. Bar consumer should use the supported zellij status-bus snapshot/JSON shape and keep formatting local to bar code.
close_reason: Completed: added optional workspace bar widget that renders through the validated pane-orchestrator status-bus transport, with schema/fixture tests and config/HM/docs updates.

title: Bar: add AI activity and token-budget widget extension points
description: Add the extension points needed for future Codex, Claude, ChatGPT, and other agent status widgets, including thinking/activity spinners and token or subscription budget displays.
design: Separate live activity facts from provider budget/accounting sources. The first implementation can expose fixture-backed and config-backed provider widgets before any networked provider integration. Keep provider-specific code isolated so adding a new agent does not fork the whole bar renderer.
acceptance_criteria: The bar has a documented widget extension shape for AI activity and provider budgets; thinking/spinner state can be rendered from status-bus facts; token or subscription budget widgets have a provider adapter boundary; existing widget behavior is unchanged.
notes: Dependency decision before implementation: extend yazelix_bar in-house with existing serde-free/static rendering boundaries unless existing crate dependencies already suffice. No provider SDKs, no network/accounting crates. Token-budget providers stay adapter-shaped and fixture/config-backed only. Packaging impact: none.
close_reason: Completed: zellij.widget_tray now accepts ai_activity and token_budget extension widgets, zjstatus commands are wired, and existing default widgets are unchanged.

title: Harden popup as a standalone-capable pane-orchestrator capability
description: Make the popup/transient-pane capability clean enough that plain Zellij users could eventually configure it without the full Yazelix runtime, while keeping implementation inside the pane orchestrator for now.
design: Start from transient.rs, transient_pane_contract.rs, yzx popup, and the existing l968.1 popup extraction bead. Separate generic popup concepts from Yazelix-specific wrapper flow: pane title/identity, command argv, cwd, geometry, toggle/focus/close semantics, and optional post-close hooks. The first milestone is a stable capability API, not a standalone repo.
acceptance_criteria: Popup identity and open/toggle request schema are explicit; single-instance behavior remains covered; Yazelix-specific runtime wrapper, menu wrapper, and sidebar-refresh hooks are isolated from generic popup policy; standalone extraction remains gated by a documented contract rather than assumed.
notes:
This updates the old standalone-extraction direction: popup has external value, but current code still depends on Yazelix runtime wrappers and sidebar refresh behavior.
2026-04-26 plan-space review: existing popup identity/argv/geometry work is valuable but not sufficient for standalone-capable use. The missing seam is isolating Yazelix runtime adapters and writing an explicit extraction gate.
close_reason: Completed: generic transient policy is isolated from Yazelix adapters in subsys.4.1, and the standalone extraction gate is documented in subsys.4.2.

title: Popup: isolate generic transient policy from Yazelix adapters
description: Separate generic popup/transient-pane policy from Yazelix runtime wrappers, menu wrappers, runtime_dir assumptions, and sidebar-refresh hooks.
design: Keep the pane orchestrator as the implementation owner. Generic policy should cover identity, command argv, cwd, geometry, focus/toggle/close semantics, and single-instance rules. Yazelix-specific wrappers and post-close hooks should be adapters passed into that policy rather than hardcoded into it.
acceptance_criteria: TransientPaneRequest and related policy surfaces no longer require Yazelix wrapper names to explain their behavior; Yazelix popup/menu behavior remains unchanged; sidebar refresh remains supported as an adapter hook; regression tests cover open, focus, toggle, close, and single-instance behavior.
notes: Rust dependency gate: no new production or dev-only crates. The transient/popup policy is existing in-house state-machine logic over pane snapshots and argv/cwd/geometry values. Rejected alternatives: adding a command-builder crate, process framework, or TUI/popup framework before standalone extraction needs are proven.
close_reason: Completed: split generic transient matching/toggle/launch policy from Yazelix popup/menu adapters, kept sidebar refresh as an adapter hook, and expanded focused contract tests.

title: Popup: define standalone-capable contract and extraction gate
description: Define what would have to be true before the popup capability becomes a standalone Zellij plugin or external package.
design: Do not extract by enthusiasm. The gate should require a generic config surface, stable request schema, documented Zellij pipe or plugin command contract, tests that do not depend on the full Yazelix runtime, and clear examples for plain Zellij users. If those are not true, keep it in-repo.
acceptance_criteria: There is a written extraction gate for popup; plain-Zellij value is documented without promising an immediate repo split; the current Yazelix implementation path remains the source of truth until the gate is met.
close_reason: Completed: added POP-005 to the floating TUI panes spec as the standalone popup extraction gate, documenting plain-Zellij value without promising an immediate split and keeping the current in-repo Yazelix implementation canonical until the gate is met.

title: Keep sidebar as a pane-orchestrator capability
description: Clarify that sidebar should remain a capability of the pane orchestrator, not a separate repo or separate live state owner, unless future work proves a generic sidebar API independent of Yazelix-managed Yazi/editor/layout state.
design: Ground the decision in current code: sidebar identity, focus policy, layout toggling, horizontal motion, workspace state, and sidebar Yazi state all share pane-orchestrator session truth. Continue isolating reusable sidebar contracts, but keep the loaded Zellij plugin as one state owner. Separate generic sidebar terms from the current Yazi file-tree adapter.
acceptance_criteria: Sidebar remains a pane-orchestrator capability for now; existing sidebar extraction beads are retargeted toward internal capability hardening; sidebar Yazi identity, enable_sidebar removal, terminology, and tab-local state guardrails have clear related beads; no standalone sidebar repo is planned unless future work proves generic use outside Yazelix.
notes:
Related existing beads: yazelix-l968.2, yazelix-l968.2.1, yazelix-l968.2.2, yazelix-l968.2.3, yazelix-kihb, yazelix-069b.
2026-04-26 plan-space review: decision is sidebar stays in pane-orchestrator now. The implementation follow-through lives in yazelix-l968.2 children, yazelix-kihb, yazelix-069b, and terminology/postmortem guardrail beads.
close_reason: Decision recorded: keep sidebar inside pane-orchestrator for now; implementation follow-through remains in related sidebar capability beads.

title: Define whether no-op yzx refresh should repair missing generated runtime configs
description: Today `yzx refresh` only regenerates runtime-owned Yazi and Zellij configs when it is already doing rebuild work. If config/input state is up to date, the command exits early with "already up to date" and does not repair missing generated runtime configs. That may be fine, or it may leave `yzx refresh` weaker as a repair entrypoint than users expect. Track the decision explicitly instead of letting the behavior drift.
design: Keep this delete-first. First decide whether missing generated runtime configs are actually part of the contract that `yzx refresh` should repair on a no-op path, or whether that responsibility belongs only to startup and Home Manager activation. If refresh should be a repair surface, prefer a narrow generated-config repair step over broad extra work or duplicate rebuild logic. If refresh should stay rebuild-only on the no-op path, document that clearly and rely on the real repair entrypoints instead.
acceptance_criteria: 1. There is an explicit decision on whether a no-op `yzx refresh` should repair missing generated Yazi/Zellij runtime configs. 2. If yes, the implementation repairs those artifacts without forcing unnecessary rebuild work. 3. If no, the decision is documented and the intended repair entrypoint is clear. 4. A focused regression or validator defends the chosen contract.
close_reason: Completed: no-op yzx refresh now repairs missing generated Yazi/Zellij runtime configs without forcing a rebuild, defended by a focused refresh regression.

title: Document distribution-vs-backend ownership trim matrix
close_reason: Documented the distinction between distribution trimming and backend trimming

title: Zellij materialization deletion pass phase 2
description: Continue shrinking zellij_materialization.rs and zellij_render_plan.rs by deleting duplicate render, fingerprint, layout, and KDL ownership paths instead of adding module seams for their own sake.
design: Deletion-first Zellij materialization pass. Preserve generated Zellij config output, keybinding semantics, layout-family contracts, status-bar integration, and cache invalidation behavior. Split only when the split exposes duplicate ownership that can be deleted in the same slice.
acceptance_criteria: At least one duplicate or stale render/fingerprint/layout ownership path is deleted or explicitly rejected with evidence; zellij_materialization.rs and/or zellij_render_plan.rs are smaller or have a materially thinner boundary; focused Zellij render/materialization tests pass; Rust budget and inventory are updated; LOC delta and rejected candidates are recorded.
close_reason: Completed: deleted the impossible string lookup path for fixed Zellij plugin artifacts, preserving plugin order for fingerprint and permission cache writes. zellij_materialization.rs dropped from 2738 to 2725 raw lines; rejected broader layout/render rewrites because current render plan and layout template paths still own distinct output and cache-invalidation behavior.

title: Fix Mandelbrot screen continuous fractal loop
description: The Mandelbrot screen producer should continuously feel like an infinite fractal zoom/loop. The current implementation does not visibly keep fractalling as expected.
design: Keep the implementation in-house/std inside yazelix_screen. Adjust the view path and tests so frames evolve through a seamless zoom loop rather than settling into a static or shallow animation. Preserve bounded CPU cost and deterministic output.
acceptance_criteria: yzx screen mandelbrot continuously evolves through a seamless fractal loop; frame output changes across the loop while exact loop-boundary frames match; CPU iteration budget remains bounded; tests defend progression and loop continuity.
notes: 2026-04-26 dependency gate: production crates unchanged; dev-only crates unchanged. Mandelbrot zoom/path logic is deterministic numeric animation and should remain in-house/std inside yazelix_screen. Rejected adding rendering/fractal crates because the bug is the view loop contract, not missing infrastructure or performance.
close_reason: Completed: Mandelbrot now uses a deep deterministic zoom loop into a boundary target, returns seamlessly at the loop boundary, keeps iteration cost bounded, and has regression tests for structural frame progression and exact loop repeatability.

title: Audit rm usage for safety and destructive-command hygiene
description: Audit the codebase for every rm invocation and decide where deletion behavior needs stronger safety checks, clearer scoping, or better diagnostics.
design: Treat this as destructive-command hygiene, not as a mechanical search-and-replace pass. For each rm usage, verify what is being deleted, how paths are resolved, whether the target can escape the intended runtime boundary, and whether errors are visible. The goal is to catch genuinely risky deletion paths without adding unnecessary friction to safe, bounded cleanup.
acceptance_criteria: All rm usage in the repo has been reviewed and any risky or unclear deletion paths have a concrete follow-up fix or direct cleanup. The audit records which rm invocations are already acceptably bounded and which ones need stronger safeguards or diagnostics.
notes: Promote this safety audit. Current repo direction is pre-packaging hardening: reduce LOC, remove dead/redundant code, and audit destructive paths. rm-call hygiene is a high-priority cleanup pass because it can hide unsafe path handling behind routine maintenance code.
close_reason: Completed: bounded managed cleanup now uses a shared root-checked delete helper, preserves lexical symlinked managed paths, and refuses root/outside targets.

title: Add Rio terminal support
description:
Add Rio as a supported Yazelix terminal target.

The first useful slice should make Rio work as a configured terminal choice instead of only being mentioned in terminal strategy notes. Treat this as support for a modern Rust terminal candidate: generated Yazelix config where practical, launch integration, preflight/doctor visibility, docs, and focused tests.

This bead is related to yazelix-zkxw, which reframed Rio as the likely dream-base candidate for a future ideal Yazelix terminal.
design:
Scope:
- Add `rio` to the supported terminal surface as a real terminal choice, not just a research candidate.
- Start with PATH-provided Rio support unless Nixpkgs/flake packaging is clearly low-risk; decide explicitly in the bead notes before adding a packaged runtime variant.
- Add generated Rio config/materialization if Rio exposes a stable config surface suitable for Yazelix-owned defaults.
- Wire launch command construction, config-mode behavior, runtime preflight/doctor messaging, settings schema/metadata, docs, and terminal selection surfaces consistently.
- Preserve Yazelix's one-packaged-terminal-variant contract unless a separate decision explicitly promotes Rio to a packaged variant.
- Keep Zellij as the in-session workspace/mux owner; Rio support should not add terminal-native tabs/splits as a Yazelix workflow dependency.

Investigation points:
- Verify Rio's current CLI/config flags, config file format, command delimiter behavior, window title/class support, and platform support.
- Verify how Rio handles Kitty graphics, Sixel, iTerm2 images, Kitty keyboard, transparency, fonts, and shell integration in practice.
- Check whether Rio is available in the locked Nixpkgs input and whether packaging it would grow the runtime closure or require special graphics wrappers.
- Decide whether Rio needs nixGL/Vulkan/wgpu handling similar to Ratty or a simpler launch path.
acceptance_criteria:
1. `terminal.terminals` accepts `rio` through schema/metadata/config surfaces.
2. `yzx launch --terminal rio` either launches Rio with correct argv/config behavior or fails fast with a clear unsupported/missing-binary diagnostic.
3. Generated config support exists if Rio has a stable config format; otherwise the bead records why Rio is user-config-only for now.
4. Runtime preflight/doctor surfaces distinguish missing Rio, unsupported platform, and `terminal.config_mode = "user"` failures.
5. README/docs/install/terminal-emulator docs describe Rio support level, whether it is PATH-provided or packaged, and which modern protocols are expected versus unverified.
6. Focused tests cover terminal materialization, launch command shape, terminal list/schema acceptance, and user-mode failure behavior where applicable.
7. The bead notes record whether Rio should remain PATH-provided, become an experimental packaged runtime variant, or wait for a separate packaging bead.
notes: 2026-05-30 upstream Rio issue raphamorim/rio#1530 is a concrete warning for basic Rio support: latest comment on 2026-05-10 says Yazi still does not display images or PDF files, with earlier reports around Iip vs Kgp adapter behavior, tmux differences, tiny/chopped previews, and overlapping images. Do not present Rio support as first-class for Yazelix image-preview workflows until this is reproduced or ruled out against the target Rio version and Yazelix/Zellij/Yazi stack.
close_reason: Superseded by the named yzxterm runtime path. Yazelix now supports the Rio-derived child package as yzxterm/#runtime_yzxterm rather than a generic host rio terminal selector.

title: Use one random pool for welcome and screen
description: Allow both core.welcome_style = random and yzx screen random to select from the same animation-family pool: Game of Life, boids, Mandelbrot, and magician. Update tests and the welcome style contract.
close_reason: Completed. Welcome random and yzx screen random now share the same four-family pool: Game of Life, boids, Mandelbrot, and magician. Updated catalog metadata, contract docs, and Rust front-door tests. Verified with focused random/magician tests, formatter, contract validator, and diff check.

title: Replace Bash-heavy terminal wrappers in devenv.nix with Nushell-first helpers
description: Reduce remaining Bash-heavy terminal wrapper logic in devenv.nix only where moving it into shared Nushell helpers meaningfully shrinks duplication without obscuring terminal-specific launch behavior.
design: Treat this as a cleanup follow-up, not an abstraction contest.
