The goal is to centralize shared resolution logic, error messaging, and config-path handling in Nushell while leaving terminal-specific CLI flags explicit where they differ materially. Do not hide per-terminal launch contracts behind generic wrappers that become harder to reason about than the current code. Any refactor must preserve fail-fast startup, deterministic terminal config resolution, and the current Yazelix versus user mode semantics. Measure the change by whether the wrappers get simpler and the ownership boundary becomes easier to understand, not by whether every line of shell disappears.
acceptance_criteria: Shared wrapper logic that is truly common moves into Nushell, while terminal-specific launch behavior remains readable and correct. Add focused unit tests for the Nushell helpers that replace wrapper logic, especially config resolution, mode validation, and error paths. Add end-to-end wrapper smoke tests with detailed logs for at least Ghostty, WezTerm, Kitty, Alacritty, and Foot, showing the resolved config path, effective mode, and final terminal invocation shape. Verification must demonstrate that cleanup reduced duplication without changing supported behavior.
notes: Dropped as obsolete on v15: devenv.nix and the Bash-heavy terminal wrapper surface targeted by this bead no longer exist. Remaining devenv.nix mentions are historical/spec text, not an active wrapper implementation to refactor.
close_reason: Dropped as obsolete: v15 removed devenv.nix and the targeted Bash-heavy terminal wrapper implementation.

title: Adopt minimal prek config for fast maintainer checks
description: Follow-up to the maintainer-pack work: clean up stale prek references that still describe an unrelated tool, add a minimal pre-commit-compatible config at the repo root, and keep the hook set intentionally small and cheap: syntax validation plus README version validation. Document the maintainer usage path without turning prek into the main enforcement layer; CI remains authoritative.
close_reason: Cleaned stale prek references, added a minimal .pre-commit-config.yaml with syntax and README version checks, documented the maintainer usage path, and verified the config with prek run --all-files.

title: Add controlled terminal override layers instead of full config ownership modes
description: Replace the coarse terminal.config_mode ownership question with a cleaner terminal-extension contract. Yazelix should keep owning launch and integration-critical terminal behavior, while users get Yazelix-specific per-terminal override files for harmless customization such as theme, fonts, opacity, padding, and similar preferences. Start with ghostty, kitty, and alacritty. Avoid reading the normal terminal config by default. This should simplify the product boundary rather than adding a generic merge engine.
close_reason: Completed: shipped controlled terminal override layers for Ghostty, Kitty, and Alacritty, kept launch-critical behavior under Yazelix ownership, and documented the contract.

title: Make Yazi file open work without requiring Esc after selection
description:
## Steps to Reproduce
1. Launch Yazelix with the managed Yazi sidebar.
2. Select a file in the sidebar after startup.
3. Press Enter or o to open it in the managed editor.
4. Observe whether opening requires pressing Esc first, especially after Yazi input/search/filter state or early startup timing.

## Expected Behavior
Normal file-selection mode opens the selected file with Enter/o and targets the existing managed editor pane.

## Actual Behavior
A tester reported that Enter/o sometimes does nothing until Esc is pressed, then the same open action works.

## Existing Context
A macOS/Home Manager tester confirmed the startup hang is fixed, but reported that after selecting a file in the Yazi sidebar they sometimes must press Esc before Enter/o opens the selected file. Previously Enter opened directly. Treat this as a separate Yazi mode/key handling regression or UX gap, not as part of the orchestrator startup hang. Investigate whether Yazi is left in input/search/filter mode, whether Yazelix keymap overrides miss a mode-specific open binding, or whether the managed opener path only works from normal manager mode.
acceptance_criteria: A user can select a file in the Yazelix Yazi sidebar and open it with Enter/o without needing an extra Esc in the normal selection workflow. If Esc is required only after entering an explicit Yazi input/search mode, that behavior is documented or surfaced clearly. The fix or decision includes a focused repro, explains which Yazi mode/keymap path was involved, and verifies the managed editor opener still targets the existing editor pane.
notes: Discussion follow-up 2026-05-03: Esc-before-open is still most likely a Yazi mode/state issue because Yazelix leaves Enter/o as upstream Yazi manager actions. The local retarget_workspace sidebar-yazi-state fix should reduce the less-likely early-startup timing case, but it does not replace the need to diagnose exact Yazi state if Esc is still required after launch. Adjacent config gap tracked as yazelix-15w0 for documented [input]/[cmp] user keymaps being dropped by merge_yazi_keymap.
close_reason: Closed as resolved by the keybinding-ownership fix plus follow-up split. The Esc report was not reproducible locally, and the GitHub screenshots point to Zellij keybinding ownership/default-mode confusion rather than a Yazi manager-mode opener bug. yazelix-583h now preserves full user-owned Zellij keybinds; remaining first-open orchestrator readiness hypothesis is tracked separately as yazelix-ek8b.

title: Merge README daily workflow into CLI section
description: Merge the standalone Daily Workflow README section into yzx Command Line Interface and split the CLI reference into intent-based groups so workflow and command reference live in one place.
close_reason: Merged README Daily Workflow into yzx Command Line Interface with grouped sections. Verified: cargo run --quiet --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_maintainer --bin yzx_repo_validator -- validate-docs-experience; cargo run --quiet --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_maintainer --bin yzx_repo_validator -- validate-readme-version; git diff --check

title: Fix empty editor shell term status widgets
description: The Zellij status line can render empty widget values like [editor: ] [shell: ] [term: ], producing a broken-looking status bar instead of useful tool labels or omitting unavailable widgets. Diagnose the generated zjstatus/widget-tray data path and make the rendered status robust when runtime facts are missing or empty.
acceptance_criteria: The status bar no longer renders empty bracket widgets for editor, shell, or terminal; valid configured/runtime values still appear; missing values either use meaningful defaults or omit the widget intentionally; regression coverage defends the rendered status payload.
notes: Implemented status-widget fix: removed missing zjstatus_widget.nu identity commands from shipped templates, render editor/shell/terminal labels statically from the Zellij render plan with sanitized defaults, and added regression coverage plus Nix source-filter support so the template assertion runs in the packaged Rust check. Verified with cargo test -p yazelix_core, focused zellij tests, git diff --check, and nix build .#runtime --no-link.
close_reason: Completed

title: Implement Ghostty source policy for non-NixOS Linux
description: Apply the Ghostty package-source policy by removing the assumption that nixpkgs Ghostty is the safe default on non-NixOS Linux.
design: Turn the package-source policy into concrete behavior. Keep Homebrew/system Ghostty on macOS, keep nixpkgs Ghostty acceptable on NixOS, and change the non-NixOS Linux story so Yazelix no longer funnels users into nixpkgs Ghostty as the unqualified default. That may mean preferring another default terminal such as WezTerm on non-NixOS Linux, preferring a system Ghostty path when present, or explicitly demoting the Nix-managed Ghostty path behind an opt-in/caveat.
acceptance_criteria: Yazelix no longer treats nixpkgs Ghostty as an unqualified default on non-NixOS Linux. The resulting default and override behavior is explicit in code and docs, and users on non-NixOS Linux are not steered into a Ghostty path that predictably fails without extra OpenGL setup.
notes: 2026-04-20 tracker hygiene: moved out of in_progress. No active code or recent notes indicate this lane is currently being worked; the non-NixOS Linux Ghostty source-policy decision remains open and should be reclaimed only when that packaging/default-behavior work resumes.
close_reason: Completed: Linux package and Home Manager defaults now use WezTerm so non-NixOS users are not steered into nixpkgs Ghostty implicitly; macOS keeps Ghostty by default, explicit Ghostty and WezTerm variants remain available, and docs/config/validator contracts were updated.

title: Package Kitty as a Yazelix runtime variant
description: Kitty is currently only supported as a PATH-provided terminal. Add a first-class kitty runtime/package variant so Home Manager can install it through programs.yazelix.extra_terminal_variants instead of a standalone pkgs.kitty package.
close_reason: Completed: added Kitty as a first-class runtime variant via #yazelix_kitty/#runtime_kitty, Home Manager runtime_variant/extra_terminal_variants support, package validators, and docs. Verified cargo fmt, cargo check for yazelix_core/yazelix_maintainer, Nix customization/flake/config/upgrade validators, nix eval outputs, and nix build .#runtime_kitty/.#yazelix_kitty.

title: Decide canonical Yazelix settings file format
description: Decide the on-disk format for the future schema-backed Yazelix settings surface before implementing config discovery or a config UI. The current leading candidate is JSONC plus JSON Schema, because it gives users a familiar editor-settings model, supports comments, can drive validation/defaults/deprecations/UI grouping, and aligns with the VS Code/Zed pattern. The decision must still compare JSONC against TOML, Pkl, CUE, Nickel, KDL, HCL, JSON5/Hjson, and YAML from Yazelix first principles.
design:
Decision 2026-05-05: use JSONC plus JSON Schema for the durable Yazelix settings surface.

Canonical file: ~/.config/yazelix/settings.jsonc. The directory already names Yazelix, so "settings" is clearer than yazelix.jsonc, and it matches the editor-settings mental model from VS Code and Zed. Keys remain snake_case to preserve the existing Yazelix vocabulary and reduce migration churn.

Scope: settings.jsonc owns Yazelix semantic settings: current main config fields, cursor settings and presets, status bar widgets, common Yazi choices, terminal selection/simple prefs, editor orchestration, shell selection, future semantic keybinding policy, and XDG/native integration mode. It does not absorb raw native sidecars: helix.toml, zellij.kdl, yazi.toml, yazi_keymap.toml, yazi_init.lua, terminal_* snippets, shell_* hooks, secrets, generated state, logs, and large binary/media assets remain outside.

Schema mechanism: introduce a JSON Schema artifact for the canonical settings surface, likely config_metadata/yazelix_settings.schema.json and a runtime-installed copy. Use standard JSON Schema validation plus annotation fields such as title, description, default, examples, deprecated, readOnly/writeOnly where applicable. Use explicit x-yazelix metadata for UI tabs/order, since_version/newness, restart_required, Home Manager option mapping, sidecar linkage, and runtime ownership. This schema should replace or be generated from the current main_config_contract.toml only after the implementation bead chooses the least fragile source-of-truth direction.

Comment/reference strategy: user-edited settings.jsonc may contain comments, and shipped/generated examples can include concise comments. The durable documentation source is the schema/reference/UI, not hand-maintained comments inside every generated config. UI saves should preserve comments when practical, but must not depend on perfect comment-preserving round trips. The canonical writer should emit stable JSONC with no trailing commas; the parser may accept common JSONC conveniences if the chosen Rust parser supports them, but generated files should stay conservative.

Home Manager: manage_config should eventually emit ~/.config/yazelix/settings.jsonc. Pure JSON emitted by builtins.toJSON is valid JSONC, and the module may prepend a short generated-file comment if the implementation keeps parsing that path as JSONC. Home Manager does not need to preserve rich comments in the generated file. Schema/reference docs and the config UI carry comments/descriptions. When Home Manager owns settings.jsonc, the config UI should show the effective settings but mark direct edits read-only or explain the declarative edit path.

Migration: no long-lived old-format runtime path. New installs create settings.jsonc. Existing installs without settings.jsonc are handled by a migration gate: if ~/.config/yazelix/yazelix.toml and/or ~/.config/yazelix/cursors.toml can be converted unambiguously, Yazelix writes settings.jsonc and moves the old inputs aside to explicit timestamped backup/migrated paths. If migration is ambiguous, unsafe, symlink/Home Manager-owned, unreadable, invalid, or would overwrite a user-managed file, Yazelix hard-errors with precise remediation. Once settings.jsonc exists, old TOML settings inputs should not be silently read as runtime settings; their presence should be either cleaned up by the migration or reported as a hard/stale-surface diagnostic unless there is an explicit already-migrated backup marker. Sparse settings.jsonc files are fine: missing fields are filled from schema defaults.

Config UI behavior: the UI reads the schema and the effective settings value graph. Fields absent from settings.jsonc appear as defaulted/unset; fields marked deprecated appear deprecated; fields unknown to the schema are surfaced as unknown/unsupported instead of silently discarded; invalid values carry schema errors. New-setting badges come from schema since_version or equivalent metadata compared against the config file or remembered last-seen schema version. Cursors is a normal first-class tab. Old TOML/cursors files may appear only as migration blockers/results, not as editable alternate runtime inputs.

Alternative evaluation:

- TOML: keep only for native TOML sidecars and as one-time migration input for current users, but not as a durable canonical settings format. It is readable and already implemented, yet weaker for schema-driven editor/UI discovery and awkward once cursor presets and nested structured settings grow.
- Pkl: strong for typed generated configuration, but too language-like and toolchain-heavy for a mutable end-user settings file.
- CUE: powerful for validation and data generation, but overkill and less friendly for direct user editing inside a terminal settings UI.
- Nickel: interesting metadata/default story, but niche and too much language/runtime surface for Yazelix user settings.
- KDL: pleasant for human config and already relevant because Zellij uses KDL, but schema/tooling/editor integration is not as strong as JSON Schema for this use case.
- HCL: good structured config language, but brings Terraform/IaC associations and a bigger language surface than Yazelix needs.
- JSON5/Hjson: more permissive than necessary. JSONC gives comments while staying closer to the JSON Schema/editor ecosystem.
- YAML: rejected because its broad syntax, implicit typing, anchors, and whitespace sensitivity add failure modes without buying a better UI/schema path.

Implementation direction: use xz1z for the first implementation slice: schema artifact, JSONC reader/normalizer, auto-migration-or-hard-error gate from yazelix.toml/cursors.toml, Home Manager output update, docs, and regression tests. Use ryx4 after this to decide/build the config UI against the accepted settings.jsonc/schema model.
acceptance_criteria: Decision completed. Accepted format: ~/.config/yazelix/settings.jsonc using snake_case keys plus JSON Schema metadata. The decision explains schema mechanism, comments/reference strategy, auto-migration-or-hard-error migration from yazelix.toml and cursors.toml, Home Manager behavior, and config UI handling for missing/defaulted/new/deprecated/invalid settings. Rejected alternatives are recorded. There is no long-lived old-format settings reader: old TOML files are migration inputs only. Downstream beads xz1z and ryx4 are updated to consume settings.jsonc instead of assuming TOML.
notes:
Maintainer correction 2026-05-05: avoid framing old TOML files as a supported alternate runtime path. Desired policy is auto-migration when unambiguous, hard error when unsafe/ambiguous, and no long-lived alternate settings reader after settings.jsonc exists.

Research refs used for the decision:
- VS Code JSON docs: JSON with Comments mode is used for VS Code configuration files such as settings.json, tasks.json, and launch.json: https://code.visualstudio.com/Docs/languages/json
- Zed configuration docs: settings use a JSON superset that allows // comments: https://zed.dev/docs/configuring-zed
- JSON Schema annotations: title, description, default, examples, deprecated, readOnly, and writeOnly are standard annotation keywords useful for docs/UI: https://json-schema.org/understanding-json-schema/reference/annotations
- TOML official spec: TOML remains strong for readable config and comments, but does not beat JSONC plus JSON Schema for this schema-driven UI path: https://toml.io/en/
- Pkl, CUE, Nickel, KDL, HCL, JSON5, and Hjson were considered as alternatives but rejected or deferred for the reasons in the design.
close_reason: Decision completed: choose ~/.config/yazelix/settings.jsonc with snake_case keys plus JSON Schema metadata; keep native/executable sidecars outside; old yazelix.toml/cursors.toml inputs are auto-migrated when safe or hard-error when unsafe; config UI consumes this model in ryx4.

title: Teach yzx doctor to detect workspace asset drift
description:
Why this issue exists and what needs to be done

The workspace layer depends on exact layouts, wrapper scripts, plugin artifacts, and integration files. Today most of that drift only shows up after a live session breaks, or in maintainer-only reasoning.

Extend doctor so it can detect the common workspace asset drift cases directly and explain them in user-facing terms: missing or stale plugin artifacts, missing managed wrapper scripts, missing built-in layouts, and other session-critical workspace asset failures.
close_reason: Implemented workspace asset drift diagnostics in yzx doctor

title: Make desktop entries launch without Terminal=true
description: Desktop launch currently relies on Terminal=true in the Linux desktop entry so the desktop environment opens a bootstrap terminal before yzx desktop launch runs. On COSMIC/GLib, the installed Home Manager desktop entry validates and gio/gtk launch exits successfully, but the Terminal=true desktop path does not start Yazelix. Direct /home/lucca/.nix-profile/bin/yzx desktop launch works and starts the configured Ghostty session, so the failure is the desktop environment terminal-backed entry handoff. Move the supported desktop-entry contract away from Terminal=true and let Yazelix own the terminal launch path.
acceptance_criteria: User-local and Home Manager Linux desktop entries render Terminal=false while still pointing at the stable yzx desktop launch command. yzx doctor accepts the new desktop-entry contract and no longer warns solely because Terminal=false is present. The desktop visible-feedback contract is updated so desktop environments are not responsible for opening the bootstrap terminal. Validation covers rendered user-local desktop entry, Home Manager desktop entry evaluation, doctor freshness behavior, and the exact COSMIC-style reproduction where gio launch should start a Yazelix terminal instead of silently no-oping when possible to automate.
close_reason: Completed: changed user-local and Home Manager Linux desktop entries to Terminal=false so desktop environments run yzx desktop launch directly instead of handling a generic Terminal=true launcher. Updated doctor to flag stale Terminal=true entries, refreshed the visible-feedback contract, and added a focused regression for Terminal=false freshness plus Terminal=true stale detection. Verified cargo fmt --check, cargo test -p yazelix_core desktop, validate-config-surface-contract, validate-contracts, git diff --check, desktop-file-validate on a Terminal=false entry, and a COSMIC/GLib-style gio launch probe with Terminal=false.

title: Tolerate stale config in session-local commands
description: Older Yazelix sessions should keep local pane/workspace/status/screen actions usable when user_configs/yazelix.toml contains stale fields from a newer config migration. Startup/materialization should remain strict, but session-local command surfaces should ignore unrelated stale config or report diagnostics gracefully.
acceptance_criteria: transient/sidebar/workspace/Yazi editor/screen explicit-style/session status surfaces do not abort on unrelated stale config; startup/materialization remain strict; behavior-backed tests cover the regression
notes:
Implemented per-window immutable session facts instead of live config reads for session-local commands. Runtime materialization writes state/session_facts.json from the normalized config; startup copies it to state/sessions/<launch_id>/session_facts.json and exports YAZELIX_SESSION_FACTS_PATH before handing off to Zellij. Popup/sidebar/workspace/Yazi editor/screen facts read that snapshot first, with lossy config fallback only for older sessions that predate the env pointer. status/inspect now return a config_problem report instead of aborting when live config has unsupported fields.
Verification: yzx dev rust fmt --check; yzx dev rust check; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core; yzx_repo_validator validate-rust-test-traceability; git diff --check; temp HOME startup smoke with YAZELIX_STARTUP_PROFILE_EXIT_BEFORE_ZELLIJ=true confirmed state/session_facts.json and a per-session sessions/<launch_id>/session_facts.json are written.
close_reason: Completed

title: All commands hang forever, with no logs
description:
Imported GitHub issue #574.

### Details

After installing yazelix, running `yzx launch` opens a terminal with zellij, but the left-hand panel just says `Loading ...` and helix never opens in the right-hand panel. I can interact with zellij just fine, but that's it.

Running `yzx doctor` with or without `--verbose` also hangs forever. No logs are emitted to `~/.config/yazelix/logs`.

### Platform

CachyOS (Arch-based), using `x86-64-v4` versions of the kernel and most system libraries/binaries (including nix).
close_reason: Closed on GitHub

title: Investigate whether Yazelix can avoid Zellij plugin permission prompts safely
description: Investigate whether Yazelix can reduce or avoid the recurring Zellij plugin permission popup without undermining the safety model or relying on brittle local hacks.
design: Start from the principle that permission prompts exist for a reason. The task is not to bypass safety blindly; it is to determine whether Yazelix can ship a safer default permission story through stable plugin paths, predictable permission state, or better first-run setup. If Zellij fundamentally requires user approval, prefer an explicit documented flow over unsafe suppression tricks.
acceptance_criteria: There is a clear decision on whether Yazelix can safely reduce or avoid Zellij plugin permission prompts. If yes, the bead identifies the legitimate mechanism. If not, it documents the best supported user flow and why forced suppression would be the wrong tradeoff.
close_reason: Superseded by the built-in Yazelix permission-recovery command; separate forced popup suppression is no longer needed as a tracked feature.

title: Replace swap-layout step logic with Zellij 0.44 override-layout transitions
description:
Post-Zellij-0.44 follow-up: evaluate replacing Yazelix's current swap-layout stepping model for layout-family changes and sidebar open/close transitions with Zellij's newer `override-layout` capability.

Why this exists:
- the pane orchestrator currently hardcodes layout family names and drives transitions via `next_swap_layout()` / `previous_swap_layout()` plus timing delays
- Zellij 0.44 introduced `override-layout` and the built-in layout manager as first-class layout transition surfaces

Scope:
- map the current Yazelix layout families and sidebar open/closed variants onto an explicit override-layout model
- determine whether sidebar toggle, family cycling, or both can stop depending on swap-step timing
- preserve managed-pane focus behavior and workspace invariants across transitions
- reduce hardcoded layout-name coupling where possible

Success means Yazelix either adopts a cleaner override-layout-based transition path for these flows, or leaves behind a documented reason why the swap-layout model still must remain.
design: Treat this as the broader override-layout evaluation umbrella. Zellij 0.44 made override-layout more realistic, but the real product question is whether Yazelix can replace swap-layout stepping without losing managed-pane invariants. The live-trial notes matter: runtime-file IO inside the plugin sandbox was one blocker, and explicit pane identity may be the deeper blocker. Use this bead to hold the broad transition question, while narrower child beads capture the concrete contracts discovered along the way.
acceptance_criteria: There is a clear keep or replace decision for swap-layout stepping versus override-layout transitions, backed by concrete evidence from trials. If override-layout remains the direction, the bead points to the narrower explicit-run and pane-ownership work needed to make it real. If not, it records why swap-layout remains necessary.
notes:
Live trial status on 2026-03-26:
- pointer-driven layout changes still work
- direct keybind actions `toggle_sidebar` and `previous_family`/`next_family` were failing even though layout detection succeeded
- root cause: the pane-orchestrator plugin could not read generated runtime layout/script files under `~/.local/share/yazelix/...` from inside the Zellij plugin sandbox
- implementation change: replace runtime file IO with embedded tracked layout templates and in-memory Yazelix placeholder expansion inside the plugin
- current state: rebuilt and synced locally; awaiting live retest after `yzx restart`
close_reason: Dropped: prior live trial showed override-layout did not preserve Yazelix sidebar/layout behavior reliably; keep swap-layout stepping until a narrower owned-pane/layout contract proves a replacement.

title: Move managed Zellij config into user_configs/zellij
description: Make Zellij follow the same ownership model as the rest of managed Yazelix config. In managed mode, the canonical user-editable Zellij input should live under ~/.config/yazelix/user_configs/zellij/ (starting with config.kdl), and Yazelix should generate the merged runtime config into ~/.local/share/yazelix/configs/zellij/. Do not keep ~/.config/zellij/config.kdl as the default managed input path; at most, treat it as an import/migration source or explicit escape hatch. This should optimize for system coherence, least surprise, self-contained flake story, and a single mental model: user edits user_configs/*, Yazelix renders runtime state into ~/.local/share/yazelix/. Include deterministic migration/import behavior, updated docs, and focused regression coverage for path ownership and merge precedence.
close_reason: Completed: managed Zellij config now lives under user_configs/zellij with deterministic legacy relocation, fail-fast duplicate detection, docs updates, and regression coverage.

title: Replace repo-centric update commands with a packaged runtime update surface
description:
Yazelix now has a package-ready and flake-installed runtime model, but the user-facing update surface still includes ❌ Failed to check git status: fatal: not a git repository (or any of the parent directories): .git, which only makes sense for clone-based workflows. Replace the repo-centric user update flow with a packaged runtime update command and simplify ℹ️ devenv CLI already matches the Yazelix-pinned version 2.0.7.
❌ Failed to check git status: fatal: not a git repository (or any of the parent directories): .git around real product updates instead of maintainer git operations.
close_reason: Removed yzx update repo, added yzx update runtime, and redefined yzx update all around the packaged runtime plus devenv update flow.

title: Make welcome launch prompt use any key instead of Enter
description: The welcome screen currently says 'Press Enter to launch Zellij and start your session...' and waits for a full line input. Change it to a true single-key continue prompt so the text and behavior match and startup feels snappier.
close_reason: Changed welcome prompt to a true any-key continue

title: Split yzx dev.nu by maintainer workflow family
description: `nushell/scripts/yzx/dev.nu` is a large, high-churn production file that currently owns unrelated maintainer flows at once: version/pin syncing, canary setup and execution, vendored asset refresh, Home Manager activation, Rust plugin build helpers, and GitHub/Beads issue sync logic. The problem is not line count alone; it is that one command module is acting as the owner for several different maintainer subsystems.
design: Delete-first plan: do not start by extracting helpers. First identify which maintainer workflows should not share one owner at all: update/canary activation, vendored-runtime sync, plugin build/sync, and GitHub-Beads reconciliation are likely separate seams. Prefer deleting cross-workflow coupling and shared incidental state before moving code. The end state should be a thin `yzx dev` command surface that dispatches to a few focused maintainer modules.
acceptance_criteria: 1. `yzx dev.nu` stops being the single owner of several unrelated maintainer workflows. 2. The resulting split is organized by workflow family, not arbitrary helper extraction. 3. At least one cross-workflow coupling or duplicate utility path is deleted rather than merely moved. 4. Maintainer regressions still defend the surviving command contracts.
notes:
2026-04-08 simplification landed:
- `refresh_home_manager_input_lock` now uses `nix flake update <input> --flake <dir>` instead of the deprecated `nix flake lock --update-input ...` form.
- The recovery message and the maintainer regression were updated to the same surviving command surface.
- Verification: `test_dev_update_home_manager_activation_refreshes_input_and_switches_requested_ref` passes; `validate_default_test_traceability.nu` passes.

2026-04-08 refinement after the hotspot scan:
- `yzx/dev.nu` is ~1194 lines and clearly mixes several maintainer subsystems.
- The visible workflow families are: update/canary activation, vendored asset sync, GitHub-Beads reconciliation, Rust plugin build/sync, and thin test/bench/profile/lint dispatch.
- Delete-first implication: the final UX can stay under one `yzx dev` umbrella, but those implementation owners should stop sharing one module.

2026-04-08 first delete-first cut landed:
- Deleted wrapper-only helpers that were not real owners:
  - `get_dev_repo_root`
  - `get_available_update_canaries`
  - `get_update_activation_modes`
  - `format_activation_command_arg`
- The surviving update workflow now uses `require_yazelix_repo_root` and inline canonical mode/canary lists directly instead of routing through one-file convenience wrappers.
- Verification: `test_dev_update_requires_explicit_activation_for_real_updates`, `test_dev_update_activation_mode_rejects_unknown_values`, `test_dev_update_home_manager_activation_refreshes_input_and_switches_requested_ref`, `validate_syntax.nu`, and `validate_default_test_traceability.nu` all pass.

2026-04-08 update workflow family split landed:
- Moved the whole update/canary activation family out of `yzx/dev.nu` into `utils/dev_update_workflow.nu`.
- `yzx dev update` is now a thin command surface that delegates to that owner.
- Maintainer tests that exercise helper-level update semantics now source `utils/dev_update_workflow.nu` directly instead of relying on `yzx/dev.nu` to own those helpers.
- Measured reduction: `yzx/dev.nu` dropped from 1194 lines to 594 lines after the update-family move.
- Verification: `test_dev_update_requires_explicit_activation_for_real_updates`, `test_dev_update_activation_mode_rejects_unknown_values`, `test_dev_update_home_manager_activation_refreshes_input_and_switches_requested_ref`, `validate_syntax.nu`, and `validate_default_test_traceability.nu` all pass.

2026-04-08 plugin build family split landed:
- Moved pane-orchestrator and popup-plugin build/sync ownership out of `yzx/dev.nu` into `utils/dev_plugin_build.nu`.
- The surviving `yzx dev build_pane_orchestrator` and `yzx dev build_popup_plugin` commands are now thin wrappers over that narrower owner.
- Verification: `use nushell/scripts/utils/dev_plugin_build.nu *`, the maintainer update regressions, `validate_syntax.nu`, and `validate_default_test_traceability.nu` all pass.

2026-04-08 GitHub-Beads sync family split landed:
- Moved GitHub issue lifecycle reconciliation and canonical comment sync out of `yzx/dev.nu` into `utils/dev_issue_sync.nu`.
- The surviving `yzx dev sync_issues` command is now a thin wrapper over that narrower owner.
- Measured reduction after the workflow-family splits: `yzx/dev.nu` is now 178 lines.
- Verification: `test_issue_bead_reconciliation_plan`, `test_issue_bead_comment_plan`, the maintainer update regressions, `validate_syntax.nu`, and `validate_default_test_traceability.nu` all pass.

Current seam after these deletions:
- `yzx/dev.nu` is now close to the intended thin command aggregator shape.
- The remaining likely follow-up is vendored asset/version sync ownership, but that is no longer required to prove the main maintainer workflow split.
close_reason: yzx dev is now a thin maintainer command dispatcher with separate update, plugin-build, and issue-sync owners

title: Extend shared Rust classification for doctor and status only where it deletes real Nushell reasoning
description: Now that `yazelix-vi8q` and `yazelix-4fi5` are complete, audit the remaining doctor/status logic and either move one genuinely shared typed classification seam behind existing `yzx_core` output or close this bead. The most likely surviving seam is generated-state classification: `yzx status` still summarizes repair need from `config-state.compute` alone, while the runtime-materialization path already owns richer generated-state decisions such as `status`, `reason`, missing managed artifacts, and the resolved managed layout path. This bead exists to delete any real remaining Nushell decision branch in that shared area, not to justify a broad doctor, status, or CLI rewrite.
design:
Keep doctor and status presentation, remediation prose, fix actions, row/table rendering, and command UX in Nushell.

Implementation approach:
1. Inventory surviving duplicated reasoning across doctor/status before changing anything.
2. Prefer reusing an existing Rust seam (`runtime-materialization.plan` first, `runtime-contract.evaluate` only if truly needed) instead of creating a new helper command family.
3. The preferred first candidate is `yzx status`: decide whether it should consume Rust-owned generated-state classification from the materialization plan instead of only `config_state.needs_refresh`.
4. If you need extra machine-readable status fields, make them additive and keep current human output broadly comparable.
5. Do not port install-artifact audits, plugin/session checks, live Zellij inspection, capability messaging, or text-heavy doctor rendering into Rust.
6. If the audit shows no meaningful shared seam remains after `vi8q` and `4fi5`, close this bead with a short note instead of forcing more Rust into the surface.
acceptance_criteria:
1) Either one real duplicated doctor/status reasoning seam is deleted via existing Rust helper output, or the bead is explicitly closed after documenting that no meaningful shared seam remains.
2) If implemented, any new shared classification comes from an existing `yzx_core` seam and Nushell no longer computes the same decision in parallel.
3) Doctor and status remain Nushell-owned presentation surfaces: prose, fix actions, JSON/human rendering, and command UX stay in Nushell.
4) Verification commands in the notes pass.
notes:
Concrete starting points:
- `nushell/scripts/utils/status_report.nu`
- `nushell/scripts/utils/environment_bootstrap.nu`
- `nushell/scripts/utils/doctor.nu`
- `nushell/scripts/utils/runtime_contract_checker.nu`
- `nushell/scripts/utils/generated_runtime_state.nu`
- `rust_core/yazelix_core/src/runtime_materialization.rs`
- `rust_core/yazelix_core/src/runtime_contract.rs`

Concrete questions to answer first:
- Does `yzx status` still have a real duplicated decision seam because it reports `generated_state_repair_needed` from `config_state.needs_refresh` while Rust already owns richer materialization status?
- Should status distinguish `refresh_required` vs `repair_missing_artifacts` vs `noop` using Rust-owned plan data?
- Are any remaining Nushell branches actually just presentation/mapping layers? If yes, keep them in Nushell.

Delete-first constraints:
- Reject additive Rust plus unchanged Nushell reasoning for the same classification.
- Keep `yzx status` and `yzx doctor` as Nushell-owned presentation surfaces.
- Preserve current doctor fix actions in Nushell.
- Avoid changing row labels / user-facing wording unless the seam you delete requires a small adjustment.
- If JSON output grows, keep it backward-compatible by adding fields rather than renaming existing ones.

Suggested minimal implementation if a seam is real:
- Route status summary generation through `compute_runtime_materialization_plan` or a narrow shared helper that consumes the same Rust plan.
- Keep `render_status_report` unchanged except for displaying any additive summary fields you intentionally expose.
- Add a focused regression proving status now reflects Rust-owned generated-state classification for a case that `config-state.compute` alone cannot describe well, such as missing managed artifacts with current hashes.

Suggested verification:
- `nix develop -c cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml`
- `nix develop -c nu -c 'source nushell/scripts/dev/test_yzx_core_commands.nu; run_core_canonical_tests'`
- `nix develop -c nu -c 'source nushell/scripts/dev/test_yzx_doctor_commands.nu; run_doctor_canonical_tests'`
- `nix develop -c nu -c 'source nushell/scripts/dev/validate_specs.nu; main'`
- `git diff --check`
close_reason: yzx status now derives generated-state repair flags and materialization status/reason from runtime-materialization.plan (yzx_core). Added JSON fields generated_state_materialization_status and generated_state_materialization_reason. Core test harness sets YAZELIX_STATE_DIR for isolated fixtures. Regression: test_yzx_status_json_reports_materialization_repair_when_artifacts_missing. Verified: run_core_canonical_tests, cargo test rust_core, validate_specs, run_doctor_canonical_tests.

title: replace welcome art with logo. make it work on half screens
description: Imported open GitHub issue #263.
close_reason: Covered by the broader welcome-screen redesign bead yazelix-2k9

title: Audit update devenv and related version-sync flows
description: Verify yzx update devenv and the surrounding version-sync behavior against the current runtime contract. Confirm the command updates to the Yazelix-pinned devenv surface and does not leave version reporting, doctor output, or runtime entrypoints in a contradictory state.
close_reason: Audit completed: yzx update devenv and yzx dev update canaries were verified; canary syntax and temp config writability bugs were fixed.

title: yzx warp: fuzzy project-switch into a new tab, replacing yzx cwd
description:
Replace yzx cwd with yzx warp — a cleaner, simpler command that fuzzy-matches a directory via zoxide and opens a full workspace tab (sidebar + editor) at that location. Optional --kill/-k flag closes the old tab, fully subsuming yzx cwd's retarget-in-place behavior.

Why warp over cwd:
- 'warp' describes the user experience (teleport to project), 'cwd' describes implementation jargon (change working directory)
- Implementation is simpler: yzx warp leverages Zellij new-tab with --cwd and lets the layout + pane orchestrator bootstrap naturally via TabUpdate/PaneUpdate events, instead of the complex retarget choreography yzx cwd uses (tab rename, keystroke injection to sync editor cwd, ya emit-to sidebar sync, error state handling)
- One command with a clear mental model instead of two commands with subtly different semantics
- With -k, warp fully subsumes cwd: create fresh tab at target, close old tab

Scope: implement after the Rust rewrite has progressed.
notes:
External ref: https://github.com/luccahuguet/yazelix/issues/429
Dependency decision, 2026-04-28:
- Production crates: none new. Reuse std::process, std::path, serde_json already in yazelix_core, and existing CoreError/command-surface helpers.
- Dev-only crates: none new. Existing assert_cmd/tempfile tests remain sufficient.
- In-house logic: parse yzx warp args, resolve path-or-zoxide targets, call zellij action new-tab/close-tab-by-id with structured argv, and parse current-tab-info JSON for --kill.
- Rejected alternatives: clap for one public subcommand, shell wrappers around zellij/zoxide, adding sesh or another session manager, and reusing the heavy yzx cwd retarget choreography for the default path.
- Packaging impact: none beyond the existing yzx_control binary; zoxide and fzf are already part of the Yazelix runtime surface.
Implementation result, 2026-04-28:
- Added public yzx warp route and Rust-owned yzx_control warp implementation.
- yzx warp [target] resolves an explicit path or zoxide query, opens a fresh Zellij tab at the target directory, names the tab from the directory basename, and passes ZELLIJ_DEFAULT_LAYOUT when available so the normal Yazelix workspace shape is used.
- yzx warp with no target opens zoxide query -i for interactive project selection; cancellation exits cleanly.
- --kill/-k captures the original stable Zellij tab id with current-tab-info --json, opens the new tab, then closes the original tab by id so it does not accidentally close the new focused tab.
- Dropped yzx cwd from the public yzx command metadata/help/extern surface and updated current docs/contracts to yzx warp. The yzx_control cwd implementation remains as the legacy/internal retarget path used by existing tests and lower-level flows.
- Verification: cargo fmt --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml --all -- --check; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core; cargo run --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_maintainer --bin yzx_repo_validator -- validate-contracts; cargo run --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core --bin yzx_control -- warp --help.
close_reason: Implemented yzx warp as the public project-switch command, updated docs/contracts/metadata, kept yzx_control cwd as legacy internal retarget support, and verified with focused plus full yazelix_core tests.

title: Add Ghostty cursor effects for Zellij pane changes
description: Explore extending the Ghostty cursor-effect system beyond editor mode changes so Yazelix can trigger visual feedback on Zellij pane focus changes, such as ripple/boom effects when switching panes. Cover feasibility, event plumbing, and whether Ghostty shader triggers can be driven cleanly from Zellij/Yazelix without breaking existing cursor behavior.
design: Treat this as an event-plumbing and UX-value question, not just shader enthusiasm. Determine which pane-focus events Yazelix can observe reliably, how those events could trigger Ghostty effects without colliding with editor-mode effects, and whether the result would actually improve navigation clarity rather than becoming distracting visual noise. Prefer a small number of meaningful pane-change effects over an open-ended effect buffet.
acceptance_criteria: There is a clear feasibility and UX-value decision for Ghostty cursor effects on Zellij pane changes, including which events can be observed, what effect shapes are worth trying, and whether the feature should move forward, stay experimental, or be dropped.
close_reason: Not planned: this Ghostty cursor-effect idea does not fit the current product direction and is not worth carrying as an exploratory backlog item.

title: Add magician GIF welcome style
description: Add a terminal-native welcome/screen style backed by the 1mposter ASCII magician GIF that the maintainer confirmed Yazelix may use, with attribution preserved in shipped metadata/docs.
design: Use the provided GIF as the source asset and render from a compact preprocessed RGB frame pack in Rust so startup does not depend on external image tools or network access. Add the style to config metadata and yzx screen. Rust dependency gate: no new production crates and no new dev crates; generate the compact asset with existing ImageMagick tooling during implementation, then render with std-only byte decoding and existing crossterm/yazelix_screen primitives. Packaging impact: Rust binary grows by the embedded frame pack; source includes the attributed GIF provenance asset.
acceptance_criteria: core.welcome_style accepts magician; yzx screen magician previews it; welcome playback renders the GIF-derived animation with visible 1mposter attribution; random selection remains unchanged; focused Rust/config/docs validations pass.
close_reason: Implemented magician welcome/screen style from the attributed 1mposter GIF. Added the source GIF, compact RGB frame pack, Rust renderer, config/schema/Home Manager/screen-saver style metadata, docs, and visible in-frame attribution. Verification: cargo check --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core front_door_render::tests; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core zellij_render_plan::tests::screen_saver_config_is_normalized_and_bounded; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core front_door_commands::tests; rustfmt --edition 2024 --check touched Rust files; yzx_repo_validator validate-config-surface-contract; yzx_repo_validator validate-contracts; yzx_repo_validator validate-rust-test-traceability; short TTY smoke tests for internal welcome and yzx screen magician. Full cargo fmt --all --check is currently blocked by an unrelated dirty launch_commands.rs change from concurrent work.

title: Clarify sidebar vs file tree terminology
description: Clarify Yazelix terminology so `sidebar` remains the generic side-surface term while `file tree` is used for the current Yazi-specific surface where that wording is clearer.
design: Do not perform a literal repo-wide rename from `sidebar` to `filetree`. The current codebase uses `sidebar` as a pane role and workspace contract term, and that should remain the generic abstraction because future side surfaces may not be file trees. The right change is terminology cleanup: preserve `sidebar` for the architectural slot and command surface, while using `file tree` or `Yazi file tree` in user-facing docs or copy when specifically referring to the current Yazi-backed side surface.
acceptance_criteria: The issue is reframed as terminology clarification rather than a mass rename. User-facing wording is clearer about when Yazelix means the generic sidebar surface versus the current Yazi file tree. Internal architectural and plugin-contract language is not renamed in a way that would block future non-Yazi sidebar surfaces.
close_reason: Completed: clarified user-facing wording so sidebar remains the generic managed side-surface term while the current default Yazi-backed surface is described as the Yazi file tree/file-tree sidebar; internal contract names were preserved.

title: Cut v17.1 as the pre-Steel escape hatch release
description: Tag v17.1 before the bundled Helix Steel transition so users who are not ready for Yazelix-owned Steel Helix can pin the last pre-Steel release line.
acceptance_criteria:
1. CHANGELOG.md and docs/upgrade_notes.toml rotate the post-v17 changes into a v17.1 release entry.
2. The v17.1 notes mention the commits after v17, including the native movement key change, usage widget/runtime helper work, popup runtime env propagation, no-op upstream update reporting, desktop icon reinstall repair, yzx screen/magician/terminal-control work, owned Yazelix Zellij/Yazi KGP forks for Kitty Graphics Protocol passthrough and Yazi previews through Zellij, runtime diagnostics, Rust-owned menu/preflight work, terminal strategy planning, and Helix Steel/config-dir planning.
3. nushell/scripts/utils/constants.nu sets YAZELIX_VERSION to v17.1.
4. The release commit is tagged v17.1.
5. README latest-release surface is synced.
notes:
Release-note correction, 2026-05-30: v17.1 must explicitly document that the Yazelix-owned `yazelix-zellij` and `yazelix-yazi` forks landed after v17 to carry the Kitty Graphics Protocol passthrough path for restoring Yazi image previews through Zellij. The concrete post-v17 commits are e85dfb02 (`Own KGP Zellij package boundary`) and 0fbf9cba (`Own KGP Yazi package boundary`). Earlier wording that only said "KGP package boundaries" was too vague, and the v17 summary needed to stop implying those owned forks already existed at the v17 tag.

Release-note audit, 2026-05-30: reread the full v17..HEAD commit list and found two additional user-visible runtime recovery fixes that were under-documented in the first v17.1 notes: 2cf9dc95 (`Report no-op upstream updates`) and 017e6f19 (`Fix desktop icon reinstall`). Public notes now call out clear no-op `yzx update upstream` reporting and desktop icon replacement over read-only copied icons.
close_reason: Completed: v17.1 release notes were expanded for post-v17 commits, version metadata was bumped, and the v17.1 tag was created as the pre-Steel escape hatch release.

title: Rename Ghostty cursor child repo
description: Rename the Ghostty-specific cursor child repository and public surfaces from yazelix-cursors to yazelix-ghostty-cursors, while preserving the short yzc CLI unless a later compatibility decision removes it.
notes: LOC scorecard for main commit 9ffd1015 vs HEAD^: runtime net +7, maintainer net 0, tests net +2, docs net +1, generated net 0, packaging net 0, assets net 0, tokei code delta +9 and total delta +10. This was a rename/path-surface commit, not a cleanup claim; child package rename commit 9b7224e is balanced 49 insertions/49 deletions.
close_reason: Completed: renamed Ghostty-specific cursor child repo to luccahuguet/yazelix-ghostty-cursors, local checkout to /home/lucca/pjs/yazelix-ghostty-cursors, child crate/package/share/config surfaces to yazelix_ghostty_cursors, and kept yzc as the short CLI. Pushed child commit 9b7224e. Main now uses flake input yazelixGhosttyCursors, package #yazelix_ghostty_cursors, compatibility #ghostty_cursor_shaders, Cargo crate yazelix_ghostty_cursors at 9b7224e, and renamed the shipped default to yazelix_ghostty_cursors_default.toml. LOC scorecard will be recorded after commit.

title: Disable Rust ownership budget default gates
description: Disable the Rust ownership/LOC budget as an enforced default/CI test gate. Keep any underlying validator available for optional manual audits unless it becomes dead code later.
notes: 2026-04-26 implementation: disabled the Rust ownership/LOC budget as an enforced gate by removing the CI step and the default maintainer static-validation call. The standalone validator command remains available for explicit manual audits. The validator's own unit test is ignored so normal cargo test does not run budget-test logic. Docs now describe the budget as manual/audit-only. Verification: cargo test -p yazelix_maintainer repo_rust_budget, cargo test -p yazelix_maintainer repo_test_runner, validate-rust-test-traceability, validate-nushell-syntax, git diff --check.
close_reason: Disabled Rust ownership budget default gates

title: Split restart rebuild requirement from missing launch-profile evidence
description:
`yzx restart` can rebuild the environment twice in one invocation. The first rebuild is the explicit restart-owned `devenv build shell` pass. The second rebuild happens when the subsequent `yzx launch --force-reenter` still sees `needs_refresh = true` only because a verified launch profile has not been recorded yet, even though the environment was already rebuilt moments earlier.

This shows that Yazelix is conflating two different conditions:
- rebuild-required input drift
- missing or unverified reusable launch-profile evidence

Those conditions should not both force a rebuild. A missing verified launch profile should force a slower shell-entry path, not a second `devenv build shell` for the same inputs.
design: Keep this delete-first. Do not paper over the symptom with restart-only flags unless the broader state model remains coherent. The likely direction is to split config-state reporting so callers can distinguish `rebuild required` from `launch profile missing`, then make restart/launch/startup rebuild only on real input drift. Preserve the current ability to fall back from fast profile activation to shell entry when reusable launch evidence is absent.
acceptance_criteria: 1. `yzx restart` performs at most one rebuild for one unchanged config/input state transition. 2. Missing verified launch-profile evidence no longer forces a rebuild by itself after an explicit successful rebuild. 3. Launch and startup paths still fall back cleanly to shell entry when reusable launch-profile evidence is missing. 4. A focused regression test defends the exact double-rebuild failure mode seen in practice.
notes:
2026-04-20 tracker hygiene: moved out of in_progress. There is no active dirty tree or recent execution on this lane; the restart-vs-launch-profile double-rebuild bug still needs a focused reproduction, state-model split, and regression test before this should be claimed again.
2026-04-21 investigation: this bead describes a backend-era failure mode that no longer exists in the live v15 startup/restart path. Current `yzx restart` in `nushell/scripts/core/yzx_session.nu` just relaunches through the stable owner wrapper and then kills the old Zellij session; it does not run a restart-owned `devenv build shell` pass. Current `yzx launch` in `nushell/scripts/yzx/launch.nu` and `nushell/scripts/core/launch_yazelix.nu` only consumes `config_state.needs_refresh` to decide whether the new terminal should start with a reload-safe detached prefix. The actual startup path in `nushell/scripts/core/start_yazelix_inner.nu` always goes through generated-state materialization (`regenerate_runtime_configs`) rather than cached launch-profile reuse. There is no live `launch_state.nu`, no live `force-reenter` path, and no live `devenv build shell` restart flow in `nushell/scripts/` anymore; remaining references are historical specs/tests only. Current branch contract already states this explicitly in `docs/specs/v15_trimmed_runtime_contract.md`: generated-state repair does not rebuild or reuse a cached backend launch profile. Verified with targeted tests: `test_desktop_fast_path_uses_direct_host_terminal_during_reload_instead_of_stale_wrapper`, `test_launch_here_path_uses_requested_directory_for_nonpersistent_sessions`, and `test_home_manager_profile_restart_uses_owner_wrapper_without_manual_surfaces`.
close_reason: Obsolete on the trimmed v15 branch: the old launch-profile/devenv restart architecture was deleted, so the reported double-rebuild path no longer exists in the live restart/launch/startup code

title: Explain and reduce duplicate shellhook entry during cold startup
description:
The structured cold-start report from yazelix-3n1 shows two distinct shellhook process invocations during shell entry. That may be architecturally justified, but today it is ambiguous and likely contributes avoidable cold-start cost.

Design:
Treat this as a startup-ownership and process-boundary investigation first. Determine why two separate environment.nu shellhook processes run during the cold path, which one is strictly required, and whether one can be deleted or narrowed. Prefer simplifying the flow over adding profiler-only complexity. If both invocations are truly required, document the contract and keep the profiler context clear enough to distinguish them.

Acceptance Criteria:
1. The cold startup path has an explicit explanation for each shellhook process invocation. 2. Unnecessary duplicate shellhook entry is removed if possible; otherwise the surviving duplication is deliberately justified. 3. The startup profile output remains clear about which shellhook process is being timed. 4. Any cleanup does not regress visible rebuild behavior, setup correctness, or noninteractive launch flows.
notes: Completed slice: refreshed startup no longer re-enters `devenv shell` after rebuild. `start_yazelix.nu` now rebuilds once, activates the fresh profile directly, and skips replaying `environment.nu` on that refreshed path. A new default-lane regression defends that ownership boundary. The remaining cold duplicate shellhook contexts were split into follow-up `yazelix-rqz4`, because they survive after this cleanup and appear to come from a deeper cold-path or upstream `devenv` interaction rather than the old Yazelix re-entry path.
close_reason: Completed refreshed-startup cleanup; remaining cold duplicate tracking moved to yazelix-rqz4

title: Expand yzx edit to additional managed surfaces
description: Replace the current narrow yzx edit surface with a picker or fuzzy-query flow for canonical Yazelix-managed config surfaces. Scope for this bead: support config, packs, helix, zellij, and yazi.toml; allow yzx edit with no args to present choices, and yzx edit QUERY to filter or disambiguate managed targets. Keep host shell rc files out of scope. Follow-up bead will cover extra Yazi sub-surfaces like keymap and init.lua.
close_reason: Completed: yzx edit now supports picker or fuzzy-query selection for config, packs, helix, zellij, and yazi.toml managed surfaces.

title: Trim deleted backend and reuse interfaces from workspace command tests
description: The workspace command test fixture still writes deleted devenv-backend, launch-profile, and startup flags into stub modules even though the real startup surface is already narrower. Rewrite the fixture to match the surviving v15 interfaces so the tests stop defending or mentioning deleted backend/reuse semantics.
close_reason: Completed: removed deleted backend/reuse stubs from the workspace-command fixture and trimmed stale DEVENV hostiles from desktop-path probes.

title: Expand user config control where the merge cost is worth it
description: This is about giving users more control over generated tool configs without turning Yazelix into a fully generic config engine. The clearest remaining gap is terminal config merging: users should be able to layer more of their own terminal-emulator config on top of Yazelix-managed config when the merge model is safe and maintainable.
design: Treat this as an evaluation-first feature, not a promise to toml-ize every config surface. Start by identifying the low-hanging configuration areas where merging is practical and the maintenance cost is acceptable. The current likely target is terminal config merging. Keep the broader idea of a more fully controlled profile as direction, but do not expand this bead to cover `devenv` config control; that should stay in a separate bead. The bar is whether the extra user control is worth the implementation and support complexity.
acceptance_criteria: There is a clear decision and scope for which config surfaces should support richer user merging. If terminal config merging is worth it, Yazelix should support it in a maintainable way. If not, the bead should still produce a documented rationale and narrower control model rather than vague ambition about controlling everything. Any implementation path should include unit tests for merge semantics, end-to-end generation checks for the affected config surfaces, and detailed logs showing the user input, managed defaults, and final merged output.
close_reason: Not planned: first-class Ghostty/WezTerm support should expand through explicit Yazelix-owned options and the existing terminal.config_mode ownership boundary, not through a generic terminal-config merge engine.

title: Rust rewrite: delete Nushell owners, not just add helpers
description: Reset the Rust rewrite around one hard rule: each implementation slice must reduce shipped Nushell LOC and delete a surviving Nushell owner seam, not merely add helper-backed Rust under the same public/control-plane surface. The goal is clear, safe, performant architecture with fewer language boundaries, less glue, and more authoritative Rust ownership where it actually simplifies the system.
design:
Delete-first constraints:
1. Measure success against shipped Nushell owners, not nushell/scripts/dev test growth.
2. Do not close a Rust slice that leaves the same Nushell owner in place behind new bridge code.
3. Prefer whole-family ownership moves over helper-by-helper partial insertions.
4. Keep only thin rendering or host-integration layers in Nushell where Rust would not simplify the system.
5. Avoid weak parity or governance tests; keep only tests that defend a real runtime contract or regression.

Progress so far:
- ulb2.1 (env/run) is complete
- ulb2.2 (status summary ownership) is complete

Current target order:
- next: doctor/preflight findings ownership in Rust, with large Nushell doctor helpers shrinking to render/fix glue
- next: bootstrap/materialization orchestration cleanup so existing yzx_core ownership deletes bigger utils/bootstrap seams
- next: public command metadata/help/completion ownership and eventual public registry deletion for the v16 Rust CLI path
- later: generator migration only if it can delete a whole Nushell owner end-to-end instead of adding another render-plan seam
acceptance_criteria: There is a concrete child sequence that targets whole Nushell owner deletion, especially in shipped control-plane and generated-config surfaces. The backlog explicitly rejects additive helper slices that increase or preserve shipped Nushell complexity without deleting an owner.
notes:
2026-04-20 aggressive-rewrite reset:
- env/run and status deletion gates are complete, but the net Nushell result is still effectively flat: tokei from c1af5ef^..HEAD is +21 code lines for the whole nushell tree and only -43 code lines when excluding nushell/scripts/dev.
- conclusion: helper-backed leaf moves improved ownership, but did not materially shrink the shipped Nushell mass. Future children under ulb2 must target large surviving owners in nushell/scripts/utils, core, setup, or public command discovery instead of another narrow helper seam.
- new target order after ulb2.1 and ulb2.2: 1) doctor/preflight findings ownership, 2) bootstrap/materialization orchestration cleanup, 3) public command metadata/help/completion ownership, 4) only then revisit generator migration if it can delete a whole owner.
2026-04-20 delete-first inventory refresh:
- runtime-facing shipped Nushell under core/setup/utils/yzx/integrations/zellij_wrappers is still large enough to dominate the product-side rewrite budget
- the remaining Rust bridge layer is still roughly 1.5k lines of shipped Nushell across config_parser.nu, runtime_contract_checker.nu, generated_runtime_state.nu, runtime_env.nu, status_report.nu, config_state.nu, the doctor_*_report files, install_ownership_report.nu, and the Yazi/Zellij render-plan bridges
- the current inventory/spec layer is stale in exactly the wrong direction: multiple docs still reference deleted owners such as nushell/scripts/yzx/env.nu, nushell/scripts/yzx/run.nu, launch_state.nu, and the old config-migration files
- revised serious-deletion order: 1) replace stale inventory/spec-roadmap docs, 2) delete the remaining yzx_core/yzx_control Nu bridge owners, 3) choose a full-owner materialization/generator migration that deletes a major Nu owner, 4) continue public command metadata/help/completion ownership work only after the real product-side delete lanes are current
2026-04-20 follow-up reopening:
- ulb2 was closed after the first serious Rust rewrite wave, but a real product-side owner seam still survives in the runtime materialization lifecycle
- ulb2.5.1 and ulb2.5.3 thinned bridge glue, but the remaining owner is now spread across core/materialization_orchestrator.nu, utils/generated_runtime_state.nu, and utils/config_state.nu rather than one monolithic file
- this follow-up should be tracked as a fresh child lane under ulb2, not forced back into the already-closed thin-bridge tasks
2026-04-20 follow-up reopening: the next serious full-owner materialization lane is terminal plus Helix. The remaining product-side delete budget is no longer the runtime lifecycle owner; it is the generated terminal and Helix owner family around helix_config_merger.nu, terminal_configs.nu, and terminal_renderers.nu. Track that work under yazelix-ulb2.10 so the rewrite order keeps following the biggest surviving Nushell materialization owners.
close_reason: Completed: all child Rust rewrite deletion lanes are closed; the epic is stale-open and already eligible for close.

title: Rust-own yzx env/run and delete the public Nushell control-plane wrapper seam
description: Move the first whole public control-plane family into Rust: yzx env and yzx run. The point is not helper reuse for its own sake; it is deleting the surviving Nushell owner path around command parsing, usage errors, machine-readable exit behavior, and the shared bridge/config helper blob that currently keeps config_parser.nu large.
design: Target a real owner deletion. Rust should become the primary owner for argv parsing, usage/help for the migrated family, control-plane request planning, and machine-readable error behavior. Nushell may keep only the thinnest host-integration or shell-activation adapter that cannot be deleted cleanly. Closing this bead should materially shrink or delete the public wrappers in nushell/scripts/yzx/env.nu and nushell/scripts/yzx/run.nu and remove a meaningful chunk of config_parser.nu bridge glue rather than adding another helper layer.
acceptance_criteria: The public yzx env/run family no longer depends on Nushell wrapper ownership for its main behavior. Shipped Nushell LOC is reduced by deleting or materially shrinking the env/run wrappers and associated bridge code, especially in config_parser.nu. Any surviving Nushell layer is clearly host-shell glue, not a second control-plane owner.
notes:
2026-04-20 audit findings before implementation:
- Current public owners are nushell/scripts/yzx/env.nu, nushell/scripts/yzx/run.nu, and their export use entries in nushell/scripts/core/yazelix.nu. Closing ulb2.1 must delete or demote those as public parsers, not merely add a Rust helper behind them.
- yzx env contract to preserve: configured-shell default mode uses login shell args, --no-shell uses the invoking shell when available, cwd is preserved, SHELL is set to the launched shell executable, and launch failure prints a clear tip about yzx env --no-shell.
- yzx run contract to preserve: child argv passthrough starts immediately after the command boundary; dash-prefixed child args and child --verbose flags must remain child argv rather than wrapper flags; cwd is preserved.
- Current inefficiency worth deleting: env/run both call prepare_environment, which computes config_state/needs_refresh even though these commands only consume config. The Rust-owned path should not carry that wasted bootstrap work.
- runtime_env.nu is still used by launch, start_yazelix, popup wrappers, and some integration helpers, so env/run cutover alone will not delete that file. ulb2.1.4 exists specifically to force post-cutover bridge cleanup rather than pretending the owner seam is gone once the obvious wrapper files disappear.
- Test policy for this slice: keep the existing high-signal yzx run passthrough regressions; add at most one focused env contract regression if needed; reject help/discovery/parity tests that do not defend real command behavior.
- Preferred transition shape to evaluate in ulb2.1.1: a Rust public dispatcher or direct command path that can short-circuit env/run before the Nushell command tree loads. Reject any plan that still routes main env/run behavior back through the existing Nushell wrappers.
2026-04-20 implementation contract recorded in docs/specs/yzx_env_run_rust_owner_transition.md. Decision: keep bin/yzx -> shells/posix/yzx_cli.sh as the public front door for now, but dispatch env/run directly to a dedicated internal Rust control-plane binary before the Nushell command tree loads. Remove public export ownership from core/yazelix.nu and delete/demote nushell/scripts/yzx/env.nu + run.nu rather than keeping them for help/completion.
close_reason: Verified: yzx_cli.sh dispatches env/run to yzx_control; Rust owns parse/exec and config via normalize+runtime-env; yazelix.nu no longer exports env/run modules (removed); extern bridge appends explicit env/run blocks; prepare_environment unused on env/run path. cargo test -p yazelix_core and yzx run passthrough tests pass.

title: Define the Rust public entrypoint and deletion budget for yzx env/run
description: Before implementation, pin down the transition shape that lets yzx env/run stop being public Nushell commands without forcing a broad all-commands Rust CLI rewrite. The output should name the exact entrypoint/dispatcher shape, the exact Nushell modules expected to stop being public owners, and the deletion budget required before the work can count as done.
design: Reject the helper-only shape. The transition must not keep nushell/scripts/yzx/env.nu and nushell/scripts/yzx/run.nu as public parsers behind a Rust shim. Prefer a public dispatcher or dedicated Rust command path that can short-circuit env/run before the Nushell command tree loads. Name what stays out of scope: broad root/help/completion migration, launch/enter/restart, status/doctor, and config/edit/import UX.
acceptance_criteria: There is a written implementation contract for the env/run migration that identifies the Rust entrypoint, the surviving Nushell role if any, the exact public Nushell owner files to delete or demote, and the minimum shipped Nushell LOC reduction expected from the slice.
notes:
2026-04-20 audit findings before implementation:
- Current public owners are nushell/scripts/yzx/env.nu, nushell/scripts/yzx/run.nu, and their export use entries in nushell/scripts/core/yazelix.nu. Closing ulb2.1 must delete or demote those as public parsers, not merely add a Rust helper behind them.
- yzx env contract to preserve: configured-shell default mode uses login shell args, --no-shell uses the invoking shell when available, cwd is preserved, SHELL is set to the launched shell executable, and launch failure prints a clear tip about yzx env --no-shell.
- yzx run contract to preserve: child argv passthrough starts immediately after the command boundary; dash-prefixed child args and child --verbose flags must remain child argv rather than wrapper flags; cwd is preserved.
- Current inefficiency worth deleting: env/run both call prepare_environment, which computes config_state/needs_refresh even though these commands only consume config. The Rust-owned path should not carry that wasted bootstrap work.
- runtime_env.nu is still used by launch, start_yazelix, popup wrappers, and some integration helpers, so env/run cutover alone will not delete that file. ulb2.1.4 exists specifically to force post-cutover bridge cleanup rather than pretending the owner seam is gone once the obvious wrapper files disappear.
- Test policy for this slice: keep the existing high-signal yzx run passthrough regressions; add at most one focused env contract regression if needed; reject help/discovery/parity tests that do not defend real command behavior.
- Preferred transition shape to evaluate in ulb2.1.1: a Rust public dispatcher or direct command path that can short-circuit env/run before the Nushell command tree loads. Reject any plan that still routes main env/run behavior back through the existing Nushell wrappers.
close_reason: Completed: wrote docs/specs/yzx_env_run_rust_owner_transition.md, linked the bead to the spec, pinned the env/run transition shape around yzx_cli.sh -> internal Rust control-plane dispatch, defined the public-owner deletion budget, and validated specs.

title: Implement Rust-owned yzx env and delete the public Nushell env owner
description: Move yzx env onto the Rust-owned control-plane path and delete nushell/scripts/yzx/env.nu as the public owner. Preserve current contract behavior such as configured-shell launch, invoking-shell no-shell mode, cwd preservation, and clear failure handling without routing main behavior back through Nushell wrappers.
design: Rust should own argv parsing for env, shell selection logic, cwd preservation, runtime-env construction, and shell/process spawn for this command. Keep only truly minimal shell-integration glue if unavoidable. Do not count this bead complete if Nushell still owns the public env command path or if the implementation simply shells back into the old env.nu wrapper.
acceptance_criteria: yzx env works through the Rust-owned path with the existing behavior contract preserved. nushell/scripts/yzx/env.nu is deleted or demoted out of public ownership, and the shipped Nushell surface is smaller by the end of the bead.
close_reason: Implemented libexec/yzx_control env via yzx_cli.sh dispatch; deleted nushell/scripts/yzx/env.nu and removed its export from yazelix.nu. Config load and runtime env use Rust (active_config_surface + normalize_config + compute_runtime_env) without prepare_environment.

title: Implement Rust-owned yzx run and delete the public Nushell run owner
description: Move yzx run onto the Rust-owned control-plane path and delete nushell/scripts/yzx/run.nu as the public owner. Preserve child argv passthrough, especially dash-prefixed child args, cwd preservation, and current runtime env activation semantics without keeping wrapper-side parsing in Nushell.
design: Rust should own argv parsing for run, including the no-wrapper-flags behavior after the command boundary, runtime-env activation, cwd handling, and child process launch. Keep the existing high-signal passthrough contract; do not add weak help/discovery tests. The implementation should not shell back into the old run.nu wrapper or reintroduce wrapper-side argument consumption.
acceptance_criteria: yzx run works through the Rust-owned path with current child-argv passthrough semantics preserved. nushell/scripts/yzx/run.nu is deleted or demoted out of public ownership, and the shipped Nushell surface is smaller by the end of the bead.
close_reason: yzx run is parsed and executed in yzx_control with child argv preserved; deleted nushell/scripts/yzx/run.nu. Integration tests invoke yzx_control with a checked-in argv-log fixture.

title: Trim shared bridge/runtime glue after env/run cut over to Rust
description: After env/run are Rust-owned, audit the shared bridge and runtime-env helpers those commands used to depend on and remove dead or no-longer-justified Nushell glue. The main targets are the env/run-specific portions of environment_bootstrap.nu, runtime_env.nu, core command export/use wiring, and any config_parser.nu bridge code that was only retained for the old public wrappers.
design: Delete-first. Do not leave a large compatibility layer just because it is already there. If a surviving helper is still needed by other Nushell-owned commands, shrink it aggressively and make the remaining ownership explicit. This is the checkpoint that decides whether ulb2.1 actually deleted a meaningful owner seam or merely moved some execution into Rust while leaving the same bridge complexity behind.
acceptance_criteria: The post-cutover audit deletes or materially shrinks the shared Nushell glue that existed only to support public env/run ownership. ulb2.1 should not close until this cleanup confirms a real shipped Nushell reduction beyond the obvious wrapper files.
close_reason: Dropped unused activate_runtime_env from runtime_env.nu; extern bridge appends explicit yzx env/run blocks. Further env/run-only glue in config_parser/environment_bootstrap is still required by other commands (status, parse_yazelix_config, popups).

title: Delete the surviving terminal and Helix compatibility seams
description: Terminal and Helix no longer form one large full-owner migration family. Generated-file ownership for both already lives in Rust; the remaining work is deleting the surviving compatibility wrappers and adjacent caller seams without recreating new helper layers.
design: Delete first. Keep terminal and Helix split. The terminal lane targets `nushell/scripts/utils/terminal_configs.nu` plus its launch-time Ghostty reroll dependencies in `nushell/scripts/core/launch_yazelix.nu`. The Helix lane targets `nushell/scripts/setup/helix_config_merger.nu` plus helper/path consumers in `nushell/scripts/utils/doctor_helix_report.nu`, `nushell/scripts/yzx/edit.nu`, and `nushell/scripts/yzx/import.nu`. Do not treat either lane as a fresh materialization port; the real work is deleting or demoting the wrappers that still survive above the already-landed Rust owners.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Backlog keeps terminal and Helix as separate wrapper-cleanup lanes, not one combined migration. 2. Each child names the exact surviving Nushell owners and callers it must delete or materially shrink. 3. No implementation lane introduces new crates or packaging surfaces without reopening the dependency gate.
close_reason: Completed: the split terminal and Helix wrapper-cleanup lanes are both closed, so the umbrella is stale-open.

title: Define the deletion budget and decide whether terminal and Helix stay one lane or split
description: Before any implementation, define the real surviving owner seam for terminal and Helix materialization and decide whether they should stay in one deletion lane or split into separate follow-ups. The result should narrow the lane around actual owner deletion, not another soft audit.
design: Delete first. Audit nushell/scripts/setup/helix_config_merger.nu, nushell/scripts/utils/terminal_configs.nu, nushell/scripts/utils/terminal_renderers.nu, and adjacent generated-file writers strictly by end-to-end ownership: merge policy, render policy, generated-file writes, and managed-state decisions. Conclude either that one combined lane still deletes the right owners or that the family should split with explicit new children. Do not approve implementation if the plan would leave the same Nu writers intact behind new Rust helpers.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The bead names the exact Nushell owners and responsibilities that still have to disappear or materially shrink. 2. It states clearly whether terminal and Helix stay one lane or split. 3. It records the Rust dependency gate and packaging impact before implementation starts.
notes:
Decision 2026-04-21: split terminal and Helix into separate cleanup lanes.
