Remaining terminal delete targets: `nushell/scripts/utils/terminal_configs.nu` plus its launch-time dependencies in `nushell/scripts/core/launch_yazelix.nu`.
Remaining Helix delete targets: `nushell/scripts/setup/helix_config_merger.nu` plus helper/path callers in `nushell/scripts/utils/doctor_helix_report.nu`, `nushell/scripts/yzx/edit.nu`, and `nushell/scripts/yzx/import.nu`.

Dependency gate: no new production or dev-only crates, no new packaging surfaces, and no new helper layers that leave the same Nushell wrappers intact. Reuse the landed Rust owners plus in-house helper extraction only where necessary.
close_reason: Completed

title: Delete the Helix compatibility wrapper and path-helper seam around nushell/scripts/setup/helix_config_merger.nu
description: Rust already owns Helix merge policy, reveal-binding enforcement, generated-file writes, and import-notice state. The remaining deletion lane is the compatibility wrapper and the callers that still use it for path truth or generated-config building.
design: Delete first. Remove or materially shrink `nushell/scripts/setup/helix_config_merger.nu`. Move any surviving path helpers or config-build callers to shared non-materialization helpers or direct Rust report requests as appropriate. Do not add another Helix helper layer; the point is to stop treating `helix_config_merger.nu` as the canonical Helix seam.
acceptance_criteria: 1. `helix_config_merger.nu` is deleted or materially demoted. 2. `doctor_helix_report.nu`, `yzx/edit.nu`, and `yzx/import.nu` no longer depend on it for end-to-end materialization ownership or canonical path truth. 3. Focused behavior tests defend the surviving Helix contract.
close_reason: Completed

title: Delete the terminal compatibility wrapper and Ghostty reroll seam around nushell/scripts/utils/terminal_configs.nu
description: Rust already owns the managed terminal write path. The remaining deletion lane is the compatibility wrapper around terminal generation plus the launch-time Ghostty reroll seam that still hangs off it.
design: Delete first. Remove or materially shrink `nushell/scripts/utils/terminal_configs.nu` and narrow `nushell/scripts/core/launch_yazelix.nu` dependence on it. Preserve the real launch and reroll behavior, but do not keep a product-side Nushell materialization owner around terminal generation or Ghostty reroll.
acceptance_criteria: 1. `terminal_configs.nu` is deleted or materially demoted. 2. `core/launch_yazelix.nu` no longer depends on it as a product-side materialization owner; any surviving reroll logic is narrowed to the irreducible launch seam. 3. Focused behavior tests defend the surviving terminal and Ghostty contracts.
close_reason: Completed

title: Fix maintainer-shell extern bridge bootstrap warning
description: The maintainer shell emitted a startup warning while regenerating the Nushell yzx extern bridge. The first shellHook pass was resolving the packaged runtime tree, so extern bridge sync used the packaged libexec/yzx_core instead of the explicit maintainer helper override.
design: Delete first. Fix the bootstrap ownership seam in maintainer_shell.nix so noninteractive startup probes resolve the live checkout runtime before bridge sync runs. Keep those probes on --no-config-file because they are bootstrap steps, not interactive shells. Do not paper over the warning with retries or fallback suppression.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Fresh nix develop startup no longer emits the yzx extern bridge warning. 2. The maintainer shell exports the live checkout as YAZELIX_RUNTIME_DIR when launched from the repo. 3. Focused extern-bridge behavior checks still pass.
notes:
Root cause: the maintainer shell hook was bootstrapping against the packaged runtime tree on first shell entry. During that first pass, get_yazelix_runtime_dir resolved the installed /nix/store runtime, so resolve_yzx_core_helper_path chose that runtime's libexec/yzx_core before the explicit maintainer helper override. That packaged helper rejected the newer yzx command-metadata bridge command surface and emitted the startup warning.

Fix: 1) maintainer_shell.nix now pins YAZELIX_RUNTIME_DIR to the live checkout root before runtime-env bootstrap when the current working tree is a real repo root, so shellHook startup and later live-shell calls resolve the same runtime owner. 2) the shellHook Nu probes stay on --no-config-file because they are noninteractive bootstrap steps. 3) the generated extern bridge now reads extern_content from yzx-command-metadata.list, and the redundant yzx-command-metadata.externs helper surface was removed from yzx_core, tests, and docs.

Validation: nix develop -c bash -lc 'printf ok\\n; printf runtime=%s\\n "$YAZELIX_RUNTIME_DIR"'; nix develop -c cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml --test yzx_core_command_metadata; nix develop -c nu -c 'source nushell/scripts/dev/test_shell_managed_config_contracts.nu; test_yzx_extern_bridge_reuses_current_fingerprint; test_yzx_extern_bridge_keeps_previous_bridge_when_refresh_fails'; nix develop -c nu nushell/scripts/dev/validate_specs.nu; git diff --check; rg -n "yzx-command-metadata\\.externs" -S . (no matches).
close_reason: Completed: maintainer-shell bootstrap now resolves the live checkout runtime before extern bridge sync, so fresh nix develop startup no longer emits the stale yzx_core warning.

title: Delete remaining Nushell status reasoning by moving status report construction behind a Rust-owned seam
description: Move the typed `yzx status` summary owner behind Rust only if that deletes the surviving Nushell reasoning in `nushell/scripts/utils/status_report.nu`. The execution target is the structured `summary` payload already defined by `docs/specs/status_doctor_machine_readable_reports.md`, not a broader doctor or clap rewrite. Composer 2 should implement one narrow Rust-backed status-compute seam and leave Nushell as a thin renderer and dispatch layer only.
design:
Execution-ready boundary:
1. Add one narrow Rust/private-bridge seam for status summary construction (for example `yzx_core status.compute`) that returns the typed `summary` payload consumed by both `yzx status` and `yzx status --json`.
2. Rust must own the current `summary` fields now assembled in `collect_status_report`: `version`, `description`, `config_file`, `runtime_dir`, `logs_dir`, `generated_state_repair_needed`, `generated_state_materialization_status`, `generated_state_materialization_reason`, `default_shell`, `terminals`, `helix_runtime`, `persistent_sessions`, and `session_name`.
3. `versions` stays out of the first Rust seam unless the implementation can move it without broadening scope; keep `--versions` attachment and rendering in Nushell if needed.
4. `render_status_report` may stay Nushell-owned, but `collect_status_report` must be deleted or reduced to a trivial bridge with no remaining summary reasoning.
5. Do not broaden this slice into `yzx doctor`, root help or completion work, clap or public CLI ownership, or text-heavy UX rewrites.
6. Stop rather than land another additive helper if the final diff leaves the same authoritative status reasoning in Nushell.
acceptance_criteria:
Composer-2-ready acceptance:
- `yzx status --json` gets its `summary` payload from a Rust-owned seam instead of Nushell-side report construction.
- A meaningful chunk of `nushell/scripts/utils/status_report.nu` is deleted; surviving Nushell code is limited to thin report rendering and optional `--versions` attachment.
- `nushell/scripts/core/yazelix.nu` keeps only thin dispatch for `yzx status`.
- Existing defended behavior remains true for default human output and JSON output.
- If the implementation cannot name concrete deleted Nushell reasoning by the end, the bead is not done.
notes:
2026-04-20 roadmap note: this slice is now the active deletion-gate candidate for the broader v16 public Rust/clap CLI path. If status cannot move behind a real Rust-owned seam that deletes surviving Nushell reasoning, that weakens the case for any broader clap rewrite and should stop future additive CLI rewrite optimism rather than spawning another helper layer.
2026-04-20 composer-2 prep:
- primary live owner targets: `nushell/scripts/utils/status_report.nu` and the `yzx status` span in `nushell/scripts/core/yazelix.nu`
- current defended checks to keep green: `test_yzx_status_reports_basic_runtime_summary`, `test_yzx_status_json_reports_typed_summary`, and `test_yzx_status_json_reports_materialization_repair_when_artifacts_missing` in `nushell/scripts/dev/test_yzx_core_commands.nu`
- recommended verification for this slice: run those status tests plus any new focused Rust unit tests for the typed status payload
- explicit non-goals for Composer 2: `yzx doctor`, broad clap or public-CLI ownership, prose-table redesign, or porting detached shell behavior into Rust
close_reason: Moved typed yzx status summary to yazelix_core status.compute; Nushell only bridges argv and renders; optional --versions still attached in Nushell.

title: Only migrate a config generator to Rust if it deletes the full Nushell owner end-to-end
description: Re-evaluate generator migration under a stricter rule: no more renderer-adjacent helper slices that preserve the same Nushell owner with more bridge code. A future generator move is acceptable only if it transfers a whole generated surface end-to-end and materially deletes the corresponding shipped Nushell generator owner.
design: Start with a short audit comparing Yazi vs Zellij as full-owner migration candidates. Prefer the smaller, clearer surface if there is one. Reject partial render-plan follow-ups by default. Good outcomes are either: 1) a concrete full-owner migration plan that can delete the generator file/class, or 2) an explicit decision that the remaining generators should stay Nushell-owned because a full Rust move would not simplify the architecture.
acceptance_criteria: There is a clear go/no-go decision for the next generator migration based on owner deletion, shipped Nushell reduction, and architecture clarity. No additive helper-only generator bead should survive this audit. If a generator is chosen, the follow-up target is whole-owner deletion rather than another plan-data seam.
notes:
2026-04-20 priority correction: keep this audit alive, but it is no longer the next serious Rust deletion target. Current tokei numbers show the rewrite is still nearly flat on shipped Nushell, so larger surviving owners in doctor/preflight, bootstrap/orchestration, and public command discovery come first. Revisit generator migration only if those bigger owners are exhausted or a full-file renderer deletion candidate becomes concrete.
2026-04-20 delete-first inventory refresh:
- after the bridge/report family, the biggest remaining product-side Nu deletion opportunity is likely a full-owner materialization/generator move rather than another small CLI slice
- treat Yazi, Zellij, and terminal materialization as serious candidates only if a chosen slice deletes the corresponding Nu owner end-to-end
- do not let the current "renderers stay in Nushell" policy become an untouchable assumption if it blocks the largest real Nushell deletions
2026-04-20 audit outcome: choose Yazi as the first full-owner generator/materialization deletion lane. Evidence: Yazi is the smallest coherent owner family still large enough to matter (about 739 raw Nu lines across yazi_config_merger.nu, yazi_bundled_assets.nu, and yazi_user_overrides.nu), its outputs are bounded, and it does not carry Zellij's larger KDL/plugin/layout/reuse state surface. Defer Zellij until after the first whole-owner cut proves we can actually delete a generator family instead of adding another helper seam. Follow-up implementation bead: yazelix-ulb2.3.1.
close_reason: Completed

title: Rust-own Yazi materialization end-to-end and delete the remaining Nushell Yazi generator owners
description: Use the generator audit outcome from yazelix-ulb2.3: Yazi is the first full-owner materialization target because it is the smallest coherent generator family that can still delete real shipped Nushell ownership. This task exists to transfer the Yazi generated surface end-to-end instead of adding another planner around the existing Nu writers.
design: Delete first. A successful slice deletes or materially demotes nushell/scripts/setup/yazi_config_merger.nu, yazi_bundled_assets.nu, and yazi_user_overrides.nu as authoritative owners. Rust should own Yazi merge policy, plugin and theme selection, bundled asset sync expectations, generated-file writes, and managed-state ownership for the generated Yazi surface. Do not accept a result where Rust computes more plan data but Nushell still owns the same writer/orchestrator seam. Keep only thin command or process glue in Nushell if the owner family genuinely disappears.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The surviving Yazi generation path deletes or materially demotes the current Nushell Yazi owner family rather than preserving it behind more Rust planning calls. 2. Rust becomes the end-to-end owner for the generated Yazi surface, including managed-file write decisions and bundled asset expectations. 3. Existing Yazi-generated-config behavior remains defended by focused tests. 4. The slice yields visible shipped Nushell reduction in the Yazi materialization family.
notes: Audit basis from yazelix-ulb2.3: the Yazi family is about 739 raw Nushell lines across yazi_config_merger.nu, yazi_bundled_assets.nu, and yazi_user_overrides.nu, with bounded outputs and lower product-risk than the larger Zellij family. Zellij remains the likely second full-owner materialization lane after this one proves that a whole generator family can actually disappear.
close_reason: Completed

title: Rust-own Zellij materialization end-to-end and delete the remaining Nushell Zellij generator owners
description: Use the Yazi full-owner deletion as the template for the next serious generator cut. Zellij is the next largest coherent materialization owner family and should move to Rust only if the Nu generator owners disappear end-to-end.
design: Delete first. Rust should own Zellij config merge policy, semantic KDL block rendering, owned top-level settings, plugin path/sync expectations, layout generation, generation-state reuse decisions, generated-file writes, and managed-state ownership for the generated Zellij surface. Target deleting or materially demoting nushell/scripts/setup/zellij_config_merger.nu, zellij_semantic_blocks.nu, zellij_base_config.nu, zellij_owned_settings.nu, zellij_plugin_paths.nu, zellij_generation_state.nu, and nushell/scripts/utils/layout_generator.nu. Reject any result where Rust computes more plan data but Nu remains the writer/orchestrator owner. Rust dependency gate: production crates stay in-house/std plus existing workspace crates serde, serde_json, toml, lexopt, sha2, thiserror; no new production crate unless a real KDL parser/formatter is required and recorded before use. Dev-only crates stay with existing assert_cmd, pretty_assertions, tempfile. Packaging impact should remain inside yzx_core.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The surviving Zellij generation path deletes or materially demotes the current Nushell Zellij owner family rather than preserving it behind more Rust planning calls. 2. Rust becomes the end-to-end owner for generated Zellij config, layouts, plugin expectations, and generation-state write decisions. 3. Existing generated-config behavior remains defended by focused tests. 4. The slice yields visible shipped Nushell reduction in the Zellij materialization family.
close_reason: Completed: Rust yzx_core now owns Zellij config/layout/plugin/generation-state materialization end-to-end; zellij_config_merger.nu is a thin wrapper; zellij_semantic_blocks.nu, zellij_base_config.nu, zellij_owned_settings.nu, zellij_plugin_paths.nu, zellij_generation_state.nu, and layout_generator.nu are deleted. Verified cargo check, zellij_materialization Rust tests, Zellij plugin contracts, shell managed config contracts, Nu syntax, and spec validation. Broader generated-config suite still has one Yazi warm static-asset sentinel failure tracked under yazelix-8h9y.

title: Rust-own doctor/preflight findings and delete large Nushell doctor check owners
description: Move doctor and preflight classification behind one Rust-owned findings engine instead of leaving nushell/scripts/utils/doctor.nu and its helper files as the main owners of runtime/install health reasoning. The goal is to materially shrink shipped Nushell in the doctor/preflight family, especially doctor.nu, doctor_helix.nu, doctor_install_artifacts.nu, and runtime_distribution_capabilities.nu, while keeping human rendering and fix actions in Nushell where shell-side effects still belong.
design: Delete-first boundary: Rust should own the structured findings model reused by yzx doctor, launch preflight, and adjacent install-smoke/reporting paths when the same reasoning already exists in yzx_core or can be consolidated there. Nushell should keep only rendering, severity-to-UX mapping when truly surface-specific, and --fix side effects. Do not land a second helper seam that leaves the same authoritative checks in doctor.nu. Prefer one findings pipeline over more one-off commands. Reuse runtime-contract.evaluate only if the end state deletes real Nushell ownership rather than expanding parallel bridge code.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The main doctor/preflight reasoning no longer lives in doctor.nu and related Nu helpers. 2. doctor.nu, doctor_helix.nu, doctor_install_artifacts.nu, and/or runtime_distribution_capabilities.nu are materially shrunk or partially deleted, not just wrapped. 3. Human-readable doctor output and fix actions still work, but consume Rust-owned structured findings. 4. Tests defend real findings contracts instead of command discovery noise.
notes: Composer 2 prep: do not hand off the whole remaining doctor surface at once. Preferred next bounded child is yazelix-ulb2.4.2 (Helix findings family), because doctor_helix.nu is still a large standalone Nu owner and the fix boundary can remain in Nushell. After that, revisit the remaining doctor.nu surfaces separately: config/reporting, shared runtime preflight/runtime distribution, and zellij plugin health.
close_reason: All bounded doctor/preflight children are complete. Remaining serious Nushell-deletion work has moved to the bridge-layer, materialization/generator, and command-registry/documentation seams tracked elsewhere under ulb2.

title: Rust-own install ownership and launcher provenance; delete install_ownership.nu and launcher_resolution.nu as decision owners
description: Move install ownership, stable wrapper selection, desktop launcher provenance, and Home Manager versus manual artifact classification behind one Rust-owned report so install_ownership.nu and launcher_resolution.nu stop being decision owners across doctor, home_manager prepare, and launch-adjacent flows.
design:
Boundary: execute as install-provenance slice, not an expansion of runtime-contract.evaluate. runtime_contract.rs owns launch/preflight (terminal, nixGL); this slice owns install ownership, wrapper provenance, desktop-entry freshness, takeover artifacts, and doctor-facing shadowing findings.

Rust seam: one new yzx_core helper (working name install-ownership.evaluate) returning typed JSON consumed by doctor, desktop/install flows, Home Manager prepare, and yzx restart.

Scope gate (b5u1): prefer dropping legacy managed shell-block upgrade path in this slice; fallback isolate shell-block into tiny Nu-only compatibility; do not port shell-block parsing into Rust unless that path is explicitly retained.

Request inputs explicit (not hidden env): runtime_dir, home_dir, user, xdg_config_home, xdg_data_home, command_search_paths, invoked_yzx_path, redirected_from_stale_yzx_path, optional include_legacy_shell_blocks.

Response covers: install_owner (manual|home-manager), stable_wrapper, desktop launcher path/provenance, desktop entry state (local/profile, Exec, Terminal, shadowing), home_manager prepare artifacts, doctor findings for freshness and wrapper shadowing.

Mutation stays in Nu: mv/backup, shell-block rewrite for prepare --apply, desktop install/uninstall, doctor prose.

Deletion gates: install_ownership.nu no longer decides ownership; launcher_resolution.nu gone or trivial; doctor_install_artifacts.nu stops path/ownership reasoning; legacy shell blocks isolated or removed.

Verification: existing test_yzx_doctor_commands.nu and test_yzx_core_commands.nu; add focused Rust tests for classification, wrapper selection, desktop parsing, shadowing, artifacts.
acceptance_criteria: 1. install_ownership.nu and launcher_resolution.nu materially shrink or disappear as decision owners. 2. Doctor, home_manager prepare, and related flows consume one Rust-owned ownership or provenance report. 3. Legacy shell-block compatibility is removed or explicitly isolated instead of silently carried forward. 4. Tests defend ownership and provenance contracts, not help text.
notes:
Execution sequence: (1) b5u1 boundary decision—this implementation retires legacy shell-block path: remove detection/cleanup from prepare and doctor, align with b5u1 acceptance. (2) Contract in bead/spec. (3) Rust module + yzx_core command. (4) Nu consumer rewrites and deletions. (5) cargo test + nu tests.

Dependency posture: std/in-house + serde; reuse patterns from runtime_contract.rs for path/exec checks.
close_reason: Completed: install-ownership.evaluate in yzx_core; Nushell install_ownership_report.nu bridge; doctor/home_manager/desktop/launcher_resolution/runtime_distribution consume Rust report; removed doctor_install_artifacts.nu and legacy shell-block doctor/prepare paths (b5u1). Integration test install_ownership_evaluate_prints_ok_envelope. Verified: cargo test -p yazelix_core, home_manager prepare tests, validate_specs.

title: Rust-own Helix doctor findings and shrink doctor_helix.nu
description: Move the Helix-focused doctor findings family behind a Rust-owned findings seam so nushell/scripts/utils/doctor_helix.nu stops being the main owner of Helix runtime conflict detection, runtime-health classification, and managed Helix reveal-integration checks. Keep shell-side fix actions and human rendering in Nushell, but delete substantive Helix reasoning from doctor_helix.nu and route yzx doctor through Rust-owned structured findings.
design: Composer 2 prep: treat this as the next bounded child under ulb2.4 instead of attempting the whole remaining doctor surface at once. Boundary: only the Helix doctor family in doctor_helix.nu plus the corresponding doctor.nu call sites. Rust should own structured findings for (1) Helix runtime conflicts, (2) Helix runtime health from hx --health / runtime directory inventory, and (3) managed Helix reveal-integration contract checks. Nushell keeps fix_helix_runtime_conflicts and any direct shell/file mutations. Prefer extending one doctor/findings seam in yzx_core rather than adding another ad-hoc helper command; a narrow helix-only request/response within that seam is acceptable for this slice. Reuse existing config.normalize or runtime-materialization seams where possible, but do not leave doctor_helix.nu as the authoritative classifier after the bridge lands. Keep doctor output text and --fix wiring in Nushell. Target file set: rust_core/yazelix_core/src/... new or extended doctor findings module; nushell/scripts/utils/doctor_helix.nu; nushell/scripts/utils/doctor.nu; tests in nushell/scripts/dev/test_yzx_doctor_commands.nu and focused Rust tests. Explicit non-goals: zellij plugin health, install ownership/provenance, shared runtime preflight, and broad config-surface reporting in this child.
acceptance_criteria: 1. doctor_helix.nu materially shrinks or becomes mostly bridge/fix glue instead of owning Helix classification. 2. yzx doctor still reports Helix runtime conflicts, runtime health, and managed reveal-integration findings through Rust-owned structured data. 3. --fix still works for Helix runtime conflict remediation from Nushell. 4. Tests defend Helix doctor behavior/regressions, not command discovery.
notes: Composer 2 handoff notes: start by inventorying the three exported doctor_helix.nu families and mapping each current result shape to a Rust finding schema. Keep the slice bounded; do not mix in zellij plugin or generic config work. High-signal existing tests live in nushell/scripts/dev/test_yzx_doctor_commands.nu; preserve or retarget only the Helix-relevant ones. Success metric is shipped Nushell owner deletion, not extra helper coverage.
close_reason: Helix doctor classification lives in yazelix_core::doctor_helix_report; yzx_core doctor-helix.evaluate bridges from doctor_helix_report.nu; doctor_helix.nu retains fix_helix_runtime_conflicts only; doctor.nu consumes collect_helix_doctor_results once; Rust and CLI integration tests added.

title: Rust-own shared runtime preflight and runtime-distribution findings
description: Move the remaining deterministic shared-runtime doctor/preflight findings out of nushell/scripts/utils/doctor.nu so Rust owns the classification and Nushell only renders results and performs shell-side fixes. This slice should cover the shared runtime preflight family currently assembled in check_shared_runtime_preflight() plus the runtime distribution capability profile currently owned by runtime_distribution_capabilities.nu, while leaving live Zellij/plugin health and broad config diagnostics untouched.
design:
Delete-first boundary: do not add another parallel helper that leaves doctor.nu as the real owner. Prefer extending existing runtime-contract.evaluate or adjacent Rust findings data so doctor.nu consumes one Rust-owned structured result for (1) generated layout and runtime-script preflight findings already derived from runtime-contract checks, and (2) runtime/distribution capability classification currently inferred from install ownership plus runtime-root shape. The expected end state is that doctor.nu loses most of the decision glue in check_shared_runtime_preflight(), runtime_distribution_capabilities.nu materially shrinks or disappears, and any remaining Nushell code is only bridge glue, rendering, or --fix side effects like repair_generated_runtime_state.

Boundaries for this child:
- In scope: doctor.nu shared runtime preflight assembly, runtime_distribution_capabilities.nu, any small bridge helpers needed to pass inputs to Rust, and focused tests covering doctor findings contracts.
- Likely Rust touchpoints: extend yzx_core findings or adjacent runtime-contract/reporting seams rather than inventing a standalone ad-hoc command if the same structured result can serve doctor/preflight consumers.
- Preserve existing fix behavior: generated-state failures that point at managed layout artifacts must still surface fix_action = repair_generated_runtime_state from Nushell.
- Non-goals: check_zellij_plugin_health(), pane-orchestrator live session reasoning, install ownership itself (already moved in ulb2.4.1), Helix findings (done in ulb2.4.2), and broad config validation/reporting.
acceptance_criteria:
1. doctor.nu no longer owns the main shared-runtime preflight and runtime-distribution classification logic; it consumes Rust-owned structured findings for those families.
2. runtime_distribution_capabilities.nu is materially shrunk or deleted, and check_shared_runtime_preflight() in doctor.nu loses real decision glue rather than merely forwarding the same logic.
3. Doctor output and --fix behavior remain intact, including repair_generated_runtime_state for managed generated-layout failures.
4. Focused tests defend the runtime-distribution and shared-runtime doctor findings contracts instead of command-discovery noise.
notes:
Composer 2 handoff: start by inventorying what is still authoritatively decided in doctor.nu after ulb2.4.2. The highest-value deletion seam is the combo of build_runtime_distribution_doctor_result + get_runtime_distribution_capability_profile + check_shared_runtime_preflight() record shaping. runtime_contract_checker.nu is already mostly a bridge, so the goal is not to move that bridge wholesale but to stop doctor.nu from re-classifying or re-assembling deterministic findings around it.

Suggested attack plan:
1. Trace the exact doctor result shapes emitted today for runtime distribution capability and shared runtime preflight.
2. Decide whether to extend an existing Rust data shape or add one narrow findings envelope that groups both families together for doctor consumption.
3. Keep Nushell ownership only for fix mapping (repair_generated_runtime_state) and user-facing rendering.
4. Add focused tests that defend real doctor finding payloads, especially the managed-layout generated-state repair path and runtime/distribution mode messaging.

Stop if the implementation mostly adds JSON argv builders while leaving doctor.nu or runtime_distribution_capabilities.nu as the true decision owners.
close_reason: Added yazelix_core doctor_runtime_report and yzx_core doctor-runtime.evaluate; Nushell doctor_runtime_report.nu bridges install_io + materialization plan into Rust; doctor.nu consumes distribution and shared preflight from Rust; deleted runtime_distribution_capabilities.nu; tests cover distribution/fix_action and CLI envelope.

title: Rust-own config doctor findings and shrink check_configuration
description: Move the remaining config-surface doctor classification out of nushell/scripts/utils/doctor.nu so Rust owns the findings contract for configuration presence, legacy-surface detection, and stale-schema diagnostics. This slice should cover the current check_configuration() family: primary surface reconciliation, custom-vs-default config detection, legacy yazelix.nix / root-level surface warnings, missing-config errors, and stale or unsupported yazelix.toml reporting. Keep Nushell responsible for rendering doctor output and shell-side fixes such as creating a default yazelix.toml.
design:
Delete-first boundary: do not leave check_configuration() in doctor.nu as the authoritative owner behind a thin wrapper. Prefer extending the existing yzx_core config/reporting seam so Rust returns the structured doctor findings directly, reusing config.normalize or adjacent diagnostics data where possible. A successful outcome materially shrinks check_configuration(), and may also shrink config_diagnostics.nu / config_surfaces.nu bridge code where they only assemble deterministic doctor-state decisions.

Boundaries for this child:
- In scope: doctor.nu check_configuration(), the config-doctor result shapes it emits today, reconcile_primary_config_surfaces usage as it relates to doctor classification, and stale-schema warning assembly that currently depends on build_config_diagnostic_report.
- Likely Rust touchpoints: extend yzx_core with a config-doctor evaluation that can classify config presence / default-template mode / legacy-surface errors alongside diagnostic-report issue counts.
- Preserve existing doctor UX: custom-config ok row, stale-or-unsupported warning text, legacy yazelix.nix detection, and no-config/default-template messaging should still render through doctor.nu. fix_available for the default-template case must still let Nushell run fix_create_config.
- Non-goals: live Zellij plugin health, runtime distribution / shared preflight (done in ulb2.4.3), Helix findings (done in ulb2.4.2), install ownership / launcher provenance (done in ulb2.4.1), and broad bootstrap/generated-state orchestration from ulb2.5.
acceptance_criteria:
1. doctor.nu no longer owns the main config doctor classification logic; it consumes Rust-owned structured findings for configuration presence, legacy surfaces, and stale-schema reporting.
2. check_configuration() is materially shrunk or deleted, and nearby config-diagnostic bridge code loses real shipped decision logic instead of only moving lines around.
3. Doctor output and --fix behavior remain intact, including fix_create_config for the default-template case.
4. Focused tests defend real config doctor finding payloads rather than command-discovery noise.
notes: Dependency decision for this Rust bead: use existing in-tree crates and std only. Production crates: none added; reuse existing serde/serde_json/toml/lexopt already present in yazelix_core. Dev-only crates: none added. In-house logic: config doctor finding classification and JSON bridge types should stay local because the domain contract is project-specific and already adjacent to config_normalize. Rejected alternatives: adding a new error/reporting helper crate or filesystem abstraction crate would add packaging churn without buying stable parsing beyond existing TOML/JSON support. Packaging impact: none expected beyond rebuilding the existing rust_core helper.
close_reason: Completed

title: Collapse bootstrap and generated-state orchestration to thin Nushell bridges
description: Config normalization, config-state hashing, runtime-env planning, runtime-contract checks, materialization planning, and status summary construction are already Rust-owned, but Nushell still carries too much orchestration and duplicate decision glue in generated_runtime_state.nu, environment_bootstrap.nu, runtime_contract_checker.nu, and nearby bridge helpers. This bead exists to delete that leftover deterministic reasoning so those modules become thin bridges or disappear, instead of letting helper-backed Rust and large Nu orchestration live forever in parallel.
design: Delete-first boundary: keep text renderers, shell process execution, and host integration in Nushell where Rust would not simplify them. Move only the surviving deterministic orchestration and classification that can be centralized behind existing yzx_core/yzx_control seams. A successful outcome materially shrinks utils/bootstrap files rather than adding more helper argv builders and bridge wrappers. Stop if the work would preserve the same Nu owner path behind more JSON plumbing.
acceptance_criteria: 1. generated_runtime_state.nu, environment_bootstrap.nu, runtime_contract_checker.nu, config_parser.nu, or adjacent bridge files lose real shipped logic, not just line reshuffles. 2. The remaining Nushell layer is clearly bridge/render/process glue rather than a second decision owner. 3. Existing startup/generated-state/runtime-contract behavior remains defended by focused tests. 4. The slice yields visible shipped Nushell reduction in large utils/bootstrap files.
notes:
Sequencing note: start with yazelix-ulb2.5.1 (generated-state/materialization orchestration + thin merger owners), then take yazelix-ulb2.5.2 (startup/launch preflight and bootstrap orchestration). This keeps the first cut focused on the biggest delete seam around generated_runtime_state.nu before touching the startup/launch wrappers and runtime_contract_checker.nu.
2026-04-20 residual-owner audit:
- ulb2.5.1 and ulb2.5.2 landed, but a non-trivial Nu bridge layer still survives in the exact family this umbrella owns
- the current high-value residual is the remaining yzx_core/yzx_control bridge/report glue in config_parser.nu, runtime_contract_checker.nu, generated_runtime_state.nu, runtime_env.nu, status_report.nu, config_state.nu, doctor_*_report.nu, install_ownership_report.nu, and the Yazi/Zellij render-plan bridges
- next work under ulb2.5 should target deleting or collapsing those bridge owners instead of adding more helper argv builders or JSON plumbing
close_reason: Completed: child lanes 5.1, 5.2, and 5.3 landed the generated-state/bootstrap/bridge collapse; any new generator deletion work belongs under ulb2.3 siblings.

title: Rust-own generated runtime materialization orchestration and thin merger Nu owners
description: Move the remaining deterministic generated-state orchestration out of nushell/scripts/utils/generated_runtime_state.nu and the Yazi/Zellij merger owners so Rust becomes the source of truth for whether generated runtime artifacts need refresh, which artifacts are expected, and which render/materialization steps should run. This slice should target the current generated_runtime_state + merger cluster: plan/apply orchestration, refresh/noop/repair classification, and the surviving deterministic merge decisions in yazi_config_merger.nu and zellij_config_merger.nu that are already adjacent to Rust-owned render plans. Keep Nushell responsible only for shell-side file writes, plugin sync/process execution, and human-facing progress output where Rust would not simplify the system.
design:
Delete-first boundary: do not preserve generated_runtime_state.nu as the real owner behind more argv builders. Prefer extending the existing runtime-materialization / render-plan seams so Rust returns the orchestration decisions directly and Nushell only performs file IO and host actions. A successful outcome materially shrinks generated_runtime_state.nu and the merger modules rather than just moving conditionals around.

Boundaries for this child:
- In scope: generated_runtime_state.nu orchestration, the decision glue around compute_runtime_materialization_plan / apply_runtime_materialization, and deterministic merge/render decisions in yazi_config_merger.nu / zellij_config_merger.nu that can be centralized in Rust.
- Likely Rust touchpoints: runtime_materialization, yazi_render_plan, zellij_render_plan, and adjacent helper request/response shapes.
- Preserve Nu-only responsibilities: plugin wasm sync, filesystem writes, progress printing, user override file reads, and shell/process integration.
- Non-goals: pane-orchestrator truth work under 0w1u.*, doctor findings under ulb2.4.*, macOS launcher work, or broad environment bootstrap ownership outside the generated-state/materialization slice.
acceptance_criteria:
1. generated_runtime_state.nu is materially shrunk and no longer owns the main generated-state orchestration/classification logic.
2. yazi_config_merger.nu and/or zellij_config_merger.nu lose real deterministic merge/render decision logic rather than only rearranging helper calls.
3. The remaining Nushell layer is clearly file-write/process glue and progress rendering, not a second orchestration owner.
4. Focused tests defend real generated-state/materialization behavior, especially noop/repair/refresh transitions.
notes:
Composer 2 handoff, tightened from code inspection:

Primary delete seam:
- `nushell/scripts/utils/generated_runtime_state.nu` is still the real owner of generated-state orchestration. The highest-value functions are `regenerate_runtime_configs`, `record_current_materialized_state`, and `repair_generated_runtime_state`.
- Today those functions still decide noop vs repair, when to print progress, when to call Yazi vs Zellij generation, when `should_sync_static_assets` matters, and how plan/apply results map into the user-facing repair flow.

Concrete adjacent owners to shrink in the same slice:
- `nushell/scripts/setup/yazi_config_merger.nu`: keep file writes and user override reads in Nushell, but target the remaining deterministic decisions around generated `yazi.toml` / `theme.toml` / `init.lua` content that are already adjacent to `compute_yazi_render_plan`.
- `nushell/scripts/setup/zellij_config_merger.nu`: keep plugin wasm sync, layout generation side effects, and writes in Nushell, but target the deterministic generation/reuse decisions and render-plan-owned config assembly around `generate_merged_zellij_config`.

Explicit non-goals for this child:
- Do not absorb plugin sync (`sync_pane_orchestrator_runtime_wasm`, `sync_zjstatus_runtime_wasm`) into Rust.
- Do not move direct filesystem writes or user override file reads into Rust unless doing so clearly deletes more Nushell than it adds.
- Do not broaden into `runtime_contract_checker.nu`, `environment_bootstrap.nu`, `start_yazelix.nu`, or `launch_yazelix.nu`; that is reserved for `yazelix-ulb2.5.2`.

Dependency decision for this Rust bead:
- Production crates: none added; reuse existing serde/serde_json/toml/lexopt and current in-tree modules.
- Dev-only crates: none added.
- In-house logic: generated-state orchestration/result modeling should stay local because the contract is Yazelix-specific and already adjacent to `runtime_materialization`, `yazi_render_plan`, and `zellij_render_plan`.
- Rejected alternatives: adding templating/render helper crates or filesystem abstraction crates would increase packaging churn without deleting the real Nushell owners.
- Packaging impact: none expected beyond rebuilding the existing rust_core helper.

Suggested implementation order:
1. First collapse `generated_runtime_state.nu` so Rust owns the plan/apply/repair classification and Nushell becomes progress + side-effect glue.
2. Only then pull the remaining deterministic Yazi/Zellij generation decisions into Rust if they delete real owner logic in the merger modules.
3. Stop if the result still leaves `generated_runtime_state.nu` deciding when regeneration happens.
close_reason: Rust: runtime-materialization.repair-evaluate + Nu thin repair/regenerate bridge

title: Rust-own startup and launch preflight/bootstrap orchestration
description: Move the remaining deterministic startup/launch bootstrap orchestration out of nushell/scripts/utils/environment_bootstrap.nu, nushell/scripts/utils/runtime_contract_checker.nu, nushell/scripts/core/start_yazelix.nu, and the adjacent launch preflight flow so Rust becomes the source of truth for preflight result selection, working-dir/runtime-script/terminal validation outcomes, and bootstrap state decisions. This slice should cover the current check_startup_preflight / check_launch_preflight result plumbing, the duplicated require_runtime_check/result extraction logic in start_yazelix.nu and launch_yazelix.nu, and the environment-bootstrap classification that still reassembles Rust-owned config/config-state/runtime-contract data in Nushell. Keep Nushell responsible for actually launching processes, exporting env vars, printing progress, and interactive shell behavior.
design:
Delete-first boundary: do not keep runtime_contract_checker.nu as the authoritative owner behind a thin wrapper. Prefer extending existing runtime-contract / runtime-env / status or control-plane seams so Rust returns the preflight/bootstrap decisions directly, and Nushell only executes the resulting shell/process actions. A successful outcome materially shrinks runtime_contract_checker.nu and the startup/launch wrappers instead of adding more check-result unpacking glue.

Boundaries for this child:
- In scope: runtime_contract_checker.nu result extraction/helpers, environment_bootstrap.nu orchestration, and duplicated startup/launch preflight selection in start_yazelix.nu and launch_yazelix.nu.
- Likely Rust touchpoints: runtime_contract, runtime_env, status/control-plane request shapes, and any narrow startup/launch preflight evaluation command needed to centralize the deterministic decisions.
- Preserve Nu-only responsibilities: shell invocation, terminal launch commands, env injection, progress output, and interactive UX.
- Non-goals: Yazi/Zellij generated config materialization (ulb2.5.1), doctor findings, pane-orchestrator/sidebar truth work, or macOS launcher work.
acceptance_criteria:
1. runtime_contract_checker.nu and environment_bootstrap.nu are materially shrunk and no longer own the main startup/launch/bootstrap classification logic.
2. start_yazelix.nu and launch_yazelix.nu lose duplicated preflight-result extraction/validation logic.
3. Remaining Nushell code in this area is clearly process-launch/env glue rather than a second preflight/bootstrap decision owner.
4. Focused tests defend real startup/launch preflight behavior rather than helper-discovery noise.
notes:
Composer 2 handoff: start by comparing the startup and launch preflight flows side by side. The highest-value deletion seam is the repeated 'call runtime-contract helper -> select required checks -> require_runtime_check -> extract path/candidates' pattern plus environment_bootstrap reassembling Rust-owned config/config-state decisions.

Suggested attack plan:
1. Inventory the duplicated startup/launch preflight extraction logic and identify the common request/response shape that Rust should own.
2. Separate bootstrap state decisions from the process-execution layer in start_yazelix.nu / launch_yazelix.nu.
3. Keep process launching and shell env wiring in Nushell, but centralize all deterministic validation/classification in Rust.
4. Preserve current startup and launch failure messages unless a Rust-owned message clearly deletes duplicate prose owners.
5. Add focused tests for real preflight/bootstrap behavior, especially missing check results, invalid working dirs, and terminal support selection.

Stop if the work mostly creates another helper wrapper while runtime_contract_checker.nu still owns result selection and classification.
close_reason: Rust: startup-launch-preflight.evaluate; Nu thin preflight + bootstrap

title: Delete the remaining yzx_core/yzx_control Nushell bridge owners
description: The helper-backed Rust slices landed, but a substantial product-side Nu bridge layer still survives around yzx_core and yzx_control. The current delete-first inventory puts the residual bridge/report family at roughly 1.5k shipped Nu lines across config_parser.nu, runtime_contract_checker.nu, generated_runtime_state.nu, runtime_env.nu, status_report.nu, config_state.nu, doctor_*_report.nu, install_ownership_report.nu, and the Yazi/Zellij render-plan bridges. This task exists to collapse that family instead of letting bridge code become a permanent second owner.
design: Delete first. Treat the target files as one surviving owner family: helper resolution, argv construction, envelope parsing, duplicated error-surface plumbing, and thin report-plan adapters should be centralized or deleted. Prefer fewer Rust entrypoints and fewer Nu wrappers, not more one-command bridge modules. A successful slice can keep minimal human-facing rendering and shell/process glue in Nu, but it must delete or collapse the current bridge/report owners materially. Do not accept a result that merely moves helper args around while the same file set remains in place.
acceptance_criteria: 1. One or more of config_parser.nu, runtime_contract_checker.nu, generated_runtime_state.nu, runtime_env.nu, status_report.nu, config_state.nu, doctor_*_report.nu, install_ownership_report.nu, yazi_render_plan.nu, or zellij_render_plan.nu is deleted outright or reduced to clearly thin glue. 2. The surviving bridge path has fewer public/authoritative owners than today and no duplicated helper resolution or error-envelope parsing stack. 3. Tests defend the remaining runtime/report contracts without discovery noise. 4. The slice yields visible shipped Nu reduction in this bridge family, not just line movement.
notes: Inventory basis: the current bridge/report layer is the clearest next deletion target after the earlier env/run, status, update, doctor, and bootstrap/materialization slices. This task should stay focused on product-side owner deletion, not maintainer-test growth.
close_reason: Completed

title: Rust-own the yzx update family via yzx_control and delete the public update block from core/yazelix.nu
description: The public yzx update family is still a sizable owner inside core/yazelix.nu. Move yzx update, yzx update upstream, yzx update home_manager, and yzx update nix behind the existing yzx_control pattern so the public Nushell definitions stop owning this family.
design:
Delete first. Extend `shells/posix/yzx_cli.sh` direct dispatch and `yzx_control` instead of layering another Rust helper behind the same public Nushell commands. Treat this bead as a narrow public-owner deletion slice, not a blanket policy change that every distribution command should move to Rust. The current public owner is concentrated inside `nushell/scripts/core/yazelix.nu`: helper prose/validation functions plus `yzx update`, `yzx update upstream`, `yzx update home_manager`, and `yzx update nix`. The desired end state is that the public update family no longer depends on those Nushell defs for argv parsing, usage/help, or exit behavior. Keep only truly shell-bound or UI-specific behavior outside Rust, and coordinate with CLI metadata work so migrated help/completion remain coherent.

Boundary guidance:
- In scope: public dispatch/parse/exit behavior for `yzx update`, `yzx update upstream`, `yzx update home_manager`, and likely `yzx update nix` if it can move cleanly.
- Keep out of scope: broad clap/root-help rewrite, generated extern ownership, detached terminal execution, renderer/template work, Nix/package policy redesign, maintainer-only update workflows.
- Important tension: `docs/specs/yzx_command_surface_backend_coupling.md` currently treats installed-runtime/distribution maintenance as a family that often stays Nushell-owned. Therefore this bead only makes sense if it deletes a real public owner seam via `yzx_control` and `yzx_cli.sh`; if the result is just a Rust shim over unchanged public Nushell commands, reject that shape.
- If `yzx update nix` turns out to be policy-heavy or too shell/interactive to fit the same cutover cleanly, split it explicitly instead of letting it block `upstream` + `home_manager` ownership deletion.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The public yzx update definitions in core/yazelix.nu disappear or lose public-owner status. 2. yzx_control becomes the public owner for the migrated update family. 3. Help, completion, and exit behavior for the migrated leaves are defended by focused parity checks. 4. The work deletes a real public Nushell owner instead of adding another private helper.
notes:
2026-04-20 Composer handoff notes:
- Current owner inventory in `nushell/scripts/core/yazelix.nu`: `print_update_owner_warning`, `print_update_path_confirmation`, `fail_if_home_manager_owned_upstream_update`, `load_default_profile_elements`, `resolve_active_yazelix_profile_entry`, `require_current_working_flake`, and the four public `yzx update*` defs.
- User contract to preserve from `docs/yzx_cli.md`: bare `yzx update` prints the owner choices; `yzx update upstream` prints and runs `nix profile upgrade --refresh <matching-entry>` only for default-profile installs; `yzx update home_manager` must run from the owning flake dir, runs `nix flake update yazelix`, and prints `home-manager switch` as a manual next step; `yzx update nix` upgrades Determinate Nix via `sudo determinate-nixd upgrade` with `--yes`/`--verbose` behavior preserved.
- Existing defended behavior already lives in `nushell/scripts/dev/test_yzx_core_commands.nu`: exact profile-entry selection for `update upstream`, early failure for Home Manager-owned installs, clear failure when no profile entry matches, and `update home_manager` updating only the current input and printing the manual switch step. Keep those high-signal tests; add at most one focused parity test for `update nix` if its contract stays in scope. Reject help/discovery-only tests.
- Suggested attack plan: (1) compare the env/run `yzx_cli.sh -> yzx_control` cutover with the update-family shape, (2) decide whether `yzx_control` owns both parser and executor for each surviving update leaf or whether some leaf should remain intentionally Nushell-owned, (3) move only the public owner surface first, (4) delete/demote the public update block in `core/yazelix.nu`, (5) keep any surviving Nushell code clearly internal helper glue, not a second public command owner.
- Crate gate: default to no new production crates; prefer existing std + current helper crates only. If argument/help modeling pressure suggests a new crate, record why the dependency buys real owner deletion rather than a thin shim.
- Coordination note: this bead should leave the repo closer to `yazelix-ulb2.7` (public metadata/help/completion ownership), not fight it. Prefer shapes where `yzx_control` can expose stable command/help metadata for the migrated leaves later.
close_reason: Moved yzx update* to yzx_control via yzx_cli.sh; removed public defs from yazelix.nu; added YAZELIX_TEST_PATH_PREPEND for integration tests; cargo test -p yazelix_core and run_core_canonical_tests pass.

title: Rust-own public command metadata/help/completion and delete the extern/public-registry seam
description: The next step toward any sane v16 Rust/clap yzx rewrite is not another helper-backed leaf command. It is deleting the current public command-discovery and metadata split between nushell/scripts/core/yazelix.nu, nushell/scripts/yzx/*.nu, and the generated extern bridge. This bead should move the public command metadata/help/completion ownership for the Rust-owned and mixed control-plane families onto a single Rust-owned source of truth, while deleting generated extern regeneration as the authoritative steady-state discovery path and materially shrinking or demoting the public command registry in core/yazelix.nu. The outcome should make a broader clap yzx root possible without permitting a clap-as-thin-shim design.
design: Delete first. Do not accept a design where Rust adds a top-level dispatcher but the existing Nushell command tree remains the public parser/help/completion owner. Prefer a Rust-owned command metadata model that can drive public help, completion, and migrated-family dispatch coherently, while intentionally still allowing some commands to remain Nushell-owned as internal helpers. Scope this around the command-owner seam, not around shell execution: do not move detached terminal execution, shell quoting, renderer/template ownership, or broad startup orchestration into Rust just because command metadata moved. Coordinate explicitly with yzx_control, shells/posix/yzx_cli.sh, core/yazelix.nu, and nushell_externs ownership so the steady-state source of truth is singular rather than mirrored.
acceptance_criteria: 1. There is one declared source of truth for public command metadata/help/completion for the migrated command families, and it is not the old generated extern plus export use Nushell tree split. 2. The generated extern bridge stops being the authoritative steady-state command-discovery owner; any remaining extern compatibility is clearly secondary/compat-only. 3. nushell/scripts/core/yazelix.nu materially shrinks or loses public-registry ownership for the migrated command families. 4. The bead defines or lands focused parity checks for public help/completion/exit behavior that defend the new owner boundary. 5. The resulting shape moves Yazelix materially closer to a real v16 clap rewrite and explicitly rejects clap as a thin shim over the unchanged public Nushell tree.
notes: Why this exists: docs/specs/v16_rust_cli_rewrite_evaluation.md says the clap path is only justified if we delete the public command registry, the generated extern bridge as command-discovery owner, and public Nushell wrapper parsing for migrated families. ulb2.1 and the current helper/core work got us closer, but they did not remove the metadata/help/completion seam. This bead should name the exact families moved onto the Rust metadata owner first (likely root/help/version/completion plus the already Rust-owned control-plane leaves), the exact Nushell public exports/extern generation that disappear or become compatibility-only, and the parity harness needed to defend help/completion/exit behavior during the hybrid stage. Reject any solution that keeps generated extern discovery or export use ../yzx/*.nu as the long-term public command source of truth.
close_reason: Completed: yzx_core now owns public command metadata/help/extern rendering, Nushell extern/menu surfaces consume Rust metadata instead of scope probes, core/yazelix.nu dropped the yzx update* discovery stubs, and focused Rust/Nu parity checks cover externs, root help, menu catalog, and launcher dispatch.

title: Replace stale inventory and transition specs that still point at deleted Nushell owners
description: The current inventory/spec layer is stale enough to misdirect the rewrite. Several docs still reference deleted product-side owners such as nushell/scripts/yzx/env.nu, nushell/scripts/yzx/run.nu, launch_state.nu, and the old config-migration files. subsystem_code_inventory.md is no longer a trustworthy delete-first inventory, and multiple transition specs still describe migrations that already landed or backend-era surfaces that no longer exist. This task exists to make the roadmap describe the real surviving owners before more serious Rust-deletion work proceeds.
design: Delete and rewrite aggressively. Replace the old subsystem LOC snapshot with a delete-first inventory organized around: stale/dead references, bridge layers to collapse, major Rust-port candidates, and likely Nu survivors. Rewrite spec_inventory.md accordingly. Delete or demote transition docs that are effectively complete or now misleading, and update any planning docs worth keeping so they stop citing deleted files. Do not preserve stale planning text just because it once matched a transition state.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The main inventory document reflects the actual surviving Nu owners and the real delete-first targets. 2. Spec inventory and related roadmap docs no longer present already-landed transitions or deleted files as current work. 3. The resulting docs give a credible ordered shortlist for serious Nu deletion. 4. Broken references to deleted Nu owner files are materially reduced or eliminated in the affected inventory/transition docs.
notes: Initial stale references found in subsystem_code_inventory.md, streamlining_audit_2026_04.md, yzx_env_run_rust_owner_transition.md, yzx_command_surface_backend_coupling.md, backend_capability_contract.md, v16_rust_cli_rewrite_evaluation.md, launch_bootstrap_rust_migration.md, and adjacent docs that still point at deleted owners.
close_reason: Completed

title: Delete the surviving runtime materialization owner seam end-to-end
description: The first Rust rewrite wave shrank the generated-state bridge, but the runtime materialization lifecycle still has a real Nushell owner spread across core/materialization_orchestrator.nu, utils/generated_runtime_state.nu, and utils/config_state.nu. This lane exists to make Rust the owner of the repair/regenerate/record lifecycle end-to-end instead of leaving Nushell as the long-term lifecycle coordinator.
design: Delete-first boundary: this only counts if Nushell stops owning noop vs repair classification, regenerate sequencing, and recorded materialized-state finalization for the runtime materialization lifecycle. Thin side-effect glue in Nushell is still acceptable for now where it only runs the already Rust-owned Yazi/Zellij/terminal/Helix materializers or prints progress, but a plan-only Rust helper with the same Nu lifecycle owner is not enough.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The surviving runtime materialization lifecycle owner is identified as the combined seam across materialization_orchestrator.nu, generated_runtime_state.nu, and config_state.nu rather than one stale filename. 2. The lane has a concrete deletion budget that names which Nushell owner logic must disappear or materially shrink. 3. The resulting architecture leaves Nushell as thin side-effect or rendering glue at most, not the lifecycle owner.
notes:
Landed outcome:
- runtime materialization planning, generation sequencing, recorded-state finalization, and repair now live in Rust
- generated_runtime_state.nu is deleted
- core/materialization_orchestrator.nu survives only as thin startup/doctor glue
- the private helper surface also shrank: runtime-materialization.apply and runtime-materialization.repair-evaluate were removed
close_reason: The runtime materialization owner seam was deleted end-to-end and reduced to a thin Nushell bridge

title: Define the deletion budget for the runtime materialization lifecycle owner cut
description: Audit the surviving runtime materialization lifecycle across core/materialization_orchestrator.nu, utils/generated_runtime_state.nu, and utils/config_state.nu and define the exact owner cut that would make a Rust migration count. This bead should answer what Rust must own end-to-end so the Nushell layer is no longer the lifecycle coordinator.
design: Name the exact responsibilities that still live in Nushell: repair evaluation flow, noop vs regenerate handling, progress-message ownership, sequencing of Yazi/Zellij generation, and materialized-state recording/finalization. A good outcome is one precise deletion budget plus the surviving thin Nushell responsibilities. A bad outcome is another plan/apply seam with the same Nu orchestration left intact.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The bead lists the exact Nushell lifecycle owners that must disappear or materially shrink for the lane to count. 2. It names the precise Rust touchpoints that should absorb those responsibilities. 3. It states what, if anything, should remain in Nushell as side-effect or rendering glue after the cut.
notes:
Deletion budget chosen:
- Rust had to own noop vs regenerate classification
- Rust had to own repair flow and missing-artifact recovery
- Rust had to own Yazi/Zellij generation sequencing
- Rust had to own recorded-state finalization
- Nushell could remain only as thin startup/doctor glue in core/materialization_orchestrator.nu for profile wrapping, request handoff, and final human-facing rendering
close_reason: Defined the exact runtime materialization owner cut and the surviving thin Nushell seam

title: Implement the runtime materialization owner cut and collapse the remaining Nu lifecycle seam
description: Implement the owner cut defined in yazelix-ulb2.9.1 so the runtime materialization lifecycle no longer depends on Nushell as the long-term repair/regenerate/record coordinator. The goal is to collapse or delete the remaining lifecycle owner seam around materialization_orchestrator.nu, generated_runtime_state.nu, and config_state.nu rather than just moving one more request builder.
design: Follow the deletion budget from ulb2.9.1. Keep this delete-first: do not add a new Rust planner above the same Nu sequencing logic. Successful work should either delete one of the surviving owner files outright or leave them as thin side-effect shells over a Rust-owned lifecycle. Preserve the current behavior contracts with focused materialization/repair tests.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The surviving Nushell runtime materialization owner seam is materially smaller or partly deleted. 2. Rust becomes the owner of the lifecycle decisions named in ulb2.9.1. 3. Focused tests defend noop, repair, regeneration, and state-recording behavior without reviving the old Nu owner path.
notes:
Implemented the full-owner runtime materialization cut:
- added Rust-owned runtime-materialization.materialize and runtime-materialization.repair helper commands
- moved lifecycle sequencing, recorded-state finalization, and repair decisions into rust_core/runtime_materialization.rs
- deleted nushell/scripts/utils/generated_runtime_state.nu
- collapsed core/materialization_orchestrator.nu into a thin request/render bridge for startup and doctor flows
- updated startup, doctor, and generated-config tests to defend the new owner seam
- removed the dead private helper wrappers runtime-materialization.apply and runtime-materialization.repair-evaluate from yzx_core
close_reason: Rust now owns the runtime materialization lifecycle; the remaining Nushell seam is a thin startup/doctor bridge

title: Split status/cache production ownership
description: Split the remaining broad Zellij status/cache command surface after c2jz. Separate cache IO, widget rendering, agent usage refresh, and public command adapters so status logic no longer blocks workspace extraction or keeps tests tied to old broad modules.
design: Delete-first private split. Keep Yazelix-owned cache/session paths local. Prefer moving cohesive production owners and deleting weak status/cache tests over adding adapter wrappers. Do not extract a public status package in this bead.
acceptance_criteria: Status/cache production responsibilities are split into clearer private owners; agent usage refresh, cache IO, widget rendering, and public command adapters have explicit boundaries; weak tests tied to old structure are deleted or strengthened; current integrated status-bar behavior is preserved; focused status/cache tests pass; LOC delta and rejected candidates are recorded.
notes:
Implemented status/cache private production split.

Moved cache path discovery, cache read/write, heartbeat merge, atomic JSON writes, and status-bus cache reads into zellij_commands/status/cache.rs. Moved cursor/workspace widget rendering, status-bus decode helpers, and active-tab session inspection rendering into zellij_commands/status/widgets.rs. Moved provider refresh/probe/cache freshness logic out of agent_usage.rs into zellij_commands/status/agent_usage/refresh.rs. Left public command adapters in status.rs and agent usage formatting/rendering in agent_usage.rs. Collapsed the three refresh parser structs into one shared parser.

Delete-first notes: this bead found no safe behavior deletion in status/cache production itself; the practical win is clearer ownership plus a small raw-line shrink. Status tests were not deleted here because the focused 32-test status set still defends real cache, freshness, timeout, and widget regressions; broad test slimming remains a later status-tests bead.

LOC/accounting: raw Rust budget moved from 72,781 lines / 151 files to 72,745 lines / 154 files. core_workspace_and_pane_integration moved from 16,588 lines / 27 files to 16,552 lines / 30 files. tokei Rust code moved from 66,645 to 66,607 LOC. This is organization debt with a small -36 raw-line/-38 code-LOC win, not a standalone extraction.

Verification: cargo fmt --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml --all -- --check; cargo check --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core --all-targets; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core zellij_commands::status; cargo run --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_maintainer --bin yzx_repo_validator -- validate-rust-ownership-budget; cargo run --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_maintainer --bin yzx_repo_validator -- validate-docs-experience; git diff --check.
close_reason: Completed status/cache production split; focused status tests and Rust budget/doc validators pass.

title: Guard package-time Rust tests from host-tool dependencies
description: A default Cargo integration test that shell-executed `nix eval` broke Home Manager package builds because Nix package test sandboxes do not provide the host `nix` command or the full repository root. Add a durable guard/policy so package-time Rust tests stay hermetic, while Nix-dependent validation lives in maintainer validators or explicit non-package lanes.
acceptance_criteria: A validator or test-lint catches default/package-time Rust tests that invoke host tools such as `nix`, `home-manager`, or repository paths outside the packaged Rust source; Nix-dependent checks are documented as maintainer/package gates instead; the previous `home_manager_option_metadata` failure mode is covered by a non-package validation path.
close_reason: Completed: added package-time Rust test purity validator, wired it into maintainer test runner, documented the package-vs-maintainer gate split, and verified the prior Nix-in-Cargo-test failure mode is rejected.

title: Inset magician frame farther from pane borders
description: The Kitty magician image can still visually crowd/overlap Zellij pane borders in the welcome screen even after black-background cleanup. Increase the child-owned magician frame inset/layout so the image stays comfortably inside bordered panes and update the main runtime lock to consume the published child fix.
close_reason: Completed: yazelix-screen now exposes consumer-owned magician frame edge insets while keeping standalone defaults; main Yazelix passes larger integrated-pane insets for bordered welcome/screen playback; child cargo test and nix build pass; main front-door tests, child-release validator, and runtime build pass.

title: Make magician GIF render on a black pane background
description: The attributed magician GIF renders over Yazelix's translucent pane/background art, so the black GIF rectangle is surrounded by the mountain backdrop. Force the terminal cells around the Kitty image sequence to use a black background while preserving the GIF asset and attribution.
acceptance_criteria: Magician welcome/screen playback forces the full terminal clear area to black while the GIF is displayed. The 1mposter GIF asset and attribution remain in use. Verification covers the relevant front-door Rust test and Rust check.
close_reason: Fixed. Magician Kitty playback now forces black terminal background cells around the GIF and avoids attribution ANSI reset clearing the black background. Verified with cargo fmt --check, git diff --check, cargo test -p yazelix_core front_door_render::tests::magician_graphics_uses_runtime_assets_and_child_kitty_sequence, and cargo check -p yazelix_core --all-targets.

title: Add Game of Life welcome style
design: Add a Game of Life welcome style seeded or shaped so it feels intentional rather than like a generic programmer screensaver. The motion should be short, bounded, and end in a readable final frame. The implementation may be deterministic live generation or pre-rendered playback, whichever best fits startup and width constraints.
acceptance_criteria: Selecting welcome_style = life renders the Life sequence within the startup budget, preserves narrow-layout readability, and is defended by tests for deterministic generation, frame bounds, and final-frame fit.
close_reason: Completed

title: Evaluate explicit XDG config integration mode for managed tools
description: A Home Manager/macOS user wants one native XDG/Home Manager-owned configuration to work both inside and outside Yazelix instead of maintaining separate Yazelix-managed configs. Evaluate an explicit setting or mode that connects Yazelix-managed tools such as Helix, Yazi, Zellij, terminal configs, and related plugin/runtime paths to their XDG counterparts where that is coherent.
design:
Decision, 2026-05-05:

Do not add a global auto-connect mode that silently points Yazelix at every native XDG app config. Yazelix should keep one canonical semantic config root, currently `YAZELIX_CONFIG_DIR` or XDG default `~/.config/yazelix`, with `settings.jsonc` as the durable settings source and managed sidecars in the same flat directory.

Accepted boundaries:
- Yazelix-owned paths: safe and already supported through `YAZELIX_CONFIG_DIR`, `XDG_CONFIG_HOME`, `YAZELIX_STATE_DIR`, and XDG data/state/cache defaults.
- Terminal configs: safe to reuse native terminal config through the existing explicit `terminal.config_mode = "user"` mode. It must keep failing fast when the native config file is missing.
- Zellij: partially safe as an explicit read-only/native source. Existing startup may read `~/.config/zellij/config.kdl` only when managed `~/.config/yazelix/zellij.kdl` is absent, and `yzx import zellij` remains the preferred explicit adoption path. User-owned `clear-defaults=true` mode belongs to `yazelix-583h`.
- Helix: keep Yazelix-managed by default. Native Helix config can be imported or reported by doctor, but direct native reuse must account for Yazelix editor/reveal bindings, runtime path hooks, theme/cursor expectations, package/runtime overrides, and future Lua/fork support.
- Yazi: keep Yazelix-managed by default. Native Yazi config can be imported, but direct native reuse is risky because Yazelix owns open-editor behavior, sidebar orchestration, generated plugin paths, and runtime opener wiring.
- Shell hooks: keep Yazelix-managed explicit sidecars. Native shell rc files are user-owned startup policy and must not be edited or sourced implicitly by Yazelix.

macOS conclusion:
Yazelix should not set global launchd XDG variables or move configs into macOS Application Support. The macOS-friendly path is a documented opt-in for Home Manager or nix-darwin users who want GUI/launched terminals and native tools to see Linux-style XDG paths consistently.

Follow-up beads:
- `yazelix-us42.1`: document macOS XDG environment opt-in for Home Manager/nix-darwin.
- `yazelix-us42.2`: design explicit native integration/status reporting for config UI and doctor without automatic takeover.
acceptance_criteria: Decision records which tool configs can safely reuse native XDG/Home Manager paths, which must stay Yazelix-managed, and why. The design respects the rule against automatic takeover of user-managed configs, defines an explicit opt-in setting if appropriate, covers Helix runtime/package overrides and Yazi/Zellij config ownership, and creates implementation follow-up beads for any accepted integration mode.
notes:
Issue comment 2026-05-04: macOS tester strongly prefers Linux-style XDG paths and reports using a nix-darwin launchd.user.agent with launchctl setenv for XDG_CONFIG_HOME=~/.config, XDG_CACHE_HOME=~/.cache, XDG_DATA_HOME=~/.local/share, XDG_STATE_HOME=~/.local/state, and XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=~/.local/run. This helps the issue by separating two concerns: Yazelix already resolves its own config through XDG_CONFIG_HOME or ~/.config/yazelix, but native tools launched inside or around Yazelix may still choose macOS Application Support paths unless the user's login/launchd environment exports XDG_* consistently. Evaluate an explicit/documented Home Manager or nix-darwin helper for propagating XDG_* on macOS, but do not silently set global launchd environment or take ownership of existing native configs as a startup side effect.
Prime-beads 2026-05-05: after the settings.jsonc consolidation, the canonical semantic settings source remains ~/.config/yazelix/settings.jsonc. Any future XDG-native integration should be an explicit sidecar/import/export mode that feeds the current settings/materialization pipeline; it must not bypass settings.jsonc or silently adopt external app configs.

title: Document macOS XDG environment opt-in
description: Document the supported macOS opt-in for exporting Linux-style XDG paths through Home Manager or nix-darwin launchd so native tools launched outside Yazelix and tools launched by Yazelix agree on ~/.config, ~/.cache, ~/.local/share, ~/.local/state, and runtime-dir expectations.
acceptance_criteria: Docs show a concise Home Manager or nix-darwin snippet, explain that Yazelix itself already respects XDG/YAZELIX_CONFIG_DIR, and explicitly say Yazelix does not silently set global launchd variables or relocate native app configs.

title: Design explicit native config integration status
description:
## Decision
Pending. Design the explicit native-config status model for Helix, Yazi, Zellij, terminals, and shell hooks.

## Rationale
Yazelix must explain managed/imported/native/read-only states without silently taking ownership of user-managed files. The config UI and doctor need the same vocabulary before implementation.

## Alternatives Considered
Keep only Yazelix-managed configs; add explicit import/export; support native read-only discovery; or expose a full bidirectional native integration mode.

## Existing Context
Design the explicit status surface for native config integration across Helix, Yazi, Zellij, terminals, and shell hooks. The result should let the config UI and doctor explain whether each tool is managed, imported, read-only native, or user-owned native without silently adopting user-managed files.
acceptance_criteria: Decision defines per-tool states, user-facing labels, failure modes for missing native files, config UI display rules, and doctor diagnostics. It must preserve settings.jsonc as the canonical semantic settings source and keep import/export as explicit user actions.
close_reason: Completed: added docs/contracts/native_config_integration_status.md with per-tool managed/imported/native/read-only/generated states, config UI display rules, doctor diagnostic levels, and explicit import boundaries; created implementation follow-up yazelix-onak.

title: Fix Alt y failing to reopen sidebar after opening an extra pane in a fresh tab
description: After opening a fresh tab where Yazi starts in the sidebar, opening one extra pane, closing the sidebar with Alt y, and then pressing Alt y again, the sidebar does not reopen. The pane-frame regression is fixed separately; this bead tracks the narrower reopen failure only.
close_reason: The override-layout path now reads the generated runtime layouts directly; this removed the stale widget injection and also fixed the sidebar reopen no-op regression.

title: Continue centralizing Yazelix declarative config semantics onto the canonical contract
description: Yazelix already has a canonical machine-readable config contract in config_metadata/main_config_contract.toml, but some real consumers still duplicate semantics across the default template, Home Manager, parser-adjacent logic, schema/diagnostics-adjacent logic, and rebuild-sensitive ownership sites. Treat this as a delete-first centralization bead: continue moving real consumers onto the canonical contract, delete duplicated semantic ownership that is no longer justified, and make the remaining duplication explicit where it must stay. Build on the already-completed 0oj1 centralization work instead of reopening the problem from scratch.
design: Treat this as a delete-first follow-up to the already completed 0oj1 centralization work, not as a fresh architecture spike. The canonical in-repo TOML contract already exists and several consumers already moved onto it; the remaining job is to identify which real semantic ownership sites still duplicate that contract, migrate at least one meaningful remaining consumer, and delete semantic duplication that is no longer justified. Keep the protocol question pragmatic: only adopt an external protocol if it clearly beats the current in-repo TOML contract for Yazelix's actual consumers and maintenance burden. Related open beads in this lane include b16x.1 for doctor freshness reporting and 53zx.* for staged migration semantics across managed surfaces; this bead should complement those, not reopen their contracts.
acceptance_criteria: The bead records a clear statement of which config-semantic ownership sites are still duplicated after the completed 0oj1 work. At least one meaningful remaining consumer is migrated further onto the canonical contract, reducing real semantic duplication instead of adding abstraction. The design makes an explicit keep-vs-adopt decision on the in-repo TOML contract versus any external protocol, with concrete reasoning tied to Yazelix's real consumers. The resulting repo shape is simpler than before: fewer duplicated semantic ownership sites, not more generation or indirection.
notes: Imported from GitHub issue #516. This should be treated as an umbrella cleanup/centralization bead for the remaining live work after the closed 0oj1.* sequence, not as evidence that the earlier centralization work failed. Likely remaining hotspots to inspect first: home_manager/module.nix, yazelix_default.toml, and any residual hand-maintained semantic lists that still sit outside config_metadata/main_config_contract.toml.
close_reason: Completed: main-config semantics are now materially more centralized on config_metadata/main_config_contract.toml. home_manager/module.nix already derives defaults/types from the contract, and config_parser.nu now iterates the contract instead of hand-upserting every field. Remaining duplication is intentionally limited to human-facing prose and a few local schema allowances rather than another maintained semantic field table.

title: Generate the TOML template and Home Manager defaults from the canonical config contract
