description: Finish the contract-centralization work by making the canonical machine-readable config contract drive more of the emitted/default surfaces directly. Focus on the remaining duplication across yazelix_default.toml, Home Manager defaults, parser-facing metadata, and similar drift-prone surfaces.
design: The repo now has a real canonical config contract, but too much maintained behavior is still duplicated around it. Treat the contract as the semantic source of truth and push more downstream surfaces to derive from it mechanically. Prefer deleting handwritten parallel default tables over adding more validation glue. Keep human-facing prose and long comments handwritten only where that actually adds value; the maintained semantics should stop living in several files at once.
acceptance_criteria: 1. At least the shipped main config template defaults and Home Manager defaults are generated from, or mechanically derived from, the canonical config contract. 2. Drift-prone duplicated default tables are removed or reduced to thin adapters. 3. Remaining handwritten config surfaces are explicitly justified as prose/presentation rather than semantic source of truth. 4. Follow-up work becomes narrower because the contract now owns more of the maintained semantics.
close_reason: Completed: Home Manager defaults and generated config surfaces now derive from the canonical config contract, the pack catalog default table is deleted from the module, and the shipped main template remains mechanically defended by the config-surface contract validator.

title: Fix Ghostty shader generation and packaging
description: Ghostty shader assets and generated shader config are still fragile in packaged/runtime installs. Recent failures showed that shader generation, copying, or cleanup can leave Ghostty without the expected shader files even when Yazelix otherwise launches.
design: Treat this as a real generated-asset contract bug. Identify which shader artifacts Ghostty actually needs at runtime, where Yazelix generates or copies them, and why installed-runtime launches can end up with missing, unreadable, or stale shader trees. Fix the asset pipeline at the source instead of relying on local cleanup or manual re-generation.
acceptance_criteria: Installed and clone-based Yazelix launches both materialize the expected Ghostty shader tree. Repeated config generation does not leave broken or empty shader directories. The maintained Ghostty config points only at shader assets that actually exist after generation/install.
notes: Completed on v15. The shader pipeline already generated referenced shader files for clone-based runs, but the packaged-runtime half of the contract was under-defended. Added installed-runtime validation to validate_flake_install.nu: it now checks the packaged runtime contains the Ghostty shader builder, trail variants, and vendored cursor-effect templates; then it materializes Ghostty terminal config through the installed yzx runtime and verifies every generated custom-shader reference exists, including generated_effects output. Verification: validate_syntax, git diff --check, targeted validate_flake_install verify against an installed temp home, and full validate_flake_install all after fixing the probe to use double-quoted Nushell module paths.
close_reason: Completed packaged-runtime Ghostty shader validation and verified installed-runtime materialization.

title: Repo pass after the Zellij major update for simplifications and new opportunities
description: After the Zellij major update lands, do a repository-wide pass looking for simplifications, workaround removals, and new feature opportunities made practical by the newer Zellij surface.
notes:
Audit snapshot after the Zellij 0.44 upgrade:

Ranked follow-up list:
1. `yazelix-tuj` — replace swap-layout stepping/delay logic with an `override-layout` transition model. This is the clearest architectural simplification because the pane orchestrator still hardcodes layout families and depends on `next_swap_layout()` / `previous_swap_layout()` timing.
2. `yazelix-anc` — evaluate replacing the custom popup-runner plugin with Zellij 0.44 CLI pane control (`list-panes --json`, pane IDs from creation commands, targeted actions). This could remove one Rust/WASM plugin if active-tab popup semantics remain reliable without plugin state.
3. `yazelix-6zx` — document and validate modern Zellij sharing flows for Yazelix, now that the built-in share UI and read-only tokens are first-class in the newer release.

No smaller shipped simplification looked clearly worth landing immediately without broadening scope or adding noisy docs that Zellij already covers in-session.
close_reason: Completed

title: Return popup and menu wrappers to Nushell
notes: User requested restoring popup/menu launch trampolines from POSIX sh back to Nushell for consistency/readability. No new crates; in-house path/launcher changes only. Scope: restore nushell/scripts/zellij_wrappers/yzx_popup_program.nu and yzx_menu_popup.nu, point pane orchestrator transient contract back to Nu wrappers, remove POSIX trampolines if unused, update budget/docs, rebuild/sync pane orchestrator wasm, and validate popup/menu transient behavior contract.
close_reason: Restored popup/menu wrappers to Nushell, updated the pane-orchestrator transient contract to the Nu wrapper paths, deleted the POSIX trampolines, ratcheted the canonical Nu budget/docs to 1880 LOC / 14 files, rebuilt/synced the pane orchestrator wasm, and revalidated transient tests plus spec/budget/syntax guards.

title: Implement a shared runtime contract checker and reuse it across launch and doctor
description: Implement a shared checker for the runtime dependency contract instead of scattering availability probes and stale assumptions across launch, doctor, and one-off helpers. The checker should be lightweight enough for normal preflight use, classify failures clearly, and expose the same underlying reasoning to yzx doctor. Avoid turning launch into a giant slow audit; the value is one authoritative dependency story with clear required/optional classifications and actionable errors.
close_reason: Duplicate of yazelix-hv2m created during parallel bead creation.

title: Define Zellij keybinding ownership modes
description: A tester reported that Yazelix merges its default Zellij keybindings with user keybindings, so replacing the unlock key with Ctrl-` still leaves Yazelix's default Ctrl-g active. This conflicts with Emacs-style workflows where Ctrl-g is cancel. Evaluate an explicit ownership mode for Zellij keybindings so users can choose merge/current behavior or a stricter user-owned/minimal mode.
design:
Decision: support three conceptual Zellij keybinding ownership modes, implemented in two phases.

1. default_merge (current default)
Yazelix keeps merging the user managed zellij.kdl keybinds block with Yazelix generated override keybinds. This remains the default because it preserves the workspace UX: popup/menu, sidebar focus/toggle, smart reveal, layout family switching, Alt tab navigation, and Yazelix conflict-avoidance remaps. Users can add native Zellij keybindings here, but should not expect this mode to fully suppress Yazelix defaults.

2. user_owned_native (advanced escape hatch)
If the managed Zellij config declares keybinds clear-defaults=true, Yazelix should preserve that header and skip appending configs/zellij/yazelix_overrides.kdl. This lets advanced users reuse one native Zellij keymap inside and outside Yazelix. It is intentionally sharp: the user owns every Zellij binding, including Zellij unlock and any desired Yazelix integration calls. Yazelix must document that sidebar/popup/layout convenience bindings disappear unless recreated by the user with MessagePlugin calls.

3. yazelix_action_map (future friendly mode)
This is not the same as native clear-defaults. It should be designed in yazelix-hg3a as a semantic action remap layer over Yazelix-owned actions, with possible profiles such as default, emacs-friendly, or minimal. It should generate the right Zellij/Yazi/Helix-facing bindings for common Yazelix actions without requiring users to understand generated KDL or pane-orchestrator pipe payloads.

Runtime-required vs convenience bindings:
There are no Zellij keybindings required for Yazelix to launch. The shipped override keybinds are product/convenience policy and conflict-avoidance policy. Therefore user_owned_native can safely skip them if the user opts into clear-defaults, as long as docs make the tradeoff explicit.

Accepted implementation boundary:
- Implement yazelix-583h exactly as the first phase: preserve keybinds clear-defaults=true, skip Yazelix override keybind injection, and keep ordinary default_merge behavior unchanged.
- Do not add a settings.jsonc knob for this first phase; the native KDL header is the right advanced signal.
- Do not block clear-defaults support on the full action-map design.
- Keep yazelix-hg3a responsible for friendly action remapping, profiles, Helix command-mode remap resilience, and any future keybinding diagnostics/context view.

Docs requirement for 583h:
Document clear-defaults as an advanced mode for users who want one native Zellij keymap. Show at least one example recreating a Yazelix integration binding via MessagePlugin. Warn that default Yazelix convenience bindings are not appended in that mode.

Source note: the concrete user patch on GitHub issue 578 is directionally correct and should be used as the implementation starting point, with tests around header preservation, override suppression, and ordinary merge behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Decision defines supported Zellij keybinding ownership modes and their exact semantics. It identifies which bindings are runtime-required versus convenience defaults, covers the Ctrl-g unlock conflict, and creates implementation follow-ups if Yazelix should support replace/minimal/user-owned keybinding generation without breaking pane-orchestrator command access.
notes: Issue 578 comment 2026-05-04: tester provided a concrete patch for honoring user-owned Zellij keybinds via `keybinds clear-defaults=true`. The current materializer extracts keybinds body lines but drops the header attribute and still appends Yazelix override keybinds, so a user cannot make one native Zellij keymap work both standalone and inside Yazelix. The patch shape is plausible as an advanced native passthrough mode: preserve `keybinds clear-defaults=true`, skip Yazelix default override keybind injection, and leave responsibility for sidebar/popup/workspace bindings with the user. This should be treated separately from the higher-level Yazelix action-keymap design: clear-defaults is an escape hatch/user-owned mode, not a friendly remap surface. If implemented, docs must warn that Yazelix convenience bindings disappear unless recreated by the user.
close_reason: Decision completed: keep default merge behavior, accept keybinds clear-defaults=true as an advanced user-owned native mode via yazelix-583h, and leave friendly semantic action remapping to yazelix-hg3a.

title: investigate FlakeHub Cache and magic-nix-cache
description: Investigate whether FlakeHub Cache or magic-nix-cache would materially benefit Yazelix CI or installs.
close_reason: Not planned after investigation: Yazelix already uses magic-nix-cache in CI, and FlakeHub Cache is not justified right now.

title: Cold one-off package launch fails before generated Zellij layout materialization
description:
Why this issue exists and what needs to be done:
GitHub issue #558 reports that a cold first-run one-off launch via `nix run github:luccahuguet/yazelix#yazelix -- launch` can create `yazelix.toml`, generate terminal configs, and then fail with a missing generated Zellij layout such as `~/.local/share/yazelix/configs/zellij/layouts/yzx_side.kdl`.

Investigation summary:
- `launch_yazelix.nu` prepares terminal configs and opens a new terminal window
- `start_yazelix.nu` resolves the expected generated layout path and requires that layout before launching the inner startup flow
- the code that actually materializes managed Yazi/Zellij runtime state lives later in `start_yazelix_inner.nu` via `regenerate_runtime_configs`
- on a true first run, startup can therefore demand a generated layout before the normal startup path has had any chance to generate it

Why it matters:
This breaks the documented package-first one-off use path and makes cold first-run behavior inconsistent with the intended product surface.

Acceptance criteria:
- Cold one-off package launch no longer fails just because the generated Zellij layout has not been materialized yet
- The startup ordering and generated-state contract are internally consistent
- The fix preserves clear failure behavior for genuinely broken generated-state cases after the normal materialization path has had a chance to run
close_reason: Completed

title: Add cold package-surface launch smoke coverage for first-run generated state
description:
Why this issue exists and what needs to be done:
The current maintainer validation surface checks the packaged runtime wrapper, `yzx run`, and terminal-config generation, but it does not appear to run a cold first-run `nix run .#yazelix -- launch` style smoke test that exercises generated-state creation all the way through startup. That left the issue-558 ordering bug undetected.

Investigation summary:
- `validate_flake_install.nu` validates package/runtime probes and terminal-config generation
- existing tests defend the missing-layout preflight behavior itself
- there is not yet a focused cold package-surface launch smoke that proves first-run launch can cross the generated-state seam successfully

Acceptance criteria:
- Maintainer validation includes a focused cold-start package-surface launch probe or equivalent high-signal regression coverage
- The coverage defends the real first-run generated-state contract instead of only probing wrapper existence or command discovery
- The coverage would fail on the issue-558 regression shape
close_reason: Completed

title: Align generated-layout recovery guidance with actual doctor repair behavior
description:
Why this issue exists and what needs to be done:
The missing generated-layout error path currently points users toward `yzx doctor`, but the doctor path does not currently repair missing generated runtime artifacts as part of `yzx doctor --fix`. That makes the recovery guidance weaker than it should be for the issue-558 failure shape.

Investigation summary:
- startup and runtime preflight report missing generated layouts as generated-state problems
- the error guidance points users toward `yzx doctor`
- `generated_runtime_state.nu` already has `repair_generated_runtime_state`, including missing-artifact repair behavior
- `doctor.nu` does not currently invoke that repair path during `yzx doctor --fix`

Acceptance criteria:
- Recovery messaging for missing generated-layout artifacts matches the real supported repair surface
- If `yzx doctor --fix` is intended to repair missing generated runtime artifacts, it actually does so
- Otherwise, the guidance is narrowed so users are not pointed at a repair path that does not exist
close_reason: Completed

title: Make built-in layout families machine-readable
description:
Why this issue exists and what needs to be done

Yazelix layout-family behavior is currently real product logic, but most of that contract is still implicit across KDL files, Rust plugin code, and docs. That makes sidebar-family evolution easy to break and hard to validate mechanically.

Introduce one maintained machine-readable contract for the built-in layout families and use it to validate the tracked KDL files and the pane-orchestrator expectations, so layout-family work stops depending on scattered string knowledge.
close_reason: Added machine-readable built-in Zellij layout family contract

title: Make validate_default_test_budget finish reliably without live Zellij hang paths
description: `nu nushell/scripts/dev/validate_default_test_budget.nu` can hang instead of completing its default-suite budget check. In the observed failure, the validator spawned `test_yzx_commands.nu`, which in turn left a live `zellij action pipe --plugin yazelix_pane_orchestrator` subprocess running, so the budget validator never returned a pass/fail result. This makes the validator unreliable as a maintainer/CI signal: instead of reporting a budget violation or success, it can block indefinitely on a leaked interactive path.
design: Keep this delete-first. Do not just add bigger timeouts around the current validator. First identify whether the validator truly needs to execute the full live path it currently reaches, or whether one default-suite test is accidentally leaking a Zellij/plugin subprocess during profiling. Prefer deleting the interactive dependency from the budget-validation path, or constraining the suite/profile runner so it terminates deterministically even when Zellij-related tests are present. The end state should be a budget validator that either passes or fails clearly; it should not hang waiting on live session plumbing.
acceptance_criteria: 1. `validate_default_test_budget.nu` completes deterministically with a pass/fail result instead of hanging indefinitely. 2. The validator no longer leaves or waits on stray live Zellij `action pipe` or equivalent plugin subprocesses. 3. If the default suite exceeds the allowed budget, the validator fails with a clear budget message rather than stalling. 4. A focused regression or harness-level check defends the specific hang path so it does not silently return via a larger timeout alone.
close_reason: Completed: validate_default_test_budget now profiles the default suite in-process, the default-lane doctor and Yazi regressions no longer contact live pane-orchestrator debug pipes, and the validator now returns a deterministic pass/fail result. Current outcome is a clear budget failure at about 82.37s rather than a hang.

title: Fix installed-runtime contract expecting deleted edit/import Nu modules
notes: CI integration job for commit 62f68ba8 fails installed-runtime contract smoke because packaged runtime validation still expects nushell/scripts/yzx/edit.nu and import.nu in built outputs, but those modules are no longer present. Need to fix the contract/packaging expectation at the owning seam, rerun the failing validator, then push the repair.
close_reason: Completed: removed stale installed-runtime validator expectations for deleted yzx edit/import Nu modules and revalidated the installed-runtime contract smoke locally.

title: Fix doctor desktop launcher validation for stable wrapper installs
description:
`yzx desktop install` correctly prefers the stable installed wrapper (`~/.local/bin/yzx`) when it exists, but `yzx doctor` still validates manual desktop entries against the runtime-pinned `bin/yzx` path. After a fresh local installer run, this creates a false warning: "Yazelix desktop entry does not use the expected launcher path."

Fix the doctor desktop-entry expected-launcher calculation so it uses the same launcher-resolution policy as `yzx desktop install` for manual installs, while preserving Home Manager profile launcher validation.
acceptance_criteria:
1. A manual install with `~/.local/bin/yzx` and a desktop Exec pointing at that wrapper is accepted by `yzx doctor`.
2. Stale runtime-pinned or old store Exec paths are still reported as stale when they do not match the resolved launcher policy.
3. Home Manager profile desktop validation remains unchanged.
close_reason: Completed: doctor now validates manual desktop entries against the same stable launcher resolver used by yzx desktop install, with regression coverage.

title: Add alien sea slug particle style to yzx screen
description: Add an original terminal-native yzx screen style inspired by the alien sea slug / feather-like parametric particle animation from the user-provided reference image. The style should live in the external yazelix-screen crate first and be consumed by Yazelix through the normal pinned child-repo path.
design: Implement one or more terminal-native yzx screen styles in the external yazelix-screen crate first, then consume them through Yazelix via the normal pinned child-repo path. Treat the three user-provided formulas as approved open-source project input; do not make implementation depend on reading source URLs, license pages, or extra attribution metadata. Credit the original X handle `@yuruyurau` in style metadata/docs and, if practical, in the source comment near the ported formula. Preserve the visual family: black background, bright organic particle trails, ribbed sea-slug/alien-feather silhouettes, deterministic time evolution, finite numeric behavior, and graceful resize behavior through the existing terminal-safe ScreenFrame pipeline. Start with an explicit style such as alien_slug or sea_slug; only include it in random after manual visual validation in narrow and full-screen terminal sizes.
acceptance_criteria: The alien sea slug particle style is implemented in yazelix-screen, exposed through yzs, and wired into yzx screen after the main repo dependency pin is updated. The implementation uses the provided formula family as approved input and credits X `@yuruyurau` in style metadata/docs. The style renders nonblank deterministic frames, advances over time, resizes without panics or malformed output, and remains readable in normal and narrow terminal sizes. Docs/style metadata list the new style. Verification includes yazelix-screen cargo tests, a render/play smoke check, main repo cargo checks/tests for screen style routing, and a recorded decision on whether the style enters random.
notes:
Reference formula family from user, approved as project input:

Primary variant:

```javascript
a=(x,y,d=mag(k=4*cos(x/21),e=y/8-20))=>circle((q=3*sin(k*2)+.3/k+sin(y/19)*k*(9+2*sin(e*14-d*3+t*2)))+50*cos(c=d-t)+200,q*sin(c)+d*39-475,k*k>15?2:1)
t=0,draw=$=>{t||createCanvas(w=400,w);background(9).noStroke().fill(w,116);for(t+=PI/240,i=1e4;i--;)a(i,i/235)}
```

Variant 2:

```javascript
a=(m,d=mag(k=9*cos(i/81),e=i/765-13)/4)=>point((q=79-2*sin(k*3)+sin(k*k<19?t*3+d*4:d/2+4)/2*k*(9+5*sin(d*d-e/6-t+m)))*sin(c=d*d/9-t/16+m)+200,(q+50)*cos(c)+200)
t=0,draw=$=>{t||createCanvas(w=400,w);background(9).stroke(w,96);for(t+=PI/45,i=2e4;i--;)a(i%2*9)}
```

Variant 3:

```javascript
a=(m,d=mag(k=9*cos(i/61),e=i/652-13)**2/89+1)=>point((q=79-e/2*sin(k)+k/d*(6+5*sin(sin(d*d+e/9-t+m))))*sin(c=d/1.9+cos(t-d*3+m)/11-t/16+m)+200,(q+40)*cos(c)+200)
t=0,draw=$=>{t||createCanvas(w=400,w);background(9).stroke(w,96);for(t+=PI/45,i=2e4;i--;)a(i%2*3)}
```

Attribution requirement: credit X handle `@yuruyurau` / display name `ア`. User-provided post date: May 5. The maintainer explicitly said not to spend implementation time reading source URLs, license pages, or extra attribution metadata. Treat the formulas above as approved open-source project input and port them directly or faithfully into Rust through the yazelix-screen ScreenFrame pipeline.

Implementation guidance: compare all three variants during implementation. Prefer one canonical `alien_slug` style if they read as one visual family in terminal rendering; use named variants only if the terminal output is visually distinct enough to justify the extra surface. Preserve deterministic time evolution, terminal-safe density/glyph rendering, scaling/centering from the original 400x400 coordinate space, and finite numeric behavior. Guard the primary variant .3/k term around k near zero, and guard any division by d in the third variant.

title: Add first-party Zellij plugin wasm toolchain validator
description:
The macOS Home Manager failure in GitHub issue 604 repeated across both first-party Zellij plugin child packages: pane-orchestrator first, then yzpp. The main child-release validator currently checks that child input revisions are published and Cargo git hashes match, but it does not instantiate the locked child wasm package derivations or verify that their build phases use the combined Fenix Cargo/Rustc with wasm32-wasip1 rust-std.

Acceptance criteria:
- `yzx_repo_validator validate-child-release-transaction` or an adjacent maintainer validator covers every first-party Zellij plugin wasm child package consumed by the main runtime: `yazelix_zellij_pane_orchestrator` and `yazelix_zellij_popup`.
- The validator instantiates `aarch64-darwin` derivation metadata without building it and fails if the child package no longer exports explicit `CARGO` and `RUSTC`, checks `rustc --print target-libdir --target wasm32-wasip1`, or builds for `--target wasm32-wasip1`.
- The check is data-driven enough that adding another first-party Zellij plugin wasm package requires one contract entry, not copied validator logic.
- Focused Rust tests cover stale/missing toolchain markers in derivation metadata.
- The first-party Zellij plugin wasm ownership contract documents this release gate.
- Verification includes the focused maintainer Rust tests and a real validator run.
close_reason: Implemented in validate-child-release-transaction. The validator now instantiates the aarch64-darwin derivation metadata for yazelix_zellij_pane_orchestrator and yazelix_zellij_popup, then fails if buildPhase loses explicit CARGO/RUSTC exports, the wasm32-wasip1 target-libdir preflight, the explicit "$CARGO" build, or --target wasm32-wasip1. Documented the gate in the first-party Zellij plugin wasm ownership contract and refreshed CLI docs. Verification: cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_maintainer repo_child_release; cargo run --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_maintainer --bin yzx_repo_validator -- validate-child-release-transaction; validate-contracts; validate-rust-test-traceability.

title: Delete the surviving Yazi and Zellij compatibility wrappers
description: After the full-owner Rust materialization cuts, `nushell/scripts/setup/yazi_config_merger.nu` and `nushell/scripts/setup/zellij_config_merger.nu` survive mainly as compatibility wrappers. Track deleting those last wrapper owners instead of letting them become permanent architecture.
design: Keep this delete-first. Do not add a fresh wrapper or second owner to preserve the old command surface. Move remaining callers onto the direct Rust-owned materialization entrypoints or a thinner already-owned surface, then delete the compatibility files. Success is measured by deleted wrappers and reduced Nu ownership, not by preserving every historical helper path unchanged.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The Yazi wrapper child names the remaining callers of `setup/yazi_config_merger.nu` and deletes the file or records an explicit blocker if one truly remains. 2. The Zellij wrapper child does the same for `setup/zellij_config_merger.nu`. 3. This epic closes only when the surviving compatibility wrappers are gone or their last blocker is documented precisely instead of hidden behind generic migration language.
close_reason: all steps complete

title: Delete the surviving Yazi compatibility wrapper
description: The real Nushell Yazi owner family is already gone. `nushell/scripts/setup/yazi_config_merger.nu` now survives mainly as a compatibility wrapper around the Rust-owned Yazi materialization path. Delete it by moving any remaining callers to the direct Rust-owned surface and removing the file.
design: Start by inventorying every surviving call site of `setup/yazi_config_merger.nu` in product code, tests, packaging, and maintainer tooling. Delete or reroute those callers to the direct Rust-owned Yazi materialization entrypoint. Do not preserve the wrapper by adding another compatibility layer. If one caller cannot move yet, name it explicitly and keep the bead blocked on that exact owner instead of hand-waving about compatibility.
acceptance_criteria: 1. All remaining callers of `setup/yazi_config_merger.nu` are identified. 2. The direct callers move to the Rust-owned Yazi materialization surface or to an already-owned thinner seam. 3. `nushell/scripts/setup/yazi_config_merger.nu` is deleted, and docs/tests no longer describe it as a live owner.
notes: Deleted `nushell/scripts/setup/yazi_config_merger.nu` after rerouting the remaining test-side callers to a new dev-only direct Rust materialization helper. There is no surviving product-side Yazi wrapper now; user docs were rewritten to point at restart/doctor flows instead of internal setup scripts.

Delete it by moving any remaining callers to the direct Rust-owned surface and removing the file.

Move those callers to the Rust-owned Zellij materialization entrypoint or another already-owned thinner seam. Do not keep a wrapper around the wrapper. The direct callers move to the Rust-owned Zellij materialization surface or to an already-owned thinner seam. 3. `nushell/scripts/setup/zellij_config_merger.nu` is deleted, and docs/tests no longer describe it as a live owner.

title: Add optional monthly OpenCode Go usage window
description: Add monthly OpenCode Go token/quota support to the opencode_go_usage status widget while keeping the default compact.
acceptance_criteria: opencode_go_usage can render a monthly mo window when enabled; default config remains compact; config/Home Manager/docs contract expose the period list; SQLite reader computes 30-day local tokens and monthly quota percent using the OpenCode Go monthly limit; tests cover render, SQLite read, and shared cache behavior.
notes:
Scope: add OpenCode Go monthly as a first-class optional window for the existing go widget. Keep default periods at 5h/week to avoid bar bloat. Use the current in-house SQLite reader for local 30-day token/cost totals and $60 monthly quota estimate; exact dashboard quota remains separate future work because official docs expose console usage but no stable quota API, and existing community tooling scrapes the web dashboard.
Implemented monthly OpenCode Go support. Added zellij.opencode_go_usage_periods with default ["5h", "week"] and allowed values 5h/week/month, plus Home Manager and docs. The OpenCode Go SQLite reader now computes 30-day local token/cost totals and stores monthly_tokens/monthly_remaining_percent in the shared cache using the $60 monthly limit. Rendering uses configurable window periods with compact labels 5h, wk, mo; default remains 5h/week to avoid bar bloat.
Verification: yzx dev rust fmt --check; yzx dev rust check; yzx dev rust test zellij_commands; yzx_repo_validator validate-config-surface-contract; yzx_repo_validator validate-docs-experience; git diff --check. Manual local check with zellij_opencode_go_usage_periods=[5h,week,month] rendered the available real DB windows as [go wk|202M|59% mo|443M|0%].
close_reason: Completed: OpenCode Go usage now supports optional monthly mo token/quota windows.

title: Fix skip_welcome_screen=false being ignored during interactive launch
description: The core.skip_welcome_screen setting is currently being ignored for interactive Yazelix launch flows. Users with skip_welcome_screen = false still do not see the welcome UI.
design: Trace so far: the interactive launch path still calls show_welcome from nushell/scripts/core/start_yazelix_inner.nu, so the setting is not being ignored because the UI code was removed. The likely regression is environment leakage: launch wrappers pass --skip-welcome into the devenv/bootstrap layer, which sets YAZELIX_SKIP_WELCOME=true, and show_welcome treats that as test_mode. The most suspicious paths are nushell/scripts/core/start_yazelix.nu, nushell/scripts/utils/environment_bootstrap.nu, and nushell/scripts/yzx/launch.nu. The likely fix is to keep bootstrap welcome suppression for environment setup while clearing or scoping YAZELIX_SKIP_WELCOME before the real interactive inner session starts.
acceptance_criteria: With core.skip_welcome_screen = false, interactive Yazelix launch shows the welcome screen again. With core.skip_welcome_screen = true, the welcome screen is skipped and the info is logged as before. The fix must not regress env-only, one-shot command, or sweep/test flows that intentionally use YAZELIX_SKIP_WELCOME.
close_reason: Cannot reproduce

Decide whether GitHub Actions, issue templates, or another automation path is the right place to assign newly opened issues to the maintainer, and verify the permission model before committing to an approach. The solution should be reliable without creating noisy side effects on transferred, reopened, or bot-created issues unless that behavior is explicitly desired.

Include validation for the chosen workflow path, with logs or dry-run evidence showing the assignment behavior for representative issue-opening scenarios.

title: Delete duplicate Nushell config diagnostics by consuming Rust config.normalize output
description: Yazelix already has a Rust-owned config.normalize core, but startup and doctor still carry duplicate Nushell-side config diagnostic reasoning. This task should make the Rust helper output the canonical machine-readable config findings consumed by the live parser, startup, and doctor paths so Nushell stops re-deriving the same schema and validation decisions.

The Rust helper should remain the typed owner of structured config diagnostics, while Nushell becomes a renderer and wrapper. Target the surviving logic around config_diagnostics.nu, config_schema.nu, config_parser.nu, and doctor consumers rather than broadening into migration machinery or public CLI changes.
acceptance_criteria: Packaged and source-checkout config parsing, startup diagnostics, and doctor consume one canonical Rust-backed config diagnostics source. Nushell keeps presentation, but no longer independently computes the same schema and validation findings in parallel.
notes: Delete-first rule: this bead is not done if Rust adds a second diagnostics path beside the existing Nushell one. By close, startup and doctor should consume one canonical structured diagnostics source or the bead should file explicit follow-up work for each surviving duplicate branch. Keep j498 blocked on this cleanup so migration evaluation does not restart before diagnostics duplication is gone.

description: Ghostty dead keys stopped working again in the Yazelix-provided Ghostty path. Restore dead-key composition without changing the default terminal contract or relying on host-specific local workarounds.

Reproduce the dead-key failure in the Yazelix-provided Ghostty integration, identify whether the breakage comes from wrapper/env handling, config generation, input-method propagation, or another launch-layer change, and restore dead-key composition without falling back to a different terminal or shipping a local-machine workaround.

The fix lives in Yazelix itself, preserves Ghostty as the intended default experience, and is verified through the real Ghostty launch path rather than a substitute terminal. Evaluate whether the command definitions for workspace/cursor/usage/CPU/RAM/version should be represented as typed adapter data instead of scattered raw KDL strings.
design: Start from configs/zellij/layouts/fragments/zjstatus_tab_template.kdl, zellij_materialization.rs, and the yazelix_bar renderer API. Keep actual runtime helper paths and status-cache-widget commands in main Yazelix, but avoid duplicated KDL snippets where typed render data can own command id, command argv, format, and interval. Prefer deletion of one-off template placeholders over adding abstraction.
acceptance_criteria: A direct simplification or rejection note states whether typed command-definition rendering removes main-repo KDL/materialization duplication. If implemented, generated layouts still use cache-widget commands, no status-bus-workspace path returns, and zellij_materialization tests cover the surviving template contract.
notes: Implemented. The zjstatus KDL fragment now delegates integrated command definitions to __YAZELIX_ZJSTATUS_COMMAND_DEFINITIONS__; zellij_materialization.rs renders workspace, cursor, Claude, Codex, OpenCode Go, CPU, RAM, and version command definitions from typed IntegratedZjstatusCommandWidget data. Runtime helper paths and cache-widget commands remain in main Yazelix. title: Spec persistent-session behavior across multiple Yazelix windows
description: Specify how multiple windows behave when `zellij.persistent_sessions = true` and Yazelix reuses one named Zellij session.
design:
Persistent sessions are not just the default behavior with more reuse. They are a different session model: multiple windows become clients of one named logical Zellij session.

This task should define the consequences of that model explicitly:
- once the named session exists, later windows reattach instead of creating fresh live sessions
- `--path` or other fresh-bootstrap intent cannot silently override the already-running session root
- last-client behavior may intentionally differ from non-persistent mode because the point of persistence is to keep the session alive
- restart/update semantics may be session-scoped rather than client-scoped

The goal is to make persistent-session surprises legible and intentional instead of accidental side effects of current Zellij attach behavior.
acceptance_criteria:
1. The docs state that persistent mode means multiple windows attach to one named logical session rather than creating independent sessions.
2. The docs state what happens when a user provides `--path` or similar fresh-start intent after the named persistent session already exists.
3. The docs state whether the last-client lifecycle for persistent sessions intentionally differs from the default non-persistent mode.
4. The docs state that persistent-session restart/update behavior is evaluated at the logical-session scope, not by pretending each attached window is independent.
5. The contract is specific enough that later lifecycle bugs can distinguish an intentional persistent detached session from a default-mode leak.
notes:
This task stays explicit about where persistent behavior intentionally diverges from the default non-persistent contract instead of restating the same rules twice.

Spec delivered:
- `docs/specs/persistent_window_session_contract.md`

Current defense:
- `nu nushell/scripts/dev/validate_specs.nu`
- `nu -c 'source nushell/scripts/dev/test_yzx_workspace_commands.nu; test_launch_here_path_warns_when_existing_persistent_session_ignores_it'`
close_reason: Completed: documented the persistent-session multi-window contract.

title: Evaluate yazelix_dev child-repo extraction versus in-repo maintainer crate
description: Revisit whether the maintainer-only Yazelix developer surface should remain as the in-repo yazelix_maintainer crate or move partly or fully into a first-party child repository such as yazelix-dev. The motivation is main-repo LOC reduction and a cleaner user-runtime/product boundary, but the current contract rejects externalizing repo-specific validators because of version-skew and contract-coupling risk.
design: Start from docs/rust_maintainer_tooling_boundary.md and yazelix-w032.10: installed/runtime yzx dev keeps only runtime diagnostics, while repo-only commands live in the maintainer shell and yazelix_maintainer. Re-evaluate only after the LOC scorecard and weak-validation cleanup pass, because a large part of the apparent extraction candidate may be weak validator clutter rather than a coherent reusable project. Classify maintainer modules into: generic dev tooling that could live outside, repo-specific validators that should stay with the files they validate, release/tag automation that likely stays with the repo, GitHub/Beads sync policy that may be repo-local, pane-orchestrator wasm sync tied to tracked runtime artifacts, and runtime diagnostics that should remain in yazelix_core. Consider options: keep in-repo, split only generic helpers, publish a child repo that consumes a Yazelix checkout, or reject extraction and instead shrink/delete.
acceptance_criteria: A decision matrix covers every yazelix_maintainer module and repo-only yzx dev command, with selected residency, LOC impact, version-skew risk, CI/devShell impact, and user-runtime impact. The result either confirms the in-repo maintainer crate, accepts a partial child-repo extraction with follow-up beads, or rejects extraction and creates deletion/shrinkage beads. The decision must explicitly preserve the rule that default Yazelix users do not install maintainer-only tooling.
close_reason: Completed: rejected off-repo yazelix-dev extraction for now, recorded module and repo-only command residency matrices in docs/rust_maintainer_tooling_boundary.md, preserved default user runtime exclusion for maintainer tooling, and created yazelix-vyas.1 to shrink/split maintainer validators before reconsidering a child repo.

title: Shrink and split maintainer validators before revisiting yazelix_dev extraction
description: The yazelix-vyas decision rejects moving the maintainer crate off-repo for now because the largest LOC owners are repo-specific validators and mutating release workflows. The next useful work is to reduce that maintainer surface inside the repo before reconsidering any yazelix-dev child repository.
design: Start with repo_contract_validation.rs, repo_validation.rs, repo_update_workflow.rs, and repo_sweep_runner.rs. Split by contract or workflow domain only when the split makes deletion easier, then remove stale validation, duplicated generated checks, and workflow code that no longer protects a live contract. Do not externalize validators unless a candidate helper can run against arbitrary checkouts through a stable machine contract. Keep yzx_repo_validator and yzx_repo_maintainer compiled from the same commit they validate.
acceptance_criteria: Largest maintainer LOC owners are classified by keep/split/delete; at least one stale or duplicated maintainer validation/workflow surface is deleted or a concrete blocked deletion trigger is recorded; no user-runtime package surface changes; validate-rust-ownership-budget and relevant maintainer validators still pass.
close_reason: Completed: classified the largest maintainer LOC owners in docs/rust_code_inventory.md and kept the maintainer crate in-repo until reusable helpers have a stable checkout-neutral machine contract. Deleted stale strength-score metadata across Rust tests/source after the score parser was removed, removed the remaining strength-rubric policy text, and collapsed the standalone validate-default-test-traceability command into validate-rust-test-traceability so CI/pre-commit run one less maintainer validator. Ratcheted Rust ownership budget from 75,231 to 74,749 tracked raw Rust lines. LOC scorecard from 00305fd5 worktree: raw diff excluding .beads is 81 insertions / 599 deletions; category net runtime -322, maintainer -49, tests -115, docs -25; tokei Rust code went 68,591 -> 68,517 (-74), raw budget 75,231 -> 74,749 (-482). Verification: cargo fmt --all --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml --check; cargo check --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_maintainer --all-targets; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_maintainer repo_validation; validate-rust-test-traceability; validate-rust-ownership-budget; validate-contracts; validate-docs-experience; validate-package-rust-test-purity; git diff --check.

title: Audit the full GitHub/Beads backlog contract and define which beads deserve public issues
description: Audit the current backlog to ensure every in-scope GitHub issue maps to exactly one bead, lifecycle state is consistent in both directions, canonical automated comments exist where required, and the repo has an explicit policy for which internal beads should or should not become public GitHub issues. The goal is to clean up the contract and publication boundary for the backlog without imposing a blanket one-to-one rule from beads to GitHub.
design: Treat this as backlog-governance work, not as a mass issue-creation task. Start from the existing GitHub/Beads contract in AGENTS.md and the current imported backlog, then classify beads into public-worthy versus internal-planning-only categories. Repair missing GitHub→Beads mappings and lifecycle mismatches first. Only create new GitHub issues for beads that are genuinely user-visible, externally discussable, or worth tracking publicly. Avoid exporting low-level decomposition, dependency scaffolding, or purely internal planning slices just to satisfy symmetry.
acceptance_criteria: There is a documented and applied policy for which beads should have GitHub issues. Every in-scope GitHub issue maps cleanly to exactly one bead with matching open/closed state and the canonical automated comment. The backlog also has a reviewed list of open beads that intentionally remain internal and therefore do not need public GitHub issues.
close_reason: Completed: documented the backlog publication policy, reviewed the intentionally internal open-bead list, synced GitHub lifecycle and canonical Beads comments, and revalidated the GitHub/Beads contract.

title: Extract reusable Yazelix components
description: Define and sequence the candidate Yazelix component extractions so the main repo can become a slimmer integration shell without losing product coherence. Each extraction must preserve the Yazelix brand, define its standalone audience, and keep a clear adapter boundary back into the main Yazelix runtime.
design: Candidate extraction line: yazelix_screen, yazelix_cursors, yazelix_zellij_popup, yazelix_ratconfig, yazelix_bar, and eventually yazelix_workspace. Treat extraction as a product boundary, not just moving files. A candidate is ready only when it can ship a useful README example that does not require the full Yazelix repo. Keep Yazelix-specific runtime/install/update/Home Manager logic in the main repo. Sequence low-coupling visual/tooling pieces first, then popup/bar, then ratconfig after the config UI stabilizes, and workspace last because it is closest to Yazelix's core identity.
acceptance_criteria: Every child decision records selected name plus two alternatives, target users, included scope, excluded scope, integration boundary back into Yazelix, migration cost, maintenance cost, release shape, and extraction readiness heuristic. The final epic decision ranks extraction order and explicitly states which components stay internal.
notes:
Scope note: do not treat the pane orchestrator itself as a standalone public extraction yet. Split reusable pieces through yazelix_zellij_popup and/or yazelix_workspace first; keep raw orchestrator internals as implementation detail unless a later decision proves a plugin-SDK-shaped boundary.
Extraction gate: complete yazelix-w032.4 before starting any implementation that moves Rust code into a separate repo/crate distribution. Scope decisions can continue, but code movement should wait for the refreshed Rust inventory and cleanup sequence.
Suggested extraction index after the Rust inventory gate:
0. Gate: yazelix-w032.4 — Refresh Rust inventory before component extraction
1. yazelix-w032.1 — yazelix_screen: lowest-coupling existing crate; prove standalone screen/visual boundary first
2. yazelix-lo9h.2 — yazelix_cursors: Ghostty-only cursor preset distribution; low runtime coupling and strong visual brand value
3. yazelix-w032.2 — yazelix_zellij_popup: reusable Zellij popup/toggle/focus machinery for users and plugin authors
4. yazelix-r9n1.1 — yazelix_bar: standalone Zellij/zjstatus bar preset with AI widgets as first-class value
5. yazelix-ryx4.6 — yazelix_ratconfig: defer until config UI editing and metadata stabilize further
6. yazelix-w032.3 — yazelix_workspace: highest-risk extraction; start internal-first only after inventory and smaller extractions clarify boundaries
close_reason: all steps complete

title: Evaluate extracting yazelix_screen
description: Evaluate extracting the existing yazelix_screen crate into a standalone Yazelix-branded terminal screen/splash surface. Selected name: yazelix_screen. Alternatives: yzx_screen, yazelix_tui_screen.
design:
Decision: extract yazelix_screen first, but keep the current package-first boundary as the immediate supported surface until the external README/example/CI shape is prepared.

Selected name: yazelix_screen. Alternatives: yzx_screen and yazelix_tui_screen. Keep the selected name because it is already the crate, binary, flake package, flake app, and strongest Yazelix-branded component name.

Standalone audience: terminal and TUI projects that want polished terminal-safe animation engines, splash screens, slideshow/welcome surfaces, or screensaver-like visuals without adopting Yazelix runtime/session/config behavior.

Current evidence: rust_core/yazelix_screen is already a separate workspace crate with a standalone binary and only one direct production dependency, crossterm. tokei reports about 2409 code LOC in the crate. cargo test -p yazelix_screen passes 32 tests. cargo run -p yazelix_screen -- --help proves the binary starts without Zellij or a Yazelix session. yazelix_core depends on yazelix_screen, but yazelix_screen does not depend on yazelix_core or maintainer code.

Crate/repo shape: phase 0 is the current monorepo crate plus .#yazelix_screen package/app. Phase 1 should be an external-ready crate/repo retaining the same crate and binary names, with a README, examples, minimal CI, semver release notes, and a Nix flake or package path that does not require the full Yazelix runtime. A separate repository is worth doing only after those external-user surfaces exist.

Asset contract: the reusable crate owns pure animation engines, frame production, terminal sizing helpers, alternate-screen/raw-mode helpers, and terminal-safe rendering. It must not read settings.jsonc, Home Manager state, Zellij session state, Yazelix generated config, or command-palette/runtime state. The branded logo/ascii-art catalog currently stays in yazelix_core unless a later decision deliberately moves that asset contract into the screen crate.

External example: keep the current yazelix_screen binary as the first example, then add examples such as render_once and play_style that demonstrate library use without Yazelix. The example contract should be simple: choose a style, create a ScreenAnimationContext, render frames, and exit cleanly.

Yazelix adapter boundary: yazelix_core/front_door_render.rs remains the adapter that maps Yazelix config, style names, CoreError diagnostics, welcome duration, and startup/screen commands onto yazelix_screen primitives. The dependency direction stays one-way: Yazelix product crates may consume yazelix_screen; yazelix_screen must not consume Yazelix product crates.

Release policy: use normal semver for the standalone crate. Breaking API changes in frame producer traits, style names, terminal-mode helpers, or cell-style parsing require a major bump once published. Yazelix can continue pinning by workspace path until extraction, then by git/crates.io/Nix input. Tags can be component-scoped, for example yazelix_screen-v0.x.y, if the repo stays monorepo during early releases.

Test and preview gates: cargo test -p yazelix_screen must stay green; cargo run -p yazelix_screen -- --help must remain noninteractive; Nix package/app gates remain nix build .#yazelix_screen and nix run .#yazelix_screen -- --help; visual previews should include at least boids, Mandelbrot, and Game of Life on narrow and wide terminal sizes. Snapshot or frame-shape tests should protect row addressing, deterministic animation steps, resize behavior, and style parsing.

Maintenance cost: low-to-medium. The crate is already isolated and small enough to extract first. The main costs are release metadata, external docs/examples, keeping the core adapter stable, and deciding whether the branded logo belongs outside Yazelix core. The extraction removes roughly 2.4k raw Rust LOC from the main repo when moved out, but it does not remove Yazelix's responsibility for the adapter and screen UX.

Ordering: yes, extract/prove yazelix_screen before the other visual extractions. It is the lowest-coupling candidate and should be the proof-of-process before yazelix_cursors, yazelix_zellij_popup, yazelix_bar, or yazelix_ratconfig. Do not let it block unrelated config UI work; treat it as the first extraction when the extraction line resumes.
acceptance_criteria: Decision records selected name plus alternatives, standalone audience, crate/repo shape, asset contract, external example, Yazelix adapter boundary, release policy, test/preview gates, maintenance cost, and whether extraction should happen before other visual extractions.
close_reason: Completed: recorded the yazelix_screen extraction decision, validated the current standalone crate/package boundary, and created yazelix-w032.5 for external README/example/release prep before any repo move.

title: Evaluate extracting yzx_dev and maintainer commands from user runtime package
description: Evaluate whether yzx dev and maintainer-only command families should move out of the default user-shipped Yazelix package so normal users install only runtime and product commands by default.
design:
Decision: split yzx dev by command residency instead of extracting the whole namespace at once.

Keep runtime-safe diagnostics available from the user runtime package for now: yzx dev inspect_session inspects live Zellij/Yazelix panes, and yzx dev profile can profile an installed runtime. These are diagnostic, not maintainer-only build orchestration.

Move repo-only maintainer commands toward a maintainer-only package/app/devShell surface: build_pane_orchestrator, bump, lint_nu, rust fmt/check/test, sync_issues, test, and update. These commands require a repository checkout, mutate maintainer state, inspect repo contracts, or run contributor-only validation. They should not be advertised as part of the default user runtime command surface.

Keep user-facing product diagnostics outside the dev namespace: doctor, status, inspect, config, and related supported runtime commands remain the public user contract.

Preferred implementation path: keep the existing yazelix_maintainer crate as the owner of repo-only command logic, expose it through the development shell and optionally a flake app, and shrink the default runtime package/docs/metadata so users see only supported runtime behavior. A thin repo checkout wrapper can preserve contributor ergonomics without making repo-only commands part of the shipped user surface.

No new Rust crates are needed; this is packaging and command-surface ownership using existing crates/binaries.
acceptance_criteria:
Decision recorded on runtime-vs-maintainer command residency.
Follow-up implementation beads created for the package/app split and command metadata shrink.
yazelix-ft4k.6.1 is unblocked to audit the surviving command-surface tests after the boundary is explicit.
close_reason: Completed: recorded runtime-vs-maintainer command residency decision and created implementation follow-ups.

title: Package repo-only yzx dev commands as maintainer-only surface
description: Move repo-only yzx dev command entrypoints toward a maintainer-only crate/package/devShell surface instead of shipping them as supported user runtime commands. Scope includes build_pane_orchestrator, bump, lint_nu, rust fmt/check/test, sync_issues, test, and update.
acceptance_criteria:
Repo-only commands are available from the maintainer environment without being advertised by the default user runtime package.
Contributor ergonomics are preserved through a thin repo/devShell command if needed.
No command implementation is duplicated across runtime and maintainer crates.
notes: Rust dependency decision for implementation: use existing std/lexopt command parsing and the existing yazelix_core/yazelix_maintainer crates; no new production or dev crates. Build the runtime-vs-maintainer split in-house by moving route ownership and packaging metadata because this is project-specific command-surface logic. Rejected alternatives: adding a CLI framework, keeping compatibility aliases in the runtime package, or duplicating command implementations across crates. Packaging impact: default user package should stop advertising repo-only dev commands; maintainer devShell/package keeps them available.
close_reason: Completed maintainer-only command split: moved fast Rust dev commands into yazelix_maintainer, trimmed user-runtime yzx_control dev to diagnostics, and added a maintainer-shell yzx wrapper that preserves yzx dev ergonomics for repo-only commands.

title: Shrink user runtime dev command metadata to diagnostics
description: After the maintainer command package boundary is implemented, shrink public command metadata and generated command surfaces so the default user runtime only describes supported runtime diagnostics and product commands, not repo-only maintainer workflows.
acceptance_criteria:
Generated command metadata no longer presents repo-only maintainer commands as user runtime commands.
Runtime-safe diagnostics such as inspect_session/profile are either explicitly retained with user-facing wording or moved to a clearer supported command surface.
Command surface validation tests defend the new boundary without help-output trivia.
notes: Rust dependency decision for implementation: no new crates. Use existing public_command_surface and command_metadata structures; remove repo-only entries from the runtime metadata table in-house. Rejected alternatives: compatibility aliases, generated filtering after the fact, or keeping dead metadata for commands now owned by the maintainer shell. Packaging impact: default user runtime help/externs describe only supported runtime diagnostics under yzx dev.
close_reason: Completed public runtime command metadata shrink: generated yzx metadata and externs now retain only runtime-safe dev diagnostics and omit repo-only maintainer commands split into the maintainer shell.

title: Update docs for runtime versus maintainer command split
description: Document the supported split between user runtime commands and maintainer-only repository commands after the yzx dev extraction work lands. Keep user docs focused on installed-runtime behavior and maintainer docs focused on repo checkout workflows.
acceptance_criteria:
README and maintainer docs describe the surviving command surfaces without exposing repo-only workflows as normal user features.
Docs use present-tense wording and avoid legacy alias promises unless a released compatibility reason exists.
close_reason: Completed: README and CLI docs now describe installed yzx dev as runtime diagnostics only, and maintainer docs point repo-only dev commands at the maintainer shell wrapper.

title: Extract yazelix_zellij_popup as a distributable plugin package
description: Turn the prepared yazelix_zellij_popup extraction gate into a real distributable in-repo package/plugin boundary that plain Zellij users can install and validate without the full Yazelix runtime.
design: Keep Yazelix adapters in the main runtime. The package should own the generic popup policy, plugin artifact, plain-Zellij config example, install/permission docs, and validation command. Do not pull workspace/sidebar/editor semantics into the package.
acceptance_criteria: A standalone yazelix_zellij_popup package or flake output exists; plain-Zellij install and permission instructions are documented; validation covers the built plugin/package and the plain-Zellij example; Yazelix continues consuming the same behavior through its adapter boundary.
close_reason: Completed: added standalone yazelix_zellij_popup Zellij plugin binary, flake package, package-local plain-Zellij example, permission/install docs, distribution contract, and synced pane-orchestrator wasm. Verified cargo fmt/check/test, nix build .#yazelix_zellij_popup, flake/contracts/docs validators, and pane-orchestrator sync.

title: Move reusable config UI pieces behind private yazelix_ratconfig boundary
description: Create the first internal yazelix_ratconfig boundary by moving reusable config UI model/editor/render pieces behind a private crate or module boundary while leaving Yazelix-specific settings and apply behavior in adapters.
design: Keep the first split in-repo and private. Reusable ownership should cover field and section models, edit actions, rendering helpers, and validation-facing diagnostics. Yazelix adapters keep settings.jsonc loading, contract/schema mapping, JSONC patching, Home Manager status, native config status, generated refresh, and apply-mode reporting.
acceptance_criteria: Reusable config UI code is reachable through a clear private yazelix_ratconfig boundary; Yazelix-specific config persistence/apply code remains adapter-owned; tests defend the reusable model/editor behavior and the Yazelix adapter boundary; no public crate/repo is published.
close_reason: Completed: moved reusable config UI model/editor/render modules behind private yazelix_ratconfig namespace, left JSONC writes/native status/Home Manager/apply behavior in config_ui adapter, and updated extraction contract. Verified cargo fmt/check, config_ui tests, settings_jsonc_patch tests, rust test traceability, config surface, contracts, and diff whitespace.

title: Evaluate extracting yazelix_zellij_popup
description:
## Decision
Keep `yazelix_zellij_popup` as the selected name, but do not extract it as a standalone repository/package yet. The reusable transient-pane policy is promising, but the currently supported product surface is still Yazelix-owned.

When the POP-005 gate is satisfied, extract popup before `yazelix_workspace` rather than from inside it. Popup is a lower-level Zellij floating-pane utility; bundling it into workspace extraction would leak sidebar/editor/session policy into a package that should stay useful to plain Zellij users.

## Rationale
The current Rust split already separates generic transient selection/toggle/launch planning from Yazelix adapters, but the public behavior still depends on Yazelix settings, runtime wrapper scripts, generated keybindings, workspace cwd snapshots, and sidebar-refresh hooks. Extracting now would publish an API that is mostly an adapter to Yazelix internals.

The right next step is `yazelix-w032.9`: prepare the standalone gate with a generic config surface, stable pipe schema, runtime-independent tests, and plain-Zellij examples. No external repo move should happen until those artifacts exist.

## Alternatives Considered
- Keep popup permanently internal: too conservative, because the single-instance floating TUI toggle has real value for plain Zellij users and plugin authors.
- Extract immediately as `yazelix_zellij_popup`: rejected because the current surface still requires Yazelix runtime paths and wrappers.
- Extract only a tiny planning crate: useful later, but insufficient as a product by itself unless paired with a Zellij plugin/pipe contract and examples.
- Defer into `yazelix_workspace`: rejected because workspace extraction is broader and would contaminate popup with editor/sidebar/session responsibilities.

## Existing Context
`transient_pane_contract.rs` already owns generic pane snapshots, identity matching, launch planning, and open/focus/close toggle planning. `transient_adapter_contract.rs`, Nushell wrappers, generated Zellij keybindings, and `transient.rs` still carry Yazelix-specific runtime adapters and Zellij host behavior.
design:
Selected name: `yazelix_zellij_popup`. Alternatives: `yazelix_popup` and `zellij_popup_kit`. Keep the selected name because it is explicitly Zellij-scoped and still Yazelix-branded.

Target users: plain Zellij users who want one-key floating TUI toggles; Zellij plugin authors who want a small open/focus/close lifecycle layer; Yazelix as the first production consumer.

Standalone shape: a future external repo/package should contain a small Rust policy library plus a Zellij plugin/WASM built on `zellij-tile`. The library owns pane identity, floating-pane snapshots, toggle planning, launch planning, geometry validation, command argv, and cwd policy. The plugin owns the Zellij host calls: pipe handling, opening floating command panes, focusing pane ids, closing panes, and hiding/restoring floating state as supported. A CLI-only package is not enough unless it can prove active-tab scoping and no-duplicate behavior without plugin state.

Plugin-author API: define named popup specs rather than hardcoded Yazelix `popup`, `menu`, and `config` kinds. A spec should include id, pane title, optional command marker, command argv or open-request argv, cwd policy, width/height percent, and close behavior. Pipe messages should support at least toggle-by-id and open-with-override-args; focus/close may be explicit if examples justify them. Payloads should be JSON and reject unknown/missing fields clearly.

Zellij version support: initial support should target Zellij 0.44 or newer, matching the current `zellij-tile = 0.44.0` dependency and the host calls Yazelix already uses: `open_command_pane_floating`, `focus_pane_with_id`, `close_pane_with_id`, `hide_floating_panes`, and pipe messages.

Command-pane identity contract: a managed popup is a non-plugin, non-exited, floating terminal pane whose title exactly matches the configured pane title or whose terminal command contains an optional configured marker. If duplicates exist, focused panes win for close behavior; otherwise a present pane should be focused rather than creating another duplicate.

Optional hook model: no Yazelix-specific hook belongs in the generic package. If hooks are added, they should be generic and explicit, such as no hook by default and possibly a future run-command argv hook. Yazelix sidebar Yazi refresh remains a Yazelix adapter concern.

Example plugin: the first plain-Zellij example should toggle a floating `lazygit` or `gitui` pane with a KDL keybind that sends a `MessagePlugin` pipe to the plugin. It should not require `settings.jsonc`, Home Manager, `yzx`, Yazelix runtime wrappers, or Yazelix generated config.

Yazelix adapter boundary: Yazelix keeps ownership of `zellij.popup_program`, popup geometry settings, Home Manager parity, generated keybindings, runtime wrapper scripts, command-palette/config popups, workspace-root cwd snapshots, and sidebar refresh. Yazelix can consume the future package, but the future package must not depend on Yazelix config, Yazi, Helix, runtime roots, or generated state.

Migration cost: medium. The pure policy code is already small and mostly isolated, but a standalone product needs config design, pipe schema, plain-Zellij examples, plugin permission and packaging docs, runtime-independent tests, release metadata, and careful adaptation back into Yazelix. Moving too early would increase support cost without reducing main-repo complexity much.

Maintenance cost: medium. The package would track Zellij plugin API changes, permissions UX, KDL examples, and WASM packaging. It is still cheaper than extracting `yazelix_workspace` because it avoids editor/sidebar/session semantics.

Ordering: prepare `yazelix_zellij_popup` after the current screen/cursor extraction line and before `yazelix_workspace`, but only as gate prep first. Do not create a standalone extraction bead until `yazelix-w032.9` proves POP-005 with generic config, stable schema, runtime-independent tests, and a plain-Zellij example.
acceptance_criteria: Decision records selected name plus alternatives, target users, standalone plugin/crate shape, plugin-author API, Zellij version support, command-pane identity contract, optional hook model, example popup plugin, Yazelix adapter boundary, migration cost, maintenance cost, extraction readiness heuristic, and whether this should be extracted before or inside `yazelix_workspace`.
close_reason: Completed: selected yazelix_zellij_popup as the future standalone name, rejected immediate extraction, recorded the standalone plugin/crate boundary, and created yazelix-w032.9 to prepare the POP-005 extraction gate before any external split.

title: Evaluate extracting yazelix_workspace
description:
## Decision
Select yazelix_workspace as the future extraction name, but keep the readiness state at internal_boundary_only. Do not create a standalone distribution until the internal workspace/editor/sidebar boundary is smaller than the integrated Yazelix product surface.

## Rationale
Workspace orchestration is the highest-coupling extraction because it owns tab-local workspace roots, managed editor/sidebar identity, Yazi adapter events, layout-family behavior, and Zellij plugin state. Premature publication would expose a half-product API tied to Yazelix runtime paths and generated config.

## Alternatives Considered
Rejected yazelix_zellij_workspace as too Zellij-only, yazelix_editor_flow as too narrow, immediate standalone extraction as too risky, and keeping the surface internal forever as unnecessarily conservative once the internal boundary is proven.

## Durable Record
See docs/contracts/yazelix_workspace_extraction.md. Session persistence/resurrection is out of scope for this decision.
design: Exact scope: tab-local workspace root/source, managed editor/sidebar pane identity, editor/sidebar focus, sidebar visibility policy, retarget_workspace, open_file, set_managed_editor_cwd, open_workspace_terminal, active-tab session snapshot, sidebar Yazi identity validation, and layout-family awareness. Public shape: structured action requests and typed results, not a second CLI parser. Relationship: pane orchestrator remains the live state owner; popup extraction is adjacent but separate; Yazi/editor stay adapters. Follow-up: yazelix-w032.3.1 splits workspace/editor/session command boundaries before any public package.
acceptance_criteria: Decision records selected name plus alternatives, exact sidebar/editor flow scope, Zellij/Yazi/editor prerequisites, public API shape, internal-first extraction plan, how it relates to yazelix_zellij_popup and pane-orchestrator code, migration risk, test strategy, and a clear go/no-go criterion for separate distribution.
close_reason: Completed: documented yazelix_workspace extraction decision in docs/contracts/yazelix_workspace_extraction.md, selected internal_boundary_only readiness, kept session persistence/resurrection out of scope, and created follow-up yazelix-w032.3.1 for the internal workspace/editor/session boundary split.

title: Split workspace/editor/session command boundary before yazelix_workspace extraction
description: Split the high-coupling workspace/editor/session command surface into focused internal boundaries before any public yazelix_workspace extraction.
design: Keep the pane orchestrator as live tab-local state owner. Isolate reusable request/response types, workspace action model, editor adapter command generation, sidebar identity handling, and Zellij transport decisions from Yazelix-specific path resolution, zoxide, runtime wrapper paths, generated config ownership, and product command text. Session persistence/resurrection stays out of scope.
acceptance_criteria: zellij_commands.rs and workspace_commands.rs no longer mix workspace/editor/popup/session responsibilities beyond clear adapters; reusable workspace request/response and pure decision helpers live in focused modules or a private internal crate; Yazelix adapters still own path resolution, Yazi emit-to execution, runtime paths, config facts, and product text; existing workspace, Yazi, pane-orchestrator, and workspace-session validator checks pass; no resurrection behavior is introduced or changed.
notes: Rust dependency decision: no new crates. Keep this as a module-boundary refactor inside yazelix_core using existing serde_json/tempfile test dependencies; move pure workspace/session structs and helpers into focused modules. Packaging impact: none. Rejected a public crate split and any resurrection/session-persistence work for this slice.
close_reason: Completed: extracted shared pane-orchestrator transport and typed active-tab workspace/session parsing into focused modules; workspace and Zellij command files now use adapter boundaries instead of duplicating session wire parsing.

title: Update workspace-session validator to read semantic Zellij action registry
description: The workspace-session validator still checks zellij_materialization.rs for old message_name literals even though semantic Zellij action metadata moved to the shared action registry.
design: Use ZELLIJ_ACTIONS as the validator source of truth for semantic pane-orchestrator message names. Keep the default config action-id check, and add a regression test so future validator drift is caught.
acceptance_criteria: validate-workspace-session-contract no longer fails because message_name literals moved out of zellij_materialization.rs; the validator checks every required semantic pane-orchestrator command against ZELLIJ_ACTIONS; a maintainer-lane Rust test defends the registry-backed check; validate-workspace-session-contract passes.
close_reason: Completed: workspace-session validator now checks semantic Zellij pane-orchestrator message names against ZELLIJ_ACTIONS instead of stale zellij_materialization literals; added maintainer regression coverage and validate-workspace-session-contract passes.

title: Refresh Rust inventory before component extraction
description: Refresh the Rust inventory before extracting any reusable Yazelix components. The current repo has about 74k Rust lines by tokei across source and tests, while the ownership budget tracks about 50k governed LOC; extraction decisions need a current map of product code, maintainer code, tests, extension surfaces, dead code risk, and overengineered seams.
design: Inventory first, refactor second, extract third. Do not use this as a license for broad churn. Classify every Rust family by keep/delete/simplify/extract, identify likely dead code through compiler warnings, cargo-udeps or equivalent where practical, rg call-site checks, and validator surfaces, then produce a ranked cleanup/extraction sequence. Separate true deletion from extraction accounting: moving code out of the repo reduces main-repo LOC but does not reduce total maintenance unless the boundary is simpler.
acceptance_criteria: Updated inventory records tokei totals, governed budget totals, source/test/maintainer/plugin split, largest files/modules, dead-code candidates with evidence, overengineering hotspots, extraction candidates and blockers, recommended pre-extraction refactors, and realistic LOC targets for 70k/65k/60k/50k. It must state which refactors should happen before extraction and which should wait until after extraction exposes cleaner boundaries.
notes: Prime-beads refinement 2026-05-05: this bead should update durable inventory artifacts, not only produce a chat summary. Use docs/rust_code_inventory.md and config_metadata/rust_ownership_budget.toml as the current source surfaces; explain the difference between tokei's ~74k Rust lines and the governed ~50k LOC budget. Prefer cheap commands first: tokei with target excluded, cargo check, validator budget checks, rg call-site probes, and local source inspection. cargo-udeps or heavier dead-code tooling is optional and should be recorded as unavailable/too heavy if it is not cleanly available.
close_reason: Completed: refreshed docs/rust_code_inventory.md and config_metadata/rust_ownership_budget.toml with current 2026-05-05 Rust inventory, no-growth budget, extraction sequence, dead-code evidence, LOC targets, cargo-udeps results, and removed unused pane-orchestrator shlex dependency.

title: Prepare yazelix_screen external extraction surface
description: Prepare the external-user surface for a future standalone yazelix_screen repository or independently published crate before moving code out of the monorepo.
design: Add README/example/CI/release scaffolding around the existing standalone crate while preserving the current Yazelix adapter boundary. This is prep work, not the actual repo move.
acceptance_criteria: Standalone README explains end-user yzs usage, integrated yzx screen usage, and library use without Yazelix; examples demonstrate render_once and play_style without Yazelix runtime/session/config; cargo test -p yazelix_screen and cargo run -p yazelix_screen --bin yzs -- --help stay green; Nix package/app gates are documented and produce/run bin/yzs; release/version/tag policy is explicit; yazelix_core remains only an adapter consumer.
notes: Implemented the extraction prep as a user-facing standalone screen surface: the installed command is yzs, the Rust crate remains yazelix_screen, and flake app/package aliases expose .#yzs while .#yazelix_screen remains available for internal clarity. Added crate-local README, render_once and play_style examples, Nix package metadata, and updated the standalone screen contract.
