title: Prepare yazelix_zellij_popup extraction gate
description:
Prepare the gate work required before `yazelix_zellij_popup` can become a standalone package or plugin.

## Problem
The current popup/transient-pane policy is partly isolated, but the supported surface is still Yazelix-owned: `settings.jsonc`, Home Manager options, generated keybindings, runtime wrapper paths, popup/menu/config kind names, and the sidebar refresh hook. That is not a plain-Zellij API.

## Expected Outcome
A future extraction can be judged against concrete artifacts instead of enthusiasm: a generic config surface, stable pipe schema, runtime-independent tests, and examples that work for plain Zellij users without the full Yazelix runtime.

## Scope
Keep the implementation in-repo until the gate is met. This bead prepares the reusable surface and examples; it does not move code to a new repository by itself.
design:
Use the decision from yazelix-w032.2 and POP-005 in docs/contracts/floating_tui_panes.md.

The prep slice should:
- define named popup specs independent of Yazelix `popup`/`menu`/`config` kinds
- define a stable JSON pipe payload for open/toggle/focus/close that does not require Yazelix runtime paths
- keep command argv, cwd policy, geometry, pane title, and optional command marker as explicit data
- keep post-close hooks generic or absent; Yazelix sidebar refresh stays in the Yazelix adapter
- add unit tests around the generic policy without requiring `settings.jsonc`, Home Manager, or runtime wrapper scripts
- add a plain Zellij example KDL/keybind showing a temporary TUI such as lazygit or gitui
- leave external repo/package extraction blocked until the example and tests prove the standalone story
acceptance_criteria: A generic non-Yazelix popup spec is documented or represented in code; a stable plain-Zellij open/toggle pipe schema is documented; tests cover the generic policy without depending on Yazelix runtime wrappers; a plain Zellij example shows how to toggle one configured floating TUI; Yazelix-specific settings, Home Manager integration, runtime wrappers, menu/config popups, workspace cwd snapshotting, and sidebar refresh remain clearly adapter-owned by Yazelix; the bead states whether the POP-005 gate is satisfied or which item still blocks external extraction.
notes: Implemented the extraction-gate prep: added generic TransientPopupSpec / TransientPopupPipeRequest schema and runtime-independent tests in transient_pane_contract.rs; documented the standalone prep schema in docs/contracts/floating_tui_panes.md; added docs/examples/zellij_popup_plain_zellij.kdl as the plain-Zellij MessagePlugin payload example. POP-005 is prepared but not fully satisfied: external extraction remains blocked on a distributable standalone plugin/package, permission/install story, and validation of the plain-Zellij example against that package. Verification passed: cargo fmt for pane orchestrator, cargo test --manifest-path rust_plugins/zellij_pane_orchestrator/Cargo.toml transient_pane_contract, cargo check --manifest-path rust_plugins/zellij_pane_orchestrator/Cargo.toml, yzx dev rust fmt --check, validate-contracts, validate-rust-test-traceability, yzx dev build_pane_orchestrator --sync, validate-pane-orchestrator-sync, full pane-orchestrator cargo test, and git diff --check.
close_reason: Completed: prepared the yazelix_zellij_popup extraction gate with a generic popup spec/request schema, runtime-independent policy tests, plain-Zellij example KDL, updated contract docs, and synced pane-orchestrator wasm; POP-005 remains blocked only on a distributable standalone plugin/package and validated install/permission story.

title: Evaluate removing persistent_sessions from Yazelix config
description: Persistent Zellij sessions complicate the per-window immutable config snapshot model and are not well-covered by current behavior tests. Evaluate whether Yazelix should remove persistent_sessions/session_name from the supported config surface, keep them behind a clearer contract, or replace them with an explicit advanced launch mode.
design:
Decision: remove `zellij.persistent_sessions` and `zellij.session_name` from the supported Yazelix config surface.

First-principles reasoning:
- Yazelix's core workspace model should let a user open as many Yazelix windows as they want, with each window owning a stable launch-time config snapshot and live session state.
- The new immutable snapshot model says config edits apply to the next window or to the current window through an explicit `yzx restart` transition.
- Persistent Zellij sessions invert that model: later launches attach to one existing named session, fresh path/layout/shell/env intent is ignored, and already-running panes keep the environment/snapshot inherited when the session was first created.
- Keeping persistent sessions would require a separate product contract: snapshot mismatch detection, attach-vs-recreate policy, shared-session restart UX, last-client lifecycle semantics, and tests for all of that.
- That complexity is not justified for the trimmed Yazelix workspace core. Zellij's native session persistence can still exist outside Yazelix for users who intentionally want raw Zellij behavior.

Implementation direction:
- Remove `persistent_sessions` and `session_name` from `yazelix_default.toml`, the main config contract, Home Manager options, onboarding, startup facts, session facts/snapshot facts, status output, generated Zellij rendering inputs, and docs/specs that present persistent sessions as live product behavior.
- Simplify startup handoff to always create a fresh Yazelix session/window path instead of conditionally running `zellij list-sessions` and `zellij attach <session_name>`.
- Keep Zellij close behavior aligned with non-persistent Yazelix semantics; `on_force_close` should not be driven by removed config fields.
- Update the non-persistent window contract into the normal window-session contract. Demote or delete the persistent-window spec from the live spec inventory.
- Migration UX should not silently change a user who explicitly had `persistent_sessions = true`. That case should produce a clear config diagnostic explaining the feature was removed and that the fields must be removed manually. Stale default/false fields can be considered for auto-pruning only if the implementation makes the no-op nature obvious and safe.
- Do not keep legacy config aliases by default. If a removed-field diagnostic exists, it is an error/migration aid, not a compatibility path.

Follow-up implementation should happen before the snapshot handoff is hardened, so the snapshot work does not spend effort preserving a shared-session branch Yazelix intends to delete.
acceptance_criteria: Decision records whether persistent_sessions stays, changes, or is removed; analysis covers multiple Yazelix windows, per-session config snapshots, attach semantics, migration impact, and test requirements before implementation
close_reason: Decision recorded: remove persistent_sessions/session_name from supported Yazelix config rather than preserving a separate shared-session product contract.

title: Drive Yazelix toward the provable irreducible Nushell floor, below 5k LOC
description: Yazelix still carries roughly 29k lines of Nushell. Existing epics attack tests, validators, maintainer surfaces, data-heavy helpers, and launch/runtime helpers, but the project still needs a stricter cross-cutting rule: every surviving Nu line must justify itself against Rust, assets, POSIX helpers, or deletion. Build the missing plan-space and implementation lanes so Yazelix reaches a provable under-5k Nu floor without losing real functionality.
design: Treat Nushell as unsafe by default. Nu survives only when direct shell, TTY, host integration, or presentation behavior is truly irreducible and cannot move to Rust, assets, or fixed POSIX helpers without adding worse wrapper debt. Do not accept historical survivor assumptions, taste-based no-gos, or convenience wrappers as proof.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The backlog contains an explicit under-5k family budget and cut order. 2. Every remaining large Nushell family has either an implementation lane or a hard proof of irreducibility. 3. Existing open Nu-reduction epics are connected to the under-5k goal. 4. The plan includes a no-growth gate so Nu cannot drift back upward after the floor is reached. 5. No proposed lane loses real functionality in the name of line-count reduction.
close_reason: all steps complete

title: Define the provable irreducible Nushell floor and hard family budgets

design: Budget by retained honest ownership, not by directory names alone. The default answer for each Nu line is delete, Rust-own, asset-own, or fixed-POSIX-own; a retained Nu remainder needs explicit proof of irreducibility.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Each remaining Nu family has a hard target budget, rationale, and proof standard. 2. The combined floor is under 5k LOC without functionality loss. 3. The cut order names which existing epics and new lanes must land to reach that floor. 4. No large family remains unplanned. 5. Every retained Nu remainder has a concrete why-not-Rust or why-not-asset proof, not a vibe.

acceptance_criteria: 1. The repo has a hard Nushell budget and allowlist policy. 2. A non-Nu-owned gate can enforce that budget. 3. New Nu growth requires an explicit recorded exception instead of silently landing.

title: Implement the hard no-growth gate for the canonical Nushell budget
description: Implement the agreed no-growth gate outside Nushell so the repo can mechanically reject unexpected Nu growth above the canonical budget or outside the allowlist.
design: Prefer a cheap Rust-owned or other non-Nu-owned validator path that can run locally and in CI without relying on the very surface it is trying to constrain.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The gate measures the canonical Nu budget and allowlist. 2. Unexpected Nu growth fails fast. 3. Allowed exceptions require an explicit recorded path. 4. The gate itself is not Nu-owned.
notes: Implementation decision: add a repo-local validator/gate for the canonical Nushell allowlist and budget instead of CI-only tribal policy; use in-house Rust or Nu validation surfaces already present in the repo and avoid new crates.
close_reason: Completed

title: Acknowledge cursor widget default config change in upgrade notes
description: CI failed because commit 5d97eb2 changed yazelix_default.toml without acknowledging the guarded config-surface change in docs/upgrade_notes.toml. Update the unreleased upgrade notes entry so validate-upgrade-contract passes again.
acceptance_criteria: validate-upgrade-contract passes locally for the current HEAD.
close_reason: Completed: unreleased upgrade notes now acknowledge yazelix_default.toml, and validate-upgrade-contract --ci passes locally.

title: Fix split-brain Yazelix ownership after Home Manager takeover
description: After a Home Manager takeover, stale Yazelix-managed shell blocks can keep interactive shells pinned to an older store runtime while the desktop entry and profile wrapper use the Home Manager-owned runtime. That creates split-brain behavior: shell launches can appear healthy, desktop launch can fail, and users can receive conflicting signals about which Yazelix install is active.
design: Do not change the desktop launch path to depend on host shell state. The desktop entry should keep targeting the profile-owned yzx wrapper. Fix the migration and diagnostics path instead: Home Manager takeover helpers should detect/archive old Yazelix-managed shell blocks, and doctor should report stale host-shell shadowing even when invoked from the profile-owned wrapper.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Home Manager takeover no longer leaves a stale Yazelix-managed shell block silently shadowing the profile-owned yzx command. 2. Desktop launch and fresh interactive-shell launch resolve to the same owned runtime after cleanup. 3. Users get clear diagnostics and recovery guidance instead of a false healthy state.
close_reason: Completed

Those blocks can keep shells pinned to an obsolete runtime after Home Manager takes ownership.
design: Extend takeover artifact discovery to include known Yazelix-managed shell blocks in supported shell startup files. Keep the scope narrow: only archive blocks Yazelix can identify as its own, and do not rewrite unrelated user shell config. Reuse the delete-first migration philosophy: remove stale ownership instead of adding more runtime indirection.

Continue? [y/N]: Aborted. archives only Yazelix-owned shell blocks and leaves unrelated user shell config untouched. 3. After takeover cleanup, a fresh shell resolves Show Yazelix help or version information

title: Archive stale Yazelix shell blocks during Home Manager takeover
description: `yzx home_manager prepare` currently handles manual config, desktop artifacts, and the legacy ~/.local/bin/yzx wrapper, but it does not detect old Yazelix-managed shell blocks that still define `yzx()` or source store-pinned runtime hooks from files like ~/.bashrc. Those blocks can keep shells pinned to an obsolete runtime after Home Manager takes ownership.
design: Extend takeover artifact discovery to include known Yazelix-managed shell blocks in supported shell startup files. Keep the scope narrow: only archive blocks Yazelix can identify as its own, and do not rewrite unrelated user shell config. Reuse the delete-first migration philosophy: remove stale ownership instead of adding more runtime indirection.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Preview mode lists stale Yazelix-managed shell blocks as Home Manager takeover artifacts. 2. `yzx home_manager prepare --apply` archives only Yazelix-owned shell blocks and leaves unrelated user shell config untouched. 3. After takeover cleanup, a fresh shell resolves `yzx` through the profile-owned command instead of a stale store path.
close_reason: Completed

title: Make `yzx doctor` detect stale host-shell shadowing without invocation-context redirects
description: The current doctor logic can warn about stale host-shell `yzx` shadowing when a modern wrapper redirect has already happened, but it can still report a false healthy state when the user invokes doctor through an older store-pinned shell function or when doctor is launched from the correct profile wrapper while stale shell blocks still exist on disk.
design: Teach doctor to inspect known shell startup files directly for Yazelix-owned stale shell blocks and store-pinned `yzx` functions or aliases. Do not rely solely on `YAZELIX_REDIRECTED_FROM_STALE_YZX_PATH` or wrapper-resolution context. The output should name the file and explain the cleanup path clearly.
acceptance_criteria: 1. `yzx doctor --verbose` warns when a stale Yazelix-managed shell block shadows or can shadow the profile-owned `yzx` command, even if doctor was launched from the profile wrapper. 2. The warning identifies the relevant startup file or managed block location. 3. The guidance points users at the supported cleanup or takeover flow instead of leaving a false healthy summary.
close_reason: Completed

title: Harden doctor startup checks so desktop-launch regressions do not pass as generic terminal support
description: Doctor currently reports "Configured terminal launch support is available" by checking command availability, not by validating the real desktop or managed-terminal startup contract. That can produce a false green result when desktop launch still fails in practice.
design: Keep the check high-signal. Either probe the real desktop and managed launch preconditions that users depend on, or narrow the doctor wording so it no longer implies end-to-end launch health. Avoid weak tests that only restate command discovery.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Doctor no longer reports a misleading healthy launch state when only terminal binary discovery succeeded. 2. The remaining launch-health signal corresponds to a defended startup contract or is explicitly scoped as command availability only. 3. The output helps users distinguish desktop-entry/runtime issues from simple PATH discovery.
close_reason: Completed

title: Add direct choice controls to config UI edits
description: Bool and enum config UI edits should behave like direct controls instead of requiring users to type values manually.
acceptance_criteria: Entering edit mode on bool or enum fields shows direct-control hints; Up/Down/Left/Right/Space toggle or cycle the value; Enter saves and Esc cancels; free-form fields keep text input.
close_reason: Completed: bool and enum edit mode now supports arrow/space toggle-cycle controls while text fields retain typed input.

title: Add cargo-crap to maintainer shell
description: Package cargo-crap for the Yazelix maintainer shell so repo maintainers can run CRAP/change-risk metrics without relying on a host cargo install. Acceptance: cargo-crap is available in the flake maintainer shell, is kept out of the user runtime, and a focused Nix evaluation/build or command smoke test verifies the package wiring.
close_reason: Completed: packaged cargo-crap 0.2.2 for the maintainer shell only, documented the maintainer/runtime boundary, built the default dev shell, and smoke-tested both cargo-crap --version and cargo crap --version.

title: Define the v14 release gate around boundary hardening and honest Core-readiness
description: Capture when Yazelix should actually jump to v14. The major bump should mean the workspace/backend boundary is real enough that a narrower Core-style omission mode would be honest, even if Yazelix still ships as one product. Treat v14 as a boundary-hardening release gate, not as a packaging milestone.
design: Treat v14 as a product-era decision about architectural honesty rather than distribution prestige. The gate should answer: which workspace flows are now proven to survive without backend ownership, whether install/update/doctor can degrade honestly in a narrowed mode, and which remaining cross-language hot paths still prevent a clean workspace-vs-backend split. Do not make nixpkgs packaging a required part of this gate while packaging remains explicitly low priority.
acceptance_criteria: There is an explicit durable definition of what must be true before v14: the required boundary-hardening milestones, the honest Core-readiness expectations, and the concrete remaining beads/issues that satisfy that gate. The decision is specific enough that future release planning can say whether v14 is actually earned without leaning on packaging progress.
notes:
2026-04-09 rescope:
- Packaging and nixpkgs submission are no longer part of the v14 gate.
- v14 now means workspace/backend boundary hardening and honest Core-readiness, not packaged Yazelix.
- The active gate is the remaining proof work around backend-free workspace UX, honest capability tiers, pane-orchestrator-owned workspace truth, sidebar-cache removal, and explicit runtime-identity seams.

Internal pipeline to follow:
1. Root and runtime-identity hygiene: `yazelix-le0s.6`.
2. Workspace truth migration branch: `yazelix-0hm3` -> `yazelix-3a0u`.
3. Honest capability-tier branch: `yazelix-zjyw`.
4. Backend-free workspace proof: `yazelix-5wao` after the two branch outputs and the root-identity cleanup.
5. v14 decision gate: close `yazelix-wg3z` only when the proof and boundary-hardening slices are complete.
6. Only after the v14 gate: Rust sequencing and slice ordering in `yazelix-kt5.1`, then later Rust implementation beads.
7. Only after the v14 gate and Core boundary proof: evaluate speculative Core backend candidates like `yazelix-qgj7.1`.
close_reason: Defined the v14 boundary-hardening gate as a durable spec tied to the completed runtime-identity, workspace-truth, capability-tier, and backend-free workspace proof slices.

title: Document configurable popup slots
description: Add user-facing docs for configuring Yazelix popup commands: bottom/top/menu named popup_commands, the unbound generic popup_program slot for an extra user popup keybinding, shared geometry, restart/apply model, and the fact that arbitrary popup_commands entries are not supported.
close_reason: Documented popup configuration in README, docs/zellij-configuration.md, and docs/customization.md. Clarified supported named popup slots, the unbound generic popup_program slot, example keybinding, one-off yzx popup commands, restart/apply behavior, unsupported arbitrary popup_commands entries, and that yzx config ui is Yazelix's ratconfig-backed JSONC settings editor. Verified docs validator, focused popup config tests, and git diff --check.

title: Evaluate support boundary for Lua-enabled Helix fork
description:
## Decision
Pending. Define the support boundary for the Lua-enabled Helix fork, including binary, runtime, config, and recovery behavior.

## Rationale
Custom Helix forks can be valuable, but bundled upstream Helix assumptions can break fork-only config fields. Yazelix needs a clear support story that avoids pretending reset/runtime changes are harmless.

## Alternatives Considered
Support only bundled upstream Helix; support user-provided Helix binaries/packages; add first-class package override docs; or add fork-specific config/runtime handling if evidence justifies it.

## Existing Context
Evaluate whether and how Yazelix should support the Lua-enabled Helix fork at https://github.com/gj1118/helix/. This came from the macOS/Home Manager tester report where the user runs a custom Helix fork and wants Yazelix to interoperate without forcing a bundled upstream Helix assumption.
acceptance_criteria: Decision defines the support boundary for the gj1118/helix fork: whether Yazelix should only support a user-provided editor binary/package, expose a first-class Helix package override, document Home Manager integration, or add runtime/config handling for fork-specific Lua behavior. It must check command compatibility with Yazelix editor flows, config/runtime path assumptions, health/check expectations, interaction with XDG integration work in yazelix-us42, and whether any implementation follow-ups are justified.
notes:
Issue 580 historical report: after yzx reset config, the tester hit Helix error: Bad config: unknown field rainbow-brackets. Likely cause: reset changed the editor/runtime back to bundled upstream Helix while the managed Helix config still contained fork-only rainbow-brackets = true. The original report mentioned old TOML paths, but the current canonical semantic settings path is ~/.config/yazelix/settings.jsonc and the current Helix sidecar path is ~/.config/yazelix/helix.toml. Support-boundary decision should cover binary, runtime, and config compatibility as one unit, and recovery docs should avoid presenting reset as harmless for users with custom Helix forks.

Reset-safety follow-up split out as yazelix-wq4j. Keep this bead focused on the broader support boundary for custom Helix forks; let yazelix-wq4j own reset/recovery messaging around adjacent managed overrides.

Prime-beads 2026-05-05: any Lua-Helix support analysis should target settings.jsonc plus the flat Helix sidecar path, not nested user_configs paths.

title: Audit main-repo simplifications for child-repo ownership transfers
description: Run a delete-first audit for simplifications similar to the yzpp popup cut: places where Yazelix main repo owns adapter code, wrapper code, validators, generated KDL/text, or lifecycle glue that could either be deleted outright or moved behind an existing child repo boundary. The audit should focus on reducing main-repo runtime LOC and ownership complexity, not creating new repos for cosmetic organization.
design: Use docs/loc_extraction_scorecard.md and docs/rust_code_inventory.md as inputs. Look first at existing child repos: yazelix-zellij-popup, yazelix-bar, yazelix-cursors, and yazelix-screen. For each candidate, record the current main-repo owner, whether the behavior is still needed, what can be deleted before extraction, what belongs in a child repo, what must remain as a thin Yazelix adapter, expected LOC/storage impact, verification surface, and risk. Bias toward existing child repos before proposing a new one.
acceptance_criteria: Produce a ranked audit report or bead notes with at least five concrete simplification candidates, each classified as delete-only, move-to-existing-child-repo, thin-adapter-refactor, or defer. For the top three candidates, create or update follow-up beads with scoped acceptance criteria and expected main-repo LOC impact. The audit must explicitly reject candidates that only move code around without reducing main-repo ownership.
notes: Completed master audit in docs/child_repo_simplification_audit.md. Ranked delete-only, move-to-existing-child-repo, thin-adapter, and defer candidates; refreshed docs/rust_code_inventory.md to the current 73,542 raw Rust line / 140 file budget; updated top follow-ups yazelix-lzlg.1 and yazelix-g7bs.2; created yazelix-00nz for the missing status-bar-to-yazelix_bar transfer candidate. The audit explicitly rejects paper extractions that preserve main-repo ownership through broad adapters or generated mirrors.
close_reason: Completed master audit, refreshed Rust inventory baseline, and created/updated ranked follow-up beads.

title: Normalize Rust formatting across both Rust workspaces
notes:
Rust dependency gate: no dependency changes. This is a repo-wide rustfmt normalization only. Production crates unchanged, dev-only crates unchanged, no in-house logic added, no rejected alternatives relevant beyond using rustfmt via the existing maintainer shell/toolchain.
Ran full rustfmt normalization with nix develop -c cargo fmt for rust_core and rust_plugins/zellij_pane_orchestrator, then verified with cargo fmt --check on both workspaces. This also cleared the immediate drift that the new file-scoped prek hooks would have flagged in touched files.
close_reason: Completed

title: Document fast verification lanes for runtime packaging work
description: Define the expected verification ladder for runtime/package work so agents avoid starting the full runtime build too early: focused Rust tests/checks, targeted Nix eval or flake checks for package contracts, optional single-package builds for touched KGP tools, and one final runtime_ghostty build after commits are ready. Include guidance to avoid parallel nix develop commands that contend on eval/cache locks.
close_reason: Completed: AGENTS.md now defines the runtime packaging verification ladder from focused Rust checks through fast KGP flake contracts, targeted single-package builds, and one final runtime_ghostty gate; it also calls out Nix lock contention from parallel validation. docs/architecture_map.md points KGP metadata work at the fast check.

title: Remove last period from agent usage widgets
description: Remove the ambiguous latest-session period from grouped Claude/Codex/OpenCode usage widgets before the grouped usage surface is pushed.
design: Keep only day and month in the supported usage-period enum/config contract. Delete session provider commands, cache targets, docs examples, and tests instead of preserving compatibility for the unpushed last value.
acceptance_criteria: zellij.*_usage_periods accepts day/month only, grouped usage rendering/tests no longer mention last, and maintainer/user default configs do not include last.
close_reason: Completed: grouped agent usage periods now support only day/month, session targets and provider commands were deleted, docs/defaults/Home Manager/config metadata were updated, and focused usage/config checks pass.

title: Make yzx reset config explicit about adjacent managed overrides
description: Define the reset-config contract for stray adjacent files in ~/.config/yazelix after the flat config move and settings.jsonc consolidation. The command should remove only Yazelix-owned generated/config files, warn clearly about adjacent user-managed files it will not touch, and avoid hiding config drift behind broad cleanup.
design:
Scope:
- Main semantic config: ~/.config/yazelix/settings.jsonc
- Current first-party sidecars: helix.toml, yazi.toml, yazi_keymap.toml, yazi_init.lua, zellij.kdl, terminal_*.conf|toml|ini, shell_*.sh|zsh|fish|nu
- Cursor settings now live in settings.jsonc and should not be treated as an adjacent sidecar file

Reset behavior should be conservative: delete or regenerate only the known Yazelix-owned set, and print explicit warnings for unknown adjacent files instead of deleting or adopting them. This protects users who intentionally keep notes, experiments, or imported configs in the same directory.
acceptance_criteria:
- reset-config has a documented allowlist for ~/.config/yazelix/settings.jsonc and current first-party sidecars.
- Unknown adjacent files under ~/.config/yazelix are preserved and surfaced in a clear warning.
- Cursor data is handled as part of settings.jsonc, not as a separate cursor sidecar.
- Tests or validator coverage defend preservation of unknown files and cleanup/regeneration of owned files.
- Documentation explains what reset-config owns and what it intentionally leaves alone.
notes: Planning update 2026-05-05: older notes referenced nested user_configs paths. Current flat paths apply, and the future canonical semantic settings target is settings.jsonc per yazelix-tlzg. This bead should remain about reset safety and sidecar warnings rather than reopening the file-format decision.
close_reason: Completed: yzx reset config now replaces only settings.jsonc, warns about preserved managed sidecars, legacy inputs, and unknown adjacent files, treats cursors as part of settings.jsonc, and removes stale yzx edit cursors / yzx reset cursor write surfaces.

title: Block weak cosmetic tests from governed suites
description: Weak tests can satisfy the current mechanical lane floor while still defending implementation trivia instead of real behavior. The Mandelbrot palette assertion is the concrete example: it checks color-code implementation details rather than a user-visible contract and should not have been created.
design: Raise the bar for governed tests without turning the rubric into a blind number game. Prefer deleting weak tests, strengthening them into behavior/regression contracts, or documenting an explicit exception in the linked bead/spec. Dependency decision: implement any validator changes in-house/std; no production or dev crates expected.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Update test-governance policy so new governed Rust/Nu tests below 8/10 are rejected by default, with any exception requiring an explicit bead/spec rationale. 2. Add or update repository validation so sub-8 governed tests fail mechanically. 3. Add rubric guidance that cosmetic/implementation-trivia assertions, such as exact palette constants, help-output trivia, or command-name discovery, are not sufficient even if scored 8. 4. Audit existing governed tests below 8 and either delete them, strengthen them, or record an explicit exception. 5. Delete or replace the Mandelbrot palette test with a behavior-level renderer contract.
close_reason: Completed: governed test validator now requires 8/10 by default across lanes, supports durable Strength exception markers, and docs/AGENTS describe the policy.

title: Workspace extraction gate phase 2
description: Thin zellij_commands/workspace.rs, workspace_commands.rs, and launch/session glue until a future yazelix_workspace extraction would not drag unrelated Yazelix runtime ownership with it.
design: Boundary-first workspace pass, but deletion-first when possible. Keep runtime/env/Yazi/sidebar policy local. Isolate pure workspace/session request shaping, terminal/editor open payloads, and command adapter glue only when it reduces coupling or removes duplication. Do not start public workspace extraction in this bead.
acceptance_criteria: Workspace command boundaries are thinner or duplicate request/session shaping is deleted; future yazelix_workspace extraction blockers are clarified; focused workspace/Zellij command tests pass; Rust budget and inventory are updated; LOC delta and rejected candidates are recorded.
close_reason: Completed: deleted duplicate workspace tab-name, target-directory, and retarget JSON shaping from workspace adapters by using workspace_session.rs as the private request/response boundary. zellij_commands/workspace.rs dropped 885 -> 864, workspace_commands.rs 361 -> 338, total Rust budget dropped by 33 raw lines. Rejected moving zoxide/path resolution, runtime facts, Yazi/sidebar sync, and popup policy because those are still Yazelix-owned adapters.

title: Refactor yzx screen around a Rust-owned framebuffer renderer
description: yzx screen rendering bugs keep recurring because front_door_render.rs still assembles animation frames directly as strings and glyph-specific terminal output. Introduce a Rust-owned internal framebuffer/render contract for yzx screen so styles render into explicit cells before flushing to the terminal. Keep the backend focused on the current front-door screen surface rather than turning Yazelix into a full TUI app.
notes:
Rust dependency gate: default to an in-house framebuffer/cell model plus the existing crossterm backend. Production crates: keep current crossterm usage; add no new production crate by default. Dev-only crates: none required initially. Rejected alternative: ratatui for the first pass, because yzx screen is a fullscreen animation surface rather than a widget-heavy TUI and ratatui would add framework surface without solving row-gap or glyph-contract bugs by itself. Revisit ratatui only if yzx screen later needs overlays, controls, inspectors, or other real application-style widgets.
Implemented first framebuffer slice for Game of Life: introduced explicit ScreenFrame/ScreenCell rendering, removed quadrant glyph packing for this path, and render live cells as 2x2 full-block tiles so canonical glider silhouettes survive terminals with row gaps or odd block glyph metrics. Verification: nix develop -c cargo fmt --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml --all; nix develop -c cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core front_door_render::tests -- --nocapture.
Second framebuffer slice: replaced 2x2 full-block Game of Life renderer with braille-packed cells. Each live cell now paints a 2x2 braille-dot tile, preserving glider shape without making welcome-screen gliders double-height. Also removed the package-breaking Home Manager option metadata integration test from the Cargo default test set; the stronger Nix-eval metadata check remains in the maintainer config-surface validator. Verification: nix develop -c cargo fmt --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml --all; nix develop -c cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core front_door_render::tests -- --nocapture; nix build .#runtime --no-link; nix develop -c cargo run --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core --bin yzx_repo_validator -- validate-config-surface-contract.
Third renderer slice: restored the old Nushell Game of Life visual contract after braille proved too tiny and still visually gappy. Rust now uses grid_width = inner_width / 2, grid_height = body_height, one life row per terminal row, and two full-block glyphs per live cell. Added regressions for the full-block glider silhouette and no inserted renderer rows. Verification: nix develop -c cargo fmt --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml --all; nix develop -c cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core front_door_render::tests -- --nocapture; nix build .#runtime --no-link.
Fourth renderer slice: added a real core.game_of_life_cell_style option with full_block default and dotted as a hardcoded scale-4 braille texture. Both styles keep the same two-terminal-columns by one-terminal-row footprint, so dotted cannot shrink gliders or insert empty rows. Also fixed yzx screen raw alternate-screen output by disabling autowrap while in screen mode and rendering each frame with absolute row cursor addresses instead of println newlines; this targets the every-other-row skipping seen only in yzx screen. Verification: nix develop -c cargo fmt --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml --all; nix develop -c cargo fmt --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml --all -- --check; nix develop -c cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core front_door_render; nix develop -c cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core normalizes_default_config_with_parser_keys_and_transforms; nix develop -c cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core --test yzx_core_owned_facts startup_facts_compute_reports_retained_startup_and_session_fields; nix develop -c cargo run --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core --bin yzx_repo_validator -- validate-config-surface-contract.
close_reason: Completed: current front-door renderer uses the Rust framebuffer/screen-frame contract for Game of Life, preserves glider footprint without inserted row gaps, supports full_block/dotted styles, and yzx screen writes absolute rows without newline-driven skipping.

title: Implement boids screen as a scaled yazelix_screen animation surface
description: Add a real boids implementation for yzx screen on top of the internal yazelix_screen framebuffer and animation extension seam. Replace canned boids frames with deterministic boid state updates and scaled terminal rendering that preserves readable size in both yzx screen and welcome contexts.
design: Do not hardcode a tiny braille-only visual. Use the screen frame-producer contract from yazelix-subsys.1.3, with deterministic positions/velocities, separation/alignment/cohesion steering, and a renderer style that can map to full_block or scaled dotted/braille cells without changing terminal-output internals. Keep the first pass O(n^2) unless profiling proves it is too slow.
acceptance_criteria: Boids render at a readable scale comparable to current Game of Life cells; no empty-line skipping appears in yzx screen; welcome-screen and screen-mode aspect ratios are explicitly checked; full_block and dotted-style rendering choices remain possible through the screen subsystem; deterministic tests cover state update and frame footprint.
notes:
Rust dependency gate: keep this in-house on top of the planned framebuffer work and current crossterm backend; do not add ratatui for the first pass. Production crates: reuse crossterm only. Dev-only crates: none required initially. External context: Craig Reynolds' steering-behavior writeup describes flocking as the combination of separation, alignment, and cohesion behaviors, which should drive the simulation model (https://www.red3d.com/cwr/steer/gdc99/index.html). Unicode Braille Patterns U+2800..U+28FF provide 256 glyphs, which makes a 2x4 subcell packer a better fit than coarse arrow glyphs for sparse moving boids (https://unicode.org/charts/PDF/U2800.pdf). Crossterm already provides cursor movement and terminal control commands, so the renderer can stay on the current terminal backend without a TUI framework (https://docs.rs/crossterm/latest/crossterm/cursor/). Inference from those sources: implement a BoidsScreenState with deterministic seed, velocity/position updates, simple O(n^2) steering first, braille-packed framebuffer rendering, and optional short trails; revisit ratatui only if yzx screen later needs overlays or app-style widgets.
2026-04-26 subsystem review: boids should build on the new internal yazelix_screen crate rather than expanding front_door_render.rs further. This bead is now blocked by yazelix-subsys.1 so the simulation lands against the stable screen/framebuffer subsystem boundary.
Rust dependency gate update before implementation: keep the boids simulation in-house in yazelix_screen on top of ScreenFrameProducer and the existing terminal framebuffer. Production crates: no additions beyond crossterm already owned by yazelix_screen. Dev-only crates: none. In-house logic: deterministic boid initialization, separation/alignment/cohesion update, speed limiting, wraparound, and scaled full_block/dotted rendering. Rejected alternatives: random-number crates are unnecessary for deterministic startup visuals; spatial-index crates are premature for the small flock size; ratatui remains unnecessary because this is an animation producer, not an app UI. Packaging impact: no new external dependencies or binaries.
Implemented: added deterministic in-house BoidsAnimation to yazelix_screen as a ScreenFrameProducer. yzx screen boids now uses live boid state with separation/alignment/cohesion, speed limiting, wraparound, resize support, and scaled 2-column by 1-row full_block/dotted rendering through the shared framebuffer. The old canned boids frames remain only for the welcome animation. Verification: cargo fmt --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml --all -- --check; cargo check --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core -p yazelix_screen; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_screen; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core front_door_render; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core --test yzx_control_front_door.
close_reason: Completed: yzx screen boids now uses scaled deterministic yazelix_screen animation instead of canned frames.

title: Delete remaining devenv maintainer/test ownership from the v15 repo
description: Yazelix v15 no longer uses devenv as a product or maintainer substrate, but the repo still contains maintainer scripts, fixtures, docs, and tests that read devenv.lock, mention repo-local devenv shells, or fabricate devenv-era checkout state. Remove those remaining seams instead of preserving them as compatibility language. This includes top-level maintainer shell artifacts if they are truly obsolete, the zjstatus update flow, test fixtures that still seed devenv.* files only to mimic old checkout state, and docs that still claim devenv.nix is the maintainer shell. The result should be that repo code, docs, and tests no longer depend on or describe devenv ownership at all.
close_reason: Completed

title: Add yzx uninstall for installer-owned Yazelix artifacts
description: Add a supported uninstall command for Yazelix. The default path should remove only installer-owned artifacts such as the stable yzx shim, runtime/current, and generated desktop integration, while preserving user-managed config by default. The command should detect Home Manager ownership and refuse or warn instead of deleting paths managed by Home Manager.
close_reason: Obsolete after package-runtime simplification: yzx uninstall was intentionally removed, and the surviving manual-to-Home-Manager migration need is already covered by yzx home_manager prepare.

title: Add yzx uninstall --all for explicit full cleanup
description: Extend the uninstall surface with an explicit yzx uninstall --all path that removes installer-owned artifacts plus Yazelix state and managed config. Keep this as an intentional destructive path, separate from the safe default uninstall behavior. Document exactly what yzx uninstall --all deletes and keep Home Manager ownership checks in place so Yazelix does not silently fight declarative ownership.
close_reason: Obsolete after package-runtime simplification: yzx uninstall was intentionally removed, and the surviving manual-to-Home-Manager migration need is already covered by yzx home_manager prepare.

title: Add a supported migration helper for manual-install to Home Manager takeover
description: Provide an explicit supported migration helper for users moving from manual flake install to Home Manager, so they do not have to guess which installer-owned artifacts to move or delete by hand. Reuse installer ownership detection from the uninstall work instead of inventing a second cleanup model.
design: Prefer one ownership engine. If the right UX is a dedicated yzx migrate-to-home-manager command, make it a thin flow over the same installer-owned artifact detection used by yzx uninstall. If a narrower uninstall submode is better, keep the command surface explicit and document exactly which paths it cleans up before Home Manager takes over. Non-goal: deleting Home Manager-managed paths or silently touching user-owned config.
acceptance_criteria: There is a supported command-driven migration path from manual install to Home Manager takeover that removes or hands off installer-owned artifacts blocking Home Manager, especially ~/.local/bin/yzx, without asking the user to guess cleanup steps. The implementation shares ownership logic with the uninstall surface instead of duplicating path rules.
notes: Keep this as the fallback/secondary path for `yazelix-isdn`, not the first design choice. The preferred takeover fix is to stop making the Home Manager path own `~/.local/bin/yzx` at all. Use this helper bead only if the ownership-boundary cleanup proves too disruptive or leaves unavoidable manual-install takeover gaps.
close_reason: Implemented via yzx home_manager prepare and shared uninstall ownership helpers.

title: Default agent usage widgets to tokens only
description: The grouped Codex/Claude/OpenCode usage widgets currently default to showing derived dollar estimates. For subscription-backed products like Codex and Claude, those dollars are model-pricing estimates, not the user's monthly bill, so the default bar is misleading.

title: Move yzx status to Rust public owner cut
description: Move yzx status from InternalNu routing to RustControl in yzx.rs, making Rust the single public owner for parsing, help, and execution. status.compute is already Rust-owned in yzx_core. This follows the mixed-family pattern from the qsb5 beads (env/run/update). It deletes the public registry role of core/yazelix.nu for the status family.
notes:
2026-04-20 tracker hygiene: confirmed active. The current dirty tree contains the expected status-migration files: `rust_core/yazelix_core/src/bin/yzx.rs`, `rust_core/yazelix_core/src/bin/yzx_control.rs`, `rust_core/yazelix_core/src/status_report.rs`, `nushell/scripts/core/yazelix.nu`, `nushell/scripts/core/yzx_internal_dispatch.nu`, `nushell/scripts/utils/status_report.nu`, and the focused status regression test updates. The tree is still mixed with finished 0w1u.3.1 cleanup files and unrelated local changes, so the next commit should separate those lanes deliberately.
2026-04-20 reopen after verification: the state-path drift was fixed, but the public Rust-owned status surface still has contract regressions. `yzx status --help` and `yzx status --versions` both silently execute the default status path even though command metadata and docs still advertise those flags. Remaining work: keep status fully Rust-owned while restoring flag/help parity and collapsing request/path assembly onto one shared materialization-request builder.
2026-04-20 completion fixup: restored the remaining public status contract in the Rust owner. `yzx status` now parses `--help`, `--json`, and `--versions` explicitly; the optional versions report is emitted for both human and JSON modes; runtime-materialization request assembly now uses one shared control-plane helper with the canonical `state/rebuild_hash` path plus layout-override parity; and duplicate version-file parsing was removed by sharing `read_yazelix_version_from_runtime` between `yzx` and `yzx_control`. Verified with focused public-status Nu regressions, cargo check, cargo test for `yzx`, control-plane lib tests, validate_syntax, and git diff --check.
close_reason: Completed: Rust-owned yzx status now uses the canonical materialized-state/layout request builder and restores help/versions/json parity without reviving the old Nu owner

title: Thin workspace_commands.rs around Yazi/sidebar/popup adapters
description: Make a second pass on workspace_commands.rs after workspace request/response shaping is private. cwd, reveal, popup, sidebar refresh, zoxide resolution, Yazi emit-to, and popup cwd policy are still bundled. Popup should stay yzpp-owned; Yazi/sidebar sync should become thinner and better isolated.
design: Keep public yzx cwd/reveal/popup/sidebar behavior stable. Do not pull yzpp popup ownership back into workspace. Split or delete only where the adapter boundary becomes clearer: path/zoxide resolution, Yazi emit-to, sidebar refresh, popup cwd policy, managed editor detection, and user-facing command rendering. Avoid creating a generic workspace child surface until the adapter is measurably thin.
acceptance_criteria: workspace_commands.rs has a smaller responsibility surface; popup remains yzpp-owned; Yazi/sidebar sync is isolated from workspace request shaping; public commands and messages stay stable unless deliberately simplified; weak tests defending old structure are deleted or replaced with behavior checks; focused workspace/Yazi/popup tests pass; LOC delta and rejected candidates are recorded.
notes: Implementation 2026-05-08: Split workspace_commands.rs from 899 to 361 lines. Added private workspace_commands/popup.rs for yzpp-backed popup toggle/open, popup cwd policy, runtime env forwarding, and sidebar-refresh close hook. Added private workspace_commands/yazi_sidebar.rs for yzx reveal, yzx sidebar refresh, active sidebar lookup, sidebar focus, command availability, and ya emit-to. Parent workspace_commands.rs now keeps yzx cwd, workspace command config loading, managed editor kind detection, zoxide/path resolution, path expansion, and current-tab retargeting. Rejected candidates: did not move zoxide/path resolution into workspace_session because it is Yazelix product adapter policy; did not pull yzpp popup ownership back into workspace extraction; did not create a public workspace package; did not split status/cache further in this bead. LOC: 580 insertions / 550 deletions excluding .beads, net +30 raw Rust lines; tokei Rust code 66,645, up 22. Accepted as organization debt rather than a Spartan deletion win. Verification: cargo fmt --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml --all -- --check; cargo check --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core --all-targets; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core workspace_commands; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core --test yzx_control_workspace_surface; validate-rust-ownership-budget; validate-contracts; validate-workspace-session-contract; validate-docs-experience; git diff --check.
close_reason: Completed: split workspace_commands.rs into private yzpp popup and Yazi/sidebar adapters, kept public behavior stable, updated workspace extraction contract and Rust inventory, and verified focused workspace gates.

title: Add CI check for README version drift

title: investigate strong flicker when opening a new tab
description:
Imported GitHub issue #468.

### Details

investigate strong flicker when opening a new tab
notes:
Resolved on 2026-03-29:
- Root cause was plugin-driven pane-frame toggling in zjstatus, not tab creation itself.
- Yazelix already enforced `pane_frames false` in the merged Zellij config, while the zjstatus tab template also enabled `hide_frame_for_single_pane "true"`.
- On Zellij 0.44.0 this interaction caused strong flicker/glitching when opening a new tab.
- Fix: remove `hide_frame_for_single_pane` from the managed zjstatus template and from the sweep-test layout fixture so frame ownership stays with the merged Zellij config.
- Verified locally by opening a new tab after the change and by rerunning focused merged-Zellij generation tests.
close_reason: Completed: removed zjstatus frame toggling from the managed template after confirming it was the root cause of the new-tab flicker on Zellij 0.44.0.

title: Drive Yazelix from 7.8k Nu to the 4.2k hard floor
description: Delete the remaining honest Nushell owners after the lj7z tranche so Yazelix can reach the current 4.2k hard floor and then re-evaluate whether that floor is now too loose.
design: This epic is delete-first. Rust should absorb deterministic planning, validation, request shaping, and report generation. Nushell may survive only where it remains the irreducible shell, TTY, process, or external-tool boundary. The tranche ends with a measured decision on whether the canonical hard Nu floor should ratchet below 4.2k.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Remaining major Nu families are split into explicit deletion-budget and implementation beads. 2. No lane is allowed to add fake Rust wrappers above unchanged Nu owners. 3. The tranche ends with a measured floor evaluation that either lowers the hard Nu ceiling below 4.2k or records an explicit stop condition.
notes:
2026-04-24: Completed the remaining Rust-rewrite / Nu-deletion backlog. The Yazi/editor integration family is now Rust-owned through `yzx_control zellij open-editor` and `open-editor-cwd`, the stale integration Nu owners are deleted, and the canonical Nushell hard target is lowered below 4.2k to 4,150 LOC after measuring 4,143 LOC across 25 `nushell/scripts/**/*.nu` files. Validators passed: `cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/yazelix_core/Cargo.toml --test yzx_control_workspace_surface`, `cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/yazelix_core/Cargo.toml --test yzx_core_yazi_materialization`, `yzx_repo_validator validate-nushell-budget`, `yzx_repo_validator validate-specs`, and `yzx_repo_validator validate-nushell-syntax`.
2026-04-24: The 4,150 LOC ceiling closed the xc82 tranche honestly, but it is not the final irreducible Nushell stop condition. Follow-up epic `yazelix-pw9j` continues the deletion program from the measured 4,143 LOC floor down to the final shell-only core.
close_reason: Completed

title: Defend pane-orchestrator sync freshness in maintainer checks
description:
Why this issue exists and what needs to be done

Pane-orchestrator Rust edits are not live until the wasm is rebuilt and synced, but the repo still relies heavily on maintainer discipline to remember that boundary. That makes it easy to ship code changes that are correct in source but stale in tracked or runtime plugin artifacts.

Add a defended maintainer workflow that makes stale pane-orchestrator sync state visible early, ideally through validator coverage or another explicit maintainer check, so source edits and synced wasm paths stop drifting silently.
close_reason: Completed: build_pane_orchestrator --sync now writes a tracked source/wasm sync stamp, validator detects stale source or wasm drift, and maintainer tests run the check before functional suites.

title: Add maintainer pack for local maintainer tooling
description: Imported GitHub issue #453 after deciding that maintainer tooling should be an opt-in pack rather than a special yzx dev mode. Add a top-level maintainer pack for repo maintenance tools that are meaningfully tied to maintaining Yazelix itself, starting with gh, prek, tru, beads-rust, and beads-viewer. Keep runtime behavior unchanged, sync the default config and Home Manager module, document the pack in the collection/docs, and add a focused regression test that verifies the pack stays aligned across configuration paths.
close_reason: Added an opt-in maintainer pack with gh, prek, tru, beads-rust, and beads-viewer; synced the default config, Home Manager module, and collection docs; and added a regression test that keeps the pack aligned across both configuration paths.

The immediate goal is not to support a second backend in main; it is to make current devenv-specific assumptions explicit, shrink accidental coupling, and leave behind a clearer seam for later experiments or a Core edition.
