title: Restrict NixCI to shipped Yazelix outputs
close_reason: Completed

title: Evaluate editor and Yazi popup workspace options
description: Evaluate two possible popup workspace surfaces: an editor popup and a Yazi popup picker. Yazi popup could become the picker path if Yazelix brings back a no-sidebar layout. Editor popup could be useful if open_from_yazi can target it instead of creating an editor pane inside the stack. The decision must weigh whether these popups make the workspace cleaner against keybinding cost, discoverability, user memory burden, pane-stack behavior, and layout complexity.
design: Treat this as a product and workspace-architecture decision before implementation. Compare at least: keep current editor/sidebar model, add only Yazi popup picker, add only editor popup, add both popups, or reject both. Reason from first principles around what each popup replaces, whether no-sidebar layouts come back, how open_from_yazi should route selected files, whether editor popups create less pane-stack drift than stack insertion, and whether extra keybindings are worth the cognitive cost. Keep the result compatible with Yazelix's Zellij keybinding rule and avoid adding a popup unless it removes real ownership or interaction complexity.
acceptance_criteria: Decision records whether Yazelix should add an editor popup, a Yazi popup picker, both, or neither. It explains the keybinding/discoverability tradeoff, how no-sidebar layouts affect the Yazi picker case, how open_from_yazi would behave for editor popup routing, what happens to existing editor/sidebar/stack behavior, and what implementation beads should exist if any option is accepted.
notes: Decision: reject both default editor popup and default Yazi popup picker for the current sidebar-first workspace. Keep the existing managed editor/sidebar model. Yazi-driven opens, including open_from_yazi flows, continue to target or create the managed editor pane. Yazi popup picker is only worth reconsidering as a replacement surface if a no-sidebar layout contract is accepted. Editor popup would require a separate managed-editor identity contract before it could replace stack insertion. No implementation beads are needed now; future accepted no-sidebar work should create a dedicated Yazi popup picker bead.
close_reason: Completed: recorded reject-both-for-now decision in docs/contracts/floating_tui_panes.md and bead notes. Current model keeps the persistent managed editor/sidebar flow; open_from_yazi keeps targeting or creating the managed editor pane; Yazi popup picker is deferred until a real no-sidebar layout contract exists; no implementation beads are needed now. Verified validate-contracts and validate-docs-experience.

title: Detect non-Steel managed Helix in doctor
description: yzx doctor should detect when Yazelix Steel config is present but the managed Helix binary was packaged without Steel support. This catches runtime/package mismatches like missing Steel plugin commands before users have to discover them inside Helix. Acceptance: Helix doctor checks the active managed hx binary for a Steel capability marker without launching interactive Helix; reports a clear actionable failure when bundled Steel config exists but hx lacks Steel support; focused regression coverage uses fake hx binaries with and without the marker.
close_reason: Completed: Helix doctor now inspects runtime/libexec/hx for the HELIX_STEEL_CONFIG Steel capability marker whenever generated Steel entrypoints exist. Missing marker reports an actionable error to update or rebuild the Yazelix runtime. Added regression coverage with fake Steel-enabled and non-Steel hx binaries. Verification: cargo fmt --check; cargo test -p yazelix_core doctor_helix_report; YAZELIX_RUNTIME_DIR=/home/lucca/pjs/yazelix cargo run -p yazelix_core --bin yzx -- doctor helix-steel --json; cargo check -p yazelix_core --all-targets.

title: Make it so that the starter window does not make yazelix open in a half window,
description:
Imported GitHub issue #573.

because this makes the welcome screens animation uglier, and tinier, and awkward after the starter window closes and the half window becomes full width

also follow up: make animated welcome screens better on half windows (or verify)
and also: same thing but for restart logic. try to make the old window vanish before the new one appears or similar strategy
notes: Implemented local fix pending manual desktop-window validation: desktop launch keeps the terminal-backed starter for visible preflight/failure feedback, but the success path now schedules the managed terminal via shells/posix/desktop_deferred_launch_probe.sh after the starter process exits. Updated desktop_launch_visible_feedback contract, unreleased notes/changelog, source-checkout chmod setup, and installed-runtime validator coverage. Local gates passed: focused Rust helper regression test, yzx dev rust check all, sh -n helper, validate-nushell-syntax, validate-rust-test-traceability, validate-contracts, validate-upgrade-contract, validate-installed-runtime-contract.
close_reason: Completed: desktop launch now defers the managed Yazelix terminal until after the terminal-backed starter exits, while preserving visible preflight/error feedback. Manual test confirmed the starter-window error path.

title: Add Cachix cache-hit verification for Yazelix installs
description: Add a documented or command-backed way to confirm the Yazelix binary cache is configured and whether a representative package can substitute instead of build locally.
design: Prefer a cheap read-only check such as nix path-info or dry-run substitution evidence. Explain whether failure means missing Cachix configuration, missing public cache trust, an uncached revision, or an expected local build.
acceptance_criteria: Users can run one documented check to see cache configuration and representative substitution status, and the failure output points to the right repair path without promising every revision is cached.
close_reason: Completed: documented a read-only Cachix verification snippet that checks configured substituter/key entries and queries the public cache for the representative KGP Zellij output path, with failure guidance for missing configuration versus uncached revisions. Verified the local output-path cache query with nix path-info --store https://yazelix.cachix.org.

title: Show only major releases in README latest block
description: Change the generated README Latest Tagged Releases block to show the latest major release tags only, e.g. v16 and v15, instead of point releases like v16.1.
acceptance_criteria: README Latest Tagged Releases shows v16 and v15, no v16.1; sync-readme-surface keeps that output; validate-readme-version passes.
notes: Implemented major-only README latest release selection. The generator now filters release entries to pure major tags, so v16.1 resolves to the v16/v15 block; README rendering trims trailing periods from generated bullet text to preserve README style. Verification: sync-readme-surface --version v16.1, cargo fmt --check, validate-readme-version, validate-upgrade-contract, cargo test -p yazelix_core repo_contract_validation, and git diff --check.
close_reason: Completed

title: Fix missing Yazelix plugin permission popup on fresh Zellij startup
description: On fresh Yazelix launches under Zellij 0.44, the pane orchestrator/popup runner permission popup may never appear even after clearing Zellij runtime/cache/share state. Only zjstatus gets a persisted permission block, while Yazelix plugins remain ungranted until permissions are seeded manually in permissions.kdl.
notes: Added repo-level recovery path on 2026-03-27: `yzx repair zellij-permissions` now seeds the tracked/runtime permission blocks for the pane orchestrator and popup runner into ~/.cache/zellij/permissions.kdl and tells the user to restart Yazelix. This is an explicit recovery/workaround path, not the root-cause fix for the missing popup.
close_reason: Superseded by the startup-handoff hardening and permission recovery work on 2026-03-27. The missing Yazelix plugin permission popup was a downstream symptom of the broken Zellij startup path; commit 5043aeb removed the boot-time plugin IPC and duplicate launch-tree hazard, and commit 142e4c8 added an explicit recovery path for stale permission state.

title: Simplify workspace bar widget to root name
description: Make the zjstatus workspace widget render only the workspace root name. Keep focus context and sidebar state in the status cache for diagnostics, but remove them from the user-facing bar label.
notes: Dependency decision: no production or dev crates. This is in-house display formatting plus a template interval tweak. Workspace widget should render only the root basename, while focus/sidebar facts remain in status cache for diagnostics. Workspace command interval should be fast enough for interactive tab/sidebar changes, so use 1s; keep expensive AI usage provider polling at 120s.
close_reason: Completed: workspace bar widget now renders only the root basename, and the workspace cache command refreshes every 1s while keeping focus/sidebar facts in the status cache.

title: Implement schema-backed explicit config discovery
description: Implement the accepted settings direction from yazelix-8x3x and yazelix-tlzg: solve stale user configs through a schema-backed ~/.config/yazelix/settings.jsonc surface and a strict old-format migration gate. Users with sparse settings.jsonc should still see every current setting as set, defaulted/unset, new, deprecated, invalid, or Home Manager-owned. Old yazelix.toml/cursors.toml inputs are auto-migrated when safe or rejected with clear errors when not.
design:
Core model after yazelix-8x3x and yazelix-tlzg:

- Canonical durable settings file target: ~/.config/yazelix/settings.jsonc.
- Keys remain snake_case.
- JSON Schema metadata is the source for defaults, descriptions, validation, deprecations, newness, UI grouping, restart requirements, Home Manager mapping, and sidecar status affordances.
- The effective settings graph must include all current main config fields plus first-class cursor settings/presets.
- Existing ~/.config/yazelix/yazelix.toml and ~/.config/yazelix/cursors.toml are migration inputs only, not a supported long-lived runtime path.
- Migration policy is auto-or-error: auto-migrate when conversion is unambiguous and safe; hard-error when ambiguous, unsafe, symlink/Home Manager-owned, invalid, unreadable, or conflicting.
- Once settings.jsonc exists, old TOML settings inputs are not read as runtime settings. Their presence should be cleaned up by migration or reported as a stale-surface diagnostic unless they were explicitly moved to backup/migrated paths.
- Permanent raw sidecars stay outside semantic settings: helix.toml, zellij.kdl, yazi.toml, yazi_keymap.toml, yazi_init.lua, terminal_* snippets, and shell_* hooks.
- Missing fields in settings.jsonc should render as defaulted/unset, not disappear.
- Saving should default to delta mode, writing only user-changed semantic settings. A materialize/export mode may write explicit defaults intentionally.
- Startup may perform the one-time safe migration, but must not silently rewrite existing settings.jsonc just to expand defaults.
- Comment preservation is desirable for user-authored settings.jsonc, but the durable documentation source is schema/reference/UI text.

Implementation slice should include:

1. Schema artifact or generator for the accepted settings surface.
2. JSONC parse/normalize path to the existing effective config shape.
3. One-time migration gate from yazelix.toml and cursors.toml to settings.jsonc.
4. Safe migration behavior: write settings.jsonc, preserve/move old inputs to timestamped backup or migrated paths, and avoid deleting user data.
5. Hard-error diagnostics for unsafe migration cases, including Home Manager-owned old symlinks and duplicate/conflicting current/old settings surfaces.
6. Home Manager manage_config output path update to settings.jsonc, with documented comment limitations.
7. yzx config/edit/reset/doctor/status/docs updates for the new canonical file and migration diagnostics.
8. Regression tests proving stale settings.jsonc discovery and migration/hard-error behavior for old TOML/cursors inputs.

Rust dependency gate for the implementation pass: decide the exact JSONC parser and JSON Schema validation strategy before code edits. Bias toward a focused JSONC parser that can produce serde_json::Value and a narrow schema/validation path over a broad framework. Generated output should be conservative JSONC without trailing commas even if the parser accepts them.
acceptance_criteria:
- ~/.config/yazelix/settings.jsonc is the canonical durable settings target, with snake_case keys.
- JSON Schema metadata exists for the current semantic settings surface, including top-level UI grouping and first-class cursor settings.
- Sparse settings.jsonc files normalize through the existing defaults and validation contract.
- Old mutable yazelix.toml/cursors.toml inputs auto-migrate when unambiguous and safe.
- Old mutable yazelix.toml/cursors.toml inputs hard-error with actionable diagnostics when migration is unsafe, ambiguous, Home Manager-owned, invalid, unreadable, stale next to settings.jsonc, or conflicting.
- Once settings.jsonc exists, old TOML settings inputs are not treated as long-lived runtime settings.
- First-run/reset/onboard behavior creates or points at settings.jsonc without deleting user data.
- Home Manager manage_config emits settings.jsonc and documents generated ownership/comment limitations.
- Docs and live contracts teach settings.jsonc as the current semantic settings surface, while historical release notes keep old names as history.
- Tests defend settings.jsonc creation, normalization, old-format auto-migration, hard-error cases, reset/onboard/edit/doctor behavior, runtime materialization, and Home Manager generated settings eval.
- Interactive config UI, delta-save policy, and richer defaulted/unset discovery UX remain in follow-up bead yazelix-ryx4.
notes:
Planning correction 2026-05-05: this bead must consume yazelix-8x3x and yazelix-tlzg. Do not assume the durable canonical settings file is TOML. If JSONC plus JSON Schema is accepted, build schema-backed discovery for the canonical settings surface, including current main config fields and first-class cursor settings/presets. Permanent raw sidecars remain outside the schema-backed semantic settings surface except for status/open/validate affordances.
Rust dependency decision 2026-05-05:

Production crates: add jsonc-parser with the serde feature for parsing ~/.config/yazelix/settings.jsonc into serde_json::Value. Keep serde/serde_json/toml as existing dependencies. Do not add a JSON Schema validation crate in this implementation slice; generate or ship schema metadata for editor/UI discovery, while runtime validation continues through the existing main_config_contract-driven normalizer adapted to JSON values.

Dev-only crates: no new dev crates expected.

In-house logic: JSON value to TOML-like effective config conversion, settings.jsonc rendering from default TOML/cursor TOML, auto-migration-or-hard-error policy, stale old-format surface diagnostics, and schema metadata generation/curation.

Rejected alternatives: ad hoc comment stripping before serde_json because JSONC parsing is subtle; json5/hjson parsers because the accepted format is JSONC; a full jsonschema validator crate because the existing contract normalizer already owns runtime semantics and a broad validator would add packaging surface before the UI needs it.

Packaging impact: one small Rust parser dependency in yazelix_core; no extra runtime binary dependency.
Implementation completed 2026-05-05:

- Added the Rust settings_surface owner for canonical ~/.config/yazelix/settings.jsonc, JSONC parsing via jsonc-parser, rendering from shipped main/cursor defaults, sparse JSONC-to-effective-config normalization, and safe old-TOML auto-migration with timestamped migrated files.
- Added config_metadata/yazelix_settings.schema.json with schema/UI metadata for the semantic settings surface and first-class cursors.
- Updated active config resolution, config-state hashing, doctor/config/reset/onboard/edit/cursors/runtime materialization, Home Manager manage_config, install-ownership prepare, session facts, and generated terminal/Yazi/Zellij copy to use settings.jsonc.
- Changed malformed settings.jsonc doctor handling back to a hard validation error instead of a stale-config warning.
- Updated README, docs, and live contracts so current docs use settings.jsonc; old yazelix.toml/cursors.toml references remain only for migration/history contexts.
- Follow-up scope: yazelix-ryx4 remains the config UI/delta-save/defaulted-unset discovery UX decision and implementation path.

Verification:
- cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/yazelix_core/Cargo.toml --tests -- --nocapture
- yzx dev rust fmt --check
- yzx dev rust check
- nix eval Home Manager manage_config settings.jsonc text
- git diff --check

Could not run yzx_repo_validator validate-contracts because yzx_repo_validator is not on the raw shell PATH and yzx run could not find it inside the Yazelix runtime tool surface.
close_reason: Completed settings.jsonc runtime/schema/migration foundation; config UI and delta-save UX remain tracked in yazelix-ryx4.

title: Add compact tab-label mode for root-widget workflows
description: Add an optional compact tab-label mode for the Yazelix/Zellij bar so tab labels consume less horizontal space. The mode should preserve the current default unless explicitly enabled, and it should work with root/workspace widgets instead of making the status line harder to scan.
design:
First-principles target:
- The default bar should remain readable for general users.
- Compact mode should be an explicit settings.jsonc option under the Zellij/status-bar settings surface if accepted.
- The renderer should shorten labels predictably rather than relying on terminal-width accidents.
- Interactions with root/workspace widgets, active tab highlighting, and codex/cpu/ram widgets should be evaluated together, because the user pain is horizontal crowding, not only tab text length.
- Avoid committing to a new abbreviation scheme until screenshots across narrow and normal widths show the tradeoff clearly.
acceptance_criteria:
- Current default tab-label behavior is documented or left unchanged.
- A concrete compact-label format is chosen with before/after screenshots or fixture output for narrow and normal widths.
- If implemented, compact mode is controlled through settings.jsonc and validated through the existing config/materialization path.
- zjstatus/yazelix-bar rendering is verified with active, inactive, and multiple-tab states.
- Documentation explains the option without implying users must enable it.
notes:
Rust dependency gate: implement in-house/std only. Compact tab labels are deterministic zjstatus format strings and config normalization already uses the existing TOML/JSON stack. No production or dev crate is needed. Rejected alternatives: using a new KDL parser just to rewrite six static tab-format lines, because the fragment/template pipeline already owns these strings; adding a zjstatus fork, because this is configurable plugin input rather than plugin logic.
Implementation 2026-05-05: added zellij.tab_label_mode with enum values full and compact. Full keeps current labels: tab_normal renders [{index}] {name}. Compact renders tab_normal as [{index}], fullscreen as [{index}] [], sync as [{index}] <>, and active labels as [{index}] plus the existing state indicator. tab_rename still includes {name} so interactive rename keeps its editing context. Verified focused Rust tests for yazelix_bar, config normalization, zellij render-plan normalization, compact layout rendering, the yzx_core zellij render-plan integration test, plus yzx_repo_validator validate-contracts and validate-config-surface-contract.
close_reason: Implemented zellij.tab_label_mode=full|compact through settings.jsonc, Home Manager, zjstatus rendering, docs, and focused tests.

title: Preserve Codex usage quota through transient probe failures
description: The Codex status widget can degrade to n/a after suspend, network interruption, or a temporary official quota probe failure even when the previous 5h/week quota windows are still valid. Track the status-cache behavior that preserves last-valid quota/reset facts during probe backoff while continuing to update local token totals.
acceptance_criteria: When the official quota probe fails during a valid prior quota window, the widget keeps rendering the previous 5h/week quota labels instead of collapsing to n/a. If there is no previous quota or the previous reset window has expired, n/a remains explicit. Regression tests cover quota preservation during backoff and partial token-cache rendering.
notes: Implemented locally in commit d4ca2afa Preserve Codex quota during probe failures; push remains separate from this planning repair.
close_reason: Completed locally in commit d4ca2afa with regression coverage; retained as historical tracking for the suspend/probe-failure cache bug.

title: Fix duplicated Yazi mgr.ratio in generated config
description: Specifying mgr.ratio in user_configs/yazi/yazi.toml duplicates the ratio values in the final generated Yazi config, preventing users from disabling the preview pane with a ratio such as [1, 4, 0].
acceptance_criteria: User-provided mgr.ratio is represented exactly once in the final Yazi config; disabling preview via mgr.ratio works; the merge path has a focused regression check; GitHub issue #593 has the canonical Beads tracking comment.
notes:
2026-05-18 implementation: changed native yazi.toml deep merge so user arrays replace generated defaults instead of appending. Kept yazi_keymap.toml on its separate append/prepend merge path. Added regression coverage for mgr.ratio = [1, 4, 0] and documented array replacement in docs/yazi-configuration.md. Verification: cargo fmt --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml --all --check; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core yazi_materialization; focused regression test yazi_materialization_generate_replaces_user_yazi_array_settings. Docs validator was not available on PATH via yzx_repo_validator or yzx run.
2026-05-18 additional verification: cargo run --quiet --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_maintainer --bin yzx_repo_validator -- validate-docs-experience passed.
close_reason: Completed: native yazi.toml sidecar arrays now replace generated defaults instead of appending, so mgr.ratio = [1, 4, 0] materializes exactly once. yazi_keymap.toml keeps its explicit append/prepend merge path. Verified with focused Yazi materialization tests and docs validator.

title: Fix Ghostty cursor effects ignoring the selected Yazelix trail palette
description:
Ghostty cursor movement/mode effect shaders are generated with iCurrentCursorColor as their color source. In practice this can resolve to the terminal theme cursor color rather than the Yazelix-selected ghostty_trail_color palette, making cursor effects look like the same purple trail even when the generated config points at a non-purple palette such as reef or ocean.

The generated Ghostty config and generated effect shaders need to share the same resolved palette value, including the single random choice for ghostty_trail_color = "random".
design: Resolve Ghostty cursor color/effect state once during terminal config generation. Render the Ghostty config from that state, and pass the resolved color literal into the generated effect shader builder. Replace both COLOR and TRAIL_COLOR declarations in vendored effect templates so tail/warp/sweep and ripple-style effects use the same Yazelix-selected color. Keep ghostty_trail_color = "none" on the existing theme/current-cursor path.
acceptance_criteria:
1. A fixed palette such as reef generates Ghostty config with the reef cursor color and generated effect shaders with the reef vec4 literal.
2. The movement effects that use TRAIL_COLOR and the mode effects that use COLOR are both covered.
3. ghostty_trail_color = "random" resolves once per generation so config and generated effect shaders cannot pick different colors.
4. Regression tests protect generated Ghostty config and generated shader color output.
close_reason: Fixed: Ghostty generated effect shaders now receive the same resolved Yazelix trail palette literal as the generated config, including single-resolution random color state; regression coverage asserts reef is embedded in both TRAIL_COLOR and COLOR shader styles.

title: Make zjstatus permission state survive runtime updates and relaunches
description: zjstatus can forget its granted permission state after a Yazelix runtime update and then prompt again on the next new window/relaunch. This is not just a desktop-entry bug. Track the broader permission-persistence problem across runtime updates, normal relaunches, and desktop launches so users do not end up with a broken transparent top bar or the Alt+Arrow + y recovery dance.
design: Treat this as a plugin-identity/permission-cache bug, not a prompt-UX quirk. Current evidence: Yazelix already preserves permission blocks for the pane orchestrator and popup runner in ~/.cache/zellij/permissions.kdl, but zjstatus is still loaded from a runtime-scoped wasm path and does not get the same preservation/seed treatment. A runtime update can therefore make Zellij see zjstatus as a new plugin and forget the prior grant. Fix the source of truth so zjstatus permission survives normal runtime updates and relaunches, and only prompt when the user truly has not granted it before. Record whether the fix is path stability, permission-cache migration, explicit seeding, or a combination.
acceptance_criteria:
1. After granting zjstatus permission once, a normal 🔄 Updating the installed Yazelix runtime...
🔄 Refreshing Yazelix shell hooks...
[36m🔧 Yazelix environment loaded! Launch the full interface in a separate terminal with 'yzx launch' or here with 'yzx launch --here'.[0m
🔄 Rebuilding generated Yazelix runtime configs...
🔄 Regenerating Zellij configuration...
Generated layout: /home/lucca/.local/share/yazelix/configs/zellij/layouts/yzx_no_side.kdl
Generated layout: /home/lucca/.local/share/yazelix/configs/zellij/layouts/yzx_no_side.swap.kdl
Generated layout: /home/lucca/.local/share/yazelix/configs/zellij/layouts/yzx_side.kdl
Generated layout: /home/lucca/.local/share/yazelix/configs/zellij/layouts/yzx_side.swap.kdl
Generated layout: /home/lucca/.local/share/yazelix/configs/zellij/layouts/yzx_sweep_test.kdl
📥 No user Zellij config found, fetching defaults...
✅ Zellij configuration generated successfully!
   📁 Config saved to: /home/lucca/.local/share/yazelix/configs/zellij/config.kdl
   🔄 Config will auto-regenerate when source files change
✅ Yazelix runtime installed.
   Runtime: /home/lucca/.local/share/yazelix/runtime/current -> /nix/store/c53jfvgfriqr2kxlc0a76izlvw387n7r-yazelix-runtime
   CLI: /home/lucca/.local/bin/yzx
   Config: /home/lucca/.config/yazelix

Next step:
  yzx launch followed by opening a new Yazelix window does not trigger a fresh zjstatus permission prompt. 2. Desktop-entry launches and ordinary relaunches behave the same way; already-granted zjstatus permission survives both. 3. The transparent top bar no longer breaks after runtime updates because zjstatus permission state is preserved. 4. The bead records the exact mechanism that now preserves the permission state.
close_reason: Completed: zjstatus now loads from a stable Yazelix plugin path and its granted permissions are preserved across runtime updates and relaunches.

title: Load zjstatus from a stable Yazelix plugin path
description: Stop generated Zellij layouts from loading zjstatus via a runtime store path. Copy the runtime-owned zjstatus.wasm into a stable Yazelix user-state plugin path and make generated layouts reference that stable plugin URL so runtime updates do not churn the plugin identity Zellij sees.
close_reason: Implemented stable zjstatus path and permission preservation

title: Preserve and seed zjstatus permission grants across updates
description: Extend the existing zellij permissions.kdl preservation logic to zjstatus so already-granted permissions migrate onto the current stable and tracked zjstatus paths. Keep required permissions explicit and do not mask genuine new-permission prompts.
close_reason: Implemented stable zjstatus path and permission preservation

title: Ghostty trail glow "none" still renders extreme glow
description:
Imported GitHub issue #466.

### Details

When glow is set to `none`, Yazelix still renders a very large spatial glow instead of disabling the effect.
close_reason: Completed: fixed Ghostty cursor glow mask math and normalized trail glow behavior so none/low/medium/high behave correctly across variants.

title: Flake desktop first launch still no-ops
description: After disabling Home Manager and testing the flake/manual desktop path, the first desktop launch still silently no-ops and the second launch works. Fix the flake/manual first-run desktop path, not just HM runtime-input refresh.
close_reason: Desktop launch now repairs generated runtime state before fast terminal handoff when flake/manual runtime state is stale; awaiting user flake desktop confirmation.

title: Evaluate Pretext-inspired text-first welcome screen
description: Use the Pretext site as inspiration for another strong Yazelix welcome-screen direction, focused on text-first composition rather than only boxed logo cards.
design:
Use the Pretext site as inspiration for a more text-forward Yazelix welcome treatment, but test the palette and copy hierarchy step by step instead of composing multiple experimental color systems at once.

Color findings from the shipped logo asset (assets/logo.png): the brand palette is red, green, yellow, blue, and purple. In practice, purple and red should stay out of readable body copy; they work better as wordmark or border accents. The next controlled trials should compare:
- Brand Calm: blue body base with yellow highlights and green only for special emphasis like SSH
- Matrix Clean: green body base with restrained yellow highlights

Preferred first trial: Matrix Clean, because it fits the terminal-native identity and the user explicitly likes that direction. Keep the wordmark multicolor, keep the border single-color, and keep the body text color system simple: one base color plus one accent color. Avoid alternating full-line palettes and avoid more than one or two highlighted terms per line.

This bead is specifically about the text card composition and palette, not about adding another animation engine.
acceptance_criteria: There is a documented, repo-backed color direction for the text-first welcome card that is derived from the shipped logo rather than ad hoc terminal guesses. At least one restrained palette variant is tested end to end on the real welcome card, with width-fit checks, readability checks on the actual background, and a clear keep/change decision before moving on to more experimentation. The implementation should preserve the existing welcome_style system and should not add extra color complexity unless a previous simpler variant has been evaluated and rejected.

title: Verify all Yazelix update and refresh commands against real installed/runtime flows
description: Audit and verify the real behavior of Yazelix update-related commands such as install, refresh, restart, desktop install, and update devenv. The goal is to catch cases where commands appear successful but leave stale runtime, hook, desktop, or generated-config state behind.
design: Treat update-related commands as a product system, not isolated commands. Verify the actual end-to-end effects of install, refresh, restart, desktop install, and update devenv against the real installed runtime, generated configs, shell hooks, desktop entry, and cached launch profile. The point is to catch stale-state failures where commands report success but leave the user on an old runtime, old desktop entry, old hook path, or mismatched generated config.
acceptance_criteria: There is an explicit audit of the supported update and refresh commands, with the real state each command is supposed to update. Commands that leave stale or misleading state have either been fixed or spun into concrete follow-up bugs. The result should make it much harder for Yazelix to report success while the user is still running an old runtime, old desktop asset, or stale generated config.
close_reason: Completed the end-to-end update audit for install, desktop install, refresh, restart, update devenv, and dev update; fixed the stale-shell, canary, and packaged update-surface bugs and spun remaining UX polish into follow-up beads.

title: Drop Alacritty and Foot support
design: Delete Alacritty and Foot from the supported terminal contract instead of keeping stale launch/config/doc surfaces. Keep historical changelog/upgrade-note references intact unless they describe current support.
acceptance_criteria: Supported terminal lists exclude alacritty and foot; launcher/preflight/materialization/user-config paths reject them as unsupported; generated config no longer creates Alacritty/Foot outputs; docs/current README/config surfaces list only Ghostty, Yazelix Terminal, WezTerm, Ratty, and Kitty; focused tests pass.
close_reason: Dropped Alacritty and Foot from the supported terminal contract, launch/materialization/native-config paths, generated terminal configs, current docs, schema, config UI metadata, and focused tests. Verified with JSON validation, fmt, cargo check, focused Rust tests, current-surface rg scan, and git diff --check.

title: Narrow backend comparison issue into a Pixi-first exploration lane
description: GitHub issue #518 asked for a broad backend comparison across devenv, mise, brioche, pixi, and prefix. That framing mixed tools from different layers. The outcome is to close the broad issue and keep the real exploration scoped to a Pixi-first experiment branch instead of a generic multi-backend bakeoff.
close_reason: Closed in favor of the narrower Pixi-first evaluation lane tracked separately in yazelix-qow.

title: Make runtime truth and recovery product-grade
description: Give users and tools one reliable way to understand what Yazelix is running, where its runtime/config came from, and what exact recovery action is needed when install, update, asset, or session state is wrong.
design: Do not add another broad doctor rewrite. First create a canonical machine-readable inspect surface, then let recovery/doctor rendering consume those facts. Keep the command surface small and human-oriented while preserving JSON output for agents, tests, and the future live docs site.
acceptance_criteria: Yazelix has a canonical runtime truth report; recovery diagnostics can explain stale assets, config ownership conflicts, update ownership, stale wasm/runtime files, and broken session state with exact next commands; the implementation improves user trust without adding persona presets or agent-specific command namespaces.
close_reason: all steps complete

title: Add yzx inspect --json runtime truth report
description: Add a canonical machine-readable runtime truth report that humans, yzx doctor/recovery code, tests, and future site examples can rely on instead of rediscovering Yazelix state through scattered probes.
design: Implement a focused inspect report, likely as yzx inspect --json, that reports the active yzx path, resolved runtime root, Yazelix version, installation/update ownership, config ownership/source, Home Manager evidence when relevant, stale runtime asset status, pane-orchestrator wasm sync stamp/status, selected tool versions, and active session/status facts when available. Keep presentation out of the JSON; render human text as a thin layer if needed.
acceptance_criteria: yzx inspect --json emits stable versioned JSON with documented fields; it works outside an active Zellij session and enriches the report when session facts are available; it does not mutate state; tests defend representative standalone, Home Manager, stale-asset, and no-active-session cases.
notes: 2026-04-26 implementation start: no new crates by default. Reuse existing serde/runtime/status/config ownership facts in yazelix_core; inspect should be a stable JSON aggregation/report surface, not another mutable doctor path.
close_reason: Completed: added yzx inspect --json runtime/config/install/generated-state/tool/session truth report with focused Rust coverage.

title: Add recovery-focused diagnostic fix plan
description: Turn common Yazelix failure states into an explicit recovery plan instead of forcing users to infer fixes from scattered doctor output or Nix/Home Manager errors.
design: Build on the inspect/runtime truth report rather than duplicating probes. The recovery surface can be yzx doctor --fix-plan --json, yzx doctor --recover-plan, or a small yzx recover command if that proves cleaner. Cover stale wasm/runtime assets, Home Manager/path-input update confusion, shadowed yzx wrappers, config ownership conflicts, missing runtime tools, and broken/stale Zellij sessions. Each finding should include severity, evidence, exact next command, and whether it is safe to run automatically.
acceptance_criteria: A recovery report exists in human and JSON form; it produces exact commands for the known high-friction failure states; it does not silently mutate user config; tests defend at least stale asset, Home Manager update-path confusion, shadowed yzx, and broken session examples.
notes: 2026-04-26 implementation start: no new crates. Recovery plan should reuse existing status/inspect facts and produce deterministic human/JSON guidance; it must not mutate files or user config.
close_reason: Completed: added non-mutating yzx doctor --fix-plan human/JSON recovery plans using existing doctor/runtime facts, with tests for stale assets, Home Manager ownership, shadowed launchers, and broken Zellij sessions.

title: Extract ratconfig into a reusable child repo
description: Extract the reusable ratatui config editor plus JSONC settings adapter into a first-party yazelix-ratconfig repository that other projects can use. If the current code is not ready, refactor it until the boundary is clean enough to extract. Yazelix should keep only its app-specific adapter duties: settings source discovery, Home Manager/user ownership reporting, runtime refresh/apply behavior, and Yazelix-specific metadata. The child repo should own generic config model/edit/render behavior, JSONC document patching primitives, deterministic migration operations, and reusable tests/docs.
design: Make extraction the goal, not a readiness question. Proceed in small verified slices: inventory, contract, boundary thinning, migration primitives, UI controls, child repo creation, main-repo consumption, cleanup. Prefer deleting main-repo ownership over moving complexity sideways. JSONC is the first-class adapter. TOML support is future evaluation, not part of the initial extraction unless it falls out naturally.
acceptance_criteria: A separate first-party yazelix-ratconfig repo exists and owns reusable ratconfig code for config UI plus JSONC settings editing. Yazelix consumes it through a normal child-repo dependency path and retains only Yazelix-specific IO/apply/runtime wiring. Main-repo moved code is deleted rather than duplicated. Focused tests pass in both repos, Yazelix validators pass, docs describe the new boundary, and follow-up beads capture deferred adapters such as TOML.
notes:
Follow-up to closed yazelix-zap7 and current ratconfig migration direction in yazelix-hq87.

2026-05-22 Frankentui evaluation from yazelix-gnflf: do not adopt or integrate Frankentui. Keep yazelix-ratconfig as the reusable Ratatui config editor owner. Useful ideas to consider separately are terminal lifecycle cleanup checks, deterministic snapshots, conservative Zellij/mux behavior, and minimal focus/accessibility metadata. Follow-up yazelix-0ytzl tracks that checklist without changing the framework boundary.
close_reason: Completed: yazelix-ratconfig now exists as a first-party child repo, owns the reusable Ratatui config model/editor/render core, JSONC patcher, migration primitives, tests, and project-agnostic docs. Yazelix consumes the published child crate through Cargo/Nix metadata, deletes the old in-repo reusable module, and keeps only Yazelix-specific schema, Home Manager/native status, validation, persistence, keybinding detail, and runtime apply adapters. Verification across child and main included child cargo test, main cargo check/config UI/settings JSONC tests, validate-config-surface-contract, validate-contracts, validate-rust-test-traceability, validate-rust-ownership-budget, git diff --check, and nix build .#runtime --no-link. Deferred TOML adapter evaluation remains open as yazelix-ylt4.10 and is intentionally outside the JSONC-first extraction completion criteria.

title: Inventory ratconfig reusable core and Yazelix adapter boundary
description: Map the current config UI/settings.jsonc implementation into reusable ratconfig concerns versus Yazelix-specific adapter concerns. Identify modules, types, tests, and docs that can move to a child repo, and the app-specific code that must remain in Yazelix.
design: Start from source evidence, not guesses. Classify each relevant file/function as reusable core, JSONC adapter, Yazelix adapter, migration primitive, UI rendering/control, test fixture, or stale/deletable code. The output should drive the extraction slices and avoid moving Yazelix-only behavior into the child repo.
acceptance_criteria: Boundary inventory records the target child-repo modules, the remaining Yazelix adapter modules, deletion candidates, current tests that should move, tests that should remain, and blockers for extraction. Follow-up child beads are updated if the inventory discovers missing slices.
notes:
2026-05-22 inventory pass:
- Relevant main-repo surface is about 5.5k LOC: config_ui.rs 3125, config_ui/apply_adapter.rs 47, yazelix_ratconfig/{model,editor,render,mod}.rs 1600, settings_jsonc_patch.rs 240, plus config UI and JSONC patch tests.
- Current reusable candidate exists under rust_core/yazelix_core/src/yazelix_ratconfig, but it is still a private namespace, not project-agnostic. model.rs owns rows, fields, tabs, search, generic JSON path helpers, state, sidecars, diagnostics, native-status display rows, and apply-status display data. editor.rs owns navigation/edit parsing and picker behavior. render.rs owns ratatui drawing.
- Hard coupling that blocks extraction: render.rs imports crate::config_ui::ConfigUiApp; editor.rs imports ConfigUiApp plus config_ui keybinding helpers/action metadata; model.rs stores Yazelix-flavored paths, config owner names, sidecars, native config statuses, and apply statuses directly; settings_jsonc_patch.rs uses CoreError/ErrorClass and Yazelix-specific error wording; config_ui.rs owns both Yazelix adapter work and reusable app event/save orchestration.
- Yazelix-only adapter duties that should remain in main repo: runtime/config path discovery, settings/default/schema/contract loading, Home Manager ownership, native config status classification, cursor registry composition, keybinding action registry expansion, generated refresh/pane refresh apply behavior, runtime apply-mode wording, and config normalization validation.
- Reusable child-repo duties to extract: generic config document model, tabs/rows/search, field edit modes, scalar parsing, bool toggle, single-select and multiselect pickers, generic detail/list rendering, JSONC patch primitives, deterministic migration primitives, and project-agnostic tests/examples.
- Main blocker order: first move ConfigUiApp-like state and event actions into the reusable namespace without request/path/apply dependencies; then replace keybinding/Yazelix-specific detail rendering with adapter-supplied structured rows; then split JSONC patching error model from CoreError; then move code/tests into the child repo.
- Existing tests are useful but mostly Yazelix integration tests. Child repo needs smaller generic tests plus one non-Yazelix fixture proving no Yazelix path/action/runtime dependency.
close_reason: Completed inventory: mapped reusable ratconfig core, Yazelix adapter duties, hard extraction blockers, and created focused boundary-thinning follow-up beads under yazelix-ylt4.3.

title: Evaluate TOML adapter for yazelix-ratconfig
description: After the JSONC-first ratconfig extraction lands, evaluate whether yazelix-ratconfig should grow a TOML adapter for projects that prefer TOML, especially Rust projects.
design: Do not block the initial extraction on TOML. Evaluate based on real child API shape, parser/editing fidelity, comment preservation, ecosystem demand, and whether TOML support stays generic without leaking Yazelix assumptions.
acceptance_criteria: Decision records whether to add a TOML adapter, defer it, or reject it. If accepted, create implementation beads covering parser/editor fidelity, tests, docs, and examples.

title: Define yazelix-ratconfig public crate and repo contract
description: Define the public boundary for the new yazelix-ratconfig child repo before moving code. The contract should cover crate layout, public APIs, ownership of JSONC settings editing, migration primitives, UI model/render controls, error model, tests, docs, versioning, and what Yazelix keeps private.
design: Keep the API small and project-agnostic. Prefer typed model/editor/render/migration primitives over Yazelix-specific concepts. Expose JSONC first. Do not expose Home Manager, Zellij, runtime refresh, Yazelix settings paths, or generated config ownership through the child API.
acceptance_criteria: A contract decision records the initial child repo/crate layout, supported JSONC adapter behavior, migration operation model, public/private API boundary, test strategy, packaging path, and explicit non-goals such as TOML support for the first extraction. Implementation beads refer to this contract.
notes: 2026-05-22 contract update: rewrote docs/contracts/yazelix_ratconfig_extraction.md from private-boundary/not-ready wording to active extraction. Contract now defines child repo ownership, Yazelix adapter ownership, public crate modules, JSONC patch contract, deterministic migration contract, non-goals for TOML, and verification before/after extraction. Verification: cargo run --quiet --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_maintainer --bin yzx_repo_validator -- validate-contracts; git diff --check.
close_reason: Completed: ratconfig extraction contract now defines the active child-repo boundary, public module shape, JSONC-first adapter contract, migration contract, and verification path.

title: Thin Yazelix config UI adapter before extraction
description: Refactor the main-repo config UI/settings code so reusable editor/model/render behavior is separated from Yazelix-specific IO, ownership reporting, Home Manager/native status, runtime refresh, and apply behavior. This is the main readiness work if the current boundary is too thick.
design: Do not change user-visible behavior. Move toward a shape where Yazelix builds a generic ratconfig document/model and delegates editing/rendering, while Yazelix-specific code owns loading settings, writing settings.jsonc, interpreting metadata, and running generated refresh/apply actions.
acceptance_criteria: Reusable ratconfig code is isolated behind a small module boundary with no direct dependencies on Yazelix runtime paths, Home Manager, Zellij/Yazi, or generated refresh. Existing config UI behavior is preserved by focused tests/manual smoke. Main-repo LOC and dependency direction are measured before extraction.
close_reason: Completed: the Yazelix config UI adapter is now thin enough for extraction. Generic ratconfig app state, edit behavior metadata, JSONC patch primitives, non-Yazelix fixture coverage, and adapter module boundaries are separated from Yazelix-specific IO/runtime/Home Manager/apply logic. Verification came through the child beads, including cargo check, config_ui unit/integration tests, settings_jsonc_patch tests, traceability validator, diff checks, and LOC scorecards.

title: Move generic ratconfig app state out of Yazelix adapter
description: Move ConfigUiApp-like navigation/search/edit state and generic event actions into the reusable ratconfig boundary so editor.rs and render.rs no longer import crate::config_ui::ConfigUiApp. Yazelix-specific request/path/apply data must stay in the adapter.
design: The reusable app state should own model, selected tab/row, search state, edit state, and notice/status display. It should expose generic actions/results for move, tab, begin edit, quick edit, unset, save request, and cancel. The Yazelix adapter handles persistence when the generic app emits a write/unset request.
acceptance_criteria: yazelix_ratconfig editor/render modules compile without importing crate::config_ui::ConfigUiApp. Config UI keyboard behavior is preserved by focused tests. Yazelix adapter still owns file IO, validation, reload, and apply behavior.
notes:
Crate decision before Rust implementation: no new crates. This slice uses the existing ratatui/crossterm/serde_json dependencies already in yazelix_core and only moves generic app state across internal modules. No packaging impact until the later child-repo extraction slice.
2026-05-22 implementation: moved generic ConfigUiApp state and ConfigUiNotice into yazelix_ratconfig/editor.rs, left Yazelix-specific ConfigUiRequest in a YazelixConfigUiApp adapter wrapper, and changed render.rs to depend only on the ratconfig-local app type. This preserves current keyboard/save behavior while removing the render/editor dependency on crate::config_ui::ConfigUiApp. Verification: cargo fmt --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml --all --check; cargo check --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core config_ui; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core settings_jsonc_patch; no crate::config_ui::ConfigUiApp imports remain under yazelix_ratconfig; git diff --check. LOC scorecard HEAD->candidate: runtime +96/-71 net +25, tokei code +20; accepted as small boundary-prep overhead before extraction deletes moved ownership.
close_reason: Completed: generic ratconfig app state now lives under yazelix_ratconfig; Yazelix request/path/apply state stays in the adapter wrapper; focused config UI checks pass.

title: Replace Yazelix-specific keybinding rendering hooks with adapter-supplied detail data
description: Remove direct action-registry/keybinding knowledge from reusable ratconfig editor/render code. Keybinding maps and action rows should become adapter-supplied structured detail data or field metadata, so the child repo can render rich project-specific rows without knowing Yazelix actions.
design: Yazelix may continue to expand zellij/yazi action rows in config_ui.rs, but the generic layer should only see field rows, detail sections, labels, options, and edit policies. Preserve the current rich keybinding details as adapter data.
acceptance_criteria: yazelix_ratconfig editor/render modules do not import Yazelix action registry or config_ui keybinding helper functions. Current keybinding detail tests still pass or are moved to adapter tests. Generic child-bound tests cover structured details without Yazelix actions.
notes:
Crate decision before Rust implementation: no new crates. This slice models project-specific edit behavior as field metadata using existing Rust types and keeps Yazelix action-registry data in the main adapter.
2026-05-22 implementation: introduced ConfigUiEditBehavior field metadata so the reusable editor can handle friendly string-list editing and structured-only notices without importing Yazelix config_ui helpers or action registries. Yazelix adapter now supplies keybinding-map/cursor structured-only notices and friendly string-list behavior for expanded action rows. Verification: cargo fmt --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml --all --check; cargo check --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core config_ui; no crate::config_ui/action_registry/YAZI/ZELLIJ keybinding imports remain under yazelix_ratconfig; git diff --check. LOC scorecard HEAD->candidate: runtime +46/-19 net +27, tokei code +25; accepted as small metadata cost that removes a hard child-repo dependency before extraction.
close_reason: Completed: reusable ratconfig editor/render no longer import Yazelix config_ui helpers or action registries; Yazelix-specific keybinding/cursor edit behavior is supplied as field metadata.

title: Split JSONC patching from Yazelix CoreError
description: Generalize settings_jsonc_patch so the reusable JSONC patch primitives can live in yazelix-ratconfig without depending on Yazelix CoreError, ErrorClass, or Yazelix-specific remediation wording.
design: Introduce a small generic patch error type in the reusable layer, then let the Yazelix adapter map it into CoreError. Preserve comment-preserving set/unset behavior and exact safety failures.
acceptance_criteria: JSONC patch primitives have project-agnostic errors and tests. Yazelix callers still receive the same user-facing error class/codes/messages through adapter mapping. Existing settings_jsonc_patch behavior tests pass.
notes:
Crate decision before Rust implementation: no new crates. This slice keeps jsonc-parser/serde_json already used by yazelix_core and introduces a local generic error type plus Yazelix adapter mapping. No packaging impact until the child repo move.
2026-05-22 implementation: split JSONC patch primitives from Yazelix CoreError by adding generic set_jsonc_value_text/unset_jsonc_value_text and SettingsJsoncPatchError, then keeping set_settings_jsonc_value_text/unset_settings_jsonc_value_text as Yazelix adapter wrappers. Moved jsonc_parse_options into the patch module so the future child JSONC backend owns parser policy. Added a regression test proving generic patch errors are project-agnostic before CoreError mapping. Verification: cargo fmt --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml --all --check; cargo check --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core --test yzx_core_settings_jsonc_patch; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core --test yzx_core_config_ui; validate-rust-test-traceability; git diff --check. LOC scorecard HEAD->candidate: runtime +122/-69 net +53, tests +15/-1 net +14, tokei code +59; accepted as generic error/adapter separation needed before child extraction.
close_reason: Completed: JSONC patch primitives now expose generic errors and Yazelix maps them into CoreError through adapter wrappers; focused patch/config UI tests pass.

title: Split config_ui.rs into Yazelix adapter modules
description: Break the 3k-line config_ui.rs adapter into coherent Yazelix-owned modules after generic app state is isolated. Separate model building, keybinding row expansion/details, persistence, terminal loop, metadata loading, cursor composition, and tests where that reduces extraction risk.
design: This is not a cosmetic split. Split only along ownership boundaries that help extraction or delete duplication. Keep public command behavior stable and avoid creating wrapper layers that will be moved again immediately.
acceptance_criteria: config_ui.rs is reduced to a small orchestration module with adapter submodules owning clear Yazelix-specific duties. Tests remain focused and pass. The split makes the child-repo move smaller rather than increasing total ownership complexity.
close_reason: Completed: split config_ui.rs into focused Yazelix adapter modules: app loop/edit persistence, detail rendering, keybinding adapter logic, model/persistence building, and tests. config_ui.rs is now the small adapter shell/types surface (3120 lines down to 161). Verification: cargo fmt --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml --all; cargo check --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core config_ui; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core --test yzx_core_config_ui; validate-rust-test-traceability; git diff --check; git diff --cached --check. LOC scorecard HEAD..candidate including new modules: runtime +2996/-2966 net +30; tokei code +29.

title: Add non-Yazelix ratconfig fixture before extraction
description: Add a small synthetic non-Yazelix config fixture that exercises the reusable ratconfig model/editor/render/JSONC path without Yazelix settings paths, actions, runtime apply modes, Home Manager, or generated refresh behavior.
design: Use the fixture as an extraction proof. It should be small and behavior-focused, not a parallel app. Prefer generic field metadata, a JSONC document, one bool, one scalar select, and one multiselect. Existing config UI tests already cover diagnostics.
acceptance_criteria: A focused test proves the reusable ratconfig layer can build/edit/render a non-Yazelix config fixture. The test fails if the generic layer reaches into Yazelix paths, action registries, or runtime concepts.
notes: Crate decision before Rust implementation: no new crates. This proof fixture uses existing ratconfig model/editor/render code plus existing generic JSONC patch primitives.
close_reason: Completed: added a compact non-Yazelix ratconfig unit fixture proving the generic model/editor/render helpers and generic JSONC patcher work with host-neutral settings paths and fields. Verification: cargo fmt --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml --all; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core non_yazelix_fixture_uses_generic_model_editor_render_and_jsonc_patch; cargo check --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core --test yzx_core_settings_jsonc_patch; cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core --test yzx_core_config_ui; cargo run --quiet --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_maintainer --bin yzx_repo_validator -- validate-rust-test-traceability; git diff --check. title: Move select and multiselect controls into generic ratconfig UI
description: Make single-select and multiselect fields first-class generic ratconfig controls instead of ad hoc Yazelix-only UI behavior. Boolean fields can remain direct space-toggle controls, but enum/select fields should use an option-picking UI that is reusable by any project using ratconfig.
design: Keep interaction semantics generic: a field may have one option, many options, or boolean toggle behavior. The child repo should own the UI state and rendering rules; Yazelix only supplies field metadata, current values, defaults, and persistence hooks.
acceptance_criteria: Single-select fields use a richer option picker UI comparable to multiselect but with one active choice. Multiselect and bool behavior still works. The generic control model has focused tests and is free of Yazelix-specific field names.
close_reason: Completed: select and multiselect controls live in the generic ratconfig editor/render path. Scalar enum fields use ConfigUiEditMode::Choice with single-choice detail rows; enum string-list fields use ConfigUiEditMode::MultiChoice with checkbox-style detail rows; bool fields remain direct toggles. Verification: cargo test --manifest-path rust_core/Cargo.toml -p yazelix_core config_ui covers scalar enum picker, enum string-list picker, cursor dynamic choices, bool direct controls, and non-Yazelix generic fixture coverage.

title: Create the yazelix-ratconfig child repository
description: Create the separate first-party yazelix-ratconfig repository and establish its initial package/crate shape. The repo should be usable independently by other projects that want a ratatui config UI over JSONC settings.
design: Prefer a minimal Rust crate/repo shape with clear README, license, tests, and examples. Keep Yazelix integration code out. If the GitHub repository does not already exist, create it under the maintainer account after the local package shape is ready enough to push.
acceptance_criteria: A sibling yazelix-ratconfig repo exists locally and remotely, with a compilable Rust crate, README describing project-agnostic usage, license metadata, focused tests, and no dependency on the Yazelix main repo. The initial public API matches the contract bead.
close_reason: Completed: created sibling child repo /home/lucca/pjs/yazelix-ratconfig and remote https://github.com/luccahuguet/yazelix-ratconfig. Initial crate has Cargo metadata, Apache-2.0 license, README, public modules for model/editor/render/jsonc/migration, a focused project-agnostic public-shape test, and no dependency on the Yazelix main repo. Verification: cargo fmt; cargo test. Child commit f0ad347 pushed to origin/main.

title: Move reusable ratconfig code and tests to child repo
description: Move the reusable config model, editor state, render controls, JSONC document editing, migration primitives, and generic tests from Yazelix into the yazelix-ratconfig child repo. Delete moved main-repo code instead of duplicating it.
design: Perform the child move after the main-repo boundary is thin enough. Preserve behavior through tests before and after the move. Keep Yazelix-specific field metadata generation, Home Manager/user ownership, runtime apply, and generated refresh in the main repo. Do not call the extraction complete until the integration bead deletes the main-repo duplicate.
acceptance_criteria: Reusable code and tests live in the child repo with no dependency on the Yazelix main repo. Child tests pass. Any temporary main-repo duplicate is not a completed extraction state and must be deleted by the integration bead that wires Yazelix to the child dependency.
notes: Rust dependency gate for child move: production crates are ratatui for generic rendering, serde_json for values, jsonc-parser for comment-preserving JSONC CST edits, and thiserror for project-agnostic error modeling if needed. Dev-only crates stay minimal; current tests use std plus serde_json. In-house logic remains the ratconfig model/editor/render control semantics and deterministic JSONC migration operation ordering. Rejected alternatives: keep ratconfig inside yazelix_core; add a broad TUI/app framework; add TOML support in the initial extraction. Packaging impact: child crate becomes a normal Git dependency for Yazelix after the integration bead, with Cargo.lock ownership in both repos.
close_reason: Completed: moved the reusable ratconfig UI core into the child repo at /home/lucca/pjs/yazelix-ratconfig and pushed commit a72563f. The child crate now owns model, editor, render, JSONC patch, migration primitives, and generic tests without depending on Yazelix main-repo code. Verification: cargo fmt; cargo test in the child repo. Main-repo duplicate deletion and dependency wiring are explicitly owned by yazelix-ylt4.7.

title: Wire Yazelix to consume yazelix-ratconfig
description: Update Yazelix to consume the new yazelix-ratconfig child repo through the normal dependency/flake/package path. Preserve the current config UI and settings.jsonc behavior while replacing in-repo reusable ratconfig ownership with the child crate.
design: Follow the cross-repo release transaction rule. Validate first through a local path/override, then push the child repo, update the main lock/dependency to the published revision, and validate without overrides before closing.
acceptance_criteria: Yazelix depends on published yazelix-ratconfig rather than local in-repo reusable code. Config UI behavior is preserved. Main repo flake/dependency metadata is updated. Normal validation passes without override inputs after the child repo is pushed.
notes: Integration dependency decision: consume yazelix-ratconfig as a direct Cargo Git dependency pinned to a published revision. No Nix flake input is needed for the library crate at this stage because yazelix_core builds it through Cargo. The main repo keeps only adapter code and maps child JSONC errors into CoreError.
close_reason: Completed: Yazelix now consumes the pushed yazelix-ratconfig child crate at 653e41f2338a46285cd6b25603390d5cea28a491. Main keeps Yazelix-specific adapters only: rich config UI detail rendering is supplied as a callback, and settings_jsonc_patch.rs maps reusable JSONC patch errors into CoreError. title: Document ratconfig extraction boundary and usage
description: Document the final ratconfig extraction boundary in both repos: how external projects use yazelix-ratconfig with JSONC settings, what Yazelix-specific behavior remains in Yazelix, and what adapters are intentionally deferred.
design: Child repo docs should be project-agnostic and example-driven. Yazelix docs should describe only the user-facing settings/config UI behavior and the maintainer integration boundary where relevant. Avoid implementation diaries in docs; keep decision history in Beads.
acceptance_criteria: yazelix-ratconfig README explains generic usage with JSONC settings and config UI concepts. Yazelix docs remain accurate about settings.jsonc/config UI behavior. Deferred TOML adapter and other future adapters are captured in Beads, not overpromised in docs.
close_reason: Completed: updated the main ratconfig extraction contract from active extraction to complete_jsonc_first, added yazelix-ratconfig to the child repo catalog and ownership audit, resolved stale 'defer public ratconfig' documentation, and pushed child README docs explaining host-owned schema/validation/persistence/apply behavior plus ratconfig-owned model/editor/render/JSONC/migration behavior. Child docs commit: luccahuguet/yazelix-ratconfig@5dc06d9. title: Clean up main-repo ratconfig leftovers after extraction
description: After Yazelix consumes the child repo, remove stale main-repo ratconfig scaffolding, duplicate tests, docs drift, local-only fixtures, and migration-era adapters that no longer own behavior.
design: Use the Spartan LOC Protocol. Delete moved or obsolete code instead of preserving compatibility wrappers. Keep only Yazelix-specific adapter tests that defend user-visible behavior. Ratchet any relevant LOC ownership budget down if applicable.
acceptance_criteria: Main repo contains no duplicate reusable ratconfig implementation. Tests and docs that referred to old in-repo ownership are updated or deleted. LOC scorecard is recorded. Yazelix validators and focused config UI tests pass.
close_reason: Completed: removed the last main-repo ratconfig cleanup leftovers from ownership metadata. Deleted in-repo ratconfig paths are no longer listed in rust_ownership_budget.toml, current config_ui adapter modules are budgeted explicitly, and rust_code_inventory.md describes yazelix-ratconfig as the reusable owner instead of a private/future boundary. The empty deleted source directory was removed from the worktree.
